description:
The South: Terminus, which combines set collection and hand management with multiple scoring avenues, is the fourth, and final, card game set in the world of The North: Provenance. Two players take turns acquiring unique Ancients that are then discarded from their hands for a variety of different in-game effects.

Players take turns acquiring Ancient cards from the top of three face up decks and playing the cards they've acquired several ways. Game play is driven by the Turn Sequence Card, which is passed back and forth throughout the game. As the game is played, players lose points, acquire new cards for their hand or Zone (play area), and score points at the end of the game for the cards beneath their Vault.

The East: Concordant, combines engine/combo building and hand management with multiple scoring avenues, and is the third card game set in the world of The North: Provenance. Two players take turns drawing and playing doublesided Facility cards a variety of ways for various in-game effects.

Players take turns drawing and playing cards: either as Facilities, Ancients, to power their IonShrine, or beneath their Furnace for end game points.  Game play is driven by the Turn Sequence Card, which is passed back and forth throughout the game. As the game is played, players lose points, acquire new cards for their hand or Zone (play area), and score points at the end of the game for the cards in their Zones and beneath their Furnace.

Help the little astronauts race around the solar system! It's time for the little astronauts first big space race. Help them fly across all the planets while avoiding space obstacles and collecting shields to be the first one to make it back to earth and win the big race!

In Little Astronauts Board Game, kids will race across the solar system by using their rocket fuel to fly through space. But they better watch out! Each round players will encounter an obstacle, and how far they move ahead will depend on how much rocket fuel they tried to use. Strategically use your rocket fuel to move ahead and avoid the space obstacles.

The first astronaut to race all the way around the solar system and make it back to earth wins the big space race!

Colorful and detailed images make this story driven game a great way for kids to develop their basic counting and strategic thinking skills. Limited reading and easy counting make this a game for kids aged 4+ to enjoy!

boardgame: My First Treasure Hunt Board Game
description:
Work together to race around the mysterious island and find the hidden treasure before the big storm covers the island! The treasure hunters have found a treasure map for the mysterious island and have arrived to find the hidden treasure. Players must work together to explore the different areas of the island to find all the treasure before they leave.

In My First Treasure Hunt kids race around the island, trying to collect all the hidden treasure while outrunning the big storm. Players will move across the board using their numbered cards to collect their treasure tokens while avoiding obstacles that will set them back. Work as a team to explore all the areas of the island in time to find the treasure.

Storm cards are mixed into the deck of cards players use to move around the board. When a storm card is picked up and played, a new storm cloud token is added to the top of the board. if all the storm cloud spaces are filled with cloud tokens before players find all the treasure and get back to their canoe, then the game is lost.

If the treasure hunters find all their treasure and make it back to their canoe before the storm covers the island, everyone wins!

My First Treasure Hunt uses creative and colorful scenes that make searching for treasure fun and imaginative. This cooperative game is a perfect introduction to basic counting, teamwork, and strategic thinking.

—description from the publisher

Help the ants work together to collect all their snacks before the pests eat everything in the garden! The ants are throwing a picnic and they must collect some food on their way, but they better be quick! Hungry pests are eating all the snacks and the ants must use teamwork to collect their snacks and make it to the picnic before everything is gone.

In Ants Picnic Party the ants are racing through the garden together to find their picnic snacks, but they must watch out for the pests that are eating up all the food. Players work together to move the ants by using image-based cards to find matching spaces and collect their snack tokens.

Pest cards are in the same deck of cards that the players use to move the ant characters. When a pest card is picked up, players remove one of the food tokens from the board, if all the food tokens are removed from the board before players make it to the end, then the game is lost.

If the ants collect all of their picnic snacks and make it to the picnic party before the pests eat all the food, then everybody wins!

Bright and colorful images and scenery make this a fun way for kids to learn communication, teamwork, strategic thinking, and image matching skills through play!
A set of 5 promo cards for the game Just One, featuring words inspired by BGG itself!

Lands of Loot is a fantasy board game for 2- 6 players. The players assume the role of an aspiring adventurer who sets out on a journey to build their reputation. At the end of the game each player will have made a tale of heroic deeds, awful blunders, epic battles, and of course loot!

Players start with creating a hero by drawing a combination of Folk and Class cards, then take turns rolling a 12 sided die and moving their meeple through a branching track. The game board itself is a map of the "Lands of Loot" and the track contains spaces that relate to their position on map.

The spaces can be events that cause the player to either gain or lose reputation points (RP)- sometimes paying the points to other players in the game. RP is represented by silver/gold coins and glass gems.

Some of the other spaces include monsters, which earn players RP based on their class, and loot chests which allow the player to draw from a limited deck of loot cards. When the loot deck runs out, loot can be taken from other players.

Once every player reaches the end of the track, the reputation for each player totaled and each player is given a title based on their finishing RP. The player with the highest RP is declared the winner.

—description from designer

Blended is a party game where players take turns drawing cards from a deck and play the mini games presented in them to win and to earn money. The deck is made up of different types of games, some of them competitive, others cooperative and some just random, (like a roulette where you bet on the color of the next card).

The game gets more interesting with the play cards that each player holds in their hands. with them, they can choose who to play with or against, and also they can attack, steal money and protect themselves from other players attacks.

The players in their turn will pick up a mini game, and using their own play cards, will choose who to play the game with or against. the winners get paid Blendollars.

The first player to get 25 Blendollars or be the last one with money is the winner.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: New Phone, Who Dis?: Bad Advice NSFW Expansion Pack
description: Add this expansion pack to your New Phone, Who Dis? core game for extra fun. Shuffle the cards together for risqué surprises, or keep them separate for a designated risky pick pile. Players draw the given number of Reply Cards, and try to create the funniest text conversations by matching them with that round's Inbox Card! The judge then picks the funniest combo and the game plays on.

Escape Room style game with a Robin Hood theme where Robin and his friend get ambushed.
—user summary

Ein Hinterhalt im Sherwood Forest
Robin Hood lauert mit seinen Gefährten einer Kutsche auf. Doch schon zu Beginn des Überfalls wird klar: Hier stimmt etwas nicht. Sie wurden erwartet. Als es ihren Gegnern gelingt zu entkommen, offenbart einer der Gefährten ein schreckliches Geheimnis. Da hat bestimmt der Sheriff von Nottingham seine Finger im Spiel! In diesem spannenden Escape Room-Kartenspiel schlüpft ihr in die Rolle von Robin Hood und müsst euch durch unzählige Hindernisse und Rätsel kämpfen, um eure Gefährten aus dem Verlies des Sheriffs zu retten. Nur wenn ihr einen scharfen Verstand, Einfallsreichtum und Gewitztheit beweist, kann euch das gelingen.
—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

description:
Truck lights. Screeching brakes. Darkness... then brilliant warmth!

You just got yourself isekai-ed into a world of monsters and magic. As the fabled hero of legend, you are destined to free the land from darkness. Neat.

Harnessing the power of the Mayaverse, call upon heroes from other timelines to join your quest to save this world from corruption. Lead your party to slay the dragon and dispel the Dark Realm Magic from this land forever!

Gameplay: Players create a party from a number of hero cards, then move across the map from location to location, fighting monsters they encounter. Attacking is done by rolling dice and keeping or re-rolling them to activate your heroes' powers.

—description from the designer

The 5th mage of "MAGISTRAR" – DRAKOR – is an exclusive add-on for our KS supporters and not included in the retail version.

Drakors special spell - additionally damage for your opponent: Put the opponent's scoring pawn there where it hurts.

49 shades of grey is a catching card game for two people. It requests to have a good eye, brains and tactical skills. The 49 cards are printed in different shades of grey on both sides and on the front side they are numbered from 1 (light grey) to 49 (dark grey). Can you guess the hidden number by assessing the shade of grey?

—description from the designer

Build Grinivil together, but win!

Players compete and collaborate to build a modern town and fulfill its residents' desires. Players take turns on the TAR (Time Action Ring) ring. Each turn a player performs takes a different time. All competing developers feel the impact of your actions and vice versa.

Players buy land, plant trees, build roads and develop the town. Effectively gain resources and construct houses, schools, shops, and other facilities wanted by Grinivil residents.

Some future inhabitants are open about their wishes for their living space. The desires of others remain hidden. To be successful, you must timely choose the proper actions, work with other developers and cunningly guide them to do what you want. Or better to say, what you think suits the modern and eco-oriented Grinivil the best.

You earn money and success victory points depending on how skillfully you develop a Grinivil.

—description from the publisher
Admin Test Item without Image

float downstream is a game like no other. Embark on a meditative journey. 36 unique river cards create a poetic landscape through which you slowly drift. With a mixture of luck and strategy, attempt to negotiate the currents of life's river, all the while maintaining a balanced mind against the thought forms that arise, threatening to pull you under. Death is inevitable, but how much of life's poetry can you experience on your journey to the river's end?

No matter what you do you will always die. Scores are calculated based on the number and type of thought counters one has in mind upon dying. The higher the score, the closer to enlightenment. This score gives one a 'ranking' within the game.

Two scoops or three? Create a sundae for each customer. The player who attracts the most customers wins!

In Sundae, players create combinations with dominoes. The better combinations are fancier sundaes to win over customers. Attract more customers than your opponent to win.

To play, seven customer tokens are placed between the players, and a double nine set of dominoes (without the double blank) is shuffled.

Players take turns playing a domino to their side of the row of customers and refilling their hand. Three dominoes (three scoops) make a sundae, and these sundaes are compared to see which the customer prefers. The player who claims four of the seven customers will win the game.

Sundae may also be played with two scoops (two dominoes per combination) and five customers. The player who claims three of the customers wins the game.

boardgame: Star Realms: Rise of Empire – Kickstarter Promo Pack
description:
Promos sent to Kickstarter backers with the Rise of Empire campaign.

This pack contains 61 Promo Cards, the largest promo pack to-date, for the game Star Realms.

The steam is up, the drivers engaged, and the first locomotives in Berlin and Paris have started clanking down the brand new tracks. Savvy, wealthy industrialists have scrambled to invest in this exciting new technology, and each hopes to reap untold fortunes from this unique venture. Some will do well, others not so much, but there will be only one that comes out on top.

Franco-German Rails is an expansion for Gulf, Mobile & Ohio that offers two different maps on which to play: France and Germany. These maps feature generic rail lines that start in specific cities with a set of share certificates for each.
family: Series: Winsome Train Games (Rio Grande Games)

boardgame: Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Expansions
description:
In Castles of Mad King Ludwig, you built the perfect castles subject to the whims of the King. But now he wants even more (he is mad, after all). Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Expansions contains several expansions that can be played separately or in any combination, as well as brand new bonus cards and favors.

Castles of Mad King Ludwig Expansions includes every previously released expansion for Castles of Mad King Ludwig and a new one, all compatible with the 2nd edition.

Renovations: Rework your castle with special double-room tiles that are placed directly on top of existing rooms.
Towers: Special rooms that provide an additional end game reward.
Moats: Surround your castle for extra VPs for each of your rooms.
Swan Tokens: Collect sets of swan tokens that can be converted to money or end game VPs.
Secret Passages: Double connection bonuses and fit rooms into hard to reach spaces.
Royal Decrees: Gain a unique, asymmetric player power at the start of the game.
New Favors & Bonus Cards: Increase the variety of end game goals.
5th Player: Add a 5th player to your game.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Castles of Mad King Ludwig

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Theros Beyond Death Planeswalker Deck: Elspeth, Undaunted Hero
description:
This Magic: The Gathering starter deck is ready to go with a 60 card ready deck, storage box and strategy guide. Battle a few times to understand how to play your deck, then open the 2 included booster packs to customize it.

Elspeth is a renowned warrior of surpassing skill and unshakable willpower, who has defeated everything from gods to death itself. Build up a battalion of devoted soldiers and lead them fearlessly to a glorious victory.

—description from the publisher

In the game, we go through earthly life and our goal is heaven. We move on the board with meeples using the dice. One by one we go through the sacraments which — as in life — help us grow and mature in faith. There are also bonuses on the board fields that can be obtained by answering questions on religious knowledge. Our movement can be accelerated by the graces of the Holy Spirit or of the Eucharist. When answering questions, we collect good or indulgence cards. There are also saints on the way to heaven, which can make children familiar with the saints.

—description from the publisher

Players take turns by rolling dice and moving their meeples through part of the board representing earthly life. While doing so they need to answer Christianity-related questions and gain positive cards that speed them up or negative ones that slow them down.

Once a player reaches the last judgment the cards gained impact on how many fields (if any!) the player has to cross in the purgatory before finally reaching heaven. While moving within the purgatory players need to do extra tasks.

After the player finally reaches heaven, they keep moving and helping other players reach there as well. The game ends once all players reach heaven in which case, they all win. But should even one of the players show up on the last judgment with a grave sin in their cards they might go to hell instead and the game is lost.

A simple card game for children. This is an 18 card deck with each card being double sided so that both sides may be played during the game. Each player must build a dinosaur using 4 cards to represent the body, head, legs and tail of the dinosaur. Whoever builds their dinosaur first wins.

—description from designer
family: Contests: Button Shy Game Design Contests and Challenges

description:
In the first single-villain expansion, Venom is trying to merge with Spiderman to win. His allies, the other symbiotes will assist in this goal, while the dastardly heros, such as the Silver Spider will be obstacles in its path.

This expansion requires another Marvel Villainous product to play.

Unfinity is the fifth Un-set (after Unglued, Unstable, Unhinged, and Unsanctioned).

Like the other 4, Unfinity also contains zany, humorous and often game-breaking cards. Again full of inside jokes, references, 4th wall breaks, strange effects, and crazy characters! It contains 244 cards.

Unlike the other 4 Un-sets, which are all not legal in any MTG format, over half of Unfinity's cards are legal in eternal formats (commander, legacy, vintage).

Unfinity is also the first Un-set with both Draft boosters and Collector boosters.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A turn-based party card game.

In this game, you’ll see how fantasy Snacks help you finish Trials, and make Puppy EleMons grow up into powerful EleMons.
The First Player who finishes enough Trials to have 9+ Trial Points is the Winner!

How to play:
1.All the players take Turns one by one, in clockwise.

2.In your Turn, you take 2 Actions, which could be same and must be one of the three below:

A) : Draw 1 card from the deck.

    Your Hand Limit is 6. You Cannot take  when you already have 6+ cards in Hand.


B) : Play 1 card in front of you, from your own Hand.AND use its Effect!

    The cards you Played must be kept in front of you, until you Discard them in .
    Some Snack has Response.Such Snack can be played in response to other player’s Snack with Effect.AND only in such situation, its Response can be resolved.


C) : Finish Trial cards as many as you can! AND get all the Trial Points!

    Each Element icon on Snack cards can only be used once, providing you 1 Element. AND at the end of the Action you must Discard the Snack cards if you have used 1+ icons from them.
    Each Element icon on flipped EleMon cards can be used once per , providing you 1 Element. AND you NEVER Discard your EleMon cards!


3.The First Player who has 9+ Trial Points becomes the Winner!

8 Horses race the track and any number of players may place bets for "Win" or "Place". Movement of the horses is determined by a spinner, which shows for each horse, depending on the space it occupies, how much spaces it may advance, if any. The race ends, when 3 horses have reached the finish line and the winnings from the bets are being payed.

The game was most likely published in the 1920s.

—user summary

The players roll the dice to move their "caps" around the board. If a player lands on another cap or a stack of caps, that player captures the stack by placing his cap on top of the stack. A player then seeks to return that stack of caps to home-base to keep them secured and free his own caps from the stack. The violet spaces are safe spaces where caps can't be captured. If a player rolls a 6, he turns the turntable one space clockwise after moving his cap. The game ends when there is only one active color of caps left on the board. The last player who has active caps left on the board is the winner.

—user summary

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach the finish wins the game.

—user summary

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach space 46 wins the game.

This game was a birthday giveaway for kids and seems to be from the 1950s.

—user summary

Die Dohlen vom Dohlenfelsen ("The Jackdaws of the Jackdaw-Rock")is a wooden family game from 5 years, made in the Urdonautal in the heart of Bavaria. Who remembers best which caves the jackdaws bring the magic stones to and, with a bit of luck, will reach the bulging caves. You always have to keep an eye on which caves the most jackdaws have flown to and which cave has not been cleared out for a long time. Whoever has collected the most stones by the end of the game is the winner.

Die Dohlen vom Dohlenfelsen is a memory game with 3D construction and a sophisticated 'cave system' into which small pebbles are thrown. If you want to clear out a cave, pull the plug out a bit and the magic jackdaw stones will crackle out of the game like in a slot machine. The game is stored in a yellow cotton bag that is certified with the Oeko-Tex label. This bag serves as a base when playing, so that one can speak of an 'unpackaged game'. In addition to the 3D game board, the game also includes 6 game figures. Also included: 22 wooden foundation stones, a handful of hand-sorted pebbles, a small piece of twig peeled with a pocket knife as a stone pick and a color cube. The silhouette of the jackdaw rock is hand-cut. Also the bite of the beaver is sometimes bigger, sometimes smaller, sometimes bitchy and sometimes jagged and sometimes other accessories have been gnawed on a bit. Each game is therefore unique, which is acknowledged with a consecutive serial number and the name of the summiteer who owns the game. These two pieces of information are found on the front of the Jackdaw Rock game board.

—description from the publisher

This world belongs to the titans. Godzilla, the alpha predator, and Kong, king of Skull Island, face off in a battle that shakes the very foundations of the Earth. Joining the fight is MechaGodzilla, a mechanical abomination created by mankind and inside which beats the infamous heart of an old enemy. Who will win?

A game of Godzilla vs Kong is played in turns, during which each player rolls the dice looking to make combinations with the six possible results, so that the titan can perform different attacks on its opponent. 
When the life of one of the titans reaches 0, or if the combat board turns to 0, the game ends.

—description form the publisher

boardgame: Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Renovations
description:
Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Renovations are special double room type tiles that you can use to renovate your castle...while you build it! Get double the completion rewards and fulfill favors twice as fast with a wide variety of renovations in every size.

This stand alone version of “Renovations” will not be released to retail.

This expansion is compatible with all versions of the base game except the Colossal Edition: 
First Edition
Second Edition
Collector’s Edition
Royal Collector’s Edition
family: Game: Castles of Mad King Ludwig

description:
Your game table is about to become infected with Gurms! Don’t worry though, coming down with a case of these cuties is a good thing! In this tile-matching strategy game for kids and adults, your mission is to complete as many pictures of the gurms as possible.

—description from the publisher

On your turn, place your tile touching at least one other tile. All edges that are touching must match, and white can't match with anything, not even other white edges. Match at least two edges to immediately take another turn.

Every edge that matches will create a Gurm. Your goal is to make connected groups of your own Gurm type, either side-to-side or corner-to-corner. Each Gurm in a group is worth as many points as the size of the group! When there are no more tiles to be placed, the player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Theros Beyond Death Planeswalker Deck: Ashiok, Sculptor of Fears
description:
This Magic: The Gathering starter deck is ready to play right out of the box. Battle a few times to master your deck’s strategy, then open the 2 included booster packs to customize it.

Ashiok has power to torment foes by conjuring their darkest memories, fears, and regrets. With Ashiok’s deck, you’ll fill your graveyard along with your opponent’s for massive value as you slowly drive them insane.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Travelers of the World Famous Destinations
description:
Travelers of the World Famous Destinations - This is a strategy board game where players follow a tour guide to collect points.

A player starts a turn by rolling 4 dice, two white and two colored (blue) dice.  The white dice controls the tour guide and the colored dice control your colored travelers (meeples). Each player has their own color but everyone uses the same tour guide.  The object is to line up your travelers (meeples) in the same column as the tour guide to collect points.  The first player to 300 points wins the game.

In the destination version, you must collect all seven destinations as well as get the 300 points. To do this you either land on a destination, get a card to collect it/steal it, or buy the destination for 100 points; but remember you must also collect the 300 points to win.

Happy Travels!!

1810: Bussaco simulates this battle in lavish historical details using the "Fix Bayonets!" tactical system. The map was carefully drawn based on multiple historical sources while the unit counters sport the colors of the uniforms that were worn by those combat units that day. Two decks of Leader cards help you determine the order of activation of each of the armies' formations.

This is Volume II of the Fix Bayonets! system, simulating combat at the battalion level between the armies of the Napoleonic Wars. The rules include an emphasis on Command and Control, a card driven initiative activation system and limit the use of informational markers. The combat resolution system differentiates between the effects of fire, infantry assaults and cavalry charges. While not overly difficult, learning the game can be a challenging but rewarding experience for all wargamers.

Counters are (mostly) Batallion (infantry), regiment (Cavalry), battery (artillery) & Leaders
Game System "Fix Bayonets!"
Hexes are 100m
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Land of Z is a tactical game in a zombie universe. Players must survive while waiting for a helicopter and flee in it. The game has two modes: cooperative and semi-competitive (with the same rules). The game mechanics are based on an original system of movements and actions on an area of sixteen cards (4x).

For the turn : a player can do one action : move, use a card equipment card or attack a zombie.

There is only one way to win: reach the helicopter that arrives at the end of the game.

And two ways to lose: all players die or there are no more life markers in the general supply.

-description from designer

boardgame: Fukuoka (fan expansion for Age of Steam)
description:
Age of Steam: Fukuoka is an expansion map for Age of Steam which introduces an area majority mechanic.  The board is divided into four areas, and when a player delivers a cube, they take a track ownership disc from that route and move it to one of the special boxes tracking the majorities for any of the regions the route passes through.  A neutral player color disc is given ownership of the track and any players may use the track.

There are special actions around reclaiming ownership of track, adding discs to the majority boxes, and swapping discs in the boxes.

boardgame: Yamaguchi (fan expansion for Age of Steam)
description: Age of Steam: Yamaguchi is a map which integrates a catch up system, based upon the history of the area. The auction in a typical Age of Steam game is replaced with a valuation based on a player's income and number of shares issued.  The typical action selection system is replaced with a rondel.

The most Influential tech event is about to begin and the launch of MAI-42 is the main event. Programmers have been working on MAI for years to change the landscape of robotics for years to come. There is only one problem. MAI-42 doesn't behave as it should. It seems that some bug or virus is preventing MAI from working properly and resists following simple verbal directions. The show is about to begin, so the programmers will have to manage with simple tasks.

In the game, all players play the role of MAI once and the rest of players play the role of programmers competing to get the highest score by getting MAI to perform the assigned task successfully. Role playing is a very important component of this game, as a robot you can enjoy improvising and applying your ingenuity when executing the instructions. At the same time, as a programmer you should make an effort to try to write specific and detailed instructions to the robot, understanding they can execute them in the most diverse and curious ways.

Each programmer reads aloud their code, line by line, allowing MAI to run the instructions. MAI listens to the instructions and carries them out to the letter as much as possible, while actively looking for ways to "misinterpret" the commands.

The game takes place over as many rounds as players. In each round, a player role plays MAI-42 and the rest of the players are programmers. A round is divided into two phases: Writing and Execution

At the end of the game, the player with the most victory points wins the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Peaky Blinders: Il Gioco di Bluff
description:
In Birmingham's dark alleys, the Peaky Blinders thrive their illegal business under the noses of their rivals and the police! In the shadows, however, their opponents are doing everything possible to reverse this balance of power!

In this game of bluffing and secret identity set in the Peaky Blinders universe, two teams compete against each other: the Shelby family, who will have to collect as many banknotes as possible before the end of the 5th round, and Billy Kimber's men, who will have to stop him at all costs! Who can you trust, though, with Inspector Campbell always lurking nearby?

Your monkey has stolen your time travel trousers, and you must fight your way through time and space to get him back.

To accomplish this, each player selects a card representing one of many parallel incarnations of Professor Elemental, each equipped with unique and unlikely weapons.  Their collective challenge is to navigate a timeline, generated from the timeline deck, and to cooperate to defeat the dangers and vexations it throws in their paths.

On their ape-pursuit, the players will find a deck of items to assist and amuse them, and will be plagued by the problems that emerge from the malfunction deck.  Once the final card of the timeline has been revealed and overcome, the players are victorious, but if just one of the many Professors runs out of health before the quest is complete, the whole team loses - that’s just how parallel timelines work, apparently.

A charming game of trousers, butlers and alternate realities inspired by the steampunk chap-hop concept album of the same name by Professor Elemental, renowned scientist, exotic dancer and explorer.

boardgame: Clank!: Catacombs – Dusty Map Promo Card
description:
A promo card to add to the game, first distributed with the preorder of the game.

Rule:
Place a new tile anywhere.
(Choose the location for the tile before revealing it, but THEN choose its orientation as usual.)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Ypres Salient: A soldier's life during the great war
description:
What was life like for the millions of professional, conscripted or recruited soldiers, who fought in the Ypres salient during the great war? The trench experience involved the terror of mud, slime and disease and the constant threat of shellfire. Heavy artillery and new weapons such as poison gas and machine guns threatened
death from afar; but hand to hand combat with clubs and knives killed many during the grisly business of trench raids. 
When troops were not fighting, they were locked into trench deadlock, at which point  boredom also became a serious issue. Luckily there where the letters from home and regular
behind the lines periods to keep the soldiers going.

This game, or rather simulation, lets you lead your character(s) through the great war.
It’s very uncertain that a soldier who starts at the beginning of the war will make it until November 11,1918 but maybe he is lucky.
Best is to take into account that this game will make you have several soldiers who will at a certain moment during the war get killed in the field, die of wounds, get wounded but rejoin their unit, get a wound that will get them home as a cripple, have them hospitalized with health problems from which they maybe recover but equally have a chance to be send home; unfit for further duty. Finaly there are mental problems like shell shock in the game. A soldier has the chance to get fit for duty after a while or be send home to a mental institute but there is also a chance that he will be threatened as a coward who is unwilling to serve. In that case he will end before the firing squad.

This is a role playing game for 1 to six players where you build the stories of soldiers around Ypres by using cards, tables and some sheets. Let your imagination create their lives and death while in Flanders fields.
Like real life soldiers you for your character will have almost no influence on it.
But you have some topics that you can get changes on. That, in the end, could become the difference between life and death.

The game is mainly card driven. Since the trench lines only fluctuated little  during the conflict around Ypres there is no use for a map of the area.
For the soldiers a trench was a trench, maps where something for the higher ranks.
Each game round plays over a two months period in which a series of events will take place.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Guns or Treasure: Parrots and Monkeys
description:
The Parrots and Monkeys expansion allows you to play up to 9 players and adds new ship cards and captains!

Captains: Mme Liza Perruche,  Bruno Barqueiro, Charlie Hightower
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
Become a detective in this Christmas adventure, in which you will have to investigate the mysteries of Santa Claus’ secret Villa.

This year, things don’t seem as magical as usual, and you’ve just received a letter from Santa Claus himself asking for your help: “Bring back Christmas!”

Santa Claus’ town, Xmas Village, has lost its magic - it looks like someone has stolen it. Without the magic, the Christmas spirit will not reach millions of people throughout the world!

Search, investigate,and discover the mysteries of Santa Claus’ secret village and its surroundings in Lapland. We have generated a realistic environment with interesting elements that will immerse you in a great Christmas adventure without precedent.

The Expansion Game introduces the Research Pod, an adorable mini spaceship that holds two ducks. Flip it around and dock it to your board in any orientation collect ducks in wild new formations.

The expansion also comes with GLITTER DUCKS, a brand new breed of duck that behaves differently. Use them them to clog up your opponents' boards in the early game, but then get them back by the endgame because they're worth points!

The Abducktion Expansion Game includes:

Expands game up to 6 players (includes 2 extra boards, and 40 extra ducks!)
Includes 6 research pods
GLITTER DUCKS!
Includes 20 new challenging Formation Cards (worth higher points!)
Games last 25-30 minutes instead of 15-20
publishers: Evan and Josh's Very Special Games Company (Very Special Games)

boardgame: Onitama: Hornet and Centipede Promo Cards
description:
A set of 2 cards packaged together in Pack E of The Dice Tower 2022 Kickstarter campaign.

These cards provide new movement options for your pieces.

Originally available as part of Pack E from the Dice Tower 2022 Kickstarter campaign.

A new token.

Opponent places all tokens currently behind screen in front of screen. Newly drawn tokens are placed behind screen.

boardgame: Basketball Coach: A Hall of Fame Game
description:
In Basketball Coach you play as a coach and call in plays as you try to become a world champion on your way to developing a hall of fame career. The game is a variant of and homage to Shanghai Rummy while incorporating the theme and tension associated with coaching basketball. Each game is made up of stops and runs.

A stop is a play with at least three of the same card. Suit does not matter when playing a stop. Think three of a kind.

A run is a play with at least four cards in sequence of players from the same suit. Think straight.

On your turn, you must: 1) draw a card from the top of the deck or the top of the free agent pool (discard pile); 2) Set your roster in hand; 3) Discard a player. The round ends when the first person gets rid of all of the cards in their hand.

You may also: Call in a play if you have the right players in hand. Plays are called after a card is drawn and before a card is discarded.

The game ends after the 7th round and the Coach given up the fewest points wins the game and is on their way to a Hall of Fame career!

Munchkin Goats is a new mini-expansion that adds a herd of new Doors and Treasures to your Munchkin decks. Be sure to wear your More Hair Coat while you try hopping over the Goat Moat on your way to take down Baphomet!

Cardoku is a head-to-head Sudoku game using Tech Decks, modernized decks of playing cards.

Players divide into two teams. Each turn, players/teams draw a card, then play a card. If they have no cards in their deck, they have additional options open to them. They can move a card or swap two cards. The game ends when either all players have no cards in hand or there are no more possible plays.

Players score points when they complete a row, column or 3x3 box.

With the wise men of the Jersi universe you will cross mountains, but beware of fools! In this chess game where rock, paper and scissors fight each other, the goal is for your king to cross the field. You move each cubic piece either individually or by stacking it on top of another which then acts as a quick mount.

Each player must bring his king to the opposite edge of the board and thus win the war.

A print n play file of the rules (without components) is available.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Throne of Eldraine Brawl Deck: Knights' Charge
description:
Brawl Decks are 60-card ready-to-play decks, all Standard-legal, and designed specifically for the Brawl format. Each has seven Standard-legal cards not available in Throne of Eldraine Draft booster packs.

The Knights' Charge deck is an aggressive creature deck focused on Knights and equipment and has Syr Gwyn, Hero of Ashvale as its commander.

Archroma is a thematic, lightweight Eurogame centered around bag management.

In Archroma, players are sorcerers who have just completed their first year course in Archromancy — the practice of colour magic. Now they must demonstrate their skills in their end-of-year examination. Who will come top of the class?

During the game, players need to create mana crystals in the six hues of magic (Red, Yellow, Green, Cyan, Blue, and Magenta) which will provide the fuel for their spells. Each player is equipped with their own magic component bag, which provides the primary mechanism for creating new mana crystals.

The winner is the player with the most points at the end of the game. Points are awarded for each spell a player has cast, with larger spells and spells using more colours of mana worth more points. The scoring system rewards players for casting the widest range of spells they can, and for using a variety of the colours of magic.

If you take a Dobby and/or Sock card during the Share Rewards phase, place the card(s) in your House’s Box and announce out loud “Dobby” and/or “Sock”.

At the end of the game, add points to your House depending on which cards were added to your House’s Box. 
• Sock card only: 0 points. 
• Dobby card only: 5 points. 
• Dobby and Sock cards: 50 points.

Become the Vice Archmage of the Empire in this turn based fast paced strategic card and dice game.

In the Imperial Twing fantasy card game, you are a witch, wizard, mage, sorceress, or other character capable of controlling the magical energy of The Orn and you are striving to become the new Vice-Archmage of the Empire.

Use and develop your spells, enjoy your steadily growing entourage of grim looking creatures and battle your competitors with everything you have, all the while hoping that the Orn is on your side.  Collect, steal, or conjure the magical objects you need. Most importantly, get a hold of the Magical Teddy of Agony.

As soon as you have the necessary magical objects you can initiate the final magical ritual of the endgame, defeat all of your opponents and victor of Imperial Twing.

Orpheus Descends is a one-card game with only a few components.

You are Orpheus and have to manage your resources Anger, Sorrow, Harmony and Trust. With enough Harmony you will be able to sing again. Descend to the Underworld (the back side of the card) and persuade the ferryman to bring you to the other side with your singing and later on even Hades the god of the dead to let Eurydice go.  With enough Trust, you will make it back to the land of the living.

Do you know how the story ends?
Hades agrees on the condition that you have to go first and Eurydice follows behind. But if you turn around on your way up she will have to stay forever.

New Avernus is a 1-4 player, competitive apocalyptic city building board game.

Over 7-10 turns of gameplay, players take up one of the four unique factions with their own starting resources, worker count, and abilities, and seek to clear the ruins of the previous cities to gain building blueprints, much needed resources, and finally to build, and fortify or beautify, your city.

At the end of each turn, an Occurrence card offers aid, resources, or harrowing events on the way to revealing the Meteor card, which is somewhere in the bottom 4 cards of the Occurrence deck. When it is revealed, play stops and the meteor strikes.

The meteor strikes a series of random spaces on each player's board, and when it's finally over, all surviving structures and points are counted up and a winner is decided.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Influencers in the Wild: The Game
description:
Influencers in the Wild is the social social media game. Do you meet the minimal requirements necessary to entertain the masses? Now you get to find out!

When it comes to your social media following, more is more. So how do you grow? Simple: travel the world, ruin every moment with incessant content creation, and we cannot emphasize this enough, overestimate your own talent. So go ahead, chase those likes like your detox tea sponsorship depends on it, because it does.

All players begin on START and make their way around the board one roll of the die at a time. Gain and lose followers along the way by completing challenges, going viral, and proving your influence by making it to the “big pink wall” in Los Angeles. Just like in real life, the player with the most followers wins! (Sarcasm font).

This game has adult content and was created for ages 17+

Help police, fire and ambulance response teams join together as part of your Emergency Force!

Emergency Force is a tile placement game in which players seek to deploy their emergency response teams (police, medical and fire) to where they are most needed. Players construct the city by placing tiles, each of which contains one or more incidents worth points to any response team deployed to that block. Each type of team scores points differently and the player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Snakes and Letters is a light word-building game in which players try to make high-scoring words using the letter cards in their hand and on the board. If they need some extra help they can also play a vowel or consonant snake card to represent any letter of that type. When a player makes or modifies a word they gain control of that word. Control a word for a whole round to capture it and score points.

The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Ark is a simple card game in which players are trying to collect pairs of animals for their ark. On each turn a player adds animals from their hand of cards to one of the boats. If this boat is now full they must take all of the animals from the boat onto their ark. Pairs of animals in a player's ark score points; unpaired animals could attract a penalty.

The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

The race across America is underway! Six cars are competing, but which ones will you support? Play speed cards to boost your chosen racers, but watch out for the police. Speeding cars will be booked! The first car to reach Los Angeles wins the race and scores the most points. The player who backed the most successful collection of cars claims victory.

Coast To Coast features a unique movement system whereby players add speed cards to a communal movement tableau, the dashboard. Each player controls only a portion of the dashboard, which determines movement of the cars. On their turn the player may try to accelerate their own cars towards the next checkpoint, or possibly even try to get their opponents' cars caught by the police.

Strange Orbit: Undermine is an action-strategy game played in quick rounds. Players only have a few turns to get in, secure their haul, and extract their spoils on a collapsing asteroid. Strange Orbit features a tactile world composed of hexagonal resource tiles, which ensures that each game's layout is unknown and requires crafty exploration using each character's unique abilities. The player who collects the most valuable haul after the asteroid collapses is deemed the winner.

On each turn, players receive 10 action points to move, climb, build, deposit, and disrupt other players on a living board that is constantly changing shape. Players search the board for valuable minerals such as gold, emeralds, and diamonds in order to extract and deposit in their personal stashes. Be careful however, as players must be careful around other players who may attempt to steal or destroy their resources.

In Strange Orbit, players will take on the role of a Triffling crew member on The Big Rig, a staging vessel for descents into the dangerous caverns of the asteroids. Due to hefty commissions and the Triffling's quarrelsome nature, miners will compete to be the one with the most glittering gems and gold. Pushing, stealing, or even undercutting the gem market are all tools for the miners to swing fortune in their favor. Don't be the one to dawdle too long, as the unstable cavern formations are soon to collapse into rolling magma!

-description from publisher

A party game version of that thing on the Internet where people rename animals like calling raccoons "trash pandas," skunks "fart squirrels," and snakes "danger noodles" and "nope ropes."

Players draw a hand of eight Hint Cards, so you don't have to think too hard, to create a name for the animal the judge that round, the Critter Critic, chooses off the Animal Card they drew.

The Critter Critic closes their eyes, or turns away from the table, so players are anonymously submitting their name creations. Once all have submitted a name, using from one to four Hint Cards, the Critter Critic reads each name out loud, as they lay flat on the table, to entertain everyone.

The top 3 choices are awarded points: 1 for Third, 2 for Second, and 3 for First. The scorecard is tallied. The role of Critter Critic passes left. Played cards are discarded, Hint Card hands replenished, and the new Critter Critic draws the next animal card, and picks an animal to be renamed.

The game ends when the pre-agreed upon number of Animal Cards in the deck runs out, and the final animal is renamed and scored. The player with the most points wins.

Summary: Richard Chalmers, an upstanding Valley Falls citizen and wealthy businessman, has passed away. He was a city favorite as Santa Claus and even won Valley Falls "Man of The Year." Yet, it appears he might've had a hidden hobby no one knew about. During an estate sale liquidating Chalmers' property, someone discovered his hunting journal. The contents are shocking to say the least. According to the entries, Chalmers has been stalking and killing people for the last few years. Based on discoveries found in the journal, he wasn't able to kill his final captured victim. The dates of those journal entries indicate there's a chance the final victim may still be alive. Journey into a serial killer's mind to discover where his last victim is before time runs out and help rescue The Last Prey!

As with all Deadbolt Mystery Society games, you step into the role of the detective. Examining the evidence provided, visiting different locations and using the password protected QR mechanic to uncover the truth.

Players win once they catch the killer, find whats missing or whatever else is needed to solve the case at hand.

Difficulty Rating – 8/10

Summary: World-renowned mystery writer, Rosemary Clark, has seen more than her fair share of adventure. In addition to authoring some of the most tightly-plotted mysteries to ever hit the Best Seller list, she has also helped solve a few as well like the case of The Dark and Stormy Night and The Old and Hidden Secret. Now comes a new Rosemary Clark mystery that places her firmly in the crosshairs of someone calling themselves The Hangman. The Hangman has challenged Rosemary either to uncover his secret or face an unspoken wrath whose details he won't divulge. Accompany Rosemary on her quest to determine The Hangman's identity and why she has been dragged into the middle of this case. Yet, beware, this mystery is anything but straightforward. In the words of Rosemary herself, this is truly "A Strange and Twisted Game" indeed!

As with all Deadbolt Mystery Society games, you step into the role of the detective. Examining the evidence provided, visiting different locations and using the password protected QR mechanic to uncover the truth.

Players win once they catch the killer, find whats missing or whatever else is needed to solve the case at hand.

Summary: Once again, your job as a Will Street Detective has landed you in the middle of a strange case. The police have called you to help investigate the homicide of a John Doe. The victim was found in the trunk of a car at Lewis’s Junkyard just before the vehicle was scheduled to be crushed. No identification was found on the victim who had been beaten past the point of recognition. As you sift through the items found scattered throughout the car, it becomes clear that this man was involved with something nefarious. Can you sort through the clues to identify this John Doe and find out who killed him?

As with all Deadbolt Mystery Society games, you step into the role of the detective. Examining the evidence provided, visiting different locations and using the password protected QR mechanic to uncover the truth.

Players win once they catch the killer, find whats missing or whatever else is needed to solve the case at hand.

The house is alive but trying to kill you, you must find what you are looking for before all the light runs out and you are trapped.
In this semi-cooperative game, you take turns collecting artifacts. If you run out of light, you lose.

Each player gets a pawn and a player board with slots for equipment. Each player will get a unique character with their own items to use. The layout of the dungeon is somewhat fixed but the setup will be different in each game. The equipment will be scattered across the board and each room will receive a set amount of light plus some extra distributed by the players at the setup of the game. Players may also trade to get what they need to win.

'The New Sunday Pastime for old & Young'.

Primarily aimed at children at Sunday School. Players collect words from the supply on the centre of the table. The objective is to form complete scripture texts. If a text has a word added correctly that play can continue taking words until a non-required word is drawn. Turn then passes to the next player.

The player who forms his or her text(s) first wins or as the rules say "Top of the class".

boardgame: Munchkin: La Pupa e il Dragone
description:
This product is a compilation of these expansions:

Munchkin Dragons: Four More
Munchkin Dragons: Dragon's trike
Munchkin Princesses

It is currently available in its Italian edition.

A pack of six Mindclash Games-themed constellation cards, given out with pre-orders of Astra.

The cards in the promo pack are:

     Cauldron
     Dreadnought
     Exosuit
     Love
     Shieldhead
     Top Hat

The Kipp Kubus-class mothership has found a solar system with many inhabited planets. Your task is to collect new technologies that can be found on these planets.

Each player controls two Cubie-class starships. These can move through space in three ways: tilting, sliding, and rotating. Three actions must be performed in each round. One of them is the mothership Kipp Kubus. The other two actions are determined by cards and a die.

Decide tactically wisely which action you carry out with which Cubie. And beware of the many asteroids in this solar system. Collect as many technologies as possible to win the game.

—description from the designer

Das Mutterschiff der Klasse Kipp Kubus hat ein Sonnensystem mit vielen bewohnten Planeten gefunden. Eure Aufgabe ist es, neue Technologien zu sammeln, die auf diesen Planeten zu finden sind.

Jeder Spieler steuert zwei Raumschiffe der Klasse Cubie. Diese können sich auf drei Arten durch den Weltraum bewegen: Kippen, Schieben und Drehen. Jede Runde müssen drei Aktionen durchgeführt werden. Eine davon gibt das Mutterschiff Kipp Kubus vor. Die anderen zwei Aktionen werden durch Karten und einen Würfel bestimmt.

Entscheidet taktisch klug, welche Aktion ihr mit welchem Cubie ausführt. Und nehmt euch in Acht vor den vielen Asteroiden in diesem Sonnensystem. Sammelt so viel Technologien wie möglich, um das Spiel zu gewinnen.

—description from the designer (German)

Requiem is a wargame for two players who will control the National and Republican sides in the Spanish Civil War. During the game, both sides take turns to play their cards and carry out movements and attacks with their troops against the most relevant towns in Spain at the time of this violent conflict.

A game of Requiem is divided into two stages, column warfare and position warfare, as can be seen in the round track on the board. Each stage is divided into 5 rounds, which in turn are divided into:


     Strategy Phase
     Battle Phase
     Logistics Phase


In each phase, both sides take turns to play cards from their hand and move units on the map, attack towns controlled by the enemy side or request reinforcements, in order to subdue the opponent militarily.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: 2v2 upgrade expansion with neoprene map expands to 4 players.  This was included in the Deluxe version of the English edition but could also be purchased separately.

Nya Gotlandsquizet is a quiz game consisting of a pack of cards; questions revolve around the culture and history of Swedish island Gotland in the Baltic Sea. In the default rules players take turns to answer questions, and a player must get both questions on a card right in order to claim it, 10 of which are needed to win. There are a few suggested rule variations, for instance that players gain points for each question answered correctly or that questions can be answered by any player.

A similar game was published in 2015 as Gotlandsquizet with slightly different rules and three questions on each card instead of two. That version also had an English edition, The Gotland Quiz.

boardgame: Dice Fishing: Roll and Catch – Double Hook Mini-expansion
description:
Double Hook Mini-expansion is a 7-card expansion for Dice Fishing: Roll & Catch.
 The expansion brings you 7 new Fish cards,  and also introduces new variant rules to play the game - Reveal two Fish cards each round and you might catch both with one roll of your wagered dice!

The best excuse wins - You have 30 seconds!

1. A judge reads a question card. Everyone else has a word card.
2. Using the word on their card, each player has 30 seconds to respond with an excuse.
3. After every player has answered, the judge must choose their favorite one.
4. Pass out new cards, switch judges, repeat!

Your word may have nothing to do with the question, so be creative and make up something absurd even if it makes no sense.

The perfect game to play at parties with friends and family! It's hilarious, fast-paced and takes only seconds to learn.

boardgame: Top Trumps Quiz: The Lord of the Rings
description: One does not simply answer all 500 questions in TOP TRUMPS QUIZ: Lord of the Rings correctly on the first try! Give your sword-arm a rest and test your cunning and intellect with this tricksy game, which covers all three Lord of the Rings films. Think you can remember the name of the second chapter of Bilbo’s book, or the three-word order Théoden delivers at Helm’s Deep? Stock up on Lembas, because you’re in for quite a journey. Each card contains high-quality photography and stills from the movies, and categories cover characters, locations, creatures and the world and history of Middle Earth. Know the name of the statue on the River Anduin? Remember which city lies between the Mountains of Mordor and Minas Tirith? Then it’s time to gather your very own Fellowship and assemble a quiz night to remember. All we have to decide is what to do with the time that is given to us - and this Lord of the Rings quiz is sure to make Frodo and Gandalf proud.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Throne of Eldraine Brawl Deck: Savage Hunger
description:
Brawl Decks are 60-card ready-to-play decks, all Standard-legal, and designed specifically for the Brawl format. Each has seven Standard-legal cards not available in Throne of Eldraine Draft booster packs.

The Savage Hunger deck takes advantage of effects that sacrifice your creatures or other permanents for profit and has Korvold, Fae-Cursed King as its commander.

Treta na Selva (Jungle Rumble) places the animals of the forest in a rumble where the best defense is to attack!

And let's face it, we won't be missing a good break-up full of likeable characters and funny powers.

This game is a fast pace card game up to 7 "fighters".

Each turn players will try to mtach the cards in the rumble area, if not, they will resolve the área by taking damage (ouch!!!) and lower their life points.

In your turn you can : 
- Play the same card in the área OR 
- Play a special card.

If you can´t play neither you must resolve the área and apply all effects of the cards.

The last player stand win the rumble!

boardgame: Zombicide: 2nd Edition – Dark Nights Metal: Pack #1
description:
From the darkest corners of the multiverse comes the Batman Who Laughs, hell-bent on destroying every version of Earth in existence. It’s up to the Justice League to stop this vile version of our own Dark Knight and all its fearsome, horrific minions.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zombicide: 2nd Edition – Dark Nights Metal: Pack #2
description:
Spanning the multiverse are many versions of Earth and its faithful guardians: The Justice League. Yet while many versions of heroes such as Wonder Woman and Cyborg exist, there are many horrors as well. Now with the dimensions opening, our own League must take on The Merciless and The Murder Machine, horrors from beyond the dimensions intent on destroying Earth as we know it!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zombicide: 2nd Edition – Dark Nights Metal: Pack #3
description:
Spanning the multiverse are many versions of Earth and its faithful guardians: The Justice League. Yet while many versions of heroes such as Aquaman and Flash exist, there are many horrors as well. Now with the dimensions opening, our own League must take on The Drowned and The Red Death, horrors from beyond the dimensions intent on destroying Earth as we know it!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zombicide: 2nd Edition – Dark Nights Metal: Pack #4
description:
Spanning the multiverse are many versions of Earth and its faithful guardians: The Justice League. Yet while many versions of heroes such as Superman and Green Lantern exist, there are many horrors as well. Now with the dimensions opening, our own League must take on The Devastator and The Dawnbreaker, horrors from beyond the dimensions intent on destroying Earth as we know it!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zombicide: 2nd Edition – Dark Nights Metal: Pack #5
description:
From the darkest corners of the multiverse comes the Batman Who Frags, hell-bent on destroying every version of Earth in existence. It’s up to the Justice League to stop this vile version of our own Dark Knight and all its fearsome, horrific minions.

—description from the publisher

As all worthy corporate legionnaires, you thirst only for total victory.

In Cubicus Rex, victory is achieved with the highest of office perks: promotions. You’ll partner with fellow legionnaires to complete milestones and projects – the steps toward the higher rank you crave – through fast-paced yet strategic rounds of play.

Choose your legion with your performance reviews, then wage war with staplers, sundials, whiteboards, and more in the quest for promotion. But take heed: your compatriots in one battle may become your foe in the next! Manage your hand wisely and stay vigilant as you build chains of cards to earn your promotions. Every card you play has another action in store – which can turn the tide of battle.

Players pair up to complete project chains.  The object is to be the first player to complete enough projects to be promoted 3 times.

Finish your milestones. Complete your projects. Get promoted three times, and you will be crowned CUBICUS REX.

Lock ‘em Up! is a game of political dirty tricks where you have to put your opponents in jail before they do it to you. The winner is the last player with a character not in jail.

The game consists of a total of three decks worth of cards split into two groups: the Character Cards, the people you’re trying to keep out of jail; and the Action Cards which give you power and influence to make strategic moves in the game.

Players choose which group of characters they want to control — the White House, the Military, Law & Order, Progressives, Conservatives, or Lobbyists. Players then take turns drawing from the Action Cards deck, using those cards to indict or save characters. When the game board resembling a courtroom is full, the convictions start. Turn by turn, the Characters on the docket are sent to a jail cell, and out of the game.

Once, there was a struggle between two races.
The "Erma" tribe, who have lived in the forest since ancient times, lived peacefully.
One day, however, the Otta invaded the forest.
The Otta tribe cut down the trees in the forest and captured the Elma tribe.
The Elma tribe, utilizing the resources of the forest, oppose the Otta tribe.

This is a board game for two players only.
Players fight for the survival of their own tribe as either the Otta or the Elma.
The Otta tribe invades the forest where the Elma tribe lives, while strengthening their own power.
The Elma build treehouses and traps to defend their forest against the Otta invasion.
The goal of the Otta is to annihilate the Elma, while the goal of the Elma is to survive.

The game is asymmetrical, so there are different things to think about depending on which tribe you are playing.
This game is recommended for those who like to think about where to move their frames on the game board and how to strengthen their own power in short rounds.
In the later stages of the game, there is more to do and more to think about.
publishers: DEBUG MONKEYS (デバッグモンキーズ)

description:
In a world that is only visible with the aid of a microscope, life is teeming with activity and tension. Germs are in a constant state of growth and propagation. In the petri dish, only one faction of germs can reign supreme. It is an epic war of microscopic proportions. Each faction will vie for control of the petri dish, one section at a time. Through sheer dominance, the strongest germ faction will win out. The fate of the petri dish begins with the roll of the dice.

In Germ War, two players will attempt to grow and propagate their germ factions, in order to conquer the petri dish. The faction that dominates a majority of the sections, reign supreme and wins!

At the beginning of each round, a player will mark off the next available spot on the round tracker. Alternating turns, players can do one of two actions: "Germ Growth or "Germ Strength Increased". When a player chooses Germ Growth, they will draft a reproduction die and apply the effects. If a player has a section(s) with a die valued at six, that player will increase their Germ Strength by marking off their germ icon and rotate the die to a one. The first player to do this three times in a section, will maintain a dominate presence in that section for the remainder of the section. If a player does this four times in the game, that player wins the game.

—description from the designer

A cooperative PnP survival game for 1-100 players, procedurally-generated with Go and XeLaTeX.  Try to survive eight nights by enduring nightly events, foraging for resources, crafting items, and managing your mental health.

Each time you play LaGulch, you generate a new game using a software program. This program randomizes and generates the game sheet. Print as many as you'd like for however many players are playing.

Each night, roll a die and apply the effect of the event. During the day, roll again to forage for items. Optionally, you can reduce your mental health to gain additional value when foraging.

Players can decide to use their foraged items to craft items that help themselves personally, or contribute them to the group to craft larger items like a shelter or fire.

—description from designer

Nujum is a relaxed-paced, abstract board game for 2 players about predicting the movement of the stars.

In a turn of Nujum, players each play one card from their hand face-down to move the Star Token on the central board according to their card's value. The catch: In your turn, your opponent's card gets resolved first. If you can't move the token to the full number of spaces indicated on your card, it's discarded - not scoring you any points.

Should the stars not align for you at any point, don't worry! Eclipse Cards allow you to alter the movement of the Star Token, changing cards' suits, replacing them with cards from your hand or scoring your card without moving the Star Token altogether.

Nujum, the Arabic word for "Stars", draws inspiration from the Islamic Golden Age when scholars with diverse backgrounds used to discuss Astrology, Astronomy and Philosophy on an equal footing.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:

Box comes with a platoon of five M12 guns and unit cards.

Embark on a wild undersea adventure with this fast-paced tile placement/puzzle game. Use clever strategies to place your tiles correctly in a colourful and growing seascape of creatures that includes the Yellow Submarine Slug and Jellyfish. Be the first to play all your tiles to win the game.

Throw Octopus! Play a tile with a special octopus icon and you'll earn an Action card that can help or hurt you. A 'Throw Octopus' card allows you to throw the Octopus Plushie at your opponent then they have to draw 2 tiles and lose their next turn!

A 1967 Italian game about transoceanic naval commerce.

Players move their ship around a map to collect cargoes from a shipping port and deliver it to its destination, collecting the transportation fees.

Movement of the ships (actually, plastic pawns) is made on a map with squares, and the speed is freely selected by the player (1 to 4 squares per movement).

Each move consumes one unit of the ship fuel reserve (up to 10 fuel units maximum), and players can only refill their fuel in a bunkerage ports. The price per unit of fuel varies from port to port. Getting out of fuel implies either paying other ships to be towed or slowly drifting away, until a bunkerage port has been reached.

If a ship moves in a bad weather zone, for each move the player draws a card that can inflict damages to the ship; upon reaching a total of six damages the ship is forced to reach the nearest port for repairing.

Fuel units and damages are recorded by turning the numbered wheels on the cardboard representing the player ship.

Available cargoes to collect and deliver are drawn randomly from a deck at the start of the game and later on during the play each time a player draws a card to check for damage. Each time a cargo has been delivered, it is immediately put back into the deck, and the deck is reshuffled.
 The first player to collect the agreed amount of cash wins the game and is given the title of "Master".

—user summary

Time of Wars is a multiplayer card driven game. Players are on the head of five superpowers in Eastern Europe – the Polish – Lithuanian Commonwealth, the Tsardom of Russia, the Ottoman Empire, the Kingdom of Sweden and the Holy Roman Empire. Minor states are also present – Denmark, Brandenburg, Moldova, Wallachia, Transylvania and Crimean Khanate.

The game covers the turn of XVI and XVII centuries. The period was rich in famous characters (rulers and commanders) and critical events – for example the Time of Troubles in the Tsardom, the Thirty Years War in the Reich, Swedish “Deluge” in the Commonwealth. All these and much more are smoothly incorporated during gameplay.

The heart of the game are five decks of cards – one for each superpower.

During gameplay there is a lot of interaction between players. They can declare wars or make alliances as effect of secret negotiations.

Won battles and wars are important, but players must take care about condition of their own states as well. In the game are shown factors like economy, domestic policy, military level and religion. All of them can affect specified players’ actions.

The game has a lot of options for each player and each state has its own problems and advantages. Despite this the gameplay is extremely well balanced. 
There are 5 scenarios. The main scenario is for 5 players. Four shorter scenarios are for 2 or 3 players.

MoneySmart is a fun game, focuses on taking risks and investing and managing capital and credit. Puts you in the investor mindset. Made with focus on fun, life realities. It has a more modern investing approach, with focus on Bitcoin, startups, Apps, stocks ...

The game is optimized for 3-5 players (even though it is possible to have more or less players),

Gameplay:

The game follow the classical board game mechanism, the player throws the dice and advances to the correct slot on the board.
The game is played counter-clock wise, and has 3 user types, a player needs to pay to switch to the other type. Upon every end of lap the player collects money based on the type (and or investments).
Based on the slot you land on, multiple scenarios are possible. these Scenarios simulate life.
- Work : makes you lose a round. (to simulate the time we lose working for others)
- Life : You get to spin an arrow attached to the board, where you land on one of life's surprises.
- Deals : You get to withdraw the deals card, and invest (only possible for business and investor)
- Choices: You get a choices card and based on the answer fill the zen card (helps detect the player's nature).
- Stocks and bitcoin: quick risk taking action, with rewards based on dice value.
- Sabotage: You get to sabotage another player (details in the rules).
- Bills: You need to pay ...
- Other important slots: Taxes, Debt, Insurance, give an even extra realistic level to the game.
- Rules get all these elements interacting in a harmonious and homogeneous way, to make sure that not only luck is the deciding factor for winning.
- There are 3 types of cards, including cards you get on each end of lap, which adds a bit more fun and entertainment to the game.

Winning condition:

There are different game settings, to allow the game to be played for different periods of time.
Thus winning conditions also vary.

- In timed games: after the timer signals the end of game, the winner, is the user with most: 
    option 1) Cash Money (for simpler game - or younger players)
    option 2) Recurring revenue

- None timed games: 
    + Elimination game: you play till all players except 1 are eliminated. Elimination is triggered by not being able to     pay debt or taxes. (simulating bankruptcy)
    
    + Goal set games: 
    option 1) First player to reach X amount in cash money (50k, 100k, 500k etc ...)
    option 2) First player to reach X amount in recurring revenue (10k, 20k, 50k etc ...)

-description from publisher

After the discovery of the Pacific Ocean by the Spanish explorer Vasco Nuñez de Balboa in 1513, it became the subject of dispute between the two great colonial empires of the time, Spain and Portugal. The Treaty of Tordesillas established a principle agreement for the division of the world, leaving this vast expanse of sea on the Spanish side, which came to be known as the "Spanish lake".

The expeditions sought quick access from South America to the Spice Islands, the Moluccas and also the mythical "Terra Australis Incognita" where they intended to find gold, spices and exotic products for the greater honour and wealth of the Spanish and Portuguese Crowns.

Thus, during the 16th and 17th centuries, the Pacific Ocean was a Spanish sea, only disturbed by some English and Dutch expeditions. During those years, Spanish navigators crossed it in various directions and discovered most of its archipelagos (Galapagos, Marquesas, Hawaii, Marianas, Philippines, Marshall Islands, Society), as well as circumnavigating New Guinea, sighting the coasts of Australia and exploring and colonising the Philippine archipelago.

One player represents the Spanish Empire based in the Viceroyalty of Peru, organizes his expedition departing from the port of El Callao.  The other player in turn organizes another expedition departing from the port of Acapulco.

The player with the most victory points wins. Victory points are earned by discovering islands and mapping them, establishing colonies, establishing contacts and alliances with natives, collecting exotic species of plants, birds and animals and bringing them to the home port. Finding gold, pearls or spices, etc.

—description from designer

Tras el descubrimiento del Océano Pacífico por el explorador español Vasco Núñez de Balboa en 1513, este fue objeto de disputa entre los dos grandes imperios coloniales de la época, España y Portugal. Mediante el tratado de Tordesillas se establecía un principio de acuerdo para el reparto del mundo, quedando está vasta extensión de mar en la parte española y llegó a ser llamado el “lago español”.
Las expediciones buscaban un acceso rápido desde Sudamérica hacia las islas de las especias, las Molucas y también la mítica “Terra Australis Incognita” donde pretendían encontrar oro, especias y productos exóticos para mayor honor y riqueza de las Coronas Española y Portuguesa.
Así pues durante los siglos XVI y XVII el océano Pacífico fue un mar español, sólo perturbado por algunas  expediciones inglesas y holandesas. Durante aquellos años, los navegantes españoles lo atravesaron en diversos sentidos y descubrieron la mayor parte de sus archipiélagos (Galápagos, Marquesas, Hawái, Marianas, Filipinas, Marshall, Sociedad), además de circunnavegar Nueva Guinea, avistar las costas de Australia y explorar y colonizar el archipiélago filipino.

Objetivo del juego :  Un jugador representa al Imperio Español con base en el Virreinato del Perú, organiza su expedición partiendo del puerto de El Callao. El otro jugador representa a Portugal y parte de las islas Molucas.  El otro jugador organiza a su vez otra expedición que parte del puerto de Acapulco.
Gana el jugador que consiga más puntos de victoria. Los puntos de victoria se consiguen descubriendo islas y cartografiándolas, estableciendo colonias, cristianizando nativos o creando alianzas, recopilando especies exóticas de plantas, aves y animales, y llevándolas al puerto base. Encontrando oro, perlas o especias, etc.

—description from designer (Spanish)
