Yinzi: Ming is a new edition of the original Yinzi — meaning "silver". Drawing on feedback from players, this version introduces updated artwork, refined graphic design, and completely revised rules to offer a fresh and immersive experience.

Set during the Ming dynasty (1368–1644), a period of unprecedented economic expansion in China, Yinzi: Ming captures the transformative power of global trade. During this era, the flow of silver from the Americas and Europe to China reshaped the world economy, marking what many consider the true beginning of globalization. Within China, the rise of social mobility led to the rapid growth of cities, particularly in the lower Yangtze region, which became the country’s main hub for wheat production. In addition to wheat and rice, large-scale cultivation of crops like tea, fruit, and sugarcane flourished. Many rural peasants, drawn by economic opportunity, migrated to cities, transforming into merchants and artisans. These newly urbanized workers found employment in private and state-run factories, producing valuable commodities such as paper, porcelain, refined sugar, and silk textiles. Fueled by the liberalized economy, trade and commerce boomed, further supported by the Ming government’s infrastructure projects, including the construction of canals, roads, and bridges. This era also saw the rise of powerful merchant clans who amassed significant wealth.

In Yinzi: Ming  players take on the role of these merchant clans, tasked with developing key regions of China in the late Ming period, specifically along the final 200 kilometers of the Yangtze River as it approaches the China Sea.

Players must plant crops, sell goods to rural and urban markets, develop raw materials, and build or upgrade factories. The goal is to produce and deliver goods to ships waiting in port. However, to succeed, players will also need to improve their river transport capabilities along the Yangtze, balancing growth and efficiency to outmaneuver their competitors.  The player with the most Wealth points, wins.

Interstellar Nomad is a set collection game where players earn points for completing the planet panoramas. In your quest for interstellar inhabitance, you’ll compete with an opponent to earn the highest Nomad Score. to win each round and the overall game.

DESCRIPTION Construct panorama’s of planets across the galaxy through twists and turns, outwit your opponent in this compact easy-to-learn, two-player card game!

HOW TO PLAY

Greetings, Interstellar Nomad. Interstellar Nomad is a set collection game where players earn points for completing the planet panoramas. In your quest for interstellar inhabitance, you’ll compete with an opponent to earn the highest Nomad Score. to win each round and the overall game.

A game of Interstellar Nomad is played over 2 or more rounds. Each round consists of 4 steps:

Setup
Shuffle all cards into a deck. Deal 2 cards to you and your opponent. Place the remaining cards in a face-down draw pile on the table. Draw 5 cards and place them face-down in a Universal Row next to the deck with each card alternating orientation. Once the Universal Row is in place, reveal all face-down cards. Randomly determine the starting player for the first round. The losing player starts subsequent rounds.

Play Cards
Players take turns placing a single card onto their own play area. Cards can be played in one of two ways:

Place a card from your hand into your play area face up and in any orientation. This card will either start or continue a set. [next Resolve Card Actions]

Select a face-up card (active card) from the Universal Row and place it in your play area to start or continue a set. The card may be placed in the correct order of the set but must maintain it's previous orientation — landscape or portrait. [next Resolve Card Actions]

Resolve Card Actions
After playing a card, the player must execute the played card’s action(s), selecting and affecting a different active card — the active card must be within their play area or the Universal Row. [see Card Actions] The player then draws a card and adds it to the end of the Universal Row following alternating orientation. Play continues until all possible cards are played.

Determine the Winner
Points are awarded as follows at the end of each round: +1 for each active card within a set -1 for each missing card in a set -1 for each inactive card -1 for possessing the Wasteland (-2 if inactive) Bonus points are awarded for each complete set: +1 for a complete set in the correct order +1 for a complete set in the correct orientation The player with the highest Nomad Score after all cards are played wins the round.

The first player to win 2 rounds is the winner of the game and continues their interstellar journey.

Conquest: Global Shuffle is an easy to learn strategic card game of world domination. Centered around territory and military management, Conquest recreates the feeling of epic war games but is played in under an hour.

This turn based card game has players collecting Continent Cards in their play area while using their armies to defend, and attack other players through use of attack cards. These actions are also aided by the skills of history's generals,

On a player's turn they will begin by drawing cards from the central game deck, they then can take actions that include playing continent cards, building up their army strength, disrupting other players through action cards, or attempt to conquer opponents' continent cards.

As players take their turns, the world and player's armies, get larger, increasing player to player engagement. Players cannot actively defend an attack and must plan ahead before their next turn.

They game is over when a player's reaches a set global empire value, calculated by the number of completed continent sets they have.

—description from designer

Get ready for a merchant’s adventure ! Go on the roads of Karanor and become a wandering merchant in search for wealth and glory !

In Merchants of Karanor, you will try to reach 10 notoriety points before your oppponents by opening new commerces, achieving quests for the locals or even buying a private villa.

During your turn, you will perform 4 actions among 6 permitted : travel, collect resources, interact with a local, open a commerce, work or realize a special action. At the end of your turn, players will receive the income of their commerce and the production of their associates.

During your travels, you will be able to gathers rewards and advantages, recruit associates, buy some illegal services to the black market or find a rare artifact to improve your character or your business activities, in order to move faster to victory.

Interactions between players are an important part of the game. Everything in the game can be traded, sold or bartered between players. Black market cards will allow you to slow your opponents  in their quest for glory and bring tensions to the negotiations !

—description from publisher

Shooting Bang is a camp party shootout game. Find ways to cooperate with your teammates, defeat all enemies, and become the last surviving player to win.

The core mechanism of the game is one card with three uses. The number of cards in hand represents life, the number of cards in hand represents position, and the card in hand represents action.

Each round, the player chooses one of the following actions to execute

     Action: Play a card in hand to activate the effect
     Escape: discard one and draw one or discard two and draw two


Snipe by guessing the opponent's number by playing attack cards. Those who are hit need to discard the specified number of cards. After a successful hit, the attacker can draw a card.

When the central deck (eight cards) is exhausted, a card will be randomly drawn from the remaining deck, which means that this number does not exist in the next game.

Remove all cards from your opponent's hand to win
There are 18 different weapons and 8 terrain expansions in the game

It's the ultimate undersea faceoff battle of speed, wit and dexterity. In this fast-paced tile placement game, you'll race against your opponent to score points as you build colourful sea creatures. You must be quick as you battle your opponent for new tiles from a shared tile pile. It's octopus Duel!

Small sea creatures (4 tiles) are worth 1 point, large sea creatures (6+ tiles) are worth 2 points at the end of the game the player with the most points wins!

Advancing Fire normally works only on deeply historical games, but this time we are proud to produce the work of an important external developer, that is a unique and very interesting release.

TOURNAMENT DIRECTOR’S PACK is the collection of ASL scenarios designed by Enrico Catanzaro (known in ASL community as ecz)  with an experience of about thirty years as ASL player and Tournament Director of ASL tournaments, as well as wargamer and chess player. His main attention turned in particular to the balance and replayability, while trying to develop interesting and innovative dynamics. The Tournament Director's Pack features 16 scenarios all using dedicated new rules which are used to decide sides and Victory Conditions at once. We made sure the scenarios were playtested by many different level players, from the best who won many tournaments to the inexperienced ones, and they have shown, with their rules for determining the VCs, to be suitable for all levels, and to have a very high replayability. These scenarios are ideal for use in tournaments or to practice competitive ASL, maintaining a high adherence to the war events they represent.

ecz1 The Bet,  Trøgstad, Norway, April 1940. An old classic first created for a scenario design contest on the Game Squad forum and later published by Le Franc Tireur (LFT 181). This is the scenario where CBS made its debut. The Germans must control a village defended by a Norwegian MG company. 5.5 turns on two boards.

ecz2 Liberating Bukovina, Bukovina, July 1941. 6.5 turns and three boards for a Romanian attack vs Russians in the first stage of Barbarossa. Cavalry and tin cans in the OB.

ecz3 Turf Wars in Mongolia, Mongolian-Manchurian border dispute in May 1939. Two boards and 5 turns for this all-cavalry action (with the exception of only two MMCs not mounted at the start). In this scenario Steppe terrain and Sun Blindness are in effect.

ecz4 Flash and Thunder, Neuville-au-plain River, Normandy June 1944, in the first hours of the D-Day. A company of Paratroopers tries to find their officers, regroup, and move toward its objective, avoiding the German garrison patrolling the area and increasing its presence turn by turn. Three boards and 7.5 turns for this scenario where movement is crucial.

ecz5 The Good and the Bad, Montefiorino Italy, July 1944. The final action of the largest Partisan battle fought in Italy. In this scenario (three boards and 7 turns), Fascists and SS using Armored Cars, Flamethrowers, OBA and a lot of firepower, jointly attack a small town defended by elements of the Partisan Division ‘Modena’. The Defenders must stay in the town as long as possible to prevent the Fascists from making a large scale encirclement and exiting off the opposite board edge, and try to save their lives by retreating into the woods before it is too late.

ecz6 A Diurnal Assault, Central Stalingrad, October 1942. 7 turns. This time the Russians are on the attack, but they must watch for a German counterattack. In this scenario, setup is sequential, counter by counter. Both players have the chance to attack in depth or to play more conservatively to parry the enemy offensive blow by blow. Soviets have Rocket OBA!

ecz7 Clash of Cans, Guadalajara, Spain, March 1937. The first attempt at a large scale armored blitz by the Italian army during the Spanish civil war in this all (tin can) armor scenario. Six Italian L35s face 4 Light Russian tanks in a three board, 6 turn scenario. Clash of Cans is a complementary scenario to ‘The Puma Prowls’ that could easily be used as a teaching tool for AFV rules, particularly the platoon-movement rules which aren’t used in the Puma Prowls scenario.

ecz8 A Hot Week in Sicily, Sicily, August 1943. Again in Sicily for the crucial moment of the battle for Troina. The men in Monte Basilio were isolated, flanked and were suffering ammo and water shortage, but they held heroically against the final push of the 15th Panzer Granadier division in this 6 turn, three board scenario.

ecz9 Culquaber, Ethiopia, November 1941. This time the action moves to the Italian East-African colony where the elite ‘Carabinieri’ fought a hopeless battle against an overwhelmingly superior enemy force on the famous ‘Cliff of the Crags’. In this 7 turn, two board scenario there are also warbands of natives fighting each other as allies of the Commonwealth and of the Italians.

ecz10 The Capture of Malta, Krendi Airfield, Malta, July 1942. A fictional, ‘what if…’ scenario in which the Italians and Germans jointly launch ‘Operation Hercules’. We can see German Paratroopers and Italian Marines of the ‘Battaglione San Marco’ fighting together against the Malta garrison in a 6.5 turn scenario on three half boards for the capture of a small airport in the south of the island.

ecz11 Blood and Brine, Calais, France, May 1940. Another scenario created for a design contest on the Game Squad forum. In the dramatic days of the great retreat of the BEF, the battle of Calais was decisive to delay the German advance to Dunkirk. Blood and Brine is a two board, 6.5 turn scenario featuring a very peculiar SSR about ‘random (German) air attacks’ on the British units tied to the SAN.

ecz12 A Tragic Victory, Vilnius, Poland, July 1944. Another scenario coming from a design contest. The dramatic last battle of the Polish Home Army, winning on the field against the Germans, but doomed to deportation by Soviet hands in the following days. In this 5.5 turn, two board scenario, with the help of the Russians of the Belorussian Front, the Polish force successfully closed any escape path for the Germans trapped in Vilnius.

ecz13 Leaving Changsha, South-Central China, October 1939. This urban, 5.5 turns, Sino-Japanese battle on two half boards depicts the attempt of the Japanese 13th Division to break the encirclement and leave Changsha. This was the first great defeat of the IJA.

ecz14 The Streets of Shanghai, Shanghai, August 1937. A large, 8 turn scenario on two full and two half-boards highlighting the major Chinese land offensive to free the city. A mostly infantry scenario with the exception of a few ACs and Chinese light tanks trying to clear their way across rubble and debris.

ecz15 The Best Defence, North of Aachen, October 1944. Facing encirclement, the Germans try to widen the gap between the two American pincers around Aachen with a local armored counterattack. 6 turns and two boards for this ‘heavy armor’ scenario.

BR11 Fields of Golden Wheat, Sicily, July 1943. This scenario is the ‘prequel’ to Scenario T9, The Niscemi Biscari Highway. It depicts the events that happened earlier in the same day that led to the capture of the pair of Italian AT guns that were later used by the Paratroopers against the Germans. Two half boards and 5.5 turns for this small scenario.

TOURNAMENT DIRECTOR’S PACK  contains the following:

16 action packed scenarios

Ownership of the following MMP/Hasbro products are required to play all of the included scenarios:  Beyond Valor, Yanks, For King and Country, The Last Hurrah, Hollow Legions, Doomed Battalions, Armies of Oblivion, Rising Sun,  boards 43, 52, 56, 57, 5b, 60, 62, 70, RB, RO, z

-description from publisher's website

In Politi-King, players are trying to get to 30 coins or kill all their opponents around the table. However, players can accept bribes, so whoever can make the best deals is sure to win!

Each player begins with 25 health and 3 coins. At the end of each one of their turns, a player draws a card. Cards have a value of 3-7 and be either coins or swords. Coin cards are immediately replaced with coin tokens. However, sword cards can be used on future turns to lower someone else's health by the indicated amount, which usually leads to lots of threats and deals.

All deals are binding on the turn they occur, but you don't have to follow up threats. Will you be able to get away with paying nothing? Will you become a coward, but also a survivor? Will you establish Communism? Will you give away your money to appear weak, only to watch a bloodbath ensue between everyone else? Nearly anything can happen in Politi-King.

-description from designer

Shadow War will tantalize your senses. Shadow War is a game based on hidden movement with miniature war game battle rules and custom campaign growth. This is a small team miniature war game with 4 v.s. 4 models facing off. You need to score more points than the enemy to win the game. Earn points by killing the enemy, scoring points on the objective, or destroying their home tower base. Choose your character then upgrade them to learn new skills.

On your turn, you can search with the 5 senses to find and destroy the enemy.

Some actions are attacking, defending, casting spells, and bluffing.

When all the models die on one side or whoever scores the highest points after 5 rounds.

This is a pvpve game. If you can destroy all their models or towers the game automatically ends. Whomever has the most points at the end of the game wins.

-description from designer

boardgame: Splendor: Marvel – Helicarrier/Hydra Base Promo Location Tile
description:
This was given out to participants of the Splendor: Marvel tournaments at Gen Con 2022.

A double-sided location tile for Splendor: Marvel with Helicarrier on one side and Hydra Base on the opposite. Both sides are worth 3 Infinity Points.

Helicarrier requires 4 Space (Blue) and 4 Power (Purple) to claim.

Hydra Base requires 3 Mind (Yellow), 3 Soul (Orange), and 3 Reality (Red) to claim.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Commander 2020: Ikoria: Lair of Behemoths Commander – Symbiotic Swarm
description:
The Symbiotic Swarm deck leverages your creatures’ many abilities—give your living creatures the abilities in your graveyard with Kathril Aspect Warper, reanimate a swarm of small creatures with Tayam Luminous Enigma, and draw cards when your buffed creatures leave the battlefield with Nikara Lair Scavenger and Yannik Scavenging Sentinel.

Symbiotic Swarm is one of five Commander Decks from Ikoria Lair of Behemoths (IKO). Each includes exclusive cards tailored to that deck—pick your favorite mechanics and dive in to battle.

Battle your way through the plane of Ikoria with monster-themed mechanics that grow your creatures build your bond and crush your opponents.

Contents one: (1) foil oversized Commander card, one hundred (100) card deck, ten (10) double-sided tokens, one (1) deck box, one (1) Life Wheel, one (1) punchout keyword counter card.

—description from publisher

boardgame: Rick and Morty: Close Rick-Counters of the Rick Kind Deck-Building Game – Promo Glootie
description:
Promo Card Glootie from Rick and Morty: Close Rick-Counters of the Rick Kind Deck-Building Game which was originally available at Gen Con 2019.

The first time you play a Beth, Summer or Jerry during each of your turns, any one foe may choose to draw a card.  If one does, draw a card and you may destroy a card in your hand.  (Only the first foe to respond draws.)
This troupe box allows you to add three new heroes from Goblin faction to the game: Doug the Flatulent, Vicious Midget and Beaky Bobby.

You own a small farm located in a humble country town. The city itself is quite simple, but the people stand out for being very welcoming and cooperative.

One day, the mayor announces that the city's economy is compromised, and the small town's debts are very high. Within a year, if the debt is not paid, unfortunately the vast green areas will have to be sold to large corporations, which could completely change the city and its economy.

You and other local farmers then offer to help raise funds to save your beloved little town. At the end of each season, each farmer must apply values ​​to a community fund, which will earn them prestige points.

But don't worry, you'll have the help of expert foremen to make your farm yield even more during this period. Enjoy their experience, but be sure to pay overtime when necessary.

Prepare your points machine in the best possible way and be a successful marketer at the Great Fair of the city. Grow crops, raise animals, set up your small industries and markets to finally become the best and most prestigious farmer in Four Seasons!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Você é dono de uma pequena fazenda localizada em uma humilde cidade no interior. A cidade em si é bem simples, mas o povo se destaca por ser bastante acolhedor e cooperativo.

Certo dia, o prefeito anuncia que a economia da cidade está comprometida, e as dívidas do pequeno município são muito altas. Dentro de um ano, se a dívida não for paga, infelizmente as vastas áreas verdes terão de ser vendidas para grandes corporações, o que poderá mudar completamente a cidade e sua economia.

Você e outros fazendeiros locais então se oferecem para ajudar a levantar fundos para salvar sua amada cidadezinha. Ao final de cada estação cada fazendeiro deverá aplicar valores em um fundo comunitário, que lhes renderá pontos de prestígio.

Mas não se preocupe, você terá a ajuda de capatazes especialistas para fazer sua fazenda render ainda mais durante este período. Aproveite a experiência deles, mas não deixe de pagar as horas extras quando necessário.

Prepare sua maquininha de pontos da melhor forma possível e seja um feirante de sucesso na Grande Feira da cidade. Cultive plantações, crie animais, monte suas pequenas indústrias e mercados para enfim se tornar o melhor e mais prestigiado fazendeiro em Quatro estações!

—description from the publisher

Best Wrong Answer is a card based game consisting of 250 question cards and 40 forfeit cards. Gameplay is quite simple and fun!

This edition also contains 10 additional "nuclear cards" as a pre-order bonus!

HOW TO PLAY...
Step 1. Each player takes 10 life tokens.
Step 2. Select a designated "question reader" or take it in turns in a clockwise direction around the group
Step 3. Set the egg timer the moment the card has been drawn.
Step 4. Read the question and each player has to give their answer within the allotted 30 seconds!
Step 5. If nobody slips up and gives the correct answer then the group decides who's answer was least entertaining. The nominee then takes a spin on the...

"THE WHEEL OF FATES" and instantly looses 1 token!
-Will you loose more tokens or...COULD YOU GET LUCKY!

The Wheel of Fates has...

     A 50% chance of landing "forfeit" grab a forfeit card!
     A 37.5% chance of landing on a "loose a token" spot. This spot instantly results in loosing 1 more token! 
     A 12.5% chance of scooping the kitty of sacrificed coins with the jackpot spot!


Step 6. Repeat the above steps until all but one player has lost their tokens to determine the winner of the game!

—description from the publisher

PatagoSaurios is the fifth author's game from the Ludogonia publishing house. In this simple and quick game you will have to reveal your Find cards. If you see that the necessary Finds appeared, Flip over the Dinosaur card!

The cards will be played in rounds, and whoever has the best score will win. But be careful, if you incorrectly cover a card, you get penalty tokens! Which subtract your final score.

PatagoSaurios was worked in conjunction with the Paleovertebrate Laboratory of the National University of Patagonia San Juan Bosco, for which it has an educational brochure on the different species that appear throughout the game.

Dino Luchas is the sixth Ludogonia game, and for the first time in a wallet format! To carry in your pocket everywhere, before a meal or at recess, there will always be a few minutes for a Dino-Fight.

This game is super simple: Each player takes their 4 Dinosaur cards. Simultaneously, each player plays a card on the table, and whoever has won the Dino-Fight advances a token corresponding to the attack or defense that he has used.

If any of the 4 pieces on your board reach their crown square, you won the game!

A tile-matching, stack-building game in which 2-4 players race to empty their hands of pentagonal tiles.  Tiles are matched by either 1 of the 5 colors or by 1 of the 5 symbols, and should a stack reach 5 colors, the stack splits in 2, increasing the odds of playable tiles on future turns. At the end of each round, once 1 player has stacked their last tile, each other player scores 1 point for each tile left in their hand. Rounds continue until one player has a score of at least 12, at which point the player with the lowest cumulative score wins.

Frontera was once a peaceful kingdom that dwell
many races from centaur that roams in forest, cyclops that dominate the mountain, and dwarf that live in dune. After the king is died of illness the kingdom is torn and fought over by 2 legitimate princes.

Each player represent the prince that try to win
this war by drafting dan deploying its army. Frontera is a 2 players game with 1 solo module for solo player, in this game each player will try to captured most token and controlling most terrain while balancing with owned resources

For more replayability, a player may choose from 3 alternative setup of modular terrain tile. With each setup a new style to attacking and defending yourself is made.

The core of this game is about placing your tokens using mancala mechanic and march them to chain battle combos while capturing enemies and controlling as many terrain as possible.

In the solo mode your objective is different than the 2 player game, because this time you will try to conquer the enemy as soon as possible before running out tokens.

boardgame: Roll Camera!: Super Hero Story Pack
description:
Superhero fever is sweeping Tinseltown, and you've been hired by the center of super cinema itself: Wonder Studios! Shelve that artsy passion project: it's time to don the mask and cape for big bucks. But it's not going to be easy - audiences demand more and more sensory assault with their super-antics, and of course that means CGI, CGI and more CGI! Shooting the scene is now just the beginning, but we've got to start somewhere! Roll Camera!

Roll Camera! Story Packs give a little thematic cinematic boost to your madcap movie-making, with all-new components and a small rules twist. The Super Hero Story Pack contains 22 brand new cards (15 superhero-themed Scenes, 1 Production Company, 1x Equipment card, 2x top and 2x bottom Scripts, 1x setup/rules card) and 5x CGI tokens. It can be played as an isolated module, replacing certain main game components for a highly thematic one-off game, or simply mixed in with the rest of the game.

—description from the publisher

Embark on an adventure and fight for survival in an area full of wild saurians. You are sent on your way with a few simple items. Everything else you have to scavenge for, fight for or craft yourself. Use the strength and skills of the saurians by adding them to your squad with a catching utensil. Collect everything you need on the plain, in the cave and on the lake to face the ultimate challenge in the jungle: the T-Rex!

You start with a deck consisting of 9 base cards and move along the hexagonal game board. During the game you will visit different locations and expand your deck with cards from a selection of 165 uniquely designed objects and saurians. With your cards you can move, attack, defend yourself, craft, trade and use card effects. As the game goes on you prepare yourself to defeat the T-Rex before the other player/team does. This wins you the game. The T-Rex is regularly moving withing the jungle, so you have to plan how to get to it safely with enough attack power in hand to best it. There are five different T-Rex variants which determine the game's difficulty/duration.

Saurvival is a mix of board and card game. Prepare for a strategic adventure full of action, fun and dinosaurs.

In this first Redwood expansion, nature becomes wilder and the challenge to achieve your panorama becomes bigger.

This expansion add new gameplay features:


     The Puma: This module adds a card to each deck of Panorama cards. Each card features a puma that uses a slot. The number of elements that can be placed on the map is reduced by 1.
     7 new animals: Each animal has its own character trait that will influence the way you play.
     Characters cards: At the start of the game, each player receives a random Character card. Each character has two unique abilities and player can use one during the game.
     3 new original movement templates: Hang glider, bike and canoe.
     Places module: Giant Redwood & tent.


—description from publisher

The Great Festival is the third, and final, installment in the family of BARPIG Adventure Party Games.

The Great Festival reveals more of the lore behind the story of the BARPIG tavern, which embraces how we grow by exploring and sharing what we’ve found: “Legend tells of an adventurous piggy, who explored the far reaches of the realm before settling down to build a tavern deep in the woods: A place where all adventuring pigs could meet kindred spirits and share tales from afar. Since then, whenever the harvest moon falls upon the equinox, everyone in the realm is invited to The Great Festival to celebrate why we explore and share what we’ve found.“

The Great Festival brings an extra 9 new Characters with their unique group challenges and a whopping additional 14 new item types to the table. Designed as a fully modular expansion that can be combined with either BARPIG - The Adventure Party Game, it's sequel BARPIG: After Hours, or both!

This game is not standalone and must be played in combination with either or both the previous versions of BARPIG.

—description from the publisher

Implosion is a middle weight euro-style dice game takes place in a sci-fi fantasy world.

The game is played in six rounds. Every round we salvage from an imploding dying star - a set of face-up cards placed in a circle on the table, with dice of different colours and values on them. Players take turns choose dice from the dying star in an Azul-like manner. After that, they will use the dice to generate resources, build cards into their tableaus, and use the dice to trigger the cards' abilities.

You can build armies to fight invaders from outer space, sell resources for points, or construct wonders for a lot of rewards.

The game ends after the sixth round. Every player sum up their VP token and VPs printed on the cards in their tableau, the one with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

Kickstarter Exclusive set. In addition to the standard retail box, this release contains the 6 additional champions (from the Art of War and Circle of Blood expansions), 11 challenge cards (duplicates for tournament play), 58 plastic tokens, 3 Wizard statues, and a GameTrayz storage solution.

Taken from retail game page:

In the land of Fabulosa, powerful magics have rendered war obsolete. With nothing else to compete for, the bored populace has turned to the Super Brawl for entertainment. These same powerful magics are now used to reach back into the timelines to pluck out the finest warriors from every civilization in order to compete against each other in the greatest entertainment spectacle ever staged.

Super Fantasy Brawl is a fun, fast-paced competitive miniatures board game. Players select a team of three champions and combine their unique action cards to create a synergistic action deck. The game is faction-free, so players have their choice of any combination of champions they wish. Each turn, the players use their hand of action cards to maneuver, attack, displace enemies, and claim objectives to score victory points.

The objectives in Super Fantasy Brawl are only scored at the beginning of a players' turn, ensuring that you always get a chance to disrupt your enemy's plans and position your champions to score the objective for yourself. This tactical to-and-fro continues until one player reaches the victory point threshold, declaring themselves the winner and advancing towards victory in the Super Brawl tournament!

Super Fantasy Brawl will also offer a 2v2 mode for 4 players to clash in an all-out melee mashup!

The Rebel Girls range is an empowering collection for both young girls and boys, showcasing the history and achievements of women of the past and present. We are proud to be a part of this important mission to inspire and instil confidence in our children.

In Card Game for Rebel Girls, the aim of the of the game is to collect and complete sets of Character Cards to score as many Rebel Reward points as possible. Each Rebel Reward Card requires a different team of Rebel Girls to complete the task, so players must use their Character Cards’ strengths and Wild Cards to build a squad of strong girls.

There are five categories of Rebel Girls in the game: Champions, Warriors, Leaders, Pioneers and Creators. Characters such as Harriet Tubman, the Bronte sisters, Eva Peron, Hypatia, Boudicca, Katia Krafft, Wilma Rudolph and Junk Tabei are all included. The game allows players to learn a little more about each Rebel Girl and what their achievements have been throughout history. Each card describes the characters and explains what made them special and unique.

This empowering card game is ideal for children aged six plus, and includes personalised certificates for anyone who is inspired to achieve greatness in their own lives. Whilst learning about incredible women throughout history, the Card Game for Rebel Girls also encourages players to be bold, work hard and be proud.

Chak is a Chess and Xiangqi style game designed by Couch Tomato in 2021 specifically to answer the hypothetical question of what a chess variant developed by mesoamerican cultures would look like. The design philosophy was to start from scratch and use only the common elements from all forms of chess: a king, a rook-type piece, a knight-like piece, and some sort of pawns; the rest would develop organically infusing a specific Mesoamerican culture -- in this case, the Maya.

The various pieces move and capture in different ways by replacing the captured piece, as in chess, and victory is achieved by checkmating or stalemating the opposing king. An alternate win condition called "altar mate" is achieved by moving the king into the opponent's space on the board called the "temple."

You are the owner of a travel agency. Passengers want the best trips, with one airline, and those trips mean more profit for you as well. Create attractive itineraries to become the most successful agent.

Plane Jane is a card game for two players. It requires 36 cards, 9 each from four airlines.

Each card represents a flight between two cities offered by an airline. Each row of a card shows the connections that flight may make to form a trip. Itineraries are sets of exactly three cards. Each card could be the beginning, middle, or end of a trip depending on which row is used.

- If an itinerary is with one airline, the player scores $5 in profit and draws one card.
- If an itinerary contains only two affiliated airlines, the player scores $4 in profit and draws two cards.
- If an itinerary contains non-affiliated airlines, the player scores $2 in profit and draws three cards

The second player will select one of the three cards on the table and add two cards from their hand to make a new set.

The game ends when neither player can create an itinerary. The player with the most money is the winner.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
As the young captain of the Obsidian, a mercenary troop that offers its military talents, your responsibility is immense: you must take the best decisions for yourself and your troops.
Employed by the King of Hauora, earn the respect of your three corps, analyze the battlefields to adopt the right strategies, listen to the advice of your lieutenants, and mount your horse. Make the right choices, because you are the hero!

L'Obsidienne is a comic combined with a game, one title in a series subtitled "The comic in which you are the hero". You embody a character in a immersive story in which your choices guide your progress. In general, gameplay consists in following the comic's panels — which contain clues, riddles and traps — and making choices about where to go and what to do, while using your visual perception to collect clues and be smart enough to resolve the riddles. As in any game, you can lose — sometimes really badly!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Le grand défi de la Culture générale pour les Nuls
description:
Roll the die, move forward as many squares as indicated and answer the question you are asked. History, arts, cinema, daily life, there is something for everyone!

And so that young and old can play together according to their level of knowledge, two levels of difficulty await you, beginner or expert.

Finally, to spice up the game, "special" boxes await players throughout their progress and will offer them challenges, super challenges and quizzes...

—description from the publisher

2048: The robots have taken over and the few human survivors are trying to hide.
You play as Carl, designated to search for a cure for the deadly epidemic that threatens.
Manage your food, limit your combat wounds, scan the environment for equipment, medicine and plants. Will you be captured by the surveillance drones? Shot down by the robots?
Make wise choices to save your community, because you are the hero!

Zone 25 is a comic combined with a game, one title in a series subtitled "The comic in which you are the hero". You embody a character in a immersive story in which your choices guide your progress. You have a character sheet akin to those used in role-playing games that tracks your possessions, your your coins, and your victory points. In general, gameplay consists in following the comic's panels — which contain clues, riddles and traps — and making choices about where to go and what to do, while using your visual perception to collect clues and be smart enough to resolve the riddles. As in any game, you can lose — sometimes really badly!

—description from the publisher

Players use their cards to program robots. Function calls can be used to repeat some actions. Loot boxes on game field give additional unique ability. Who gets more batteries wins!

—description from the publisher

Dark crimes, perpetrated by The Black Blade, have thrown London into terror. Scotland Yard has failed to find the murderer, but you can help identify him.

Play as Detective Holmes or Dr. Watson, tracking down clues, interviewing witnesses and using your deductive skills. Make the right choices to solve these three investigations, because you are the hero!

Sherlock Holmes: Darkness over London is a comic combined with a game, one title in a series subtitled "The comic in which you are the hero". You embody a character in a immersive story in which your choices guide your progress. You have a character sheet akin to those used in role-playing games that tracks your possessions, your special abilities, your coins, and your victory points. In general, gameplay consists in following the comic's panels — which contain clues, riddles and traps — and making choices about where to go and what to do, while using your visual perception to collect clues and be smart enough to resolve the riddles. As in any game, you can lose — sometimes really badly!

-description from the publisher's website

Dead By Dawn is the debut title from Retro Active Games

Will you survive the night?
Will you find The Truck Key and escape??
Or will you end up...Dead By Dawn???

"Head for that Farmhouse!" you think to yourself as the moans of the recently undead trail at your heels. You pass a Truck and some gas pumps outside the house...maybe there is a key somewhere? No time now! You just hope to make it inside and find some supplies to survive until the National Guard can sweep through in the next 12 to 24 hours. You see faint other figures on the horizon, assuredly living. Will they be friend or foe?

"Dead By Dawn" is a fast paced, strategic zombie kill-a-thon with a tinge of survival horror. Play with 2-6 players in a time (and part luck) based game of desperation and intuition. Survive until the game ends (Dawn) and have the most kills to win. Or hunt down The Truck Key and escape! But it's a big house, with many locked doors, and the key could be anywhere! You'll have to look for other keys to open any doors along the way, along with gather Weapons and Items to help you survive and win the game!

Gameplay:
Each player gets their own character card with unique abilities for the duration of each game. (Random distribution recommended.) Players attempt to roll and move, while strategically avoiding, fighting, placing, and moving the growing horde of zombies that keep filling the board. Players will attempt to reach the farmhouse at the center of the board with a "KEY" CARD or Character Ability to be able to unlock one of the the many doors in the place. 
   Inside these rooms, there are plenty of useful cards in the form of Items and Weapons, and sometimes even Key Tokens, to help you gain points via zombie kills or help you gain the upper hand and survive long enough to escape. 
    But along the way, you may encounter an unlucky or helpful event via the EVENT spaces that litter the board and force players to draw an EVENT CARD- that could change the course of the game!

Games are timed. A 2 player game is an hour, 3-4 player game is 2 hours, and a 5-6 player game is 3 hours. A sand-timer is included to track turns. Players have 2 minutes to complete their turn (barring house rules).

Players have limited inventory space likened to the mechanic present in "Resident Evil" video games. This can be increased by 1 slot by finding "The Backpack" ITEM CARD.

During each standard turn, players take possession of two dice. One black D-6 die with numeric values of 1 through 3 on all faces(these add zombies to the board every turn!). And one blue D-8, 1 - 8 die that is used for player and zombie movement. (Example: player rolls an 8. They may move 8 spaces, but zombies also get to move 8 spaces after player has made their movement. The Zombie Movement can be split any way. (One zombie can move 8 spaces, or two can move 4. etc)
    The player also gets use and possession of the sand timer. During a turn, the player starts the timer, rolls and FIRST places zombies, THEN moves their player Pawn, use any cards, equip items, trigger an event/etc, THEN LAST- You may move any zombies on the board (this matches your rolled movement)

To win- survive until time runs out and have the most kills. OR alternatively, hunt down the keys to the truck outside and refill the gas tank and make it to the ESCAPE space at the back of the board. (This ends the game early and regardless of kill score)

—description from the publisher

Multi-layer team tic-tac-toe with cards! With 4 ways to score you'll need all your Wit & Whiskers to come out on top of this dog pile.

The game starts by first splitting players into two teams, this should be done using alternating seat positions going around the play area so no two teammates are sitting next to eachother. The game cards are shuffled and 4 are dealt to each player to form their hands. Play starts with the player who most recently pet an animal.

The objective of the game is to be the first team to score a total of 15 cards. Cards are scored whenever a scoring pattern, called a Whisker, is formed in the 3 by 3 play area. The player who played the final card to form the Whisker scores all cards used to create that Whisker. There are 4 types of Whiskers: same animal in line of 3, same weapon in line of 3, total 10 in square of 4, or same color in solid area of 5.

Players take turns going clockwise around the table, playing one card into the 3 by 3 play area, and then drawing a card from the deck. Players can play their card into any empty space or on top of a card with a different background (e.g. play a day-time card on top of a night-time card). Stacking cards can be strategic, but is also a risk as creatings Whiskers will score the entire pile of cards!

-description from designer

This game like a cross between "Wordle" and "Whirling Witchcraft."
Combine tiles and cards with letters of the alphabet to generate more English words and longer English words.

The goal of the game is to reduce or eliminate the number of cards in your hand (server). By generating English words, you can reduce the number of cards and send them to your opponent.
This means that cards will be sent to you by your opponent at the same time, so use your English vocabulary and flexibility to keep the server from filling up with cards!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Corporate Archives Promo Card
description:
Fall Season Promo 2022 of FryxGames, which you can buy on its own, or get for free if you order something at their webstore during Fall 2022.

Corporate Archives is a Prelude-card with a Science-tag.

You get 13 M€ and may look at the top 7 cards from the deck. Take 2 of them into your hand and discard the other 5.

Promo card X39.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
There are five play variants based on each other:

"Festmark-Pferderennen" is a horse race for kids.
"Lachendes Lotto" introduces the basic game rules.
"Frohe Fahrt (mit Hindernissen)" is a simple family game.
"Wer weiß wie?" adds some difficulty.
"Expertentest" is the expert game.

The players take turns to roll and move. Whenever they get on a space the players have to follow the instructions printed there. The two last game variants add quiz questions. 
To win the game a player has to win a certain amount of money or have the most money after an agreed on time.

–user summary

The Battle of Aqbat al-Bakr is a small and simple postcard game published as a freebie by SNAFU Design, as a gift for attendees to the Pax Ludica 2022 Wargaming convention (Cadis, Spain). It's the fourth of the Snafu Postcard Games.

It's a 2-player game depicting a small battle during the civil war in the Caliphate of Cordoba in 1010 AD. Some Christian Catalan counts joined forces with one of the Muslim candidates (Muhammad al-Mahdi) and fought against the other candidate to the caliphate, Sulaiman al-Mustain, backed by berber (imazighen) troops.

On his turn the player rolls two dice and the results are the number of Movement Points (one die) and the number of Combat Points (the other die). Then the player assigns these points among his units as he sees fit to move, rally, fight, charge (heavy units) and/or harass (light units). The first one to disorganize or kill all enemy units wins.

—description from the designer

Recreate the atmosphere of a escape room at home! You have 60 minutes to find the clues hidden by the Game Master that will allow you to solve all the puzzles. The game offers three scenarios, all different, to test the strongest nerves.

boardgame: Escape Box: La Reine des Neiges II
description:
Welcome to Arendelle...
You have 45 minutes and no more to solve the puzzles and find Olaf!
 On a beautiful day, Anna, Elsa, Olaf, Kristoff and Sven are playing hide and seek. It's Olaf's turn to hide, but when his friends finish counting, they discover a strange letter left by Olaf.
 To find where he is hiding, you must search for the clues left by Olaf and solve the puzzles.
 It's your turn ! Be curious and clever!

boardgame: メイドギルドへようこそ (Welcome to the Maid Guild)
description:
As wealthy patrons, players have traveled to the local maid guild in an attempt to hire new staff for their estates.

Each round a group of four maids becomes available to hire.  However, each player only learns the identity of half the group.  They must then bid for the order to choose new maid staff based on what they know and subtle clues from their opponents' bids.

After bidding is complete, every player learns the identity of all four maids.  One by one starting with the player who made the highest bid, players choose who to hire.  Of course, the player making the lowest bid will likely end up stuck with undesireable staff, including the incompetent maids in black.

Each hired maid is placed around a player's estate board to fulfill jobs such as cooking, cleaning, and accounting.  Each maid has several icons denoting their strengths, which can earn extra points if they match their assigned job.

When the deck of maid cards runs out, or a player is out of money, the game ends.  Players add up respect points from the maids they hired as well as bonus points from fulfilled jobs around their estate.  However, the player who hired the most maids in black loses their social standing and is eliminated from the game.  The remaining player with the most respect points is the winner.
These 20 unique hero feats, 1 for each hero, will allow every faction to aid their allies in new ways.

Build an army, conquer lands, slay dragons and steal taxes! Medi-evil is the fast changing strategy Card game for 3-5 players.

Choose to strengthen your defenses or build your army. Collect taxes and protect your land. Send your army out to war to take other players' land and taxes. Beware of dragons, famine and invaders!

Each player starts with 2 castles/land and collects taxes on each go. The more castles you have the more taxes you collect.

On each players go they go through 2 phases - the 'build' phase and the 'action' phase.

The Build Phase
During the build phase players can strengthen their defenses or choose to build an army.

The Action Phase
The action phase requires players to make a choice to send out their army to war or to to draw more cards from the middle deck.
Special Cards

Sometimes a player is forced to play or draws out one of the special cards - these are the 'Dragon' card, the 'Famine' card and the 'To War' card. Each of these cards has an immediate effect on the player or a player of their choice.

Send your army to war!
When a player chooses to send out his/her army they can choose to attack any other players castle or fight off dragons. If they win they claim that players land and all taxes.

Winning the Game
The winner of the game is the player with the most taxes after all the agreed rounds have been played.

Inside the box is all 197 cards you'll need to play the game, This includes:

18 Villagers
18 Archers
18 Knights
18 Vikings
5 Dragons
5 Famine
5 To War
10 Castles
100 Taxes

A trick-taking tarot card game that mixes the tradition of a historical deck and the innovation of a Bridge-inspired bidding system.

Bid the perfect contract with your partner and play your cards to win points and tricks.

Be aware of the special properties of cards like the Bagatto, the Fool and the World.

A deep but fast-paced card game.

—description from the designer

The players take turns to roll and move. The goal is to reach space 100 to rescue a dachshound family. To get from one floor to the next, the players have to use a ladder. On the roll of a 6 the player may take one of the four ladders to reach the window above his position.

Tired of ordinary games, Are you a Potty Mouth? is a laugh out loud game full of surprising and hilarious facts about swearing. As a creator of the game I wanted to make sure that this game is not sexual, derogatory, or prejudicial.
 The game consists of 150 laugh out loud cards filled with obscure facts and pop culture about swearing. There are 10 categories: Geography, Going abroad, History, Social trends, Texting acronyms, True and false, TV/movies/music, Word definitions Wordplay and Visual cards (guess the phrase).

The game is super easy to learn and fast paced.

It can be played as individuals 3+ or in groups. It is very adaptable and has been played on Zoom as well. The object of the game is the first person to collect 10 correctly answered cards wins. The twist of the game is when a player has collected 9 cards anyone can challenge them to take a "Challenge card". Once challenged you have to play. If the player answers correctly its an automatic win. If not, the challenger earns the card.

The game is designed for 16 + due to the use of swear words.

-description from designer
This troupe box allows you to add three new heroes from Dominion faction of the Goblin race to the game: Seasick Stu, Shabbaroon, Boom Boom Mc Boom.

It's the Age of Sail and the Crown is distilling rum on colonized islands for enormous profit. Meanwhile pirates intend to sail in, steal that rum, and drink it all . These two factions (*Crown* and *Pirate*) play their conflict out over a two player asymmetric card game. Each player has a deck, a hand of cards, coins, and a menu of 3 actions to choose from each turn. But what each does varies substantially.

The *Crown* player will work in secret, laying traps and pushing for an economic victory. They score rum by founding it on an island and investing coins into it over time. They protect that investment by placing hazards in front of the island face-down. When the Pirate player tries to raid that island the Crown can decide to spend their coin to raise those hazards against the Pirate, stopping them or making them pay a terrible price. The Crown may alternatively place traps or economic investments on their islands, luring the Pirate to certain doom or increasing the momentum of the Crown's victory. If the Crown manages to hold out and score four points of rum, they win the game.

The *Pirate* player will assemble ships and crew. These will hopefully raid their way to where the rum is with a combination of deduction and perseverance. But regardless they will trigger and combo together unique crew's unique abilities to gain an advantage. If the Pirate finds four bottles of rum (quality doesn't matter), they win the game.

—description from designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Mauseschlau & Bärenstark bring movement into the quartet game!
If you want to collect the most quartets in the movement quartet, you have to ask the other players for cards and also demonstrate the movement exercises shown.

Chance it is a gambling game in which everyone reaches for the stars without losing their last heart.

The die is cast. Now it's time to keep your nerve! Who reaches — often expensively — for the stars? Who prefers — sometimes lucratively — to avoid all risks? And who gambles away their last heart, and with it their very last chance to finish the game as a winner?

Purchased special cards and one chance-it card per player make your own earnings bubble up even more. But whoever uses them must — often and unintentionally — participate in the next high-risk game round. That can be a real heartbreaker!

Refined risk assessment and dice that can lead any speculation astray make Chance it an outstanding gambler's game with heart.

—description from the publisher

Animericals is a devious dice & card matching game. Players take turns drawing cards to match randomly rolled dice.

But these dice are like no other. Instead of the abstract shapes we all know as numbers, colorful animals represent 0-9. Instead of the number one, a unicorn horn reminds us that all mythical creatures in the game match '1'.

Use mechanics like, color stacking to build strategies and replace all the dice using super combos.

Whoever can recite the the full hidden sequence (without interruption) wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Κυνήγι ερωτήσεων: Ζώα-Φυτά – Συμπλήρωμα (Quiz Hunt: Animals-Plants – Supplement)
description: 384 supplementary questions to go alongside the base game

The war of 1870 was marked by major sieges such as those of Metz, Strasbourg and Paris. The one of Belfort, from November 3, 1870 to February 18, 1871, is one of the last episodes of the war and the garrison will be the last French army to lay down its arms.

To simulate the siege, the Storm over Diksmuide (VaeVictis 133) system was copied and modified. The soggy plains of Flanders are replaced here by rugged, wooded terrain dominated by the citadel of Belfort.

Storm over Belfort tries to show the difficult conditions of an ultimately dynamic siege where bitter fighting is fought in the snow and under the shells for more political than strategic gains.

A game by Jean-Philippe Gury

Complexity: 4/10
Solo playability: 2/10
Duration: 2 to 3 hours
One turn = two weeks
A battalion = a few hundred men

Published in VaeVictis #164.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Each player is dealt 6 word-cards.
A story is read out and players are invited to add one of their word-cards at certain parts of the story. If a word is accepted, the player gets a Joker which scores points at the end.
When the story is finished players score-up;
Any unused word cards - -1
Any Joker - +2
Play a pre-agreed number of stories and the highest score wins.

Inspired by the eponymous book, which explores the tremedous variety of colour nuances, Colorama is a colour-themed trivia game in which players compete to obtain colour cards, named Colorama card, which match the objectives they are given at the start of the game.

Each player starts with randomly-assigned 6 objective cards, each representing a colour, and then players take turn answering colour-themed questions, which are on Colorama cards matching a single type of colour. If the player answers right, they win the card and place it in their collection. Each Colorama card has one featured colour which matches one of the objective cards. For example, the Black Beetle Colorama card would match the Black objective card

However, there are also Surprise cards which have various immediate effect ranging from swapping a colour card with an opponent to giving a previously acquired Colorama card to another player.

The first player obtaining one Colorama card for each of their six objective cards is the winner!

ZAG is a visual game about solving graphic puzzles in zig-zag.

Each player has a mini-board, five double-sided tiles and a deck of cards showing visual challenging patterns to solve as fast as one can.

Each card shows a number of tiles to be placed and solve its pattern, from only two to five tiles. But don´t be fooled. Sometimes, a two piece puzzle may be surprisingly challenging.
 The deck comes with tutorial cards that help players learn the tile placing rules and practice a little bit before diving into the real ZAG. First: be careful, the board has a specific orientation. Second: tiles are always placed on the board inside its limits. Third: tiles are always placed parallel to the board´s sides, so they are never placed diagonally. And fourth, tiles never overlap!

The game comes with two decks of puzzles: a beginner´s set of 12 cards with hints that show a thumbnail of each tile used to solve that puzzle. Once those cards become too easy, and one might look for a more challenging experience, there is a set of 45 cards showing only the puzzle, without any hint whatsoever.

A game of ZAG is played in rounds. 
Each round, give each player a card and they have to solve it simultaneously.
When someone has finished, flip the card face down and say: - ZAG!
Wait until everyone finishes, check if the puzzles were correctly solved and earn the card as points.
After a round for each count of tiles, from 2 to 5, the game is over.

—description from designer

Find a solution even in a hopeless situation!

Overview:
In UA: Bravery Time, you and your teammates take control of Ukraine and must win the war together. This is a cooperative game where the team either wins or loses together. 
The task is to complete all civil needs and liberate the territories from occupation. Build effective interaction with each other to achieve better results.

How to play

Each game turn is a week consisting of 7 days. The move includes four stages that follow each other in sequence.

Stage «Selection of cards»
At this stage, each player receives new cards.

Stage «Planning»
In the second stage, players must plan their game week - place the cards in a row in front of them. Each card takes as long as indicated in the upper left corner of the card. Cards that are not included in the plan cannot be used in the stage «Player Actions».

Choice of a strategy and search for optimal solutions

Players can actively help each other by exchanging cards to optimize the overall plan. Only active communication and cooperation will allow you to succeed in the game.

Great scope for communication and interaction

Stage "Enemy Actions"

Cards are drawn from the enemy's action deck, as a result of which players receive new challenges and may lose their territories.

Command challenge. Your rival is a game

Stage «Player Actions»

Players use actions that they have previously planned to help the people of Ukraine, counter enemy actions, and liberate territories.

Room for strategic and tactical planning

Territories map
The game takes place in the territory of Ukraine, where there is a war. The liberation of these territories (turn over to the freed side) is one of the main tasks of the game.

The role of cards in the game

The game cards display the tasks that the people of Ukraine face on their way to victory. You and your team receive cards from which you need to make an optimal action plan for the current move. In all combinations, the cards affect each other in a unique way.

In UA: Bravery time, each game card is an action. There are four types of cards in the deck:

Civil need cards are the actions that need to be completed to win the game. They have two subtypes - ordinary and urgent.

People’s actions - help from the people of Ukraine (game bonuses). They are helpful to simplify other actions.

Military actions - cards that allow you to defend and liberate territories.

Enemy actions. Some of them are enemy attacks due to which you can lose territories. Others are additional problems that complicate the game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Euthia: Torment of Resurrection – Necromancer Hero
description:
This hero kit turns Adraghor, the Soul Reaper, into a playable hero, bringing the total playable heroes up to seven with the core game, and eight with the Fierce Powers & Crawling Shadows expansion.

—description from the publisher

The Lost Brigades Expansion is special because it allows players to incorporate these new units into their faction's core units, without requiring a Commander Card call-in. Alternatively the 60 building board extender tiles offer a play-mode that allows players to add units from across their entire collection into their faction's building boards.

Lost Brigades vehicles also emphasize the new Assault rules found in the second edition. This can dramatically flush out a faction's options and strategic decisions from early on in the game. Each Lost Brigades vehicle also comes with two commanders that allow players to further specialize and upgrade that vehicle, or alternatively they can field the core Lost Brigades vehicle and choose an entirely different commander to gain access to an even wider range of units.

—description from publisher

The 2nd Edition Solo and Cooperative Expansion is a completely revised product drawing on the strengths of the 1st Edition but now with a much more intelligent A.I to play against or on a human player's team.  The A.I. now secures resource and victory control points like a human player allowing for standard victory conditions rather than the previous abstract round limit of 1st Edition.  Players can also use the 250 high quality acrylic unit tiles to play in multiple Fog of War modes including the 45 minute Fog of War Lite Mode for quick games.

—description from publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Throne of Eldraine Planeswalker Deck: Rowan, Fearless Sparkmage
description:
This Magic: The Gathering starter deck is ready to play right out of the box. Battle a few times to master your deck’s strategy, then open the 2 included booster packs to customize it.

Rowan inspires great loyalty in those who follow her. Attack relentlessly, using abilities to power up your cards, then use Rowan herself to push through the final points of damage and finish off your opponent.

—description from publisher

A cross between UNO and Dominos. ION is a deck of domino cards with special actions on some that are taken during play. The first player to get rid of all the cards in their hand wins.

NOTE: This edition has dice and less cards.

The goal of Catch That Mouse is simple: be the first to lose all your cards. But to do that, you'll have to be fast and sharp-eyed.

You take turns flipping a card and putting it on the central pile. As soon as a Mouse card appears after a Cheese card, you will all try to catch the mouse. The player to last slap, takes the pile.

An easy to learn, yet very entertaining game. The presence of decoys (objects that look just like cheeses and animals that look like mice) makes it easy to make mistakes.

—description from publisher

A compilation of previously released expansions for Sprawlopolis, plus the new Roadwork expansion.


     Sprawlopolis: Points of Interest
     Sprawlopolis: Construction Zones
     Sprawlopolis: Wrecktar
     Sprawlopolis: Beaches
     Sprawlopolis: Interstate
     Sprawlopolis: Roadwork

Each player controls their own set of colored stacking pieces. Lay out the Letter Cards, flip a Category Card and the one-minute timer and the game is on! Players, playing simultaneously, shout out words that fit the category and place one of their stacking pieces on the corresponding letter card. When time’s up, the player who has their colored stacking piece on the top of the stack scores for that card. The more pieces under their piece, the higher the score!

-description from publisher

Heropolis is a humorous yet strategic building & adventure game, in which players take on the role of heroes and win the favor of the citizens with their heroic deeds. The ultimate immersive board game, full of surprises.  Slip into the skin of a funny Heropolian and find out if you too are a little everyday hero.  Provide your fellow citizens with an new metropolis, discover long-abandoned neighborhoods and fill the "News" with your headlines! Together, you protect the townspeople from "Rocktopus" and "Crocodealer" or make off with the latest "high wheels"!

Using a "Rondel-Mechanic", Heropolis can be strategically planned, but can also easily get beginners into the game. These simply choose one of the three possible actions, while the strategists are planning their actions far into the future.

Victory or defeat are decided through victory points. You come to the victory points through various game actions like: Constructing buildings, fighting enemies, soloing Missions, photographing landmarks, etc. You will be rewarded with stars for your good deeds. Whoever collects the most stars at the end of the session becomes the "Hero of the Day" and wins the game!

—description from the publisher

It is Midsummer's Day and a group of Shakespearean characters try to collect the four missing items from their play from the Forest of Arden. Lurking in the heart of the forest is their nemesis who they must try to avoid at all costs.

Each player needs to carefully plan their route to pick up the 4 items that are needed for their play making sure that they do not get blocked by any of the other players. Crossing the clearing containing their nemesis will take a mixture of luck and strategic movement. At the start of each turn players roll a die to determine what order they will get to move their character. Will you take the quickest route or will you play to delay your rivals?

The game ends when all of the players have made it safely out of the forest or been abducted by the fae.

The first player to exit the forest with all 4 items for their play is the winner.

If you are forced to confront your nemesis will be lucky enough to overcome them or will you be robbed of the items that you have secured so far and forced back into the forest?

Take too long and if darkness falls then the evil fae will emerge to come to claim you.

—description from designer

boardgame: Twisted Fables: Mulan Playmat with Stage Card
description:
650mm x 345mm playmat featuring the artwork of Mulan with stage card - Hive Outpost. It offers a glimpse into her bleak, violent interstellar universe, launching you onto the dangerous platform of a satellite outpost where you do not want to fall off the edge.

The Hive Outpost reads: Each time a fable inflicts damage on an opponent, she gains Damage +1 if the opponent is on the edge of the fighting track.

You’re at the end of your 3rd year studying garden design at a prestigious college and are starting to get a little frustrated. Yes, you’re having the time of your life and loving all your classes, but nobody is taking you seriously. You keep telling your parents that there is a career for you and you won’t become a starving artist. When you find out that your local botanic garden is holding an open competition for a succulent wall installation, you’re ecstatic. Finally, a way to prove your legitimacy! However, your professor knows the board and cautions you to submit two designs. Since succulents are trendy, the board will not choose the design that is the most popular, but the one that will be the least controversial. Can you make the best arrangement while navigating the shifting tide of public opinion and secure your first installation?

In this game, you and your opponent will select cards from a display to place in one of your two succulent walls. Cards must follow three simple rules when being placed

     In each column, each card must have a higher number than the card above it.
     In each row, each card must have a color further to the right in the ROYBGV coloring order.
     No orthogonally adjacent cards may share the same number or color.


At the end of each round, you and your opponent will place one card to increase or decrease the point values of each type of succulent. At the end of the game, only your lowest scoring wall will count, so be careful in your choices!

—description from designer

Space Bumpercraft is a game designed based on the Grid Movement mechanism. However, your opponents are not everyone else, but all your own Bumpercraft on the map.

Bumpercrafts all have limited movement patterns, and within the limited options, collisions are inevitable.

How you move your Bumpercrafts and clear the path before the movement for the clunky big Bumpercrafts is the key to victory.

Compare the points with your opponents and see who has the most valued Bumpercrafts surviving.

—description from publisher

Dicing Farmland is a game based on the Grid Coverage mechanism.

Different from the game with established shapes, Dicing Farmland requires players to draw the shape of each crop by themselves.

Then place the crop in the big Farmland. After that, the harvest action becomes the last Coverage - Cross out the planted crops. Each step is related, manage the farmland carefully!

Grow the most valued Farmland and gain the most points against your opponents to win the game.

—description from the publisher

It's a busy first day for you and your team at the new Unicorn Clinic. Like any good clinic, you not only want to give your patients (who are all unicorns, of course!) the best care, but you also need to get those positive reviews to grow your clinic's reputation. Unfortunately the supply closet is a mess and the line of waiting patients is growing by the minute. While you scramble to find the supplies you need, each waiting unicorn is wondering where their doctor is and rapidly losing their patience. Work together to quickly treat patients and get those sweet 5-star reviews!

In Unicorn Clinic, you'll work together to run your own clinic for sick and injured unicorns. On your turn, you'll decide which patient to visit and roll your dice to try to complete their treatment. If at first you don't succeed, you can try to roll again, but you'll have to be smart about which dice you reroll and where you spend your time. Do you go after a new patient who still has a rosy 5-star view of the clinic and hope to treat them fast, possibly earning more time to take on another patient? Or do you choose a patient who has been waiting a while and try to finally get them out the door? You'll also want to consider where your fellow doctors are- every doctor in the room will give you an additional specialty die to roll and increase your odds for success.

After your turn, any unicorns left unattended in other rooms will lower their potential star rating. If any unicorns reach zero stars, it's CODE U! You have one last shot to join forces as a team to treat the unicorn before they leave the clinic in disappointment, ruining your reputation (and ending the game.)

If you DO successfully treat a unicorn, of course they leave you a review to read! Score enough review stars and you'll secure your clinic's reputation as the best place for excellent unicorn care.

"They totally removed the crab from my butt. 5 stars!" - Actual Unicorn Patient.

—description from designer

Stellarium is a board game for people of different ages to have fun together, while being enchanted by the discoveries of the constellations looking at the night sky formed by pieces with different star patterns.

To play, you have to assemble a 5x5 night sky with the sky pieces and separate the constellation cards by their degree of difficulty. Players will always have 2 constellation cards in hand and must try to identify one of them in the sky, on their turn, within 30 seconds. If he succeeds, he takes for himself the number of pieces indicated on the letter and puts the constellation identified in his personal record.

Whoever has the highest score has made the most valuable constellation record and won this astronomical race!

—description from the publisher

Gift ved første blik ("Married at First Sight") is a reality tv show of Danish origin.
This game casts two players as newlyweds, and the other players are friendly advisors who draw cards with marital dilemmas. Each advisor gets to vote yes or no to each dilemma, and then the newlyweds has to agree on whether to say yes or no to the dilemma.

After four dilemmas, the cards are flipped, and the consequences on four parameters are tallied up: communication, sex life, social life, and economy. If any parameter goes too far out of balance, the marriage fails and the game ends. Otherwise, play goes on to next round, again of four dilemmas. The game ends at latest after three rounds.

Points:
The newlyweds get 1 point for each dilemma they say 'yes' to, and 1 point for 'surviving' the first round, 2 points for the second round, and 3 points for the third round.
The advisors each get a point for each time the newlyweds follow their advice.
However, should the game end because the marriage fails, the advisors who gave the advice that was the final straw of the marriage, each lose 2 points.

—user summary

Wherever werewolves are up to mischief, vampires are not far away. This is also the case with the sequel to the communication game, Werewolves - Night of the Vampires, in which the bloodsuckers are loose. The proven game principle remains the same: Every night the vampires secretly devour a human. The following day, all players discuss who among them could be a vampire - and can lynch the most suspicious person after voting. Special characters like the insomniac and the investigator can unmask the vampires, but will people believe them the next day?

Werewolves - Night of the Vampires is stand-alone, but can also be combined with Werewolves New Edition to play with up to 48 people in three teams (vampire, werewolf, village).

—description from publisher

Boss Monster offers players the chance to become the ultimate villain: the boss of their own sidescroller video game-style dungeon! For anyone who has ever spent hours navigating the pixelated perils of an 8-bit dungeon, Boss Monster is just the ticket! Up to six players lure hapless heroines into the self-made dungeon with the right treasures to devour their souls. But beware: the dungeon must be as deadly as it is alluring, or these puny invaders will survive long enough to reach the boss room and wound the boss monster. The best beast wins!

The Boss Monster Big Box is the ultimate all-in-one package for fans of pixel dungeons and black humor. It includes, in addition to the base game, the expansions Next Level, Rise of the Minibosses, Total Destruction, and Vault of the Rogues. In addition, six dungeon tableaus are included to appropriately set the scene for the map dungeons.

—description from publisher

A dream wedding in the Caribbean: In Deadly Dinner, the small wedding party has arrived to celebrate the most beautiful day in the life of the bride and groom. But before the celebration can really begin, the groom is found dead.

The host invites the guests to a dinner party, where they take on the roles of murder suspects. One of them has committed the murder, but all of them have secrets they would rather keep to themselves. Over the course of three rounds, they try to get behind the secrets of the other players without making themselves suspects. The common goal is to solve the murder case - or get away with the bloody deed.

Deadly Dinner - Tödliches Versprechen is the fourth game of the Deadly Dinner series.

A game of Deadly Dinner consists of three rounds, in which everyone receives new information about the crime. In each round, the players also receive secret objects that can be found in envelopes in the package. The players must discuss their information and clues in the group in order to identify the perpetrator at the end. In order to prepare, the players should receive the starting information about their character a few days before playing.

—description from publisher

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Storm Hero Pack
description:
Once worshipped as a goddess for her ability to control the weather, Ororo Munroe left that life behind when she answered Charles Xavier’s call for help. Now, Ororo uses her powers to defend mutants around the world as one of the mighty X-Men, Storm.

This mighty mutant soars into battle with this expansion pack, which introduces Storm as a new playable hero along with her fifteen signature cards. With a pre-built Leadership deck ready to play from the get-go, you’ll be able to blast through the villain’s forces with Storm’s weather control from the moment you open the box. With her raw power and ability to shape the climate of the battle, Storm is a potent hero that can benefit any team.

This hero pack also includes a bonus modular encounter set featuring the devious Shadow King!

For players eager to challenge Magneto and his Brotherhood of Mutants, they can get started with the Storm Hero Pack! This expansion comes with a 40-card pre-built deck, giving players the chance to start playing right out of the box.

—description from the publisher
