boardgame: Tercio de la Muerte:  The Battle of the Ebro River, July-August, 1938
description:
1938 went from bad to worse for the Republican cause in Spain. Successes the previous year at Guadalajara and Teruel and defeats at Brunete and Aragon did not stop Nationalist victories that overran the whole of Republican North Spain (Vizcaya, Asturias, Cantabria) while continued military support from Nazi Germany and fascist Italy proved more effective than Soviet backing of the Republic.

Madrid , Central and Eastern Spain held, but after Teruel´s winter reconquest by the Nationalists and their Spring march to the Mediterranean Sea, which cut Republican territory in two, many outside of Spain felt the Republican cause was lost. 
However, there was some hope in the Republican enclave centered on Barcelona. A new and friendly government came to power in France that reopened shipments of badly needed arms and supplies, and new shipments of arms from the Soviet Union successfully made it past the Nationalist blockade, reinvigorating the Republican forces still in the field. 
A larger war in Europe was increasingly apparent, and it was hoped that if the Republican forces could hold out long enough, they could be part of this larger conflict. As such, the Republican military decided upon a bold offensive across the Ebro River to retake ground and stop Nationalist offensive towards Valencia, as well as demonstrate that all was not lost.

Each copy of Tercio de la Muerte is composed of the following:


     Two 11 by 17 inch map sections with Turn and Game Record tracks.
     154 un-mounted, double sided units.
     This set of rules.


Players will also need a standard deck of playing cards and a six-sided die (D6) to play the game. Units represent regiments or brigades or regiments (infantry, cavalry) and companies (infantry detachments and armor). Each turn represents three days of time. A map hex is a little less than 1.5 miles across.

Players use a standard deck of playing cards (a custom card set is available from the publisher) to activate their units and determine some game events (Random Event and End of Turn occurrence). A six-sided die is used to determine how air and artillery strikes a player has each turn, as well to resolve those types of attacks as well as for resolving ground combat between opposing units.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Magic: the Gathering – Battle for Baldur`s Gate Commander: Exit from Exile
description:
Commander Legends: Battle for Baldur's Gate introduces four new Commander decks. Each Commander deck includes a legendary "face" Commander as well as an alternate legend, plus a Background to use as Commanders. In total, each deck contains ten new-to-Magic cards.
Each deck also contains a Collector Booster Sample Pack. Each Sample Pack contains two cards:

1 Booster Fun rare or mythic rare (a traditional foil in 20% of Sample Packs)
1 Traditional foil rulebook common or uncommon
The Collector Booster Sample Pack might contain a special version of one of the cards in your Commander deck or a card perfect to add to another Commander deck. Most Commander Legends: Battle for Baldur's Gate Commander decks contain a Collector Booster Sample Pack in the same language as the deck. However, if your Commander deck is in Chinese, Italian, Portuguese, or Spanish, your Collector Booster Sample Pack will be in English.

There are two versions of packaging you can find for these decks: the full-color box and a minimal-packaging version found online. The minimal packaging has the same contents but uses less packaging. Both are recyclable and reduce plastic waste!

—description from the publisher
Gefragt Gejagt: Erweiterung contains new expansions for this quiz game as well as components that allow for play with up to six players.

In Floh im Zoo, you want to remove all the fleas from the four animals under your care. They'll hop back and forth from one animal to another, but if you manage to de-flea your animals first, you win!

In Monsterjäger: Das Kartenspiel, you hunt for red, blue, and yellow monsters as quickly as you can, attacking the cards of other players by slapping them or defending your own cards by getting your hand on the table first.

boardgame: Tiny Towns: McPherson Manor Promo Card
description:
Promotional Monument card exclusively available at AEG’s booth at 2022 conventions, or alternately through Tantrum House’s Season 9 Kickstarter campaign and later available in Pack Archer from Dice Tower 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

Worth 5 VP. After constructing this monument, that player may only construct buildings adjacent to other buildings.

Grab 3-5 of your most fun and least mature friends for a memorable game of Happy Endings! Any answer will do as you fill-in-the-blanks of 150 playful and suggestive sentences. Match players’ answers correctly on your turn and write the favorite answer on everyone else’s turn to score some “amusing” Happy Ending cards and, hopefully, some extra points. The player with the most points after three rounds wins the game!

You and 6 friends are trapped in a building while being hunted by a killer. Your goal is to move yourself and your friends from floor to floor to delivery supplies to the right players while trying to avoid the killer. If everybody survives long enough to collect their supplies you and your friends are able to defend yourself and escape.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Core Set 2020 Planeswalker Deck: Sorin, Vampire Lord
description:
Planeswalker Decks familiarize players who are interested in Magic with basic strategy, plus the games setting, characters, and mechanics of the latest set. Each deck comes with a foil Planeswalker card not found anywhere else.

Sorin Markov is the lord of a proud, ancient lineage of vampire aristocrats. Gather an army of loyal vampires to drain the life from your opponents.

Contents: 1 ready-to-play 60-card deck featuring a foil Planeswalker card, 1 Core Set 2020 booster pack, 1 strategy insert, 1 Magic reference guide, 1 MTG Arena code card, 1 tuck box

—description from the publisher
A pack of 40 new cards for Monsdrawsity featuring monsters... from real life! Axolotls, tardigrades and more!

A 40 card pack that brings new monsters to expand your options in Monsdrawsity. Terrorsaurs are part dinosaur, part monster, all terror! Can you break through the fear to describe them to your friends?
A 40 card promo pack of new monsters for Monsdrawsity, illustrated by legendary artists.

Garden gnomes have a secret life. Unbeknownst to you, the gnomes are mining your yard for gems! And, all this time you thought you had a gopher problem!

Your goal in the game is to collect as many sets of gems as you can in this cute and fanciful mining game.

You'll start out by setting up a mess of cards in the center of the table, that will be made up of gems and mushrooms. These cards will act as the "mine".

Each player will then take turns building tunnels through the mine collecting gems and mushrooms. Gems can be sold through-out the game to get more tunnels and other tools. Mushrooms can only be used at the end of the game as to expand the size of gem sets that you can score.

Sets must be made of all the same type of gem/mushroom, or all different ones. The bigger the set you collect, the more the set is worth.

The game ends once any Gnome has 50 money, or any Gnome is out of tunnel segments they can build. The Gnome that has the most money at the end of the game is the winner!

—description from the designer

boardgame: MonsDRAWsity: Bring Your Kid To Work Deck
description: Bring Your Kid to Work makes drawing more difficult as artists try to interview the witness while also keeping an eye on their kid who is shadowing them during bring your kid to work day. Why is your marker suddenly sticky?! With kids around, who knows? I guess you'll just have to draw while holding it with only the tips of your fingers for the next round.

boardgame: MonsDRAWsity: Shut Up & Start Drawing + Technically Difficult
description: Technically Difficult makes being the witness more difficult as they have to memorize each creature under strange circumstances. It's already tricky to memorize a creature in 20 seconds, but what if that creatures is hanging you upside down at the same time? What if you have to talk to the D.R.A.W. agents while barely moving a muscle because you're still thawing out from the monster's freeze ray?

boardgame: MonsDRAWsity: Back For More Refresh Pack
description: A promo pack containing the monsters designed by the people who pledged at the "Back For More" tier in the Monsdrawsity: Unusual Suspects kickstarter campaign.

1 - 4 players

Enjoy both expansions on the go!

60 animal tokens (punchboard)
12 traveler tokens (wood)
21 campfire tokens (wood)

- Fits in the clamshell case with all 1-4p cards. Compatible with the Travel, Super Travel, and Deluxe Editions. Components are not waterproof.

- Includes both Animal Expansion and Adventurer Expansion. Physical rulebooks and Solo Achievement sheet not included, QR code and link to rules and sheet provided. Key rules summary card also provided.

Note that the Animal Expansion and Adventurer Expansion are already contained in the Standard and Deluxe Editions with enough components for 1-8 players. This expansion is considered "Travel-Friendly" because it replaces those components with smaller components, and only enough for 1-4 players, so they will fit in the clamshell container. The clamshell container is part of the Travel, Super Travel and, Deluxe Editions but not the Standard Edition.

Bar Fight - The Spirits Battle is a two player game is simple, strategic and drinkable! There will be only 4 actions: Move, Attack, Place and Ability. Within these actions, players can choose to use their wisdom and strategies to defeat their opponent. Or, they can take as many shots as they want to stop their opponents to win the game.  Anyhow, once players are into this match, it is hard to walk out sober. P.S. This game is definitely playable without alcohol!


    5 minutes quick match
    Everyone knows the rule, but they never play it before
    Casually but strategical
    Shot holder miniatures
    A new drinking game

Greenrock Village: A small town never rests.

After the series of murders was finally cleared up in the local municipal theater, it seemed as if the community would be able to go about their daily business without worries. But that shouldn't last long, because a new horrific series of murders interrupts the short period of calm. Who murdered the owner? Was it Mrs. Kunz? Or Mr. Müller? Someone is up to mischief in the allotment gardens. In each of the five gardens there is an object that could have served as a murder weapon. Owners and visitors visit these gardens at different times. But one of them thinks less about gardening or relaxing...

Who will expose the killer? This game for one person with a total of 50 murder cases requires criminalistic skills and a good nose, because the 3 different levels of difficulty are a tricky task for amateur detectives. Who accepts the challenge so that the murders in the allotment gardens finally come to an end?

Match, puzzle and find the killer. Choose one of the 50 cases (these are marked in three levels of difficulty) and use the clues on the card to put the pieces in the right order. So you end up with an accurate picture of who the killer is. This game is just right for all hobby detectives, brain joggers, riddles and riddle friends and can also be taken with you on trips thanks to the practical packaging.
family: Admin: Outside the Scope of BGG

boardgame: Island Alone: The Secret of Storm Island
description:
The shipwreck that brought you to this island is not the end of your troubles. The storms that hit this area are not natural. To survive, you must first solve the mystery of this island...

The Secret of Storm Island is the first story campaign and expansion for Island Alone. To succeed, players throughout the game must complete the tasks on the campaign cards before the final storm occurs.

This expansion includes a new map for "Storm Island" consisting of 6 parts, new character cards, a goals card, and 9 new campaign objective cards.

The expansion follows classic gameplay but introduces a few changes and new rules. Instead of one scenario, players complete the objectives from the campaign cards starting with card #1. Game length is determined by the storm time designated on the objective card.

boardgame: Island Alone: The Search for Captain Smith
description:
You have come to the island in search of revenge against Captain Smith. You believed he died many years ago, but recently evidence surfaced that he may still be alive and hiding here....

The Search for Captain Smith is the second story campaign and expansion for Island Alone. To succeed, players throughout the game must complete the tasks on the campaign cards before the all supplies of food and water run out.

This expansion includes a new map for "The Search for Captain Smith" consisting of 6 parts, new character cards, a goals card, and 9 new campaign objective cards.

The expansion follows classic gameplay but introduces a few changes and new rules. Instead of one scenario, players complete the objectives from the campaign cards starting with card #1.  Players will encounter outposts guarded by rebels and will need to resolve combat to survive.

boardgame: Chordsway: (NOT) A Music Thoery Card Game
description:
Chordsway is a fast-paced and simply card game for everyone. Despite designed based on music theory, it DOES NOT require musical knowledge. The goal is to match the cards to make chords and finish all the cards in hand.

—description from the publisher

In Flick Maze, the first fully 3D-printed flicking board game, 1-4 players will flick their discs as quickly and skillfully as possible to score goals through a maze, which can be different each time you play. You can play Flick Maze with 25 different custom tracks or design your own tracks. The game includes five modes of play.

Can you outflick your opponents? Can you be the quickest flicker in the universe?

They are already here! And they are sooooo hungry.
These thug monsters eat everything, and valuable objects get stuck between their teeth.
Prove to be the fastest following the instructions of your team but...
blindfolded!

Form two teams.
Deal each one a monster card and a monster mask.

Turn over a face card.

Each team will have to place the item tokens on their monster card in the correct place.
The first team to do so will have to roar like a monster and will have earned a victory point.

The first team to get 10 points wins the game.

-description from publisher

A tip-off sends the guards of the world's rarest works of art on the hunt for the most audacious picture thief. What they did not expect is that thief ​​​​is one of them!

A game of hidden roles where finding the culprit and going unnoticed becomes increasingly difficult. The thief will make up lies, divert attention, and even make you look guilty to get his way.

The goal is to become the person with the most victory points at the end of the 8 game rounds.

To do this, the participants must connect their minds by selecting the same card when they are security guards and trick their rivals when they are the thief.

-description from publisher
This is a series of Dutch trivia games, with 330 questions in 6 categories printed on 55 cards per pack. As of November 2022 there are 16 packs available!

boardgame: Photophantom: The Young Detective and the Shy Ghost
description:
Tommy is a young detective who specialized himself in very peculiar cases.

Despite his age, he is not afraid to explore old abandoned castles, armed only with a camera, looking for scary ghosts. However, Tommy is looking for a very shy ghost, which tries to deceive the young detective with tricks.

Will you help him?

Each round, a trick card is revealed. To score the card, players have to solve the trick. They compare the revealed card’s element with the upmost one on the trick card pile. The fastest player wins.

—description from the publisher

Fragments is a game where you create stories of which you are the heroes.
Immerse yourself in the world of the OUTSPHERE saga from Guy-Roger Duvert.
Draw from your imagination, discover the secrets of Eden, the planet that should host humanity.

The ship went through space in dead silence. It went, smoothly, through skies that no human had explored before. Its hull, of a metallised brown, was in a perfect state. 
The passageways were soaked in a cold and freezing silence. Suddenly, the metal and glass cylinders started shaking and the powerful lights installed on the ceilings, turned on. You emerge from your cryo-sleep as the Arche stops in front of Eden 
Embody the characters from the OUTSPHERE saga and live an adventure full of twists through 5 secret envelopes to unlock.
Each turn, pick a Word card and tell a fragment of the story.
The Symbol-Icon match will change your adventure in a good or a bad way.
Your mission: create OUTSPHERE and save Humanity.

—description from the publisher

Dwar7s Legendary Forest is a compilation of some of the most hard to find Dwar7s expansions AND the much anticipated Snow White miniature!

Dwar7s Legendary Forest includes the Dragon's Forge, Legendary, Enchanted Forest, and Elven Uprising expansions.

The goal is to have 6 big boxes to complete the Dwar7s World Boardgame Collection: Dwar7s Fall, Dwar7s Winter, Dwar7s Spring, Dwar7s The Lost Tribes, Dwar7s Legendary Forest and Dwar7s Summer!

—description from the publisher

In this robot anime-inspired deckbuilding card game for 2-4 players, you can pick between 6 different pilots and robots as the game progresses - 36 possibilities in all. And in true robot anime fashion, each pilot and robot gets to Awaken and unleash its true abilities when things look bleakest, creating truly memorable moments of hot-blooded action. Only you and your friends can defeat the encroaching hordes and thwart the plans of Nabla's Heart.

Two years ago, Atlantis, conquerors of the ancient world, rose from the depths of the Pacific. As the world stood paralyzed in the face of their onslaught, seven girls stepped forward to oppose it. They possessed the Royal Wavelength necessary to activate the humanoid weapons known as Resonants—the only means of stopping Atlantis. Caught in a maelstrom of conflict, they battled against the hordes, eventually slaying Ennosigaios, the Lord of Atlantis.

Now, peace slowly returns as the world licks its wounds and rebuilds. The girls enjoy a return to their normal lives… while feeling ever—so—slightly like something’s missing.

Through four phases of their turn, players purchase cards, add attachments to their mechs, play tactics and then enter battle! After the discard phase, play moves on. Defeat monsters to gain energy and gear up to battle stronger enemies together. First player to reach 20 energy announces the end of the game is coming - or reach 30 energy to win the game immediately!

-description from publisher

The aim of the game is to get dressed in the colors of the rainbow before the sun come out. The game can be played by 2–4 children either cooperative or competitive.

At each turn the player rolls a dice and moves his figure as indicated by the dice 1 to 3 fields to the next unoccupied square. Depending on the landing field the player usually takes a wooden stick (representing a dress) in the specified color. Alternatively the turn is passed to the next player or - after passing on a special square for the fourth time - the game ends.

When a player finishes dressing their character by all 6 colors, they can either continue to play to help their friends or win immediately.

This print and play was inspired by the realization that my wife and I loved Patchwork but had pretty much figured the game out.

The variations are carefully built to not make the game mean or random- it stays a friendly competition. The game also stays full information from the get go (no dice or cards or luck), so you can puzzle as hard as you want while playing the game.

All you need to do is print the PDF under the "file" tab that has the rule and pattern variations. One player picks the pattern, while the other player picks the rule change. The page even folds up nicely to fit in the Patchwork box. Enjoy!

—description from the designer

Use tiny paws and claws to complete challenges in this party game. Compete one-on-one or in teams. Draw challenge cards and complete wacky challenges with your tiny paws and claws to progress on the board.

Core Connection is a deck-building card game for 2-4 players in which you take on the role of a pilot of a Resonant, a giant mecha, fighting to free society from the tyrannical reign of its oppressors!

UNRAVEL THE TRUTH HIDDEN IN JADE 
Delve into 3 different replayable scenarios depicting the story of Core Connection Nabla Conspiracy!

All 3 scenarios feature new cards and rules which turn everything you thought you knew about the game upside down. Are you ready to unravel the truth hidden in the shards of jade?


    This game is an expansion. Core Connection Nabla Conspiracy is necessary to play this game.


-description from publisher

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Core Set 2020 Planeswalker Deck: Mu Yanling, Celestial Wind
description:
Planeswalker Decks familiarize players who are interested in Magic with basic strategy, plus the games setting, characters, and mechanics of the latest set. Each deck comes with a foil Planeswalker card not found anywhere else.

Mu Yanling relies on her quick wits and elemental magic to travel safely through strange, dangerous worlds. Disrupt your opponents with spells as your creatures fly over their defenses.

Contents: 1 ready-to-play 60-card deck featuring a foil Planeswalker card, 1 Core Set 2020 booster pack, 1 strategy insert, 1 Magic reference guide, 1 MTG Arena code card, 1 tuck box

—description from publisher

Collect dice, capture monsters, and sabotage your friends in a race to capture a Mythic Monster!

Long ago, Mythic Monsters roamed the land, bringing awe and wonder to anyone lucky enough to catch a glimpse of them. Last Thursday they all went missing.

Dice Monsters is a fast, light strategy game for 3 to 6 players that takes just a few minutes to learn, and 30-40 minutes to play.

With fast setup, simultaneous actions, and no end of game score-tallying, games move quickly.

Each round, every player will pick a monster to try to capture, or choose to place a wager, risking dice for juicy payoffs. Choose and use your monsters wisely, each monster comes with a special ability that could improve future rolls, provide bigger dice bonuses with greater risk, steal dice from other players, or even sabotage their dice rolls.

Upgrade your dice to tilt the odds in your favor. Finding the right combination of monsters and dice upgrades is key to being the first player to capture their own Mythic Monster.

Options: players consistently have a variety of meaningful and interesting decisions to make
Action: fast setup, simultaneous player actions, and no player elimination minimize time spent waiting
Crescendo finish: games finish with a roar and a laugh, not with an accounting exercise
Focused interaction: meaningful player interaction without information overload

—description from designer

A kids' card game that can be used with younger children to start them on the road to more card games.

The classic schoolyard game is now a fast-action, quick-to-learn card game. Just follow the pattern: Red Light – Green Light – 1 – 2 – 3. Play as many cards on your turn as you can in that order. Can't play a card? Draw one from the deck and play passes to the next driver. Play all your cards first to dodge traffic and win the game!

Consists of a 105 card deck, with 21 copies of each of the 5 cards.

- description from the publisher's website

Napoleon in Egypt is a two-player, card-driven, operational wargame that allows you to recreate the French campaign in Egypt and Syria, from the landing of the French troops to the surrender of General Menou on August 31st, 1801. The game draws inspiration from other top-selling CDGs like Here I Stand and Twilight Struggle. A good and fast-playing introductory wargame thanks to its tight ruleset with few exceptions, the game will also please the veteran wargamers with 100 cards dripping with flavor, challenging players with the hand management every CDG fan has come to love!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
This is a set collection game in the style of "Old Maid." All cards are dealt out to players and each sets aside complete pairs. After that, the dealer fans their remaining cards and offers them to the player on their left for a blind choice. The next player attempts to select a card to make another pair, and then the process repeats.

The game ends when all pairs have been completed, the winner having rid themselves of a full hand. The loser of the game is left with a single, unpairable "Wolf" card.

Consists of 35 cards.

This game was released by Noble and Noble Co. as part of their "Games My Children Love Best" series.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

boardgame: The Game of The Old Woman and Her Pig
description:
This is essentially a card-based game of Dominoes, using cards instead of plastic or wooden tokens. It is themed around the folk tale of the Old Woman and Her Pig, where an old woman goes to successive figures and encourages each one to do something to the previous one, creating a domino effect that will get her pig over a stile: the butcher killing the ox that drinks the water that quenches the fire that burns the stick...and so on.

Most of the 60 cards have a number of dots on one half (like dominoes) and a picture of a character on the other. Players can match numbers or pictures. Pictures are considered "doubles" and must be placed at right angles to the match.

The game ends when one player runs out of cards in their hand; that person then scores based on cards remaining in their opponents' hands. The game is repeated until someone reaches 100 and becomes the overall winner.

This game was released by Noble and Noble Co. as part of their "Games My Children Love Best" series. It is functionally 'identical' to "House that Jack Built," which was released simultaneously as part of the same series, albeit with different pictures on the cards.

boardgame: The Game of the Three Bears
description:
This is a very simple children's game, based on "Snap," in which players are dealt cards with characters and situations on them based on the well-known story of Goldilocks and the Three Bears.

The full deck of 32 cards is dealt at the start of the game, and players take turns revealing the top card of their stacks. When a match is revealed, the first of the two matching players to shout "Boo!" takes control of the other's revealed cards up to that point.

The game ends when one player succeeds in taking all the cards.

This game was released by Noble and Noble Co. as part of their "Games My Children Love Best" series.

—user summary
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Goldilocks and the Three Bears

boardgame: Warhammer Underworlds: Nethermaze – Gorechosen of Dromm
description:
Dromm, Wounder of Worlds, lord amongst the Slaughterpriests of Khorne, seeks to commit the ultimate act of violence in the name of his murderous god. He leads his group of Gorechosen to the very nadir of the Nethermaze, where he plans to enact a grand blood ritual upon tectonic weak points to wound Ulgu itself, sending black, liquid lava spilling into the seas above, and thereby poisoning them beyond recovery. His companions are the Skullgrinder Herax, a creator of grim and bloody artefacts, and the muscle-bound Gorehulk, an ex-Slaughterpriest who is now more akin to a beast than man. Nothing will stop Dromm in his quest to bleed Ulgu dry.

The Gorechosen of Dromm offer a ritualistic approach to the violent worship of Khorne. The warband claims blood tithe tokens by dealing and suffering damage throughout the battle, then expends them to fuel powerful abilities – such as Dromm's unique Enrage action, which can send friend and foe charging across the Nethermaze in a frenzy. This box includes a complete Rivals deck, plus loads of extra cards allowing you to try out a few tricks, as well as universal cards and Grand Alliance cards to use with your other warbands.

This set includes:
- 3x push-fit Gorechosen of Dromm miniatures – no glue required to assemble, cast in bloody red plastic to stand out even when unpainted.
- 3x double-sided fighter cards, one for each warrior in the warband – Skullgrinder Herax, the Gorehulk, and Dromm, Wounder of Worlds.
- A further 66 cards to enhance your fighters and provide new tactics and challenges for your games of Warhammer Underworlds: Nethermaze, including:
– 12x Gorechosen of Dromm objective cards
– 10x Gorechosen of Dromm upgrade cards
– 10x Gorechosen of Dromm gambit cards (includes 3x Domain card)
– 6x Universal objective cards, usable by any warband
– 7x Universal upgrade cards, usable by any warband (includes 1x Map card and 1x Domain card)
– 7x Universal gambit cards, usable by any warband (includes 2x Domain cards)
– 12x Grand Alliance cards, usable by any warband belonging to the specified Grand Alliance
– 1x Gorechosen of Dromm Warband Background / Rivals Deck Card
– 1x cover card

In Hole In The Wall, each player leads a gang of outlaws, each aiming to get their hands on as much money as possible.

At the beginning of the game, each player is dealt four outlaws from the Outlaw deck. These represent his gang, and determine his gang's capabilities.

Every turn, a player can perform one of five actions:

     Go “Along The Trail” by drawing a card from the "Along the Trail" deck, and see what happens (usually little risk and little gain).
     Go “Into Town” and try and pull a bank job by drawing a card from the "Town" deck (usually large risk and large gain).
     Go to “Hole In The Wall” and attempt to recruit another outlaw into the gang.
     Attempt a “Jailbreak” to attempt to free an outlaw lost in a previous turn.
     “Call Out” another outlaw for a gunfight. The fight is resolved by rolling the dice.


There are three ways in which the game can be played. This determines when the game ends.

     Fight to the Finish: Game ends if all outlaws are killed, the player with the most money wins.
     Time Limit Game: Game ends at predetermined time, player with most money wins.
     Wealth Game: Game ends when first player reaches $50,000
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Union Pacific Express
description:
A small map expansion with express rules to streamline the phases and shorten the game length.

Published in Spring 2023 issue of TRAXX Mainline magazine.

This expansion focuses on the Union Pacific railroad network in Nebraska, Kansas, Wyoming, and Colorado and features a special use of Bailey Yard (the largest rail yard in the world) to transfer goods cubes when delivering through this area.

-description from designer

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Everest / Zimbabwe
description:
This is a two map release for Age of Steam 2022. Maps are printed on high quality poster stock front and back.

Age of Steam: Everest features a race of climbers to reach the summit of Everest and return to base camp first. Climbers have to deal with additional effort to climb the Death Zone and will use the oxygen and resupply special actions to gain on edge on the competitors.

Age of Steam: Zimbabwe will test players ability to overcome difficult events related to the real-world financial crises that have affected the Zimbabwe railways. This is a very tough map where bad things happen.

-description from designer

Hidden well beneath the great mountains, there exists a gloomy and gargantuan cavern inhabited by the dark civilizations of the world. Goblins, Dark Elves, and Dark Dwarves build their armies and thrive in the empty spaces between the stone. Your mine will dig deep and reach the fabled Underdark. Will you navigate the dangers of the Underdark and survive in the dark place beneath the mountain? Or will the dangerous creatures spell your doom. Play to find out, as your mine tells its story!

Underdark is an expansion to Dwarf Mine (the Dwarf Mine base game is required to play).

Like Dwarf Mine, Underdark is a game played by drawing a mine on graph paper. On your turn, you will first draw (dig) rooms, hallways, and ladders for your mine! Rooms will benefit your mine, and hallways and ladders let you reach other areas of your mountain.

After drawing, you will make two dice rolls - a treasure roll, and a combat roll. These rolls determine what treasure you find, and what creatures you may encounter in the mountain - after all, mining is dangerous business!

Each mine made has a story, and every story has a beginning, middle, and sadly, an end. Your game ends when your population reaches 0. By the end of the game, you’ll have created rich and unique history for your mine – no two games are the same!

Players are getting VP during the game and trying to see how many VP they can get each time they play. Kind of like a high score.

-description from designer

boardgame: Hellboy: The Board Game – Drinking With Skeletons
description:
The Drinking With Skeletons resin diorama features a removable Hellboy figure that you can use in your adventures. Also includes a brand new Deck Of Doom card that's specific to Hellboy.

Resin model supplied unpainted and unbuilt.

Includes:
- 1 x unpainted & unassembled resin diorama with detachable Hellboy miniature
- 1 x Deck Of Doom card (Drinking with Skeletons)

boardgame: Hellboy: The Board Game – Big Box Of Doom
description:
The Big Box Of Doom contains all the expansions and stretch goals from the 2021 Kickstarter's 'Pledge of Doom'.

INCLUDES:

    The Storm and the Fury expansion
    Hell on Earth expansion
    Pandemonium expansion
    End of Days expansion
    Hellboy: The Dice Game
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In broad daylight, an armed gang had stolen five valuable paintings from the art museum. The police are under enormous pressure to track down the thieves as quickly as possible and secure the loot worth millions!

In the competitive dice and card game Großstadtdjungel, players take on the roles of detectives who are investigating various locations around the city. In doing so, they not only have the common objective of the investigation in mind, but also their own personal advantage. Because at the end of the day, fame and honor await the cleverest investigator in the urban jungle.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Theme: Fine Art and Art Museums

boardgame: Die geheimnisvolle Hütte in den Bergen
description:
You have come across a seemingly uninhabited mountain hut in the Alps — and stepped into the middle of a mysterious criminal case.

Die geheimnisvolle Hütte in den Bergen is played in a team of 3-7 people, one of whom takes on the role of the game master. The game master tells the crime story and guides the other players step by step through the adventure and the puzzles using the game master's booklet. They know the solution to the current puzzle and can decide whether to give the players hints or not. It's even possible to switch the game master from puzzle to puzzle, giving everyone a chance to solve puzzles as the game progresses.

Many of the puzzles are designed to spread information evenly among players. To solve these puzzles, the co-operation of all players is mandatory. The game ends when the players have reached the exit of the mountain hut. The time elapsed up to that point and any penalty minutes result in the final score.

In the animal world, canned food is an unprecedented boom! In Can Can Coming!, you become the president of a canned food manufacturer and aim to announce an attractive product line-up with the help of animals. However, the co-operation of other companies is essential for product development. Control the market by announcing collaborative products that benefit each other (or only you), gain popularity, and aim to be company of the year.

During the game, you play cards between players, with those players sharing the benefits of a card. Some cards activate various effects when put in play; others bonuses when calculating scores.

Crows & Crawdads is a small, non-essential pack of 23 cards that provide a touch of variety to Hildegard, with 11 new illustrations by Diego Frias.

It adds additional story cards for the game with items, encounters and endings.

-description from designer

In Queue 4, you must go through a standard deck of cards by playing the top card into one of four ordered Queues. However, you don't get to see the card before you play it, and placing a card in the wrong position discards the Queue, bringing the end of the game ever closer. In order to win and score big, you must play the odds, take calculated risks, and press your luck.

The game also comes with three Variants: The Royal Ball, where each face card has a special effect when played; Bouquets, where the player must strategically place and move cards in order to create one long Queue with alternating colors; and a Campaign mode, where several games are chained together.

—description from the designer

A man's body was found beaten to death in an open-air bath.
The suspects were novelists, editors, hot spring writers, and guests at hot spring inns.
"Five dancers from a nearby theater who happened to visit the inn, and a wet towel dealer who came and went."
While they were both suspicious, a pistol with a silencer was found in the victim's dressing basket.
Things are getting more and more chaotic...

boardgame: Among Cultists: Down into the Abyss
description: 1st expansion for Among Cultists. Enter a forgotten laboratory on 5 islands and try to avoid  Tentacles of an slumbering evil. This map indroduces new mechanics: boats & tentacles, also new hidden roles to evolve your gameplay!

Generations ago, these mines were sealed. All miners who worked here were taken by madness and no one should ever again dig so deep for valuable minerals. To make sure that no one ever sees this place again, each new generation attaches new types of locks of their time to the entrance shaft. Locks, seals, runes, traps. Everything to lock the chaos... in the mountains of chaos.

The Mountains of Chaos Expansion brings a completely new and interactive map to your Among Cultists experience. Thanks to new mechanics like revolving doors & elevators, exciting elements are added, plus new secret roles await you

Fantasy Animals In Links

A quick 2 player card game where players take turn choosing animals to make a better link of animals than the other player.

Each card have an animal depicted and half symbols on each of the left and right border of it. Matching symbols earns you points while failing to do so do not.

Every time you chose a card it must be placed directly to the right or left of your already chosen cards.

The setup of cards alternate between cards faced down and cards faced up, giving the players some but not full information on what lies ahead.

—description from the designer

Starlit Gate is laughing again, is it time to tempt your fate?

Starlit Gate is a hybrid adventure/skirmish game for 1 to 6 players. Explore post apocalyptic science fantasy of a City frozen in time, in multiple coop and versus game modes. Investigate Rumors and face Perils while evading (or fighting) Voidstruck guardians of the time loop. Collect wondrous artifact from the time before the Storms and unravel the mysteries of the time loop.

In versus mode, recruit your team, bend the threads of fate to your will and fight for resources and survival against one or more of your friends in a one shot or campaign game (that can be finished in an evening).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Κυνήγι ερωτήσεων: Γράμματα-Τέχνες - Συμπλήρωμα (Quiz Hunt: Arts and Letters – Supplement)
description: 384 supplementary questions to go alongside the base game

Bigtop Rumble it’s an engine building game where 2-5 players compete to create the best show and attract the larger audience. Each player can select one of the 5 real contemporary circuses companies and create a show with card depicting real artists!

Build your own show and try to attract the largest amount of public but, be aware:

Not everyone among the audience is armed with good intentions!

The game is played over 5 turns, each player choses one of the 5 real contemporary circus BigTops. With their own special rules.

Each Turn in clockwise order each player buys up to two cards from the main board, cards are divided in artists cards and tools cards.

—description from the publisher

This family board game helps teach you fun and interesting facts about the planet, the animals and wildlife that inhabit it. A special website provides beautiful videos and trivia questions.

The aim of the game is to answer questions, collect animal cards and win by being the leading player after the final trivia round.

The game also includes photo cards to enable play without an internet connection.

All footage is supplied by the BBC, featuring iconic moments across many of their award winning BBC Earth series and titles.

You have a frog, a birch, and something that looks like a mountain. Try assembling those into the Eiffel Tower. Or a New Year’s Eve. Or Charlie Chaplin! Guide your opponents with your meaningful collage to the secret word and guess the other players’ masterpieces. What could this yellow and blue-striped nonsense mean?

Cluellage is a game about guessing words through collages.


     Every player gets a screen, dice, 6 tokens numbered 1 to 6 and 5 cards with abstract pictures.
     They roll the dice behind the screen to know the word that they will show.
     Players put the top card from their stack in the middle of the table and simultaneously try to select the best one for them.
     They try to explain a given word using 5 cards they got in the previous phase.
     You receive 1 point for each correct guess and 1 point for everyone who guessed you correctly.


After 3 rounds the player with the most points wins.

boardgame: B17 Twenty Five Missions: Pack I
description:
This pack contains 15 additional missions (31-45) that can be played seamlessly with the B 17 Twenty five mission base game.

—description from the designer

In Rune Rite, your goal is to summon the most mystical energy while performing your ritual! You do this by reading Runes to generate powerful Glyphs and by etching those Glyphs into your Altar.

Rune Rite is a flip-and-write build-your-own polyomino game in which you use playing cards to produce shapes that you’ll draw on your player sheet. To win, you’ll need to score the most points by arranging non-matching Runes in specified areas, and by arranging matching Runes in large clusters.

description from the designer

Build your own Koi carp collection by making sets of differently colored fish cards.
First player reveals a number of cards, then he must divide the cards into sets. The player on their left chooses one of the sets of cards.
Players collect different types of fish, each type has their individual points value. The person, who gathers the most fish of one type receives points for the set. Be careful! You can collect Ghost Koi carp cards, which can bring you negative points.
The player with the most points wins.

Players take roles of train company managers.
Every round of this game is divided into two phases. The fist phase is planning. Players plan their moves by playing move cards. Next in the action phase, players perform actions (load cargo, deliver cargo, upgrade rolling stock.
Players earn points for fulfiling contracts. The more cargo you transport, the higher the score.

A fusion of trick-taking and racing games!

A racehorse who loves junk food. Use food cards to make them run!　
The horse with the most favorite food will be ranked higher! Predict 2 of the top 3
finishers. Speed ​​up for the next race! I have a feeling that the final race will be rough...? !

"EXACTA!" is a new genre: trick racing game that summarizes only the interesting parts of horse racing into 56 cards.

At the beginning of each race, each player will choose one food card from their hand and place it face down in front of them which will serve as a prediction for the race.

Each race consists of 7 tricks. At the end of each trick, sum up the values of each food type. The food type with the highest value wins the trick and the corresponding horse will move up 1 spot if not hungry or 2 spots if hungry. On the 7th trick, the corresponding horse always moves up 2 spots. The remaining card in each player's hand serves as a second prediction for the race.

If both predictions have matching types, the player scores 0. If both predictions are in the top 3 finishers, multiply the card values to get the score. If the first prediction wins the race and the second prediction gets second place, then multiply the score by 5. Double the score for each hungry horse in the prediction.

Famous New York celebrity magazine is looking for a new paparazzo. You have to be fast to take photos of celebrities before others do it.

Players take roles of paparazzo photographers. Their task is to take photos of famous celebrities in everyday situations (in a supermarket, at the swimming pool etc.)
Players reveal the card from the celebrity deck. Each card shows a celebrity and the location where he is spotted. The task is to show the card with the time, depending on what time the celebrity was spotted. The goal of the game is to collect six different celebrity photo cards.

Paparazzi has another end game trigger, as well as some game mechanics tweaks and minor rule changes from Chameleon Time.

The war in the shadows escalates! Amidst ruined cityscapes, carnivorous jungles and hidden strongholds, new kill teams join the fray. From the gruesome Gellerpox Infected to the noble Elucidian Starstriders, each band of warriors brings their own method of war to every desperate battle – in struggles that seem more varied and deadly than ever. Expert assassins and saboteurs slip through patrols of enemy sentries, hoping to complete their bloody tasks and slip away before they are detected, while bands of grizzled warriors faced with impossible odds choose to make their last stand, taking as many of the foe as they can with them to the grave.

Kill Team: Annual 2022 offers new ways to enjoy the sci-fi skirmish game you know and love, with fresh missions and game modes, kill teams collected from the pages of White Dwarf like the Harlequin Void-dancer Troupes and the Hunter Clades of the Adeptus Mechanicus, and two classic kill teams returning to the game for the first time in its new edition. It's the perfect way to breathe new life into your games.

Inside this 152-page softcover book, you’ll find:

- Full rules and background information for six kill teams, including datacards, Strategic and Tactical Ploys, equipment, and complete Spec Ops rules:
– Elucidian Starstriders, the avaricious crew and coterie of Rogue Trader Elucia Vhane
– Gellerpox Infected, festering fanatics of Nurgle's warpborne sickness, led by Vulgrar Thrice-cursed
– Hunter Clade, disciplined cybernetic Skitarii who stalk the killzone for the Adeptus Mechanicus
– Warpcoven, mighty Sorcerers of the Thousand Sons and their gathered thralls, both mutant and Rubric
– Wyrmblade, cunning Neophytes of the Genestealer Cults, guided by elite cult agents
– Void-dancer Troupes, agile Aeldari Harlequins for whom every battle is a performance

- Three mission packs, each offering new ways to play in a total of 15 missions:
– 6 Sentries missions allow you to sneak closer to enemy lines, or guard against infiltrators
– 6 Multiplayer missions open up battle royale games featuring three or even four players
– 3 Last Stand missions make the perfect capstone to a hard-fought narrative campaign

- A stunning collection of model photography, hobby inspiration, and artwork featuring kill teams and their operatives

Legendary Tea Farmer is a game for 2 to 5 players who each represent a different tea estate. To run a successful tea business, farmers firstly have to rehabilitate abandoned tea gardens, then grow and produce good quality tea, find a market to sell it to and also give back to the local community. As you are newbies in the tea world, then Badass Bidjo is there to mentor you. While each farmer can produce tea, only the one who manages to hire Badass Bidjo, will produce tea with enough good quality to earn a reputation on the tea market and become a legendary tea farmer. Badass Bidjo is a busy man though and tends to only one farmer per season...

Do you have what it takes to be the next Legendary Tea Farmer? With all the twists and turns that life throws at you and competition breathing on your neck, can you make tea that people would love and at the same time run an economically viable farm? Be it a cow eating your harvest, bad weather conditions affecting the harvest or an unexpected free day due to a wedding in the village, you need to find a way to adapt and come out of it as the best tea farmer in the region.

This game was created by actual tea farmers, so the game includes real-life adventures, surprises, failures and successes from the life of tea farmers.

—description from the publisher

The [Pokémon GO] TCG expansion draws its inspiration from Pokémon GO with artwork that captures Pokémon in a real-world setting.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Sword & Shield – Lost Origin
description:
The Lost Zone returns to the Pokémon TCG in the Sword & Shield—Lost Origin expansion. Giratina ripped apart the very fabric of space to create a rift into the Lost Zone. Many cards, including Giratina V and Giratina VSTAR, will take advantage of this exciting mechanic. While cards placed in the Lost Zone are out of play for the rest of the games, strategic players can make the most of this new opportunity.

—description from the publisher

After years of waiting, you are finally getting the chance to return to your beloved island. You want to see it all, but can you craft the perfect itinerary for your return to Cuba?

In this drafting game for 2 players, create the perfect itinerary by adding cards to your schedule tableau. Each game, one scoring card is used to determine how your tableau scores.

The illustrations in Cuba Mia were done by Priscilla Benitez, whose family immigrated from Cuba in the 1960's.

—description from the designer

The kickstarter exclusive promos contain two new brides, Demetra and Cesarina, and one new Common Character, Giovanni.

Also included is a magnet and a postcard signed by the authors.

These promos came in a plastic bag taped to the outside of the box of the base game.

boardgame: Dune: War for Arrakis –  The Spacing Guild
description:
The Spacing Guild expansion introduces a Space Board where Guild Heighliners will race through space to bring reinforcements to Arrakis, as well as one Heighliner Board for each faction, where players will stow the reinforcements they’re able to get from the Imperium forces and send hurtling across space to deploy at Arrakis.

Players are able to perform special Imperium Actions throughout the game, in order to coax either the Spacing Guild, CHOAM, or Landsraad to help them in their plight.

-description from the Kickstarter

Two players launch their spinners in a battle arena simultaneously.
Last spinner spinning wins the game.

boardgame: The Mother Road: Route 66 – Pink Cadillac Expansion
description: Pink Cadillac expansion which allows a 5th player (or substitutes for one of the other player colors)

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Core Set 2020 Planeswalker Deck: Ajani, Inspiring Leader
description:
Planeswalker Decks familiarize players who are interested in Magic with basic strategy, plus the games setting, characters, and mechanics of the latest set. Each deck comes with a foil Planeswalker card not found anywhere else.

Ajani is renowned for his courage and compassion in equal measures. Gain life faster than your opponents can take it away, then turn the tide as soon as they run out of gas.

Contents: 1 ready-to-play 60-card deck featuring a foil Planeswalker card, 1 Core Set 2020 booster pack, 1 strategy insert, 1 Magic reference guide, 1 MTG Arena code card, 1 tuck box

—description from publisher

The Battle at the Wkra River took place in August 1920 during the Polish-Soviet War (1919–20). It was an important part of the Battle of Warsaw, during which Polish forces managed to repulse the Soviet armies marching on Poland's capital. During the Battle at the Wkra River, Władysław Sikorski's Polish 5th Army fought against numerically superior Soviet forces (three Soviet armies); yet, they succeeded in halting the Soviet advance, forcing them to retreat. During this battle, Polish 5th Army tied the three Soviet armies, making it impossible for them to reinforce the Soviet assault on Warsaw. Thus, Poles foiled the Soviet plan to capture the capital of Poland and advance further to the west of Europe to spread communism.

The game consists of:


     map
     216 die-cut counters
     basic rule set (in Polish and English) – pdf version in Polish can be downloaded for free from the publisher's store
     scenario specific rules (in Polish and English)
     two scenarios of identical length but different victory conditions
     additional aids for players (combat tables, reinforcement tracks, etc.)
     one d10 dice


Additional info for players:


     no. of players: 2
     time: up to approx. 8 hrs
     age: over 12
     difficulty – easy
     playing solo is possible but all information concerning Polish and Soviet units will be available to the solo player (i.e. no fog of war, no bot system, etc.)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: In this expansion to Card Assassins, you’ll find an additional arsenal of over 250 Kill Words along with 6 new Character and Target cards. Merge the base game with this expansion to play with up to 12 people!

boardgame: Doctor Wars: The Hospital Card Game
description:
Doctor Wars: The Hospital Card Game is a card game version of Doctor Wars: The Hospital Strategy Game -- it has only cards; there are no boards, no rooms, no player pieces, and no spatial elements.

The object is to collect the most points by curing patients.

boardgame: G.I. JOE Deck-Building Game: Shadow of the Serpent Bonus Box #2
description:
A promo pack for G.I. Joe Deck-Building Game containing:

     2 Promo Character Cards (Solo Snake Eyes & Team Player Scarlett)
     Punchboard G.I. Joe Base Backdrop

A whole new ball game! Bounce the balls off the cards and into the barrel in order to avoid the deceptively difficult questions. For every questions that is answered incorrectly, the card is kept as a forfeit.

boardgame: WWE Heroclix: Mixed Match Challenge Starter Set
description:
HeroClix is a tactical combat miniatures game where you put heroes and villains from your favorite comics, movies, and TV shows in head-to-head combat. It utilizes the patented Combat Dial System originally found in the collectible Mage Knight miniatures game.

Now you can have your favorite WWE Superstars battling it out on your tabletop.

HeroClix miniatures use the combat dial to reflect a change in a characters' combat values, powers, and abilities over time. Some characters will pivot from long range to close quarters combat styles, others will move from acting aggressively to stealing their opponent's energy. The easiest thing to observe will be which characters are worn down by battle and which ones experience rage from getting hurt that helps them strike back at their foes! HeroClix uses gridded maps instead of a measure-and-move system, as well as die rolling.

Comes with Wrestling Ring and the following Superstars
- AJ Styles
- Charlotte Flair
- Finn Balor
- Sasha Banks

The Beach Combing Expansion is an expansion set for Aqua Garden with picking up items on the beach, or simply "Beach Combing Activities", as its theme.

Players place fishes and beachcombing tokens on the gallery board at the start of each round.
Players will gain not only the fishes, but also the beachcombing tokens that are placed in the same space.

Compete for set collection and majority with what you have picked up.

—description from the publisher

Players compete in a spicy contest as they try to play and collect chilli cards to make sets. Chilli cards are valued from 1 to 12, with the number representing the set's value, and also how many of that card are in the deck.

Players take turns and on your turn you MUST play a card from your hand (even if you really, really don't want to!). To end your turn, draw cards to refill your hand and then it is the next player's turn.

You'll either be playing chilli cards to make sets, or special cards to change things up at the table!

At the end of the game, the sets will be scored and players compare how many of each card they played. A player with outright majority scores points equal to the set's value. Points are split evenly in the event of a tie, but if you start a set and end up coming outright last you're in for some negative points!

The lower value chillies are easier to win, but the high value chillies offer greater rewards. How much are you willing to push your luck, and how stubborn will you be about claiming superiority in your sets?

Murders at Tealwoods Manor is a reverse murder mystery worker placement and drafting game.
You already know who the murderer is. You already know who the Investigator is. What you're trying to uncover is which guests become victims during the course of the night!
Draft different characters, and place them into different rooms of the manor to activate those room abilities. If the murderer is left alone with someone they will die!
Help the investigator discover the murderer by collecting clues, or help the murderer kill the investigator!
Score points by having your hidden goals of which characters live or die at the end of the game!

—description from the publisher

Orbital Decay is a 55 card expansion pack introducing new build-defining Pilot Abilities, Ultimate Techniques, and pivotal character developments that rock the world of Giga-Robo.

The expansion features the following:

21 new Pilot Abilities depicting each pilot in the next chapter of their story
6 new Ultimate Techniques
4 upgraded Ultimate Techniques
24 new Impact Cards

-description from designer

The game consists of the playing board, fifteen coloured pigeon cards and a large Nest Box card, which has a hole at its centre.  The object is to move the large Nest Box card across the board towards the coloured pigeon which matches an upturned pigeon card.  Movement is made one square at a time, trying to avoid squares containing two eyes.  If two eyes appear in the hole of the Nest Box, play passes to the player on your left.  The player who reaches the coloured pigeon retains the upturned card and the process is repeated by revealing another card.  The winner is the player who collects most cards.

In Zillertal you are a mountain photographer aiming to win contracts while travelling the beautiful villages of the Austrian Alps. Add to the ever-changing alpine landscape and complete tasks and contracts based on what can been seen on the landscape.

On your turn, play a landscape card from their hand onto the shared, constantly-changing mountain landscape in the centre. Then try and complete one or more tasks from the contracts cards in your hand, based on what can be seen across the whole landscape. So if there are exactly 8 cows, you can complete a corresponding contract task.

You can earn bonus abilities to give you new ways to complete contract tasks - add or hide features from the landscape, split the landscape with a gorge or add summit crosses to find new ways to score. The winner is the player who scores the most points from completed contracts once there are no more landscape cards to play.

-description from designer

Three card promo pack from Looney Labs that can be used with both Chrononauts and Early American Chononauts.

This trio of Chrononauts cards can be added to either version of the game. Each represents a sarsen stone that was removed from Stonehenge during the Dark Ages and lost to history. Each is slightly different; collect all 3 and you can recreate a trilithon. Doing so is a bonus Mission that becomes an alternate way to win. But you can also discard a Megalith in conjunction with Sell an Artifact for a whopping 3 card gain.

boardgame: Host Your Own Murder Mystery: On The Dance Floor
description:
Dangerous deeds have been taking place at Boogieland 77, the glitziest club in New York. Can your guests work out whodunnit? You’ll find all you need to throw a ‘killer’ 1970s murder mystery dinner party from the comfort of your home…3 alternative endings for multiple game nights!

The characters are an equal mix of genders; four male, four female and four which can be either gender.
There is a specific character for the host to play; Sammy C Quinn (the host is not a suspect but still a participant!) and the victim is not one of your guests - the instruction booklet provides more detail on this.

With 3 alternative endings you can reuse this kit for multiple nights of murder mystery fun. A great excuse to get your friends and family together and have some dress up fun! Also playable via video call or Zoom by mailing the components in advance or adapting the usual game play.

5 to 12 players and recommended for ages 16+.

Contents includes:
An instruction booklet, welcome speech for the host, 12 place cards, 12 name cards, 12 trait cards, 12 occupation cards, 12 murder weapon props, 2 police crime files, 28 murder clues, 48 accusation forms, 6 solutions and 3 award trophies

Frozen II Snowflake Journey is an exciting game where Elsa journeys far beyond the gates of Arendelle in search of answers … and you. It’s up to you to spin the spinner, collect colorful snowflakes and create a trail of 7 card pairs to join her. Two special secret power spinner spaces can help you reach her even faster!

Kids learn visual and counting skills while playing a game featuring characters they know and love. It’s fast-paced fun as you make snowflake card matches and build your trail. So let the wintry journey begin.

Includes: 1 Gameboard, 1 Back Panel, 2 Connector Clips, 1 Spinner, 4 Cardboard Movers, 4 Plastic Mover Stands, 44 Snowflake cards, 1 Instruction Sheet.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – War of the Spark Planeswalker Deck: Jace, Arcane Strategist
description:
Planeswalker Decks familiarize players who are interested in Magic with basic strategy, plus the games setting, characters, and mechanics of the latest set. Each deck comes with a foil Planeswalker card not found anywhere else.

Jace Beleren likes to stay two steps ahead of his enemies. (The telepathy helps with that...) Dodge your opponents’ defenses, disrupt their plans, and relentlessly take over the board.

Contents: 1 ready-to-play 60-card deck featuring a foil Planeswalker card, 2 War of the Spark booster packs, 1 strategy insert, 1 Magic reference guide, 1 MTG Arena code card, 1 tuck box

Time to get tiny and decorate the Dollhouse for the party with all your friends. Collect charms for your headband as you go. Whoever can collect the most charms wins the special heart-shaped charm!

Work together to help Gabby and her friends get ready for the sleepover. Move through rooms of the dollhouse and collect charms. The player with the most charms at the end of the game wins.

Celebrate at the party with all your friends by saying a big, “Meow!”

Includes 1 dollhouse game board, 1 roof gameboard, 1 elevator cab, 12 room cards, 3 punch sheets (41 charms, 4 movers), 2 dice, 1 plastic Meow Meow Delivery dice shaker, 4 wearable plastic headbands, 4 plastic mover stands, instructions.

In Statefall, you are given command over a randomly assembled army group. It’s your mission as the commanding General to get the most out of the units at your disposal. To anticipate and deal with each new enemy move and to secure control over the state!

The game features a starting sequence, that generate a wide variety of scenarios to deal with, both in environmental obstacles and army group compositions.

The army group consisting of 8 units is drawn from 15 available units for each player. Divided into 11 different unit types, beloning to one of four categories. Attack, defence, support and Control.

Attack units, Mortar(2), Tank(3), Infantry, Marine, Fighter, and Sapper. Each attack unit has their own unique ability to attack enemy units on the board, thereby fliping owenership of the attacked targets and increasing your control over the board.

Defence units, Minefield and Anti-Air will together with environmental river tiles and the coastline, help you to protect high value control units from enemy attacks.

Support units, Logistics, may allow you to reactivate previously committed attack units.

Control tiles, Capital, Suburb and Rural(2), will give you an increased level of control given owenership and certain criterias. These are high value targets and should be defended if possible!

The battle sequence will see both players take turns, playing one unit card from their hand, followed be placing the corresponding unit tile on the board, next to an already placed unit. This continues until both players have committed all their 8 units to the battlefield.

The scoring sequence is comprised of two stages. Each player will start by counting each unit tile under their control. The next step is to look at any control units on the board, Capital, Suburbs and Rurals. These may give a lot of additional control-points to the owner.

The player with the highest amount of control-points is the winner of the battle!

-description from publisher

description: Jack and Detectives is a game with the theme of the Jack the Ripper incident that occurred in London in the 19th century. The player will be the character in this case as the murderer ‘Jack the Ripper’ (Jack), the ‘Detective’ chasing Jack, or the ‘devoted” who cooperates with Jack. If two victims are killed, the investigation will be terminated, and Jack will be nominated and arrested among the detectives, and the game will be settled.

Welcome to 1538, in the middle of the 16th century in Holland, where the Empire needs to explore new lands in order to find new trade routes. All the interests of the crown are now in your hands with your decisions in this adventure through the seas, where you can find treasures in other confines, but beware that the sea has many dangers and fears such as meeting the terrible monster of the Kraken.

In an adventure and decision-making game, you must get the highest number of stars in the fewest number of moves presented in 3 independent adventures in different years, 1538, 1542, and 1548 in print and play format.

In order to move about the map, throw the dice. Next, find ‘Start’ in the top row (on the navigation bar around the map) and count the number of cells you’ve thrown. For example, if you find yourself on cell 5L this will mean that you can move 5 cells to the left and 2 cells to the right. The L in 5L stands for left, you can move in the opposite direction, but only half of the cells are specified by the number. When you are moving about on the map, you may encounter a question mark ‘?’. If you happen to land on it, you will partake in a random event. Take notice that the map contains Krakens, fish, and ships. They have damage and hp number written next to them. When you land on a cell with one of those things, you will have to battle what’s on it. The battle will be decided by throwing the dice. The purpose of the game is to collect more stars in fewer moves. You will earn the number of stars as written next to it. Keep in mind, that if the star is within a red square, you’ll have to fight the boss guarding that terrain first.

The game comes in 6 different languages, something that makes it reach more patrons and is not limited by language, as well as low ink and color versions.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Tethys is a collaborative space dungeon crawler where you, along with your teammates, must explore the mining colony on the surface of Saturn's moon, Tethys, complete missions and defeat the Alien Queen. At each turn, you will open new rooms, face fearsome aliens, conquer new weapons, modifiers and items that will enhance your arsenal: your precious dice. Find and open the chests to receive loot and access codes that will lead you to unexplored places, until you discover the underground hangar that hides a terrible truth ...

During your turn, you can perform 2 Actions, Move and Attack, as well as collect loot and open rooms.

If you complete the mission objective, you win!
If, on the other hand, you and all the players who participated in the mission lose all Energy, bringing the battery to 0, the Mission has failed.

-description from publisher

As our knowledge of the Universe expands so does our ability to roam the stars and widen the horizon of our lives. You take the role of explorers who will gather knowledge, analyze it and with that knowledge expand our vision.

In Stars Arcana, players try to set up the most efficient engine builder by collecting cards, building up their tableau and storing cards in their vault. The game's goal is to score as many points as possible by having cataloged (collected) the most valuable stars and constellations before the deck is depleted and all of the stars are cataloged.

All of the stars, constellations, and night-sky wonders you see on the cards are hand-picked by our astronomer friend Nataša Stanić and appear in the sky as depicted and described on the cards.

Enhance your Heroes' capabilities with a Master Skill or Recipe for each Profession! Heroes may learn these skills and recipes to achieve truly amazing feats of artifice and knowledge. Unlock such recipes as the Alchemist’s Draught of Veils, the Inscriber’s Resurrection Scroll, and many others.

The promo pack will also feature two rituals to bolster the strength of the Heroes through magical means: Aiuncraft and Aoxcraft. These powerful rituals tie in with the Heroes' choices during the campaign.

The promo pack will feature nine additional standard-size cards, which will be exclusive to the promo pack and not printed with the base game.

—description from publisher's website

Test your movie knowledge, and maybe learn some fun facts about your favorite movies! We've gathered it all - actors, plots, characters, music ... see who can remember or guess the most to win this Hollywood hoopla!

Designed for the participants of the 2021 West Coast Rumble, these six scenarios for ASL are designed for minimum set up time, resulting in maximum playing time.

Q19 – Raff’s Ruffians: Diersfordt, Germany, 24 March 1945, Germans v. Americans, Estimated Playing Time 2 hours 43 minutes

Q20 – Boxcloth: Otto Island, Apamama Atoll, 23 November 1943, Japanese v. Marines, Estimated Playing Time 2 hours 4 minutes

Q21 – Hold Pokhlebin!: Pokhlebin, Russia, 5 December 1942, Germans v. Russians, Estimated Playing Time 3 hours 24 minutes

Q22 – French Spirt: Oise-Aisne Canal, France, 5 June 1940, Germans v. French, Estimated Playing Time 3 hours 45 minutes

Q23 – Campoleone Salient: Campoleone, Italy, 30 January 1944, Germans v. British, Estimated Playing Time 3 hours 7 minutes

Q24 – Moldavian Precursor: Zgardesti, Moldavia, 3 July 1941, Romanians v. Russians, Estimated Playing Time 3 hours 21 minutes

ASL Map Boards Required: 2a, 6, 6a, 85, & m

ASL Scenario Pack issued in conjunction with the 10th Anniversary of the North Texas Shoot Out ASL Tournament in 2019. Contains the following scenarios:

NTX01: Stalingrad-1 Depot: Stalingrad, USSR    
NTX02: They Came Like The Rain: Wandumi Ridge, New Guinea

Overview:
Palimpsests occur when a piece of parchment (typically used as writing material in the past) is scraped to remove the top layer of writing. It was a form of erasure that allowed for reuse of the piece of parchment for a new piece of writing. Then, writing materials were difficult to come across and labor intensive to create, leading to the practice of scraping that resulted in the faint appearance of previous text. In recent years, the concept has been used by social scientists trying to explore and understand how the past continues to influence the present in variety of fields. It calls us to reflect on how we inherit institutions, laws, policies, neighborhoods, ideas, and histories from the past and how these influence the present.

Gameplay:
In this game you will be playing the role of a scribe trying to leave their mark on history. Each turn you will erase a letter and come up with a new word. If you cannot create a new word or there are no spaces able to be erased, you are lost to history. Players will continue doing this, passing their card around clockwise at the end of each turn, until there is only one person remaining. The last player able to make a word is the winner.

—description from the designer

Luminescence is a solo tile laying and hand management game were a player is trying to connect point A to point B. The game is a metaphor about the struggle with depression and anxiety. On your turn, you draw three tiles and then either play them faceup or facedown. When playing a tile faceup, you must match all sides and lines to the tiles your are connecting to. Additionally, any tile you play must be higher numerically than the one you are connecting to. Any unused tiles are flipped facedown. You have five light tiles that help you with hand management and tile laying while trying to connect to point B.

—description from the designer

A grand tactical game about six major battles between Frederick the Great’s Prussia and Maria Theresa’s Austria during the Seven Years War (1756-1763).

The battles:
Lobositz, October 1st, 1756
Kolin, June 18th, 1757
Leuthen, December 5th, 1757
Hochkirch, October 14th, 1758
Torgau, November 3rd, 1760
Freiberg, October 29th, 1762

description:
Turn Queen of 12 into a real Roll & Write game with this expansion. It includes a completely new game mode with new rules for game preparation, 2 completely new score sheets and 2 new characters (8 cards).

Instead of starting with 4 cards as in the basic game, you have directly a big deck of 8 cards. The gameplay is the same, but the sheets work much differently: per die, you may check the number shown AND the color. Of course, you still want to have a lot of both, but now it is even more important to get exactly the ones you are missing.

You can specialize or work towards set bonuses. Colors also give bonus points, but more importantly, certain color combinations let you use powerful extra abilities. And if you weren't allowed to make any crosses, or you got exactly one die, you get to pick a special bonus, or save up for an even more powerful bonus.

So the question of which dice you get becomes even more important, and sometimes you may even want to intentionally not take any dice at all. So it's even more important that you keep an eye on the others and their cards! Depending on the card selection, there are a number of different strategies you can use to get the maximum points out of your slips.

There are also refill sets that include scoring blocks for both the base game and the expansion.

Translated from the crowdfunding page

description: From 2 to 8 players agree in advance on the measure (glass, sips, etc.) as well as which drink is in the game. The number of laps is also agreed. Players take turns rolling the dice and moving around the board for the number they get. The first player to get back to the start wins the round. The winner is the player who wins the most rounds. First of all, the goal of the game is fun.

From the vaults and drainage shafts one can hear the unholy muttering of ancient ceremonies. The followers of this cult worship a god who is known in the vernacular only as the Unspeakable. His arrival is near.

Dive deeper into the world of Alka Sowa. The expansion "the Cult" contains a mini-campaign, which consists of two full-length scenarios and offers some new gameplay mechanics.

To play the expansion you need both Materia Prima - The Alchemists Guild and Materia Prima - The Inquisition.

Something is stirring in the shadows of the woods. There are rumours of a horrible creature that climbs through windows at night and snatches the little ones from their beds.

Dive deeper into the world of Alka Sowa. The expansion "the Hunt" contains a mini-campaign, which consists of two full-length scenarios and offers some new gameplay mechanics.

To play the expansion you need both Materia Prima - The Alchemists Guild and Materia Prima - The Inquisition.

Dive deeper into the world of Alka Sowa. The expansion "the Last Stand" contains a mini-campaign, which consists of two full-length scenarios and offers some new gameplay mechanics.

To play the expansion you need both Materia Prima - The Alchemists Guild and Materia Prima - The Inquisition.

—description from the publisher

A game to test one's knowledge in literature. 2 000 questions on themes like "Authors", "Works of literature", "Titles" and "Characters". 180 challenges to take part in: quotes, creation or unusual.

The game contains a board, 6 pawns, 1 die, 1 rules booklet, 1 45 seconds hourglass, 342 cards and 4 drawing boxes.

Playable by 2 to 6 players, ages 15 and up. Duration of a game: about 30 minutes. 3 difficulty levels.

Un jeu pour s'amuser et tester ses connaissances en littérature. 2 000 questions thématiques sur les auteurs, les oeuvres, les titres et les personnages. 180 défis à relever : citation, création ou insolite.

Experience new strategies and gameplay with The Animal Specialist Expansion!
