solo/cooperative deck builder in a mint tin.  Play cards to complete quests before you and the other pirates run out of morale in Pirate Cove.

Players will plan to either attempt a quest solo, together, or not at all and stay at Pirate Cove.  When attempting a quest, play cards to meet the quest requirements.  If players take damage while questing, they will become stranded and lose morale.

Any quests not completed in a turn, will have rumors of cursed quests by your crew.  If you do not pay them off with the gold you earn from completing quests, you will lose morale.

Lose all your morale and you lose the game.

During the game you will earn gold from quests to spend on upgrades to your ship and hiring crew member cards.  Upgrades to your ship give you a permanent ability while adding cards to your deck.  Crew member cards go right into your deck and have abilities you can use when you draw them.

Become the pirate legend by completing all the quests, maintaining the morale of your crew, and managing your gold correctly.

Fly Fight Win: Afterburner is a hex-based, miniatures wargame rules set for air-to-air combat. The game emphasizes the importance of speed, altitude, and pilot skill and minimizes tedium. It includes statistics for many frontline fighters from the early Korean War era through the end of the Cold War.

Afterburner is a standalone rules set and the second game in the Fly Fight Win series. All games in the series are designed for playability but retain sufficient detail to recreate plausibly historic outcomes. Most scenarios can be played in under 60 minutes.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Commander 2020: Ikoria: Lair of Behemoths Commander – Timeless Wisdom
description:
The Timeless Wisdom deck lets you do something every MTG player loves: draw tons of cards! Create tokens when you draw extra cards with Gavi, Nest Warden; strike twice with your army of tokens with Akim, the Soaring Wind; or gain life and power up for a unexpected flying attack with Brallin, Skyshark Rider and Shabraz, the Skyshark.

Timeless Wisdom is one of five Commander Decks from Ikoria Lair of Behemoths (IKO). Each includes exclusive cards tailored to that deck—pick your favorite mechanics and dive in to battle.

Battle your way through the plane of Ikoria with monster-themed mechanics that grow your creatures build your bond and crush your opponents.

Contents one: (1) foil oversized Commander card, one hundred (100) card deck, ten (10) double-sided tokens, one (1) deck box, one (1) Life Wheel.

—description from publisher

Take to the High seas as commander of one of three formidable fleets and cut a swathe through the storied Oceans of Thargos! You'll face an endless array of dangers, from the seas themselves, the killers that sail them and the dark horrors which lurk beneath them. Will you prevail or will a watery grave be your only prize?
​

Command a war galley in a gritty fantasy setting using solo/coop or vs game modes. You decide what risks to take to avoid being sunk. You complete epic voyages for the ultimate prize.

Players choose their fleet, which vessels to play on specific voyages and then manage the hull integrity, crew morale and supplies as you come up against pirates, enemy fleets, foul weather and sea creatures througgh the use of an event card deck.

You then roll dice to determine the success of each encounter, choosing to pursue a sea battle or make a hasty retreat to fight another day for example. Ecah vessel has unique attributes.

The game is won on a Victory Point basis with rewards for completing voyages, sinking enemy tonnage and more. The game ends after completring all 5 voyages included.

WGoT also contains bonus Mission Cards for the Heroes of Thargos CG enabling the two games to be combined.

-description from designer

description:
ヤンタン (YANG TAN) is a card game from Japan, based upon ideas from Fan Tan.
The suits and ranks are on separate cards and are used in combination.

Place up to two cards on your turn. The winner is the player who runs out of cards.

Ascendia - Seasons of Thargos is a semi-cooperative, table top role-playing game that requires you to set out as an ill-equipped adventurer in the hidden Valley of Ascendia, in the fantasy World of Thargos.

Collect raw materials, craft, trade and interact with wild animals and merchants to improve your equipment and skills, whilst striving to survive the approaching winter. But beware as each season brings its own challenges and some more fearsome creatures, often pushed by hunger, venture down from the mountain peaks into the valley.

You compete and cooperate for survival for the ultimate goal as a future leader of Ascendia. Encounters with wild beasts and fell creatures require special dice rolls, modified by equipment and character skills that you can improve as the game progresses through trading and crafting. You explore a Hex-based board that flips over to a winter side when that season comes.

During the game you accumulate Renown points by performing various tasks and interacting with the different mechanics of the game. Once you have at least one Renown token you can decide to apply in front of the inhabitants of the village for the title of Lord of Ascendia. If you are successful, you have won over the villagers and are elected as Lord of Ascendia, winning the game.

—description from designer

Based on sword and sorcery stories by C.L. Moore (one of the first women to publish in the genre, and a contemporary of Conan's Robert E. Howard), Jirel of Joiry’s kingdom has been overtaken by the warlord Guillame. Her last ditch attempt to save her kingdom involves travel into the realm of the Black God to search for a means to destroy her enemy and take back her kingdom... potentially at the cost of her own soul.

One or two players can assume the role of either Jirel (tempestuous female warrior) or the Black God (a phantasm with unknowable intrigues). Each role will be forced to make difficult choices in this fast-paced, rules lite, GM’less tabletop "micro game". Can Jirel claim the Black God's deadly kiss and retain her sanity? Will it be worth the effort, and will she ultimately regret her choice?

The Kickstarter was funded in September of 2022 and at that time the game and its parent boxed rpg set were planned for release sometime in late 2023. As of June 2025, the game is still unprinted & unreleased.

El alquimista de los dados ("The alchemist of the dice") is a family game of 2 to 4 players, between 20 and 40 minutes, of set collection, and taking risk with the dice rolls, where you will try to be the first to elaborate the necessary potions to recover the trust of the count.

To do this, you will gather flasks of ingredients, special potions, ancient grimoires, and you will have a small chance to bother your rivals, with the aim of being the first to score 10 points with your potions.

On each turn, players will be able to perform one of 4 actions. Draw 2 flask cards, either from the deck or face up cards. Get special flask, these modify the result of the dice or change the color of an ingredient flask.
Claim a grimoire, paying its cost in cards. Or try to brew a potion, discarding the corresponding cards.

To be successful brewing a potion, it will be necessary to at least obtain the required value with a roll of the dices. That's where the grimoires and special flask will come into play if you have them.

If you are successful, place the collected potion in your play area. If not, you must discard 2 of your used cards when trying to brew the potion. If you get the exact amount, you will have an extra turn.

Players will alternate turns until one of them achieves at least 10 points with their potions.

There is a mole in commissar Kluftinger's department, and only you can find him! This is done by solving classic Escape room style puzzles.

Unlike other Escape room games, this one does not have a hard time limit, instead some events put the game clock forward, thus limiting your actions. If you fail, you have to start over again.

Mirianth, a kingdom in constant war against terrible monsters and with nations separated by personal interests. Only those who have prepared for war will survive the coming winter.
Be among them!

In Mirianth Wars, players are creating their factions trying to earn the loyalty of warriors, build the best dwellings, find the most valuable treasures, and commit to engage in memorable battles against monsters, all through rounds of negotiation with their opponents for the cards.

However, when player strategies conflict, diplomacy will be the last resort before luck comes into play. If the players do not come to an agreement, they will face the plague deck, and the outcome will only depend on luck.

After everyone has created their faction, they will go through the tests that will define how good their strategy was.

First, players need to check that all their heroes can be accommodated in residences, from simple huts to sumptuous castles, otherwise they will scatter and leave the player.

From the heroes who were accommodated and did not perish to the plague, the battle will begin against the monsters, which if not defeated, will destroy houses, steal treasures or kill the heroes. After the battle, you will once again need to accommodate your heroes in order for them to recover.

Finally, from the surviving heroes, saved treasures, and defeated monsters, the player's points will be counted.

Mirianth Wars presents a game of diplomacy and strategy based on the balance between ambition and luck.

-description from publisher

In Banking, players must amass money and stock values by rolling the dice and advancing one of their three pawns. When they land on a square of their color (or when they use a "banking" card if they have one), players can put all their assets in the bank, where they will be safe from fire, thieves and other hazards that can occur during the game.

The game ends when one of the players brought all his pawns back to the starting point. The player who has the most assets in the bank wins.

—user summary

The Condemned is a Collectible Card Game designed to be fast paced, with an easy learning curve and plenty of depth. You step into the role of a condemned inmate on a space prison. You're duking it out, gladiator-style, in a no-holds-barred arena deathmatch for the entertainment of other inmates. Condemned have weapon and armor slots to equip various cards. Some weapon types are stronger or weaker, depending on the type of armor they're hitting. In addition, there are events that change the flow of battle and preparations that give you an edge in the fight.

You win by reducing your opponent's life to 0. Simple, right? Not necessarily! There's lots of play and counterplay in The Condemned. Armor cards have weaknesses and resistances to different damage types. Events can change the course of the game when you least expect it. That Alpha Strike you were planning on using to win you the game? Nope! Your opponent just used Repossess to take your weapon away and now you can't attack!

The game is fast, fun, and has plenty of depth! Gear up and Enter The Arena!

—description from the designer

Sometimes walls separate people, but the ABCD wall brings people together to play!

Find the letters that make up your word among the boxes that make up the ABCD wall!

By touching only one of the boxes on the wall each turn, find the boxes needed to complete your word without knocking down the wall or you will lose your turn.

The first to complete their word will be the winner.

Every round, one player gets the opportunity to fart. But... they have to do it secretly, because the other players shouldn't find out it was you who farted. This is a game of paying attention to each other to figure who farted, because if you guess right, you get a point.

—user summary

Wie o wie heeft de scheet gelaten! In dit grappige spel gaat het er om wie kan raden welke speler, de vader speelfiguur in het midden op de tafel, een scheet heeft doen laten. Alle speler krijgen een scheetzak die verbonden is met de vader en houden deze onzichtbaar voor de anderen onder tafel vast. In willekeurige volgorde gaat er bij een van de spelers een rood lampje branden en deze speler drukt zonder dat de anderen het merken in zijn/haar scheetzak. Dan volgt er natuurlijk het geluid van een stevige scheet. Iedereen mag raden wie de scheet heeft gelaten. Met een beetje bluf en goed raden wordt je de winnaar van dit spel.

—description from the publisher

In the first expansion for Paper Dungeons every player will continue to search for glory and power, in new and more dangerous Dungeons. Deadliest traps, Lieutenants, strong bosses, will be present in the new adventures. Side Quests will motivate even more divergent paths, and new powerful artifacts will help you group to defeat all perils and monsters.

This expansion brings new elements to enhance the difficult of a game, using a new dungeon sheet, presenting new and harder challenges

Are you ready to be the most glorious group again?

description:
Hide in a mystic castle and try to avoid all ghosts, who has decided to find all intruders.

In this ghostly adventure family game, one of the players (the intruder) hide somewhere in the castle... the other players are the ghosts who tries to locate the intruder.
All players can gather points up to the very end... to find out who won.

The Intruder puts on the cardboard glasses, so that the other players can't see where the intruder's eyes are looking. He chooses one field inside the castle and one on the graveyard... and writes it down. The hour glass is activated, and the ghosts need to find the intruder.
Ghosts use "Castle cards", asking the intruder: "are you somewhere in this region?".
While it goes on, it is about collecting points - either for not being found, or for "being close".

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – War of the Spark Planeswalker Deck: Gideon, the Oathsworn
description:
Planeswalker Decks familiarize players who are interested in Magic with basic strategy, plus the games setting, characters, and mechanics of the latest set. Each deck comes with a foil Planeswalker card not found anywhere else.

Gideon Jura is a stalwart hero who never hesitates to throw himself between his allies and danger. Rally a massive army, then lead them into battle and vanquish your opponent in a single overwhelming attack.

—description from publisher

boardgame: Intime Conviction n° 5: L'affaire des huîtres
description:
Jurors, will you make the right decision?

A Christmas dinner, a Breton family and the body of an accountant found at the bottom of a cliff.

What happened at the Duguenecs that night?

Slip into the shoes of a juror, judge a criminal case and deliver your verdict.

HOW TO PLAY ?
1. Perform the requested actions according to the indications and the order of the 12 Juror Cards
2. Reveal the 4 fact sheets throughout the game and start the debate!
3. Cast your verdict by voting anonymously with the Final Vote Cards. Guilty or Not Guilty?
4. Discover the Final Revelation

Play with friends for an evening by recreating the atmosphere of a closed session! Or make the fun last by revealing only a few items a day!

Content:
- 1 Rule of the game
- 1 Portrait of the accused & her statement
- 1 Scene of the crime
- 12 Juror Cards
- 1 Discovery Pass
- 24 Final Voting Cards
- 3 sealed fact sheets
- 1 FINAL REVELATION! To reveal at the end of the game

Intime conviction is a craft object entirely designed, made and assembled by hand and in Belgium. The format of the game is unusual as well: this game is in an envelope.

boardgame: Intime Conviction n° 6: L'affaire des flammèches
description:
Jurors, will you make the right decision?

Slip into the shoes of a juror, judge a criminal case and deliver your verdict.

The Quebec band JAZZEUR BORÉAL is on tour in Europe. Is their backing singer responsible for causing a fire and the death of a show technician?

In Intime Conviction, players embody a group of jurors for an evening or a week. First, they will have to analyze the facts against the accused. Once all the actions of the juror cards have been completed, the game ends with a final vote where the players will have to decide by majority whether or not the accused is guilty in a criminal case.

In pairs or more, the participants will see their opinions put to the test more than once over the cards and the debates! During the final vote, it is alone in the face of their intimate conviction that they will have to find the way to victory.

Play with friends for an evening by recreating the atmosphere of a closed session! Or make the fun last by revealing only a few items a day!

Content:
- 1 Rule of the game
- 1 Portrait of the accused & her statement
- 1 Scene of the crime

Intime conviction is a craft object entirely designed, made and assembled by hand and in Belgium. The format of the game is unusual as well: this game is in an envelope.

The Inner Sphere sourcebook provides information on the five great Houses- Kurita, Davion, Liao, Marik, Steiner -as well as the lesser powers of the Periphery.  The Inner Sphere also offers general histories of each society, comprehensive maps and a section detailing ten key worlds of the Inner Sphere.

Expedición Humedales is a deck of cards about the flora and fauna of the Argentine Sierras. Through four different game modes you will be able to get acquainted with the inhabitants of the sierras by sharing a fun moment with family or friends.

Playing you will learn the names, characteristics and conservation status of birds, land animals, fish and trees.

Dare to discover the endangered species and many more that inhabit our common home.

Knowing our nature we can take better care of it.

Enmity Engine is a horror-themed card fighting game for 2 players, where both players control a team of 3 Fighters. Launch Attacks to take down your opponent’s Fighters and Switch your Fighters’ positions to maneuver yourself into advantageous situations. Anticipate your opponent’s strategies and outwit them. Throughout the game, you’ll gain Insight, which you can use to Awaken your Fighters, permanently upgrading their abilities, or unleash their powerful Signature Moves. You win when you defeat all of the opposing Fighters.

Hedron Collision is a dice flicking party game for 2 players. Flick your dice to get as close as possible to the center of the board to score more points, but watch out! Your opponent can knock your dice out on their turn! Fast, simple, and addictive. Will you go the safe route and try to score lots of points or play offensively to knock your opponent’s dice out at the risk of overshooting? It’s also easy to carry and waterproof, so you can take it out to the pub or a party and not have to worry about babying it when you’re drunk off your ass! It’s also printed on a playmat so you can roll up to a TCG tournament with it and jam some games between rounds.

A map showing a rich sea of ​​amazing beings has been discovered. The world's greatest divers heard the news and rushed to explore this new underwater universe. You and your crew race to catalog the marine life. But in this ocean adventure, your progress will depend on your strategy to make the most of oxygen in the dives and the best stops to refill your air tank.

During a game of Dive Adventures, players will roll dice and use the result to move their diver into the sea depicted on the map, trying to catalog the greatest amount of marine life.

Each time the diver reaches a location with a helm, he fills this icon on his sheet and can spend this icon later to reroll some dice.

After 4 rounds, players can have one last round with the remaining helm icons on their sheet (the number of helms will be the number of movement points in the last round).

boardgame: Hero or Villain: Battle Royale – The Card Game
description:
Hero or Villain: Battle Royale –The Card Game- is a fast-paced game where you and your friends trade blows as super-powered individuals. Aside from combat elimination, the game also has social deduction and Russian-roulette mechanisms that will ensure only one is left alive by the time players go through one entire deck. The game is designed for 2 to 5 players, and takes 10 – 15 minutes to play.

On your turn, you can play any number of power cards to influence the game, or try to attack other players to eliminate them. Players may verbally challenge you if they feel your character does not have the ability to use a certain power, forcing you to lose some cards if you were indeed not telling the truth. At the end of your turn you must finish by drawing one card, and if you draw a Demolition card you will be eliminated from the game if you do not have any cards left to defend or heal yourself. If you are still alive after you draw a demolition card, then you will place the card back in the deck at a place of your choice, which may be later triggered by another player and remove them from the game!

There are a total of 6 playable characters, all appearing in the Hero or Villain gamebook series.

Do you want to save the world with the help of a colorful mix of characters from the past and the present? Do you want to have fun creating stories while learning about human rights? Then BARABAR is the game for you!

In BARABAR, the players will have to collaborate with each other and play with characters of important people who have achieved great things for mankind. They belong to 5 clans: activists, environmentalists, artists, ecologists, and entrepreneurs. You will have to create stories on how these characters will help to solve challenges, some of them real or some of them more science fiction. Have you ever thought how Martin Luther King could help in a zombie apocalypse?

BARABAR is both competitive and collaborative, as well as educational. It can be played in a variety of different ways depending on how you want to do it. 
Some of the rules make the game more didactic while others make it more competitive.
It is ideal to play with friends, ideally from 2 to 6 players. The game usually takes from 20 minutes to 1 hours.

—description from the publisher

Cartzzle is a new concept of board game containing a game, a work of art and puzzles. Compose this graphic interpretation of Pauline Détraz, then redo it by taking up challenges.

The box holds a single Cartzzle, an endless one you can make with both sides of the 50 cards. Cartzzles are pictures you must first piece together by laying cards on top of each other (with or without the template, depending on your dedication). Then, start again, using challenges this time! Removing cards, flipping them, and laying them elsewhere will change the picture and also allow you to meet some challenges (as example: Compose a scene that features entirely and at the same time the Dragon and the Fisher King).

Puttycat Brawls is a strategic, deck-building, turn-based card game, whose main protagonists are cats. 
Two opposing factions meet at the Battlefield. The game is intended for 2, 4 or 6 people, as long as both sides have the same number of Players.

The opposing parties start with a certain amount of Resources. The aim is to use units, spells and strategies to reduce the opponent's Resources. Each Player has 6 fields on his disposal where he can place his army. The formation is important, thus, proper deployment of troops is essential.

The game ends when one party reaches 0 on the Resource bar.

—description from the designer

Slovanský Opijáš is party board game for 4-12 players, where nobody will be sober after this game.
 You move your figure on the board by rolling dice and try to get the figure to the square marked with its color and the arrow pointing to castle. Whoever gets there first wins the game.
But this will not be so easy. There are several squares on the board in different places where you have to draw a card.  The card describes some task that you need to do. If you successfully complete the task, you do not need to drink, otherwise you must drink one shot. You must drink one shot even if the other player moves his figure to the square where your piece is already standing.

The game includes:
- 1 board game 
- 6 figures
- 74 cards
- 1 dice
- 6 plastic shot glasses

Letters, glasses, ladders, brooms and other everyday objects got lost in the vicinity of Cinderella's Castle, Snow White's hut, Aladdin's market and many other wonderful places. In "W Krainie Baśni" players take on the role of fairy-tale detectives, whose task is to find these items.

This is an observation game with several game modes. In the main mode, each player draws one board at random. When everyone is ready, the card showing the item is revealed. Now the players try to find the item on their board as quickly as possible. Whoever does this first, receives a card as a reward. Finally, the person with the most cards wins.

There are two opposing player camps in the game. One is the player (s) who play for the prisoners. The second is the player on the side of the prison.

The game consists of 10 rounds. Each round consists of a basic movement of prisoner tokens (first camp) and penitentiary tokens (second camp) in areas on the main board to collect items tokens or to make it difficult to collect it.Additionally, both camps have the option of placing a special prisoner card (first camp) and a penitentiary card (second camp) in most rounds, which strengthen the effects of tokens when collecting item tokens or making it difficult to collect them.

In addition, in some rounds there are some extra functions, such as the necessity to provide information by the prisoners' camp to the opponent how much has been collected for the plan at a given moment, or the possibility of exchanging the collected item tokens from the main board for those needed for a specific escape plan from outside the board etc.

At the beginning of the game, the first camp draws the escaping prisoner card (1, 2 or 3 escaping cards) and additional suspect prisoner cards. He/They also draws an escape plan card that shows which item tokens he/they must collect for the plan. The second camp sees all these cards, but doesn't know who is escaping and with what plan.

The goal of the game for the first camp is to collect all items for the plan within 10 rounds for the appropriate prisoners or a special field on the main board in such a way that the second camp does not guess who is escaping and how. The object of the game for the second camp is to guess these unknowns within 10 rounds.
The game may end with a victory for one of the camps but there also can be a draw.

-description from designer

The Atrum is what comes after death, a dark and desolate place. Any creature or being that dies on the material plane ends up in the Atrum. The bodies are transferred from one plane to another through the earth, being buried in the middle of both places. The only exception is the Crawlers, who roam the Atrum engaging in fierce battles where they unleash their full power.

Atrum Arena is a large format Premium Trading Card Game (TCG) created by Estudio Fedorian, where each participant makes a team of three lords and/or ladies of darkness, known as “Crawlers”. These Crawlers are represented in the game in the form of spectacular holographic premium cards. With them you will face the teams of your opponents, trying to defeat all of your opponets Crawlers, one by one and lead your team to victory. All of this using the combined powers of your Crawlers, the synergy between them and the faithful Minions, which will be drawn from a common deck as a consumable resourse to make the Crawler's powers.

Atrum Arena is a true new game concept, not only because of its mechanics that mix resource management, hand management and a bit of luck forcing, but also because it brings to the tables its exclusive “build and choice of actions menu”.

At the same time it is about luxury collecting, but extremely friendly and accessible, being the game of this type that can be started faster and more easily.

—description from the publisher

description:
Crop Circle Champions is a puzzly tile laying game where players are competing in the Inter-Galactic Crop Circle Championship.

On their turn, players draft 2 tiles from the 3 available pairs, and add them to their crop circle making sure connections match up with each other (replenishing the market afterwards). Players score points by making crop circles based on a new set of patterns each game. When scoring, players place between 1-6 UFO tokens depending on the difficulty of the pattern.

After 10 turns the game ends and players gain/lose UFO tokens based on how many open connections they have and score bonus UFO tokens for each 4+ connection tile they scored.

Whoever has the most UFO tokens left wins!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering Universes Beyond — Warhammer 40,000 Commander Deck: Forces of the Imperium
description:
Forces of the Imperium is one of four preconstructed Commander decks from Magic: The Gathering's "Universes Beyond" crossover with Warhammer 40,000, along with Necron Dynasties, Tyranid Swarm, and The Ruinous Powers. Each of the decks ties into one of the factions from the Warhammer 40,000 universe and is available in both a standard and a collector's edition; Forces of the Imperium is a white, blue, and black deck based on the Imperium and led by Inquisitor Greyfax and Marneus Calgar.

The deck contains 100 cards, 1 foil-etched display commander, 10 double-sided tokens, 1 life tracker, and 1 deck box. The contents of the Collector's Edition are the same, except that all cards (including the tokens and the display commander) have a unique premium "Surge Foil" treatment.

"Libertadores" is a board game divided into areas based on an impulse-based turn system. Players alternate to move several elements, not all, by chronological cards that work as impulse triggers. Libertadores is designed for two players and can be adapted to solitaire mode or several players but not rules yet.

Libertadores depicts the wars of independence in Hispanic America during the Napoleonic world context. Players can lead the forces of the Spanish monarchy or be on the side of the Latin-American republics.

Libertadores is an asymmetric conflict simulation with operational and strategy elements. It deals with major campaigns and international events, including land warfare (military and insurgent), maritime warfare, and the political-diplomatic conflict.  However, the game is of low difficulty and short duration, lasting a maximum of a few hours.

The player draws three cards and discards one. With the cards he can conquer regions through a dice roll. The game ends when the winning player can obtain a number of regions.

Elements
- A map of the American continent of 891x21 cm a
- Date, Reinforcement and battle tracks
- Tokens of national flags of 12 mm
- Cards portraying warlords, sailors, and diplomats
- Dice: blue, red, and white.
- A rulebook

-description from publisher

Libertadores es un juego que simula las guerras de independencia en la América española que sucedieron durante el contexto mundial napoleónico. Una guerra de muy escasos recursos donde los jugadores pueden dirigir las fuerzas que defienden la unidad de la monarquía española o formar parte del bando que busca la independencia de los países hispanoamericanos.

Libertadores es un juego de tablero dividido en áreas con un movimiento de fichas mediante el impulso de una baraja de cartas. De esta forma los jugadores se alternan para mover un número de fichas, no todas, mediante un motor de cartas cronológicas. Libertadores está diseñado para el jugador solitario y dos jugadores.

Libertadores es una simulación de conflicto asimétrico, con elementos operacionales y de estrategia.  Se ocupa de las grandes campañas y de los eventos internacionales. Abarca la guerra terrestre, las grandes batallas de los ejércitos regulares y la lucha de las  guerrillas, asimismo la guerra marítima, las armadas nacionales y el Corso insurgente y los eventos del conflicto político y diplomático. Sin embargo, es un juego de baja complejidad y de una duración corta, que puede acabar en pocas horas.

Componentes:
El cuadernillo con el manual de reglamentos
Un mapa del continente americano con dos hemisféros
Una hoja con las tablas de turnos, de ejércitos expedicionarios y de flotas y navíos de guerra.
Tres barajas sumando 36 cartas de eventos políticos (12), de caudillos patriotas (12) y realistas (12).
Fichas de counters o tokens de fuerzas y de buques en cada bando y de banderas nacionales. Fichas de cubos en color azul o rojo (alternativo a las fichas)
2 dados en color azul y en rojo.

description:

The deck contains 100 cards, 1 foil-etched display commander, 10 double-sided tokens, 1 life tracker, and 1 deck box. The contents of the Collector's Edition are the same, except that all cards (including the tokens and the display commander) have a unique premium "Surge Foil" treatment.

A 6-card combo pack that allows you to combine Sprawlopolis and Naturopolis (to add some natural features to your urban environment).

Unlike the original rules for both standalone games, this variant eliminates the use of card-passing. Rather, players shuffle each game deck individually, flipping a single card from each to reveal 2 scoring conditions. Then, a 3rd card is drawn at random from the 6-card combo pack to reveal the 3rd and final scoring condition. A separate card from this pack is used as the starting tile, displaying 2 "blocks" from those terrains included in each game respectively.

On a player's turn, they draw 1 card from each game deck and must choose 1 to play and 1 to discard. In this way, both game decks with run out at the same rate. End game occurs after the last 2 cards are drawn and played/discarded.

Players score for the largest 2 block groups from each game, lose points for individual roads, and score/lose points according to the 3 scoring condition cards.

—description from the publisher

On a player's turn, they draw 1 card from each game deck and must choose 1 to play and 1 to discard. In this way, both game decks with run out at the same rate. End game occurs after the last 2 cards are drawn and played/discarded.

The Smugglers that make a living on Arrakis are ostensibly a neutral faction. However, the great powers at play are able to use them to their advantage in different ways. As their allegiance shifts, so does the dynamics of the game.

When the Smugglers expansion is added, the game is basically divided into two stages. At first, the Smugglers are neutral, with the Harkonnens turning a blind eye to their illegal operations in exchange for a spice tribute. Later in the game, the Atreides should be able to sway their allegiance, gaining the support of their Leader, Staban Tuek, as well as their soldiers and ‘Thopters.

–description from the Kickstarter

A 6-card combo pack that allows you to combine Sprawlopolis, Agropolis and Naturopolis.

Unlike the original rules for the standalone games, this variant eliminates the use of card-passing. Rather, players shuffle each game deck individually, flipping a single card from each to reveal 3 scoring conditions. Then, a 4th card is drawn at random from the 6-card combo pack to reveal the 4th and final scoring condition. A separate card from this pack is used as the starting tile, displaying "blocks" from those terrains included in each game respectively.

On a player's turn, they draw 1 card from each game deck and must choose 1 to play and 2 to discard. In this way, all three game decks will run out at the same rate. End game occurs after the last 3 cards are drawn and played/discarded.

Players score for the largest block group from each game, get -1 point for each road, and score/lose points according to the 4 scoring condition cards.

Oli is a drawless connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, cover a 1×1, 1×2, 1×3 or 2×2 empty area of the board with stones of your color.

At the end of your turn, for any two diagonally adjacent stones of your color there must be another stone of your color adjacent to both, and there must be no 2×3 patterns of two empty points, two black stones a knight's jump away from each other and two white stones a knight's jump away from each other. Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

-description from designer

boardgame: Fortress of Terror: Pyramid of the Mummy
description:
Deep inside an ancient pyramid lies a hidden tomb, well-guarded with traps, puzzles, and monsters that are no longer among the living. Find the secret entrance into the pyramid, explore to discover the tomb - and meet its terrible guardian.

Pyramid of the Mummy is the fifth Fantasy Adventure Module in the Roll-With-It system, a modular, solitaire roll-and-write adventure game system with endless possibilities for adventure through an accomplish-the-task-and-escape-the-maze-style gameplay.

Gameplay is centered around a maze or map, through which the player must navigate, accomplish some objective(s), and escape the map under pressure from the enemy's attacks via the Doom die. The simple, puzzley, maze-style gameplay is approached differently in each adventure, building on the basic rules with new rules, concepts, and components that make each adventure feel fresh and unique.

This expansion consists of the stretch goals and is filled to the brim with upgraded components and more game play content!

Featuring new wooden casks and barrels to replace your small action and score tokens. Includes deluxe worker meeples, metal coins, and a beautiful new velour bag! There are also added gameplay options such as Goal cards that add events and the Terrior expansion!

-description from the back of the box

Japan, the old capital Kamakura. Here, in this age, you are an apprentice patissier, making traditional japanese pastries.
One day, the master gathered all his apprentices and proclaimed:
"I will employ the one who most beautifully can decorate these pastries"
Will that person be you? Can you line up the pastries prettier than everyone else?
This is an abstract open information strategy game, where you exchange places of pastries of four different colours, in order to line them up orthogonally and diagonally, and score the corresponding points.

Setup
Place the board in the play area.
Give each player a summary.
Place a cake tile on each of the 16 squares on the board, according to colour.
Put the 40 point tiles on the board edge, according to colour, with the point side face up. See to that the 10 chits in each colour are shuffled.
Choose the starting player in any suitable fashion.
Turn goes clockwise around the table; everyone takes their corresponding order chit and places before them. Everyone but the start player flips their order chit so that the special effect is face up (this effect is usable from each player's first turn)

Flow of the game
Turn goes clockwise around the table. Each turn consists of the two steps below, in order.

1: Optional: Exchange places of 2 adjacent cake tiles
Choose two orthogonally adjacent cake tiles, and swap their places.
Cake tiles are not adjacent diagonally.

2: Gain point chit
If the number of icons on a point chit matches the number of cake tiles of the chit's colour, in that row, then you gain that point chit.

You can only gain 1 point chit per turn (unless a special effect allows you otherwise).
The point chit you gain doesn't have to be related to the tiles you moved (if any)
However, if you cannot gain a point chit (or cannot see how you would be able to), regardless of whether you move cake tiles or not, you must pass your turn, without moving any cake tiles and may not take another turn until the game ends.

When you gain a point chit, put it face down to show the special effect. You cannot use a power the very turn you gained it.

Special effects of turn chits and point chits
You may use the effects of turn chits and point chits at any time during your own turn. You may use several on the same turn, but each chit is a one-time use. Once used, flip the chit to face up, so that it shows the number instead.

Explanation of the special effects
You may use the effect up to many times as the number indicates.

Turn order thics, red point chits:
You may do extra move actions

Yellow point chits:
You may gain extra point chits. However, all point chits you gain must be from the same row, and all of them have to fulfill their conditions. You may use several yellow point chits on the same turn, but all chits you gain must come from the same row.

Green point chits:
When you want to gain a point chit, you may lower the number of necessary cake tiles.
Each green point chit can only be valid for gaining one point chit. If you use several green point chits on the same turn, they have to be used for different point chits (i.e. in combination with yellow point chits)

Blue point chits have no special effect. Instead, they are worth double the points of normal point chits. Their full total points are noted on their back sides.


     For all point chits, the power of the special effect is higher, the rarer the chit is.


Game and and victor
The game ends when either all point chits have been gained, or when all players have passed their turn due to not being able to gain a point chit.

The player with the highest total points wins the game. Blue point chits have the value printed on their back side (the front side doesn't count).

In case of a tie, the last player to go breaks the tie.


     Tricks and tips


If you round the total points to be gained as 100, and try to aim for exceeding your player's share of those points (33 points on 3 players, 25 points on 4 players), you'll have an easier time to judge whether you're doing well or not.

boardgame: Ritter Trenk: Ritterduell für starke Ritter
description:
Duel with opponents in a knight tournament and win pairs of items. Beware of the Black Knight, he gives you a negative point at the end of the game and you can only get rid of him with a little piggy card.

The game consists of two phases. In the first phase, players draw from the common deck faceup cards equal to the number of players. Then all players roll a die. Whoever rolls the highest chooses the card first, second highest - second, and so on. When all players select a card, draw the next cards from the deck and repeat the process.

The second phase started when there are no cards in the common deck. Whoever rolls the lowest number - goes first in this phase. Players choose an unpair card and could select an opponent to duel with him for the second card of this kind. Both players roll a die, and whoever has the highest result wins a duel. When all pairs are collected game ends, and players score victory points.

Pair of the same cards: 1 VP
Black Knight: -1VP
Piggy Card: 0VP, Disable Black Knight card
Red Knight Trenk: 2VP

boardgame: Labarnas: The final struggle of the Hittite Empire
description:
Labarnas is a narrative game. Strategy is involved, but if the gods are not in your favour, there won't be a happy end. So lean back and let the story unfold.

You have to guide your people through a series of 30 random events. At the start of the game, you will influence the likelihood of certain events in the Feast for the gods. But your main task through the game will be the distribution of your population between food production and border defence.

The game is set in ancient types, in about 1200 B.C. You are Šuppiluliuma II., the last Labarna (i.e. king) of the Hittite Empire. The glorious days of your ancestors are past. The extraordinary victory against the Egyptians at Kadesh has deteriorated to being merely a story. Nature itself, as well as your neighbours have conspired against you. Your realm is crumbling.

But do not despair. Your people are strong and have shaped the region for the better part of a millennium. With a wise leader like you, destiny can be altered.

A game of Fortitude lasts 16 hands during which you will play a trick-taking game against a German bot. Each round consists of eight tricks--four played in the Calais, France location and four in Trondheim, Normay--with a final ninth trick, that you're trying to lose, played in Normandy.

Between rounds, if you've played tactically then you can earn upgrades that help modify your future cards. But be forewarned because you'll draw periodic setback cards that might well turn a winning position into a loss.

While you want to win tricks in Calais and Trondheim, winning in Normandy will eventually clue Germany into its importance to the Allied forces. So every Normandy win requires you to fill in spaces in a limited polygonal area. If you can, then you continue your campaign. But if you can't fill in the required boxes, you lose.

Fortitude is a trick-taking and write game that asks if you can successfully deceive the Germany military intelligence long enough to ensure D-Day happens as planned.

-description from designer

description:

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Honey Buzz Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards inspired by Honey Buzz!
To play this realm, mark dice numbers in hexes. When all spaces surrounding a honey hex are marked, you gain all benefits adjacent to that honey hex. The benefits include stars, so by playing smart you can buzz your way to 6 stars!

Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards inspired by Smitten!
To play this realm, fill cards in one of the 2 grids. Write the number of a die, and simultaneously write the sum of both rolled dice on a card in the other grid, while making sure no numbers repeat within a grid. You gain benefits when completing cards sharing an intersection. Additionally, you score stars for completed grids.

In Beach Dogs you do your best to help your human collect shells on the beach. Not only will you find the best shells, but you'll also protect him from angry crabs by stomping on them! Crabs are tasty so those are good too. When the day is done the doggo that helped their human the most wins!

Beach Dogs is a quick game played across 12 rounds(waves). Each wave you choose to help out your human on the beach in the sand or dive into the surf to try and grab a shell. All sorts of surprises await in the beach and waves decks as you try to collect sets of cards worth points and counter cards that could hurt your score.

When the wave deck runs out the game is over. Determine the winner by tallying up all the shells you've found, subtracting any points for live crabs and adding points for any you were able to stomp. The player with the most points is Top Dog!

-description from publisher

A boardless pattern-matching game for 2 players, designed to be played with Looney Pyramids. Players score points by laying “words” gridwise in a crossword-like manner. The possible words are determined by a “dictionary,” which shrinks as the game goes on.
Promo version with 28 cards (6 happpy and one sad clown in 4 colors) instead of 56 cards plus one extra bell card instead of a real bell.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Le Clan de la Muette is a board game of secret actions, atmosphere and bluffing in a fantasized 19th century, populated by anthropomorphic gangsters

Members of the famous Mute Clan, you will have to collect enough money by the end of the game to pay your dues to the Silent Sisters.
Failure would mean the end of the Clan, honoring your debt will ensure you a collective victory, and whoever has known how to set aside the most money for their own account will also win an individual victory

At each turn choose a different lieutenant and play his powers day or night, the day you work for the Clan. At night you indulge in selfish and shameful acts, but be careful not to fall on someone smarter than you!

—description from the designer

Dracula has come to London to steal the love of his life, the Vampire Hunters do not intend to let him act and will seek to identify his victim in order to snatch him from the fangs of the vampire count before he leaves for Transylvania

At each game a player will embody Dracula, he is known to the other players.

Dracula, after having chosen his victim, will seek to discover the secret identity of the other players by observing them searching places. At night, he can attempt to vampirize a Hunter in order to convert him to his cause.

The other players are Vampire Hunters, they seek to identify the name of the victim in order to free him. Every day, each Hunter can search a place thanks to his secret identity. At the end of the game they will name one of them who will go and face Dracula.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Imperial Settlers: Empires of the North – Workers In Training
description:
A bonus for preordering Empires of the North : Wrath of the Lighthouse from the publisher.

4 Workers In Training tiles. Worker in training tile gives another action choice to the player.

During the setup, place the 4 workers in training tiles in the middle of the game area. On their turn, as an action, a player may assign 2 workers to one of the tiles and then place that tile in their Empire. During the cleanup phase, players return 1 worker to their supply.

The King has chosen you to be the warrior who finally ends the bloody war that has been going on for years between the two strongest races that inhabit the lands of Lundor, the Elves and the Barbarians. After thousands of casualties on both sides, there is only one option left to end the war once and for all. THE FINAL DUEL. This fight to the death between the two best warriors of the two factions will determine who will be the victor and who will claim for himself the lands of Lundor. Since ancient times, the final duel has been used as a last resort and takes place in Rindel's castle. Which warrior will be the victor? The Barbarian or the Elf?

Super Castle Fighter is a card-based combat game for 1-2 players ages 12+ that plays in 15-30 minutes created by Victor Robles, designed by Lizuren Games and published by The Game Crafter. Each player chooses a character and must fight to the death using cards as special techniques. The player who wins 2 rounds by reducing the opponent's life to 0 is the winner. To use an attack card or special move, you must pay the cost with your controller cards. The opponent can counter those attacks by blocking or using instant cards on the opponent's turn. Some attacks require power points. Accumulate power and spend it at the right time to deal the final blow and claim victory.

-description from designer

A fast-paced strategic board game set in a post-apocalyptic future. Resa City fuels the players' excitement by encouraging gameplay through racing mixed with strategy and combat. All methods of play affect players' chances at outwitting or outracing others on the board.

The game is broken into several turns; 
1) Choosing technology that assists in the race 
2) Placing obstacles on the track - the board plays against you on every turn randomizing obstacle placement and giving the obstacles technology and weapons actions against racers.
3) Racing; where all players use their advantages from garnering tech to move faster or combat other racers around a track.

The game uses dice, cards, and pieces for movement to win. Winning is determined by who finishes first, if at all.

—description from the designer

It's a card game intended to bring everyone to the table and invoke laughter centered around YOU, the game players.  What crazy things might you be up to when you and your friends are Left Unsupervised?

Here's how gameplay works:
- Each player gets 5 yellow cards and 5 red cards.
- Choose a player to be the judge for the first round.
- The judge rolls the special die and determines which player the arrow on the die is pointing to.
- That player is the unsupervised player for this turn.
- The judge chooses any card from their hand to play about the unsupervised player.
- This creates part of a story: "Brad [blank] and that's how legends are made."
- All other players play a card to complete the story and the judge picks their favorite.
- Cards are replenished and the judging role moves to the next player.

Think of Left Unsupervised as kind of like *that other* similar party card game, but staring the players at the table.

The mature edition of Left Unsupervised (ages 17+). It's a card game intended to bring everyone to the table and invoke laughter centered around YOU, the game players.  What crazy things might you be up to when you and your friends are Left Unsupervised?

Here's how gameplay works:
- Each player gets 5 yellow cards and 5 red cards.
- Choose a player to be the judge for the first round.
- The judge rolls the special die and determines which player the arrow on the die is pointing to.
- That player is the unsupervised player for this turn.
- The judge chooses any card from their hand to play about the unsupervised player.
- This creates part of a story: "Brad [blank] and that's how legends are made."
- All other players play a card to complete the story and the judge picks their favorite.
- Cards are replenished and the judging role moves to the next player.

Think of Left Unsupervised as kind of like *that other* similar party card game, but staring the players at the table.

The game simulates the largest battle of the Polish Defensive War of 1939. The battle was fought near the Bzura River, west of Warsaw, between 9 and 19 September, 1939. The first stage of the battle was a Polish counter-offensive, which was successful. However, gradually, the Germans managed to halt it, outflanking the Polish forces and counter-attacking. Eventually, as a result of the battle, Polish Poznań and Pomorze Armies that took part in the battle were destroyed. The road to Warsaw was now open to German armies marching from Silesia.

This is a new edition of the "Bzura 1939" game that is based on the WB-95 wargaming system developed by Taktyka i Strategia. The previous editions of the game were based on different wargaming systems (e.g. W-39).
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: Wielkie Bitwy 1939-45 (Dragon / Taktyka i Strategia)

description:
The game simulates one of the biggest battles of the Polish Defensive War of 1939 that took place near the town of Piotrków Trybunalski from 5 to 6 September, 1939. After two-day fierce fighting, the Polish forces suffered a defeat and were forced to retreat.

This is a new edition of the "Piotrków 1939" game that is based on the WB-95 wargaming system developed by Taktyka i Strategia. The previous editions of the game were based on different wargaming systems (e.g. W-39).
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
