description:
Siren includes 4 Cards.

This is a small, non-essential way to add a touch of diversity to Spire's End! If you would like to change things up, swap out card 51 with a new Story Card, which includes a new choice that can lead to the Siren!

Mega Mushroom includes 2 Cards.

This is a small, non-essential way to add a touch of diversity to Spire's End! If you would like to change things up, swap out cards 38-39 with the Mega Mushroom! It's a more challenging ending to the Mushroom Man encounter with a new alternative story card outcome.

Each  player  pulls  out  5  pieces  from  the  bag  (not  to  be  seen  by  opposing  players)  and  puts each  piece  onto  their  stand.



     Each  player  then  pulls  another  Trominii  from  the  bag  and  totals  the  score  on  that  one piece.  The  highest total  dictates  who  goes  first.  The  play  continues  clockwise.



     The  first  player  then  places  their  chosen  Trominii  from  their  5  (on  the  racks)  in  the  center  of the  chosen  playing  area  (70cmx70cm  area  approx.)    No  score  is  tallied for  this  first  piece played.  



     The  next  player  then  places  their  Trominii  to  match  the numbers  butting  them  to  the  angled  end, in  any  direction.



     The score  comes  from addition  of  the same touching numbers.  For  example,  2  pieces  touching with  4  on  will  be  8,  3  with  4  will  be  12,  up  to  a  possible  8  pieces  precisely  touching.



     After  each  player  plays  their  piece,  that  player  takes  out  another  piece  of  Trominii  from  the bag  and  places  it  on  their  stand.  No  player  should  have  more  or  less  than  5  Trominii pieces during  any  time  until  the  end  of  the  play.



     If  no  move  can  be  made,  that  player  misses  a  turn.



     Blanks  that  are  played  and  touch  to  join  gain  no  score.  Blanks  can  be  laid  as a  way  to  force no  score  from  the  other  opponents.



     Wild  cards  can  be  joined  to  any  other  card  at  any  point  in  the  game  including  another  wild card  but  will  not  score  in the  total.



     The winner  is the  highest Total scorer.  The Total  score  will  be  the  addition  of  the  individual score  from  each  round  as  you  play  OR  for  beginners/younger  players  it  could  be  a  nonscoring  easy  game  and  play  until  all  the  Trominii  pieces  are  gone  from  the  bag.

"X XXX" - a hypothetical scenario in Taktyka i Strategia magazine no. 45 (2022), in which the main British thrust comes from the southern part of the front at El-Alamein.

It is just an additional scenario with an alternative unit set-up. No additional counters are offered.
family: Series: Wielkie Bitwy 1939-45 (Dragon / Taktyka i Strategia)

description:
The game is played on a map of France, with each player represented by a hot air balloon token. The goal of the game is for the player to visit all 19 regions on the map.

On a player’s turn, they draw a card into their hand. If the player has 3 cards in their hand depicting 3 adjacent regions on the map, with at least one region adjacent to the current location of their balloon, then the player can play these cards, claiming each region with a trip-marker. The player then draws 2 cards, and play passes to the next player. If a player does not have 3 cards in their hand depicting 3 adjacent regions, then they discard a card from their hand, and play passes to the next player.

The first player to have a trip-marker on each region of the map wins.

You are stranded on a volcanic jungle island... What a start to a grand adventure! Aventurine Expedition is a card/board game thought up by 2 game enthusiasts with a dream to design a quickly played, action-packed game where you scheme against the others in an attempt to become the greatest Aventurine Miner the world has ever known.

Aventurine Expedition is a fast paced card/board game, (it has the elements of a board game, without the board itself) that has you playing destructive cards against your opponents to stop them from acquiring more Aventurine and Gadgets, in an epic vector art format.

You begin with 2 Aventurine Mines, they are used to gain Aventurine (think of Mines as your source of income).  You gain 1 Aventurine per mine you have every turn. You take 1 Recruit every turn from the Recruit Display into your hand. Aventurine is used to activate Recruits abilities which involve many different unique strategy building mechanics, some abilities include, "all opponents loose 2 mines" "lock a Gadget from an opponent" and "look at an opponents Recruit hand and take 1". Playing Recruits also allow you access to their mines.  Another use for Aventurine is to purchase Materials. Materials are used to purchase Gadget cards, which help you ultimately win. When you acquire a Gadget card you flip it upside down and receive the reward denoted at the top left of the card. However you cannot buy Materials and Gadgets on the same turn. Collect 3 Gadget Cards to be the victor.

A basic turn would go something like this:

James collects 5 Aventurine for the 5 Aventurine Mines he has, he looks at the Recruit Display and take his 1 card, the Mighty Boom. He then spends the required 4 Aventurine to play a card from his hand. Mighty Boom goes into play and makes his opponents loose 2 mines each, they proceed to complain and James uses his remaining Aventurine to purchase a Water Drop costing him 1 Aventurine. He has enough materials for a Gadget, however he bought a material on this same turn so he must wait at least one turn before he can purchase.

Individually packaged module for Endless Winter.

At the end of each Eclipse phase, players have the opportunity to take a Ceremonial Grounds card from those available in a supply, and immediately score it. These cards offer immediate points for certain achievements. You can gain a lot of points by meeting the criteria, but the catch is twofold: you can only score one of these cards per game—and you can no longer bury any cards thereafter! In order to signify this, the card is placed landscape orientation on top of your buried cards pile. Timing is key for playing these well: Too soon, you might not score very many points; too late, the card you most want might get taken and scored by an opponent.

This is a horse racing game, supposedly based on (and obviously promoting) the 1939 United Artists film of the same name.

Six horses run the race no matter how many players are involved, and three dice are thrown on each turn, with each horse of a different color assigned a number. Horses will therefore move 0-3 spaces per turn. The first horse to win the race on an exact count wins the race.

Rule variations are also included to win on doublets or triplets, as well as a more complex game of chance called "The Game of Bucking Broncos," where doublets or triplets require horses to move back.
Event Cards are random events that get shuffled into the hideout decks and have an effect whenever they get drawn by either player.

Hillman: The Game is a new board game that allows you to take on the roles of characters from the hit late 80's - mid 90's TV show "A Different World" in order to be the player with the most cash at the "Season Finale".  Whether you're familiar with the classic Black television show or you've never heard of a Dwayne Wayne in your life, you will have great time playing this game

—description from publisher

POLITIKO is a card game of wacky scheming and cynical realpolitik – in other words: it is a game about winning a Malaysian election!

Play as one of 10 distinct (yet familiar) political parties. Collect voters divided along ethnic and ideological lines.

Use dirty schemes to backstab your opponents. Promise free smartphones. Dole out GLC directorships. Deal with Ex-PMs. Court royalty.

New to POLITIKO 3.0: double cards! Turn schemes into voters; twist voters into schemes. But don’t get confused by your own spin – your backroom plots might backfire on you!

Are you ready to become the Ace of the New Frontier? Then jump into your personalized M.A.W. and prove yourself!

Scorched Core is a card driven, scenario based, tactical simulation of mecha combat in the far future. Play movement cards to maneuver around the battlefield and dodge enemy fire, play weapon cards to shoot and interrupt your enemies and install powerful upgrades to give you an edge over your opposition.

Chose a one off scenario or play through introductory campaign, leading you from a lowly Rookie to a Lance Commander in multiple connected coop and versus scenarios.

—description from the publisher

A mini-expansion to Good Puppers providing automated opponent for a solo experience or to add an extra player to the table. The expansion A set of four friends that each have it's own set of rules to play against.

We established our strength in a time of great strife. Whilst the rest of Crayne was celebrating the reconnection of the world with the Magic Realm and the subsequent defeat of the Titans, Mindassa came under threat from an an entirely new but ancient enemy. One that had been buried in the sands of the desert and fogotten for over 4000 years.

The Tide of Dust had unsealed their time-worn tombs and released their ancient curses, but without the flow of magic in the world the dead lay dormant. That is until the day Archmage Tymirus completed the Re-weaving and magic was allowed to flow into Crayne once more. Magical energy surged into world and the curse of the Ancients was fueled.

The archaic dead rose from the cracks in their tombs and shambled across the desert in search for the living. As if one invasion wasn't enough another unknown enemy appeared: a swarm of deadly giant citinous insectoids began destroying small villages before any resistance could be mustered.

Were these two new enemies working together? Were The Hive and The Ancients under the guidance of the same mastermind? Was their even a mastermind at all? Or did the sudden world changes awaken both factions, disturbing their rest and rousing their hunger?

Only one thing is certain. The Mindassan lords must muster their forces and fight. Lest they too become just another casualty of history for future scholars to uncover.

Mercenaries of Mindassa expands the Crayne universe by introducing two new factions, both with a unique playstyle, and by adding a new game variant called the Mercenary Market.

The two new factions increases Crayne's replay ability and the Mercenary Market adds a new dimension to the strategy.

There will also be more solo play mission content: Defend the Spires and Expansion of the Hive

The Mercenary Market and both new factions can be played with all variants of Crayne, including solo-play, multiplayer and team play.

A battle-royale game for 1-X players in which you control a cow trying to avoid his own "products" while moving on the pasture. The last-standing cow wins.

Developed as a promotional game for the 25th edition of the Mucca Games convention ("Mucca" = "Cow" in Italian), Cattle Royale is meant to be played by a group of people using a sheet of the game board, a pen/pencil and a simple pawn for each player.

A specific player, the "herdsman", also rolls a die for every turn and announce the result to everyone else.

The game is not meant for commercial distribution.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The One Hundred Torii: Matcha Mini-Expansion
description:
A set of extra cards, activated with any 3 small tokens, provides unique benefits that might affect characters, tokens, or torii. Only a single card is available to each player, though, so the decisions vary from play to play.

Each player receives 2 random cards during setup and chooses 1 to keep during the game. This card may be activated by placing any 3 small tokens on it.

Card abilities vary from gaining more tokens in certain situations to hiring characters for fewer tokens.

The card's benefit happens every turn, as long as the card has 3 tokens on it. Tokens may be swapped, but if there are ever 5 matching small tokens (including on the Matcha card), they must be turned in for a large token.

Contains 10 cards.

-description from publisher

boardgame: The One Hundred Torii: Sake Mini-Expansion
description:
Different types of sake have full and empty sides. A sake card provides an immediate benefit, such as gaining an extra token or additional tiles. However, a special effect creates a challenge on the following turn.

Cards begin play with their full side face up. A player may drink (or claim) a sake card once per turn, which grants the benefit of the full side. However, the negative effect of the empty side must be taken on their nex turn.

Benefits include gaining tokens, hiring a character for fewer tokens, and having more tiles in hand. Negative effects include losing token, paying more to hire characters, and playing with a single tile in hand.

Contains 9 cards.

-description from publisher

Life of Farming is a game where you build your farm from the ground over three years.

It's an exciting game where you will have to make ends meet with your farming operation, and find out whether you have earned a profit by the end of the year. You might even perhaps be crowned farmer of the year.

It won’t be a joyride, as the turnable 2-wheeled tractor might make or break your fate and have an impact on your fortune and the weather throughout the year.

Will your harvest come to a standstill due to bad weather, or will your bull win a prize for best bull at the show, all this while struggling to keep track of fields, crops, animals, machinery and helpers.

You probably shouldn’t trust your neighbors. They might not be as friendly as they look. Will they prevent you from getting any more fields, or hinder you from growing corn? They might however also lend you a tractor because your own is in the workshop, the choices may not always be so true, but they are wise.

It’s always a good idea to keep a good relation with the trading stations, as they can supply you with important crops or animals and the machines needed for your farming operation when your money is limited. It is important that you are smart and run your farm with hard work to achieve your goals.

—description from the designer

Create bonuses for completing specific containers or the planter. During setup, 3 pairs of cards provide random bonuses for completely filling each depicted container (or 1 scorable row or column in the planter).

Bonuses include ignoring placement restrictions, turning a vegetable / fruit / tool into something else, or planting an extra vegetable.

Contains 7 cards.

Available in Pack Bard from Dice Tower 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Legendary Creatures: Wild Creature Pack Mini-Expansion
description:
Mix in more variety with 3 brand new wild creatures! Replace some of the creatures from the base game with some of the following:

- Medusa: Horrifying Visage allows you to take resources from other players.
- Hydra: Prismatic Scales allows you to play a round as if you are in possession of all the gifts.
- Quetzalcoatl: Serpent Rides for All allows you to move all familiars in a realm forward.

Contains 12 cards.

-description from publisher

Brief Border Wars 2 is the second set of four small operational level games on short border conflicts. The game system uses a card-driven system first introduced in Brief Border Wars that models the chaotic, stop-and-start nature of these impromptu wars. Four sets of exclusive rules reflect the peculiar nature of each conflict. Your sense of improvisation will be challenged as you explore four of the more interesting small wars fought in the early 20th century.

The four conflicts include:

1913: Second Balkan War (also known as “The Interallied War”) - Bulgaria vs Romania and Ottoman forces.
1919: The Seven-Day War (also known as “Teschen”): Czechoslovakia and Poland fight for the Duchy of Teschen (Cieszyn in Polish).
1939: The Nomonhan Incident (also known as “The Battle of Khalkin Gol”) - Japan and the Soviet Union clash in Mongolia.
1940: The Italo-Greek War (also known as “The Balkan Defiance”) - The first two months of the Italian invasion of Greece in the fall/winter of 1940.

These games are ideal short matches to finish off an evening of gaming, or fill a lunch hour.

Product information

Complexity: 4 out of 10
Solitaire Suitability: 5 out of 10
Time Scale: variable (1 day to 1 week per turn)
Map Scale: variable (area movement)
Unit Scale: variable (paramilitary “groups” to divisions)
Players: 1-2 (best with 2)
Playing Time: 1-2 hours each

-description from publisher's website
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: Limited edition of Trivial Pursuit celebrating 30 years of Knack magazine.

Eeach player starts with his three planes at his airport. The first player draws a flight order card and rolls tree times. If the combined results are 8 or higher, he has passed the runway and is in flight. When a plane is in flight the player rolls once each turn. This is done by each player for each plane.

The players complete their flight orders and return to their home airport. The first player to completes 3 orders wins the game.

Ready. Set. Rhyme. One-syllable. Two-syllable. Three syllable, more. Watch out for "spit" cards and the dreaded "alpaca." It's an all out battle of rhymes for all ages.

210 cards that players write as many rhyming words as they can. The player with the most wins the round.

The Armchair Quarterback is an enjoyable game of skill designed for two teams consisting of one or more players each. Object of the game-- as in professional football --is to outscore your opponent. This is done through analyzing the opposition and selecting the best play (either defensively or offensively) to counteract his play.

"All the thrills and excitement of REAL football" -Florida Gators Head Coach, Ray Graves

—description from the rulebook
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A card game to spice up your chess life. NOT CHESS is the ridiculous remix your dusty chess set has been waiting for. 
A highly strategic (and highly goofy) party card game expansion to chess. Whether you’re a chess fanatic, or never played a game in your life, you’re ready to play. Just add NOT CHESS to your own chess set, or buy the Ultimate Box for all the glory.

In NOT CHESS, players work in teams to eliminate all of the opponent's pieces. These pieces are divided in to two types - each team has 1 Queen and 15 Not-Queens. While Queens can move any number of spaces, and kill opponents at any time, Not-Queens can only move 1 space, and can only kill pieces if coupled with a NOT CHESS action card. These 90 wacky cards allow pieces to move and murder in increasingly ridiculous ways, from teleporting across the board, to moving multiple pieces in a 'battering ram' to knock out the opponent.

—description from the publisher

Billed as "an adaptation of Chinese Checkers" for only two players, 'Cheno' is an abstract space or alignment game in the vein of a simplified Halma, using only a 5x4 grid.

Two players, red and green, each have a back row of five counters on a 5x4 grid. On each turn, one counter can move one space forward or back, left or right, or jump an opponents' counter. The player who gets all their counters, in numerical order, on to the opponents' numbered spaces wins the game.

While the game board's art includes minimally stereotyped representations of Chinese mothers and children, flowers, and butterflies, the "Chinese" characters on one side are almost meaningless and were clearly chosen for aesthetics.

Gator Goal challenges you to knock out Gatorific's teeth with your soccer ball.

Launch the ball with your soccer ball launcher.

Gatorific's eyes spin and his flag pops up.

This small expansion adds 2 new idols to be played with the base game, both in the Alliance and Dominion modes.

Card 1 (Sailors): When played, place two canoes on top of this card for free. You can use them later on as part of a sea exploration.

Card 2 (Warriors): You can use stones as weapons during one battle as if they were obsidian or horns.

A party game for teams of two or more players.
Active player take a card turn and trying  to get a player to find an animal.
Depend of the position on the track, the chalenge can be a mimic, a sound or others chalenge.

The cover and the box are the boardgame. You roll a die, move along the track and complete the challenge.

The game was published in 500 copies.

—user summary

Letter Boxing is a word game featuring a 72-card deck of single- and double-letter cards. Players take turns placing new cards into a line in the middle of the table, and a player may fold (if they can’t go on) or challenge (if they think the opponent can’t complete a word). It’s a fast game with a mix of word play and risk management. A risky bluff at the right time could earn a huge reward!

Justice is a new game system in which the players will participate in a trial, one of you will be the defense attorney, the prosecutor will be in charge of bringing the accusation and the rest will form the court.

Each case proposes a unique enigma, but with multiple resolutions that each game will lead to a completely different trial.

After long days of illness, on January 21, 1907, the 36-year-old journalist and writer Bertram Fletcher Robinson is found dead at his home in London. Although the symptoms fit with typhoid fever and peritonitis, there are indications that the victim may have been poisoned. Scotland Yard narrows the investigation down to three suspicious people, although only one of them is finally brought to trial a few months later...

—description from the publisher (translated)

Justice es un nuevo sistema de juego en el que los jugadores participaréis en un juicio, uno de vosotros será el abogado defensor, el fiscal se encargará de llevar la acusación y el resto formará el tribunal.

Cada caso propone un único enigma, pero con múltiples resoluciones que cada partida llevarán a un juicio completamente distinto.

Tras largos días de enfermedad, el 21 de enero de 1907 aparece muerto en su domicilio en Londres el periodista y escritor Bertram Fletcher Robinson, de 36 años de edad. Aunque los síntomas encajan con unas fiebres tifoideas y una peritonitis, se hallan indicios de que la víctima pudo haber sido envenenada. Scotland Yard reduce la investigación a tres personas sospechosas, aunque finalmente sólo una de ellas es llevada a juicio unos meses después…

—description from the publisher

Justice is a new game system in which the players will participate in a trial, one of you will be the defense attorney, the prosecutor will be in charge of bringing the accusation and the rest will form the court.

Each case proposes a unique enigma, but with multiple resolutions that each game will lead to a completely different trial.

Mary Ann Nichols, Annie Chapman, Elizabeth Stride, Catherine Eddowes, Mary Jane Kelly and who knows how many more victims passed through the knives of the notorious killer known as Jack the Ripper. Many were the suspects but none of them could ever be tried. Can you do justice?

—description from the publisher (translated)

Justice es un nuevo sistema de juego en el que los jugadores participaréis en un juicio, uno de vosotros será el abogado defensor, el fiscal se encargará de llevar la acusación y el resto formará el tribunal.

Cada caso propone un único enigma, pero con múltiples resoluciones que cada partida llevarán a un juicio completamente distinto.

Mary Ann Nichols, Annie Chapman, Elizabeth Stride, Catherine Eddowes, Mary Jane Kelly y quién sabe cuántas víctimas más pasaron por los cuchillos del famoso asesino conocido como Jack el destripador. Muchos fueron los sospechosos pero nunca se pudo juzgar a ninguno de ellos. ¿Podréis hacer justicia?

—description from the publisher

Thỏ Tỉnh Táo (Awake Rabbit) is a party game that presents challenges with three levels of difficulty, requiring you to stay sharp and have quick reflexes!

Continuously count numbers when it is your turn, but with a twist: some numbers are assigned to specific actions. So instead of saying the number, you must remember correctly and do the action(s).

Containing all 38 miniatures, plus Bardsung cards, an adventure booklet, and battle maps, the Bardsung Pledge will test your Bardsung party’s mettle against a host of grievous foes and give them new areas to explore.

Miniatures -

boardgame: Oẳn Tù Tì: Vàng Xám Đại Chiến
description:
In Oẳn Tù Tì: Vàng Xám Đại Chiến (Rock-Paper-Scissors: Great War of Gray Gold), you want to capture as many cards as possible.

To start, divide the deck evenly among all players. Take turns one at a time flipping your top card onto your personal discard pile. If two revealed cards have the same background color and they meet a rock beats scissors beats paper beats rock condition, then slap the drum card in the center of play. If you're first and correct, you collect all of the cards on the table; if incorrect, give each other player one card.

When a player runs out of cards, the game ends and whoever has the most cards wins.

Childrens Scrabble in two game modes.
One is regular Scrabble with a smaller board and simplified bonus scoring.
The other mode has two rows of words opposite each other. Players takes turns laying three tiles on the words on their side of the board. When completed the word is scored by length and if the opponent has any letters in an unfinished word directly opposite, the player gains those letters to place immediately also.

On your turn, you spend resources like "drumsticks" (chicken thighs) to fight and capture mischievous monsters on the board. A key strategy is using the monsters you've already captured to help you take on the four giant super beasts for bigger rewards. At game's end of the game, whoever has accumulated the most points from their captures wins.

boardgame: Watches and Clocks of Hickory Docks
description:
The sun rises over the ocean, and the fog lifts from Hickory Docks. A bell chimes, and the workshop comes alive with the sound of tiny, scurrying feet. The clock mice are awake and already hard at work.

Watches and Clocks is a strategy board game of time-track management. Play a team of industrious mice that is building and marketing mechanical timepieces. Speed is of the essence...but build too quickly, and your clocks won't impress the collectors who value quality over quantity.

Take the mantle and see your masterpiece atop the newly built clock tower. Scurry to it! The world of innovation waits for no mouse.

—description from the publisher

Férias em Dupla (Double On Vacation!) is a card game as light and fun as travelling with best friends.

A fast game for all the family, from 2 to 4 players. It has two modes of playing: with or without the special effects.

Play a card on the table to join two halves of a beetle, you don't even need to match colors!

But if you do, gain one trip point if the car has both driver and a best friend riding shotgun. And gain two trip points if you match two Beetles correctly.

At the end, check your secret plans dealt on the beginning:
What is your luggage? Gain two points for each valid Beetle with it.
Do you have a driver or a friend riding shotgun? Gain one point for each valid Beetle with them.
What is your secret Beetle´s color? Gain one point for each valid Beetle on that color.

Have a nice trip!

—description from the designer

milliarium is an abstract-strategy board game for 2 players, competing to be the first to build a stack of 4 blocks of their color.

Contents
24 pieces, 1 board game, 1 Rule book.

Setup
Each player receive 12 components of the same color. Place the board in the center of the table.

Players turn
At every turn you can do one of the following steps:

     Place one component on the board.
     Move your component/stack that contains only your components, that already on the board,  1 tile in any direction in a straight line or diagonally.
     Step-up on 1  level on any component on the board.
     Step-down 1 level.
     Move your component on another component at the same level.


Wining  conditions
To be the first to build a stack of 4 blocks of your color, or when one of the players don't have any moves to do.

Notes

     You can't move from one border to another.
     You can only move one level up or down. Example: component in level 2, can step-up to level 3 but not to level 4.
     If you need, It is allowed to stack more than 4 level.

boardgame: Volcanic Bird Enemy and the Voiced Concern
description:
"Volcanic Bird Enemy and the Voiced Concern" is a fantasy strategy wargame intended for 1-2 players designed by Lil Ugly Mane.

As Dennis, you must wander the board searching for keys which will unlock your memory. You will spend your turns moving, exploring hidden counters, eating, and entering the Duck cube Phase. When all memories have been unlocked, Dennis may exit the game board triumphant.

As Officer Beatrice, kill Dennis before he remembers stuff- Dennis must never remember stuff. You will spend your turns moving, exploring hidden counters, combating enemy creatures, Hunting Dennis, and entering the Duck cube Phase. You Once Dennis has been killed, Officer Beatrice may exit the board triumphant, yet remorseful.

'The night air feels frigid and wet, leaving it's brine on any traveler that dare wonder the city's streets. The steam rising out of the piss filled sewer dances with the low hanging fog, cobbling together a viscous wall of brume that paints the street lamps as rows of dreary giants, disdainfully permitting your passage.

Rising up in the distance, beyond the borders of the city, across the ruins and swamps, sits a lonely tower. It's foreboding spire piercing the hazy, tattered velvet of night like a beak. Through the hiss of the streets you hear a faint quacking...

"Dennis...is that you?..."'
In Đường Đua Tài Chính (Financial Race), players experience the path of cash flow through income, spending, investing, and giving.

Zodiac Rush (Cuộc Đua Sao Chổi) lets you unleash your zodiac powers and embrace the chaos!

You race each other as zodiac characters to collect supply crates before a flaming comet hits and the city explodes. In more detail, you roll the dice, make strategic moves on the board, and engage in open drafting to collect powerful equipment cards. Also, make the best use of your zodiac character's special skills to steal crates, sabotage other players' moves, or otherwise gain a competitive advantage and increase your chances of winning.

description:
The Key is a cooperative PARTY GAME that brings together different generations through free-flowing creative expression.
The goal of this party game is to get to the presents locked in the sack.
The key to unlocking them is on the end of a spiral. The spiral holds many challenges. Players will have to act, imagine, draw, dance and connect through cooperative effort while the sand trickles into mystery.
No learning necessary, easy start. Suitable for large groups of children, younger teenagers and family events. Children that can read can play by themselves.

—description from the designer

Race to the Moon is an All-Fair combined toy/game (as with so many of their products), involving a beautiful wooden standee decorated with one each of red, yellow, green, blue, and orange rockets. Five red cardboard rockets, each labeled with a different number of miles, are attached to the standee by taut rubber bands.

Each player gets a colored ball, which they use to roll and hit the tails of all five rockets, hopefully releasing each rubber band and sending each rocket "shooting" up. In most cases, not every rocket will work.

After their turn, the player adds up the collected miles of the rockets they were able to activate. The winner is the first one who is able to "reach" a combined 239,000 miles - the distance between the Earth and the moon!

A very similar (and probably just as rare) board game from Sweden Raket Flug shows wonderfully similar graphics and game play. The objective and scoring differ but the graphics have similar rocket launching pads and somewhat military theme.

—user summary

A mad scientist has created a virus that turns anyone who catches it into a chicken. At first only a small group of people were infected, but gradually they multiplied so much that 80% of the world's human population became chickens, chickens packed with virus that will be released into the air when they're killed.

Bắn Gà Là Tạch is a shooting survival board game in which players each have only a small amount of food to fuel their efforts to both fight off other players and deal with chickens to be the last player standing. Players don't know the each other's location until they come close to one another. Everyone's location — as well as the location of loot boxes, traps and loot — is secret and known only to the game administrator.
In Yêu Nhầm F.A, players must use their resources to overcome challenges, despite one players secretly trying to sabotage their efforts.

Dozen War (Thập Nhị chiến) is a strategy board game, designed by Vietnameses. It takes a fantasy setting called Mativen, where there is a raging war between 12 heroes.

The game have 2 seperated game modes:
- "Đối đầu" (Engage): 2 players only, with game time between 5 to 15 minutes.
- "Chiến thần" (War gods): 3 to 4 players, either Free for all or Co-op, with game time between 90 to 150 minutes.

Dozen war is inspired by many classic games like Chess, Xiangqi, and MOBA video games like DotA.

description:
ERIS Seed of Discord is an auction game in which players take on the role of a Greek god in search of the prized apples of discord.

The auction deck will be made up of two types of cards: apples and calamities.

Apple cards contain the victory points that players will seek to acquire in order to win the game.

Apples vary in value from 3 to 7. These can be purchased through a Turn Order Until Pass auction. Players will have their God's power and their bribes to be able to bid on these apples.

Secondly, Calamity Cards. These will enter the auction not to be acquired, but to cause chaos, jealousy, and discord among the players.

The effects of calamity cards will be permanent until the players decide to pay the necessary bribes to remove them from the game.

Plan your strategy and bid to get the golden apples that will recognize you as The Fairest!

—description from the designer

This is a card game that can be played by 2 to 5 players for about 10 minutes, depending on the number of players.

The goal of the game is not to exceed the revealed Road Card number each round. The player who exceeds the Road Card number will receive a Speeding Ticket.

The game ends when the total selected Road Cards on the game are finished. The player with the highest number of Speeding Tickets loses the game.

—description from the designer

Curious basilica is a new opus from the Cartzzle range. Cartzzle is a new concept of board game containing a game, a work of art and puzzles.

The box holds a single Cartzzle, an endless one you can make with both sides of the 50 cards. Cartzzles are pictures you must first piece together by laying cards on top of each other (with or without the template, depending on your dedication). Then, start again, using challenges this time! Removing cards, flipping them, and laying them elsewhere will change the picture and also allow you to meet some challenges.

A 2-player game of skill played on a hexagonal board. A, distant descendant of the game of Go. But, in its feel, its volatility and its length it is closer to Chess.

The aim of the game is to have more tiles of your colour on the board than your opponent. You achieve this mostly by killing opposing tiles by depriving them of oxygen, and by making your own tiles hard to kill. All tiles have connections printed on them; as long as your tiles connect to an empty space, they have oxygen.

description:
Mix some or all of these double-edged actions, modifiers, and reactions into your Runes of Zun deck. As part of the theme, there's a reverse Boost-like card called "Reduce," which can be used to modify any card that allows Boost by lowering effects and even flipping them completely! Most of the other cards have something good and something bad, so by adding modifiers like Reverse and Boost, you can twist them into double-good and double-bad effects.

Manolete is very relaxed on the deckchair sunbathing and drinking lemonade. But look at him, he is getting redder and redder!! Don't leave him too long in the sun without sun cream or he will turn very red and jump off the sun lounger, and... Oh what a shame! Your swimsuit will fall off! You have to avoid pressing the button on the glass of lemonade too many times and run to protect yourself with the sunscreen before Manolete turns too red, jumps off the lounger and his swimsuit falls off. The player who makes Manolete jump from the lounger loses the round. The last player left after everyone else has been eliminated wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Manolete está muy relajado en la tumbona tomando el sol y bebiendo limonada. Pero mírale, ¡¡está poniéndose cada vez más rojo!! ¡No le dejes demasiado tiempo bajo el sol sin crema solar o se pondrá muy rojo y saltará de la tumbona, y…. ¡Ay qué vergüenza! ¡¡Se le caerá el bañador!! Tienes que evitar presionar el botón del vaso de la limonada demasiadas veces y correr a protegerse con el protector solar antes de que Manolete se ponga demasiado rojo, salte de la tumbona y se le caiga el bañador. El jugador que hace saltar a Manolete de la tumbona pierde la ronda. El último jugador que quede después de que todos los demás hayan sido eliminados gana.

Postcard Midway is a lightspeed fast, simple little Battle of Midway postcard wargame.

Although it shows 16 turns, early destruction of the Japanese Kido Butai carrier striking force can decide the game in the first 4, as historically. Fog of War has been fully worked in: the US has only a 50% codebreaking chance (of setting up a Point Luck or wherever ambush), and even with that only a further 50% chance of Yorktown being repaired in time for the battle.

Movement EXTREMELY simplified, and the design emphasis is on the 2 factors critical in the battle: intelligence and timing of air attacks. Airstrikes can be sent out (up to a 1-hex range) push-your-luck blindly or (push-your-luck vulnerably, if readied) held back on deck for a daylight turn, to wait to find out what Recon finds (as the Japanese fatally did, historically).

description:
A game of challenge for two players in which Doctor Who and the Legendary Legion join battle with the Alien Hordes from deep space. A coin is spun to decide who plays first. Each player receives a shuffled deck of their trump cards.

Each trump card has three characteristics, the first player reveals their card, announces a characteristic, then the second player announces the corresponding characteristic and both cards are revealed. The lower scoring trump card is forfeit to the other player and adds it to a win pile.

The player who captures all the trump cards wins.

Mord Couture: Claude Doedel - Master of Fashion and Haute Couture - is dead. His dead body is found in his villa with a High Heel stuck in his chest. Murder! His closest aquaintances sit together and try to find out who did it!
It's a 6 player murder mystery game were you can lie, betray, investigate and accuse! Included in the mix is a cook book to fully enjoy a perfect crime dinner evening.

Rusty Dagger: Solo Adventure Zine Game is a set of print and play rules that require very few additional items to start playing. All the features of the big box games have been packed in: hero creation, companions, Item list, leveling up, Enemy AI, and skirmish combat. Time for an Adventure!

You play a hero and follow an adventure booklet.
In this booklet you will alternate between narrative challenges (convincing a companion to follow you, avoiding a trap,...) and combat challenges.
The combat challenges are played on a tile that you can print or build yourself.
In these challenges you have to eliminate the enemies.
You must defeat the end boss to complete the adventure and gain experience.
With this experience you can upgrade your character and participate in more difficult adventures.

Gamebox with more than 800 crazy questions & tasks.
Draw, guess, impersonate and win!

4 par 4 is a game of logic and strategy for two, three and four players which mixes cleverly thinking, observation and anticipation. Accessible for everyone in the family, it has this little great thing of being playable in two complementary rounds, but that's not all...

Very quickly assimilated in a few minutes, the mechanism leads to animated and fun games. Winner or looser, you will only want one thing: Replay !

Purpose of the game: each player has to create the highest number of 4 different colors combinations in row, column, diagonal or square.

Hundreds of thousands gaming possibilities, games always different. Complexity and thinking are increasing, you should never give 1 more point to your opponent.
publishers: Les jeux de société de Nico

description:
Oh Bleep! is a bidding and trick-taking game based on Oh Hell! The game is played over 10 hands (or fewer, in higher player counts), with the number of tricks increasing each hand.

At the start of each hand, a trump is revealed and players bid on the number of tricks with will take. They will only score points if they make their bid exactly, but higher bids earn more points. The bids cannot add up to the number of tricks, so each hand will be either underbid or overbid. The card-play is similar to most trick-takers, but players must follow suit only on face cards. If a player cannot follow suit, they break the chain and the following players do not have to follow suit either. This leads to an emphasis on hand management and short-suiting.

Oh Bleep was designed by Ed White in the 1970s-80s and self-published in 1991. The self-published version includes 2 decks of playing cards with the face cards specially marked "follow suit", a custom score-pad, and a list of "discreet" expletives.

Battlebond is the first Magic: The Gathering expansion designed exclusively for two-headed giant booster draft! In this 254-card booster expansion, players form teams of two and tandem-draft cards two at a time from booster packs. After each team has drafted four booster packs' worth of cards, players will build two decks per team, and then face off in 2v2 matches.

Because Battlebond is designed for two-headed giant play, many of the mechanics in the set are tailor-made for team games. The "Assist" mechanic allows both players in a team to contribute mana towards a spell. The "Partner With" mechanic appears in pairs, and when a player plays half of a pair, they can have themselves or their teammate search their library for the other half. And the "Support" mechanic (returning from Oath of the Gatewatch) allows you to buff up your own creatures or your allies.

An easy to learn, but hard to master micro game in a very distinctive 1980s era CGA-style.

Numbered as Promo 01-04

Promo02 Pillager (dive or build): Take any 2 resources from the supply
Promo03 Taskmaster (build): After you complete a build action, take another build action immediately

A one card disaster hits Naturopolis.

Nessie is a one-card monster intent on wrecking your beautiful landscape, unless you connect enough rivers to neutralize the threat.


1-card expansion that mirrors Sprawlopolis: Wrecktar.

A one card assistant visits Naturopolis.

Points of Interest is a starting card that boosts your score if you surround it with the same type of block.

This is the first Point of Interest that introduces new rules. This rainbow is also a road, and it's a road that does not count towards your road tax. If you ever needed a little buff to help your score, Points of Interest is there for you, and this one can help prevent you from losing yet another point (actually 2 points in Naturopolis!).

This 1-card expansion (almost) mirrors Sprawlopolis: Points of Interest.

A set of 4 cards given to people whom pre-ordered the game at the Board & Dice website or whom will buy it at Spiel 2022.

They can create an asymmetric start: each player places their architect and merchant as indicated on the cards and gets the indicated bonuses.

When playing with the promo cards, the amount of starting gold for the 2nd/3rd/4th player is also reduced to 2/3/4 gold.

Full promo card rules have been posted in the Files section of this entry.

RAVENS is a modern twist on a classic German card game, Tod und Leben, for 2 to 6 players.

You'll have to move fast, very fast. The goal is simple, gather all the cards into your hand but getting there...that's quite the challenge. Forget what you know about fighting fair and time your attacks by counting other Raider's cards.

In clockwise order, players lay the top card of their face-down hand in the center of the field. All players must watch carefully for certain rune combinations/color series and slap the pile first to lay claim to it. Each weapon card challenges the gathering cards in the playing field, the strength of each indicated by hash marks along the edge of the card. The player following a weapon card may choose to meet the challenge or forfeit the plunder.

Watch carefully for the Ravens, they hold the fate of the game. The Blue Raven is an instant slap condition whereas an ill-timed claim of the Red Raven may leave you amongst the ranks of the dead.

A player who has emptied their hand has become a Valkyrie and they may no longer challenge or add to the field. Valkyrie may at any time slap combinations. These cards enter Valhalla and are no longer in play thereby thinning the deck and raising the stakes. If the Valkyrie is able to gather both Ravens into their hand, they have won the game.

Will you dominate your foes by implementing Weapons of War or join the ranks of the Valkyrie as harbingers of Ragnarök? Be cautious of strangers, a new Raider may join the fray at any given opportunity.

-description from designer

The game is a kind of memory. The difference to ordinary memory is that you have a deck of cards, four of which you hold in your hand. At the beginning of his turn, the player plays a card from his hand and then has to find the right card from the face-down cards on the table. If he succeeds, he can continue playing. At the end of his turn, he refills his hand to four. Whoever runs out of cards first is the winner.

The game about clash at Ras al-Unuf at 2011, one of the very first battles of First Libyan Civil War (February-October 2011). Several scenarios covers every stage of this 6 days long clash, including initial loyalist's assault, siege, final assault and rebel counterattack. There is also a scenario that covers the entire six days of battle.

The game was published in the 46 issue of the Tactics and Strategy magazine. It contains all the counters and board, but the ownership of any boxed game of the Battles of XXI Century is required.

It is first "African" game published in this system. It uses company-platoon scale, although some units are pictured as battalions. Several special rules are applied to show special conditions of this war.

Oneover is a game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the spaces (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal grid of hexagons (board).

				
				
					DefinitionsA group is a stone along with all stones one can reach from it through a series of steps onto adjacent stones of the same color.

The value of a group is one divided by the number of stones in it. For example, the value of a 5-stone group is 0.2.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty cell.

When the board is full, the player with the lower score wins. Your score is the sum of the values of your groups. If there is a tie, whoever has fewer groups wins. If the tie persists, White wins.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

				
				
					NotesStrategy revolves around having as few groups as possible and having group values as uniform as possible. It is possible to follow these general principles without much actual arithmetic.

-description from designer

Zon: Cosmic Warfare places you in control of an alien race in a vast political struggle, competing for success with other races. You will explore the Zon cosmos, upgrade your starting deck, and craft spaceships with which to conquer, defend, and fight. There are many different paths you choose to win, so you will need to adapt to any situation that can come your way while exploring the vastness of space.

Zon: Cosmic Warfare is based in Draconem Ignem Comics' Zon Universe with many alien races and political struggles.

The Nanga Parbat: Missions Pack is an expansion pack for Nanga Parbat.  The pack includes a total of 14 cards: 11 game cards (5 mission cards + 6 reward cards) and 3 rules cards.

At the start of the game, the 5 mission cards are laid out in a row, showing the different missions for the game.  Paired with each mission is a reward card. The first player to accomplish a mission earns the reward card. Since the mission cards are double-sided, there is a wide variety of mission combinations.  Plus, with 6 different rewards and only 5 used per game, the reward combinations also differ from game to game.

More specifically, each mission shows 3 hikers in a particular pattern in a region.  To accomplish the mission, a player must have their own hikers in the exact combination shown in one of the six regions.

The reward cards include an immediate point reward and/or a special power. The front side of the cards show the rewards using icons, while the backside describe the rewards in text.

-description from designer

description:
A crime has been committed! One of the players has witnessed the crime and has seen the perpetrator. Now the other players must ask the witness questions to try and identify the person responsible through their sketch drawing!

—description from the publisher

The Battle of Brovary was a clash during the Kyiv offensive of the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine for control of the suburb of Brovary, to the east of the capital city of Ukraine, Kyiv. Russian forces advanced west from southern Chernihiv Oblast and were engaged by Ukrainian forces. Control of the suburb was contested until Russian forces withdrew on 2 April 2022.

Brovary 2022 is a company scale game about the first part of the battle, covering three days long Russian assault on Brovary, led by the Russian 90th Guards Tank Division. It is a part of the Battles of XXI Century (B-XXI) system, highly detailed company scale system dedicated for the modern combat.

Game was published in the 46 issue of the Tactics and Strategy magazine, in both Polish and English languages. A copy of any boxed game of B-XXI system is required.

description: Kisfröccs is an accessory to the fast-paced and fun Hungarian-developed party game. In addition to new characters and sucker cards, players can also gain special cup abilities.

Travel through Croatia with this fast, simple and unique card game/souvenir.

You will need luck, tactics and bidding to win the game.

All players simultaneously roll their dice and perform actions with them:


     Use money to pay for a new trip at a local travel agency. If multiple players want the same trip, resolve it by bidding.
     Collect Baggage: The player must match the numbers on their travel cards with the totals on their dice to complete the journey.
     Job: Place dice at the workplace and get money for further travel.


All completed travel cards go to the field of memories where hearts (victory points) and attractions are collected.
Attractions bring souvenirs that players can use for special moves or hearts at the end of the game.

The player with the most victory points wins!

—description from the designer

Mentsd meg a bolygót ("Save the Planet") is an environmental and ecological family board game in which you can learn how to become an active participant in the fight to save the Earth.
The mission cards ask the players to replace the polluting power plants on the world map with renewable energy plants.
The goal is to save our beautiful planet, and we can't help that better than by making children think.
Players must answer questions about energy, nature or the environment.
Event cards can not only help players, they can also cause unpleasant surprises and turn the tide of the game.
Example of questions for the cards:

     how much water do we lose on average per day due to a dripping tap? - 4 liters, or 9 liters, or 10 liters? (9 liters is the correct answer, which can be seen underlined on the card)
     which tree's fruit is edible? - chestnut, sweet chestnut, oak, beech, birch? (chestnut can be seen underlined)
     which of the following plants can help with orientation? - wild violet, nettle, moss, lace bush? (Moss)


Example of a mission card task:

     At least half of the power plants in Asia should be your wind farms!


A very beautiful, detailed world map board game for children and adults, which provides everyone with obtainable knowledge, new information, a new way of thinking, and adequate motivation to minimize the ecological footprint.

In Mysthea Solo Expansion you will find yourself facing the very skilled former Champion Maliath who, after years of endless travels through the Mysthea Mist, now is back, stronger and more stubborn than before. Maliath's forces rose like an unstoppable plague, driven by unfathomable hatred for everything our people have touched.

Maliath is a highly skilled strategist and a relentless fighter who has no intention of leaving you alone while you fight for the control of the five unexplored floating islands.
It will get in the way of your plans in many ways, compromising island attunements, interfering in fights with monsters, and even directly starting battles against you.

The solo mode expansion of this area control and hand-management eurogame will make you meet an incredible new opponent, but beware, she will be an actual player, just like you, and she’s not easily fooled!

—description from the publisher

In Icaion Solo Expansion, you will face a duel to the death against Velkhorne, one of the Organization's worst Grand Masters that has returned to deprive everyone of Qoam. 
A stubborn and insatiable annihilator, adorned with armor that guarantees effortless survival: a match against him is a truly intense and difficult game. He will find everything you desire and destroy it.

Your hunt for treasures, Qoam and artifacts from ancient times is now made even more challenging by the presence of Velkhorne. Wherever it goes affects the surrounding lands, taking crucial resources away from you and spreading corruption.
You will have to use all your skill to accomplish your mission, handling both him and the fearsome Colossus.

The solo mode expansion of this area control and hand-management eurogame will make you meet an incredible new opponent, but beware, he will be an actual player, just like you, and he’s not easily fooled!

—description from the publisher

The United World Government has almost finished building their Warhead Antimatter Response (W.A.R) facility. Your Heist Crew cannot let that happen. Are you up for the challenge?

Grand Theft Antimatter is a groundbreaking printable escape room game that brings all the fun of escape rooms into your own home. The game is designed to be played by teens and families at their own pace, in their own space.

Players will take on the role of one of the members of the Heist Crew, and work together to steal the antimatter before the W.A.R facility goes online. Gameplay is centered on a series of puzzle cards that players must solve. Each card solved progresses the story and brings players closer to completing the heist.

Game setup is quick and spoiler-free, and hints are handled by an online hint system that players access on their smartphones. This means that no game master is required to supervise the experience, so you can join the game alongside your players.

The Warhead Antimatter Response facility is nearly online... do you accept the mission?

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 1: Masters of the Universe Faction
description:
The Warrior Goddess Teela and the mysterious Sorceress appear on the battlefield. The magical abilities of the Masters of the Universe are growing in strength!

From now on, the enemies will have to face new spells and force fields set in the arena. By the power of Grayskull! The forces of Evil will be overcome!

EXPANSION PACK INCLUDES:
1x set of  additional rules
1x manual
2x character minis
3x terrain accessories
16x cards
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The dreadful Onibi has never been more dangerous… He has set foot in the Circle of Spirits and is ferociously attacking the Sacred Tree, while his flames threaten all living things in the forest. But the Spirits of Nature aren’t fighting alone: Sanki has sent small beings of light, the Kodama, to help them face this growing threat.

Play as one of the four Spirits of Nature and make the most of the Kodama’s help to save the Sacred Tree!

A NEW ACTION:
You can now use the flowers from your Help Line, your Forest and your victory tiles to perform the new action: Call a Kodama.

Thanks to this new action you can, each turn, buy one (and only one) Kodama card which is placed on your personal deck, like a Guardian Animal card.

These Kodama cards, in addition to providing you with items like Guardian Animals do, provide you with a Kodama symbol. At the start of your Action phase, add the number of Kodama symbols on your Help Line and your Forest:
• With 2 visible Kodama symbols, you can take 1 Fragment tile. Put it next to your Forest.
• With 3 visible Kodama symbols, you can take 1 Element tile. Put it next to your Forest.
• With 4 visible Kodama symbols, you can perform an additional basic Action during this turn.

And icing on the cake, you increase the corresponding victory condition from 13 to 15 by emptying a pile of Kodama cards. A good way to slow down your opponents!

NEW GUARDIAN ANIMAL
Two types of Guardian Animal complete your teams:
• 5 new Level 2 Guardian Animal cards allow to perform twice the same basic Action shown on the card, when you normally have to perform two different actions.
• 5 new Level 3 Guardian Animal allow you to perform a Bonus Action of the type shown on the card, in addition to your basic Actions.

NEW PROTECTIVE TREES RELATED TO KODAMA
Six new types of Trees are available, these are the Trees related to Kodama. You can only plant one of each type, so a maximum of 6 different new Trees.

ONIBI ENTERS THE CIRCLE OF SPIRITS
Each time you buy a Kodama card, Onibi retaliates and moves forward on the Circle of Spirits. For each Spirit he jumps, you must add a Fire tile of value 2 to the center of the Circle of Spirits.

And that’s not everything, there is also a Solo mode!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 2: Legends of Preternia
description:
The combined powers of the Cosmic Key and the Central Tower allow our heroes to travel back in time to Preternia! During their travels, they will experience a new battleground – The Desolation – multiple terrain pieces, such as swamps and lava pits, and a brand-new challenge – The Hunt. A mighty Tyrantisaurus is lurking in the jungles of this prehistoric world; our brave heroes and not-so-brave villains sooner or later will have to face this monstrosity. Who will be victorious? There is only one way to find out!

—description from the publisher
