description:
Traffic congestion is a high point. Ease your commuters’ anguish with new roads to keep the city moving.

In Roadwork,  each player will get one of the four cards in this expansion. If they don’t play the card before the end of the game, it will cost them points. The catch, of course, is that the card must integrate seamlessly with roads in your city in order to be placed, which is rarely going to be as easy to accomplish as it sounds. You can also scale up the difficulty, giving more of these cards to the group playing.

Mrs. Lansdowne has been murdered! And at her very own morning tea party, too! One of the players around the table must be the guilty one!

Elevenses: The Guilty Party is a card game that has each player setting their tea table with delicious foods, all the while casting suspicion cubes on other players in efforts to assist the constable in their arrest for murder!

With each turn, players will choose to either snack on a card and get its special ability, adding it to their tableaux, or rearrange their tableaux to set up for future turns. The player with the best meal when the game ends gets the most suspicion cast on their fellow guests. The least suspicious innocent guest wins the game - unless the murderer got away with it, then the murderer is victorious.

The fairy godmother (Die gute Fee) is a postcard-sized promotional giveaway for the game Magic Mountain to cut out.

Once per turn, the active player can place the fairy on any intersection that is not the same color as the drawn wisp. In this way, the will-o'-the-wisp can be directed more easily.

boardgame: Benzene (fan expansion for Age of Steam)
description:
In this expansion map, the players are researchers in a chemical laboratory, creating Benzene-based aromatic compounds.  A research phase is added before cube production in which players can exchange cubes they have delivered to move up on a research track.

The track includes spaces for gaining additional cubes, cash, points, or unlocking certain locomotive levels.

An alien parasite has latched onto ROVE and is crawling all over your modules. You’ll need to figure out how to deal with this new challenge in order to complete your mission.

ROVE: Crawler is a single Module card expansion. Crawler is a new module that adds a neat little twist to the game: It has a new restriction (it must always cover a single module), its own ability and movement, and a special sort of passive ability. It's sometimes in your way and sometimes useful.

Dragons vs Unicorns is an dual randomized game (dice and cards) where you can build dragon and/or unicorn armies.

You are able to take 5 actions per turn, which starts as either drawing cards from your respective pile or playing connecting cards. Each creature has either a 3-card or a 5-card version which are different colors that will connect seamlessly.

Once your army starts taking shape you can choose to Battle which will be the only action you can take on that turn. Once you have started a battle you must continue until either players attacked creature is defeated.

Battles consist of rolling dice to determine the amount of hit points weighted against your opponent. When individual cards are defeated they are flipped over. Once all cards of the either creature are flipped over, the opposing player gets those cards as points.

The game ends when either player can not build or battle and their card pile has been depleted. At the end of the game the players combine the defeated points earned and points of their surviving creatures and whoever has the most points wins.

boardgame: Tainted Grail: Kings of Ruin (Gamefound Edition)
description:
Tainted Grail: Kings of Ruin (Gamefound Edition) included all the stretch goals packaged into the core game's box. Stretch goals will not be part of the retail edition. Any stretch goals released at retail will be individually packaged as opposed to a "stretch goals box".

Includes:
Echoes of Ruin Expansion - Relive the dark and turbulent past of your character, earn personal achievements, an unlock unique feats, actions, and story-releated combat/diplomacy cards.

In the expansion, Echoes of Ruin, each Character receives 9 small personal achievements they may complete at any point in the game.

Whenever you mark an achievement on your sheet, you unlock an entry in the Book of Memories that shows an important scene from the Character’s past, and grants a related bonus card - either a special one-time Feat, or a custom Combat / Diplomacy card. 
 Marking all these achievements unlocks a small Personal Side Quest that leads to the final memory, and the final reward: a brand new second Character Action! 
 Note that these memories and side quests are related to things our Characters did in the past and exist independently from the rich personal storyline every Character already has in the base game. 
Roguelite Mode Into the Mists
Places From Beyind Expansion
Lost Chapter: Quest for Igraime's Tomb
Lost Chapter: The Knight's Wager
Wyrdform Characters
Standees Wyrd Encounters
Companions
Variety Packs
Trackers, Plastic Color Reveal, Notebook

Layers+(plus) is a pattern-matching game that requires players to turn, flip, and overlap a set of five layers to complete the pattern shown on a mission card. The faster you complete the pattern, the higher your score will be.

With a variety of levels, you can try to match different patterns on the mission cards using 2 layers up to 5 layers. Each layer has a unique shape and the colors are different in the front and back, so you must be careful as you overlap different layers into one pattern. Complete the pattern quicker than the others to gain more points and win the game!

Layers+ is an upgraded version of Layers. It is more compact than the original Layers and includes additional components. 
The rules were also revised - missions using two layers added -  and easier and more dynamic gameplay would be available.

-description from publisher

In this bilingual card game, you learn numbers, colors, and phrases in Norwegian or Icelandic - or both. The game is primarily for beginner learners, but phrases are beginner to intermediate.

Cards are played on matching numbers or colors, and the Norwegian/Icelandic words must be read aloud as they are played. Ville and Rosa cards spice things up with player interaction, double-digit numbers, and super simple arithmetic! The goal is to get rid of all of your cards first and declare "Null Kort" or "Núll Spil" ("zero cards").

Fire in the hole! is a brand new social deduction game. Players take on the role of animal soldiers. But there are spies among us! The spies will try to interfere with our mission.

The way to complete the mission beyond the spies' disturbance is an unshakable body and mind and precise finger power!

Complete the mission by flicking the weapon assigned by the commander to the enemy's bunker exactly!

If 3 out of 5 missions are successful, the soldiers win. Otherwise, the spy wins.

—description from the publisher

Hunt together! By the power of the God Key, the Blazkowicz twins have opened the doors to the multiverse. Veterans from World War II, Kreisau Circle Legends and the American Revolution can hunt for the Nazis together! Wolfenstein: All-Stars offers brand new heroes, a map generator and plastic tokens.

boardgame: Wolfenstein: The Board Game – Hans Grösse
description: Guten Tag! Are you ready for another encounter with Hans Grosse? The mighty enforcer, Wilhelm 'Deathshead' Strasse's personal bodyguard is armed with two chain guns and will be waiting for you! Can you win and send him running to his Mutti?

This promo card was included in Pegasus Spiele Magazin Ringbote #3/2022.
Rules:
"Before the game starts, shuffle Bluey into the pile of hunting cards. Bluey is a noble person.
She has no special function, but will earn you 4 blood points if you hunt her successfully. However, in return she reduces your
speed by -1."

A clever quiz game with fun questions where the first letter of the two nouns are twisted. Identify the correct question and give the answer.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Rally Point Volume #19: ASL Starter Kit Special Study III
description:
Rally Point Volume 19: Starter Kit Special Study III is an ASL/ASL Starter Kit Compatible scenario pack that requires Starter Kits 1-4, Starter Kit Expansion Pack #1 and Starter Kit Bonus Pack #1. Each scenario is fully compatible with full ASL rules and ASL Starter Kit Rules. These 10 scenarios were playtested with consideration for both rules sets and are presented in the Schwerpunkt easy-to-read format on high-quality, collector grade cardstock.

RPT181 Changsha Chainsaw: CHANGSHA, China, 4 January 1942: A company sized element of the 10th Chinese Army with a supporting 70mm infantry gun defends the board k village against the 3rd Japanese Division which is attacking with 11 squads and a Type 92B light tank. Brook White designed this 5.5 turn scenario.

RPT182 The Horiuchi Hustle: LANGOAN, Celebes Island, The Netherlands East Indies, 11 January 1942: The Japanese 1st Company, 1st Yokosuka Special Naval Landing Force, 41st Brigade attacks to capture an airfield defended by three platoons of Dutch infantry with a 40mm AA gun from Tactical Command Kakas, Koninklijk Nederlands Indische Leger. This scenario uses boards q and t. Game Length is 4.5 turns.

RPT183 The Gotanda Gambit: BAKRI, Malaya, 18 January 1942: Three Type 95 Ha-Go tanks and a company of Japanese infantry from the Gotanda Tank Company and 5th Konoye Regiment, Imperial Guards Division attack to eliminate two Australian platoons of Company C, 2/29th Battalion, 45th Indian Infantry Brigade infantry and a 2-pounder AT gun that are defending a jungle road and village. This 4.5 turn scenario uses boards m and o.

RPT184 Cadets at the Crossroads: TOLHEK, Java, The Netherlands East Indies, 6 March 1942: A company of Japanese infantry from the Wakamatsu Battle Group attack a reinforced Dutch platoon from the Paardekoper “Improvised” Company, Koninklijk Nederlands Indische Leger (KNIL) that is defending a road junction and then launches a small Dutch counterattack. This 4.5 turn scenario is set on boards i and m.

RPT185 Sumatra Skirmish: PORSEA, Sumatra, The Netherlands East Indies, 14 March 1942: Group Van de Ploeg (consisting of two infantry platoons with two supporting Marmon Harrington armored cars) attempts to rescue the Porsea Garrison which is about to be surrounded by a Japanese infantry company from the Kokushi Detachment, Imperial Guards Division. Will Lieutenant Van de Ploeg rescue the garrison or will he have to stay and fight? This 5.5 turn scenario uses boards o and q.

RPT186 The Nut Lane Blockhouse: NAMUR, Kwajalein Atoll, The Marshall Islands, 2 February 1944: Medal of Honor recipient LTC Aquilla J. Dyess leads the 1st Battalion Landing Team and Company L, 24th Marines and Company B, 4th Tank Battalion, 4th Marine Division in an attack to clear fortified buildings defended by the Japanese 61st Guard Force Dispatched Force. This scenario uses board l. Game length is 4.5 turns.

RPT187 Herman’s Heater: Near LIMON, Leyte, Phillippines, 7 December 1944: Elements of 3rd Battalion, 127th Infantry Regiment, 32nd Infantry Division attacks with 19 squads to clear a ridge defended by a company of infantry from the Japanese 1st Division. Mike Augustine designed this 4.5 turn, action-packed demo charge and flamethrower thriller that uses boards m and n.

RPT188 Plugged By Vlug: South of LIMON, Leyte, Phillippines, 15 December 1944: Mike Augustine designed this 5-turn gem that honors Medal of Honor recipient PFC Dirk Vlug and uses boards n and o. It features a company of Japanese infantry from the 1st Division attacking with five supporting Type 95 Ha-Go tanks to clear a village defended by the American 1st Battalion, 126th Infantry Regiment, 32nd Infantry Division.

RPT189 Manila Madness: MANILA, near the Tondo District, 5 February 1945: The Tampa Group’s own Philippine scout, Hugh Downing designed this 5.5 turn gem. It features elements of the 145th Infantry, 37th Division (with two supporting M8 armored cars) attacking to clear the Japanese 1st Independent Naval Battalion from the town on boards j and k.

RPT190 Tenacious Takikawa: MUTANCHIANG, Manchuria, China, 16 January 1943: Mike Augustine designed this 4.5-turn action that features Russians from the 1st Red Banner Army attacking with two companies of infantry (and three supporting T-34 tanks) to clear elements of the 278th Infantry Regiment and the Takikawa Battalion from multi-hex stone buildings on boards m and u.
family: Series: Advanced Squad Leader Starter Kit

description: Too young to read? No problem! Picture Charades makes it easy for the wee ones to act out. The best part - the pictures are open to interpretation, and it's up to the children to decide what they are supposed to be acting. Lets kids develop critical thinking skills while having oodles of fun!

boardgame: The Forest of Fyri: Perilous Pursuit
description:
Fyri is where all those forest creatures told around campfire and scary tales told to naughty children come from. And no one needed to know they were real until 6 strangers from all around the world decided to bet their fate in the mythical forest of Fyri.

The Forest of Fyri: Perilous Pursuit is about hunting monsters and collecting bounties in the forest, then turning them in for victory points. Every player has up to two Move actions. To start your turn, exit toward a certain rune of their choice from the tile they stand on, then onto a new tile in the direction of matching rune. (1st Move) There are 2 types of tiles they will land and interact with. First type is Forest tiles, which means the player will have to flip an Encounter card to face a monster in the forest. Second type is Location tiles, which the player will skip the Encounter card and interact with the specific tile. If the player flips an Encounter card with no monsters or decides not to interact with the tile, they can move onto another tile and encounter/interact with it instead (2nd Move).

There will be repeated death & revival throughout the gameplay, but it is done without harsh consequence as the game is created for every level of players. When a player dies, their next turn is automatically moved to the last place in the turn order. And by rolling the Champion die, the player will find out where their champion will respawn and how much health they will be able to restore.

The goal of the game is to turn in certain number of bounties to the town before other champions do, racking up Victory points. The first player to reach a set amount of Victory points will win the game. How you interact between the forest and the town will be the key to victory in The Forest of Fyri.

—description from the publisher

KLAW: Were-Wars is a card-and-dice game of conquest and combat set amidst a secret, ancient struggle between supernormal animal spirits and their human Dizhi hosts since pre-recorded time. The goal of the game is simple: Become the most powerful Dizhi, eliminating all contenders.

Based on the international best-selling graphic novel series by Antoine Ozanam, Joel Jurion, and Yoann Guille, KLAW is an exciting world of noble heroes, despicable villains, unpredictable intrigue, and action-packed combat -- the perfect setting for an exciting card game!

Featuring a vast and diverse array of unique human Host characters and two dozen interchangeable animal Totems that can be combined almost indefinitely, the possible number of Dizhi power configurations is in the millions.

KLAW: Were-Wars has been designed to be incredibly easy to learn, but with so many unique combinations of powers, defenses, and game-changing actions available, every session is guaranteed to be unique and unpredictable. There's no such thing as an unbeatable hand or strategy, and the tables can turn 180 degrees in an instant, pulling a player from last place into the lead with a lucky dice roll or draw from the deck. Stay sharp and be prepared to avoid having the rug pulled out from under you!

—description from the publisher

On a board consisting of 32 tiles, each player has 4 to 7 sheep in 3 barns near 3 corners and wants to bring them back to the barn which is near the 4th corner. You move 1 space orthogonally or diagonally onto a green space. When you leave a tile, flip it to the other (brown) side. You cannot stop on brown tiles but jump over it in a straight line. If you flip to the brown side and this side shows a salad or watering can, you are entitled to jump once a sheep or a green tile resp. to flip back a tile to the green side. Winner is the player who first gets all his sheep in the barn.

Each player chooses a balloon, takes it and inflates it.
     Each player has a maximum of one minute to inflate their balloon.
     Once the balloon is inflated, the player must put it on the table.
     If the balloon is not inflated after one minute, puncture it.
     If the balloon is not on the table after one minute, puncture it.
     The player whose balloon is the last to deflate completely wins the game.


—description from the designer

Strike It Rich is a new board game that is played in the classic or traditional format of players taking turns rolling the dice to travel around a game board. The game is great family entertainment that is easy to learn and play, is fast paced, exciting, educational, and has fun player inaction. Each time around the board is equivalent to a year. Each player will get a chance each year for ten years to experience the uncertainty of either “Striking It Rich or having a setback on their quest to accumulate their fortunes. The gameboard has 4 unique sides that the players get a yearly chance to accumulate their wealth by how large of a Wage they will collect, what adventures they get into at the Family section, getting a chance on the Investment side of the board where you can test your risk/reward appetite, and then to find out what type of escapades you run into on the Lifestyle portion of the board.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Stranger Things: Escape Game – Salvate Will & Undici
description:
A card game that simulates an escape room, full of references to the Stranger Things series! Following the game stages, you will explore the city of Hawkins immersing yourself totally in a fantastic adventure !!

—description from the publisher (translated)

A promo resin miniature included with Ashes Magazine: Essen Special Edition #6 from Ludus Magnus Studio.  The miniature is a resin model that comes in 6 pieces and will need to be glued together using a cyanoacrylate glue or similar.  In the magazine are detailed assembly instructions.

Along with an enemy card to use Helblindi as an enemy in Runar, it also comes with a card to use him as an enemy in Nova Aetas Renaissance, and the cards to use him as a mage in Black Rose Wars.

Contents:
1x Mage Card, 3x Personal Spell Cards (to play Helblindi as Mage in Black Rose Wars)
1x Enemy Card, 1x Enemy Draft Card (to play Helblindi, the Cryomancer as Enemy in Nova Aetas Renaissance)
1x Enemy Card (to play Helblindi as Enemy in Runar)

Lead your steampunk dwarf clan to mine the most mysterious ore in the Mountain. Electricity strangely doesn't work in this Mountain; the dwarves want the ore to build machines and weapons to take back their previous home. More than a deck-builder, you build mines for one-time effects, you send your dwarves into the mountain to dig tunnels, upgrade structures, and activate powerful effects. Into the Mountain is packed with meaningful decisions; in fact every card has 3 functions: you can spend them as resources, play them for abilities, and even permanently build them as a construct.

There are many paths to victory in this competitive strategy game. During the game you pledge to a clan to receive the clan banner, and the clan leader grants you immediate and lasting benefits. The decisions and play flow in this high powered deck builder.

—description from the designer

Pandas and Trainers team up to perform tricks and score points in this silly, exciting kids' game!

Please Don't Feed the Pandas game challenges players to toss and catch in a panda-feeding frenzy. The Pandas are hungry, but they need to work for their "food"! One teammate, the Panda, wears a panda mask with an open mouth to catch the balls while another, the Trainer, "feeds" them. Draw a Trick Card to determine how to feed your Panda (off a bounce, over the shoulder, etc.), then start tossing!

Things get wild when Trainers have to spin, close their eyes or perform an all-play Trick Throwdown. The first team to score 5 points wins! Please Feed the Pandas is action-packed fun with a side of silliness that's perfect for a party or playtime with 4 to 8 kids, 7 years old and up.

Ratten Reich: The Board Game is a physical tabletop companion to the upcoming RTS wargame, Ratten Reich. It is a grim and unforgiving world, where war has continued for many years. The war is fought between rats, mice, roaches and lizards alike in their never-ending struggle for power.

Your task is to lead your faction to victory. Whatever country you play as, you have to muster supplies and equip your armies, lead them into battle, face losses and recoup them.

In the Ratten Reich board game, your task is to lead your faction to victory. Whatever country you play as, you have to muster and equip your armies, lead them into battle, lose them and collect new ones. Under your command, three infantry armies are given, each of which can fit five infantry tokens. Military tokens are tied to military cards, so you can equip your armies with both elite units and simple cannon fodder. And so, the era of great conquest begins.

Naval battles are a common feature of imperialist wars and will therefore play a significant part in Ratten Reich: The Board Game. Large empires are typically surrounded by seas and ocean, allowing the transfer and logistics of military forces to travel from one continent to another. This opportunity pushes the development of military shipbuilding.

In this tabletop experience, each player is given one fleet figure to start. Choose to reinforce your fleet with heavy battleships and/or ironclads, intercept the enemy at sea, blockade ports or assist with naval bombardment. You can even execute an amphibious assault on your enemies.

Your army gains an attack bonus if it lands with naval support so ensure you utilize the whole of your forces! A fleet is also needed to transfer your armies to another continent.

Game pads are divided into two main types: the Main Player pads and the Autocrat Trade Military pad. The regular Player tablet allows you to design armies as you wish. The Autocrat tablet allows the player to create specialized armies and provides an opportunity to regulate the resource market by setting prices for mercenary armies.

Tokens are the same for all players except the Autocrat. Players can assemble armies themselves as they please. Meanwhile, the Autocrat collects and sells mercenaries whilst regulating the game market.

There is an additional battle pad that creates positive or negative conditions for the combatants. When the offensive player rolls the dice at the start of battle, the number rolled correlates to one of the six possible conditions that affect combat.

You may be lucky with fair weather conditions or you may be afflicted with corruption in your army, which will give certain disadvantages in the battle.

Tokens are an important part of the game. All resources, buildings, troops, and finances are considered tokens. Tokens in Ratten Reich: The Board Game are divided into two groups: military and economic.

Military tokens allow you to build armies on your side of the board and dually display the territories you have conquered. Economic tokens allow you to place buildings on the map, establish trade routes, conduct diplomacy, and arrange “meanness.”

Cards are ‘paper helpers’ in building your empire. Ratten Reich: The Board Game gives you the opportunity to build an economy, create your own armies, and create ‘meanness’ at your enemy’s doorstep.

Your faction’s main power source is the army. At your discretion, assemble your army by selecting from several types of infantry and vehicles - all of which will be under your control. If you are lacking in resources, gather a cheap army on a whim to capture colonies or to defend your cities. Perhaps you might prefer to invest in high-quality troops with impressive armor and equipment to best represent your faction.

To dominate the sea, build and tend to your fleet. Whether you play aggressively or systematically, develop and evolve your troops to master the board. You will only be dealt a few moves of peace and tranquility during the game; when these moves end, no one will have the right to stop you.

The economy is a vital part of the game. Without a reliable economy, you simply won’t be able to afford an army to face off against your enemies. Ratten Reich: The Board Game will offer you a standard set of building cards that includes: a bunker, factory, munitions factory, secret society, and market. Each building has a unique purpose.

Be wise with the limited resources you have! Construction will help create a defensive basis for negotiations, a stable economy, and eventual peace talks. You can play aggressively by contributing all of your resources to the army, but such tactics can end up being a lost cause. By spending some of your resources on the secret society and market, you can aim for a swift defeat of the enemy with both prosperity and gunfire.

A strong economy is the key to maintaining the army and vice versa.

Looking to harm your enemies? For this sole purpose, we have provided an opportunity for the player to build a secret society.

Players will have the opportunity to inflict greater harm upon their enemies through the construction of a secret society. A secret society grants access to cards of ‘meanness.’

Each faction has two unique villainy cards that the player can activate once per game. Each card gives a special ability to the player. For example, the player can build an underground city on the territory of the enemy and gain half of his resources or a player can burn their enemy’s lands. There are eleven unique cards in total, each with their own abilities.

To make Ratten Reich: The Board Game more interesting, the game will interfere with your plans. Be it famine, plague, or a brutal winter, these events are meant to annoy everyone at the table as you recalculate your next move and adapt in battle.

But the game does not merely take. It gives. You may come across useful event cards that offer small advantages or stability. If the Autocrat takes part in the game, then they are free to choose the event cards themselves. In the absence of an Autocrat, the cards are drawn randomly.

Six unique factions await you! Each faction will have an original design, playstyle, a variety of abilities, and different military and economic potential. Revive the old empires in the eastern part of the central continent. Build a formidable fleet. Control the colonies. Determine which of you will become the world’s new hegemony and whose country will turn to ashes.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
For over 10 years, Cards Against Humanity has brought laughter to homes across America. Now, we are bringing you the unforgettable experience of pulling a sealed pack of 30 all-new cards out of a jar of creamy clam-flavored mayonnaise, and then slathering that smooth, tangy mayonnaise all over a turkey sandwich. We're not messing around: we worked with a team of professional chefs and food scientists to bring you a shockingly delicious mayonnaise made with real clams! So open wide, America-it's time for CLAM-O-NAISE.

—description from the publisher

Geas is a card game intended to be played with the Tarokka deck included in the Forbidden Lore boxed set. The deck consists of 54 cards: four suits of ten cards each numbered 1-9 plus an "archetype", and 14 unsuited cards.

In each hand players are dealt five cards and take turns drawing a card from the deck or discard pile and then playing or discarding a card.

A suited card can be played as part of a player's adventuring party or to attack a member of another player's party. A party consists of one card of each suit, preferably all of the same alignment: the numbered cards are divided into good, neutral, and evil alignments while the archetypes are wild.

If a player has a single-alignment party they can play an unsuited card, which is an adventure the party completes to gain a point.

A hand ends when the deck has been gone through three times. The game ends when each player has had a chance to deal.

Optional rules allow for such things as two-dimensional alignments (adding the chaotic-lawful axis).

boardgame: Zombicide: Gears and Guns – Steampunk Extras
description:
“These Survivors are geared up and ready to steamroll their way through the Gears and Guns campaign!”

The Steampunk Extras box contains 9 exclusive Survivor miniatures to join your Posse in the Zombicide: Gears and Guns campaign expansion.

Contains:
9 miniatures and Character Cards…
 • Dotty
 • Violet
 • Zao
 • Old Cuss
 • Calvin
 • Doc Umber
 • Jeans
 • Snake Eyes
 • Lord Forsaken

Two Item cards…
 • Steam Drifter
 • Steam Chair

Requires: Zombicide:Undead or Alive + Zombicide: Gears and Guns

This pack was available as an Add On for people that purchased the Gears and Guns expansion during the Undead or Alive Kickstarter campaign, and contains the “Kickstarter Exclusive” characters.

The Little Prince board game inspired by classic novel book + coaching concept + positive parenting concept. This game allow you to create safety environment to play and learn parent philosophy quality time with a group of 2-4 players that you can choose yourself. To share, discussion become "The Old New World" of your own.

For the game mechanic is very simple for understand and play. Which life the space for sharing and learn each other via playing game.

For the developer (Thailand Coaching Institute and Learning From Boardgames) believe this game will help you to create "The Quality Time" and visualize how become parents the way you choose to be

You may choose you boss and design your career path. But for you children, they can't choose their parents or quit being your child.

The player try to collect the card set to convert to the Quote Card to gain more points. and the most point is the winner.

The Color in the game;
1) Red (Rose) 1 point
2) Yellow (Fox) 2 points
3) Blue (The Little Prince) 3 points

The set of Red Yellow and blue able to covert 1 Quote Card (that contain 10 points). Alternatively, 5 reds or 4 yellow or 3 blue able to ruin the black (Snake) that contain -5 points from their score.

4) White (Sheep) 4 points:the set of 2 white able to convert 1 quote card (that contain 10 points)

5) Black (Snake) - 5 points: The set of 3 black are guarantee you win the game.

The Family Game of Awful Presents

There are some strange things in the gift exchange this year. A tuna-flavored candy cane? A wind-up fruitcake? An inflatable birdhouse?

They may be horrible . . . but some of them are just what YOU want! Pick your favorites and try to get them as fast as you can, because the game might end at any time . . .

Each gift-giver makes a list of their desired gifts, and trades gift cards with their "friends" to get what they want . . . or get stuck with a real stinker!

What do you want for Christmas?

In Microchip, 2 to 5 players compete to create a structure that looks like a microchip with tiles depicting paths and connections.

Rules overview (please refer to rulebook):

MATERIAL

•  5 boards
    •  Bag
    •  Box
    •  60 tiles in 5 colours, with the same distribution for each colour.

The initial setup depends on the number of players.

Players draw tiles according to some simple constraint and place them on their boards so that:

- The tile colours must match colours of the spaces they’re placed on. The square colours are a bit lighter for clarity.

- The connections of the tiles must match with those of the adjacent tiles (if any).

- Open connections towards the board edge are forbidden.

They can test and re-arrange the tiles on their boards during play.

After all the tiles have been drawn, the players will enjoy a few minutes to build their final microchips. There can be some tiles unused.

The player that created the most valuable microchip wins. The microchip value is the sum of the connections of each of its tiles. Unused tiles are worth minus 2 points.

-description from designer

Tarots fulfill their responsibility faithfully until a mysterious purple force born from the chaos. It disrupted the long-standing balance and destroyed the shackles that restrained Tarots.
Tarots were thus contaminated and the world began to be in turmoil...

At this point, the creator of Tarot: "Oracle", has called you to be his messenger.

You will gain the ability to summon other Tarots for a short time so as to find out the "true identity" of the contaminated Tarots. 
Now, raise the Tarot Sword to defeat and purify them. Bring the world back to peace again!

This is a logical reasoning game.
Players will incarnate into a hidden tarot messenger to assist Oracle. Use the ability of Tarots to find out the contaminated Tarots and purify them by guessing their numbers. ( Each Tarot has a corresponding number. )

How to play:
Each player chooses 1 card in the hand and face it down to become a "identity card". The identity card is hidden information and should not be seen by other players.

In your turn, you can perform one of the following actions:

1. Activate card effects: Face-up the tarot card to the corresponding position.

2. Guess Identity: Face-down the tarot card to the hidden area and guess a player's identity card.

3. Change Identity: Discard your original identify card and 1 card in your hand, and then face-down another card as your new identity card.

Each Tarot Cards has it own effect and you can use them to reason your opponents' identity card number.

When doing Guess Identity, if you correctly guess your opponent's identity card number then the player will be out. When you Guess Identity successfully, you can do one more round.

The game ends when there is only one player left.

-description from publisher
Be the first player to answer 12 questions. A bilingual two-level game: french-english.

boardgame: After the Virus: The Long Cold
description:
Summer is coming to an end and it’s time to prepare for a long and cold winter, full of new dangers…

The Long Cold takes the apocalypse of After the Virus to a whole new level with new characters, special zombies, and droves of new weapons, traps, and equipment. The new campaign lets players save progress between missions, as well as develop their deck over time.

Will you specialize in armor and striking weapons? Or expand your range of companions and animals?

-description from designer

boardgame: The Key: Theft in Cliffrock Villa – The Crown Witness
description:
"Commissioner, we were able to establish contact with a crown witness! But she is suspicious and asking for proof.
She won‘t tell us anything without assurances that we are actually investigating this case!"

"The Crown Witness" is an additional variant for "The Key – Theft in Cliffrock Villa".

This is a mini expansion for the very first "The Key" Game. It was a free promo on Spiel Essen 2022 and consists of 30 Cards: 6 rule cards, 6 Questions and 18 hints.

The Promo adds a new aspect to the game: At the start of the game one card is drawn that lets the crown witness ask a question concerning the case.

When playing you can focus to answer that question. When you can answer it, you note the answer and take a free info. only if you are last to answer, you get nothing.

It therefore adds a little race at the start of the game.

Cartas promocionales que incluyen tres goblins y tres nuevos conjuros.


La bruja necesita "conejillos de indias" para probar sus nuevas pociones y vosotros, sus leales esbirros goblins, habéis sido designados como voluntarios forzosos para esta ocasión. No es tan terrible como parece; vale, puede que algunas de esas pociones tengan un efecto mortal… Pero la jefa, siempre generosa, ha prometido que uno de vosotros saldrá ileso, como premio por su astucia, por su instinto y porque digo yo que alguien tendrá que limpiarlo todo al final."

Mal Trago es un party-goblin para grupos de goblins de 4 a 10 goblins, a partir de 8 años de edad goblin y con una duración de 15-20 minutos. Pociones, conjuros, antídotos, GOBLINS y sobretodo ¡¡MUCHAS RISAS!!

https://rocketlemongames.com/22-mal-trago.html

boardgame: Cas & Cato: Het grote zoek & vind familiespel
description:
Will you be the first to find Zack and Zoe (Cas & Cato)?

Imagine yourself in the world of Zack and Zoe, where there is an incredible amount to see and experience! Challenge a fellow player to be the first to find the search items on the playing card. Can you rely on your memory and find the items on the gameboard within thirty seconds? The game consists of five categories. When you win a category, you earn a token. Make sure that you have collected all the tokens and be the first to complete your player board; then you’ll be the winner of this super fun search and find family game!

—description from the publisher

The 1958 World Expo, an international exhibition, took place in  Brussels from April 17 to October 19, 1958. It was the first World Expo since World War II. As many as 42 million visitors were able to discover pavilions from 44 countries. The Atomium was constructed especially for the occasion, and today still represents one of Belgium's most recognizable symbols.

Expo 58 is a management game in which players are visitors of the World Expo. During the game, players try to co-ordinate their visit to the exhibition by optimizing the use of their Expo cards and Pavilion tiles.

Travel across the exhibition grounds, stroll through the gardens, enjoy fancy food in the restaurants, and vote for your favorite exhibitors. By carrying out all of these actions, you're trying to achieve goals that will score you victory points and bonuses.

At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins.

The card game for unlucky travelers!

The objective of the game is to travel without being caught by a Catastrophe.
There are 4 means of transport (ship, plane, car and train) and different Catastrophes (earthquake, electrical storm, icebergs, tsunami...) that can affect the different means of transport.
Each turn a Global Catastrophe arises randomly that affects all the players, in addition all the players have their game turn in which they can acquire resources and send catastrophes to the rivals.
All players have to try to avoid the catastrophes that affect their trips through Adventure cards!

—description from the designer

Each player draws a start-destination-card and travels from west to east and back by using their stock of transportation chips (train, ship, car, plane, sled). Additionally, each player draws 3 city cards, which determine which cities he has to visit on his journey.

The players take turns to roll 1 die and move their transportation chips on the corresponding spaces of the game board (blue = train, light blue = ship, green = car, orange = plane, yellow = sled). Each turn the players draw a quartet card. When the card pile is empty the players get a new traffic chip for each quartet collected. Three times per game the players may exchange cards to complete their quartets. On the spaces marked with a star, the players have to change their vehicle (transportation chip).

The first player back to his starting space wins the game.

Designed by James Ernest and gaming mathematician Joe Kisenwether, and illustrated by John Kovalic, Ducks in a Bucket is a press-your-luck game in which players attempt to build their best hands through several rolls of the dice.

In brief, players start their inning by rolling six dice: five matching dice, and one unique “Boss” die. The Boss determines which of the other dice can be promoted to the “bucket,” and which must be held back for the next roll. After any promotion, the player may choose to stop or roll again. But if they roll again without promoting a die, they could lose everything!

The player with the best bucket after everyone has rolled wins the round.  (The strength of hands is similar to poker, except that a Straight beats a Full House.)

boardgame: A game about quickly grabbing cards and judging them against three descriptions.
description:
Cards have descriptions on one side, objects on the other. Leave 2 more objects than the number of players in the middle of the table.

One player is the judge. They turn their back, then read out three descriptions. Everyone quickly grabs a card, and puts them into a pile near the judge.

The judge now turns around, and makes a decision as to which object best first the 3 descriptions.

You can also play without the judge turning around, and take turns presenting arguments to the judge after the grabbing.

Either way, one person gets a point and the judge rotates.

Play until everyone has been the judge once.

-description from designer

boardgame: Tainted Grail: Kings of Ruin – Black Goat of the Moors

The Winds of Magic expansion adds more ways to interact with the weather deck in Mythwind. You will find this interaction present in the new buildings, event cards and adventure cards.

The self-contained stories in the event and adventure cards seamlessly integrate with their respective decks. In addition, five new buildings can now be constructed, some of them providing a new type of action not included in the core box.

Components:

     10 new Event Cards
     10 new Adventure Cards
     5 new Building Cards
     2 alternative Season Tiles


(The Winds of Magics expansion was used as the fulfillment mechanism for many of the Stretch Goals from the original Kickstarter Campaign.)

The Expanded Horizons expansion adds a 5th player option for Mythwind through a new character. The Innkeeper focuses on bringing many patrons to her establishment to feed and entertain day after day. In addition to new adventures and goals to work through, a unique story with some history of the valley is provided that seamlessly integrates with the event deck. Five new buildings can now be constructed, providing a new type of action to benefit players as they expand their town.

Components:

     26 Event Cards
     25 Adventure Cards
     8  Goal Cards
     5  Building Cards
     2  Worker Dice
     2  Sprite Dice
     50 Additional Coins
     Hermit & Moses Tokens
     Innkeeper Storage Tray
     Updated Base Tray


— Description and Components from Expanded Horizons v1.1
A pack of Kickstarter Exclusive Cards for Mythwind, containing 4 Events Cards and 4 Adventure Cards.

boardgame: Forbidden Psalm: They Came from the Necropolis
description:
We're going on a hunt!

The world of Forbidden Psalm is brutal and uncaring, but for the right amount of coin there are those that will join you on your adventures.

They Came From The Necropolis, the latest mini-expansion for the Forbidden Psalm skirmish miniatures game, is chock-full of help and horror for those willing to part with enough dosh.
In this book you will find:

• new mercenaries like Pigmen (OINK!)
• a new monster, the Horse Head Knight!

Before starting a Scenario, any warband in Forbidden Psalm can replace any number of their members with mercenaries from this book. They are hired for one-off jobs, are selfish, and never drop their equipment or trade it willingly.

You need a copy of the Forbidden Psalm Core Book to play.

boardgame: Orp: The Market for Space Merchant Translators
description:
A wealthy alien has promised you riches beyond your wildest dreams if you can collect five of the items on their shopping list. There's only one issue: the wares of the market are only marked with Orpian symbols! You only know one symbol and must deduce the other players' tidbits of alien symbology to be the first to fulfill your wealthy client's request and profit from the intergalactic bazaar of Orp.

Instead of traditional hidden roles, every player knows the translation of one Orpian symbol. Players take turns asking each-other questions about their symbols or their meaning. They may also additionally ask a second question, swap cards with another player, or draw locked container cards from the deck. Once a player believes they have won, they will declare victory and then secretly check the results. If they have not won, they are eliminated from the game and play continues, so one must be fairly certain of victory before declaring it.

This game is a must-have for lovers of matrix logic puzzles, alien linguistics, trading, and social deduction.

-description from designer

In Macoco, you're trying to grab cards as quickly as possible.

Each player takes an even part of the shuffled deck face down in front of them, and on a turn, the next player reveals their top card face up in the center of the table. If more monkeys appear than any fruit or other animal on the card, yell "Macoco!" If a fruit or non-monkey animal appears most frequently, yell the name of that object. If nothing has a majority, yell "Nada!" The one exception: If a giant coconut appears in the center of the card, say nothing and slap the card.

If you are the first to correctly say the right thing or slap the card, you win it. If you're wrong, you suffer no penalty, but are out for the round. If everyone is wrong or multiple people do the right thing at the same time, no one wins the card. Leave it in the middle of the table, and whoever wins next takes all cards piled up.

Once all the cards have been played, whoever has collected the most cards wins.

Wander: LaCluck's Revenge is the second game in the Wander series. A fully cooperative, campaign-driven, miniatures board game. The Cult of Barnacle Bay was meddling with magic far beyond their understanding and accidentally resurrected the Ghost Pirate Chicken Lord LaCluck and her mutinous crew of Ghost Pirate Monkey Grunts, Ghastly Pig Gunners, Wailing Albatross Banshees, and Rampaging Rhino Cannoneers!

In Wander: LaCluck's Revenge, players will select one of five unique heroes from the Wanderer's Guild and join their friends to fight back against these ghost pirate phantoms to save the poor town of Barnacle Bay. Defeating foes and surviving random encounters earns players experience points and loot. Loot, like weapons and armor, is a persistent upgrade, staying with the heroes as they continue on their adventure. Experience points are used to level up heroes during their current adventure in the campaign. A dual-facing upgrade tree allows players to continually try new combinations of skills each adventure to determine what fits their play style, or current set of foes, best.

Using a modular tiles, players assemble maps as indicated in the choose your own adventure campaign book, then use action points to move or attack with their hero.

All enemies in the game are controlled by a simple line of sight A.I., and automatically hit the players' heroes once they're able to. This means there is no player rolling for success for enemies' attacks. Heroes will, however, be able to roll for defense in an effort to prevent incoming wounds. One set of custom dice determines a hero's success for both defending and attacking. Random encounter cards, which are revealed when players enter an unexplored "darkness" space, also promise that no campaign adventure is ever be the same.

-description from designer

The wicked witches are dead and the lands of Oz are free from tyranny! Surely there’s no place like home, but in the meantime, maybe Dorothy would like to visit Winkie Country? Collect the right tokens and play your cards right, and maybe Tin Man will join you in Quadling Country.

In this family friendly game, each player represents one of the lands of Oz. Each round, players will draw a card and play one from their hand. Depending on which card is played, they will  gather tokens of various types. If they collect the most tokens of a given type, they will attract heroes to their lands and score points. After the entire deck of cards has been played and all players have had an equal number of turns, they will score points and declare a winner!

-description from publisher

Minikin Saga: Jewels is a competitive micro game for 2 to 3 players. Where players as thieves stole a gem from a masquerade and must escape undetected, by revealing the opponents' identities and use them as scapegoat before they discover theirs.
At the beginning of the game, players will draw 2 Pawns from a bag, the combinations of those pawn's colors will determine is your identity.

On your turn you must do 1 Action from the 3 possible:
-Interrogate: Move a card (You can flip it too) to another empty space and ask a question. This question must refer to what the opponent's character can see. ex. Your Suspect see a Bird? Your Suspect can't see the color, Yellow? This is going to give you information about their identity.

-Investigate: At random and without looking inside the bag, take a pawn and look at it in secret. This will reveal what color is not in play and its not part of someone’s identity.

-Accuse: Point to an opponent (The other player closes their eyes in a 3 Player Game) and point to a card, if you discover the opponent's identity you win the game (In a 3 Player Game you must discover the other player's identity too) If not you lose that action and you must reveal one of your pawns to the accused player.

The game ends when a player discovers all the opponents' identities, that player is the winner!

-description from designer

This quest was posted online as an advertisement for the HasbroPulse crowdfunding of HeroQuest.

"As soon as I saw the fantastic sculpt for Mentor I wanted to write a Quest that would use the new miniature. Rogar’s Hall is a training Quest for Heroes that is run by Mentor. It is a tough challenge. One that will require the Heroes work as a team and truly optimize their skills, capabilities and resources. Mentor accompanies you and may help. But this is a test for the Heroes. Even though it is a training adventure Mentor promises you, “There will still be bruises and sore limbs in the morning.”

-description from publisher's website.

The website was taken offline in 2022.

Bluff your way free!

The hare, fox and hedgehog aren't actually best friends. But for once they must work together to reach the woodland on the other side of the glade.

ZWEI WEGE is a perfect game of bluff. The players' task is to get their three animals to the other side of the game field. The figures have one motive: to hide their intended route. The chances of getting ahead are 50:50. Because from every position there are precisely two possible paths to the next destination, and the opponent is only allowed to block one path.

A thrilling "cat-and-mouse" game for two poker faces and a lesson in the art of the bluff!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kampf gegen das Spiessertum: Family Edition
description:
"A party game for horrible German families"

Play begins with a judge, known as the "Round Boss", choosing a red fill-in-the-blank card from the top of the deck and showing it to all players. Each player holds a hand of eight white answer cards at the beginning of each round, and passes a card (sometimes more) to the round boss, face-down, representing their answer to the question on the card. The round boss determines which answer card(s) are funniest in the context of the fill-in-the-blank card. The player who submitted the chosen card(s) is given the question card to represent a point, and then the player to the left of the Round Boss becomes the new Boss for the next round. Play continues until the players agree to stop, or until they are thrown out of the pub, at which point the player with the most Points is the winner.

Kampf gegen das Spießertum – Family Edition is the family friendly version of Kampf gegen das Spiessertum. It contains 600 new cards. All the answer cards are illustrated.

Dice & Divination is a print and play, roll and write set of games. Each player may pick the same or different divination method as another player. Each game lasts around 25 minutes. Each player will compete to give the best reading to the client by applying the three rolled numbers to their sheet at the beginning of each of 6 rounds.

Each of the 5 games in the set plays completely different from each other and caters to a different play style for different gamers. Currently the games in the set are Astrology, Bone Casting, Tea Leaf Reading, and Palm Reading. There is a fifth that will be announced as part of the kickstarter campaign.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing (Second Edition) – Hotshots and Aces II Reinforcements Pack
description:
New pilots launch into Star Wars: X-Wing in this new pack! The Hotshots and Aces II pack gives players even more options for their squadrons of every faction with 35 new ship cards for a wide variety of ships. Among them, players will find a number of iconic pilots with all new ways to benefit their squadrons, including Tycho Celchu, Kit Fisto, Aurra Sing, Poe Dameron, Hondo Ohnaka, and Doctor Aphra.

-description from publisher's website
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

description:
This fourth expansion for Interplanetary, titled Skywards, simply adds additional directive cards which are added to the base game's pool of these components.

This increases variety even further, since the number of possible campaign deck configurations grows.

—description from the designer

The story of civilizations is punctuated with wars and disasters. Civilizations either survived them and continued on their journey, or succumbed to them and faltered. Will your civilization be strong enough to weather the storm?

Mosaic: Wars and Disasters contains many new elements to allow you to explore deeper strategies and face new challenges.

Admin note: While this box is listed for 1-6 players, no rules are included for incorporating the expansion elements into solo play.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Postman Pat: Big Letter Day Board Game
description:
Help Pat & Jess  race around the board delivering their letters. Whose bag  empty first? But watch out, there's a ship on the loose which keeps blocking the road!

The aim of the game is to be the first player to deliver all your letters to the Greendale residents' post boxes and return to the Greendale station.

Woodwalkers: The Game is a board game that allows two to four players to compete with multiple animal characters in two levels. In the role of "shapeshifter students" the players learn everything they need to know about their lives as humans and animals. In the subject "Combat and Survival" exiting team battles are on the schedule, in which they have to prove their skills to finally triumph over the others.

In each round the start player chooses one of three battle mode tiles, which describes the category of duels in this round like endurance, speed, intelligence or strength. Once each player has rolled a dice (maximum number of spaces animal tiles can move on the board), moved and possibly carried out an action such as turning over a forest tile or dueling another player, a players turn ends and the next player left of the start player continues by choosing again one of three battle mode tiles.

If there are fewer than three battle mode tiles left the game ends and players getting scores for each animal tiles which are on a space with a flag. The player with the most points wins the game.

On the legendary island of Dei, advanced technologies have allowed the creation of an artificial paradise.
Through genetic engineering, Humans have reached immortality.
Now self-proclaimed deities, they manipulate Nature to provide for their needs, and entertainment.
Here, Versus is the national sport. Inspired by gladiatorial combat, the Versus Games are held in the Arena.
The fighters are named Aeons. They are powerful bio-synthetic creatures, conceived with the sole purpose of killing.

In this card game of strategy, players take one the role of Humans, capable of summoning Aeons in the Arena.
To win the game, you must eliminate all of your opponent's cards.

All cards are unique, balanced, independent, and versatile. If you consistently find better synergies than your opponent, you'll win.
Versus is easy to learn (10min), and quick to get started (1min). No deck building needed. Baffling simplicity, frightening depth.
Adjust the game duration (from 10 to 40min), and the level of complexity. Make it chill, or epic.

Play 1v1, or up to 6v6. Yes, you can form 2 teams of up to 6 players each, everyone playing on the same board !

—description from the designer

boardgame: Karak: Elspeth – The Warrior Princess Promo Character
description:
Elspeth is the only daughter of the mighty ruler of the Western Kingdoms. Alas, its airy palaces were, for her, more like a prison. While her brothers were telling stories of their brave adventures, the beautiful princess was taught how to sing and sew. But this was not to be the destiny for stubborn ELSPETH. With the secret help of the king's master-at-arms, she practiced her swordsmanship every night – at first with one, but soon with two swords. It was her very teacher who told the princess about the evil across the Great Sea, lurking in the castle Karak. Elspeth was charmed by the master-at-arms' tale. She longed for her own tale, a story in which a womancould achieve as much glory as a man. Or perhaps even more! On the day ofher 18th birthday, she sneaked out of the palace and on a small schooner she sailed for her own adventure.

Dual Wielding: The Warrior Princess has been trained in the dual wield technique since her childhood. In every combat she can reroll one of her die if the roll is not to her liking. If she does, she has to use the rerolled value.

Tactical Reposition: The Warrior Princess can move through a tile containing a monster for a cost of one move and one HP. Afterwards the warrior princess is moved to a tile adjacent to the tile she has charged through. 
This skill can be used even when entering an undiscovered zone; in that case use the rules for entering an undiscovered zone.

Elspeth was originally released as bonus character available only in specialized retails. She was included as the seventh character in the English edition Catacombs of Karak (English-only edition) published by Outset Media and also in later Czech editions of the game.

The publisher (Albi) later released a printable pdf with Elspeth, usable for home use/printing.

Later publisher decided to add her into the mini expansion Karak: Sidhar, Kirima, Elspeth.

Drama Mayhem is an improvisational party game. Each round, one player is designated as the judge. The judge draws a scenario card (such as "A customer is short of money at checkout"), then selects two other players, who will have 1 minute to act that scenario out. Each of those two players draws a quirk card (such as "Overly Cheerful" or "A Famous Opera Singer") that modifies their performance. After the minute is up, the judge decides which of the two actors wins a point for the round. Then the judge token is passed to the left, and a new round begins. First to 5 points wins!

You have to find and rescue endangered animals scattered here and there. Check where the animals are, add their numbers, and make 10 — then you can bring them to a safe place! The first to rescue ten animals wins!

Memory Island is a memory/arithmetic game for 2-4 players. Make the sum of the numbers equal to 10, and be the first player to collect ten cards to win.

The wind howls, the sea beats against the dunes. The storm signal is on! The islanders rush to the beach to comb washed up wood.

You leave with your pawn from your village to the beach where you will go beach combing. Whoever has first collected 10 pieces of wood, has moored his boat in the harbor and has driven the jeep back to his house, wins.

Turn sequence: 

     If you have colluded enough wood for a truck or boat, place it
     Draw a card and follow instructions on placing new wood and floating wood
     Throw the dice
     Move your pieces and collect wood in the fields where you end your move

Xixx utilises six multi coloured and multi symbolled dice with which the players try to score a total of 100 points. Different combinations of the dice produce different scores. There are six shapes, circle, eye, triangle, square, pentagon and hexagon in four colours, red, blue, yellow and green. Harder combinations are worth more points.

—description from the designer

Sado is an island off the coast of Niigata prefecture, Japan. It has never had trains (and likely never will), but it is a beautiful island and a fantastic place in many other respects. It would be even better if it had trains...

So the city council decided that Sado should have trains indeed, and gave a small budget to three companies to develop a rail network for tourists and freight on Sado, connecting the main ports (Ryotsu and Ogi) to the main tourist destinations and centers of (mainly agricultural) production.

AoS Sado doesn't deviate much from standard Age of Steam. It is a 3-player variant with a few twists and turns. Black cubes represent tourists, and consequently, these come in at the ports (Ryotsu and Ogi) only. Towns start with one cube each. And players/companies start with a little bit more money thanks to a government grant to promote tourism and the local industry (through railroad development). That extra money is necessary, however, as finances tend to be extremely tight in the beginning of the game.

-description from designer

Summary:
- Trigahex is a strategic abstract card game for 2-6 players.
- Play individually or in a team.
- Suitable for ages 8+.
- Players can choose the length of play by deciding how many Hexagons win the game (1, 2 or 3). Approximately 10 minutes - 40 minutes.

The Playing Cards:
Trigahex includes three Triangle coloured decks of cards (54 x Red, 54 x Blue and 54 x Green).
Each player or team chooses a colour to play with.

The Object of the Game:
The first person or team to create the winning number of Hexagons wins the game.

Win a Hexagon by placing 6 of your cards together to create a Hexagon shape.  All the cards in the Hexagon MUST have your chosen playing card colour in the centre of the card.

Before you play:
Decide how many Hexagons to play to win the game (1, 2, or 3).

The first player places a card in the game area,  Players then take turns to place a card adjacent to the card/s placed.  The colours on the edges of the cards MUST match the edges of the cards they are placed next to.

Try to create your own Hexagon/s or stop your opponent from creating their Hexagon/s.

If a player places a card that has a White Triangle in the middle, they also pick up an action card.

The 'Trash' action card:
A player trashes a card from the game area and it is returned to the bottom of the draw stack.

The 'Replace' action card:
A player replaces a card from the game area with a card in their hand.  The edges of the card must match any cards in the game area.  The replaced card is returned to the the bottom of the draw stack.

The 'Stack' action card:
A player stacks a card from their hand on top of a card in the game area.  The edges of the card must match the edges of any cards it is adjacent to.  The card underneath remains and is stuck in the game area.

-description from designer

boardgame: Fireball Island: The Curse of Vul-Kar – Deep Thoughts Promo Card
description:
Card was available during the Secret Cabal 2021 Kickstarter campaign.
 DEEP THOUGHTS INJURY: You can never move more than 6 spaces on your turn.

Roll the die then draw a card and read the corresponding question/direction. Some require the use of the map (e.g. which country borders this country on the east). If you are correct, draw a token, which is worth the number of points shown. 1st P to collect 100 pts in tokens wins.

boardgame: Strength & Honour: Wargames Rules for Epic Battles in the Ancient World
description:
The game allows you to recreate battles from the start of the Marian reforms in Rome around 105BC, when the professional Roman legionaries organised in cohorts replaced the older Republican Legion structure of maniples, through to about 200AD.

Command armies of the Ancient World on a massive scale with these innovative and fun rules that put you in charge for the classic encounters of antiquity.

Designed for large battles in smaller scales, Strength & Honour allows you to take to the field at the head of massed ranks of thousands of warriors.

With scenarios included for 10 battles you will take the role of leaders such as Julius Caesar, Boudicca and Pompey.

In addition to the rules, Strength & Honour includes twenty army lists covering the Ancient world from the island of Britannia in the west to the mystic East beyond the Mediterranean; Germania’s forests in the north and the deserts and mountains of north Africa in the south.

Battles focus on large units so for example a legion is the basic unit building block of a Roman army.

While the rules were designed for 2mm figures they are playable with any sized figures, or indeed printable top down units which are also available.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Dated is a trivia game for people who suck at trivia. The rules are simple. Each round, one player picks a category and reads the title out loud. All other players take turns guessing the year it was released. The closest guess wins the card! The first player to ten cards wins the game.

-description from publisher

Cards are distributed unevenly with the following system: Players take turns revealing cards from the deck. If it matches a card already on the table, both are taken by the player who revealed them. If there is no match, the new card is left on the table. 
When all cards have been distributed, players shuffle their decks. Then they take turn revealing a random card from their decks and place it in a pyramid layout. When revealing a card that already is present, these cards are stacked up. When revealing a card of the same color of an existing one, take both. When one player runs out of cards, the game ends, and players count the points on their cards. Highest score wins.

Historians estimate that the Kurukshetra (koo-rook-shay-trah) war occurred during the Iron Age of India, around 10th century BCE. The conflict arose from a succession dispute over the Throne of Hastinapur (has-tee-nah-poor). Duryodhana was the eldest from the senior branch of the family line, the Kauravas. But he was younger than Yudhishthira, the eldest Pandava. Who would become the next ruler of the vast Kuru kingdom?

The epic struggle over the Throne of Hastinapur has begun! Use your influence over the ancient heroes of India to sway them to join your side, enlist the help of other Rajas, and surprise your opponents by making your own preparations for war before they are ready to fight!

In Throne of Hastinapur, players act as local supporters who are supplying resources and political leverage to either the Pandavas or Kauravas. To show support, you must place tokens on shared Hero Cards or your personal Pati (Pah-tee) in order to win Victory Points (VP), shown as Lotus flowers. Players will “snake draft” dice from a pool in order to place tokens that match. After the Endgame is triggered, players play a final round. To win, score the most Victory Points!

-description from designer

The board has 4 rows w/10 spaces each representing a player’s lab bench. Each player places their cat standee at the start of their bench then conducts one of 4 classic modern physics experiments by drawing a card for the experiment and resolving it. Most cards move your cat and cats move a different way for each experiment. If you reach the end of a bench, take a party hat. First to get 4 hats wins.

Blackbody Radiation Experiment. Includes die with faces 0/black, 2 red/1, 2 green/2, and 1 blue/3. Roll the dice to see what color photon comes out of the blackbody oven. The rarer the photon, the more your cat advances.

Rutherford Gold Foil Experiment. Includes spinner w/4 spaces: red/1 (takes up half of circumference), 2 gold/2 spaces (each takes up 1/6 of the circumference), and blue/3 (takes up 1/6 of the circumference). Spin the spinner to see in what direction your alpha particle scattered. The rarer the outcome, the farther you can advance.

Double-Slit experiment. Includes 2 dice each w/3 white (0/1/2) and 3 black (0/1/2) faces. Roll both to see if your electron ends up in a region of constructive or destructive interference. If faces are same color (constructive interference), add them. If different, subtract the smaller from the larger.

Photoelectric Effect. Includes 3 dice w/faces: red/1000 Kelvin (move 1 space), green/3000K (move 2 spaces), and blue/200k (move 3 spaces). Roll the dice to see what color lamp your can has chosen. Some colors of light will eject electrons from the metal at higher velocity, meaning your cat can advance further.

Each deck consists of 10 “Run the Experiment” cards (if you draw one, run the experiment as above) plus a number of special cards. If you draw the Wolfgang Pauli card, Pauli shows up to observe and breaks your experiment. “Save this Card” allows you to place a note on the door to direct auli to another room. “Ummm???” – someone measure the momentum of your cat so precisely that its position is uncertain. Roll 1 double-slit die and move forward if you roll white and backward if you roll black. “Rawr!” – you encounter a potential energy barrier and have a 50% chance to quantum tunnel through it (by rolling double-slit die as above). “Yikes!”: your cat ruins the experiment so you don’t move. “Hooray”: your cat did something to help you – move again.

The Outskirts cards bring new challenges and incentives to Fliptown. This mini-expansion is compatible with both multi-player games and solo play. Also included are two new Cowbots (Annie Oakley and Doc Holliday) and two new Characters (The Gambler and The Snakeoil Salesman).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering — Commander 2019: Mystic Intellect
description: Sevinne the Chronoclasm headlines this white-blue-red preconstructed Commander deck themed around the Flashback mechanic. Mystic Intellect includes a 100-card deck, a foil oversized display commander, 10 double-sided tokens, and a cardboard deck box.

When an asteroid crashes into Earth, the very foundations of society are left in ruins. With no remaining institutions, chaos erupts as governments crumble. Some blame inaction or even conspiracy for not deflecting the asteroid. Others see the possibility of creating a new and better world. Eight mega-organizations emerge, vying for the chance to take charge of rebuilding our post-apocalyptic planet. Each has a plan that relies on emerging technology and prevailing public opinion. The plans are wildly different: from building a green utopia to merging with machines in a matrix to fleeing Earth entirely and colonizing a nearby planet. But there are only enough resources left for one of these plans to succeed. And each step of the way, other players will try to compete for the same resources or even oppose you directly.

You are the chosen leader in a mega-organization. You must convince the public that not only are your ideas the best of those presented by the mega-organizations—but also that you are the one who can save the world and lead it safely into the future. Your task is to assemble teams focused on technologies to influence humanity's future. It is up to you whether to turn Earth into an authoritarian regime, liberate people from their oppressors, change what it means to be human, return to living in harmony with nature, let the free market reign, or control human behavior with a supercomputer.

You begin the game by choosing a mega-organization card, two technology cards, and four agent cards. Each turn, you draw one card and get two points you can spend to draw cards, move agents, execute special abilities, or research new technologies. You can also trade cards for points to execute more complex turns. The table is split into three lanes: development, commercial, and monopoly, representing stages of bringing a technology to market. Each gives you prestige, public opinion, or effect bonuses you can use during or at the end of the game. You can move a card to the next stage when the assigned team's skills fulfill the card's requirements. When resolving conflicts with other teams working on the same technology, your agents' skills determine who strikes first and how much damage an agent can cause and withstand.

The game ends when all technologies in the game are developed, or if you win the tug-of-war of public opinion and successfully initiate a revolution. In this case, tally the prestige from your developed technologies and add bonus prestige from your technologies that align with the prevailing public opinion. The player with the most prestige wins the game and the chance to make an IMPACT BEYOND.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering — Commander 2019: Merciless Rage
description: Anje Falkenrath headlines this red-black preconstructed Commander deck themed around the Madness mechanic. Merciless Rage includes a 100-card deck, a foil oversized display commander, 10 double-sided tokens, and a cardboard deck box.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering — Commander 2019: Primal Genesis
description: Ghired, Conclave Exile headlines this white-green-red preconstructed Commander deck themed around the Populate mechanic. Primal Genesis includes a 100-card deck, a foil oversized display commander, 10 double-sided tokens, and a cardboard deck box.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Summer Aya Promo Cards
description:
Beta Rules - 2 x new Beta Gameplay Cards, 1 x new gear and 1 x new Strange Resource that were included with the Summer Aya First Run Collector's Edition that was not part of the based game.

The Summer Aya is packaged in a hand-stamped kraft box and limited to 500. It includes:

-1 x Photoresin Summer Aya
-1 x Hard Plastic Kingdom Death 30mm round insert and base
-2 x Beta Gameplay Cards, gear and Strange Resource
-First run collector's edition: Signed and numbered authenticity note
-Large Matte Art Print
-Character Art Card

Aya travels to the sand dunes below the larval pools that form in the iron clouds overhead. Here the sun never sets and perpetual summer reigns. The metabolic mutation of the skypoles super heats the fluids of the pool, roiling and bubbling over, occasionally disgorging them to the sands below. Massive globs of fluids rain down, creating cryptic glass formations in the sand.

Beta Rules - 3 x new Pattern cards and 3 x new Pattern gear cards that were included with the Summer Goth First Run Collector's Edition.

The Summer Goth kit comes with 2 miniatures. A painter's scale model in repose, perfect for painters and a player's scale model for gamers looking for a fun survivor stand-in.
The Summer Goth is packaged in a seasonal box and includes:

-1 x Painter's scale Summer Goth hard plastic miniature
-1 x Player's scale Summer Goth hard plastic miniature
-1 x Hard Plastic Kingdom Death 30mm round insert and base
-Large Matte Art Print
-Character Art Card
-3 x Pattern cards 
-3 x Pattern gear cards

On the glass coast of the boiling sea, Twilight Knight Allison waits. She has paid the dark seamstress a vial of her own tears of anguish for the dress and finery that is acceptable at the court of the uncrowned king of Teltia. She awaits a summon at the domain's edge so she may learn how he has created this slice of miserable, sunny paradise.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering — Commander 2019: Faceless Menace
description: Kadena, Slinking Sorcerer headlines this blue-black-green preconstructed Commander deck themed around the Morph mechanic. Faceless Menace includes a 100-card deck, a foil oversized display commander, 10 double-sided tokens, and a cardboard deck box.

Promo Pack 1 for Ragnarocks contains 2 mythology cards: “Huginn & Muninn - Odin's ravens” and “Ullr - God of hunting”.

The mythology cards represents personal player powers (like the god and hero powers in Santorini, also designed by Gord!) that fundamentally change the way the game is played.

The base game of Ragnarocks come with 32 mythology cards, and this promo expansion adds two more:

“Huginn & Muninn“: You only have two Vikings. However, each of your normadic (moveable) Vikings moves and then summons.

“Ullr”: You only have two Vikings. When your opponent moves, their selected Viking must move as far as possible (in the chosen direction).

LET'S SAIL THIS ADVENTURE TOGETHER!
A HIGH SEA adventure game created for the whole family! Dungeons of Kards EDITION 10 is a card game, in the style of the great RPGs, where you will explore THE SEA OF DUNGEONS OF KARDS WORLD, full of magical portals, taking YOU TO A VERY DIFFERENT CHALLENGE!

The deck you've acquired will give you hours of adventure, but there's always room for more! THIS ADVENTURE IS A DUNGEONS OF KARDS EXPANSION, bringing THE FIRST VEHICLE EXPANSION TO THE DUNGEONS OF KARDS WORLD BRINGING MORE excitement to the game!

—description from the designer

boardgame: LOOP: The Elevated Card Game – Renovated Edition
description:
The hit transit card game LOOP now has a standalone expansion! The new LOOP: Renovated Edition contains all new event cards, plus some new wild cards and a full set of station cards. It can be played on its own, or combined with the original edition of LOOP to double the fast-paced, chaotic fun!

Ride the rails of the elevated trains in Chicago’s famous Loop! Match the line colors and stations to make your way around town, but beware the everyday perils of signal delays, broken escalators, and unexplainable smells as you get where you’re going!

Like the original, LOOP: Renovated Edition is a 10-20 minute game suitable for ages 9 and up. One deck can support a game of 2-7 players, but when combined with the original edition of LOOP you can play with up to 14 people!

-description from publisher's website

Ein lustiges Würfelspiel für und mit dem Nachwuchs.
Wer erwürfelt sich zuerst seinen Seehund und baut ihn zusammen? Simples Kinderspiel für zwischendurch.

Reihum wird gewürfelt, jeder Körperteil des Seelöwen hat eine Zahl (1-6). Die Würfelziffer bestimmt, welcher Teil des Seelöwen zusammengesteckt wird, wobei eine bestimmte Reihenfolge zu beachten ist. Schritt für Schritt wird der Seelöwe fertiggestellt. Wer zuerst alle Teile zusammengesetzt hat, gewinnt das Spiel.

English:

A fun dice game for and with the offspring.
Who gets his seal first and builds it together? Simple children's game for in between.
