description:
Tabletop baseball just got faster, with this quick-play companion to History Maker Baseball! Finish full games in 10 minutes or less, without losing the full-play drama and excitement! Use the HMB cards you already have, watch players perform with the same realism as its sports board game big brother. Includes game charts and rules booklet, use dice and cards from the full-play version of the game.

Please note that the express game does not include dice or teams, it’s designed to be used in tandem with the HISTORY MAKER BASEBALL game you already own! While the express game’s rules booklet is comprehensive, we’re assuming that you already own the full-play game and understand the basic mechanics and procedures. We don’t, for example, explain in the rules booklet what a WHIFFER is, or what makes a pitcher FRESH. Unless otherwise specified, full-play concepts apply to the express game and you should refer to your full-play rules booklet for interpretations.

—description from the publisher

An easier variant of Rummy played with 110 "The Simpsons"-themed cards.

The game has the groups of cards: number-cards from 1-5, family-cards (Homer, Marge, Bart, Lisa, Maggie) and non-family-cards (Gramps, Otto, Krusty) in 4 different colors. In addition there are 6 jokers.

Even though the general game flow is similar to Rummy - with the exception, that the whole discard deck needs to be taken instead of the top cards - the allowed combinations differ:

Laying out cards allows any combination of all-different cards out of two of the three groups mentioned above. Having all in the same or all in different colors gives extra-points.

—user summary

Mafia: Crime Scene is a party game in which you have to bluff and suspect. In Mafia: Cards, designed for 7-20 players, some players take on the roles of mafia members, while others become civilians of the criminal community. Every night the mafia kills another person, and every day the civilians have a chance to discuss the murder, choose suspects, and sentence one of them to death. Because each role is secret, you never know who is an innocent bystander and who is part of the mafia. If the mob kills enough civilians, the community will become a permanent haven for criminals, but if the civilians rid their community of the mob, they win.

Mafia: Crime Scene offers 24 different character cards, as well as 55 superpower cards for a fun game. You can play as a don, a doctor, an innocent bystander, or a cunning minion. Whatever your role, play it convincingly, make your choices carefully, and trust no one.

As a gift also comes a premium subscription - 1 month, for our application, with which your game will be even cooler and more interesting.

—description from the publisher

Read the lights right or get mixed up with the exciting Crossed Signals electronic game! Choose one of 4 different games on the unit. Players see the lights and must act fast! Is there an arrow? Move the stick in that direction. See an 'X'? Move in the opposite direction. Are both arrows lit? Shake it! Play solo or with up to 3 friends. Utilizing light and voice commands, The Crossed Signals game will get you on your feet to challenge your speed and accuracy -- including a dueling showdown where 2 players each take a stick! The player with the highest score wins! Up to 4 players can enjoy this frenzied game of lights and action! The Crossed Signals game brings active indoor and outdoor fun to any game night or party.

—description from the publisher

The word-explaining game Med andra ord made into a child friendly edition.

You play in pairs, where one has to explain the word on the card "in other words" and the other guesses. In 45 seconds, you should try to explain and guess correctly on as many cards as possible. When the game is over, the points are added up according to the denomination on the cards. The team with the highest score wins.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Fuglafár is an educational game about 30 native bird species to Iceland. Players will learn the names, type, weight (í. þyngd), length (í. lengd), brood (í. urpt), population size in pairs (í. varppör), gestation period (í. álega) and age (í. aldur) of these birds as they play. The information is both available on player board and a deck of cards. 
The card deck also includes additional educational text about each bird.

There are two game variants in the rule book: 
- "Gettu hver fuglinn er" (similar to Guess Who?): The objective is if find out what bird your opponent has before he realizes yours, by taking turns asking yes or no questions. 
- "Fuglatromp" (similar to Top Trumps): All cards are dealt equally amongst the players face down. Each player can only look at the top card from their deck. The player who starts the round chooses one of six bird attributes listed he believes is the strongest for all top cards that round. The player with the highest value wins the trick and places it at the bottom of his deck and decides the attribute for the next round. The game ends when a single player has gained all cards to his player deck.

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Second Season Edition – Amazon Team: Kara Temple Harpies
description:
Highly skilled and agile, the Amazons are among the best players in the sacred game of Nuffle. These warrior-athletes have an explosive blend of agility, tactical skill, and violence that makes their game exciting to watch, with an acrobatic style honed by years of running through the thick rainforest to escape superpredators. With tactics inspired by the most dangerous animals in their native jungles, these players apply harassment maneuvers that dazzle Blood Bowl fans and opponents alike.

On the field, the Amazon teams are a force to be reckoned with. Although they wear light armor, their agility allows them to dance around slow opponents, while drowning the weaker inhabitants of the Old World with blows. Known to be warriors as well as players, the Amazons that make up these teams unsurprisingly manage to routinely crush their opponents, even at the highest levels of competition.

This plastic kit allows you to assemble the Kara Temple Harpies, a team of Amazons for use in Blood Bowl games. It consists of 6 Eagle Warrior Linewomen, 2 Piranha Warrior Blitzers, 2 Python Warrior Throwers and 2 Jaguar Warrior Blockers. In addition, this box contains 2 double-sided Blood Bowl pieces, 2 turn and raise counters, 4 balloons and a sheet containing 292 decals, all in the style of the Amazon teams.

Rules for using the Amazon teams in your Blood Bowl games are available in the Spike! Journal 15.

This kit includes 102 plastic components and comes with 12x 32mm round Citadel bases and 1x Amazon Team Decal Sheet. These figures are supplied unpainted and require assembly.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): Amazon Pitch – Double-sided Pitch and Dugouts
description:
Most of the land owned by the Amazons is located deep in the jungles of Lustria, hidden in secret clearings or groves. But the jungle climate is unpredictable at best, and the stifling heat can suddenly give way to torrential rains. When a downpour arrives, it can awaken various jungle creatures. These saurian spectators rarely pay for their tickets, and they tend to attack unsuspecting players who get too close to the edge.

One side of the field is a dense jungle floor, dotted with poisonous snakes and featuring a huge Team Amazon icon in the center. On the other side, torrential rains have flooded the field, attracting colorful birds and huge scaly beasts native to Lustria.

You'll also find two matching pits to complete the theme. Redesigned for the new edition of the game, each pit features score, turn, and re-roll counter tracks, as well as Reserve, Knocked Out Players, and Dead and Injured Players boxes.

-description from publisher's website

Watch Ya' Mouth with colored mouth openers and 200 cards in 4 new categories:  Ultimate Phrases, Rhyme Time, Head-to-Head, & Pop Culture. Can you get your team to guess your phrase with a mouth opener in the way?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): Amazon Team Card Pack
description:
From the Publisher:

Unveil the mysteries of Lustria's guardians! This deck of 44 cards will help you keep track of stats and profiles for your Amazons, as well as your positions and Star Players. It also includes Special Play cards exclusively for use with Amazon teams.

CONTENTS
4x Player Reference cards for the Python Warrior Thrower, Piranha Warrior Blitzer, Jaguar Warrior Blocker, and Eagle Warrior Linewoman
4x Star Player cards for Boa Kon'ssstriktr, Estelle la Veneaux, Glotl Stop, and Karla von Kill
8x Random Events Special Play cards
8x Heroic Feats Special Play cards
19x Blank Positional Cards, to fill in for each player on your team: 2x Python Warrior Thrower, 2x Piranha Warrior Blitzer, 2x Jaguar Warrior Blocker, and 13x Eagle Warrior Linewomen
One all-important card that explains how to use all of the above cards, with a red card on the reverse

You'll need a copy of Blood Bowl – The Official Rules and Spike! Journal Issue 15 to make full use of

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Second Season Edition – Spike! Journal #15
description:
This issue’s Guest Editor Meghera Lafes ventures out of the thick and steaming rainforests of Lustria to bring you a Spike! Journal packed with scrimmaging serpents and tropical touchdown gameplay. That’s right, this issue brings the Amazon team to your games of Blood Bowl!

This journal covers the history, rules, and rosters of the Amazon team. As well as stories of mysterious relics, acrobatic antics, and some truly spectacular ritual sacrifice shows at half-time, you'll find all the fantastic fun and essential commentary you've come to expect from Spike! Journal. As such, this magazine is the perfect companion for any jungle-dwelling coach.

This issue includes:
- Warriors of Legend: background on the origins of Amazon teams in Blood Bowl, and how they play on the pitch. Includes information on their team positions, Star Players, and a team roster.
- Famous Amazon Teams: descriptions and background on some of the more famous Norse teams, including the Feathered Jaguars, Patraxi Pythons, Skywatch Sentinels, and Great River Wardens.
- Kara Temple Harpies Team Spotlight: an in-depth article focusing on the Kara Temple Harpies, the most recent Amazon team to have taken the Blood Bowl pitch by storm. Includes details of their history, team profile and colours, honours, Spike! team rating, as well as the roster of their triumphant 2499-2500 squad, and loads more.
- Star Player – Estelle la Veneaux: a profile on the Baroness of the Bayou, said by some to an embodiment of the Amazonian goddess Rigg – and by others to be a half-crazed Amazon covered in frogs – detailing her uncanny powers and vengeful career.
- Dirt from the Dugout: this time Mindy Piewhistle investigates the scandalous rumours surrounding the Genaina Javelins and their new, beautifully-scaled fashion brand.
- Star Player – Glotl Stop: a profile on this infamous Kroxigor Blocker, known to many as the Primordial Nightmare – or the Scaled Slasher. Though he often has trouble distinguishing friend from foe on the pitch, that's only added to the impressive body count Glotl's racked up in his explosive career.
- Amazonian Temple Leagues: brand new rules and advice for running the Amazonian Temple Leagues – a variant on the standard Blood Bowl league season, allowing players to compete in the Jungle Bowl – open to all teams with the usual budget of 1,000,000 gold pieces – for the mysterious Jungle Bowl Cup. Also includes unique tables for Sweltering Jungle Weather, Temple-City Kick-off, Lustrian Locals, and the effects of Ancient Artefacts.
- Overgrown Jungle Blood Bowl Pitch: includes rules for using the Overgrown Jungle Blood Bowl Pitch in your games, including special rules for the Jungle Floor and Torrential Downpour. (These rules are also included with the Amazon Team Double-sided Pitch and Dugouts Set.)
- Coffin Corner: Spike! Journal's obituaries column commemorating those players who are no longer with us, whether trampled on the pitch, torn apart, or possibly eaten.
- Chat with the Rat: ace Skaven reporter Hackspit ‘the Scribbler’ Quillchewer meets with Klara Vytis, Groundswoman for the Obsidian Ocelots, to discuss the many, many challenges of maintaining a game-ready Blood Bowl pitch in the depths of the Lustrian jungle.
- Amazon Balls: includes background and rules for two unique Amazon Balls – the Crystal Skull Ball and the Snake-Swallowed Ball.
- Secrets of the Amazons: a detailed introduction to coaching Amazon teams. Includes an overview of the strengths of their team, positions, tactics, and more.
- Star Player – Boa Kon'ssstriktr: a profile on the 'Scheming Serpent' himself, detailing how this mysterious and hypnotic Snakeman rose to prominence by tripping up enemy teams.
- Dungeon Bowl Update – Amazon Teams: provides a narrative and rules update for players to use their Amazon team miniatures in games of Dungeon Bowl.
- Mighty Blow! – Jungle Fever: a look back on some of the tall tales from the career of Bob Bifford. Includes a comic strip Bob Bifford reminiscing about his time in Lustria.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Blade & Bow: The Ancient World at War – Volume I: Rise of the Hoplite
description:
Blade & Bow is a moderately complex game depicting combat between ancient armies with the goal of showing the evolution of infantry combat from the adoption of the Greek hoplite formation through the Roman maniple and cohort. Two players take control of historical armies to see how well they might fare as compared to their historical counterparts. In what will hopefully be the first in a series of volumes covering this evolution, four battles are included: Marathon (490 BCE), Thermopylae (480 BCE), Plataea (479 BCE), and Mycale (479 BCE). Each battle takes two to three hours to complete.

The game system is a departure from other ancient combat games in several key ways:

The game uses a square grid, rather than hexes, to regulate movement. This provides eight vectors of advance (rather than six) and allows for more realistic linear movement and combat.

The game uses a unique card-based activation system that provides armies with better leadership to be more flexible with regards to their actions on the battlefield. Unlike traditional "IGO-UGO" activations, formations may activate multiple times during their turn to provide more historically accurate outcomes.

The game simulates hoplite formations in a manner allowing players to allocate where their forces' strength lays, unknown to their oppoenent until combat units actually engage. This allows a player to decide where to allocate their strengths, rather than relaying upon the values printed on a combat unit. Additionally, these denser units take damage differently, allowing them to be ground down while pressing forward in a manner that does not require record keeping.

Finally, the deck of cards allows for special effects that can help a side turn the tide of a battle in their favor or stave off disaster at the last moment.

-description from designer

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Rivals: The Dragon & The Rogue
description: The Dragon & The Rogue is an expansion for Vampire: The Masquerade - Rivals that adds two new clans:  Tzimisce & Ravnos.  Where Tzimisce seek to possess and distort the physical world around them, Ravnos constantly leave their world behind in search of fresh opportunities to exploit, and their distortions are more of the mental variety.  You may play these two clans head-to-head or integrate them with your Core Set and other expansions to create unique strategies for conquering your foes.

boardgame: Mystery Party in the Box: El Misterio de los Muertos Vivientes
description:
In this deductive genre, each player plays the role of a character with the dual purpose of unraveling a mystery and simultaneously achieving his or her own objectives. The investigation, the negotiations with the rest of the cast and the exchange of information are fundamental pieces to solve the crime, but do not forget that the murderer is one of you. You can't believe everything you are told, but you can't win without trusting someone...

Japan, early 21st century. Civilization, as we know it, has succumbed as a result of the spread of a virus that turns its victims into the living dead. The arrival of a stranger in a white coat to a small community of survivors augurs the possibility of developing a vaccine to put an end to the plague, but within hours the stranger turns up dead. Incursions of zombie worshippers dot the country, rumors of a mysterious killer strangling his victims, plague survivors disappearing without explanation... and a strange melody that night after night seems to come from underground. Who, among you all, will delve into the depths of the mystery and perhaps find refuge in this new promised land called Sanctuary?

Playing is very simple, just follow these steps:
1. Hand out a character for each player and introduce yourselves to the rest.
2. Try to uncover the mystery while you find out who your allies might be.
3. Your actions will lead you to one of the many possible endings.

boardgame: Survive The Titanic! The Card Game
description:
The RMS Titanic was the most luxurious, grandest ship in the world. On April 14, 1912, four nights into her maiden voyage, the Titanic struck an iceberg. Two hours and forty minutes later, she sunk into the sea, taking 1,496 souls to the bottom of the ocean. 
What caused this modern marvel to sink? There are many answers; the responses as vast as the ocean itself.

Travel back to that night of April 14, 1912, and prepare your lifeboat with necessities, save passengers, and discard excess baggage to escape the sinking ship. Learn fascinating facts about passengers who don’t often make headlines, collect vital equipment from the early 1900s ocean voyage period, see items from the ship's true manifest, and discover what it was like to sail RMS Titanic – and how people survived the horror.

The card deck contains 70 cards for 2-4 players. Players receive a Lifeboat card, then play to gather a number of survivor cards and equipment cards while discarding luggage cards. Each card has a point value. There are two ways to win using points and strategy. The game is based on real life incidents (each survivor is correctly matched with their actual lifeboat, for example) and years of research into Titanic's history.

—description from the publisher
family: History: The Sinking of the Titanic
publishers: Best True Crime Books, Games & Video LLC

description:
Ruthless Merchants is a tabletop game in the pick up and deliver genre. The goal is simple, hold 5 coins at the end of a round and you win. You can choose to play peacefully and perform deliveries. Or you can play aggressively and punch/throw rocks at other players to get them to drop their coins.

There are 6 characters you can choose to play. Move them around the marketplace and interact with other players. You can play one of six actions each turn. Move, pickup a coin or item, deliver an item to your destination for coins, punch a player, throw a rock at a player, or take a nap to remove your dizziness.

You will need to balance between being peaceful and collecting your coins through deliveries and aggressively stealing other players coins.

The fun of the action happens around the turn mechanic which determines the order of play. All players choose their action card, places it in a pile and then the pile is shuffled. Cards are dealt from the pile which determines the order of play. This means you can play a punch card, but your victim might move away from you before you can strike.

-description from publisher

Big world oceans chart but small, fast 3-12 turn "sudden-death" game system.  15 minutes?  Free print-and-play.

Russian doomsday super submarine Belgorod - it's even a mothership for little subs, although that's not in the game - is trying to get on the loose to threaten one of our coasts.  Can you detect and shadow it for 4 of the 12 turns to win?

Or can it stay UNdetected for 3 turns, then ending up near one of our coasts to win?

She starts in the Arctic Ocean, and our US Navy and allied Anti-Sub Task Groups ... and a couple missioned attack submarines (Illinois and Minnesota) ... must find and ride her.
VERY good solitaire game as the Allied player. Just use the 2-side Russian pieces upside-down, randomly mix them up, and move each logically.

With 1 or more combinations of coin tosses or 50% die rolls, the Allied player's naval units attempt to detect her and then if successful shadow her from on or beneath the sea area she is in, with improved odds of continuing to do so.
3 continuous turns of successfully shadowing her wins a quick/sudden-death victory.  However, if she can remain (or again become) unshadowed - whether or not she is detected by satellite - for 3 turns, the last one being in a U.S. coastal sea area, the Russian player instead wins the game.

12 playing pieces - only 1 more than SPI's Strike Force One's 11! - Belgorod and 3 Decoys and 4 Allied ASTGs and 2 SSNs and *their* decoys.

And I can attest that she and her Decoys can bedevil a solitaire Allied player at least as much as a second, Russian player could.

Applicable quotes from the film (and presumably the book) The Hunt for Red October of (Sean Connery's) Marko Ramius:

"Once more, we play our dangerous game, a game of chess against our old adversary - The American Navy. For forty years, your fathers before you and your older brothers played this game and played it well. But today the game is different. We have the advantage.

We will pass through the American patrols, past their sonar nets, and lay off their largest city, and listen to their rock'n'roll while we conduct miss-ile drills. Then, and when we are finished, the only sound they will hear is our laughter, while we sail to Havana, where the sun is warm, and so is the comradeship. A great day, comrades! We sail into history!"

And later:

"... years I have been at sea. A war at sea. A war with no battles. No monuments. Only casualties."

-description from designer

In Fashion Witch you play a card in order to dress a witch, this can be your own, a common or even the witch of another player. You can either put on clothes or replace existing clothing.

Depending on the card you played, you can have special abilities, remove another played card, switch cards, etc.

The game finishes if all witches are fully clothed or the deck runs out.

You score points depending on the secret goal you receive at the beginning of the game, for every witch dressed with the same clothes as you (shoes, dress & hat) and minus point with some cards.

The players take turns to roll and move. Each player has his own route in his color. The Flight starts in Germany and the first player who gets back to his airport of origin wins the game.
On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

The game was produced in the GDR.
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events benefit or hinder the players. The first to reach space 100 wins the game.

Donut! is a simple trick-taking race game for 2 to 6 players. In this game-winning tricks represents selling donuts. Each player owns a Donut Shop with a great reputation. But a great reputation is just not enough in today’s world. You need your Donut Shop to be famous that’s the only way to make it. Each round players will compete to sell the best donuts by playing Donut Cards from their hands. For every Donut Sale they win, their fame will increase by one.

This single-tile mini-expansion can be added to any Carcassonne game. The design, however, matches that of the New Edition of Carcassonne (2014).

This promo was given out (for free) when making a purchase (of at least 20€) at the Hans im Glück booth at Spiel 2022, Essen.

This tile presents a road segment with an inn. No specific rules are included with the tile. The tile is simply mixed with the other landscape tiles. When a player draws the tile, he or she should place the tile according to the usual rules.

Screw'D is a trick-taking game in which the objective is to earn the highest number of points. It consists of a full number-ed deck (1-10), face cards (J, Q, K) of 4 new suits and eight Special cards (one “Flip” card in each suit, two “Wild Screw’D” cards and two “End Trick” cards).

The Ukraine tile was released by HiG in 2022. This limited promo tile was available for the fair in Essen and remainders were sold on the CundCo webshop. The sales proceeds and more go to the NGO Come Back Alive.

There are no special rules. You can use it as a normal tile, as an additional tile for the 1st expansion Exp. 1 - Inns & Cathedrals, where it can be used as an inn, and/or as an additional tile for the Mini 7 - The Crop Circles.

The white building by the lake represents a traditional Ukranian house, that may serve as an inn in the game.

Taotl is a trick-taking game using a 72-card deck that consists of the traditional 52-card deck (1-13 in four suits) along with the "magical cards": a Taotl (the highest card), four Skulls (the lowest cards), a yellow trump suit called Totems, a Red God "Quetzalcoatl" and a Black God "Tezcatlipoca" that can be either high or low situationally.
s) along with four Wizards (high) and four Jesters (low).

Players compete over multiple rounds based on the number of players, and whoever ends with the highest score wins. In each round, players are dealt a hand of cards — one card in the first round, two cards in the second, three in the third, etc.

The playing and winning of the tricks uses mostly standard trick-taking rules. If a player leads a suited card, then all other players must follow suit or play a magical card. If a player leads a Skull, then the second player determines the suit led. If a player leads a Taotl, then those who follow can play whatever they want.

After each player has played a card, determine the winner of the trick as follows: If the Taotl was played, that player wins, collects the cards, and leads to the next trick. If not, whoever played the highest Totem wins the trick, unless someone played a Red God on a red-suited trick or a Black God on a black-suited trick, in these cases the player has the option of declaring their god as higher than a Ten of Totems, but lower than a Jack of Totems. If not, whoever played the highest card of the suit led wins the trick. If all players played Skulls, whoever played the first Skull wins.

After all tricks have been played, players tally their score for the round. If a player matched their bid, winning exactly as many tricks as stated at the start of the round, they score 10 points, plus 3 points for each trick taken. If a player missed their bid, they lose 5 points for each trick that they were off, whether they took more or fewer than predicted.

The total number of tricks bid in a round cannot match the round number, thereby forcing (at least) one player to be off each round.

The booklet attached to the papers was written by the well-known author Alfonso Del Bello, and in 64 pages he explains in the first part the meaning of the cards (which, as far as the traditional suits are concerned, is almost always modeled on the classic one of Etteilla) and a short divination game, and in the second the real game.

The worshippers of a monstrous entity hold unspeakable ceremonies to invoke their idol and enslave humanity. To foil their plan, Professor Armitage enlists the help of an adventurer to infiltrate the cultists and steal their cursed books.
Call Me Cthulhu is a hidden role team game based on the mechanics of the famous Nosferatu.

This time, the good guys become the bad guys, and vice versa!

Call Me Cthulhu brings the same sensations as Nosferatu, but reveals an additional strategic dimension. The games often lead the cultists to make a fundamental choice, much more marked than in Nosferatu: try to eliminate the infiltrator, at the risk of making a mistake, or count on the possibility of bringing his mental health to zero by summoning the most powerful creatures?

—description from the publisher

England, 876. 2-4 Players wrangle the collective conscience of entire cultures, commit horrible sins in the name of their gods, or parcel away tools of revenge in the name of propaganda through acts of clemency. 
Construct a fleet, connect discovered routes build a trade empire - or - tear someone else's down.
The goal of Danelaw is to win either through connecting trade routes and taking over shires and minor interconnected kingdoms by means of military or propaganda and religious heroes.
The catch of Danelaw is players' cultures will be converted back and forth potentially multiple times in a game based on their roles and cards played.
Players roll special D'10s which always bear the potential for critical religious favor or delicious downfall.
Players have access to a unique array of hero cards of three distinct classes. Kin, Propaganda, and Fervor.
Blood feuds bubbling from within your own borders can be vented and tapped through fervor or quelled with propaganda heroes. Be sure not to let them get too high... The mob always comes for the man in the high tower. 
Protect yourselves from them with only your most trusted Kin heroes. But even family can be lost to the voice of god or fate. As heroes of your culture die their legacies remain and new legacies fill their shoes.
Each game of Danelaw is different with unordered map additions added in random variations presenting opportunities that force players to think on their feet and change their approach. 
Convert or subjugate, stoke the coals of war or shackle those who would with propaganda and the axe. 
Keep your kin close, but keep your enemies closer.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Blood & Steel: Victorian Age Combat 1837-1901 – Core Rulebook
description:
Blood & Steel is a skirmish level miniatures game set during the reign of Queen Victoria. It focuses on combat at the fringes of larger battles and is based on the same mechanics as Blood & Plunder and Blood & Valor, but with key changes that makes it a different gaming experience. The following conflicts (and force lists) are included:

– 2nd Seminole War (United States & Seminoles)
– Mexican American War (United States & Mexico)
– US Civil War (Union & Confederacy)
– 2nd Taranaki War (Great Britain & Māori)
– Anglo-Zulu War (Great Britain & Zulu)
- Spanish American War (United States & Spain)

boardgame: Eleven: Football Manager Board Game (Gamefound Edition)
description:
Eleven — the number of players you have on the pitch at any given time, with those players making all the difference between being the best team and the worst. But every team knows that to be the best in the league it takes a lot more than players; it also takes an incredible manager.

Eleven: Football Manager Board Game is an economic strategy game set in a world of sport. Your task is to manage and grow your own football club over the course of a season. During the game, you hire staff members, including trainers, physical therapists, PR specialists, and directors. You acquire sponsors, expand the stadium infrastructure, and take care of your club's position in social media. Among the many tasks on the list are transferring new players and choosing the right tactics for each of the upcoming matches.

Eleven can be played multiplayer or solo. The solo mode includes six different scenarios that challenge players with different starting situations and goals for the season. In the beginning, the task is simple: You have to climb the steps of the football leagues and achieve the appropriate experience. You may have to manage the club in a crisis, and at other times you will have to rejuvenate a football team of players that are not so young anymore. You may also have to fight against time to try to complete the stadium before the deadline!

—description from publisher

family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
Individually packaged module for Endless Winter.

Place the 12 Canine Familiars face up near the board in a deck.

When you place a figure on your Rest action space, instead of drawing 1 card from your deck, you may take 1 Canine Familiar card and add it to your hand. When taking a Canine Familiar, you may look through the deck and choose any one. Canine Familiar cards may be played in step 2 of your turn as if they were a Tribe card. Their abilities work in the same way as Tribe cards (gain 1/2 Labor and 1 Tool if you have placed a figure on the appropriate Action Column).

Contains 12 Canine Familiar cards and 2 instruction cards.

Individually packaged module for Endless Winter.

Each player places their Aurora Borealis card face down at the bottom of their draw pile. Aurora Borealis cards are not considered Tribe or Culture cads, but can be Buried or discarded, following the usual rules. When in your hand, you may play your Aurora Borealis card at any time on your turn (even before playing Culture cards). Play it face up above your Player board). Once in play, yo may use the ability of your Aurora card as many times as you wish until the card is discarded at the end of you turn.

Contains 4 Aurora Borealis cards and 2 instruction cards.

Individually packaged module for Endless Winter.

Place the Mammoth miniature near the Terrain board. During the game, whenever you gain the ability to move Camps, you may forgo 2 Camp movements to move the Mammoth to any empty juncture of the Terrain board (a place where 3 hexes touch). Unlike Villages, this ca include a juncture touching the Base Terrain tile. During the Eclipse phase, the Mammoth competes for Influence over the hexes it touches as if it is another player. Its Influence is equal to the current Round number. During the Eclipse phase, if you have less influence on a hex than the Mammoth, you do not receive the benefit(s) of that hex. However, if you have equal or greater Influence than the Mammoth (and any other players), gain the depicted benefit(s) of the hex twice. Also compatible with River & Rafts expansion and Glacier tokens.

Contains 1 Mammoth miniature and instructions.

An Endless Winter promo card available through the Endless Winter: Paleoamericans Kickstarter. This promo will have limited availability from the publisher at conventions, publisher's website, etc.

Animal card (shuffled into the bottom half of the deck at setup)

Effect: During the Eclipse Phase, score 1 point for each Sacred Stone tile you have on your Player board.

Contains: 1 Yeti card and 1 instruction card

boardgame: Endless Winter: Squirrel & Nut Promo Cards
description:
An Endless Winter promo card available through Endless Winter: Paleoamericans Kickstarter. These promo cards will have limited availability from the publisher at conventions, publisher's website, etc.

Shuffle the Squirrel card into the top half of the deck at setup.
Shuffle The Nut card into the bottom half of the deck at setup.

Squirrel Effect: During the Eclipse Phase, you may place 2 cards of the Animal deck face up in the Hunting Grounds, then choose 1 Animal card.

Nut Effect: Exchange the Nut with 1 other Animal card in the Hunting Grounds. At the end of the game, if you also have the Squirrel, gain 8 points; if you do not have the Squirrel, lose 5 points.

Imagine Rock-Paper-Scissors, but good. Imagine four people could play at the same time (and it was still good).

Jack Labrador is a high-action, take-that beer and pretzels card game. It adds two new symbols to traditional Rock-Paper-Scissors, ability cards, and a soupçon of strategy to keep things interesting.

The Jack Labrador deck consists of 110 cards, comprising three games:

MOTOR CITY SHOWDOWN
The flagship game, for 2-4 players. 30-60 minutes.
Players attempt to out-strategize or out-guess their opponents, playing and anteing cards and throwing Rock-Paper-Scissors(-Jack-Labrador). Action cards can affect who wins ties, steal an opponent’s anted cards, or if the stars align, cause a Motor City Showdown, where the entire deck of points is on the line! Cards are points, and the first player to reach a predetermined total (usually 500 or 1000), wins.

SPELL MY NAME
A quicker, heads-up game, 2-4 players. 30 minutes.
Players attempt to win five cards spelling J-A-C-K-Labrador via a series of one-on-one Rock-Paper-Scissors(-Jack-Labrador). Play a single hand or play for points.

PINK SLIP
Per the creator, “The ultimate card game for delivering the thrill of victory and the agony of defeat.” Each copy of Jack Labrador comes with a single Pink Slip, meant to be signed and dated by the owner. This can be anted against another owner’s Pink Slip—winner keeps both Pink Slips and earns bragging rights for life.

Squirrel Nation is a family card game for 3-4 players. In the game, players compete to build the happiest and most well-fed community of squirrels in the city park. To succeed, you will need to overcome many threats to your community such as a tree-destroying woodpecker, a trash-devouring raccoon, acorn-stealing chipmunks, and one very hungry owl!

A game of Squirrel Nation is made up of rounds where players bid using acorns to take columns of cards from a 3x3 grid (3 players) or 4x3 grid (4 players). In a "once-around" round of open bidding, the players select a column of cards, going from highest to lowest bidder. Most cards will be beneficial by adding squirrels, trees and food to your community. Many squirrels come with special actions or abilities, such as adding trees or acorns to your community. However, dangers lurk! Other critters have their own agenda that may get in the way of your plans.

The game ends after 6 rounds of play. Any squirrels not in a tree run away. All other squirrels are worth points based on how many Food cards you have of their preferred food type (Walnuts, Seeds or Peanuts). Highest score is the winner.

-description from designer

Tournament Fishing: Night Fishing Expansion is a stand-alone, solo version of the hit game Tournament Fishing.

This expansion can be be played as a solo game or integrated into the base game by simply adding the starter deck to the base game allowing for a 5th player.  The Bait Shop and Lake Cards can also be integrated into the game but are not necessary.

This expansion also adds a third AI angler to the two that come with the base game

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 新幕 桜降る代に決闘を 第七拡張：徒桜団円 (A Duel for Falling Cherry Blossoms: New Act – 7th Expansion)
description:
The seventh expansion, Sakura Danen, concludes the storyline from the epic battle that started with the third expansion.

This box set includes two alternate Goddess tarots for Yurina and Yatsuha, the 6th series of Megami Challenges and update cards for other goddesses for the latest balance update (S7-2).

『第七拡張：徒桜団円』は『桜降る代に決闘を』シリーズの拡張です。『第参拡張』より始まった新たな物語が最後の戦いを終え、ついに結末を迎えます。

『第七拡張』は新しい形態での中型セットであり、２柱の新たなアナザー版メガミと特殊な対戦モードであるメガミへの挑戦シリーズ６作が収録されています。さらに既存のメガミたちに新たなる力を授けるカード更新パックも同梱。

通常ゲームモードの新たなる環境を、そして特殊ゲームモードの物語とクレイジーなカードたちをお楽しみください。

boardgame: Aleph Null: Promo – The Penis Tree
description:
Promo card for Aleph Null handed out at Spiel 2022.

Cost to summon: 2

Effect while in play:

Sacrifice -> Choose 2 different effects:
- Discard or scrap 1 card.
- Sacrifice 1 card from your hand.
- Gain 1 Magic Power.
- Draw 1 card.

It replaces the "The Weeping Tree" card in the deck.

Aside (from Tony): During playtesting, this alternative art version was used and we joked - with Matthias of Frosted Games - that it could not really be printed in the final version. As it turned out, I arrived on their booth for Day 1 of Spiel 2022 to find they had printed it as a separate promo card...in English and German (Penisbaum) versions! It's functionally-identical to 'The Weeping Tree' but with Alex's version of a REAL Medieval manuscript cartoon - there are lots of this sort of thing out there on those old books: done by miffed/horny monks!

In the distant future, a mysterious bioweapon called Downez that started as a malicious computer virus, achieved sentience and annihilated most of human civilization!! Bummer.. though a few techno-radical groups had foreseen this, for they developed GridL, a super-computer program which tried to preserve as much life as possible by storing them as advanced code on an infinite grid. It's goal was to mimic natural selection and restart life, once conditions were favorable in the outside world.

Luckily, you are one of the few humans who were saved by GridL. Now locked permanently inside the program since ages, you must try to get out by passing the GridL Test - a bizarre game that will decide if you are worthy enough to survive outside! GridL will assay your decoding and communication strengths - are you smart enough to work as a team, and qualify for the same?

In the game of GridL, two teams must successfully transmit finite solutions coded on a 5x5 Grid, to their teammates. The catch - each axis of the grid gets a random generated word, and communication limiters force players to use restricted word clues connecting these two words! Teams hope to cross the barrier of 12 points before the opponent team to pass the GridL test and win the game!

In a 2-3 player variant, all players work together as a team and try to gather enough points before specific number of turns! In an advanced variant, teams can choose some mini programs (12 in number) - powers designed to help teams optimize their scores if timed and played strategically!

-description from designer
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description: 100 new cards with 2000 answers.

From the mid-18 th century through the 19 th century, the Western world experienced a period of rapid technological innovation that transformed its manufacturing industries.
You are the mayor of a British city, striving to achieve new prosperity in the midst of the Industry Revolution by developing your municipality into a hub of industry through the introduction of factories and railroads.
Simply expanding your city is not enough; you will also need to secure coal to fuel the steam engines powering these technological marvels.
Will you succeed in creating the world’s greatest industrial city and leave a lasting legacy as its mayor?

In the game, players are bidding in an auction for tiles that they will add to their city, generally overlapping already existing tiles.  In the auction, rather than bidding money, players bid with how many squares of their existing city they will cover with the tile they acquire.  However, at the end of the game, some of the scoring will be based upon who has the most squares showing of certain types. Some squares require special resources to operate, and other squares cannot be covered.
publishers: analog lunchbox (アナログランチボックス)

description:
In the Oceans: Solo expansion, you face an automated opponent named Oceanic. Explore the depths alone and discover if your species can survive and thrive against the challenges of unknown waters.

The expansion features no changes to the core rules of Oceans and an automated opponent that is simple to learn. Oceans: Solo can be used both to learn how to play the base game of Oceans as well as to play by yourself.

After achieving the minimum score for victory, players have the option of multiple levels that increase the difficulty. Between various ways to modify, players are able to control the complexity and challenge that best suits them.

—description from the designer

Micro Mars is a roll-and-write game of open space adventure and exploration. Each player is the starship's captain and requires a single printed game sheet, a pencil, and two dice.

In order to move about the map, roll 2 dice. In the route, the field finds the figures for the dropped values. Since you have two figures, you are able to combine them into one in the planning field. Then, you can put it on the map as a completed figure, so that will be your move.

During your move, you must avoid meteorites and asteroids. The game's main aim is to collect 5 repair parts for your spaceship. If you are lucky, you can stumble upon an oxygen balloon, which may help you along the way. Remember that you have only 13 moves.

—description from the designer

This is the two players only stategic game. Players take the role of the army general and maneuver their land units. Terrain on the map limits units' moves and impacts their powers.

Goal:
Using the land units generals try to:
   1. eliminate all enemy units
OR 2. imprison the enemy leader
OR 3. conquer the enemy leader's starting point.

Warships is a straightforward game. There is no random element here. It is only two players, two armies.

Special Promo for backers of the 2022 Dice Tower Kickstarter contained in Promo Pack Egg.

Woodcutter = Gain 1 Gold or 1 Ink Well when taking a Build action
Overseer =  Gain 1 Ink Well or 1 Stone when taking a Noble action

boardgame: 1 CAR D: Stock Car Racing Game
description:
You drive a powerful next generation Cube Blackstang. Go as fast as you can, but watch out, the tires wear out, the fuel runs out and the engine gets hot. To advance you must make careful drafting maneuvers. Try to find a good place in the pack and try to stay calm when you get hit. If you reach the checkered flag first you will be the winner!

The game is like a kind of puzzle that is based on the relative speed of the cars: the fastest cars you go up, and the slowest you go down. And the upper and lower limits of the track help keep the cars from getting too far apart.
The game requires patience, strategy and some luck to win. Perhaps you will be the first on the track, but the rivals will not allow it for a long time... Your position may seem secure, but everything can change in an instant. Let's run!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Eagle Leader: Expansion #1 – Luftwaffe and RAF
description:
Take to the skies over Germany with the West Germans and British in the Luftwaffe and RAF pack. Featuring aircraft such as the Panavia Tornado, the F-4F Phantom, the Jaguar A, this pack expands the fight to feature the plight of the RAF and the Luftwaffe in the 1980s. This set contains:

This set adds 54 cards and 50 counters to your Eagle Leader game, including:


     54 x Aircraft cards (Tornado GR1 / Tornado ADV / Phantom FGR Mk2 / SEPECAT Jaguar RAF // Tornado IDS / F-4F Phantom / F-104G Luftwaffe)
     2 x Squadron Asset cards (Alpha Jet)
     18 x Skill counters
     1 x Pilot Skills sheet

-description from publisher

boardgame: Eagle Leader: Expansion #2 – Hellenic Air Force
description:
The pilots of the Hellenic Air Force were charged with defeating the Soviet Black Sea Fleet.  With a mix of American aircraft from across three generations of jet fighters (Including the F-102 Delta Dagger, the A-7H Corsair II, and the F-16 ), the Greeks had their work cut out for them.

This set adds 54 cards and 48 counters to your Eagle Leader game, including:


     42 x Aircraft cards (F-4E / F-5 / F-16C / F-102 / A-7H)
     12 x Target cards
     4 x Skill counters
     1 x Pilot Skills sheet
     3 x Campaign sheets


-description from publisher

boardgame: Eagle Leader: Expansion #3 – Italian Air Force
description:
During the 1980s, the Italian Air Force was one of the most capable in NATO. Flying the Panavia Tornado IDS, as well as the A-11 Ghibli and older aircraft such as the G. 91, the Italian Air Force found itself split between helping the Greeks on the Mediterranean front and NATO in Germany. The Italian Air Force also fought the Libyans and Syrians. Take to the skies with the Italian Air Force and face all new foes as you fight to stop the crushing onslaught of global communism!

This set adds 54 cards and 32 counters to your Eagle Leader game, including:


     45 x Aircraft cards (Tornado IDS / F-104S / G-91 / G-19Y / AMX A-11Bs)
     3 x Target cards
     4 x Event cards
     2 x Special Condition cards
     9 x Skill counters
     1 x Pilot Skills sheet
     3 x Campaign sheets


-description from publisher

boardgame: Eagle Leader: Expansion #4 – French Air Force
description:
In the Eagle Leader timeline, France maintained their unusual separation from NATO, but as the situation on the ground worsened, the French government felt compelled to intervene. While most of NATO’s Air Forces flew American aircraft, or at least used American weapons, the French had their own capable aerospace industry. This set adds 56 cards and 88 counters to your Eagle Leader game, including:


     54 x Aircraft cards (Mirage F1C / Mirage IIIE / Mirage 2000C / SEPECAT Jaguar)
     2 x Event cards
     3 x Campaign sheets


-description from publisher

NATO never fights alone. Even the smallest nations of NATO have well-trained pilots and capable aircraft. In the Minor NATO pack, you’ll gain access to seven new nations, each with a distinctive aircraft. The Dutch bring F-16s for multi-role work. The Norwegians bring nimble F-5s, while the Portuguese bring heavy hitting A-7 strike aircraft. The Danish fly the J-35 Draken, and the Spanish bring their Hornets. The Belgians enter with Mirage 5 strike aircraft, while the Turkish fly their F-4s. Finally, each nation will have national skills to help their pilots feel distinct and get the most out of their airframes.

This set adds 56 cards and 54 counters to your Eagle Leader game, including:


     54 x Aircraft cards (F-4E Turkey / F-5 Norway / F-16C Netherlands / EF/A-18 Spain / J-35 Denmark / Mirage 5BA Belgium / A-7P Portugal)
     2 x Event cards
     14 x Skill counters
     1 x Pilot Skills sheet


-description from publisher

The iconic USAF fighter pilot dominates the idea of what a fighter pilot should be. In the Eagle Leader Aces pack, you’ll gain the ability to take legendary aces into your squadron. This set adds 54 cards and 54 counters to your Eagle Leader game, including:


     54 x Aircraft cards (F-15C / F-15E Strike Eagle / F-16C / F-16 ADF / F-4G Wild Weasel / F-111F / A-4D)
     54 x Aircraft "Ace" counters


-description from publisher

As the enemy advances, prototypes are hurriedly rushed into battle, while trainers and aggressor units are drafted for frontline work. The Experimental Aircraft pack offers a speculative, "what if?" designs which never made it past the design stage in real-life, allowing you to take some of these cutting-edge, innovative aircraft into action.      54 x Aircraft cards (F-15 STOL / F-16XL / F-18 HARV / F-20 Tigershark / AST-21 Tomcat / F-29 / X-31 / F-21 Lion / Mirage G)

boardgame: Twin Stars: Adventure Series III – Scenario 13: Topple the Giant
description:
2 new characters and a scenario that were never released. These were created in 2020(?) with big silly plans to work on Twin Stars III and a combination of life and computer crashes slowed it down. We lost a few things, but still had parts of these, so we cobbled the rest together as a prototype edition. Enjoy!

Topple the Giant is a scenario in the Twin Stars solo system by Jason Tagmire and Mike Mullins. The game consists of just 3 cards and players will provide their own dice and coins/tokens. Each scenario comes with 2 all-new characters and can be interchanged with others for a very different gaming experience.


This scenario has been released separately at ShyCon Nashville 2022.

Wolf & Raven is a 2-player cooperative asymmetrical deck-building campaign game.

In Wolf & Raven, 2 players take on the two roles: one plays the Wolf and the other plays the Raven. They must pay costs for each other to hone hunting skills and overcome hazards on their journey to spot and eventually catch their prey before hunger overwhelms them.

Primary mechanics include deck-building, hand management and path selection. With 4 suits: Eyes, Paws, Fangs and Feathers, the gameplay is highly tactical and also boasts a deep overarching strategy as the campaign develops.

Wolf & Raven has a rogue-like campaign structure through the four seasons: Spring, Summer, Autumn and finally Winter.

—description from the designer

Tokyo, 2034 AD. After the "regeneration phenomenon" happened ten years, 1 out of 1,000 people in this gigantic Far Eastern city has acquired magic. Worried about the frequent occurrence of "magic crimes", the authorities selected "wizards" who manipulate particularly powerful magic as special police officers. In view of the public opinion that magic is dangerous, they are called two-headed demon dogs that guard the gates of hell. "Magic Police Orthros" ― ― It was the birth of the Metropolitan Police Department Criminal Division Magical Crimes Section.

Every player chooses two characters as his enforcer pair, and mix the cards from that two character as their deck. During the game, players simutaneously choose cards from their hand to make corresponding actions, cast magic, and deal damage to criminals. Who makes the criminal strengthless arrests the criminal and earns credits. Players race to get 12 credits and whoever has the most credits is the winner.

The dinner rush has just started in the town of hamfort.

As the hungry customers file in, it’s up to you to send your heroes out into the wilds and retrieve the precious ingredients that your chefs will need to satisfy each and every patron in your restaurant.

At the beginning of each round, players roll their hero dice to form their hero pools, then take turns recruiting their heroes to send on expeditions to defeat the monsters in the 4 wild zones, or even send their heroes out into their restaurant to take new orders. Defeated monsters will award players with ingredients which will be used to craft delicious dishes and earn tips. After 10 rounds, the player with the most tips is declared the winner, and best chef in Hamfort.

Roll, plan, and cook your way to victory in this fast-paced dice placement game for 2-4 players. Simple to understand, and deep with strategy, Appetite For Adventure is designed to be perfect for casual and hardcore gamers alike.

—description from the designer

This prologue to Flashback: Zombie Kidz lets you visit a mini memory.

The Kidz have launched an expedition to retrieve a valuable gizmo, and getting back into he barricaded school was no easy task! Using the Flashback technology, reconstruct the day's events.

In HINT, your teammates have to guess what you are trying to tell them. Along the way you both mime, draw, hum and talk. You must be both quick and creative, but beware! There is always one forbidden word that you may not use. An entertaining party game for everyone that gets the hint.

HINT is ein Partyspiel, im dem du auf verschiedene Arten Hinweise gibst. Mime, summe, erkläre und zeichne die verrücktesten Dinge, um dein Team auf die richtige Spur zu führen!

MetaCorps is an “easy to learn, hard to master” card game for two to four players aged 14+ and set in a bleak Cyberpunk background where MetaCorporations fight for Intel. 
It is meant to fit a pocket and be played on small tables in about half an hour, somewhat  independently of the number of players (add more time with variants).

MetaCorps is meant to have a steep learning curve and be a  real challenge for the most seasoned and competitive players, with variants allowing to diminish the randomness to a bare minimum.

If the  circumstances allow it, it is also meant to receive a few expansions and stand alone/expansions that will create a whole narrative arc that describes a world slowly dissolving into a nightmarish apocalypse.

—description from the publisher

R'lyehian seizures:
"While there is a wound of this card, you may only communicate in gestures and gibberish. If you don't remove the token and take two wounds.

If there is a wound on this card remove the wound token and heal all your stress. Otherwise, place a wound token on this card".

Ozeango is a two-player only game inspired by chess. Two players use their six sea creatures in a 4x4 grid to try to capture the other player's animals and checkmate their Octopus. Once a player has captured an opponents animal, they can use it in their team. Each animal has a different way it can move around the grid.

—user summary

Checkmate the king of the deep sea.
Simple rules, sophisticated ways to play, animals with individual characteristics. If you capture an opponent's animal, you can reintroduce it as your own.
Includes 2 expansions.

—description from the publisher

The game comes with two bonus animals - the Dolphin and the Pufferfish - to add some variety to the game.

Hero Brawl is a wrestling card game where you compete for the Hero Brawl Title Belt while trying to knock out all the cards in your opponents' hands.

Each player starts with 3 cards in-hand. On each turn, the player will take a card and play a card (taking whatever action is detailed on the card played).

The goal of the game is to force your opponents to discard all their cards, while also collecting the title belt for yourself.

—description from the designer

These 4 special new Workshop tiles allow you to perform more powerful actions every time you visit the Market.

During setup, starting with the last player and finishing with the first, each player chooses 1 of these tiles. If there
are any extra tiles, they are returned to the bag.

During the game, instead of placing the tile you obtain normally when claiming a Cathedral section, you can discard the one you just obtained and place your special Workshop tile.
The cost is not the usual cost: instead, the cost is equal to the number of available spaces in your Workshop: if you have 6 empty spaces (i.e., you are going to place your first tile), it will cost you 6 Rubles to place it. If you have only 2 spaces
free (i.e., you have already placed 4), it will cost you 2 Rubles, and so forth.

Tile effects:

- Obtain 1 Diamond (Its function is the same as described in the Jeweler's Guild.)
- Obtain 1 Material of your choice and 1 Ruble.
- Activate the Market die of your choice.
- Activate any 1 of the 4 Guilds as usual.

-description by the publisher's website

Promo was included in the first print run of the game.

The game simulates a number of engagements that took place during the Battle of the Falaise pocket, which took place between 12 and 21 August, 1944. As a result of this battle, most of Army Group B deployed west of the Seine river was annihilated by the Allied Forces. This opened the way to Paris, and eventually the liberation of France from Nazi occupation.

This is the second edition of the Falaise 1944 game by Taktyka i Strategia published in 2022, and it uses the B-35 wargaming system (i.e. Bitwy II wojny światowej 1943-45).

Falaise 1944, 2nd ed., features the following four scenarios:


     Totalize
     Falaise
     Black Division
     Montormel

description:
In August 1995, the whole world was engulfed in war for the third time. There were several causes, but at the end of the day, it was simply a matter of the Eastern powers tipping the scales to correct the "balance of power" that had begun to crumble. But with tanks and missiles in both hands.
The focus of the fighting was in Central Europe, the economic and cultural center. Two hundred thousand Red Army soldiers, who were supposed to be reduced in number, crossed the East-West German border one after another, supplementing and defeating NATO (North Atlantic Treaty Organization) forces that were moving in surprise attacks at various points. Hamburg and Haber fell on the second day of the faith, and Bremen on the third day. On the North German Plain, the traditional West German armored divisions also faced the oncoming Red Storm, but they had a hard time.
The fighting within West Germany was coming to an end. A Red Army tank division surrounded the Ruhr industrial area. The garrison resisted stubbornly, but its destruction was only a matter of time.
Around that time, the U.S. contingent, finally on European soil, began its advance. To save Europe, which was in dire straits.

This game simulates a possible battle in Europe in the near future. If the Warsaw Pact forces, led by the Soviet Union, invade West

—description from the back of the box (translated)

「1995年8月、三度全世界が戦火に包まれた。その原因は幾つかあったが、詰まる所は崩れ始めた「力の均衡（バランス・オブ・パワー）」を是正するために、東側諸国が秤に手を加えただけの事である。ただし、両手に戦車とミサイルを持って。
戦いの焦点は経済・文化の中心、中央ヨーロッパであった。削減されたはずの赤軍兵士20万人は、次々と東西ドイツ国境を越え、各所で奇襲に動じるNATO（北大西洋条約機構）軍を補足し、撃破していった。信仰2日目にしてハンブルクとハーバーが、3日目にはブレーメンが陥落した。北ドイツ平原でも伝統ある西ドイツ装甲師団が襲いくる赤い嵐に立ち向かっていたが、苦戦を強いられた。
西ドイツ国内の戦いは終わりを迎えようとしていた。赤軍戦車師団がルール工業地帯を包囲したのである。守備隊は頑強に抵抗したが、壊滅は時間の問題だった。
その頃、ようやくヨーロッパの地を踏んだアメリカ派遣軍が進撃を開始した。窮地に立たされたヨーロッパを救うために。

このゲームは近い将来、起こりうるヨーロッパでの戦闘をシミュレートしています。もし、ソ連を中心とするワルシャワ条約機構軍が西ドイツに攻め込んできたら、果たしてヨーロッパは誰の手に落ちるのでしょうか？ 同シリーズ第4作『北海道侵攻』と並んだ問題作です。」

—description from the back of the box

boardgame: Nothing to Lose: The Attack on Tan Son Nhut Airbase January 31, 1968
description:
“I will never forget the night, that Tan Son Nhut was hit. “C Flight was on duty then, we knew that this was it. We hit the dirt and looked around with anxious waiting eyes, and said a prayer as mortars came raining from the skies” –Opening stanza of an anonymous veteran’s poem about the attack at Tan Son Nhut.

When the Communist forces of North and South Vietnam launched the 1968 Tet Offensive one component was an attack on the Tan Son Nhut airbase north and west if Saigon. This was the largest airbase in South Vietnam, with its aircraft ferrying men and material from the US, as well as basing combat aircraft that support military operations throughout all the region.

The Communist offensive was supported by the National Liberation Front’s 9th Infantry Division  that had recently been rebuilt following extensive losses fighting the US and ARVN (Army of the Republic of [South] Vietnam) by the attachment of the 267th and 269th Main Force Battalions. The assault force would also include sappers attached to the division’s 1st Battalion of the 271st Regiment who were armed with satchel demolition charges used to destroy the base’s aircraft and air control towers, as well as the division’s mortar and rocket artillery batteries. The battle here would usher in a new phase of the Vietnam conflict. Can you do as well or better than your historical counterpart?

Each copy of Nothing to Lose is composed of the following:

Two 11 by 17 inch map area map sheets with game tracks and tables.

One sheet of 80 double-sided, unmounted counters

One rules booklet

A standard deck of playing cards (a custom card set is available) and ten sided die are needed to play the game.

Each turn of the game represents one hour of time. Units are mostly companies (I). Tank units represent 2 (full strength) or 1 (reduced) vehicles. An inch on the map is about 200 yards.

Players use playing cards to determine which player gets to activate and with how many units, as well as determine when to determine random events for the turn and the end of the turn. The die is used to resolve combats (assault, air and artillery strikes), rally units and perform other game functions. The game is designed by Paul Rohrbaugh and features graphics by Nils Johansson.

-description from designer

description:
Alternative Airborne Landing Locations for Holland '44 by Trevor Bender: Trevor Bender drops into Operation Market Garden again with a "variant (that) is a follow-on to my previous article in Special Ops Magazine #4, pp. 10-13 in 2013 titled, Operation Market Garden as a Topic for Operations Research. There I laid out rules for alternative drop locations and timing for MMP’s Monty’s Gamble."

This variant contains 4 pages of instructions and a set of counters, included in C3i Magazine Issue #33.

Join Peppa Pig and her friends to celebrate Fruit Day. You will have the opportunity to make your own smoothie from three random (the first game mode) or selected fruits (the second game mode). This seeming easy task will require you to be perceptive and a little bit lucky. The first one make their smoothie wins the game.

Send letters from South Korea to North Korea and back again, connecting separated families through correspondence.

The goal of the game is to be the first to complete 3 mission cards by connecting the cities on the map.

Each round you play a card from your hand and build a road at the corresponding triangle.

Theme: NHL Ice Hockey
Game-Play: Dice and strategical movement
Acquire a certain number of goals before the other player (rolled randomly at the start).

Win face-offs, move tactically, pass and shoot to score goals. The roll of the die tells you how many action points you have to spend and you repeat the same number until you lose the puck while the defender rolls anew each round until they steal the puck. Saves and penalties stop the play. Penalties are played like Battleship with each player selecting, in secret, penalty spots.

It's been a way too long since you've played with the old band. Now you're all together in Music City for one last performance.

Players work together to make an improvised song out loud. Each card is an intrument with a limited range of sounds. Once you receive a card, you may contribute to the song using only those sounds.

After a short delay to let the first instrument find their groove (rhythm, pitch and genre are up to you), the next player draws a card and joins in. Repeat this until all players have a card. Now you've got a band!

Once all players have a card, anyone can point to another player to nominate them to do their solo. After a solo is completed, the soloist will point to another player that hasn't performed a solo yet. Continue until all players have performed a solo. The last player to perform their solo should wrap up the song.

The game consists of a roulette spinner and rules for a number of games of chance. While older versions include rules for 4 games and a spinner with one ring of numbers, newer ones have rules for 14 games and a spinner with two rings of numbers.

—user summary

boardgame: Hipp, hipp, hurrah: Das Fußballspiel zu Hause
description: The game board consists of a soccer field wich goals on each side. The players take turns to roll and move. The first player to get his three meeples from his corner of the game board (Anstoß) into the opposite goal wins the game. Where the tracks cross, meeples may be captured and returned to their "Anstoß" space.

Each player receives a dachshund figure, the footplates of the figures are provided with different numbers to distinguish them. The players take turns to roll and move.
If a player gets to a red dot on the game board, he has to go forwards or backwards to the next brown dot in the running direction of the dachshund depicted.
The red lines running to the center of the board entitle the player to proceed towards the goal (120).
The black lines, on the other hand, force him to go outwards. But if you are unlucky enough to get to 114 or 104 or 55, you have to start again from 1.
The game is only won if the number 120 is reached exactly.

HAVE YOU EVER WANTED TO BE A DETECTIVE? NOW IS YOUR CHANCE!
Contains three cases - each one is an exciting mystery to solve and a fun story to unravel. And this time, it's an adventure for the whole family!

Delve into the world of The Detective Society, interact with the characters, solve the puzzles and see if you can crack the case!

THE MISSING POTION
Professor Errol has lost his invisibility potion and needs your help to get it back before everything starts to disappear!

TIME TRAVEL TROUBLE
A time travelling clock has been stolen from a museum, and four of its artefacts have vanished from history. Can you work out who is responsible?

THE SMASHED PIÑATA
Gavin has had his birthday piñata smashed and you need to find out who did it! But the biggest mystery you need to solve is why...

Age guidance 8+.
Recommended adult supervision.
Internet enabled device required for digital parts.

End of June 1866. After several months of growing tensions, war broke out between Austria and Prussia for control of the primacy in Germany. Prussia plans to take its troops through the Sudetenland and into Bohemia through four different passes, with the goal of facing the Austrian main army in a decisive Napoleonic battle.
The end of one of these passages is located here, near the town of Trautenau. On June 27, the 10th Austrian corps pushed back the 1st Prussian corps there. But this tactical victory, the only one for Austria in Bohemia, obtained at the cost of heavy losses will have no strategic consequences: the next day, the 10th Corps is swept away by the Prussian Guard during the Battle of Soor.

New module in the series "Battles in the time of Napoleon III", Trautenau et Soor, 1866 is the second wargame in a series that will cover the main clashes of the Bohemian campaign of 1866 and will conclude with the battle of Sadowa (or Könnigrätz). Players will have the opportunity to chain battles in a campaign mode and end up, perhaps, in a very different situation during the decisive confrontation of July 3, 1866.

Using the series "The Great Battles of Napoleon III" (VaeVictis n° 55, n° 73, 138 and 157), the system is simple and dynamic, emphasizing the engagement of troops in the face of deadly defensive fire.

Scenarios by Arnold Giraud-Moser on a system by Luc Olivier.

One turn: one hour
Counters: regiments and battalion
Map: one hex. = 750m
Complexity: 5/10
Duration of a game: 2 to 4 hours depending on the scenario

The first volume of the campaign of 1866 is Nachod/Skalitz (https://boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/340004/nachod-and-skalitz-1866)
The third volume of the campaign of 1866 is Münchengrätz/Gitschin (https://boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/418334/munchengratz-gitschin-1866)

description:
In this construction game you will be in charge of a work team and you will have to manage the materials and tools to build a house. Whoever best carries out the work, will be the winner of the game. Final de Obra (End of Work), a great challenge for builders of all ages.

Players must build a house by choosing the most convenient workers, tools and materials and combining them to get the highest possible score. Each player has a board, which on one side is a work and on the other the finished house. You must take tiles from a bag and complete your board. You must match the workers with the tools or materials. As you complete it, you add points.

The player who obtains the most points, once the houses are built, will be the winner of the game.


¿Te animas a dirigir una obra? En este juego de construcción estarás a cargo de un equipo de trabajo y deberás administrar los materiales y herramientas para construir una casa.  Quien mejor lleve adelante la obra, será el ganador de la partida. Final de Obra, un gran reto para constructores de todas las edades.

Los jugadores deben construir una casa, para ello deberán elegir los trabajadores, herramientas y materiales más convenientes y combinarlos para obtener el mayor puntaje posible. Cada jugador tiene un tablero, que de un lado es una obra y del otro la casa terminada. Debe sacar losetas de una bolsa e ir completando su tablero. Debe combinar los trabajadores con las herramientas o materiales. A medida que lo va completando va sumando puntos.

El jugador que obtenga más puntos, una vez construidas las casas, será el ganador de la partida.

How far will you go to fight food waste? Play with food to find out!

Tøm Køleskabet (English translation: "Empty the Fridge") is a game that challenges your willingness to fight food waste, by making the players make fun and/or macabre sandwiches. Each player adds one ingredient to a sandwich each turn.

Will you add the pickled herring to the sandwich with the gummy bears? Or perhaps sprinkle watercress on top of the gorgonzola?

Once the sandwiches are made, the active player in a round has to secretly pick a "most likely to eat" and "most unlikely to eat" sandwich from the ones on display.

The other players then secretly tries to guess the active player's preferences, and can score up to two points if they get both "most likely" and "most unlikely" correct.


This game was made for the NGO Folkekirkens Nødhjælp (DanChurchAid)'s 100th anniversary. The main goal of their work is to "assist the world’s poorest to lead a life in dignity, regardless of race, creed, political or religious affiliation. This focus is centered around three core principals:


     Save lives
     Build resilient communities
     Fight extreme inequality worldwide


Folkekirkens Nødhjælp is also a member of the global organisation ACT Alliance

—description from the designer
