Un jeu de cartes, de classement et d’optimisation.

Five years after the death of the Serbian emperor Stefan Dušan the Strong, regional lords are gaining strength in the empire. Some became independent while others were loyal to the young heir to the throne. How you will maintain power and which possessions you will unite is up to you.

Roll dice and gather an army to win the area over to your side. You can acquire "free" territories or from your opponents.But opponent defends with a defense dice. Whoever appropriates more areas and has more points at the end is the winner.

boardgame: Chronicles of Crime: Tantrum House 2021 Promo Cards
description:
Includes 3 new characters to use in Chronicles of Crime: 70, 71, 72.

Distributed as part of the Tantrum House Season 8 Kickstarter campaign in 2021.

Un Tetto In Prato ("A Roof in Prato") is the game of bargaining and investment for those who feel at home in the chaotic creativity of Prato.
Retrace the narrow streets of the center, the agricultural areas of Tavola or the hills of Galceti to invest and give prestige to this "blessed" city!
In this fun game, suitable for young and old, challenge Prato and not to discover the most important monuments and the most particular curiosities of the city to see who will be the richest person in Prato!

—description from the publisher (translated)

The yes or no game for those that love funny Boris Johnson quotes.


     56 quotes from the man who invented Brexit
     Try to decide if Boris said it or not
     Answer correctly to win cards
     Win the most cards to win the game
     It’s a simple yes or no game. Or is it…


—description from the publisher

“Behold, I, even I, will bring a sword upon you, and I will destroy your high places. Your altars shall become desolate, and your incense altars shall be broken, and I will cast down your slain before your idols…”

Save a dying land before darkness comes in this kingdom-defense board game.

The Fall of Oakhaven is a fully cooperative, strategy-adventure game for 1 to 4 players. Players will choose one of four Heroes to hide and preserve the Scrolls in a pick-up-and-deliver fashion. However, the land is being filled with horrible creatures during each round, which will eventually make saving the kingdom much harder. Do you fight the creatures or race to hide the Scrolls before time runs out? Defeating the threats gives you experience points in the form of Blessing, Wisdom, Power and Gold tokens. These tokens strengthen your Skills and Armory cards. Playing your hand of Armory Cards and Skills will require strategy once you enter the 6 Stronghold locations, where boss fights will test your endurance. Will you have enough time?

In the spirit of Defenders of the Realm and Legends of Andor, The Fall of Oakhaven pits Heroes against time and creatures of darkness, in an attempt to preserve the kingdom from complete and utter doom.

—description from designer

Bull Tish is the all-new, all-play, guessing game in reverse!
We give you the answer first, then you're given six facts or clues. Five are true, one is complete Bull Tish... but which one?
As soon as you think a fact or clue is fake, yell it out... Bull Tish!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zap Trap: The Jolt Jumpin' Electronic Game!
description: A multipiece maze with a metallic coating that can be energized by a battery in the base. The players take turns running a wand through the maze. If you hit the maze a headpiece will play a tone and vibrate. Fastest through the maze without hitting the maze wins.

Pixel Star is an expandable and easy to learn 2-4 player space skirmish game, with unique dice drafting action and little pixel ships that pop out of stat cards. An entire fleet can fit in your pocket and be played on any table.

Build your fleet of pixel ships, pop them out of their stat cards, roll all the action dice for the round, and take turns claiming those dice to assign to your fleet. Keep an eye on all the actions available each round though, as you and all your opponents are taking from the same pool of dice!

Each ship has a unique ability or action that might need a particular die side to activate. So knowing what's available to you and your opponents each round is crucial to the strategy of dice drafting. Do you claim a die that isn't as useful to you right now but cuts off your opponent from using it? Or do you claim a die that gets your fleet into a better position straight away?

Easy to teach and playable on any table, Pixel Star gives players all the robust and strategic elements of miniature heavy space skirmish games, in a much smaller package. A full fleet, with all the tokens and dice you need is designed to fit in a standard deck box.

—description from the publisher

The Lands of Lundor are in grave danger. 
A Lich Lord called Ol'zon has been recruiting all kind of Monsters with the help of the ancient "Book of Souls". 
The Queen has chosen you Heroes to be the warriors who finally end this madness. The Lich Lord is hidden in the deepest Dungeon inside the "Calodon Mountains".
Are you brave enough to enter the Dungeon and defeat the Lich Lord?

HeirQuest PockeT is a cooperative game for 1 to 4 players in which a group of Heroes will have to explore a dungeon until they complete the chosen Quest. During the adventure, they will have to eliminate all the threats that come their way and claim all the treasures they can. The game is for ages 12+ that plays in 30-60 minutes created by Victor Robles, designed by Lizuren Games and published by The Game Crafter. Each player chooses a Hero (Elf, Wizard, Amazon, Knight) and must complete a Quest. Players explore the dungeon opening doors and fighting Monsters. They will find treasures like armor and weapons to improve their chances to survive. To attack, the player must roll the combat dice. if his toughness points reach zero, the hero dies.

—description from the publisher

Hattie the Hare and her friends are in the park for a game of hide and seek!

Hide nor Hare combines dice-rolling with sudoku. The game consists of a board, 40 tiles, 10 markers, and 3 dice. Alternately, the game can be played as a roll & write, with just 3 dice and 1 sheet of paper.

Players use dice rolls to fill the park (a 6x6 grid) with Hattie, her Friends, and plenty of Trees to hide behind. To win, each row and column must have exactly one Tree and exactly one Friend. Additionally, players must place at least ten Pigeons in the park. A Pigeon must correctly show the number of Friends visible from its position, looking horizontally and vertically, with Trees blocking the view of Friends.

Each roll, the player can assign one die to a Friend, one to a Tree and one to a Pigeon. If it is impossible to place a Friend or a Tree, they must place a Pigeon instead. If it is impossible to place a Pigeon, they must replace a Pigeon wit  Stone. Too many Stones on the board and it's Game Over. The player has 3 Flowers they can use at any time to move tiles, remove stones, or reroll dice.

—description from the designer

Volley back and forth with your opponent and score the most points to win.

Set & Attack was voted best 2 or more player game, most innovative mechanic, best rulebook, best card-only game, and took 3rd place overall in the 2022 1-Card PnP Design Contest on BoardGameGeek.

GAMEPLAY
After an initial SERVE and RECEIVE, each turn you may SET (pass the ball within your court) or send the ball to the opponent’s court with an ATTACK or SPIKE (strong attack). But be careful – SETS can be misplayed,and SPIKES can be BLOCKED!

For each action you will:

     draw an arrow or other symbol in an empty ball, and
     flip the card. (You may NOT look at the back of the card before flipping) The card is flipped vertically for a SET, RECEIVE or BLOCK, and horizontally for a SERVE, ATTACK, or SPIKE.


You score a point when:

     There are no empty balls in the path of your ATTACK, SPIKE or BLOCK. (Successful ATTACK, SPIKE, or BLOCK)
     There are no empty balls in the path of your opponent's RECEIVE, SET, or BLOCK. (Failed RECEIVE, SET, or BLOCK)


The game ends when there are no empty balls in the back court to serve from.

The BASIC GAME is played with just two actions: RECEIVE & ATTACK.

THE ADVANCED GAME is played with all six actions.

—description from the designer

In this trick-taking card game you are splitting up your haul from the museum in four categories: Jewels, Paintings, Artifacts, and Junk from the Gift Shop. After all the goods have been handed out, whichever thief has left behind the most fingerprints gets busted. Of the remaining crew members, the one with the most valuable stash is the winner!

This game has must-follow rule.

Originally distributed at Essen '22 when you bought the base game.

This promo pack consists of 10 double-letter beasts (like II, DD, RR, ...) to make the base game harder.

A miniature called Hugo with a dice in a fancy box with magnetic lid.

This miniature was given as a promo at Spiel’22 Essen to testplayers to promote their upcoming game ‘The Breach’.

This miniature is playable whit the new game that comes out later.

With this mini-expansion you place bets on how many tiles a road or a city will consist of upon completion. Influence these areas in your favor and get the extra points.
Components: 10 betting place tiles, 36 betting chips, 1 rule.

Amanita is an enchanting set-collecting board game that will win over nature lovers and mushroom enthusiasts with its hand-drawn illustrations and unique mechanics. In the game, players take on the role of mushroom hunters, exploring the world of mushrooms in four different roles over four rounds. To win, they have to collect different species according to their goals for photography, research, trading (market) and cooking (dinner). Players who choose the right strategy can win various bonuses during the rounds, such as an amanita basket or gourmet dinner.

In this medium-weight board game for 2-6 players, players place mushroom cards into their own basket and from their hand onto the field each round, creating four ever-expanding piles of mushrooms in the centre of the playing field. The game consists of four main rounds, with a round consisting of five turns. This makes the total playing time between 30 and 60 minutes.

Mushroom picking is also aided by special rule modifier cards...

—description from the publisher (translated)

Prefect is a print and play game and a spin-off of the anticipated 2023 board game, Age of Rome.

The motivation for its creation is best drawn from the official announcement: “Is it just me, or does anyone else get tired of waiting for a KS game to arrive? Well, we are sure a lot of you might be feeling impatient (in a good way, we hope) about Age of Rome's delivery, so here is a little gift to ease your waiting. We thought it would be cool to design a small spinoff pnp game while Age of Rome is moving into production. And we present to you, Prefect.”

—description from the publisher

In the game, one or more players take the role of Prefect, an administrator of a Roman province managing religion, politics, farming, trade, and military. During each round, players roll three dice and select one to grow the province and two to apply as waste. Players balance growth and waste as well as carefully prioritize their choices to earn the most glory points.

—user summary

The players each have their own player boards and take turns to roll and move. A special tilt die is used to generate numbers between 0 und 59. Rules for three games are included.

Marathon-Prämienspiel (Plan 1)
For this game the tilt die is used like a normal die to get results from 1 to 6. The players start at "Start" and have to get their five meeples, numbered from 1 to 5, to the space marked with "Ziel". If a meeple reaches a red space marked with five dots (tournament bonus space) it advances to the next red spot marked with a "+" (additive bonus space) from wich it advances further according to its own number. If the result of the die and the number of the moved meeple add to "5" its number is considered to be "5" when landing on an additive bonus space. The spaces marked with a double ring (pairing spaces) may be occupied by two meeples, which then move together as a stack.

Lotto-Turnierspiel (Plan 2)
Six meeples, marked 0 to 5, are placed on the board by using the tilt die to get results from 0 to 59. Then all meeples standing on white spaces are placed on the red space marked as "Start B". All meeples standing on the white/red spaces (tournament pre spaces) are placed on the associated red tournament spaces, stacked if necessary. In the next phase of the game the tilt die is uses to produce the digits 0 to 5. The goal of the game is to get all numbered meeples on the tournament spaces with digits match their number. The space "Start A" is used as an interim space to move meeples from a tournament space where they don't belong.

Lotto-Königs-Turnierspiel (Plan 2)
The placement of the meeples is like in the Lotto-Turnierspiel. But in this game the meeples move from ant to the boards of the other players till all meeples end up on the same board and the owner of this board wins the game. The movement of the meeples between the bords is controlled by strict rules wich allows the players no own decisions. So, it's a pure game of chance.

—user summary

Players will challenge a fierce DJ battle tonight as a DJ at a dance club in Yokohama. There is only one turntable available, so you have to respond quickly and accurately to the request cards from the floor.
During the game, you have to throw your own record (with blue and red sides) into the turntable every time the request card fulfills the conditions. Records that do not enter the turntable are invalid, so not only speed but also accuracy is required.
Also, in the "floor killer" that occurs when multiple conditions are met, players will turn around and throw records. In "Chance Card", only certain players can throw records, but it doesn't always go smoothly.

It's a great action game that you can play right away, and it's fun just watching from the side.

Description by-arts1k

A certain musical alchemist’s cat joins the fray! Fight with her against others or compete to take down her terrifying Boss mode!

One new heroine—Beethoven, the feisty magical cat. Her boss form is also included!

boardgame: Trailblazer: The John Muir Trail – Hiking Card Pack
description:
This pack includes 14 trail cards (with unique effects not found in the base game), and six skills cards.
Note that the version included with the Kickstarter edition of the base game only includes the trail cards. The skills cards are already mixed into that deck.

Roll the dice, then be the first to grab the right sweater!

Place the 36 ugly sweater cards on a table, then roll the dice to give a pattern, colour and design. The first player to grab the correct sweater using the provided grabbers wins the round! Be the first player to grab 5 correct sweaters to win the game!

A fun, family-friendly card game that will question your ability to recognize colors. Stroop'd is based off the Stroop Effect. Stroop’d is a cognitive speed game that utilizes this effect.

On your turn you must play 1 card from your hand onto the discard pile and the background color must match the requirements of the top card. If the top card has splattered paint you must play a card from your hand where it's background color matches the FONT of the word on the discard pile. If the top card has a speech bubble then you must play a card where it's background color matches the WORD on the card. There are no turns so play quickly, but only use one hand and only card at a time.

Be the first one to play all of your cards to win. Keep score if you like, no scoring is listed.

So it begins…
…the annual Gulyás Festival!

Everything is ready for the year’s most prestigious event to be successful. Big cauldron. Quality ingredients. Awesome vibe. What could be missing? You probably got it by now... Yes, You! The famous Gulyás Masters. As soon as it turned out that you’ll be cooking the main course, many Gourmets and Locals flocked around the fire to see what you are up to. Their expectations are high and very much different. You will really have to get it together to impress these guys! And you will have to pay attention whether it is a critical kitty or a seasoned chef you want to blow away. Well, what are you waiting for? Start cooking like the Eskimo dress up! (Quick!)

Gulyás is a half-cooperative game where you will need to cook a soup under changing circumstances but working together the whole time. That is until one of you thinks that it’s ready and yells ‘Gulyás!’. Your gulyás is considered successful when its value reaches 13 points. During the course of the game, you will have to cook more than one gulyás. You will get a different amount of victory points for every meal you cook, depending on whether the soup was successfully made, or which Gourmets were impressed. The ultimate winner will be the person who is the best at navigating between the sneaky Locals, the other chefs and the whims of the Gourmets. And most importantly whoever reaches 7 victory points first.

The game is played through several rounds, the players cooking a separate gulyás in each one of them. The players take actions in clockwise order and can decide between three actions: put an Ingredient in the cauldron, distract a Local or stir the soup. Each player has one Ingredient card in their hands, the active player draws another card - unless they stir the soup - and pick one of the two to take an action. The cards you use always go face down on the game board. Each Ingredient has a separate value so you cannot possibly know the exact value of the gulyás, plus the Locals can steal ingredients from the cauldron if you don't distract them. So take extra care before yelling 'Gulyás!'. If you were brave enough and yelled 'Gulyás!' you will have to check the point value of the soup. If you were right and the cauldron does have 13 points worth of Ingredients in it, you get 2 Victory Points. If you were wrong, all the other players get 1 Victory Point. The players have the option to get additional Victory Points by guessing the Gourmet conditions right. After yelling 'Gulyás!' but before calculating the value of the soup, the players have to guess if any (or both) of the Gourmet conditions are met. If you guessed right, you get an additional Victory Point for each Gourmet. Good luck chefs!

—description from the publisher

Live the full, rich and exciting experience of Cricket in just 18 cards: 10 for the Fielding team, 8 for the Batting team. In addition, you need a good-sized table and three dice to represent the 'in' batsmen and the bowler's delivery result.

The fielding team lays out their fielders, nominates a bowler for the Over and then tests the Batsman with a series of testing deliveries; the batsman, in turn, chooses the appropriate shot (3-2-1!) to score as many runs as possible.

Play five overs, 20 overs or a full 5-day test: it's entirely up to you!

—description from the publisher

Abduct specimens for your galactic zoo. Join the Earth Abduction Project and fly your saucer to Earth to gather as many specimens as possible and thus gain the key to success and Earth Domination!

In this exciting card and dice game, you will play as 1 of 6 randomly chosen Aliens.

By throwing dice up to 3 times per turn, you have to charge up your saucer’s beam power and abduct specimens, whether they are humans, animals or objects. Use your harvested DNA tokens for rerolls and play action cards to boost your abilities, or thwart your opponent’s plans.

This fast paced dice action will keep you and your companions guessing who the winner will be until the very end!

—description from the designer

boardgame: War in the West: Expansion Module
description:
An expansion module that allowed players to play the monster war game, War in Europe using the components from War in the West as a base. This is only compatible with the original 1976, Flat Pack edition of War in the West.

According to an SPI ad from the time, the module contains the Exclusive Rules to'-War in the East, the Exclusive Rules to War in Europe, and the attendant charts and tables. It also contains 1200 Soviet and Finnish counters.

The ad further states that "Purchase of the Expansion Module will equip the Player with a complete War in Europe."

Get all the fish tokens and avoid all the obstacles of the city before the other players in order to get into cat's paradise. But, watch out! Don't get distracted along the way. Would you be able to be the first one?

Remember you are playing in two different rooms at the same time.

—description from the publisher

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. If a player gets on an occupied space meeple already there is taken back to its starting space. When reaching space 7 the player gets a golden pearl. If he gets the pearl to his home space, he gets a card from the golden tower and has freed the character pictured on the card. If a meeple gets on a space were a meeple with a pearl is sitting, the first takes the pearl and the later is sent back to its home space. When all 12 characters are freed the player with the most cards wins the game.

boardgame: Les Secrets de la Tour Eiffel
description:
Volume II of the Secret Stories Series.
Competitive mode: 3-5 players
Cooperative mode: 2-10 players


When the Eiffel Tower was inaugurated in 1889, the whole world wondered where this idea came from.

The author of this book, lost for years, was the only person who knew the real story.

This book will reveal all 4 riddles to you to this day.

-With whom did Gustave Eiffel come up with the idea?

-How did you meet?

-Where in Paris?

-What food did you share?

-A player will assume the role of author of the book, without speaking, try to guide the readers with his author cards, to discover the secret code created by the dice.

-The readers will try to discover the 4 riddles. In competitive mode, the first reader to guess the code wins the game along with the author.

Help Elsa navigate the rough waves to bring her face-to-face with the Nokk. But be careful the water is choppy, one wrong move and you may get swept back to shore! The first player to help Elsa reach the Nokk is the winner.

Each player gets 4 Elsas to play with. You move your Elsa by drawing cards from common stock. You can move 1-3 steps forward, 1 step back or rotate the Nokk. By rotating the Nokk random field is opened and if your Elsa is standing on it, she is drawn in ice cold water. And you have to forward with your other Elsa.

Players compete in a "festival," each playing as one of the protagonists from Persona 3, Persona 4, or Persona 5. Each player starts with 30 HP and battle to get their opponent to 0 HP.

Each player character comes with a prebuilt deck consisting of Persona cards and support or react cards featuring other characters from the games.

Released as part of the Persona 25th Anniversary events.
publishers: Banso Inc. (株式会社バンソウ)

description:
You and your friends decide to go on a road trip together. Unfortunately, nature calls!

Players gather a specific set of rest area tiles, determined by the destination card chosen. These tiles are shuffled and placed face-down to form a road. Players are then dealt a role card that tells them how big their stomach is and a starting value card that tells them how full they are at the beginning of the game. If they go under their stomach capacity minimum, they starve; if they go over their maximum, they poop their pants. Without showing anyone else, players grab the number of poop tokens indicated on their starting value cards and hide them with their left hand.

A rest area tile gets revealed every turn, showing how many bathrooms are available to use. Players choose a number between 1 and 5 and take that number of poop tokens with their right hand. Without telling each other how many poop tokens they have, players decide who gets to go to the bathroom. At the end of the turn, all players reveal the number of poop tokens in their right hand. If the player went to the bathroom, they discard that number of poop tokens; if they did not, they take that number of poop tokens from the supply.

If the group successfully makes it to the destination without anyone starving or pooping their pants, they win!

A two-player dice game based on the sport of cricket.
Players roll twelve dice, and plays two dice each "delivery". The sum of each player's dice is compared, which determines the result of that delivery. After a certain number of overs (sets of six deliveries) are completed, the players swap roles, and see who can make the highest score.

—description from the designer

Bring all the fast-thinking fun of the pub quiz to your home with this trivia set that's bursting with questions!

Including 500 questions on everything from sports and pop culture to technology and movies, plus the ever-popular picture rounds, hosting your own pub quiz has never been easier. Simply pick your teams, open the box and get ready for an action-packed evening!

Set contains: 85-page question and answer pad with 500 questions and 10 picture rounds, 6 joker cards, dry wipe scoreboard, dry wipe pen, winners trophy and instructions.

boardgame: Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them
description:
The family game Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them takes you into the magical world of the famous film series.  In the roles of the most popular characters, you try to recapture the escaped beasts in New York.  You can collect powerful spells and magical artifacts that will help you in your hunt.

Exciting event cards bring unpredictable twists to the game.  With a total of three different game modes (ages 4+, 7+, 12+), two competitive and one cooperative, Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them offers magical experiences for all ages.

In the most challenging mode (coop mode for ages 12+) the players work together to capture the escaped beasts. Each player can spend up to 4 action points to move between locations, fight against enemies (Salemers) to liberate occupied locations and gather resources the are needed to capture the beasts. If all beasts are captured, the heroes win. If the Salemers get to strong, the heroes loose.

The vaunted 501st Legion joins Star Wars: Legion with this pack! Led by Jedi General Anakin Skywalker and known for its bravery and unorthodox tactics, the 501st is one of the most renowned units in the Grand Army of the Republic and this set puts an impressive fighting force at players’ command. In addition to Skywalker, players will find two units of Phase II Clone Troopers, a Republic AT-RT, and three units of ARC Troopers to build their Republic Battle Force. To ensure these units are operating at peak efficiency, this set also contains three new Command Cards for use by the 501st Legion as well as all of Anakin Skywalker’s Command Cards and copies of the four generic Command Cards. Finally, a set of 32 reprinted upgrade cards gives players everything they need to outfit their legion to fit their strategy.

—description from the publisher

Party game of trivia questions relating to words that end in 'able'.

Each guest or team is given a card with 32 questions on it. During the evening they are encouraged to fill in all the correct answers, or just guess the ones they don't know. When the host calls "Time", the guest or team with the most correct answers, wins.

The objective of the game is to be the first player to ring the bell by discovering 5 identical vegetables on the game table, and try to eliminate the opponents by obtaining all 56 cards from the deck.


Reimplementation of Halli Galli.

boardgame: Lass die Kirche ins Dorf (2nd Edition)
description:
This is a strategic game of church positioning. Settlements grow & fall, farmers move around & rivers expand through the land.
As the land changes, the clerics want to build or rebuild churches in hopes of gathering the biggest flock. For that to happen, they need to stay close to the settlers.

The objective of the game is to fill the drawer with the six bottles of different colors. To achieve this, each player must answer questions and fulfill the requirements indicated by the "Tomo y Obligo" cards. The first player to get all 6 bottles and manage to stand up to complete one last requirement will be the winner.

Spontaneous is the game to bring out those songs that resonate in your head. Become a Director and make the whole table sing for you.

If there are times when you find yourself singing impulsively, you are a Spontaneous!


Think of a song you remember, take a single word of the lyrics and write it on a piece of paper to make your top five. That word will be one of your keywords. Think of another song and do the same until you have a top five that contains 5 keywords. Once all the participants have their list of keywords, the director starts the game and launches the first spontaneous challenge.

The first Director must flip the hourglass as he announces a word from his own Top Five. The rest of the players must compete to see who is the first to sing a fragment of at least 5 words from any song that contains the keyword.

The first player who begins to sing before the end of time must roll the dice and advance the number of squares that they indicate.

If no one managed to sing all five words of a song, the Director must flip the hourglass again and sing at least five words of a song containing their own keyword before time runs out. Once he has done so, he rolls the dice and moves on. If he fails to prove that the keyword belongs to a real song, he must roll the dice and back off.

When a player reaches the finish square, the participant who is in third place becomes the challenger and challenges the player who is about to win (leader). The leader must sing a song (at least five words) of a keyword written by the challenger at that time.

If the leader meets the challenge, he will be the winner.

A 2-player game with an ordinary deck, with elements of ulti, tarock, schnapsen. Players bid to take 100 card points and for achieving certain feats in winning tricks, such as winning the last trick with a trump 7, or capturing all four aces. Declarer plays against a defender playing two cards to the trick: one from their hand and one from a mini-dummy hand that gets replenished after every trick.

In Ultimo each and every time you make a bid in the auction you must first donate one card to the kitty that will be available to your opponent right after, and donate another card to what will become a dummy played by whomever turns out to be defender. Deciding which cards to plant in the dummy and which to give your opponent is a conundrum, especially before you know if you’ll be playing the dummy or against it. You can even raise your own bid or bluff trying to get better cards–but when do you stop this game of chicken?

(Part of the Traditional Two Player Trilogy: Esculenta, Ultimo, Schmegegge)

—description from the designer

Before the start of the game, the enclosed stars are distributed equally to all participants as a means of payment. The indivisible remainder shall be placed in the larger center space of the board (7-12). Now 2 dice are thrown in turn. According to the result of the throw one star must be placed on the corresponding space - or if a star is already in the space, it can be taken out as won. 
An exception, however, is the roll 7. In this case, a star must never be taken out, and so a larger cluster of stars forms in the center space, which can be collected by the lucky one who manages to roll a 12.
The Space 1 is also to be considered (a 1 can never be thrown with 2 dice). If the dice fall inaccurately (tipper) or if a die rolls off the table, the dice may not be rolled again as usual, but a star is lost and must be placed on space 1.
If someone now rolls the number of his birth month (for January 2 x 1 - for February also 2 x 1), he can empty space 1 if possible. 
The game is played for an agreed amount of time. The player who has won the most stars, is the winner.

Fast-paced combination strategy game. Botzy uses a 62-card deck, 50 main deck cards, 10 Botzy champion cards, and 2 instructional cards.

Botzy champions are used to aid players during their turns. Each Botzy champion has their own unique ability. Each player has 5 champions, once all champions are destroyed you lose the game.

Both players play from the same 50-card main deck, sharing the discard, and graveyard piles. (A unique twist to most competitive games.)

There are 3 ways of destroying your opponent's champion cards. Either by discarding the last card in your opponent's hand, drawing the last card in the main deck or by a card effect.

—description from the designer

The players take turns to roll and move. They have to get their four cars from one corner of the game board to the opposite corner of their color, for example the red cars from Marseille to Paris, while minding the traffic rules. The players have to pay fines, if they violate the traffic rules or have to pay for having their car repaired after a crash, after which the car has to start anew. The results of the two dice may be split up among two cars.

The first player, who gets his four cars to his destination, wins the game and the paid fins and repair costs.

Rivet Wars: Reloaded expansion featuring 10 additional miniatures for both the Allied and Blight factions.

Contents:
10 Allied miniatures - Allied Forces: Cher Ami, M3 Vertical Tanks, M7 Bosses, Macleod's Guard
10 Blight miniatures - Elsa Frost, Landkrieger, Sturmrads, Baron Munchen's Guard
10 stat cards

boardgame: Ophidian Redux: Legacy of the Arenas
description:
Ophidian Redux: Legacy of the Arenas is the first release for the Ophidian Redux line. So what you can expect from this new Ophidian Redux? We're going back to the basics, as we are proud to repurpose, review, and republish the original 5 digital full-text card sets and print them with some graphics added and with a fully updated text to fit into the new audiences and new potential Gladiators, on TheGameCrafter in print format, adding new additional content, all compatible with the original Ophidian 2350 CCG as well as the more recent spin-off Ophidian 2360.

The Ophidian Redux: Legacy of the Arenas storage box holds 625 unsleeved Ophidian (2350 CCG, Wars, 2360 or Redux) cards or 460 sleeved Ophidian cards. But more, Legacy of the Arenas will come with the following contents:

> 2 - Ophidian Redux: Preview Decks fully playable out of the box. Each deck contains 30 cards previewing all the upcoming Ophidian Redux releases, 1 never seen before Gladiator card exclusive to each deck, plus 3 add-on cards each.
>> 1 double-faced Ophidian Redux Credit Points Tracker / Steps Of The Breather card.
>> 1 double-faced Ophidian Redux Cheer Points Tracker / Ophidian Redux Preview Deck Cardlist card.
>> 1 Ophidian Credit Points Rewards card, with ½ CP.

> 1 - Ophidian Redux: Legendary Xmas full collection expansion set. A set contains 34 cards, plus 6 add-on cards.
>> 1 double-faced Symbols For Cards card. 
>> 1 double-faced New Card Types card.
>> 2 double-faced New Mechanisms cards.
>> 1 double-faced Redux Checklist / Special Thanks card.
>> 1 Ophidian Credit Points Rewards card, with 2 CP.

This release also comes with a digital download Ophidian Redux: Quick-Rules Booklet .pdf file, which features a brand new Ophidian Universe Fiction, plus the quick rules and can be downloaded on TheGameCrafter game page or by scanning the QR-Code on the box linking to the Ophidian Universe website where you will also find it, alongside the complete rules for all our Ophidian Games.

And the Ophidian Redux: Legacy of the Arenas storage box plus the 108 cards will have an MSRP of 27.99 USD.

XVII century. The Original Peoples and the Aboriginal Nation are all sovereign in the Abya Yala del Sud. The white huinca is hardly a distant threat to the Children of the Earth.

In that Land without evil, people live respecting nature, the well-being of the people and the bravery of their caciques.
But not everything is harmony in the confines of the pachamama. In the tolderías, the smell of war is felt in the air of meadows and mountains, jungles and deserts, mountains and steppes. The caciques and their warriors will fight for the territories and their possibilities.

The end of the story is beginning...

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME

Players dive into the fight for territories starting with 12 starting totems each. Throughout seven rounds they will fight for each territory betting totems and adding alliances with the tribes of their original region; without losing sight of the alliances of the others and the final support of the cacique to the weakest alliance. Each round is divided into five phases: two alliance phases, two betting phases, and a fight phase. A fight for supremacy where you won't have to lose sight of the impossibility of participating in all the contests, so you will have to choose very well which battle to fight.

The player with the most totems at the end of the dispute over the 7 territories wins the game and is proclaimed the new Grand Cacique of Abya Yala del Sud.

May 1810. Conspiracies and betrayals are the order of the day. Patriots and absolutists sink into collusion to force mutually exclusive projects that can cost them their lives. They are writing history and there are members of the town who want much more than "to know what it is about". They want to fight for that new Nation that is brewing...

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME

Each player represents an urban worker from colonial Buenos Ayres (a water carrier, a lamplighter, a milkman, a seller of candles, cakes or feather dusters) and -adhering to the revolution that is brewing- tries to play his support by moving between patriots and absolutists (cards of all 20 characters) in such a way as to visit only one per table series so as not to be singled out as a conspirator by the absolutist side.

If he can successfully raffle 6 visits to patriots in the same table series, he will have gained two degrees of prominence among the revolutionary forces, raising his prestige among the patriotic "sparks" who are stationed in front of the town hall. If he is unsuccessful, everyone else except him will move up a step in prominence in the revolution.

The player who reaches the level 7 Town Hall arcade will have won the game.

The Nation is being built. Internal struggles and intrigues struggle to impose two antagonistic and mutually exclusive country models. Dust and horror of 24 patriots and villains who will try to impose their political project with blood and fire.

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME

Take as many barracks from your opponent as possible and end the fratricidal fight. For this and in turns, each player will alternate deducing which federal leader or unitary leader defends each barracks. The situation becomes more complicated when these bosses will be relieved but perhaps at too high a price...

The game ends when all three headquarters of one side (Unitary or Federal) have been eliminated.

But beware, you can still be defeated if -even after winning the three barracks- your opponent gets more victory points...

An all or nothing game!

1930. A thousand oligarchic families own "La Argentina" and manage the State as part of their properties, making all kinds of deals and looting of the national treasury. In this decade, electoral and economic fraud, repression of popular sovereignty and abuses of power of all kinds are the order of the day.

To carry out its negotiations, the Argentine Oligarchy uses collaborators of the same kind. It is precisely there, where our players come into play...

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME

The representative of the thousand families delegates his affairs to the first player next to the loot box, which is passed from hand to hand around the table. Each player takes advantage of the opportunity to get hold of the deals that he contains for individual benefit, or to take a character token with whom he collaborates.

After the round and with the box again in possession of the representative of the Argentine Oligarchy, he sees that some of his peers have cheated the interest of the ruling class for personal gain. Immediately afterwards he -offended and betrayed- he interrogates them to identify his henchmen trying to discover who or who have "abusers of his trust" and thus be able to recover his businesses for the set of the thousand oligarchic families. But if the deadline is met, if the Argentine Oligarchy has not managed to recover all the negotiations, it loses the game and the Prosecutors of the Homeland win.

Will the Argentine oligarchy be able to recover all the stolen contracts? Will he take a false step by accusing a National Prosecutor in vain? What will be the fate of the thieves if the oligarchy falls? What luck will the Ruffians have with the different outcomes? A game of simulation and deception where each group of interests will want to win.

On September 16, 1955, the coup d'état of the Fusiladora Revolution took place against the constitutional government of General Juan Domingo Perón, after which he began a long exile of 18 years. The last ten will take place at Quinta 17 de Octubre, located in the small town of Puerta de Hierro, on the outskirts of Madrid, Spain. From there, he will begin to navigate a political labyrinth of intrigues, loyalties, and betrayals that will finally lead him to return triumphantly to the country in 1972 and, the following year, to be elected constitutional president of Argentina for the third time.

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME

This game takes historical facts as its basis and translates them into the cards of the notable personalities of that turbulent time, which players must secretly handle to obtain a specified number of legitimation tokens, thus achieving ultimate victory.

Throughout the game, each player will keep a secret card in his hand (which could vary on his turn) and that represents his contact of influence and the one who will be the one to carry his message to General Perón. You will want to make sure that the person closest to the General has the message from him at the end of the day, so that it can be delivered with priority.

The game takes place in rounds that ends when the insert deck is exhausted at the end of a turn, or if all the players except one are eliminated from it. In the first case, the player with the most influence wins a standing token; in the second case, the one left standing wins.

A player is declared the winner of the game after collecting a certain number of influence tokens, based on the number of players.

Argentina, 1976. The country is submerged in a terminal crisis.

While some fight for a Nation that represents the inclusive, national and popular interests of the great majorities, others operate to make the country an elitist, neo-colonial, surrendering and excluding appendix of the majorities, representing foreign interests and local minorities. In this framework, a collection of perfidious characters sink into lies, pettiness, greed, impunity, fanaticism, betrayal and death, forming part of the complex web of power in Argentine daily life in the 70s.

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME

Each player must stay alive as he fights to remove the power of his opponents until all influence is wiped out. They will have to know how to hide their secrets very well, be cautious, intelligent, cunning and take care of all the dangers that lurk in each action, infiltrating the ranks of sinister characters to be able to use them until they achieve their goal, die or commit suicide.

Only one (or none) will manage to reach the end of the game, and will try - together with the help of notable personalities - a way out towards the democratic spring of the country.

(Des)memoria is a dynamic social game of deduction and dissimulation where interaction between players is everything. Playful experience that immerses us in the deep memory of our people, from the English invasions to the fall of De la Rua.

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME

Players assume the role of Argentines who travel back in time to a certain historical moment of relevance.
 Everyone knows that place and that time, except one of them, the "forgetful one", who has lost his memory.

Through questions and answers, the players who know the location must try to discover the oblivious, and he, in turn, must try to find out where they are. And all this within an interval of 7 minutes.

Depending on what happened in each round, the players will score in a certain way.

The game ends after the last round and the player with the most points will be declared the winner.

Economic imperialism -along with its local sepoy partners- have launched a soft coup d'état and plan to carry it out during the third presidential term of the Kirchner national government.

While the enemies of the country plan this from obscure foreign offices, the national forces must hurry to make effective the political measures to strengthen the political sovereignty, economic independence and social justice of the popular majorities. A game of hidden roles, intrigue and deception.

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME

At the beginning of the game, and in strict secrecy, you will discover which side you belong to (the patriots or the sepoys). Once this is determined, you will try to identify your companions without being unmasked by your enemies.

On each turn, you will choose a secret Action Card from the other players and try to play it to obtain the necessary political achievements to strengthen the Nation, or to trigger a Soft Coup d'état that will make the anti-patriotic forces triumph, depending on your identity. .

The Patriotic Side will win the game if they manage to make the Pending Political Actions effective, while the Sepoy Side will win if they manage to carry out the Soft Coup d'Etat or run out of game time without the patriots achieving their objective.

Argentina 2015. Manipulated public opinion, the electorate has chosen the most corrupt, looters and traitors to the Homeland since the Infamous Decade.

Immersed in this time of ignominy, we will have to wander through a republic among economic, political and especially moral ruins; trying that the decline in the offer of public personalities does not lead us to the definitive defeat...

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME

Obtain the most welfare points (or, alternatively, the fewest decay points) at the end of the game.
Each player receives a national and popular figure card that will help him stop the decline and 11 well-being tokens, which they will keep hidden behind the screen.

On his turn, the player must choose between:

     take a decay card (with values ​​from -2 to -42) and take its negative cost in decay points, but at the same time take any welfare tokens that can be found on it; either
     place one of his welfare tokens (by losing it) on the sepoy card and pass it to the next player. If he takes the sepoy card, he will put it in his playing area trying to make sequences (ladders) with future or previous cards taken, being the national and popular characters wild cards that can help join sequences to reduce negative decay points.


The game ends when the last card of the deck has been dealt. The player with the most wellness points (or the least decay) is the winner.

Patria Grande is a fast and intense simulation game that requires nerves of steel, daring and subtle strategy. Everyone starts the game with 4 cards, neither more nor less: 3 LATIN AMERICAN cards and one IMPERIALIST card. With that alone, he must have the diplomacy to emerge victorious, relying on his Latin American brothers and against the imperialists who will harm his chances.

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME

Win two bets.

To win the game you will have to make the highest bet. Once it is yours, hit the number of cards corresponding to the bet, without encountering a single imperialist card on the way, and do it twice!...

How much risk are you willing to take?

If you are too timid, another player will outbid you to try and steal the win from you. If you are too reckless, you risk losing by revealing one of the genocidal imperialist cards.

Good luck!...

Jester is a "trick" based game, like hearts or bridge, using a standard 52 card deck with 2 jokers (Jesters). Players bid on the number of tricks they believe they can take in each hand.

Jester is one of the most enjoyable and sophisticated two player card games.  It is a fairly straightforward game to learn, but the strategies are challenging and there are multiple modes of play that add to the fun.

A game consists of multiple hands and a cumulative score is kept. The score is based on the accuracy of the bidding as well as the number of tricks taken. Games can be very short, or quite long, as scores can increase or decrease based on the success or failure in any given hand.

Jester Rules

This historical game highlights the life and work of Don José Francisco de San Martín from his return to his homeland in 1812 until 1845, already exiled in Europe.

The most important personalities and events of his public life appear on his cards for players to share in their circumstances, struggles and destiny, in order to win the trust and favor of the Big Boss.

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME

Obtain the highest number of medals of honor at the end of the game, in order to become the Lieutenant of the Liberator José de San Martín.

GAME MECHANICS

Each player receives a character card at the start of a round.
Then, the first player takes a second character and decides which of the two characters in his hand to play, revealing him to the other players and executing his effect.

The game continues in turns until all the characters have been played or there is only one player left in the round. If the latter happens, it is defined who of the players has won the medal corresponding to the event in dispute and the next round ends.

Repeating these rounds for each of the 15 events, the player who has the most medals or is the last one to reach the highest number of medals will win.

This is a game of deduction, memory and chance, where the participants playfully immerse themselves in the revolutionary passions of freedom and justice; knowing heroes and villains, victories and failures; unjustly forgotten patriots or false heroes turned traitors and, nevertheless, revered by liberal history.

Los 7 Locos is a game of hidden identities in which players secretly embody the masks of one of seven great Argentine and Uruguayan writers. Keeping his identity to himself, each player must simulate, deceive and deduce before the others to win the Silvio Ballan Award for best storyteller.

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME

Each player must try to discover the identity of the other players without revealing their own. To achieve this, he must perform actions in order to obtain resources. He must then use these resources to discover information about the other players in order to eliminate them.

The storytellers take turns and carry out one or several actions depending on the resources and the decisions they decide to make.

Thus, each player shows off their best capacities for imagination, lies, deception and deduction -embodying the skills of the different writers, most of the time without being them- while trying to unmask the rest of the players.

The last player left without being eliminated wins...

This is an expansion that totally modifies the mechanics of the original game and the political economic conception of the classic Estanciero game. This expansion cannot be played without a copy of the base game.

Each player starts from the base of a sum of values ​​together with an initial Field Deed. Gradually more Deeds of Fields, Chacras and Estancias are acquired, with which they are collected, rents are paid and grains or hacienda can be produced.

Railroads and Companies are bought and profits are collected. Seeds for planting or reproducers for the farm are purchased and the sales of what is produced are collected. Upgrades are purchased and penalties are paid. Taxes are paid for a Solidarity Fund intended for those players who go bankrupt to be able to resume the game.

The game takes place over 4 years of 6 game turns per year.

The game ends when the last player finishes playing the last turn of his fourth year or when there are no more players left who have yet to liquidate their hacienda or their harvest.

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME

Be the player with the most wealth at the end of the game.

Become the best wrestling team in this fast-paced card placement game. From two to four players fight in this 10 minutes card game. Place a secret card from a hand of four every round. Following the turn order, reveal any card on the board. The owner of the card activates the revealed card to move in the ring or cover another luchador to nullify their card value. At the end of the fourth round, the team with the highest sum of their total cards is the victor in Lucha Arena.

boardgame: World of Tanks Miniatures Game: American – M7 Priest
description:
The American M7 Priest gives your Americans some fire support. Use Requesting Fire to get information from your scouting tanks to hit hidden targets, load Burst HE Shells to stun enemies or load Light HEAT Shells for when damage matters more than critical hits.

Contents:
1 Painted Plastic Tank
1 Tank Card
5 Upgrade Cards
Crew Stunned Tokens
Online Bonus Code for New and Existing Players
family: Game: World of Tanks Miniatures Game (Wargaming.net / GF9)

boardgame: World of Tanks Miniatures Game: British – Sexton II
description:
The British Sexton II is an SPG for the commander on a budget. Use Burst HE Shells to cause a massive explosion, stunning all nearby tanks, or load Light HEAT Shells for more damage. Add Requesting Fire and your British can now receive support anywhere on the table.

Contents:
1 Painted Plastic Tank
1 Tank Card
5 Upgrade Cards
Crew Stunned Tokens
Online Bonus Code for New and Existing Players
family: Game: World of Tanks Miniatures Game (Wargaming.net / GF9)

boardgame: World of Tanks Miniatures Game: German – Hummel
description:
The German Hummel SPG hits hard, packing 7 Firepower. Loading Heavy HEAT Shells removes HE and adds Big Gun for when damage matters more than critical hits. Use the SPG-specific upgrades, Burst HE Shell and Requesting Fire, to provide support for your platoon.

Contents:
1 Painted Plastic Tank
1 Tank Card
5 Upgrade Cards
Crew Stunned Tokens
Online Bonus Code for New and Existing Players
family: Game: World of Tanks Miniatures Game (Wargaming.net / GF9)

boardgame: World of Tanks Miniatures Game: Soviet – SU-76M
description:
The Soviet SU-76M is a low-cost Tank Destroyer for the budget conscious commander needing some additional support. Swap out to the SU-122A, and you have an SPG with a massive 7 Firepower ready to fling shells across the entire battlefield.

Contents:
1 Painted Plastic Tank
1 Tank Card
5 Upgrade Cards
Crew Stunned Tokens
Online Bonus Code for New and Existing Players
family: Game: World of Tanks Miniatures Game (Wargaming.net / GF9)

description:
Deception! Destruction! Dexcitement!
Damage your friendships like never before with Detonators!
In this poker-face game of pretending to be a role you aren’t you must carefully build your own winning hand with cards from the deck - but beware, because one of your friends has your Detonator, and if they clue in that you are their target your hopes of winning could be up in smoke!

There are two goals in Detonators - to assemble your winning hand, or to detonate your target. The game is played using cards of five colors - each color has its own ability and rarity in the deck.

At the start of the game each player is given in secret their role card and their detonator. On their role card is written their winning hand - each of the five roles has a unique winning hand. Each detonator is coded to one of the other players’ role cards, but nobody knows who has which role. The deck is then shuffled and placed between all the players, and plays three cards onto the table face-up for everyone to see.

Play is turn-based, and on a player’s turn they can do one of four things:
        
DRAW a card either from the face-up set of three or from the deck itself.
PLAY a card from their hand, activating its ability and putting it in a discard pile
LOCK a card face-up in front of them, registering it towards their winning hand, or swap one of their already face-up cards with one from their hand.
DETONATE their target by playing two of their target’s winning hand cards, and correctly call out the player with that role. That player loses the game, and gives the player who detonated them their new target.

The game ends whenever a player has assembled their role’s winning hand in front of them, or whenever there is only one player left standing.

—description from the publisher

A party game for those who feel the need to say something totally inappropriate.

You have been warned.

The game is very simple. We draw a black card with a question. Then everyone chooses a white card with an answer that matches the question. The funniest and best-read answer wins.

PsychoDropsy  is similar to the games "Cards Against Humanity" and "Apples to Apples", but they are in Polish. They have improved rules of the game and completely new cards.

This game is not suitable for people without a sense of humor. In people who are not devoid of good taste, it can cause a sharp cough, redness, difficulty breathing, and remorse. It should not be used with alcohol or other legal abusers. Let's face it - it should also not be used under the influence of any illegal means, even if legalization is only a matter of time.

Psychodropsy: Season 2 is a standalone game. No Psychodropsy: Season 1 or Limited Edition Psychodropsy required to play. Psychodropsy: Season 2 can be mixed freely with Season 1 cards.

The cards of both seasons can be mixed in any combination without fear - each card has an indication of which season it comes from. The Limited Edition cards are slightly larger than the Season 2 cards, but can be mixed and matched for tons of new possibilities.

Psychodropsy: Season 2 is the continuation of the first season of the game. The brand new cards are kept in the same cultural (though quite open) style as the first season. In the second season, there are cards that talk about deviations. And it's just about those deviations that really affect people. You won't believe how strange things can...lift people's spirits.

Welcome to Fat Cats Inc, the exciting new board game from Board and Sorcery! In this game, players will compete to earn as much money as possible before the world inevitably comes to an end.

As the CEO of your own company, you’ll have to make tough decisions and strategic moves to outwit your opponents and come out on top. You’ll have a variety of cards at your disposal, including Production, Action, and Funding, which you can use to benefit your company, hinder your rivals, or earn revenue.

But beware – every move you make will have consequences, and your choices may contribute to the ultimate demise of the world. Do you risk everything to come out on top, or do you try to save the world at the cost of your own profits? It’s up to you to decide in Fat Cats Inc.

So screw your friends over and earn the most to win – but be warned, in Fat Cats Inc, there can be only one true fat cat!

boardgame: Because the Lonely Novice Hope to Enter the Dungeon…
description:
Because the Lonely Novice Hope to Enter the Dungeon… is a roguelike dice game for solo play.
You are a novice who dreams of an adventure (or some gold) in the dungeon .
However, other adventurers in the village do not accept you as a party member, as they only give preferential treatment to highly experienced veteran players.
After hitting the training dummy for nothing all day, you realize that this is no use. Finally you head to the entrance of the dungeon alone in search of a wonderful adventure story to tell you at the tavern.

You explore a dungeon with a total of 3 levels.
Each turn you roll the dice to fight the monster.   
Eliminate monsters and receive gold and experience points as rewards, and use them to select various talents. Defeat the final boss lurking deep in the dungeon and win the game.

boardgame: Ora et Labora: Die kleinen Wunder der Abtei Liesborn
description:
26 new "Wonder" tiles that replace the eight tiles from the base game. Unlike the old tiles, the new tiles have a variable value, that depend on a player's situation.
This mini-expansion was part of the German edition of the customer magazine "Neues vom Ausguck" (News from the Lookout) 18.

The Ordinary Wizarding Level, or O.W.L. exams, the most important test for all Hogwarts students, begins!

Choose a house, choose a character, and show what you know in the theoretical and practical exams of the Transfiguration, Charms, and Defence Agains the Dark Arts subjects!

Do you know all the charms well? Do you know the right wand movements? So let's see what you know! On your cards, you'll see one of the 218 magic spells from the Harry Potter universe. Memorize them well from magic spells to wand moves, then check your knowledge in the presence of the examination board so that while the game is getting tougher from level to level, you will score as many master magic points as possible, and as the most skilled witch or wizard You will be the champion of the game, become the Master of the Magic Spells!

A game with learning, magic spells, wand movements, drawings.

Who will be the Master of Magic Spells?

—description from the back of the publisher

Mizano presents:
'Muloodi' AKA 'Mulodi'; A title bestowed upon the landlords in Uganda. A title widely respected and sought after across the country.
Build, rent and hustle your way to the top!
Balodi reigns Supreme!

—description from the back of the box

Mulodi is a Monopoly inspired cardgame with places in Uganda.
Players compete to complete sets of properties and make money.
Most cards have a dual function and can be used either as a property/action, or as money.

—user summary

It's time to take your hustle on the road.
Do you have what it takes to be a Mulodi in the Wild?
Build, Travel and Explore yout way through the Wonders of Uganda.

—description from the back of the box

Mulodi is a Monopoly inspired cardgame with places in Uganda.
Players compete to complete sets of properties and make money.
Most cards have a dual function and can be used either as a propery/action, or as money.
In Mulodi Adventures the properties are national parks, and players are tourist guides. Adventures can be played as a standalone variant, or added to the base game to play with more people.

—user summary

The second edition of World Facts includes many more cities, countries and categories than the first edition. In addition the rules got simplified so that new players can join the game immediately.

World–Facts is an entertaining geographical guessing game for 2-6 players aged 8 and up. Does Canada or Australia count more inhabitants? Does Rio de Janeiro or Sydney indicate a higher average temperature? And does London or Rome have more annual rainfall? These are just a few questions, where the players need to find an answer. In doing so it is not necessary to know the exact values, but rather to estimate and compare them in between different countries and cities. Through the different categories of questions the game offers a lot of variety and excitement.

Another special feature of World-Facts is its internationality. Via translations of all categories into 5 languages, global topics and the frequent use of symbols, World-Facts overcomes the usual focus of quiz games on just one language and culture. Thus, World-Facts is very suitable for multicultural groups.

​-description from designer

Das Geheimnis von San Marco ("The Secret of San Marco") is an escape game full of riddles and puzzles that consists only of cards.

A stranger has called the players into the state library of Venice to entrust them with an ancient legacy. A mysterious manuscript is hidden somewhere in San Marco. Can the players solve all the riddles and puzzles to reveal the hiding place and thus prove themselves as worthy heirs?

How to play:
The players' adventure takes place in the library. All 77 playing cards contain a riddle to be solved. As soon as the players have solved all the riddles on the cards, they have to face one last challenge: where is the secret manuscript hidden?

By the way: The game material remains intact and can therefore be used again and again.

The Battle of Leuthen is a low to moderate complexity tactical game on Frederick the Great's classic battle against the Austrians in 1757 during the Seven Years War. The game takes about 90 minutes to play. One player commands the Prussian Forces and the other player the Austrian forces. Cards allow a variety in play
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Series: Battle Series of World Battles (Historical Game Company)

boardgame: Unsolved Crime Cases: Der Fall Lindberg
description:
Unsolved Crime Cases - Will you succeed in solving the case?

In this thrilling Crime Escape Game, the exciting case of the missing Lindberg couple awaits you. Newlyweds and just on their honeymoon in Scandinavia, the couple suddenly disappears seemingly without a trace. What has happened? A kidnapping? Blackmail? Or was it murder? Maybe the abandoned forest cabin will shed light on the mysterious disappearance? Visit all the places related to the case, collect clues as well as evidence, question the witnesses and interrogate your suspects in a thrilling multimedia finale.

A missing couple, an abandoned forest cabin in Scandinavia and a growing mountain of questions: What happened? Kidnapping? Blackmail? Murder? [...]
 Assist Detective Aleksander Dahlqvist in this mysterious case and collect real evidence. You can record all your investigative progress on the clue board. Who will think creatively enough and convict the perpetrator? Only in the multimedia finale will you see if you are right with your conclusions. A case for real investigators!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Power Rangers: Deck-Building Game – Flying Higher
description:
Let’s Rocket! Led by Astronema, the Princess of Evil, the forces of Dark Spector are spreading out to conquer the galaxy. Only Andros and his Space Rangers can save the universe from being plunged into darkness. Hop on your Galaxy Glider and rocket into action!

Play as Andros and his team of Space Rangers as they join the roster of heroes. Astronema and the lieutenants of Darkonda make their appearance as playable villains! Featuring new ways to play and win the game using the Dark Specter and Zordon objective cards!

This expansion requires the Power Rangers Deck-Building Game Core Set or Zeo – Stronger Than Before to play.

-description from publisher

boardgame: My Little Pony: Adventures in Equestria Deck-Building Game – True Talents Expansion
description:
We’ll Find Your Mark!

Come together and find your calling with the True Talents expansion for My Little Pony: Adventures in Equestria! Play as the Cutie Mark Crusaders and use the Discover ability to rally Classmates and Crusaders to your cause! With new characters, cards, hurdles, and challenges, True Talents adds lots of variety and deck-building strategies to your game!

New Discover mechanic encourages themed deck-building! Play as Apple Bloom, Scootaloo, Sweetie Belle, and Zecora! Features new player cards, hurdles, and challenges to enhance your game!

This expansion requires the My Little Pony: Adventures in Equestria Deck-Building Game.

-description from publisher

boardgame: G.I. JOE Deck-Building Game: Coldsnap Expansion
description:
It’s Cold-Blooded Chaos…

Bundle up and prepare to face the Coldsnap! Battle Destro and the Weather dominator device before the world freezes. Then, face off against Zartan and his Dreadnok gang before the world goes crazy. Hook up the new Towable Assets against chilling new threats like Snowblind cards and Snow Serpents.

In this simple card game you and 1-3 other players try to gather the most points by scoring animal cards from your hand. Each animal has a nature reserve, strength and a worth. Strong animals are worth less than weaker ones. Simultaneously, everyone plays a card. The strenght of each animal played determines the order in which the players get to select a card from the market (which contains the same amount of cards as the amount of players). Only played animals matching the same nature reserve (colour) as those in the market allow you to take a card and score the animal played. If you cannot take a card matching your nature reserve, you draw a blind card and score no points. However, do you play a strong animal to be guaranteed of a card and points, or do you play a weaker animal which will give you more points, yet may allow the other players to take all the suitable cards from the market?

Objective
To be last one standing or have the highest score when the game ends.

The Basics
In Warlord Chess players move, capture, check, and checkmate just like in standard chess. However there is no en passant or castling. Instead, special spaces on the board allow the pieces to make special moves.
Duration: Play lasts for 40 ROUNDS after which TIME’S UP.
Round: A round is counted after every ACTIVE (non-Mated) player has taken their turn.
Winning: If a player checkmates their last opponent, they win the game.
Otherwise after TIME’S UP all active players add up the points of the pieces they captured during the game.
The active player with the highest score is the winner.
If there is a tie, then the tying player with the most
checkmates wins. If there is still a tie, then all tying
players tie for 1st place.

A murder has been committed in London’s famous Monastery Road Studios. Renowned saxophonist and songwriter, Roland “Stubby” Hackett, died during a recording session due to an allergic reaction, but initial forensic investigations have brought up clues to suspect foul play is at hand.

26 PIECES OF PHYSICAL EVIDENCE - Inside the case file, you'll find magazine articles, crime scene investigation documents, suspect statements, a parking ticket, police reports, 7" record sleeve, a loyalty card, phone/text records, a coroners report, a map, photographs and more.

You will be required to access the internet to view other important evidence linked to the case.

A difficulty rating of 4.5/5 this "whodunnit" style thriller.

In any Campaign conducted in the Age of Reason, there will come a time when the effect of naval actions becomes important to the outcome of a carefully planned strategy.

Most wargamers would prefer to use a set of rules that would give them the flavour of naval combat without getting bogged down in the intricacies and minutiae of combat in the Age of Sail. A further advantage would be that the rules would have similar mechanisms to the land warfare rules used by the protagonists. The author's personal preference for 'Volley & Bayonet' prompted him to write these rules.

Scales and Assumptions:

These rules are designed to be played on a table top onto which has been super-imposed a grid of approximately 3.25 inches (83mm) square. This grid is used for movement and the calculation of firing ranges as well as being perfect for indicating wind direction and points of turn. For the purpose of these rules the distance across the diagonals of the grid square are counted as being the same as the distance across the flat sides.

Each grid square represents about 500 yards.

Each move represents about 30 minutes.

—user summary

description:
The intent of these rules is to provide a fast, easy, fun game for ancient fleet battles. The base turn sequence and mechanics are based on the game Volley & Bayonet by Frank Chadwick & Greg Novak.

Each game turn is composed of two sequential parts: First player actions followed by Second player actions. Generally, the first player action represent the attacking side in the battle. Within each player action there are four phases: 1) Command, 2) Movement, 3) Combat, and 4) Exhaustion. There is no individual ship morale phase.

(formerly known as Ancient Warfare)

These rules cover classical warfare in the ancient world from the rise of Egypt to the fall of Rome.

The essence of Ancient Conquest is command and the role of the general on the battlefield. Using simple mechanics, the player will find himself thrust into the role of battle manager on a very personal level, and in a very chaotic environment.

Soldiers in the game are rated for their equipment, but also their experience level and specific skills. There are no abstract "troop classes", although you will find that certain combinations of training and equipment necessarily encourage specific deployments on the battlefield.

Ancient Conquest, on the other hand, was created from the very beginning with historical encounters in mind. Units were tested not against the theory of how they ought to have done against different troop types, but rather against the reality of their historical performance in actual battles.

More than anything else, the atmosphere of the game is different from that of most other ancients rules. Many rules are rigid and chess-like in their portrayal of ancient warfare, so that the games are very cerebral in nature. Ancient Conquest is anything but that. Ancient battles were confused and 
violent. They were personal and visceral, not impersonal and abstract. Above all else, Ancient Conquest aims at reproducing that sense.

Oracle cards introduce an additional element of unpredictability to the game, and provide players with an additional means of influencing play.

The ground scale using the recommended base size is 1:1500, which means that one millimeter equals one and a half meters of actual ground. (One inch equals about 40 meters of real terrain.) This means that the frontage of a base is 90 meters (about 100 yards).

In general, a stand represents infantry or cavalry deployed four to six ranks deep and as many files wide as will fit in a 90 meter frontage (about 100 files for infantry or 65 files for cavalry). Formations of greater depth than four to six ranks are represented by placing multiple bases one behind another.

Strength of Stands
Infantry: 500 infantry (4-6 ranks of about 100 files each).
Cavalry: 330 cavalry (4-6 ranks of about 65 files each).
Chariots: 100 small (2-3 man) or 50 large (4-5-man, and all scythed) chariots
Elephants: 10 elephants (in one or two ranks)
Engines: 20 bolt throwers or 10 stone throwers.
 For very large battles, double scale armies (1 stand per 1000 infantry or 650 cavalry) may be used, and for small actions half scale may be used (1 stand per 250 infantry or 160 cavalry) may be used.

—description from the rulebook

description:
The name 3KSES is a combination of words "Three games" and "Tricks" into one word TRIKSES. 
Three easy-to-learn games in one box. 
66 triangular play chips(tokens) in this game are made from wood and both side printed, from one side is a thematic colourful painting of one of famous world architectural jewels. From other side is printed an ornament. Each chip has on the sides 3 different symbols, made as shaped cutouts, it is a triangle, a square and a circle. 6 play chips from 66 are rare POWERCHIPS, they are described with cut-through marks. Game 1 | FIND A MATCH  - 66 play chips are placed on table in 11 groups, 6 pieces of chips i each group. Players are trying to find 2 pictures of the same building to get a MATCH, in the end of the game the winner with more pairs wins the game. IT is good memory fitness training.

Game 2 | JOINTS - 66 play chips are distributed to players, each player gets same amount of chips and put in a stack in front of them in a column. The shaped cutout symbols on the chips are used here. First player put first play chip from his stack on a desk and other player can lay one of his chips to connect it only with the same symbol to one of the chips laying on the table, it makes a JOINT between two connected chip. For each JOINT made player plays one new chip, so if it makes 2 JOINTs - is playing 2 times in his turn, gives quicklier chips from his stack to the table. The trick is to get a combination of more chips near each other to build 2 or 3 JOINTs in one time. 
POWERCHIPS is special, of course, doesn't need to be played out when in top of stack, but can be put near for later use and play normal chip instead. POWERCHIP has the ability to turn chips to get a compatible shape for connection.   
Player or team who first gets rid of all chips of their stack is a Winner. It is great logic fitness training.

Game 3 | TAMITADY - 66 play chips are mixed and each player gets 1 POWERCHIP and any 6 pieces of normal chips. -description from designer

Connect Five is a crossword-style word game.

With only five letters (cards) available at any time, players take turns making words, crossword-style.

The set contains 48 letter cards, each with a point value.

Six letters are always in play, but you can only use five of them to form interconnected words. One of the six letters must be discarded at the end of each turn.

Players take turns drawing one new letter and trying to form one to four interlinked (crossed) words. All letters must be used to form the words. If the letters cannot form word(s), the player's turn is over.

On each turn, a player:
- draws a new card
- adds the letter next to the existing five cards
- discards one card
- rearranges the cards to form one to four words
- score only the new words formed.

If all words cannot be formed, the turn is over.
Typically two or three words will be formed.
On a rare occasion, four words can be formed.
All words must be valid.

The game includes five dry erase scoreboards.

-description from designer

Try before you buy. Online game is at https://brainy.games/connect5.php
