Players use Basic Move Cards to create chains of attacks. Each move alters the stats: Stun, Control, or Potential to set up the next move. If played strategically, you can avoid a major reversal from your enemy.

Use deception, luck, or physical challenges to raise the stakes at any time with Psychology Cards!

Unlock your Signature Move by completing its unique Signature Sequence of Basic Move Types. Every time you pull off a Signature Move you go for the pin and might just win!

—description from the designer

Swimmers compete to collect the most sets of beautiful sea creatures as they snorkel.

Snorkel is a family card game that combines memory and set collection to create an easy to learn and engaging game.

There is a 3x3 grid of sea cards set in the middle of the table. On a players turn they flip over three cards and try to create sets, a set is three cards with something in common or three cards with absolutely nothing in common. For each set created players get to keep 1 card. The remaining cards are flipped back over and the taken players replaced from the deck. Ultimately players are trying to create sets from the cards they have collected.

The game ends when there is only 3 cards left in the grid. The player who has created the most sets wins.

Free Promotional game of Wizard.

The standard game consists of a 60-card deck that consists of the traditional 52-card deck (1-13 in four suits) along with four Wizards (high) and four Jesters (low). In the promotional game, the amount of wizards and Jesters is the same, but the four suits only number up to 11.
Otherwise the game works completely the same.

"Your loose tarot readings help me to heal."
It's a communication game where everyone can enjoy talking with each other about real problems.
Tarot reading has been developed as a fun game to play!
You can consult your worries and feel like a fortune teller with the original tarot cards that can be loosely enjoyed even for the first time, while maintaining the deep meaning of tarot cards.
The game can be played face-to-face, online, or alone as an easy way to enjoy tarot reading.
One person is the questioner, and the others are the fortune tellers, and they draw tarot cards and get inspiration from the cards or connect them to keywords or patterns.
The person who gives the best fortune-telling wins. 
Let's find out what kind of fortune-telling will make the person think "I like that!

Touted as "the only game that is up-to-date," this card set aims to add "all new questions" to any other trivia game, or to be playable on its own. The cards and questions are in a decidedly Trivial Pursuit-like format. Categories are People, Entertainment, Sports, Politics, Business, and Art & Literature.

Take Gem All reinvents word games by mixing mechanics from 2 games from our childhood: Stad - Land - River and Jungle Speed.
Be the fastest player to find a word that starts with 1 of the 4 letters on the table and is related to the current theme.
Player with the most gems wins.
Watch out for the special cards and the diamonds that can slow you down and some twists.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Take Gem All réinvente les jeux de mots en alliant la mécanique du célébrissime Petit Bac de notre enfance à la folie d'un Jungle Speed. Emparez-vous d’un maximum de pierres précieuses en étant le joueur le plus rapide à donner une réponse correspondant au thème joué et commençant par l’une des quatre lettres en jeu ! Mais attention, méfiez-vous des cartes pièges qui pourraient vous ralentir et des diamants qui risqueraient de bouleverser le cours du jeu...

À vos marques, prêts ? TAKE GEM ALL !

—description from the publisher

SIMPLE. FUNNY. UPROARIOUS. If you can read, you can play. Perfect for anyone who likes to laugh – a lot. Like, till it hurts a bit.
EASY TO PLAY. Take two cards, spot the only matching pair of words, and be the first to sound out the answer. Bring on your BWEEEOEW NOWNOW BWOW guitar solo, your WHOOOOSH BANG firework, your BUCKCAUUUK? confused chicken.
MULTI-GENERATIONAL. This party game is perfect for adults, grannies, grumpy uncles, trendy teens, and kids aged 8 years plus.
LOOKING FOR DRINKING GAMES? Yup, you found one. Our pocket-sized, purse-perfect pack can go anywhere: a friend’s birthday party, board game night, a family gathering where you’d rather play a hilarious new word game over answering questions about your life choices.

—description from the publisher

Radical Queer Witches is a prompt and answer style card game with 140 prompt cards and 300 answer cards.

There is absolutely nothing in this game against marginalized people. Zero racism, transphobia, homophobia, sexism, Islamophobia, anti-Semitism, fatphobia, or ableism. Instead, we address issues of race, oppression, and white supremacy by focusing on and holding those who benefit accountable.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Last Word (fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories)
description:
This fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories is set in 1926 in St. Paul, Minnesota, USA.  The disappearance of architect Frank Lloyd Wright has disrupted the time stream.  To find him your team will have to gain access to private parties and drawing rooms of roaring 20's high society, and explore the city's hidden gangster underbelly.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Der Fluch des Waldes IV (fan expansion for The Adventures of Robin Hood)
description:
With this adventure booklet you can experience another exciting adventure of Robin Hood and his companions. It plays around the same time as chapter 4. The story of the adventure plays independently of the others and therefore no prior knowledge of other chapters is necessary.

Since new elements and tiles from later adventures have been added, it is recommended that you have already played the basic game. All outlaws already have their own special abilities. The functions for new items can be found on the following page. No prior knowledge of this adventure is required.

The outlaws had managed to stop the curse and track down the magician Alatar.  They ended up accompanying the magician Alatar to the castle, because that's where his friend, Walter Fitzgerald, was supposed to be, probably in the dungeon.  However, not all of the outlaws were convinced by this act to accompany the magician into the castle and instead stayed outside the castle walls.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

description:
Your (sugar)uncle Lucas Ollie got entangled in the chewing gum while making the biggest consignment of candy ever. He now needs your help in his candy factory to get everything ready before Halloween. If only you had paid more attention during all those (boring) tours... But then you accidentally discover Lucas' huge secret!

—description from the publisher (translated)
family: Series: Escape Room – The Game (Identity Games International B.V.)

description:
Welcome those who enter the territory of the Sack Men. Krampus, the supreme lord, and tyrant, leads a legion of sack monsters that snatch unwary souls roaming around in the dark of night.

Your goal is to help a crew equipping themselves with the best sacks available and seize the highest numbers of souls, so you can gain the favor of your supreme lord.

The wining mechanic is based on collecting Victory Points from multiple game strategies. The player with the highest number of VPs will win the game.

The Sack Man is an auction based game that combines deck building and competitive gameplay.

—description from the designer

After an worldwide economic crisis, players take on the role of professional investor to earn money on the A-share market through buying and selling stocks on a fluctuating market. But new crisis might arrive at any time. Your goal is to earn more money than any other investors before new rounds of economic crisis.

Stocks of different companies would cause different market effects. On the market phase, players turn over the market cards and adjust to the corresponding prices.(Crisis cards mixed with market cards in the pile) Then players move to the buying and selling phase and optionally decide if they wish to buy a new stock or sell an old stock first. The current market price is determined by both market cards and stock cards. These phases recurring until all crisis cards occur.

—description from the designer

boardgame: No Thank you, Evil!: It's Raining Cats and Dinosaurs
description:
A creature called Kabloom! is stealing all the power from the Flowering Fields.  Can the heroes stop them and restore this beautiful corner of Out the Window?
Four new adventures.

     It’s Raining Cats and Dinosaurs!
     Oh No, Snow!
     Flower Power!
     Gloom and Doom Steal the Moon

Make a 9-card, 3x3 grid of ally cards that defend you from monsters that set up around the parameter. Banish monsters into candles by making certain patterns on your grid.

Each turn, draw the top card from the face-up deck. If an ally, place it on one of the 9 stacks comprising the ally grid. If a monster, place it on the parameter of the grid. Each ally has a number, colour, and picture that help you meet the condition to banish a monster on the same row or column.

You win if you make a ring of candles by banishing all monsters around the parameter. If your deck runs out before you have a ring of candles, monsters break through and munch your bones.

Designed for solo play, co-op & heads-up capable.

-description from designer

A volley of rockets to the right while you dodge left and 2 enemy walkers are left scattered across the country side. All is going well until a plasma beam knocks out the targeting pod. Gotta deal with the enemy elite on manual.


     * * * * 


Welcome to Weasel-Tech, a solo campaign game of giant anime robot action.

Lead your Strike Force against the enemy forces, engaging swarms of enemy mecha and dangerous elites in deadly combat. Built from the ground up for fast-moving warfare, the features include:


    Solo game play from the ground up. All you need is some miniatures and you can start your own game play. 



    People wishing to play with friends can use the Dueling mechanics to face off against each other or can take a pilot each and help each other against the enemy army.



    Full campaign game including random events, operational progress and character progression.



    Your pilots both gain in experience and skill but so does the command staff guiding you on your missions. 



    Fast playing combat rules inspired by anime mecha shows and comics. No plodding robots here.



    Undertake randomly generated missions with random events and side objectives.



    Call down reinforcements, supporting fire or other HQ support during battles.



    Outfit your mechs with weapons and equipment researched during the campaign.



    Defeated villains may return to haunt you with upgraded weapons and equipment.



    Includes a full Social System, allowing you to follow your pilots and crew as they make friends, get in fights or even fall in love.



    Usable with any mech figures you have available (or for that matter many power armor miniatures).

boardgame: Dice Throne: Santa's Workshop Promo Pack
description:
22 cards for holiday content for Dice Throne Adventures.
4x Minion cards Level III
3x Minion cards Level IV (foiled)
1x Nice Throne difficulty card
2x Loot cards Common
4x Loot cards Rare
3x Loot cards Epic
5x Loot cards Legendary (foiled)

In this small expansion to Carcassonne, Santa Claus gives you a gift if you either place a tile next to a cloister owned by another player, or place a tile that only has roads (and fields).

Gifts in the game are represented by 2 decks of cards from which the players draw when they are entitled to a gift. Some gifts must be revealed and resolved immediately, others can be kept to be played on a later turn or at the end of the game.

This print-and-play expansion was included in the Hungarian magazine Játékos Emberek Magazinja (JEM) with permission from Piatnik Hungary.
family: Series: Carcassonne Official Cut & Play Expansions

description:
Nothing Left to Bomb is a two-player game set in WWII and that recreates the Axis efforts to gain air superiority or even air supremacy in the skies above the pivotal island of Malta, and the British struggle to prevent it.

The game uses a modified system initially devised and used for the game Operation Pointblank. Each turn players commit their forces to the different map sectors secretly and then air battles are fought and antiaircraft fire is resolved.

The aim of the Axis Player is that of achieving air superiority over Malta. Failing this by the last turn of the Scenario, the British Player wins.

description:
The Beetle Game is a party game where players or teams draw pictures of beetles on a playing card containing 10 small squares. 
On the roll of a dice, players can draw the Beetle body if they get a six, then if a 5 is rolled can then draw a head. Others numbers then permit other parts to be drawn. First to draw a beetle stops the game and points are tallied for the parts drawn. This proceed to the 2nd round and repeated until 10 rounds are drawn in the squares provided on the card. The winning team or player is the one with the most points after 10 rounds.

The game is also identified and included under the BGG entry for Cootie. This being the paper and pencil version of the well known plastic beetle pieces that are constructed with the roll of a dice

—user summary

Competitive game about managing finances during the month lifecycle. Trying to learn players effectively manage your finances as in real life. You get your salary (if not fired), have to deal with regular payments (eg. rent, groceries,...) and other unpredictable events that can make you more money or cost you extra expenses. Good management and lot of luck needed to win the game.

You move around 31 tiles (representing 31 days in month) by rolling a dice (standard d6). There are several different tile types:

     mandatory payment which you have to pay when passing by (groceries, rent, mortgage, phone bill,..)
     draw a card tiles - gain, pay, monthly experiences - dozens of life situations that impacts your budget
     wheel of luck tiles
     salary - you get paid your monthly salary (everyone starts on the same salary, can be increased in the game, but can be also 0 if you get fired)
     few more extraordinary tiles (eg. vacation, being fired, etc.)


Last player standing wins the game.

—user summary

Trust us, it's Turtles All the Way Down!
In Munchkin: Turtle Carnage, we've got a shell-load of great new cards for you! (30, to be exact!) Be careful not to wake the Napping Turtle while you battle the undead Rigor Tortoise with your Hot Rock. And always remember, Slow and Steady wins the race to Level 10!

—description from the publisher

The Stock Car Racing Team Game is themed around American stock car auto racing that began in the late 1940's in the southeast U.S. that grew from a regional to a national auto racing sport.

The game requires 2 to 6 players, each player has six like colored race cars to choose from, Red, Yellow, Blue, White, Green and Orange.  Each race car has it's own unique number on the roof of the car.  For each team, the first digit on the roof will start with 1,2,3,4,5 and 6.  Each player navigates their six race cars by the roll of three dice.  Two white dotted dice that determined the number of spaces to move forward on the race track and one green six sided numbered die that determines which car is to be moved.

The object of the game is to be the first player to move all six of like colored race cars around the race track and past the start/finish line but only after earning a predetermined number of race tire tokens for each teams six race cars.  This is accomplished by making green flag or yellow flag pit stops and avoiding engine failure or running out of fuel.

This is a game of attrition, the first player to successfully cross the start/finish line and remove all six of their race cars wins the race.  Their is also a point system for those that want to play multiple races and therefore there is incentive for players that do not win the race to finish 2nd, 3rd etc.

—user summary

An abstract strategy game, nominally themed around a battle for political supremacy in which every decision trades one issue or dimension against another.


     Payers take turns to place black and white stones on a diagonal grid board.
     The aim is to end the game with more points than your opponent
     You get points by winning “tricks”, which you do by being the first to complete all your squares in a row or column.


Majority can be played with or without hidden information. The hidden information adds a level of difficulty and an element of deception. Beginners can start off by playing without the hidden information. This is called deterministic mode and I will describe it first.

A wargame for two players whose theme is the civil war which pitted the liberal rebels against the conservative government in Colombia between 1899 and 1902. This conflict was partly responsible for the secession of Panama in 1903.

Un wargame pour deux joueurs dont le thème est la guerre civile qui opposa les rebelles libéraux contre le gouvernement conservateur entre 1899 et 1902. Ce conflit fut en partie responsable de la sécession de Panama en 1903.

description:
In Trivionary you have to draw, mimic or define a word for your mate/s to guess it. Played in teams of 2, 3 of 4 players, each team rolls a dice in its turn to advance in the board. The opponents take a card from the pile with 4 words and their definitions. They chose one word that has to be guess by the other team. Each tile has a sign (D for drawing, M for mimic and d for definition) indicating how the player have to explain the word. If the team guesses the word (just one try is available) they take a new turn rolling the dice again and trying to guess, etc. The first tea reaching the top of the board wins.

The game (language dependent) tries to take the best from Pictionary and other party games.

—user summary

There are six cheeses to visit on the board and six crackers on the plate. Go around the board, visit the cheeses and eat the crackers. The first to clear their placate of crackers wins! But watch out for the pesky cat squares as victory is never just one throw away!

—description from the publisher
Oha! is a word guessing game, like Cards against humanity. With 480 cards for the family. Includes funny specialcards like " next round do..." .

-Phantom thief laughs, detective roars. The battle over the hidden treasure.

This is a mystery board game for two players in which the detective and the phantom thief can enjoy a brain battle over the treasure. Planning and production is led by board game designer Kengo Otsuka. Character designer Tatsuro Iwamoto(岩元辰郎), whose works include the "Ace Attorney" series, designed the characters for the cards.

The novel included with the game, which is a prequel to the main game, was written by Takeshi Yamazaki(山﨑剛), scenario director of the "Ace Attorney" series.
The well-crafted worldview is appealing.

The rules are simple. Turn over a card from a deck of your choice and place it in front of the treasure.
The phantom thief aims to steal the treasure, while the detective discovers who the phantom thief is disguised as.
The key is observation. Keep thinking, "Why did he put it there?" and keep thinking "Why did he put it there?
-Every choice you make is being watched by your enemies.

On the phantom thief's side...
Where is the real treasure hidden?
How and when should they steal it so that their disguise will not be revealed?

On the detective's side
How should the cards be arranged to protect the real treasure?
Who is the phantom thief disguised as among the six suspects?

Although what you do on your turn is simple, various thoughts intersect with each other.
It is a game of deduction and psychological warfare.

Planning and executive producer is board game designer  Kengo Ōtsuka (大塚健吾).

—description from the designer

This is a speedy game of the Battle of Midway. Players secretly allocate airplanes to missions: Fighters can escort bombers or fly high or low CAP (Combat Air Patrol), while bombers can carry either torpedoes or bombs. Players then reveal their choices and  follow a procedure that creates aircraft losses and hits on ships. When one side has scored enough hits, it is the winner.

A D6 is used for some randomization, but the main sources of uncertainty are the players' own allocations of aircraft to missions, and their initial load-outs of airplanes: airplanes are represented by colored M&M candies, each bag of which embodies a different mixture. Players' strategies are thus in part determined by their initial inventories of aircraft, which can vary.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Second Season Edition – Khorne Bloodspawn
description:
The Blood God gifts his most dedicated slaughterers with mutations that better enable them to kill in his name. A Bloodspawn is especially 'blessed' in this manner, having become horribly swollen and distorted with Khorne's murderous power. Huge, bulging muscles, razor-sharp bone spurs, vicious talons, and more are the hallmarks of a Bloodspawn. When one is herded onto a pitch and unleashed in all its destructive glory, the ensuing carnage can turn the stomach of even the most bloodthirsty fans.

The Bloodspawn will inevitably dominate any area of the pitch, either through the application of extreme violence or the intimidation factor of its murderous capabilities. If you want a player to deal with other Big Guys or smash a cage with impunity, waste no time in adding the spectacularly brutal Bloodspawn to your Khorne team's roster.

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Second Season Edition – Norse Yhetee
description:
One of the great mysteries of Norse teams is how they ever managed to convince the blood-crazed and savage Yhetees to play Blood Bowl. That said, fans of Norse teams are too enthralled by the displays of Yhetee brutality to ask many questions! These hulking, muscular beasts are equipped with dagger-like teeth and rending claws and, of course, an unkempt mane of white fur – perfect for blending into a mid-game snowstorm and catching an unsuspecting ball carrier off-guard.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Second Season Edition – Kroxigor
description:
Surprising as it may seem, Kroxigor are the gentle giants of the Lizardmen race. Thought to have been created as builders and labourers to be strong and powerful, Kroxigor coexist contentedly with Skinks. Yet there are few sights more terrifying than a Kroxigor roused to anger. Capable of effortlessly tearing a man limb from limb, these mighty creatures have served in the armies of the Slann for millennia. Today, they are considered a great boon to any Blood Bowl team. Their long muscular arms and rows upon rows of serrated teeth set into powerful jaws make them a terror on the Line of Scrimmage. Woe betide any player unfortunate enough to be tasked with marking a Kroxigor!

Add some raw strength to the speed and skill of your Lizardman team with a mighty Kroxigor. They'll smash through any player foolish enough to try and stand in their way on the Line of Scrimmage.

—description from the publisher

Puti (White) is a cooperative card game where players must escape a haunted house being haunted by a White Lady. The only way out is by a series of clues left to you by the only person who can see the White Lady: a little child. Remember the clues, work together as a team, and escape or be trapped in the house forever!

—description from the designer

It's treason time in the empire of lies, and the stakes have never been higher.

Your crew of specialists has just one objective: take down the United World Government's W.A.R facility, FAST! Failure isn't an option. If that facility comes online, our Resistance won't stand a chance.

So gear up rebels, you've got a suicide mission to win!

Rebel Revolt is a printable escape room game that transforms any space into a daring escape room adventure. What's it like? Well, imagine throwing the best bits of 'Mission Impossible' into your next house party. That's Rebel Revolt.

This printable escape room kit combines the teamwork, tension, and puzzle-solving of escape rooms with the laidback ease of group party games. Players will work together to sabotage the W.A.R facility before the United World Government can crush the Resistance, and rule the world! Gameplay is centered around a series of puzzle cards, divided into 4 'challenges'. Players will need to solve each challenge in order to progress the story, bringing them one step closer to completing their mission.

Game setup is quick and spoiler-free, and hints are handled by an online hint system that players access on their smartphones. This means that no game master is required to supervise the experience, so you can join the game alongside your players.

The game can expand to accommodate any number of players by dividing your group into teams of 2-4, and then pitting them against each other to see who finishes the game first. In addition, you can edit the game file before printing to customize your own escape room experience.

So, last chance to save the world... are you in?

Coins is a trick taking card game for three players. A Spanish deck of 48 cards is used. Coins is distinctive in that the suits are ordered in a cyclic fashion – similar to Rock-Paper-Scissors. The goal is to be the first player to win a given number of coin-cards.

Mark Steere designed Coins in October, 2022.

Become a garden architect and build hanging gardens for Queen Amytis. Arrange the stepped garden cards in three floor levels so that at the end of the game you have connected the most of the
same symbols. Thanks to spatial imagination, concentration and planning will the winner manage to create a true seventh wonder of antiquity, which is breathtaking.

Tour de France Board Game is a realistic simulation of the largest professional bicycle race in the world. The basic game set contains game plans for four stages that copy real stages of the 109th Tour de France race. The game can be further extended by additional individual stages.

Each player uses pawns to represent their cycling team. The movement of pawns is determined by throwing dice. Dice of different colors have different functions, depending on whether the terrain is flat or hilly, or when a tactical situation emerges.

The game is played as a sequence of individual moves in which the order of pawns on the game board is determined by arrows. Players take turns to move their pawns in a given order. The whole sequence is completed after all pawns are moved, upon which a new sequence begins. The beginning of each new sequence is confirmed using an application, which adds up all moves and calculates the finish times and final standings.

The game mechanism is not based on accident only but rather on the reasoning for each individual move, as the hexagonal net allows a relatively large freedom of movement. The placement of a pawn (rider) can be planned with respect to anticipated moves of other players' pawns. The choice of dice provides the opportunity to plan a strategic maneuver that can lead to the breaking up of a group, initiate an attack, or slow down pursuers.

boardgame: Care Bears and the Magic Fountain
description: Recreate a 6 pieces puzzles in this turn and move game. One of the novelty is the pop-up board.

Bunny Kingdom: Bunny Express is a micro-expansion for Bunny Kingdom that allows players to build railroad tracks on the game board. Whoever has the most tracks becomes sheriff, which affects turn order.

MARVEL GAME. Recover the gems and gain control of the universe in this challenging strategy game between Marvel heroes and villains. The object of the game is to be the first team to retrieve the infinity gems. Improve your strategy game.

FEATURES. The content is: 1 double view board in 6 pieces, 2 dice with sticker, 2 point dice, 4 boards and 24 gems. Position the tiles on the board and remove the obstacles by rolling the dice.

Pins & Needles is a climbing/shedding game for 2 to 3 players using 2 to 3 sets of double six dominoes. Your goal is to shed out all your tiles.

The unique twist to this game is that you can climb up or down the ranks depending on the combination lead to the play. Each player gets a double blank tile, which is a wild tile, to offer flexibility.

When you shed out, for each opponent with 5 or more tiles in their hands, you score bonus points. Therefore, you must skillfully shed out all your tiles while also denying others from getting bonus points.

This game is inspired by Sean Ross’s domino games (Clackerjack, Daiquiri, and Fiddich). Pins & Needles is a gentler introduction to those games by being easier to setup, quicker to parse through the hand, and without the complexities of partnership play.

This game proposes a twist of the classic word game. Tiles have poker-style decoration, and the scoring system is based on that, with one exception: all letter have the same value (1 point), but if all the letters in a word are the same color (black or red) the score is doubled, and if they all are the same suit tripled. Additionally, the first word in the board is also played according to a different rule: it has to start in one of the four poker tiles symbols printed on the board.

A hybrid between a tetromino-puzzler and a roll&write, for one to infinitely many players, about agriculture in ancient Egypt.

Allot land to the farm workers, invest in irrigation canals and make sure you do not overflood the land while doing so.

Leveraging classic roll&write tropes makes this an easy-to-learn but hard-to-master experience that sees you choosing die values to write and draw on your sheet, aiming for a high score. 
Akhet plays in 30 minutes or less, thanks to the fact that all players act simultaneously.

The box contains two games.

The goal of the first game is to get the three pieces of the gift puzzle that we are going to give Peppa Pig. To get the pieces of the gift, we have to guess and perform physical tasks.

The key to the second game is to get to the kindergarten table as quickly as possible. Along the way, you can find special fields that cause you to lose the queue, extra traffic, and so on.

2 points if adjacent to a feed house. When constructed, smash together two adjacent buldings so they are on the same space. They score normally and are not adjacent to each other.

Get your buns warmed up for this hotdog building game! Players compete to acquire ingredients, complete recipes, and score points in this rummy style card game. Turn for the wurst uses a tongue in cheek art style to flavor this rummy style recipe completion card game. Players begin the game with seven condiment cards and three recipe (goal) cards. On their turn players may do one of the following...
1. Draw two cards off the deck then discard one condiment card.
2. Dig a card from the discard pile, keeping every card on top of it, then discard a condiment card.
3. Draw two recipe cards keeping one and placing one in the community recipe pile.
4. Claim a recipe card from the community pile.

Players may complete a recipe card from their hand or play a set (3 of a kind) of condiment cards at anytime to score points.

A round ends when one player has finished at least three recipe cards and discarded their last card OR "Knockwursts", similar to knocking in gin.

When a round ends all players tally up their score from that round. A full game is played until a player has reached 100 total points.

-description from designer

This is a resort hotel where only dogs can stay.
There are five elegant hotels on the premises, and there is an adjoining big dog park.
As co-owners of this resort hotel, you aim to create rooms in the hotel under construction and invite dogs.

However, in order to have many dogs stay at the hotel, it is necessary to win the competition among owners and create guest rooms that are highly satisfying.
Can you become a reputable hotel owner?

A player places a tile on the board and places a dog token on the tile.
When a tile is placed on the board, the dog token in the dog run moves according to its location.
This dog token serves as a time track.
The player behind the time track always takes a turn.

The game ends when the pile of tiles is exhausted, and a battle for majority is played in each area.
The Victory Points are awarded according to the ranking in the battle for majority, and the player with the highest total score wins the game.

boardgame: The Way Of The Barbarian: A Solitaire Role Playing Game
description:
The Way Of The Barbarian (SOLO RPG)

Your family has been executed in front of your eyes, your village has been burned down, and all of your friends have been massacred or enslaved. Only you survived. You have endured, suffered, trained and waited for the right moment all these years. Your sword is sharpened, the rough land has hardened you. Now is the time. Time to take revenge on those who let you experience the horror of your childhood. There will be blood, much blood, and you will not rest until the evil threat has been brought to its righteousness - by your blade.

How To Play

Never forget what you are, The rest of the world will not

Start the game with creating your character. Roll a die on the character creation tables to determine your hero’s characteristics such as gender, name and his/her background story. After you have created your character and rolled your starting location, you begin your journey with a one-handed sword (Damage +0), three bandages, 10 supply and no armor. Step onto one of your adjacent fields to meet your first encounter. Step onto one of the adjacent fields to take your first step on your way to revenge - barbarian style.

equip

What do you need to play?

To enjoy the game you need three six-sided dice, a pencil, an eraser, the enclosed sheets, a piece of paper and of course your imagination. The game is designed in such a way that most likely no extra purchases are necessary. Six-sided dice can be found in almost every household, which is why the use of special dice was avoided.

The Way Of The Barbarian is a solo RPG, which means you don't need a Game Master to play.

boardgame: Adventures in Devil's Creek: A Solitaire Dungeon Crawler RPG in the Wild West
description:
ADVENTURES IN DEVIL´S CREEK

Adventures in Devil’s Creek is set in the wild west of a fictional America in the small town of Fortune’s Creek.

Fortune’s Creek was once a thriving small town that was a sought-after destination for treasure hunters and traders. The mines held vast amounts of gold and precious stones. The river that flows through the town kept bringing smaller crystals and sometimes even diamonds with it. This earned the city the name Fortune’s Creek. Anyone could get rich here, you just have to reach out and grab your hand.

But with wealth, corruption also found its way into the city and criminals soon regulated all trade routes. The legislature found itself powerless, but after three sheriffs disappeared in a very short time, the government sent a small army from Washington to ensure law and order in the city. However, the current rulers of Fortune’s Creek were unwilling to give up their gold mine without resistance and so it came to a wild, brutal battle that lasted for several days. Not only did criminals and crooks fall, many innocent people also died in the hail of bullets. After two days the last bullet was fired and the then corrupt mayor McKanzy was executed by the American army shortly afterwards. The city was now back in the hands of the state.

The events seemed to have affected not only the city but also the countryside, because the gold veins in the mines dried up and the river no longer brought any glittering stones with it. The once rich trading city became poorer and poorer. Where there was one joy and confidence, there was now more and more despair. This is how the city got its nickname Devil’s Creek. Only those who ventured deep into the mines could find gold - if they came out again. Those who made it reported strange creatures that have since found their home there. Soon hardly any people dared to venture into the widely ramified mine systems and the city was forgotten by history. You and your small group will end up in Devil’s Creek. Maybe you can get to the bottom of this and bring the city back to its former size.

The setting is enriched with "low fantasy" elements. You take on the role of three adventurers, take jobs in the local saloon and roam the countless mines of Fortune’s Creek, which were once rich in gold veins and are now inhabited by all sorts of creatures and other rabble.

WHAT DO I NEED TO PLAY?

To play, you need a piece of squared paper, a pencil, at least three 6-sided dice and one 4-sided dice. You should also have the attached cards printed out and arranged in a stack ready to hand.

HOW TO PLAY

Adventures in Devil’s Creek can best be classified under the category of "Dungeon Diver". You put together a group of three different characters (Brawler, Valkyrie, Voodoo Priest, White Witch, Gunslinger or Bounty Hunter) and set off to explore the local mines. Before or between the explorations, supplies can be replenished in the city, quests can be accepted or you can just relax and heal your wounds. It can be played by 1 to 3 players. The opponents are rolled and their behavior is determined by the drawing of cards.

GAMEPLAY

After creating the characters, you take a piece of squared paper, on which you draw the spaces that have been thrown out of the mine. The use of a DIN A5 sheet is recommended. Dice out the first room and draw it in the middle on one edge. Then you roll the dice on the space and resolve the encounters. If you defeat a boss, you have cleared the mine. The mine systems are reset and rerolled with each visit.

—description from the designer

7 Turns To Die
Welcome to the zombie apocalypse! You are one of the last survivors and try to defend your home against the attacking zombies. However, it will not be easy, you have to hold out until help will arrive in 7 rounds.
7 Turns To Die is a fun variant of Escalero or other dice poker games, in which you have to defend your house against the attacking horde of zombies. Use your result to roll the dice and put up walls or other defenses to stop the creatures. Play alone, competitively or together against the undead.
Good luck, you will need it!
Preparation
Each player needs a pencil, an eraser and the prefabricated "base sheet". You also need five standard 6-sided dice.

Each player puts his "base sheet" in front of him - then decide who starts.

How To Play
A game consists of 3 rounds in which you expand your base and thus keep the attacking zombie hordes off. In a round, each player has 7 turns to expand his base and thus protect it from the waves of zombies. You can roll the dice up to 3 times per turn. If you are already satisfied with the result after your first throw, you can end your turn early. After 7 turns have been completed, a wave of zombies appears.

—description from the designer

Bestiary is a simple card driven draft game in which players create chimeras by adding different creature parts.

Each turn, players take turns to draw a card from a pool, then use that card to begin a new chimera, continue one in progress, or complete one. Creature parts include tails, bodies and heads. The game ends when the draw deck runs out; then, the player with the most points among those printed in their chimeras wins.

—description from the designer

This is a Tiddledy Winks-style game in which two players take turns using a metal "snap" catapult to launch weighted wooden "figures" on to a colorful grid of 4x4 squares. Each square has a different points value and the goal is to be the first to reach a score of 250.
A flick race game. Build a track with what's on your table and race to victory. En route you may collect weapons and special powers.

Shan Khay is a game where you have to use math to win

You have to get the minimum number turning different bricks with figures from 1 to 9 (and 0). Brick have at their back the "Shan Khay" (finished with the unicorn logo at the end for the 0 figure) word. You roll two dices and you can turn several brick if the sum of their figures in the same than that of the two dices.

Incountry is a real time tabletop wargame for one or more players. Players will assume command over several different modern day forces or be given the option to create their own before facing off in intense tactical battles and military intelligence gambles.

Incountry features a quick to learn and in-depth game system with the absolute bare minimum for Tokens. Incountry plays intuitively and features a unique pregame battle where Players will faceoff with their opponent gambling on intelligence and bluffing their way to victory! Shape the battle to come by drawing the right cards or bluffing the intel you have to throw your opponents off!

boardgame: The Last Labour of Hercules: The English at Tangier versus the Sultanate of Morocco 1662-1684
description:
The Last Labour of Hercules is a game about the English occupation of Tangier during the second half of the 17th Century. The port was bequeathed to King Charles II as part of a dowry when he married the Portuguese princess, Catherine of Braganza. It would be another generation before the English captured Gibraltar from Spain, but Tangier seemed an ideal base for many of the same reasons. It would be a handy staging post for ships bound for West Africa and the Caribbean, it would place a watch upon the Catholic Spanish, and it might facilitate trade with the Moors, the port being one terminus of the ancient caravan route for slaves and gold-dust. There were only two problems. First, the Moors hated outsiders. Second, but more importantly, the port lay open to the worst the Atlantic could throw at it. The anchorage was deep, but it was unprotected. For twenty-two years the English tried to maintain themselves at Tangier, and withstood at least two significant sieges by the Moors, but at last the Government decided they had wasted enough money, though the fact that the Moors had at last forged a unified (and powerful) kingdom might have had something to do with the decision to pull out.

The Last Labour of Hercules is part card game and part wargame. Covering a period of twenty years, it does not represent a single siege of Tangier but many attempts, some large and some small, by the Moors, to dislodge the English. For long periods the protagonists tolerated each other, and the English even formed the odd local alliance. The Moors spent much of the period of occupation engaged in a brutal civil war — or put another way, they were in the process of emerging from Medieval anarchy. Also, they had to ward off attempts by the Ottoman Sultan to reestablish his suzerainty over the land.

As the English player you must keep the Moors at bay while gauging how many of your precious resources you can spare to build Tangier’s Great Mole – a breakwater that will protect your ships. As the Moorish player you must endevour to make the English inclined to leave, ideally by sacking Tangier. At the same time, you have two additional problems to deal with. First, there is the small matter of a civil war among the Berber tribes. Then, there is the Sultan at Istanbul. He seems to be under the false impression that Morocco belongs to him. If the Moors ever take the town's Citadel they win outright. If the English ever complete the Great Mole they win outright. If neither side can achieve their main objective, someone might or might not win on points, by comparing the English player’s progress on the Mole against the Moorish player’s ability to secure Morocco for his dynasty.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Witcher: Old World – Big Box
description:
In The Witcher: Old World, you become a witcher — a professional monster slayer — and immerse yourself in the legendary universe of The Witcher franchise.

Set years before the saga of Geralt of Rivia, The Witcher: Old World explores a time when monsters roamed the Continent in greater numbers, creating a constant peril that required the attention of expertly trained monster slayers, known as witchers. Five competing schools trained their adepts through brutal regimes, and once fully prepared, these now-recognized witchers set off to explore the land, seeking trouble and adventures and helping others for coin.

In this competitive adventure board game, 2-5 players travel across a vast map, embarking on masterfully penned quests, encountering and making ambiguous moral choices, fighting monsters — and sometimes brawling with other witchers to defend their school's honor!

The game lets players construct their own unique decks of cards by choosing from a wide range of abilities: attacks, dodges, and witcher combat magic — known as "signs". Through card synergy, players aim to achieve powerful combos as they utilize their witcher school's hallmark abilities to their full potential. Quests, battles, and even dice poker allow each player to earn money, obtain new items, and train their skills.

The first player to acquire 4-6 trophies, with the number being set at the start of play, wins the game instantly. You can obtain trophies by killing monsters, instigating and winning chaotic tavern brawls against another witcher, training attributes to their highest level, and resolving certain quests throughout their adventure.

The game will include a Solo Mode that will allow for a solitaire adventure on the Continent.

This Big Box version includes every expansion available during the Kickstarter.

October 15th, 1600 saw the bloodiest and most decisive battle of feudal Japan: the battle of Sekigahara.  The victor- the Tokugawa Shogunate- would rule Japan for the next 250 years and was the Last Daimyo. The battles leading up to that decisive moment became known as 'The Warring States period'. Now you can shape the course of Japanese history through skillful use of your loyal clans and manipulation of your allies whilst taking full advantage of natural events as they occur.
As one of 2-4 players, you will control one or more of feudal clans trying to establish supremacy over all of Japan. The game will start in 1554 and turns will represent about two years of time, ending no later than 1614 (30 turns). The game will include those events that defined this great struggle. Featuring floods, earthquakes, typhoons, peasant rebellions, pirates, famine and much more. There will be epic sieges and titanic open battles, as well as the chance to assassinate hated enemies.

The map will consist of one 22"x34" map covering all of mainland Japan and the two southern islands (Kyushu and Shikoku). Map labels will be in easy-to-read English font with accompanying Japanese characters to add historical flavor to proceedings. There will be 2 countersheets of 9/16" counters representing the 8 different major clans and 8 different minor clans, troop markers, plus various game effect counters. Two decks of cards (110 total) will be included.  A full color rulebook will include synopses of some of the major players of this period (the Sengoku Jidai) as well as other fascinating historical tidbits to enhance the flavor of the game.

The basic game will be quick to learn and easy to play but the players will have a host of optional rules that will add greater detail, such as battle tactics, different types of soldiers (spearmen, samurai, arquebusiers, etc.), defections, and alliances. There will be a number of scenarios, allowing one to play anything from the full war (1554-1614) to shorter campaigns.

Depending on interest, there may also be an extension to allow players to start from the traditional beginning of the Sengoku Jidai, 1467. If you think you are ready to test your skill to become the Last Daimyo, Bring It On Games is ready for you to say, “bring it on”.

—description from the publisher

description:
In June of 1962, Frank Morris, Clarence and John Anglin did the impossible.  They escaped from the inescapable prison of Alcatraz.  Or did they?  Situated in San Francisco Bay, over a mile from the mainland, with icy, cold waters infested with man-eating sharks, the prison was said to be impossible for any inmate to find freedom.  Over 14 previous escaped had proven that - but after their escape attempt, Morris and the Anglin brothers were never found.  Many people think they escaped and had remained hidden for the rest of their lives.

Escape from the Rock is a new game designed by Adam Starkweather.  From one to six players each play a prisoner that must find a way to escape from Alcatraz.  The prisoners a player will play will be one of the actual convicts of the famous prison. These include such notables as AL Capone, Meyer “Mickey” Cohen, Frank Morris (who actually escaped), John Anglin (who also escaped), Robert “The Birdman” Stroud, Whitey Bulger, Creepy Karpis, Machine Gun Kelly, as well as many others. Each prisoner has a unique set of skills that will make playing that prisoner a unique experience.

The game will have a 22 by 34 inch map, two decks of 55 poker quality cards, a sheet of 5/8ths counters, dice, and a beautiful box to keep everything nice and locked up for when you aren’t playing.  The game will have three general phases to the game - getting out of your cell, navigating your way to the water’s edge, and then finding a way to cross the icy waters of San Francisco Bay.  The first one to escape and live to tell the tale is the winner.  The game will be a wargame mechanically so this will be an ideal game to introduce the concepts of wargaming to your non-wargaming friends - have a copy of a Bulge game standing by.

—description from the publisher

I know, this doesn't look good... trapped on the 28th floor, between a pizza-hungry zombie horde, and the last deep dish in town...

You'll need to think quickly if you have any hope of surviving this apocalypse (and saving your pizza). It's time for an escape plan!

Escape Room Z is a printable game pack that transforms any space into an edge-of-your-seat escape room experience. Just imagine the perfect blend of 'escape room' and 'house party'... then add zombies!

This printable escape room kit combines the teamwork, tension, and puzzle-solving of escape rooms with the funloving elements of group party games. But be warned, this is a challenging game. Players will work together to escape the zombie horde by: finding a weapon, securing their pizza, and unlocking the balcony door. If time runs out... the zombies run in! Gameplay is centered around a series of puzzle and clue card which are hidden around the room. Players will need to logically connect elements on each card in order to solve puzzles. Each puzzle solved puts players one step closer to escaping the zombie horde.

Game setup is quick and spoiler-free. Hints and puzzle solutions are handled by an online hint system that players access on their smartphones. This means that no game master is required to supervise the experience, so you can join the game alongside your players.

The game can expand to accommodate any number of players by dividing your group into teams of 2-4, and then pitting them against each other to see who finishes the game first. In addition, you can edit the game file before printing to customize your own escape room experience.

So, think you've got what it takes to survive the zombie apocalypse? Let's find out!

Oh no, we're lost in time!

You and your friends were having a blast, when you were suddenly sucked right through space and time! Maybe it had something to do with that strange machine you found...

Either way, you better figure out how to fix it. It's getting dark, and those dinosaurs don't look happy to see you...

Escape Quest is a printable escape room game that transforms any space into a kids escape room experience. It's designed for young children (age 6-9), and plays like an immersive party game.

This printable escape room kit combines the teamwork, tension, and puzzle-solving of escape rooms with the laidback ease of group party games, all packaged for young children. The game is primarily centered around scavenger hunt mechanics, with some age-appropriate puzzles. Kids will need to find puzzle cards in each 'quest', and combine them in the correct order to fix their time machine and move to the next 'quest'. Progressing through 6 'quests' will finally see the kids find their back to their own timeline.

Game setup is quick and easy for parents. At least one adult will be required to run the game as the 'game master'. A step-by-step guide, complete with narrative text guides you through everything you need to know. So there's no prior experience required.

The game can expand to accommodate any number of players by dividing your group into teams of 2-4, and then pitting them against each other to see who finishes the game first. In addition, you can edit the game file before printing to customize your own escape room experience.

Counterinsurgency in Rhodesia 1966-1979.

The Rhodesian War is a 3 player game that traces insurgency and counter-insurgency (COIN) operations in Rhodesia (now Zimbabwe) from the late 1960s to the late 1970s Each player takes control of a Faction seeking to enforce their agenda: the Rhodesian Government or one of two insurgent Factions—the Marxist-Leninist group Zimbabwe African People's Union (ZAPU). , or the Maoist group of ZANU (Zimbabwe African National Union).
Through the use of military, political, economic actions and by exploiting various events, players will build and maneuver their forces to influence the populace, extract resources, and fight their opponents to achieve their Faction's goals. A stack of cards regulates events, tempo, victory conditions, and other mechanics. The Rhodesian War  uses the COIN series system.

Operation OVERLORD was the historic invasion of the European continent and the beginning of the battle to breach the “impenetrable” Atlantic Wall. Drop Zone: Sainte-Mère-Église is the opening act that takes place in and around the sleepy Norman country town of Sainte-Mère-Église located behind Utah Beach, a crucial crossroads for the U.S. Fourth Infantry Division as it fought its way inland. Ten days before D-Day, the plans for dropping the 82nd Airborne Division shifted it from the west side of the Cotentin Peninsula over to the east side, closer to Utah Beach, and responsibility for capturing Sainte-Mère-Église shifted from the 101st Airborne Division to the 82nd, specifically the 505th Parachute Infantry Regiment. Second and Third Battalions, under Lt. Colonels Benjamin Vandervoort and Edward Krause respectively, found themselves tasked with protecting the vital crossroads from German counterattacks aimed at bottling up the Americans on Utah Beach. Facing the paratroopers and the units driving off the beaches were a mixed bag of defenders: Georgians of the coastal defense unit Ost Bataillon 795, Infanterie-Division 709; regular troops from Grenadier-Regiment 1058, Luftlande-Infanterie-Division 91; and the well-equipped Sturm-Bataillon AOK 7—all supported by ad-hoc armor units.

Drop Zone: Sainte-Mère-Église is a Historical Advanced Squad Leader (HASL) module covering the first two days of the battles around the important Norman town of Sainte-Mère-Église. Each side attacks and defends in an effort to do better than their historical counterpart. Can you as the American not only hold the town but also push the German forces off the map as you expand your perimeter? Or can you as the German retake the town from the Americans and delay the invasion?

Drop Zone: Sainte-Mère-Église comes with three historically-accurate maps of the town of Sainte-Mère-Église and its environs jointly crafted by Sharon Boyd, designer Ken Dunn, and artist Charlie Kibler. Additionally, there are 11 individual scenarios each recreating a portion of the battle, as well as three Campaign Games of various sizes depicting either the entire battle or smaller portions of it.

—description from the publisher

This is the age of technology. Age of progress. Age of experiments. And You are one of its most famous inventors - or want to be one.

But successfully devising and patenting something is only half the game. The other - and most exciting - half is lawsuits: it is possible that inventions patented don't belong to the player that filed them... If such an invention is challenged, the fraud loses points.

You are the 1st Marine division tasked with holding Guadalcanal against the Japanese. Your Marines are tough but isolated as the Japanese build up around you. Your biggest asset is Henderson Field, a small airstrip that gives you control of the sea lanes during the day; at night, the Japanese sneak in boats with men and supplies, and sometimes bombard the field directly. You must weigh the various threats against you and defend the island until relief comes.

You are the American player in the game as it centers on Japanese attempts to capture Henderson Field by land. They have three avenues of attack: from the east (Koli Point), west (Point Cruz), or south (Bloody Ridge). Your mission will not be easy. Good luck. Now you are in command and the decisions are yours…

All you need to provide is a die, a pen, and a sense of adventure to play.

GAME SUMMARY
Guadalcanal: Solitaire contains 24 game plays of the solitaire gameboard. Each game play includes a Japanese Actions/Events table and The American Actions/Events table with a map of Guadalcanal. Each play you can write directly on the game map to show unit reinforcements, movement, and losses. Also, track your turns, air power, and the state of Henderson Field all on the board for each game play.

Game plays take about 30 minutes or less to finish, with no set up or clean up, open the bookgame and re-fight the battle of Guadalcanal anywhere.

description:
The game is very simple and is based on 30 different and fun challenges, which can be played in no time.

Some challenges are to be played at home, because they need typical objects such as spoons, a napkin, a comb or a pillow.
Others do not need any additional items, or simply use game components, such as the box, the 2 wooden dice or the 6 wooden thumb pieces.

Polyminix is ​​a game of logic and concentration, with different levels of difficulty. Each player must combine the individual pieces (pentominoes, tetrominoes, etc.) to be able to cover the geometric shape drawn on the card.

Each card shows an animal and 12 characteristics have been selected, including a peculiarity and a curiosity, to highlight some of the most interesting aspects that are not normally explored.

Each card also shows the conservation status of the animal depicted, a fact that makes us reflect on the delicate balance of nature, to be admired and safeguarded.

Many possible games can be played with these cards: "Discover it" where players need to guess the selected animal among 25 cards knowing only certain information; "Animal line" where players have to put animals in ascending order of the chosen characteristic (e.g. from the smallest to the biggest); "Battle" is similar to "Top Trumps" but with a twist!

All players roll a die. Whoever rolls highest pays a coin into the till and dons the jester's cap. The other players take turns to roll two dice. Each player has a card, where he marks off the numbers he just rolled by closing the corresponding flap. Whenever he closes a flap, the player may roll again. If a player rolls a number which flap is already closed, he pays a coin to the jester. On the roll of a 7, the player pays a coin into the till and takes over the jester's cap and hands over his card to the former jester. When a player has closed all flaps on either half of his card, he gets half the content of the till. The game ends, when a player has closed al his flaps. This player wins the game and the till.

Niemen 1920 simulates the Battle of Niemen, one of the biggest battles of the Polish-Soviet War 1919-1921. The battle took place between 15 and 25 September, 1920, near the Niemen River, approximately within the area designated by the cities of Suwałki, Grodno, and Białystok. Soviet armies, defeated by Polish forces during the Battle of Warsaw in August, 1920, tried to establish a defensive line, extending from Polish-Lithuanian border to the Polesie region (Polesia), with the city of Grodno being a pivotal point in the Soviet defences. However, they were outflanked by Polish armies and destroyed, which allowed Polish forces to move further east and eventually reach the Tarnopol-Dubno-Minsk-Drissa line in October, 1920.

The games uses the WB-95 wargaming system by Taktyka i Strategia.

Each player receives 15 Word cards at the beginning of the game. On a player's turn they must lay out multiple Word cards on the board in order to make a complete sentence. The longer the sentence made the more points that sentence is worth. The first player to get 300 points wins.

There are a number of common and "sexy" words that are included in the game. Players are encouraged to create sentences that their fellow players find sensual, cheeky, and amusing. This is a game that encourages consensual "adult play".

—user summary

Rotten Rampage is a standalone solo game that is included with the Token Terror board game set. The objective of the game is to assume the role of a zombie and conquer the village. As the game progresses, players are given the opportunity to acquire additional zombies to add to their arsenal. However, the task of being a zombie is not easy, as your parts can become detached, and the villagers have an array of weapons at their disposal. The fewer the villagers, the more aggressive they become, requiring players to exhibit great tenacity.

The game comprises several rounds, which continue until either the villagers have destroyed five out of the six zombie parts or all the villagers have become infected. The zombie parts represent the entire army's health tracker, making all infected targets vulnerable to damage, particularly toward the end. Some of the zombie parts are required for actions, such as using the zombie head to infect villagers with a coin toss. While these actions are necessary to win the game, there are spaces where players can regenerate lost parts.

Each zombie part and villager has a number. The villagers move through revealed cards each round, with a battle triggered whenever a villager and a zombie share the same space. Once the battle is resolved, the moving phases come to a halt. During the encounter, a dice is rolled, and if the result falls within the range listed on the attacking villager's tile, the associated number is detached from the zombie. The attack range for each villager's number is clearly printed on each tile, allowing players to develop strategies at a glance. However, the villagers could enter spaces that level up their range, flipping their tile over to give them a wider range of attacks. The tile underneath them also allows for alternative maneuvers or actions, adding to the complexity of the game. Hence, effective positioning is vital for success.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – The Brothers' War
description:
Get ready for a blast from the past as Dominaria's heroes must launch an expedition into ancient history in order to stop the New Phyrexian planar invasion in the present day! This Magic: The Gathering expansion uses time travel as a framing device in order to revisit the epic conflict first glimpsed in 1994's Antiquities in the form of excavated artifacts. Nearly 30 years of real-time later, the continent-destroying war between the two brothers, Urza and Mishra, is finally getting its time in the spotlight.

The Brothers' War is packed with soldiers, armies, giant robots, and all the horror and destruction of mechanized warfare. It boasts two new mechanics: Prototype, which allows players to cast a less powerful version of an artifact at a lower mana cost; and Powerstones, artifact tokens that can tap for mana that can't be used to cast nonartifact spells. Joining them are the returning mechanics of Unearth (first seen in Shards of Alara) and Meld (first seen in Eldritch Moon).

Similar to the Timeshifted cards in Time Spiral or the Mystical Archive in Strixhaven: School of Mages, there is a bonus artifact sheet in The Brothers' War: every booster pack of The Brothers' War contains a reprint of a classic artifact, from Magic's past, in the retro frame.

Two preconstructed Commander decks featuring Urza and Mishra were released alongside the main set. Additionally, to play into the "giant robots" theme, this set also includes a crossover special in the form of 15 cards depicting characters from another Hasbro intellectual property about giant robots fighting: Transformers. These cards are not in draft boosters, but can be found in set boosters, collector boosters, and bundles.

description:
Bosnian War: 1992-1995 is a two-player Strategic level board-wargame of the brutal conflict that took place in Bosnia and Herzegovina in 1992-1995. There are two players: the Bosnian government (BiH) player and the Bosnian Serb (VRS) player. The Bosnian Government player controls the units of the Croatian Defense Council (HVO) although certain game events allow the Serb player to control the HVO units. The Serb player can also control the units of the Krajina Serb Army (SKV) through random events or NATO intervention. The Bosnian Government player can also control units of the Croatian Army (HV) under certain conditions. In this game, the enemy population is not a victim of collateral damage, rather it is a target, so conquering towns and cities is a key aspect of the game.

Each game turn represents three months of time. Each hex represents 12 km (7.5 miles). Each combat unit represents a task force ranging in size from a regiment to a division.

22 x 34-inch game map, 176 5/8-inch counters
Included in Strategy & Tactics #351, January 2025.

-description from designer
