Legends of StormCity is a roll & write game, in which each player takes the role of one of the heroes of StormCity who will fight against villainous leaders and their henchmen who intend to carry out their evil plans to conquer the city.

Each player will control a hero sheet in which they will write down the damage they receive and the powers they can use. Villains and minions have their own game sheets.

During a game of Legends of StormCity, the villain's team activates first by rolling their dice, depending on the results they play special abilities, then try to activate their main plan and any other plans that are in play, and then initiate attacks against the heroes. Once the turn of the villains is finishes, the heroes’ turn begins. Hero players take turns clockwise, starting with the player who has the initiative token. Players roll their respective dice, depending on the result heroes can activate their special abilities, deactivate enemy plans, and then attack the villains and their henchmen until they are knocked out and they can be taken to Red Cube.

In the game you can assemble your own adventures, you can choose among several heroes, villains, henchmen and different plans. You can also play a campaign mode with pre-built scenarios.

With arts by Daniel Tejerina and Alejandra Catalán.

-description from designer

Ready to save the world? Of course you are. You're a wizard, after all. With a good heart. A hero, some would say. Alas, there are bad wizards, too. Dark wizards. Wizards with ideas as to how society should function that are different than your own. The kind you'll need to Murder by Magic, in order to do all that saving of the world you fancy so proudly. However, they're trying to murder you, as well. Because, although you think they're the bad guys, they think you're the bad guy. Only one of you is right.

How To Play:
Each wizard is given a number of cards from the white deck depending on the player count (7/6/5 for player counts 2/3/4) which are then placed face up in a line for all to see. These are each players' total health points. The player with the lowest total amount of health takes the first turn. For each turn, each player must make 3 moves. A move consists of either drawing one card from the black deck or playing one card from their hand.

Spell cards are played on top of any opponents health cards in order to deplete said opponent's health. When the value of the combination of attack spells meets or exceeds the value of health points listed on the card the opponent must place your attack cards on the discard pile and flip over their attacked health point card to show it has been depleted.

When played, each attack spell card counts as one move. The only exception is if you have 2 or more of the same type of spell. They can be played together and it will count as one move. Other cards may help you to heal, add additional health or provide an extra turn.

When a player's total health is completely depleted, they are dead and out of the game. Be the last wizard standing at the end and you win!

A 2 player only version of Stoopido with fewer components.

For setup each player gets a big red pair of plastic glasses on which different parts of masks can be attached: one part for the hat (or top of the head), one for the nose, two for the ears, and one for the eyes (some of the later also hinder sight).

Player take turns to roll a six sided custom die with faces, which determine, which part can be added to a face. Then everybody tries to first hit the correct "button" tile in front of the players, to be allowed to place such a part onto another player. Additionally there is a "wild" die face, which allows to place any part and a "bad luck" one, were another player may put a part onto the player, who rolled the die.

Whoever is first full attached with all pieces looses the game and a photography can be taken from him or her.

It's your final stand. The rest of your adventuring party has gotten lost (or worse) and you find yourself alone in the wilderness preparing for the fight of your life. The monsters are not far behind. After hours of running you decide your only choice is to turn and fight. It's then that you look up. There, in the bright moonlight, you see Griphold Tower. The legendary structure where dozens of heroes in ages past fought bravely to the end. It's as good a place as any to make your stand. Perhaps your story will be added to the legends.

Griphold Tower is a no-table-needed solo game. You play as a lone adventurer in the top room of an ancient tower. You will use your character's strength and unique abilities to defeat waves of monsters coming through the room's only entrance. At the end of the final wave you'll face a final boss that presents a distinct challenge requiring every ounce of your creativity to defeat.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Palaces of Carrara (Second Edition): Retro Pack
description: With this Retro Pack of 31 playing cards, you can play the original Palaces of Carrara with the game materials of the 2nd edition.

On a volcanic moon, your mission is to rescue a prisoner from a bunker by avoiding robot sentries. Time is limited!

You lay out a grid of 6x8 floor tiles (incl. 2 lava tiles that evrything dies on) plus a door tile on top where the prisoner is. Then place 6 robot sentries on the grid and your 4 rescuers carrying a crate with a protective suit for the prisoner below the grid.

You move your team onto the grid from any point below trying to reach the prisoner behind the door on top. Once freed, you must take the prisoner back to the bottom of the grid where your team started. When successful, you win if you have at least one team member and the prisoner still in play.

During a turn first you roll a 1D6 for the robot movement. The robots kill any team member that is on the same tile with them. Then you can move each team member 1 tile in any direction.
Repeat until either you rescued the prisoner or all of your team members are dead. Easy mode gives you 25 turns, hard mode only 20 turns to accomplish this.

So, are you up to the challenge? Can you rescue the prisoner in time?

How do you make a game about the great city of Sioux Falls, South Dakota?

While it's impossible to include everything, we jammed all the Sioux Falls we could into this box. It's full of outdoor adventures, family attractions, rich traditions, and of course...Jazzfest - A little something for everyone! Looking for a fun way to experience Sioux Fall?

Here's a quick idea of how to play!

Buy your favorite Sioux Falls properties, and increase your rent by collecting City Blocks and trading them in for Keys to the City. Sound easy? Well, add in some traffic jams, property tax, and the IRS and it gets a little more difficult and a lot more fun!

Get your game on!

Choose your token and advance to Go Sioux Falls! You may soon be the Mayor...or out of the game eating too many Almond Croissants!
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
The Net Zero Game 2050 is an event-driven business strategy, sustainability and negotiation board game based on stakeholder capitalism.

All business leaders will get 15 Money coins, Company Cards and Team cards and will decide which Company and team to keep.

The objective for each business leader (gamer) is to create a portfolio of net zero greenhouse gas emission companies considering value chains and synergies.

The business leader in his/her turn will take a Carbon Event Card and will react to it by choosing the event with the best impact on their portfolio. The others players can also act deploying green executives to change the events.

All the Carbon events are based on real events to open a great nd fun discussion about the impact of technology, market and trends into business and daily life.

—description from the designer

HEXIAN is a 2-players shoot 'em up boardgame, inspired by the glorious retro arcade space shooters.
Will you beat your opponent and reach a new High Score?

You create a 6x4 grid with 24 alien invader ships and Flying Saucer above that moves to the right turn by turn. Below the attackers you place your defender ships for both players. You draw 4 cards each turn from the action card deck that you use to steer your ship left and right below the invaders, gain shields, fire lasers, place decoys, also to negatively influence your opponent, like pushing his ship away.
When the last of the 24 alien ships is destroyed, the game is over and you score the alien ships that you shot down (different ships are worth different points). 
The player with the higher score wins. Keep track of the high score and try to beat it in the next game.

The Carrier Expansion adds Carriers, Carrier Wings, and Carrier Squadrons to the game. Use Carrier Squadrons to attack Soviet Ships and Subs on the Tactical Display, or attack Targets a distance from the Carrier. Soviet Bandits and Air Defenses can reduce your Squadron strength during the mission.

This expansion includes:
8 Carriers (64 cards)
4 Carrier Commanders (24 cards)
13 Carrier Air Wings (13 cards)
1 Counter Sheet
3 Campaigns
Carrier Expansion Rulebook
Carrier Combat Card
Summary Sheet
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The SSN Expansion adds SSNs for use instead of Task Force ships.  Your SSN acts alone to clear out Soviet forces, adjusting your SSN depth to attack ships and subs above and below the Thermal layer.

This expansion includes:
8 SSNs (48 cards)
4 SSN Commanders (24 cards)
1 Counter Sheet
4 Campaigns
SSN Expansion Rulebook
Summary Sheet

The Allies Expansion adds NATO and other Western ships from around the world to your fleets. This includes ships from: Canada, UK, Australia, France, Italy, and Germany

This expansion includes:
50+ NATO and Allied Ship Cards
50+ Ship Counters

All Aboard The Christmas Express Game that's holiday fun for everyone! Will you be the first player to the top in this mad dash to the North Pole? Be careful! There are hazards and other players looking to delay your progress! You'll have to avoid the Hustle Bustle, the Reindeer Crossings, and the Snow Storms. A simple plan or a clever strategy might get you there... or you may need help from Santa himself! Take a trip on a magic train and share the fun-filled ride with family and friends. The classic card-based movement system and lots of player interaction make this a great game for any day, not just the holidays. And may your days be merry and bright!

—description from the publisher
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
In the 1850s, Americans were turning their eyes to the West. Hardy settlers had been traveling the plains for the past decade. In 1856, a group of men from Columbus, Ohio selected a spot near the confluence of the Platte and Loup Rivers as the place they would start a new town, in Nebraska, called Columbus. 150 years later, Columbus, Nebraska celebrates its Sesquicentennial or 150th birthday.

From a proud past filled with characters like Buffalo Bill Cody and the North Brothers to a current city of 21,000 filled with industry and advanced agriculture, it has been 150 years of "Power and Progress."

Enjoy this collector's edition of the world's favorite board game, Monopoly, with images and properties drawn from Columbus, Nebraska in 2006.

ZomBined is a fast-paced card game where players mash together zombies to attack other survivors. A Survivor must use the weapon or action cards strategically to outlive the others. Should a survivor fail, the game continues for them as they join the ranks of the undead to take down other players! In this survival horror card game, the last survivor wins!
2 games in 1: Scrabble plus Scrabble Junior.
A small version of the game with cards that need to be cut apart. It only has around half the cards of the full game.
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
Play as the heroes from Stranger Things and try to save Hawkins from the roaming monsters. Be careful: some characters could be corrupted by the Mind Flayer, hidden in the shadows, and could turn against their friends!

In this bluffing game with secret identities, each player try to accomplish their objective while preventing others to accomplish theirs!

Components:

    1 board
    2 pawns  
    1 rulebook
    6 character cards
    12 objective/corruption card
    50 action cards

The party game of intimate questions.

In Ohh, Yes all you have to do is to know the other players... or guess their habits and thoughts. You only score points if you can tell, how many of them would like to lead a country, or how many of them says 'bless you', if somebody sneezes. Tis a conversation-starting party game developed in Hungary - about sex.

When betting, you can choose between what the majority thinks about the question, or which two players are least or most likely to answer yes to the question. And don't be afraid that some topic will creep too deep into your intimate zone: each player has a taboo token that can be used to stay away from a too sensitive question.

boardgame: A Game About Writing Words, Aiming to Match Other Folk Exactly 3 Times.
description:
Cards have 2 letters on one side and 2 topics on the other (unrelated by theme but related through whimsical wordplay).

Each round, 2 letters and 2 topics are simultaneously revealed, and then everyone has a minute to write as many words as they want, starting with either letter and relating (even tangentially) to either topic.

After the minute, everyone reveals their answers and scores for how many they match with other players.

Match 1 word for 1pt, 2 words for 2pts, 3 words for 4pts!

If you match more than 3 words, you 'bust' and score nothing.

Play 6, 10, or 15 rounds for a short, medium, or long game.

At the end, the person with the most points is the winner.

-description from designer

Players hold 3 cards with different types of ghosts in their hands, numbered 1-5 (independent from the type). In each round, players think about which of their ghosts to play, then a 20 second timer is started and everyone looks down. While the timer runs, they can only communicate with each other by knocking under the table. After time is up, everyone plays a ghost card simultaneously. Depending on which ghosts meet in which place and in which combo, points are scored. First player(s) to 8 points win(s) the game.

(The game mentions no year of publication, but it was available at SPIEL 2022.)
publishers: Adventure Planning Service (冒険企画局 / Bouken)

description:
This is a fixed partnership, may-follow trick taker that features a hex board.  Players will be playing out tricks and determining turn order resolution based on a variable suit strength track and card rank.

Every trick will result in at least 2 spaces being claimed on the hex board for each team, along with the tiles from those board locations for potential end game majority scoring.

Clever teamwork to maximize points on majority scoring and board points will determine the winner.

This is a fixed partnership, may-follow trick-taker with a variable suit strength track that features pick-up and deliver on a hex board.

Every trick will result in special actions awarded to players based on their position within a trick.  The trick winner will also get a bonus reward for winning a trick.

Clever teamwork to maximize points on rails scoring and delivery points will determine the winner.

Family Favorites is a trick taker that is inspired by Tarot style card games.  Players will play out tricks and collect ingredients to add to their soup pots in an attempt to complete a family favorite recipe.  In addition, players can collect bonus points in a variety of ways based on the style that they make their soups.

You and your friends (or people you hire off Craigslist) each take the role of a competitive shark, battling it out to eat tourists, locals, or even a dead whale carcass in a race to get 50 points.  There's tricks and treats and fights and bites along the way.  It's a back and forth tile-laying-take-that-oh-no-you-didn't finfunfest (I think that's German for shark-party.)

With so many different tile types and draws, each game is delightfully different, like the friends you hire off Craigslist.  There's luck in the tile pulls and dice rolls. There's strategy in tile usage, plans of attack, and fight or flight choices.  Adults and kids and hobos alike will love the quick-natured fun battle of Sharks Feeding Frenzy!

—descriptions from the designer

Khalkin-Gol War: Struggle for Mongolia, 1939 is an operational-level, two-player wargame covering a “what if” Japanese-Soviet war in Mongolia in 1939. The historical campaign saw a series of limited actions in the late spring and early summer of 1939 along the Khalka River (Khalkin Gol) on the Manchukuoan/Outer Mongolian border. The campaign ended in a corps-level battle in August 1939 in which the Soviets decisively defeated the Japanese and produced a cease-fire between the two antagonists. The assumption of the game is that both Tokyo and Moscow decided instead to turn this into a full-scale war.

Each turn of play represents a month of operations. Each hex on the map is approximately 30 kilometers across. Ground units are mostly built around divisions, with breakdowns into brigades and regiments. Air units represent anything from an elite squadron to a mediocre group.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: War Plan Z: The Kriegsmarine Strikes
description:
War Plan Z: The Kriegsmarine Strikes assumes that Adolf Hitler had not canceled the Kriegsmarine (Germany Navy) expansion program in 1939. World War II in Europe does not begin September of that year. Instead, Hitler waits until War Plan Z is complete (in various stages). The game is a strategic two player simulation of the ensuing hypothetical naval campaigns fought between the Germans and the Allies sometime in the 1940s. One assumption of the game is that since Germany concentrates on increasing the Navy, there is no campaign in the west, so France remains an Allied power. Germany and the USSR still divide Poland, and an uneasy peace remains in effect on the Eastern Front.

The game system shows the effects of various operations. Players conduct actions that encompass discrete combat, logistical, intelligence and other operations. A player can conduct one or more actions per turn, depending on control of bases.

Each area on the map is about 550 miles across. Each turn represents one month of operations. Ship units represent two fleet aircraft carriers, divisions of two or three battleships, four to eight cruisers, flotillas of 19-40 destroyers, or various numbers of other ship types. Most ground units in the game represent corps or divisions. Aircraft units represent six to twelve squadrons of aircraft.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Drive on Leningrad: 1941 (Campaigns in Europe Volume 1)
description:
Drive on Leningrad: 1941 (CIE Volume 1) is a two-player series of games focused on campaign scenarios at the division/corps level. The system uses the same maps as War in Europe (WIE), with updated graphics and a more detailed terrain analysis. The counters have been upgraded and are presented with historical identifications. The game system utilizes a Move/Fight, Fight/Move sequence of play to portray simply doctrinal differences among contending armed forces. It also utilizes headquarters units to project logistics and combat support capabilities.

Drive on Leningrad covers the Army Group North drive on Leningrad in 1941, and the ensuing Soviet counteroffensive. It ends with the German relief of the Demyansk Pocket.

Each game turn (GT) represents around one week in clear weather and two weeks in mud and snow game turns. Each hex represents 20 miles (32.5 km) across. German, Finnish, and Western Allied units are shown mostly as division-sized units, with Soviet and Axis Allied units depicted as corps.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Once upon a long long time ago, there was a kingdom far far away. This kingdom had knights, kings, princesses, and, first and foremost, lots of witches. The witches were up to no good at all: they talked with animals, shoved innocent travelers into the river and Cursed the queen’s children (the princes and princesses, basically). Sometimes, them witches quarreled; who may use the cauldron first, who may shove this particular traveler, who may hang out at the graveyard – actually, to be honest, most of the time they quarreled. Mightiest of them all was – dramatic music – the Archwitch. Was. The Archwitch is dead.

Over multiple rounds, all witches decide secretly and simultaneously what they want to do where this round. You might want to collect poisonous mushrooms in the forest, or magical toads in the mountains, or maybe you want to mislead travellers in the bog. Any time multiple witches go to the same location, no productive witching is possible there. However, you may try to change the other witches’ minds by giving them one of your "Favour" markers, making them retreat to their cottage.

As soon as those problems are solved, items are collected, favours called, and quests fulfilled, all with the aim to gain prestigious titles. Whoever gains three titles first is appointed the new Archwitch (Erzhexe) and wins.

—description from the designer

A game of battles and strategy in which 40 territories of Tuscany are fought over by five tribes of wild boars: tourist guides, farmers, citizens, mushroom hunters and "pottini" (bragging people).
In this party game ideal for an evening with friends, you will have to show your strategic side to defeat your opponents with cards, dice and fangs.
Start fighting with only one goal: to conquer all the Great Pigs Reign with the shout of… Gnamo! ("let's go" in Tuscany)

—description from the publisher (translated)

Un gioco di battaglie e strategia in cui 40 territori della Toscana vengono contesi fra cinque tribù di cinghiali: guide turistiche, contadini, cittadini, fungaioli e pottini.
In questo gioco di società ideale per accendere una serata fra amici, dovrai sfoderare il tuo lato strategico per sconfiggere gli avversari a suon di carte, dadi e zanne.
Inizia a combattere con un solo obiettivo: conquistare tutto il Gransuinato al grido di… Gnamo!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A Sci-Fi themed card game. Points are earned by winning challenges, capturing enemy cards, and earning credits. Governments use military and civilian abilities to achieve their objectives. This game features a "hand-building" mechanic. Draft cards into your hand (not into a discard pile) at the beginning of each round; unused cards are rolled over to the next two rounds.

Players will play four cards per challenge (trick). The most powerful card will win the hand, but there are exceptions. Military can combine power, diplomats prevent military from winning, cards can be captured or eliminated. Assassins, Diplomats, Mechs, Merchants, Emissaries, Sappers and Spies are just some cards you will encounter.  A complete game consists of 3 challenges.

—description from the publisher

Set in the legendary science fiction universe of the Traveller roleplaying game, To Honor Grandfather lets players take on the role of  young aliens (Droyne) competing to gain the favor of their leader (Grandfather).

Using the custom dice, players will travel between a variety of unique planetary systems and engage with a huge selection of events, each with the potential to draw Grandfather's approving eye. Those with the most "honor" after all planets have been explored will be dubbed the victor!

—description from the publisher

Launch Crew, additional Sphere Goal cards, and additional Resistance ships mini-expansions for Solar Sphere

Launch Crew: Begin the game with a unique crew member and slightly asymmetrical setup.

Additional Sphere Goal cards

Additional Resistance ships: more resistance ships for more variety

The Kickstarter promo pack also comes with deluxe turn order markers

Head Spin is a lively new family game. Quick fingers and minds race to crack the color code and dial it in on their fidget spinner. The first to get their spinner on the landing pad with the right color combo wins the round!

This small expansion facilitates playing Raccoon Tycoon solitaire.

The pack contains 9 Action cards and 16 Railroad Auction cards.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
This small expansion facilitates playing Lizard Wizard solitaire.

The pack contains 9 Action cards and 9 Dungeon Delve cards.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Go beyond the Regina subsector in this expansion for the dice-rolling game, To Honor Grandfather. Set in the science fiction universe of the legendary Traveller roleplaying game, this expansion will allow you to explore and interact with the Vilis subsector in a whole new way!

Included in the Vilis expansion:

     Player boards for a 5th and 6th player.
     Secret Objectives to help shape your strategies.
     Droyne Caste Abilities, giving each player a unique power.
     New System cards charting the Vilis subsector.
     New Encounter cards, with strange new Abilities and Effects.


The Vilis expansion can be freely integrated with the base game, allowing easy and seamless play!

—description from the publisher

Exocolony is set in the not-so-distant future, just as humans are beginning to colonize the solar system, but haven't yet colonized other stars.  1-5 players jockey for position, buy and sell resources, build colonies and discover new lifeforms.  The main path to victory is to build up enough resources to send a ship to another star system.

Players will collect resources in the form of wooden blocks each turn.  These are then spent to place wooden colonies and purchase cards.  Victory points are awarded for settling colonies and buying action cards.  16 Victory points are needed to win.

—description from the designer

Rêves de Gloire ("Dreams of Glory") is a solitaire, strategic-level wargame of the Napoleonic Wars between 1805-14. The player takes the role of Napoleon Bonaparte, the newly self-crowned Emperor of France, determining French foreign and military policy, establishing Bonapartist dynasties in satellite states, and raising and leading French and allied armies and fleets in an era of ever-shifting alliances. The game system controls the opposing Coalition forces led by Great Britain. The objective of the Empire is to conquer or control either Great Britain, or as much of Europe as possible, without upsetting the balance of Power, and thus triggering radical revolution or conservative reaction.
Each game turn represents one year. Sizes of the regions vary but are generally at least 100 miles across. Unit sizes are army level (10,000+) with individually named leaders.

—description from the publisher

description:
Put together the best team of ninjas to succeed in your missions! The village of Konoha is counting on you!


     Pass a Ninja card to your neighbor!
     Evaluate the strength of your team and communicate... without saying too much!
     If you think you're strong enough, go on a mission!



A thrilling team game that will delight all NARUTO fans!

description:
This promotionnal card is a small expand to play with Trek 12 Amazonia

Carte observation promotionnelle pour le jeu Trek 12 Amazonie. Cette carte s'ajoutent aux autres du jeu.

This promotionnal observation card is a small expansion for trek 12 amazonia.

Cette carte d'observation promotionnelle s'ajoute aux cartes incluses dans le jeu Trek 12 Amazonie.

Berlin Connection is a fast network-/portfolio-building train game with the objective of having the most valuable stock portfolio at the end of the game. Players must start new railroads, they may expand existing railroads and they are able to trade stocks in the railroads.


There are 6 colors of wooden locomotives, each color representing a railroad. Players are dealt random hands of 5-10 (depending on number of players) locomotives, which are essentially a stock portfolio; the rest of each color are placed in a pool. On your turn, you can start a railroad or increase the value of a single railroad by placing 1-5 locomotives from the remaining pool of that company or you can trade one locomotive in your portfolio for one or two locomotives from the remaining pool in a railroad.
 The game board is a map of Germany, with cities worth anywhere from 1-8 points. Connecting to a city adds to a company's value. The game holds 3-6 players for about 30 minutes.
 The game ends when there is a railroad in every purple city or when it is agreed that this is not possible. Players' scores are determined by the value of each railroad at the end of the game, multiplied by the number of stocks the player has for that railroad.

Berlin Connection uses the components found in Queen Games’ Paris Connection game. The Berlin Connection game comes with 2 11x17 maps of Germany that require trimming and the rules necessary to play Berlin Connection. You must own Paris Connection to play this game.

You have joined the team at Kauket Analytics Operating Systems and you are tasked with investigating the strange events that surround your predecessor's work. In this Lovecraft-inspired narrative adventure game you will scour for clues, solve riddles and puzzles, and put together pieces of the story in attempt to find the hidden temple and unveil the secrets within.

There is no turn order or specific roles, players work collaboratively to piece together clues hidden throughout specially crafted artefacts, documents, and digital content. The game ends when you uncover the full truth of the narrative mystery.

Will you discover the purpose of the temple? Was it created to praise a new god, a memorial to those who died there, or was it a cage, keeping something dangerous inside?

It's up to you, the players, to find out.

A game of cards and speed. Those fast games, that everyone enjoys. They explain and play quickly. They don't fail. =)

HOW TO PLAY?

All cards are dealt. Each player places the pile of cards received in front of him, without seeing them.

In turn, they turn over the first card of their personal pile and put it in the middle saying a color.

If they hit the color, different actions happen.

There are two jokers where everyone must participate... that's why they must be attentive to what always happens.

Whoever runs out of cards wins!

Whoever doesn't want to play loses!

POWERFUL THE LITTLE ONE!

With Boo! We inaugurate our pocket Party Games, to take it anywhere and laugh a lot.

You receive a package in the mail from your favourite summer camp counselor. The camp has been shut down in a hurry, the grounds abandoned. Something has happened. Nothing is as it should be. They need your help.

Inspired by classic 1980s coming of age stories like Stand by Me, E.T., and even Stranger Things, Camp Moonforest pulls players into a narrative adventure filled with puzzles and different challenges to overcome.

Suitable for young adventures, the game provides a beginner-level introduction to a world of intrigue and mystery. Players work together to search through physical props, artefacts, and documents that allow the story to unfold through a narrative investigation.

Together you'll scour for clues, solve different riddles and puzzles, and ultimately unravel the mystery surrounding Camp Moonforest. The game ends when players learn what has really been going on in the quiet of the abandoned camp.

This fearsome immersive adventure invites players to work collaboratively as they scour for clues, solve riddles and puzzles, and unravel the mystery surrounding a story of demonology, horror, and the occult.

In this Lovecraft-inspired narrative horror adventure you must use all your strength to withstand the ochre of madness, in order to sort through the many mysteries and puzzles within. Will you save the rest of the world or will you serve them up to the evil that dwells in the space between the stars?

There is no turn order or traditional board, players receive an uncooperative wooden crate filled to the brim with relics, rune scrolls, star-charts, and mysterious notes that must be pieced together to decipher the mystery of the King in Yellow.

Buried by time and forgotten by history, the greatest treasures of an ancient civilization finally await discovery: riches beyond wealth and power beyond life. In this Egyptology inspired adventure game players work together to unravel the mystery of a lost tomb, following the documented search of a long-lost Egyptologist.

There is no turn order or traditional board - everything you need comes packed into an uncooperative wooden crate. The game comes packed with specially-crafted relics, diary entries, notes, and other pieces that will aid in the player's investigation. Players will work together to scour for clues, solve a range of puzzles, and piece together the mystery surrounding the forgotten tomb and the missing Egyptologist.

At the end, the player's will uncover the truth of the forgotten tomb.

-description from publisher

Newsworthy is a social game where players are investigative news hounds. Players seek articles on the internet from their smart phones, tablets, or PCs. The game can be played in person or remotely. The topics to investigate each round are guided by Tech Deck cards. Newsworthy is a blend of a conversational game, an investigative game, and a social game. A player voting mechanic allows articles to be rated to score points. It has a unique game mechanic because players seek information from outside of the game on the internet.

OBJECTIVE – Players/teams try to score points by finding news articles that meet the objectives set by Tech Deck cards.
GAME PLAY – Each round, a Tech Deck card is revealed which sets the topic. Players then search for articles based on the topic for that round. There is a voting mechanic where players can vote on the articles that have been found to score points.
SCORING AND WINNING - If playing in Blitz play mode, the game ends in one round. In normal mode, with more than 2 players, the game ends when a player scores 10 points; otherwise, it ends when a player has 5 points. If there are ties, the tied players share the victory. Players may decide to continue to play and continue the conversation! I have seen rounds go an entire hour! The point of the game is to foster interesting conversation after all.
THEME – Players are investigative reports for a news agency

—description from the designer

Players will join the search for Cadet Turner in this narrative puzzle adventure. You will dive into a world of 80s nostalgia and classic science fiction, searching through letters, comics, and digital content to find clues and puzzles that you'll need to solve in order to solve the mystery surrounding the disappearance of Cadet Turner.

This experience uses a free companion app (available for both iOS and Android) and website that allows players to interact with the physical contents of the experience.

Players work cooperatively to piece together the clues and evidence scattered throughout physical props, documents, and digital content in order to solve this mystery.

boardgame: The Lost Treasure of John Augur
description:
Prepare for an adventure on the high seas, from the glory days of piracy in the 18th century to present day. Can you help the intrepid Kelsey Franklin piece together one of the ocean's deepest mysteries?

The Lost Treasure of John Augur is a narrative puzzle game experience where players will collaboratively search through a trove of artefacts, treasures, official documents, diary entries, pirate maps, and other windblown scraps to seek out the lost treasure of pirate John Augur.

There is no traditional game board or pieces, no turn order or set roles. Instead players will unbox the mystery together, scouring for clues, searching for hidden messages, and ultimately following the treasure trail hidden within the contents.

Will you find the lost treasure or be doomed by the unfathomable powers beneath the waves?

boardgame: Post Mortem London Gothic: The Ghost in the Machine
description:
Prepare to explore Victorian-Era London in this choose-you-own-adventure style murder mystery. You will scour clues, interview suspects, and follow the trail of tragedy and woe that surrounds a deadly cotton mill in the midst of the industrial revolution.

In this murder-mystery game you are the detective. Players will work collaboratively to choose which leads to follow and what paths to take. With a dossier of police reports, coroner inquests, and various pieces of physical evidence players will work to unravel the mystery of the Ghost in the Machine, following prompts in the two penny blood novels included with the game that contain the narrative pieces of the story.

When your investigation comes to a close, check the Case Memorandum for the resolution and see how well you made out playing detective.
Argentinian war game from 1950's.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Old Argentinian economic game.
Verses is a party game in which players play song cards to match moment cards, hoping that their song is voted best in that round.

A 1950's Argentinian wargame.
2-player adaptation of Aernaval.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Does your sign know you better than your friends do?

Sun Moon Rising comes with 300 double-sided cards, with 50 statements tailored to each zodiac sign, and each round of the game you vote on whether statements are true or false about your friends and attempt to match what they'll say about themselves — with them guessing what you'll say about yourself, of course.

Choose to play with your sun, moon, or rising sign deck to test your friends on three different sides of your personality. Your sun sign deck is a great go-to for anyone, your moon sign deck works best with close friends and family, and your rising sign deck is a good pick for casual acquaintances.

2 game modes: "Circuit Race" and "Sightseeing Trip".

Players start in Buenos Aires. In "Circuit Race" mode, everyone must ride the main routes (marked on the map). In the "Tourism Trip" mode (to the North or to the South), the cities to be covered by each player are distributed equally, and each player decides the route according to his goals.

Players will roll the dice on their turn and advance along the routes, touring cities and picking up "luck" cards. The winner will be the one who visits the objective cities and routes, and returns first to the Capital Federal.

boardgame: Women in Sports Special Edition Trivia Pack
description:
This Women in Sports Special Edition Trivia Pack celebrates the achievements of over five generations of athletes.

100 trivia questions. Strategy needed to win. Hints by comedians. This Special Edition set is playable on its own, but for the best experience, shuffle the cards into the core GENSMAK! game.

All profits donated to the Women’s Sport Foundation.

—description from the publisher

In the hellish close confines of the space hulk Gallowdark, elite kill teams stalk each other through haunted corridors. Millenia ago, an Imperial Inquisitor and a regiment of loyal Cadian Kasrkin recovered an ancient xenos artefact, but their ship was lost in the warp and fused with the colossal floating mass of Gallowdark. Only a few years have passed for the Kasrkin survivors trapped within its time-bending mass, though thousands have gone by in the galaxy outside. Now, the Necron owners of that relic have awoken from eons of slumber – and Technomancer Hamanet the Relentless of the Tsarakura Dynasty will stop at nothing to reclaim his lost creation.

Kill Team: Shadowvaults is a full expansion box for Warhammer 40,000: Kill Team, the ultimate sci-fi skirmish game. It includes miniatures for two complete new Kill Teams – the hard-bitten elite Kasrkin shock troops of Cadia, and the newly-awoken Necrons of the Hierotek Circle. They’re packed together with loads of space hulk terrain, which is fully compatible with the set found in Kill Team: Into the Dark, along with transfers, a double-sided game board, and a rules manual including missions, background material exploring the mysterious setting, and full rosters for the two kill teams.

—description from the publisher

The Amapatoliztli Tonalpohualli is a deck of cards based on the Nahuatl ritual calendar or Tonalpohuall.

The game presents different game modes, you may play by numbers, symbols, colors, or elements.
Then, you have different rules according to what variation of the game you want to play.
One of the most common ways to win is by playing all of the cards in your hand before anybody else.

In a form of free download - 7 Scary Objective cards to be dealt at random at the start of the game, one for each bandit in play. Unless otherwise stated, your Scary Objective remains hidden until the end of the game.

You are a contractor, submitting estimates for jobs and trying to get the best customers.

The savviest homeowners know to accept the second-highest estimate. Too high, and they worry you’re spending all that extra profit on a rhinestone hammer. Too low, and they worry you’re cutting corners on materials. Costs will change, so be ready to change with them. After the dust settles, be the most successful company to win.

Hammer Glamour is a card game for 4 to 6 people where everyone simultaneously plays a contractor card from their hand. The customer cards that players receive will be based on the strength (profitability) of their contractor cards.

The player who played the second strongest contractor card will take the most valuable customer card, and so on in order. The player who played the strongest card (tried to get the most profit) will take the last customer. That customer didn't really want to pay so much, but all of the other contractors are busy!

Materials and labor costs change, and what was profitable now won’t necessarily be profitable in the future. The player who played the weakest card may move the suit strength card of the suit that they played to any position within the card row to change its strength for the next round.

When all cards have been played, each contractor adds up the value of their customers. The most successful contractor wins.

To score in RoRop, you must collect wooden blocks.

The game consists of a wooden game board and 36 blocks, six each in six colors. To set up, set the game board up diagonally, then take one block of each color and place one at the bottom of each of the six columns in the game board. Finally, add the other blocks in the columns, taking care not to place the same color orthogonally adjacent.

On a turn, a player chooses a color present on the bottom row of the game board, then takes one or more blocks of this color from the bottom of the board. Keep taking turns until all of the blocks have been claimed, then tally your score.

In the basic game, your score is the number of blocks you claimed. In the intermediate game add to this number the product of the number of different colors you claimed and the number of blocks in your most prevalent color. In the expert game, score the product of the number of different colors you claimed and the number of blocks in your first claimed color, then add the number of blocks you collected.

You can also use the RoRop components to play a four-in-a-row game. Starting with the empty game board, on a turn either (a) add a block to the top of any column or (b) remove a block from the bottom of any column, then place it on the top of any column. If a player takes the (b) action, the next player must take an (a) action, if possible. If you create a line of four blocks of the same color, whether orthogonally or diagonally, you win.

"Tactic" is a strategy game. Create your battlefield, joining the various panels, place your starting base, shuffle the cards, roll the dice and then start moving your pieces until you are within a certain distance from the enemy, and then shoot! Each ship or tank (and more) is a point. Whoever hits the most wins.

boardgame: ならべてカンブリア (Line Up Cambria)
description:
Let's travel back 541 million years ago to the Cambrian sea where a variety of new organisms were born. In fact, let's try our hand at making those organisms prosper!

In ならべてカンブリア (Line Up Cambria), you build a miniature version of the Cambrian sea out of eight cards, trying to score as many points as possible. To set up, shuffle the landscape cards and scoring cards face down, then lay them out in four piles. Choose a first player.

In a round, starting with the first player, each player chooses a pile of cards not in front of themselves, selects a card from it, then adds it to the left or right of all cards they already have in play. Landscape cards are placed horizontally and have two elements on them; scoring cards have one element on them and one of three types of scoring conditions, and they are placed a line vertically (so the scoring condition is present below their line of cards).

Pass the starting marker, then start a new round. After eight rounds, tally your points based on the scoring cards you've collected and how well you've met those conditions. The player with the highest score wins.

This small pack contains all nine promotional cards from the "Wave One" release of the game To Honor Grandfather.

System Cards:

     Mortrog
     Polybius
     Yuggoth


Encounter Cards:

     Lost City of Squindor
     The Sum of All Evils
     Droyne Coyns
     Space Station VII
     Dark Dreamscapes
     Vampire Ship


Note: All included cards were previously released as individual promos, either through crowdfunding or at conventions.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Climate Change 2050: Who can save the most?
description:
The world's four leading nations are facing climate change and resource scarcity. Their goal is to build trade routes to get food and water.

But as they continue to emit CO2, climate change advances and the temperature rises. The destruction of the world progresses little by little and the affected people flee to the nations.

Technologies must be built to transform to green energy, food crops improved, and desalination plants developed. At the same time, energy supplies must not collapse.

The nations pass through five ages in which the situation becomes more and more precarious. In the end, the nation that destroys nature the least and saves the most people wins.

Climate Change: Who can save the most is a strategic resource management & area control game that implements the coming reality on the table at home.

Yer all pirates, down to y’r very core…

Ye laugh together, drink together, fight yer foes together…

Merchants and Monsters alike fear yer mighty crew. Yer ship has ruled the open seas for more ‘n a decade.

And as captain, it’s up to you to load yer vessel with the shiniest gold, the deadliest cannons, the most despicable crew, and enough vittles and rum to keep ‘em from plotting a mutiny.

Game play:

From the designer of the medieval card game Of Knights and Ninjas comes There Once was a Ship, a 20 minute roll and write game for 1-6 skallywags.


     Choose a group of dice (various colors represent gold, weapons, infamy, crew, and supplies).
     Record yer scores on a simple half page print out.
     Reroll yer dice, organizing them back into groups based on their number. 


Will ye stash gold, stockpile weapons, or recruit an infamous crew?

After five short rounds, tally up yer score. Best out o’ three wins. If ye ave any pirate blood in ye, ye’ll play again. And again. And again!

Till every last fool is a feared of so much as whisperin yer name.

Baltic Offensive: Fall 1944 is a two player simulation of the Soviet offensive in the Baltic countries in the Fall of 1944. The Soviet player must conquer as much territory as possible within a limited time frame, while the German forces must try to delay the Soviet advance as much as possible. The Soviet player has a positional advantage and numerical superiority, but it has a tight timetable to clear the Baltic countries so that the Red Army can return to the decisive direction of the war: the Vistula-Oder-Berlin axis. Each game turn represents 10 days. Each hexagon is 10 miles (15 kilometers) from side to opposite side. Units are corps, divisions, brigades or equivalents, and specialized smaller units.

22 x 34-inch game map 176 5/8-inch counters.

World at War 93, October 2023

-description from designer

description:
Kesselring’s War: Decision in Italy 1943-44 is an operational level two player wargame covering the campaign in Sicily and southern Italy, July 1943 to February 1944. The game covers the months from Operation Husky through the initial landings in the southern peninsula up to the Anzio invasion and first battle of Monte Cassino. This was the time when the Allies could have gained a decisive victory but were stopped when German forces under the command of Field Marshal Albert Kesselring fought a series of delaying actions and then held a line south of Rome. The game system portrays operations across the theater within a framework of logistical and command/control restraints, modeled as Action Points. The objective of the Allied player is to seize major objectives in Italy, while the Germans are trying to stop them or, by judicious counterattacks, turn the tide in the Mediterranean Theater of Operations.

Each hex on the map is approximately 30 kilometers across. Each turn of play represents a month of operations. Ground units represent mostly divisions, independent brigades, and regiments. Air units represent the equivalent of a Luftwaffe Geschwader, USAAF wing or RAF group (9 to 15 squadrons). Naval units represent task forces.

22 x 34-inch game map 176 5/8-inch counters.

World at War #94, December 2023

description:
An ancient papyrus waiting to be restored has arrived at the largest Egyptian museum in the world. Who among you researchers will add the most fragments for its reconstruction?

In Papyrus, you play cards with the correct hieroglyphs, take as many fragments as possible, and complete your secret dossiers to score bonus points.

Ranking is a party game for the whole family. Each player must order the 5 options creating their own Ranking, trying to guess the actual order. To do this, players use 5 cards that shares icons with options.

This self-playing sequel is based on the film and series. It can also be mixed with the original game.

—description from the publisher

Ranking es un party game para toda la familia. Cada jugador deberá ordenar las 5 opciones creando su propio Ranking, tratando de acertar el orden real. Para ello, los jugadores usarán 5 cartas que comparten iconos con las opciones.

Esta secuela autojugable se basa en el cine y las series. Además se puede mezclar con el juego original.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

boardgame: Lizzie Borden's Ghost: The Board Game
description:
Fall River, Mass. 1927: Elizabeth “Lizzie” Andrew Borden, the main suspect in the August 4, 1892, ax murders of her father and stepmother, has died of natural causes. She has left a faded letter to the four people who can finally solve the crime. One player takes the role of Lizzie Borden. The remaining take the roles of City Marshal, Judge, Neighbor, and News Reporter, called the “Seekers.” The Seekers move room to room while picking up 19th century equipment used for paranormal exploring. They attempt to capture Lizzie’s ghost as it travels the scene of the crime: the Borden home.

The game board is a map of the first and second floors, back and front yards of the Borden home, Massachusetts, circa late 1800s. Each room and noted areas is a “Hot Spot,” or haunting.
Seekers gather equipment for capturing Lizzie’s ghost moving room to room only by using marked doorways. Lizzie moves in secret using the "Hot Spot" location cards. By various means, the Seekers attempt to deduce and uncover Lizzie’s location.
A roll of the dice, a turn of a card, a decision to "seek" or "hover"- will a Seeker catch Lizzie's ghost and force her to admit her guilt? Or will she escape by haunting five hot spots without being found?

Adult themes of criminal activity, gore, paranormal
Mystery, roll ‘n’ play, selection
This game was created by an all-female team of talented writers, artists, and designers from all over the world. The game includes trivia, facts, and history of the case and the 19th century.

—description from the designer
publishers: Best True Crime Books, Games & Video LLC

description:
The snow has amply covered the mountains of the ski area ‘The 4 VALLEYS’ and the resort is about to welcome the first skiers! This season promises to be amazing.

Your goal: to become the champion of the 4 VALLEYS. To do this, you must have collected the highest number of victory points after  completing challenges in the ski area. The game ends when a player has exceeded the level ‘Étoile d’Or’ (Gold Star). The winner is the 
skier who has won the most points.

-description from developer

boardgame: Back O' Beyond: Tales of Blood & Salt
description:
Deep within the vast blue and grey of the sea and the endless storm lies a long-forgotten land. Stories tell of a safe passage within the roaring belly of the ocean. Legends tell that hiding behind rocks, clouds, and the unforgiving force of the sea lies an island, an island that pirates call home, a place where dreaded captains spend their downtime trading in the marketplace, drinking spiced rum in the tavern, testing their reputation at the Council Hall, or mingling in mischief in the Back Alley.

Seek infamy and become the most dreaded pirate captain! Create a powerful ship by carefully selecting and managing your crew, do battle and plunder ships full of loot, and make a fortune that no living man has ever made! And when you're tired, come back to the only land you feel is safe for you to rest and get ready for your next adventure. What treasures will you find? How many ships will you capture? Will your crew look up to you or start a mutiny? Will the sound of your name bring fear? There’s only one way to find out...

Back O' Beyond is a highly thematic mid-weight 2-4 player euro game that takes around 30 minutes per player to complete. In the game, you take the role of a pirate captain, and your goal is to become the most renowned pirate, that is, the one with the most prestige points.

The game is played over five rounds, and each round consists of three phases. To start, new contracts that pirates compete over are revealed, the tavern is filled with potential crew, ships that can be captured arrive, and players repair or upgrade their ships...and possibly sabotage others. Next, by using a dice-based combat system, players attempt to plunder trading ships to gather precious loot and raise their dread, morale, reputation, and (most importantly) infamy. To end the round, players decide who to keep or dismiss from their crew through the power of food, morale, and rum.

Take on the role of builders to design the most beautiful town, but be careful: you must never lose sight of your opponent's plans! A fun and addictive game to train basic cognitive functions, indispensable for the development of soft skills.
Each turn consists of three steps: Purchase one of the 4 tiles in the centre of the table by paying the cost in gems, Place the tile on your lawn board, Reveal a new tile. The game ends when all the tiles are finished. Points are counted according to how you have placed the tiles, combinations of buildings and other urban elements that earn victory points in different ways.

Masu Math is a card game where players will use the cards from their hand to add or subtract to reach certain numbers. Specifically, the number a player needs to reach is the number of cards previously played in the round.

That is, if there are 11 cards in play at the start of a player's turn, they need to play cards which they can combine using addition or subtraction to reach 11; if they play 3 cards to do so, the next player will need to play cards which can reach 14.

Players are eliminated from a round when they cannot play, and the person who is the last to be eliminated earns the most points.  The game is played over a series of rounds and the player with the most points wins.

A twist on the traditional word-smithing genre, HEXX is a game about creating, adding, and expanding on words in a variety of directions (up, down, diagonally / forward and backwards), and then adding or expanding onto those originally played words to flip the most tiles to your color.

The player with the most tiles of their color on the board at the end of the game wins.

—description from the publisher

D!C!ASSETTE, a stylized display of the Italian for 17 (diciassette), is a bluffing card game in which players try to time when a set of cards on the table sum to 17 or more.

The deck consists of cards numbered 1 to 5 and cards with special effects, such as reversing the direction of play. Some of the numbered cards show their number on both the front and back of the card.

Each player has a hand of three cards. On their turn, they choose to play a card face up or face down to a row on the table, then draw a replacement card. Other players can then state "Diciassette!" if they think the cards in the row sum to 17 or more. If they are correct, they earn a daisy chip and the last person to place a card in the row takes a ghost chip; if not, the ghost chip and daisy chip are handed out the other way.

Players lose one daisy chip for every two ghost chips they obtain, and the first person to collect three daisy chips wins.

A trick-taking card game in which each suit is allocated a specific role to play (trump, penalty, reverse strength and plain). Must-follow trick-taking rules apply and Aces are normally the highest card in a suit. Try to win as many tricks as possible, but as few penalty cards as possible. The game ends after either four rounds, or when someone reaches 250 points. The game is best at 4 players, but also includes variations for 2, 3, and 5 player counts.

SCORING:

Scoring occurs at the end of each 13-trick round. You score 10 points per trick divided by the number of penalty cards you took, rounding down to the nearest whole number. If you took no penalty cards, instead of dividing you add a bonus of 10 points. For taking no tricks, you score a flat 25 points.

The fourth player cannot get the 10 point bonus, and instead chooses one of the three scoring "privileges" or conditions under which to play:
        Immunity - You will score a flat 5 points per trick, regardless of any penalty cards they contain.
        Warranty - You will score a flat 25 points regardless of how many tricks or penalty cards you win.
        Poverty - Instead of scoring for the number of tricks you win, you will score for the number you win less than five - in other words, 1 trick counts as 4, 2 as 3, and so on

Age of Will is a minimalist drafting and building card game.

The King has ordered the construction of walls and buildings, and wants them to be beautiful!

Over the course of two rounds of drafting, players draft cards to gather resources, befor contributing those resources to a building, and its decoration. Players get points for contributing resources and decorations, but lose points for wasted resources.

This latest in the "Age of" series from designer N2 was designed with the 2022 Tokyo Game Market design contraints in mind: 32 cards or less, 5 minutes or less rules explanation, 15 minutes or less playtime, and no cooperative games..
publishers: Southern Cross Games (サザンクロスゲームズ)

description:
Each of the players has two objectives to win.

The first objective is to get to the opponent's base.

Then go find the talisman, and bring it back to your own base.

All of this is accomplished by rolling the dice. On each turn you can choose to create paths to move along or simply block your opponent.

boardgame: The Aragón Front: The Spanish Civil War in Eastern Spain 1936/39
description:
The Aragón Front is an operational simulation depicting the successive campaigns along the Ebro river valley, in Eastern Spain, during her last bloody Civil War (1936/39).

The game is a two player, chit pulling and hex movement design; counters range from regiment/brigade to division and hexes span 10km (6 miles) from side to side.

The game can be played as a whole campaign or in smaller limited scenarios; these include:

     Durruti´s drive on Zaragoza.
     Belchite.
     Teruel.
     Franco´s drive to the Mediterranean.
     Valencia.
     The Ebro.
     The invasión of Catalonia.

description:
Rescue is a game of collecting equipment and survivors to overcome obstacles and being crowned as the winner. Players will take on the role of a wandering wastelander attempting to survive post-apocalyptic earth. Players must use equipment and action cards to overcome various obstacles and threats. In the process, they will enhance themselves and the survivors they rescue with loot, uncover lore, and stab their fellow players in the back.

This game is played in a series of 10 rounds consisting of two phases. The first phase of the round is the player action phase.

Phase 1: Action Phase
During this round, players may take the following actions. Steal a card from another player (excluding a survivor card), apply a piece of equipment to a survivor, or draw from the cache deck.

Steal a Card: When stealing a card, the individual having the card stolen will shuffle the fail card and the desired card together. The thief will then pick from one of the two cards. The card is not stolen if the fail card is selected from the two.

Apply Survivor Equipment: A player may apply spare equipment cards to any survivors they may have acquired. If the survivor is lost due to failing an event, the equipment applied to the survivor is also lost.

Draw a Card: If a player selects this option, draw one card from the cache deck.

After performing one of the above described actions, the player may then apply or remove equipment cards to themselves by placing them in front of themselves.

Finally, the first phase ends with players ensuring they have no more than five cards in their hands (unless they have a backpack applied). To accomplish this, they may discard any additional cards and place them in the discard pile or give spare cards to any other player.

Phase 2: Event Phase
During this phase, all players will be presented with an event. If they defeat the obstacle, they gain a prize as annotated on the card. If a player cannot defeat an obstacle, they must perform the loss action. Each player does not get an individual event. One event card is drawn, and all players must overcome that same event. If a player has zero survivors and the loss condition is to discard a survivor card, that player is eliminated from the game.

To defeat an obstacle, the players must have a stat number equal to or greater than the stat/stats being challenged. There are four stats that could be challenged, and those are Strength (STR), Accuracy (ACC), Intelligence (INT), and Stamina (STA). Each piece of equipment has stat/stats. Add the stat/stats required for the challenge to determine if you will defeat it.
Note: Equipment cards can not be equipped or unequipped during this phase.

Winning: Have the most survivor cards at the end of the ten rounds.

-description from designer

In Mikey Wonder Land, the players are running a theme park and need to make sure that the park's mascots do not appear in more than one area at once, or it will ruin the children's dreams that the characters are real!

The game begins at level 1, and there are 2 characters the children are looking for.  Each player simultaneously reveals a character from their hand.  If more than one player reveals one of the target characters, they receive failure chips.  This process of revealing and checking for failure is repeated until the players are out of cards.

At the end of a round, if one player has at least 4 failure chips, the game is over and the player with the least failure chips wins.

Otherwise, increase the level and play another round.  If the players make it to the end of level 6, they all win.

With the possibility of doing different tricks, decide which teams deserve to go to the next round and put together the matches trying to get the most results right.

Contains: Special game block + manual

Block Party is an expansion pack for Not So Neighborly. With this pack, your goal is to be the first to complete building a neighborhood by collecting a total of 14 build points. There are many new Build card additions, like food trucks and moon bounce. Have even more fun with cards, such as Firework "Accident," Dumpster Dive, and more! You need the base game, Not So Neighborly, in order to play.

-description from publisher

Nakayoshi Furenzua is a trick-taking game where players typically want to avoid taking tricks. It is a must follow game, and the winner of a trick will collect any cards played to the trick which did not follow suit.  Later, the ranks of these cards will be worth points - which players do not want to earn.

There are special rules around the cards with ranks 3/6/9/12. Specifically, these are the "good friend" cards. When a player plays one of these cards, they also will play a second card. The second card must have a different color and lower rank than the friend.

The player with the fewest points when the game ends is the winner.

Get ready for a fluffy, explosion of a good time. In fact, most players don't even sit down! This fast-paced card game is easy to pick up, but hard to put down. There are 5 different card types. Place bombs on your opponents fluffs, and fix your fluffs with med kits. Keep the bomb gloves you find to protect your fingertips and look out for the flip card that can extend the round. Have the most fluff's by playing your cards faster than everyone else to win. Oh... and don't go boom.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Not That Movie!: Carte Promo Serie TV
description: Promo cards deck with TV Series titles.

Oh What a Night is a casual co-operative party game where two teams work together to piece together what one of the teams did last night (along with where they did it and with whom!) Decks of cards randomly determine what the team did last night.

Each round, the team of guessers will receive a set of clues from the other team. The clues will get more specific and easier to decipher as the round progresses, and players get more points for figuring out the answers early.

The first clue will be a three-word hashtag, the second clue will be a four-word text message (with no vowels), and the third clue will be five-word newspaper headline- sensational and vague.

Coropenquest is a roll-and-write game where the area you can mark in will depend upon physically rolling the pencil onto the gameboard and checking the space above which your color points.

The game uses a 2-sided pencil, with each player taking on the color of a different end.  The center of the pencil is also used as a die, determining how many warriors or wizards a player will add to the map on their turn.

The game comes with 2 methods of play, short and long.  In the short scenario, the first player to capture 3 forts wins.  In the long scenario, the first player to 10 stars wins - which are achieved in various ways, such as killing sets of monsters.

Doozy Dice is a quick dice rolling game where players use 5 dice to match unique “doozy” combinations. At the start of the game, the 26 doozy cards are shuffled and 5 are dealt in the center of the table. These are the 5 doozy combinations each player will need to complete during this game. Players take turns rolling the dice and making combinations in as few rolls as possible. Two of the dice come with a doozy face on one side. These are either needed to complete a combination or act as a wild.

Players also collect doozy dots along the way either when anyone rolls a doozy face, or by completing combinations in 3 or fewer rolls. Use Doozy dots to change a roll and complete a combination. Any remaining doozy dots are subtracted from your roll total at the end of the game. The player who completes the 5 “doozy” combinations in the fewest number of rolls wins.

During Doozy Dice, the third roll is a key decision point for each player. If you complete a combination in 1-3 rolls, you get a bonus, 1 doozy dot, while all their opponents lose a doozy dot. Players need to decide: “Do I use the doozy dots I have to get this combination in 3 rolls, or save them for later?” To keep the game moving right along, there is a 13 roll limit. If a player rolls to 13, they note 13 rolls for that combination, and lose a doozy dot.

-description from publisher

Once upon a time, a fisherman who lived in a simple hut on the shore of a lake caught a goldfish who fulfilled his greatest wishes. It is said that the goldfish has been seen again, swimming near the shore.
So go to the lake and search for the goldfish! Perhaps the goldfish will make your wishes come true, too!

HOW TO PLAY

     Roll the dice and move your pawn by the resulting value.
     If you catch the goldfish, select a tile and see whether it fulfills one of your wishes.
     When the goldfish fulfills all your wishes - you win.


—description from the publisher

Pewien rybak, żyjący w skromnej chacie nad brzegiem jeziora, wyłowił kiedyś złotą rybkę, która spełniła jego
największe marzenia. Plotka głosi, że złotą rybkę znów zauważono pływającą niedaleko brzegu.
Chwytaj za wędkę i wyruszaj na połów. Może złota rybka spełni również Twoje życzenia!

JAK GRAĆ?

     Rzuć kostką i przesuń pionek o wskazaną na oczkach wartość.
     Jeśli wyłowisz złotą rybkę, dobierz kafelek i sprawdź, czy spełni ona jedno z Twoich życzeń.
     Gdy złota rybka spełni wszystkie Twoje życzenia – wygrywasz.


—description from the publisher (translated)

description:
Legends say that at the end of every rainbow lays a pot of gold guarded by the mischievous mythical creature, the Leprechaun. In Dice Treasure players must carefully match the colors and values of their cards to invoke the seven colors of the rainbow and steal the Leprechaun’s treasure.

Over multiple rounds, players take turns rolling dice from the bag and placing them on their cards looking to fill the patterns and collect gold coins. As the turns (composing a round) progress, the dice count decreases by one die and when the second-to-last turn starts, players incomplete cards will be available for opponents to fill and collect too and dice from the reserve and the common pool will become very useful.

At the end of the game, players add the gold collected from their pattern cards plus the color tokens and public objective cards earned to find out who is the best treasure hunter.
family: Components: Dice Cup / Dice Tower / Dice Tray

description:
Challenge the strongest national teams in the world of Holly e Benji (aka Captain Tsubasa)
All the best players of the Japanese league come together to challenge the great world national teams: Italy, Germany, France and Argentina.

Choose a team and put your players on the pitch.
Use the special abilities of the characters to be able to move and shoot.
Always keep your defenses high and be alert to make a quick retreat, the opposing coach will certainly not miss the opportunity to be able to move quietly towards your goal.
Unmark your players and advance towards the opponent's goal, being careful not to discover yourself too much.
Play an exciting tournament and, by choosing winning strategies, allow your players to score or defend the result.
Show your opponent the validity of the game tactics you set up and lead your team to victory.

—user summary

Lodestones is an abstract strategy game for two players in which participants compete to achieve the highest score by pushing pieces out of play, into the boards outer rim. Players move along the board according to the interaction between surrounding pieces.

—description from the designer

Au Contact is a miniature ruleset for modern day combat, using 15mm or 28mm miniatures primarily. It depicts the harsh realties of modern warfare at the skirmish level.

The rules are of medium-complexity, and can be adapted to solitaire play. There are two phases in one turn, an action phase (where platoons, squad and fireteams will shoot, move, use or repair equipments, etc), and management phase (where the players will heal their wounded, check morale, etc).

Troops are divided in different based on their preparation, and their willingness to fight. In today's war, stress level is an important component, which the game takes into account successfully. Night combat, fog of war, and civilian presence can also be added for more realism!

With 14 scenarios and 11 army lists (7 modern : American, British, French, Russian, different types of insurgents, and 4 Vietnam war lists), players have a wide variety of themes they can play with.

The players take turns one after the other.
Each player moves his pawn round the board, from hex to hex as per the die result.
Each hex describes an event and its result. You either gain or loose money.
Each player uses his money to buy plastic pieces that simulate oil facilities and place them on the 6 slots of his property which is drawn on the board. Those pieces are oil wells (2 piecess - slots), gas tanks (2 pieces - slots) and oil tanks (one small and one big, one slot each).
The player who accomplishes this first, wins the game.

–user summary

description:
In the early 1990’s a Russian antarctic expedition began drilling through the ice to a fresh water Lake dubbed Lake Vostok.

On Feb 2012 they succeeded in drilling more than 2,000 miles. It took more than 30 years.

Many raised concerns of the risks of breaking a barrier to a lake sealed off between 15 and 34 million years. But someone made the call that breaking this barrier was worth the risk.

Because the quantity of oxygen in Lake Vostok exceeds anywhere on our planet by 10-20X. Scientists predicted they would find new species that evolved far differently than any seen before.

After the initial research was completed a conspiracy theory propagated online. One of the Russian scientists defected. He claimed a highly intelligent creature, a new organism, engaged with them and killed some team members. This is where our Vostok story begins.

After a distress signal was received an unprecedented storm cut all contact with Vostok Station. As the storm raged on without any signs of letting up, multiple recon teams were sent. Your team is the only one to make it. After crashing 10 clicks away, you arrive at the aftermath of a massacre. Early into the reconnaissance you realize the story of the station goes much deeper.

The game is based on a First Strike Combat System. Players must battle physically and meta physically. Players also learn to control insanity and weaponize it.

An AI is provided to assist your team to off-set the intelligence of the new organism. The AI assists in many ways including scientific, weapon and ammunition recipes.

There are several player characters to choose from with varying capabilities. Players are free to assemble the kind of team they prefer.

Your team must fully explore the Russian station, re-establish communications, report back on the status and insure the chamber to the lake is sealed. Your team wins if they can make it the chamber and confirm it is sealed.

—description from the publisher

John Carter, a civil war veteran, unexpectedly found a gateway to Mars and became an unwitting space explorer and hero.

You live in 2089, the year a distress signal from deep space revealed a shocking surprise - that same John Carter is still alive. After finding another gateway, this one through both time and space, he has found an unknown world and sends for help. Your quest is to find him and bring him home. To do this you will have to improve your ship, bring on the right crew, and succeed in a series of missions that will lead you to fulfill your ultimate goal - find John Carter!

At the start of each round, each player draws 5 cubes from a draw bag.  This is the player's crew. They may consist of unskilled workers, merchants, officers, mechanics, smugglers, navigators, and gunners. Each player then places a player token on one of several spaces on the board.  They may go to the academy to improve a crew member's skill (gain new cubes) or recruit a specialist, to the market to get fuel, crystals, or money. These items are used to fulfil missions and trade in the black market. They may go to the cantina to gain a smuggler--they could take a smuggler to the black market to gain any good. They may go to the shipyard to improve their space craft. Or they may choose to fulfil a mission.

The goal of the game is to gain the most points from their ship, full sets of crew members, sets of crystals, sets of specialists, and missions.  Each mission is in a mission row and the player to have the most completed missions in a row also gets a bonus. Each mission also has been assigned by an agency. Gaining multiple agency mission tiles also gives a bonus. The game ends when John Carter is found.  All players will finish the current round and the game ends.

—description from the designer

description:
Deal 25 cards face down Opening layoutin a 5x5 square, then turn the next card face up and place it at the centre, as illustrated. (Here it happens to be diamondJ.) Use the remainder to deal a hand of 13 cards to each player.

To add cards to the grid in a spiral direction in such a way as to form and score for combinations based on those of Cribbage, namely pairs, prials (three of a kind), double pair royals (four of a kind), sequences of three or more, and (with some restrictions) flushes of three or more. There is no score for fifteens or thirty-one, unless you really want to make it complicated. With scores being made as you go along, it may help to use a Cribbage board.

Each plays in turn, starting with the non-dealer, who plays a card face up to the right of the starter. The next card must be placed immediately below this, the next to its left, and so on, until the spiral is complete at top right, leaving you each with one card unused. (The purpose of this is to give you a choice of two on your last turn.)

The rules are available on Parlett's website here: https://www.parlettgames.uk/oricards/snailspace.html

It's the UNO matching card game everyone knows and loves but featuring graphics from the video game Sonic the Hedgehog. Players still race to get rid of all their cards by matching color, number and symbol, but when someone plays a Wild Victory Lap Card, all other players must draw one card! And when a player is down to one card, they can't forget to shout, "UNO!"
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release
