description:
"Quando un tuo BANG! viene Mancato!, pesca quel Mancato!; quando manchi un BANG!, pesca quel BANG!"
"Each time your BANG! is Missed!, draw that Missed! card. When you miss a BANG!, draw that BANG! card."

A promotional card released for the 20th anniversary of BANG!. The illustration depicts the author Emiliano Sciarra.

Originally distributed during the Lucca Comics & Games 2022, the card is now included in the BANG! Dynamite Box.

Decisive Action is a game of tactical level warfare between modern American and Russian forces. Players are in the role of the battalion commander and must decide how to order subordinate units to fire and maneuver, how to allocate assets like helicopters and drones, and how to choose the best battle plan each turn via selection of activation cards.

The game is based on the US Army Combined Arms Battalion (CAB) conducting traditional high intensity force-on-force missions against variable-sized Russian forces, which include a reinforced Motor Rifle Company, a Battalion Tactical Group (BTG), or a Composite Brigade.

—description from the publisher

Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A1 is an easy/medium difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Free Mission A1

Lost!

We are lost. We had lots of zombies behind us,
ammunitions were running low... We panicked, and
here is the result. We have no idea where we are.
Neither do the zombies running after us, but it's not
like we can’t find zombies anywhere.
We’d better calm down. Then we will explore the
area to find food, weapons and a map. With method
and a little luck, all is going to be fine.

boardgame: Night Parade of a Hundred Yokai: Cursed Throne Expansion
description:
It has been 200 years since the king disappearance. After King Enma vanished, the yokai clans stormed the streets parading in the night for souls. Many tried to rule over us, but their reign never lasted more than a couple decades. You see, the throne is cursed and it feasts on the ruler's power. and the weaker they get, the more our magic fades away. Soon the gates to the spirit realm will be forever closed, and our legend forgotten... Are you ready to lead our rebellion?

The Cursed Throne Expansion brings: a new type of action - Reign: that changes effect based on which clan sits on the Cursed Throne; Spirit as wooden tokens, the Champions, the Challenge board, and more yokai to join your parades!

—description from the designer

The improv party game! Players assume the role of Actors performing in improvised scenes that are (almost) completely unscripted.
Create hundreds of scenes on the fly on what's been described as "the improving actor's toolkit!"
Generate scenes by drawing a Relationship and Location card, and grab a secret Script card for each player. Actors that can use their script before their scene ends gain a victory point! The first to 5 victory points wins!

Woodland Craft is a competitive roll and write game of gathering and crafting. You will guide two animal friends around the Forest to gather Forest items in order to make crafts and score the most points before fall is over.

Woodland Craft is played in rounds with three phases per round.

     Roll Dice: At the beginning of each round, each pair of dice is rolled. If doubles are rolled for any of the pairs, all players fill in the next open space on their Fall Track.
     Move & Gather: Simultaneously, players will use any one of these pairs of dice to move one or both of their animal friends around the Forest to gather Forest items.
     Crafts & Shed: Player may use gathered Forest items to make crafts and/or place items in their Shed.


When one or more of the following conditions is met, finish the round and proceed to Final Scoring.

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Varamyr Sixskins
description:
Among the most powerful Skinchangers beyond the Wall, Varamyr Sixskins commands a huge snow bear, three wolves, a shadowcat, and an eagle simultaneously. In battle he rides astride his great snowbear, commanding his various beasts in coordinated attacks. Nothing escapes his eagle’s gaze, nor his shadowcat’s patient stalking. Any that approach his person face a savage mauling. One may defeat the individual creatures, but Varamyr will find more. To face an ordinary skinchanger is to square off against a skilled combatant with their creature’s strength backing them up. To face Sixskins, one faces an entire unit unto themselves.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Martell Starter Set
description:
House Martell rules the desert, plains, and badlands of southern Westeros under the Sunspear banner. Their troop are garbed in desert robes under light armor, and rank among the fastest forces in the War of the Five Kings. House Martell’s doctrine of battle is to strike fast, strike hard, and evade counterattack. Their Spearmen and Skirmishers form the bulk of their forces, while the elite Dune Vipers fill a flanking and pinning role. But nothing embodies the might of House Martell so much as its leadership. Prince Doran rules the great house from the Sunspear while Prince Oberyn leads from the front. Areo Hotah’s valor has seen many battles won by strength-of-will alone, and the political machinations of Ellaria Sand keep enemies guessing where and when House Martell will strike next.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Sunspear Royal Guard
description:
House Martell’s famous Sunspear Royal Guards are few in number but count among the best-trained units across all of Westeros. Their adaptive style lends itself well to both offensive and defensive initiatives, and while their armor is heavier than most Martell units, their physical conditioning allows them to keep pace. As a hard center to a Martell vanguard, the Royal Guards have no match in Martell forces, and their flexibility lets them seize the initiative on the field as the tide of battle shifts.

—description from the publisher

description:
Although somewhat removed from the court at King's Landing, the Sunspear of House Martell wields its influence like a patiently coiled viper. It waits, watches, chooses its moments, then strikes with sudden, shocking speed.

Although one of the deadliest warriors afoot, Prince Oberyn Martell's political machinations are not to be undervalued, nor those of his daughters, the infamous "Sand Snakes." Obara, Nymeria, Tyene, and Elia Sand manipulate their enemies both on and off the battlefield with their father's same lethal precision. And Prince Oberyn's loyal ally Harmen Uller is always ready to raise his blade in House Martell's service.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Sunspear Dervishes
description:
Sunspear Dervishes raise House Martell’s doctrine of high mobility to a fine art. Their training allows them to rapidly engage, strike hard, and just as rapidly disengage to either prep for another run or choose a more opportune target. Enemies so besieged are often so rattled by the spinning, whirling dance of blades that their own response is badly hampered. While Dervishes can hold in a melee for a time, they’re best kept on-the-move or working together with other Martell units to wear down a particularly determined foe.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

description:
Coba is a cooperative strategy game that will take players to a mystical temple in search of hidden treasure.

Each round, players will attempt to ascend the temple as a team by simultaneously playing their cards, and then resolving their actions in numerical order. Cards are not just used for actions and priorities, they also form the path that the players must traverse in ascending order to win. If that doesn't sound tricky enough, players also have limited communication as they confront the cursed spirits that guard the temple.

Through clever planning and anticipation of teammates' actions, you just might claim the treasure in this cooperative game for two to four players.

—description from the designer

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Sand Skirmishers
description:
House Martell’s Sand Skirmishers turn their doctrine of high mobility into a deadly dance of blade and bow. Their light armor allows them superior speed, rivaling that of the fastest enemy units, and their training allows for deadly hit-and-fade attacks, all under a rain of incoming arrows. If locked down in a protracted melee, Skirmishers can hold their own as well as any light unit, but a wise commander will free them as soon as possible, allowing their dance to begin anew. They work particularly well savaging enemy lines already engaged with Martell Spearmen.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Targaryen Heroes 3
description:
In far-off Essos, Queen Daenerys Targaryen rules over a court rife with intrigue. But while her subjects may involve themselves in plot after plot against each other (and indeed against the Queen herself), they stand united against their enemies in the field. With advisors and councilors such as Mirri Maz Duur, Hizdahr Zo Loraq, the scribe Missandei, and the mysterious Quaithe, the Dragon Queen sows confusion upon her enemies, while her commanders Marselen and Skahaz Mo Kandaq keep their troops in line, both by example and the whip’s bloody lash.

—description from the publisher

Animal rescuers gather objects and actions to rescue and release animals in need.  Each animal needs a different combination of cards to secure a positive rescue or release, with others unable to be released scoring negative points.  Each player has slightly different rules depending on their Meeple.  Vet cards throw a random element in typical of what is faced in animal rescue.

In addition, there are a range of map type score tracks your Meeple moves along, with bonus points to be picked up.

The game is asymmetrical in 2 ways - when each player rehomes their own animal type, they receive a different bonus.  Also, each animal player type moves differently -

- The dog player can take a shortcut through a dog agility tunnel
- The cat player can hop between adjacent cardboard boxes at no movement cost
- The rabbit player can move diagonally between obstacles as well as orthogonally
- The pigeon player can fly directly over and land on obstacles and fly over water

There will be 2 options for the board in the base game, additional Kickstart exclusive boards, and various expansions including a Horse Player (which can gallop on some moves), additional maps and different full versions of the game to come.  Be ready for a lot of twists on the theme and unexpected variants.

Game is designed by the CEO of Farplace Animal Rescue, an avid board gamer, and 100% of the profits will support animal rescue via Farplace.
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
Neo Grimorium is a card crafting game for 2-5 players. Assume the role of a mage as you build your book of spells. It's a race against time as you work diligently to transcribe more arcane knowledge than anyone else.

This fast-paced game uses elements of card crafting, worker placement, hand management, and a point salad style scoring. With over 16 denizen cards that change each game and over 40 clear spell cards, no two games will ever be the same. The game comes in a small form box for easy travel and storage.

Each turn, you play a page from your book and take the action listed on the card. You are given a scorecard that you must use to mark off adornments and focus your power as you transcribe spells and adorn your book you gain levels of the magical elements. But don't forget to increase your influence over the many denizens of the Citadel of Magic so they will lend you their powers.

Will you rise to the top to become the most powerful mage who ever lived?

—description from the designer

Scenario book for the "Bloody Big BATTLES!" wargames rules. The major states of India, especially the Maratha Confederacy and the Sikh state in the Punjab, were among the most redoubtable opponents of the extension of British power in the sub-continent. The size and technological sophistication of their forces posed major problems for East India Company commanders in the field and frequently threatened them with defeat. BBBI lets you refight sixteen of the most important battles of the period from the Second Maratha War (1803-1806) to the Sepoy Uprising (‘Indian Mutiny’) of 1857-1858.
This collection gives you:

     The battles where Wellington made his name!
     A wide range of terrain and tactical situations!
     Balanced scenarios with asymmetrical armies!
     Unusual troops – Gurkhas, camel rockets, ghazis!
     An engaging game of constant decision-making!
     Battles you can fight to the finish in an evening!


Many also offer scenario options to explore ‘what-ifs’ and increase replay value. Campaign options allow players to link battles so that each has consequences for the next.

—description from the publisher

description:
The Hungarian War of Independence was the largest and longest of the conflicts arising from Europe’s Year of Revolution in 1848. It lasted a year, saw half a million troops engaged, and generated dozens of significant engagements on three major fronts. Dedicated research now makes this neglected conflict accessible to wargamers. BBHB lets you refight sixteen of the most important battles of the war! Each scenario gives you:


     A clear and accurate historical situation
     New decisions for both sides every turn
     Simple but ingenious victory conditions
     A battle you can fight to the finish in an evening
     A great game that will ebb and flow to the end! 


Most also offer scenario options to explore ‘what-ifs’ and increase replay value. Campaign options allow players to link battles so that each has consequences for the next.

—description from the publisher

The Level Up - Expansion brings several new elements to your board, the most notable one being the solo mode.

We felt that if you played a solo mode, you don't want to play a reduced version of a cooperative game. You want to use all elements of the game - and since all expansions are compatible with one another, you would want to use all the material from all the expansions, right?

Inside Level Up you'll find the rules for the Solo mode. In this new game mode, you'll take on the night shift alone. But you'll get help not only from staff members, but also from automated HEROes that you either chose or select randomly before the night shift starts. These automated HEROes can counsel with you, treat patients on their own and hence add to the feeling of a real night shift.

In the expansion, you'll also find 4 brand new HEROes for you to play - in all available game modes for HE.R.O. These four have been on many adventures together and for some reason, they got thrown into a board game and have to face a night shift now.

There's also 15 new patient cards and 5 more diagnosis to add to your copy of HE.R.O - so you'll face even more different patients and have to adapt.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Ratcatcher: The Solo Adventure Game – Swag Rat Promo
description:
For a limited time, this peculiar rat hurriedly leaves the safety of its hidden treasure horde in search of not only magic cheese but anything shiny, unique or limited.

A ratcatcher who can snatch a loaded swag rat will find themselves flush with goodies. 
So don't miss the chance to catch him.

-description from designer

This is a may follow trick-taker that features a hex board that players will be interacting with to score points.  Every trick will result in each player claiming a space on the board depending on their order in the trick and the cards played.

The trick winner will also get a potential bonus reward, mixing paints, for winning a trick.  Primary color cards that are played in a trick can be mixed to create a secondary color.

The player that scores the most points from their board positioning and color majorities from tiles collected will win the game.

Immediate boarding to find the most eco-responsible traveler! It's a strategy game where you have to use the means of transport to go as far as possible while having a limited ecological impact. Once your destinations are open, you will multiply the travel experiences to score as many points as possible and gain the upper hand over other players. Luck will also be in the game: it's up to you to take up the eco-responsible challenges!
Laughter in perspective but also discovery... enough to make you want to go on a trip.

—description from the publisher (translation)

Embarquement immédiat pour dénicher le voyageur le plus éco-responsable ! C’est un jeu de stratégie où il faudra utiliser les moyens de transports pour aller le plus loin possible tout en ayant un impact écologique limité. Une fois vos destinations ouvertes, vous multiplierez les expériences de voyage pour marquer un maximum de points et prendre l’ascendant sur les autres joueurs. La chance sera également de la partie : à vous de relever les défis éco-responsables !
Du rire en perspective mais aussi de la découverte… de quoi vous donner envie de partir en voyage.

—description from the publisher (French)

A collection of self-contained small card mysteries, brain teasers, and mystery grams. The canister is filled with cards small enough fit in your pocket and ready to take anywhere anytime.

Players can tackle the visual puzzles solo or as teams. Each card is a self-contained mystery providing  isual and wordplay clues to solve individually.

Competitive players or teams can set a target number of cards to solve within a certain amount of time.
Enjoy America's Favorite Quiz Show with the kid-friendly Junior Edition card game. Each deck plays out like a full episode, complete with Jeopardy!, Double Jeopardy!, and Final Jeopardy! rounds.

boardgame: I've Got Bear Parts! Card Game
description:
Collect, attack, and defend your way to victory in this exciting game of bear battles! It takes four bear parts to complete each bear: Head, Torso, Arms, and Legs. Get three complete bears first and win the game.

But wait... it's not that simple. Your opponents will be vying for the same prize and will do anything in their bear power to stop you!

Use the power of all eight bear species to gain the ultimate edge with things like the strength of a "Grumpy" Grizzly Bear or magic of the spectacled "Wizard" Bear!

Who will be the first to complete a trio of bears?

Players will become supporters of famous people who have passed away this year, and campaign to make their funerals as wonderful as possible.

However, in order for them to be given state funerals, the consent of the people is required. Now is the time to question the pros and cons of state funerals!

Players vote secretly on which celebrities will move up the ladder to the top. When a celebrity gets to the top of the ladder, players vote on whether the celebrity deserves a state funeral. Players score according to the end rank of the celebrities they secretly chose.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Ghostlink Pumpkin Promo
description:
Beta Rules - 3 x new Pattern cards and 3 x new Pattern gear cards that were included with the Erza of Dedheim First Run Collector's Edition miniature, that is not part of the game.

Gear card sneak peek: Ghostlink Pumpkin (item, consumable)- Consume: Suffer frenzy and become doomed until end of round. Convert all your attribute tokens to +1 strength tokens. Limit, once per showdown.

Erza of Dedheim is packaged in an illustrated box that includes:

1 x Hard Plastic Painters scale Erza of Dedheim miniature
1 x Hard Plastic Players scale Erza of Dedheim miniature

3 x Pattern gear cards

Led by Grozharous, the Pale Turnip King. Dedheim was established in the ruins of a forgotten town. The monstrous lord claims that within Dedheim’s crystaline gates the dead are collected.
Erza of Dedheim guards the settlement's black tourmaline gates. She bars the denizens of Dedheim from leaving and spilling into the world of the living.

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Starfall Outriders
description:
House Martell rules the desert, plains, and badlands of southern Westeros under the Sunspear banner. Their troops are garbed in desert robes under light armor, and rank among the fastest forces in the War of the Five Kings. House Martell's doctrine of battle is to strike fast, strike hard, and evade counterattack.

The Starfall Outriders are House Martell's first cavalry unit and scheduled for release in 2023.
family: Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

boardgame: Australia (fan expansion for Age of Steam)
description:
A fan expansion for Age of Steam by Goodsound

This expansion brings the player to the most Eastern continent of the World.
Australia has a lot of desert and some high hills, so building railroad won't be cheap.
It also introduces a new special action called Subvention which can help to the weakest player.

boardgame: Hegemony: Lead Your Class to Victory – Crisis & Control
description:
You have successfully led your class to victory... but the battle of ideologies is far from over! The old world is in crisis, and the new world struggles to regain control. Now is the time to make a difference!

Crisis & Control offers new challenges and additional game modes that will test your governance skills like never before! Crisis Response cards and Alternative Events add more diversity to your games, class-specific Hidden Agendas incentivize new politico-economic strategies, and the Automas provided allow you to experience all of the game’s roles, no matter the player count!

—description from the rulebook

Crisis & Control contains all the gameplay material that was given in the form of stretch goals during Hegemony's Kickstarter campaign. All that content was included in the Extended Edition of Hegemony which is what the campaign's backers received.

description:
Players take on the role of families planning their grocery shopping and meals for the next seven days of the week. Each round, they buy groceries at the market, compose meals, and take care of the well-being of their family members. Players earn points for a properly balanced diet and good management of their own time and money. At the same time, they must remember to minimize the waste left over from preparing meals and to meet their family's different daily and weekly goals. The player with the most points after seven rounds wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Gracze wcielają się w rodziny planujące swoje zakupy spożywcze oraz posiłki przez kolejne siedem dni tygodnia. W każdej rundzie kupują artykuły spożywcze na targu, komponują posiłki i dbają o samopoczucie członków swojej rodziny. Za właściwie zbalansowaną dietę oraz dobre gospodarowanie własnym czasem i pieniędzmi gracze zdobywają punkty. Równocześnie muszą pamiętać o minimalizowaniu odpadków pozostałych po przygotowaniu posiłków oraz spełnianiu różnych dziennych i tygodniowych celów swojej rodziny. Gracz z największą liczbą punktów po siedmiu rundach wygrywa.

—description from the publisher (Polish)

A card game with "trick-taking + draft elements" that uses the world view of the Werewolf game. It is a difficulty level for those with experience in trick taking.

After cards have been dealt, players passes two cards to their left ("drafting").

Players need to follow the lead suit ("trick-taking". Some of the cards have effects that will be resolved at the end of a trick.  When any player has one or less card in the their hand, the hand ends.  Every player then reveals cards remaining in their hands to determine whether Demon Fox, Villagers or Werewolves win ("Werewolf").  The winner side score victory points based on those remaining cards in their hand.

Elestrals is a brand new trading card game from the mind of Dan "aDrive" Clap. Dan and his team have been very involved with many different TCGs, like Pokemon.

In a game of Elestrals, your Spirit cards are your most important resource: they are your health and fuel when playing cards and attacking your opponent! Knowing when to use your Spirits and when to save them is a key strategy.

There are five types of Spirits. Most decks have a mix of different types, and the Elestrals and Runes that you Cast will often need specific Spirits or even combinations of them!

To get started, each player has to have the following:

     A Spirit deck with exactly 20 Spirits in it. You can mix and match different Spirits or play as many of the same ones as you like!
     A Main deck with exactly 40 cards in it. This is where your Runes and Elestrals go; you can only have up to three copies of any single card, so you need some variety.


And that’s it! You can look through your own Spirit Deck any time you like and ask your opponent how many cards are left in their Spirit Deck. But you never* get to look through your opponent’s decks, so you won’t know exactly which Spirits they have left. Once your Main Deck is shuffled at the start of a game, you can’t look through it unless a card tells you.

—description from the publisher

Roll and Write Revolution returns with an asymmetric 1-2 player polyomino placing roll and write game. Players are seeking to either help a supposed Anastasia flee from the country or trap her for questioning. The players take up the sides of the White (Loyalist) and Red (Bolshevik) Armies. The supposed Anastasia though moves on her own with the help of cards which guide the movement each turn.

Players draft from a decreasing pool of dice to place their polyomino shapes to the map and may also play cards to influence the movement cards or the dice.

Players will fine challenge in this game as it plays in certain ways like a cat and mouse game with each side attempting to direct the mouse to a goal while avoiding the traps.

—description from the publisher

The call comes in, Engine House Number 9, there is a building on fire downtown. Upon arriving at the scene, it becomes clear to the firefighters that the fire is no ordinary fire. That most of the city is also on fire or has been leveled by a giant monster spitting flame and doing the Twist down 4th Street. Fire hoses and axes are not going to stop a monster, let the national guard handle that! Focus on putting out the fire in this high rise office building, chaps! Up the stairs, no power, keep those people safe!

In Towering Inferno, Roll and Write Revolution goes 3D! Just print, fold, and tape then you're ready to play!

The game sheet becomes a building with 3 or 4 sides for the players to mark the progress of the fire and their firefighters as they strive for the safety of the structure.

Each turn a pool of dice is roll and players select 1 die to act as their firefighter and one to act as their fire. The fire fighter has to enter the building then progress upward as they follow the path to save rooms and control the fire as it burns. The fire will burn downward or to the left or right ignoring paths, but avoiding rooms that have already been secured.

Players can also utilize the special powers of each side of the building they are on to help them move up or laterally. Clever play in this game finds the players having to plan for their moves but also plan for where the fire might go.

Can you secure the fire before the entire structure becomes a Towering Inferno? Or will your dreams go up in flames in this new roll and write game!

—description from the designer

Play Switzerland radio's ABC DRS 3 call-in word game at home!

The candidate player has 45 seconds to find a word (first letter is known) in response to a definition read aloud by the moderator. The listener players bet on how many correct answers the candidate can provide.

From the box (in German): ABC DRS 3 weckt die grauen Zellen. Genau wie im Radio! Die Aufgabe ist klar: Wer innert 45 Sekunden am meisten Wörter zu vorgegebenen Buchstaben findet, gewinnt.

Description in Polish: 
W grze Absolwenci wcielisz się w osobę prowadzącą firmę doradczą. Będziesz pozyskiwać absolwentów o różnych kompetencjach i podnosić ich kwalifikacje. Właściwie dobrany zespół pozwoli Ci na realizację coraz bardziej wymagających kontraktów. Będziesz zdobywać pieniądze na rozwój, szkolić pracowników i dbać o prestiż swojej firmy. W zarządzaniu składem zespołu będą Ci przeszkadzać headhunterzy podkupujący pracowników. Niekiedy pracownicy sami zdecydują się opuścić szeregi zespołu. Na szczęście, nadal możesz sięgnąć po absolwentów Wydziału Zarządzania.

English translation:
In the game Graduates you will play the role of a person running a consulting company. You will attract graduates with various competences and raise their qualifications. A properly selected team will allow you to implement more and more demanding contracts. You will earn money for development, train employees and take care of the prestige of your company. Headhunters who subscribe to employees will hinder you in managing the team composition. Sometimes employees decide to leave the ranks of the team themselves. Fortunately, you can still reach for graduates of the Faculty of Management.

The turn of the game consists of several steps:
- uncover bonus and event cards for the round
- lay out graduate and contract cards
- earn income
- bid for graduates
- assign and train graduates/employees
- fulfill contracts
- resolve bonus and event cards

The flow of the game is simple: after learning what bonus and event cards will bring at the end of round, players bid for graduates with specific sets of skills. After acquiring them players invest in graduates' development by paying for their training and marking the progress on their player boards. Once the players developed teams of employees matching the requirements of laid out contracts, they can fulfill contract cards and earn rewards (points, money or further employee development). At the end of every round some employees leave the teams and replacements will need to be found in consecutive rounds.

Main sources of points are:
- contract cards (about 55% of points)
- bonus cards (about 15%)
- end-game bonuses (about 20%)
- dividend (about 10%)

-description from designer
publishers: Faculty of Management, University of Warsaw (Poland)

description: A roll-and-move game with a Zorro theme.

An expansion to Próba Ognia. It introduces new Character on the cards, new Scenario, new cards for Firefighters, status cards, scenario specific tokens and more...

It was released during Próba Ognia Championship 2022.

This is a fixed partnership, may-follow trick taker that uses dominoes and features a checkers board.  The game is played over the course of two hands with each hand consisting of 9 dominoes per player.

Players will be playing out tricks and determining which team earns points for winning tricks and which team is allowed to place control markers on the checkerboard for potential points at the end of the game.

Clever teamwork to maximize points on trick scoring and board points will determine the winner.

The „Genderstein“ (gender tile) is a promotional tile or the German version of Scrabble with the letter combination *IN and a value of 10 points. It has been released on World Scrabble Day (April 13th) 2022 and distributed at various events like Internationale Spieletage SPIEL in Essen, Germany and can also be ordered at Mattel Germany’s customer support for free.

In the German language, nouns describing people (especially job titles) default to the male form ending with -e(r), while the female form ends with -(er)in (e. g. Bäcker – Bäckerin = baker – female baker). Activist groups are demanding a neutral form using the asterisk (*) as the so-called „Gendersternchen“ (“gender starlet”). In that example, you would write „Bäcker*in“ to emphasize that you mean both genders.

boardgame: Guess in 10: All Around The Town
description:
Get ready to play the most exciting game of questions! Ask up to 10 questions to guess what’s on the card. Think hard, ask intelligent questions, use your clue cards wisely & be the first person to win 7 game cards!

—description from the publisher

Build Trick is a combination of a trick-taking game and a deck building game. A game is played based on the size of the number, and the winner gets points for the number they put out, and the loser gets the money for the number they put out and gets the purchase right. The first to score 50 points wins!

Each player has an initial hand of 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5.  Players can buy cards of value 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, and 25.

The game comes with 54 cards, 1 market sheet (A4).  A bunch of score tokens (around 142 pieces) and money tokens (67 pieces) and a leader marker.

Surrounded by lush green plants and delicious fruits, the curious little sloth wants to explore the jungle. They usually move quite slowly – but then can suddenly disappear very quickly! Three movement-packed games let children follow the trail of the cute little sloth. This stimulates both fine and gross motor skills, association skills, and imagination. Who will be fastest to find the little sloth in one of their favorite spots? Brings movement to playing fun!

—description from the publisher

The 18Mag Expansion Kit comes with four modules. You may use one or more or all of the new modules for a new game experience.

Module 1: Supporter Cards
Six new cards which are drafted at the beginning of the game and which grant the owners special powers.

Module 2: New Minor Companies
Three new player companies which offer alternate starting points. These are drafted like the other companies. For a challenging and longer game, they can simply added to the existing set, allowing one more company for each player. The starting hexes for these three companies are F19, F23, and C8.

Module 3: New Major Company
The company „Compagnie Internationale des Wagons-Lits“ (CIWL) is added to the game. It consists of a 40% director share and three more 20% shares. It adds a Ft30/Ft50 bonus to a company that runs a train from one red off-board location to another.

Module 4: All or nothing
A rules variant where you can no longer select how much the major companies pay out. It's simply all or nothing, which makes the decisions on which shares to buy or sell more tricky.

In the Pijersi game, you and your opponent must master distance, speed and rhythm, while moving, stacking and unstacking your cubes which play the "rock-paper-scissors" roles of fighters, or the "wise" roles of blockers and carriers. You move each cubic piece either individually or by stacking it on top of another which then acts as a quick carrier.

To win, you must bring at least one of your "rock-paper-scissors" cubes to the opposite edge of the board.

Pijersi is a major variant of the Jersi board game which has distilled its core mechanics keeping only 4 types of cubes (all starting on the board) and whose simplified rules are expressed in one and a half page.

A print n play file of the rules (without components) is available.

—description from the designer

"Brrr, it's cold..." - this was your first thought when you blinked and looked around. The very previous moment, as you remembered, you and your daughter were at home, drinking hot
chocolate wearing your ugly Christmas sweaters, staring at the snowfall through the window, and waiting for the clock to hit midnight as usual on 30th November. But now, everything seemed so... frosty and strange.
- What on earth just happened? - your daughter asked, but before you could answer...
- Hello! - You heard from behind... What? A speaking snowman?
- Would you like some hot tea? You two look tired. - he said cheerfully.
And then quickly added: - You can pay with snow! - he smiled widely.
- Whoah, pay with... snow? - You were unable to hide your surprise.

The players are wandering in a magical winter land, and their most important quest is to find a way home. In order to do that they have to help some peculiar locals with their tiny little problems while looking for the magic snowglobe as well, which will be able to send them home when they finally reach the Christmas tree. The players start their journey in front of a snowman's hut, where the adventure begins, exploring objects and mysterious piles of snow, finding useful tools, and winding up in all kinds of challenging situations. Meanwhile, it is snowing continuously, which sometimes might be helpful, but other times it blocks their way. As time goes by, the weather gets drearier and drearier, fog and immense snowdrifts could close off the players' paths. Time passes differently in this unusual world, too. Ten minutes here is a whole day in the real world, so they must hurry with their mission to get home by Christmas Eve. At least that's what the snowman in the hut said, and I really hope he was right.

—description from the designer

The king is dead, blown to pieces. Who's behind it? Not important! What's important now is who gets the dead king's crown. You take the role as one of the royal family members, AKA the Royal Punks, and your goal is to outsmart your opponents and take the crown for yourself.

There are three ways to win:

1) Defeat the other players
2) Control the 5 cities of the Kingdom
3) Level up your Royal Punk

The game is turn-based and every turn consists of two steps:

a) Collection of resources

b) Action
- buying new production, military units and Power cards
- moving your military units, and moving into enemy territory means battle

Royal Punks is an aggressive strategy game with brutal battles where you might end up in chains or even dead. Player interaction is high as multiple players can participate in the same fight.

To win you must be strategic and manage your resources, position yourself wisely and strike on the basis of probability. But first and foremost, you must be active and adaptable - your opponents are also going for the win!

-description from designer

Imperium Progeny is an expansion for Imperium Classics and Imperium Legends. It contains the following 6 cards:
- Bountiful (Fame/Region) variant replacing one of the base cards
- Heir Presumptive (White & Green) added to the Green deck
- Abundance x4 is an alternative to nations who have Prosperity

The promo pack was available during Turczi Junior Promo Pack gamefound campaign.

boardgame: 72 ソロモンの後継者 (72 Heirs of Solomon)
description:
The standalone expansion of 72 (ななじゅうに) (72 Demons of Goetia)

You can play with the base game of this game alone.  This game is specific for 2 players but can be mixed with the base game to support 4 players.  It also has 8 randomizer suit cards to select # of player + 2 suits.

This expansion has 4 additional suits and a new effect "cut-in" (interruption) (blue text) to respond to the effect of other cards.  In addition, there is a very special card - Phoenix.

It comes with additional clarification rulesheet for the base game.

A game of dice collecting and strategy

SET UP
Dice must be placed one by one on the 12 available squares of the first half of the board (the first 3 columns).
Then the players dispute the die who starts the game. Whoever is first has the right to choose the exit point before others, but whoever makes the first move is the player who came in second and so on.

HOW TO PLAY
On their turn, each player may move along the paths in 2 different ways:
1) Along the asphalt streets (gray): they may move 1 square at a time, ahead or to the sides.
2) Along the fast bike lanes (brown): 2 squares at a time, ahead or to the sides.
Each player may make their move and collect the die at the door with a number equal to the die in the center.
In this case, the Player has reached door #3 of the green house and may collect the die with number 3.

CHOOSING THE DICE
You must choose and collect dice, trying to pick up those which will provide the best total, and find the best trajectory to take them to their destination.
Choices must take into consideration the number and color of the dice, which grants special features to each of their number:
- Blue - Increase the number on the die by 1 (+1);
- Red - Reduce the number on the die by 1 (-1);
- Green - Allows an extra move (>1);
- Orange - Double the number of the next die to be picked up (x 2).
Dice that are collected go to each player's marker on the TALLY CHART.

END OF THE FIRST STAGE
When a player reaches any position of the 4th column - where there is a traffic light in the middle of the game board - the first stage of the game ends.
All the dice to the left of that column are removed and rolled again, completing the 12 squares of the second stage, from the 5th to the 8th column.
Players keep their current position and game resumes with the next player after the one who first reached the middle of the game.

IMPORTANT DETAILS
Picking up 2 dice
In some cases, a player's movement will allow them to pick up 2 dice (or more):
- When they pick up a green die and make an extra move, they may pick up a second die;
- If they reach a square in which both door numbers allow them to pick up both dice;
- On the fast lane, if they move 2 squares and pass by doors with the same numbers of the dice, they may pick up both.
Extra movement
Picking up 2 green dice only allows for 1 extra move. On the fast lane, picking up a green die also provides an extra move (i.e., a third move).
Changing direction
If a player wants to change direction during the game, they may spend one action/movement to make that inversion.
Doubled dice
The player who picked up an orange die will pick that one with its normal value and double the next die - even if that just happens on the next round. The die with doubled value will go to the gray area of dice tally.
Blocking
A player may not occupy the same square as another player. To move, they must go in a different direction. A player may also choose not to move.
Movement from the brown lane to the gray lane
When leaving the brown lane to the gray lane, a player doesn't lose the second movement. It may be performed in the gray area. To have a second movement in the brown area, the first movement must be in that lane as well.

ENDING THE GAME
To end a game, each player must have picked up at least 1 die of each color.
When a player reaches one of the arrival points, the game ends, and the other players may still play their last turn.
Besides the scoring from the dice, some Bonuses* may also be acquired by players:
- First in Stage 1 (+2 points);
- First in the Final Stage (+2 points);
- Player with the most dice (+3 points).

The total sum of dice and bonuses determines the winner. If they are tied, the winner is the player who collected the most dice.

-description from designer

In this game, you are a leader of a group of fantastic creatures in a world where civilization is surrounded by creature-infested wilderness. Your goal is to get the biggest number of creatures in your group. At the start of the game the players decide on a number of creature that they need to get to win and the first player to get those creature and return to start wins. In the setup cards are distributed into numerous piles. Each turn, players can travel to adjacent piles and decide whether to encounter a creature there. By putting forward the best creatures for each encounter, you can befriend and recruit new creatures or battle and subdue them. Your success depends on whether they like each other, what the creatures are good and bad at fighting, and what type of creatures they are. Remembering the characteristics of the other creatures that have been encountered can help you to strategically match creatures in an encounter so as to grow your group. Players may also encounter each other and trade or battle.

description:
If you've never played Space Weirdos, you might wonder what will make Sword Weirdos different from other fantasy skirmish games. Not much! It takes what I like from other games and mashes it all together.

Low model count: 4-10 models or so for each side.
Use any minis you have.
Short but comprehensive. Everything in one 16ish page zine.
Minimal tokens, but still a few because I like them.
No tape measures or rulers, all movement and ranges are measured with 5” sticks.
Uses all your RPG dice (except the d20, d20's get enough love) because dice are fun.
A points system, but you shouldn’t worry about it too much.
Alternating activations, opposed rolls, Maneuver Points, and random tables mean everyone is involved and rolling dice all the time.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Impractical Jokers the Game: Ultimate Challenge Pack

Whether you like pulling off undercover challenges on friends or just laughing at hilariously awkward card combinations, this game has something for you. boardgame: Hunt A Killer: Dead on the Vine
description:
One night in July, members of the Llewyn family gather at the family vineyard to celebrate a birthday. After a tumultuous night full of arguments and a big factory malfunction, the clan matriarch, Gail, is found dead in her bed. The police are called and the death is ruled an accident—until the autopsy returns and it’s discovered that Gail was poisoned. Comb through the belongings of the family, analyze their witness statements, and piece together a timeline to determine who killed the vineyard matriarch.

Do you have what it takes to Hunt A Killer? Sort through evidence, decode ciphers, piece together clues, and catch the killer.

Difficulty: 3/5 - Medium

A slightly new take on variable strength chits (more granular with up to 5 steps per unit vs. the usual three) covering more obscure battles of the Eastern Front. This is the first entry in a three part simulation of the failed Soviet offensive to eliminate the salient of the German Ninth Army during the winter of 1942-43 known as Operation Mars (25 Nov - 12 Dec 1942). Players will conduct operations as either Soviet Direction Commander Zhukov or the German commander of the Ninth Army, Model. Several possibilities existed given the terrain and the weather situation and some of these will be possible to explore with this game. Included are some hypothetical options that could have altered the outcome. This work is primarily based on Zhukov's Greatest Defeat by David Glantz with some OOB fleshing by Nafziger and maps based on post-war USGS surveys (which were themselves taken from wartime Soviet surveys).

Each volume has one 22"x34" mapsheet (using 20mm hexes to be less cramped) with all three volumes able to link together covering 43" x 48" total area at 1.5 miles/hex and one day per turn.
Each game uses 0.6" counters of mainly German battalions and Soviet regiments or brigades - the first volume (Belyy) has 352 counters with two double-sided and three single-sided PAC accompanying a 18 page rule book, the second volume (Olenino) has 440 counters with three double-sided and two single-sided PAC accompanying a 20 page rule book, and the final volume (Konev) will have 528 counters with three double-sided and three single-sided PAC accompanying a 20 page rule book.

Included scenario:
Belyy - Volume I - #1 Belyy Thrust - The included scenario provides the players a look at the attacks of Soviet 41st Army which managed the best gains of the operation. The goal of this scenario is to present players with a fluid breakthrough and timely reinforcements. Both sides hoping their command decisions will carry the day. Scenario Duration: Turns 1-10 (25 Nov - 4 Dec) on Map KS.

—description from the designer

The players take turns to roll and move. The first player to reach the peak at space 55 wins the game. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

Using the latest genetic technologies, you will fulfill your dream of creating a cretaceous park! To do so, you will place dinosaurs in your park to attract tourists. But be aware of rival parks, attacks will be constant! Who will get more tourists?

Each player starts with a hand of three dinosaur cards. There are two common stacks: one for dinosaurs and another one for tourists.

Players will make turns and in their turn they will follow these two steps:
1.Draw one card: a dinosaur or a tourist.
2.Do one action choosing between these two options:
 2a.Expand your park by placing one dinosaur in it.
 2b.Attack and steal one dinosaur from another park using one stronger dinosaur from your hand, which will be discarded afterwards.

When you get two dinosaurs of the same type in your park, a tourist will come to see them and give you profits so you can take another dinosaur card from the common stack.
When you get three dinosaurs of the same type in your park, you can place a Ranger that will protect dinosaurs and tourists in that group.
When you get four dinosaurs of the same type in your park, you can steal a tourist from another park.
After an attack, if a carnivorous dinosaur is left without a partner it will eat its tourist which goes to his owners' hand. The tourist also goes to his owners hand when the last herbivorous dinosaur from a group is lost.

When both common dinosaur and tourist stacks are depleted, a last round is played and then the game ends. The park with more tourists is the winner.

-description from designer

description:
A rotating card game where each card has four letters or bigram fragments. Players spin cards to form words that match specific attributes. The game is played in several rounds with successively longer words.

COMPONENTS:
54 2-inch two-sided square cards. One side has letters, the back side has attributes. A timer from a mobile device is required.

GOAL:
Get the most points by cobbling together letters into words.

INITIAL SETUP:
Shuffle the deck and deal three letter cards face up to each player. Flip over three attribute cards.

GAME PLAY:
Players play simultaneously. Each player rotates their cards and melds them together to form a word. The letters of the word must come from adjacent cards, but not all cards need to be used. Words that match an attribute score +1 bonus for each matching attribute. The first player to call out a word and their score starts a one-minute timer. At the end of one minute, each player calls out their word and score. The player with the lowest score can risk one attempt to better their score. However, that player must be able to beat the highest score else they score zero. That player has one minute for the attempt.

Each score is recorded at the end of the round.

Players now decide if they collectively want to increase the number of cards dealt in the next round. Game play continues after shuffling and dealing the next sets of cards as per SETUP above (maybe with more cards).

After the final round of five cards, the player with the highest score is the winner.

ATTRIBUTES:
There are many attributes for bonus points. 
For example, is a food, ends in X, has two vowels.
Although players can make any word, they should strive to make words that match one or more attributes to get the bonus points.

SOLO PLAY:
Deal five cards and three attribute cards. Try to make successively longer words with higher and higher points. Record your best score. Repeat to attempt an even higher score.

-description from designer

Охотники ("Hunters") is a family board game, where each player need to trace a prey on prehistoric hunt. When a player turns a tile, he compares the power of animal and hunter. It's a world of saber-toothed tigers, woolly rhinos and a tribe of hungry hunters.

The game hase three modes: competitive, cooperative and command. All the tiles are placed on the table, this is a hunting field. A player makes following actions:

1. Turns over 2 tiles on the hunting field.
2. Evaluates the results of hunt. If it's possible, make additional actions.
3. Take the tiles, if the hunt was successful (matched), or turn back the tiles, if the hunt was unsuccessful.

A player wins in competitive mode if he has more hunted tiles or has the highest values of tiles. In cooperative mode, players need to collect a certain number of tiles in 30 rounds.

Hell-o and welcome to Demoniac, the devilish new card game for 2-4 players. The goal is to possess your hapless victim, and then use them to defeat the exorcist. The first player to convert their victim and the exorcists into Demoniacs wins.

Each player has a victim with mind and body strengths. Demon cards are played from your hand to torment your victims mind and body with precision. Once a victim is properly tormented, a dice roll determines whether they become fully possessed.

Players must watch out for the exorcist, who gets a chance every turn to use their holy power to disrupt players. "Divinity" cards are revealed each turn which change the rules of the game and can set players back considerably. Players can use "Disturbance" cards to cancel them out and keep their diabolical plans moving forward.

The first player to possess their victim and then attack the exorcist with a horde of matching demons wins the game. Can you conquer goodness and be the first to snatch souls? Choose your victim and get possessing!

When Beth Ferris-Hendricks’s death is declared an accident in the small town of Mallory Rock, Maine, her sister Gwen sets out to prove Beth was murdered. Gwen, having grown up on the New England island, must investigate the residents she has known all of her life.

To catch the killer, Gwen will need your help to expose her hometown and uncover the secrets that exist on Mallory Rock.

Difficulty: 3/5

The object of The Last Tower is to capture as many tokens of your opponents, by forming towers of tokens, as possible - while avoiding having your own captured as much as possible. You always try to keep up with the latest home-decorating trends, and now that might pay off if your home catches the eye of a judge from Decor Rate magazine. You could win a prize and have your home featured in their publication.

Players start Ulterior Design by revealing one current trend card face up in a central area, then they place one card from their hand face up in their area. On each turn after that, a player plays one of their three hand cards to add to their own house (redecorate), to influence what is trending (by adding a card to the central area), or to sabotage a rival (by adding their card to an opponent's house). Cards are split down the middle with some combination of interior decorations (houseplants, paintings and lamps) on each half, and they must be played so that they overlap at least one half of an already played card.

To end their turn, they refill their hand, then reveal an event card, which may be a judge, a reshuffle, or crickets. Judges are looking for the right number of plants, portraits, or lamps. The player who most closely matches the total number of items in the current trend receives an award; in a tie, the award goes to whoever played least recently as they were trending before it was cool.

The first player to collect three awards wins.

boardgame: ウロボロスの傭兵 (Mercenary of Ouroboros)
description:
In Mercenary of Ouroboros, each player has an identical deck of cards ranked 1 to 3 in three suits.  The players arrange them in a random grid of 3x3 cards and reveal the front row.

Playing with this front row of cards, one player leads to a trick, and the other player follows with one of their cards.  The winner of the trick will have one less card in the column, but the loser of the trick will collect the winners cards and slide it into the back of the column they played a card from.

The first player to empty two of their columns wins.  The game also includes some tactics cards which affect trump suits, one time rank bonuses, etc.

Marijampolė is an educational strategy inspired by the history of the city of Marijampolė. In the game, 2-4 players are tasked to build a thriving city. However, as history teaches us, every city builder has its own vision and agenda, so it's not an easy task. And in some cases, it can become quite competitive. By building historical buildings, establishing companies, using the strengths of famous Lithuanian historical figures, and surviving the difficult times that affected the whole of Europe, Marijampolė players are able to shape this city as a trading stop, an industrial metropolis or a cradle of culture.

The game features two distinct rule sets. The beginner's version will allow the whole family to take part in building a city while the advanced rule version is for board game enthusiasts who don't mind longer and more strategic play sessions. In addition, “Marijampolė” can also be a good educational tool. Each card in the game is a real historic building, event, or person and has a detailed fact-checked description. All players build their own vision of Marijampolė but the most prosperous player wins the game!

During the game, players take turns collecting cards from a shared market and playing those cards in front of their tableau by paying the required resources. Game cards are also treated as resources so hand management is an important mechanism in "Marijampolė". Greater points are scored by having the most cards in the tableau corresponding to the state of the city, that will often change depending on what cards are being played. Sometimes a player will gain points by building wondrous churches while at other times initiating commerce will grant the most progress points.

The game also has Leader cards that grant players unique powers helping to achieve their goals and manipulate the board. In order to slow down the opponent's progress, the player can play special Crisis cards that trigger hardships for the city. It may be used to stop other players from gaining points but prolonged crises can also continue for multiple turns. In the game, each action has a strong effect on other players' scoring potential. Players can cooperate with other players to gain more progress points or counter them by going in a different direction.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 老師敬服～正派対邪教～ (Master of Respect: Orthodox vs. Heresy)
description:
In this 2 player version of Master of Respect, the players are rival instructors at different martial arts organizations.  On their turn, a player will play an action tile from their hand and resolve its effects.  The other player may then choose to "pay respect" to the player in order to also copy the effect of the tile.  If they do, the active player's students may gain resources which the player will have access to in the next round.

The general effects of the action tiles are to acquire either of the games' two basic resources, additional respect tiles, buy more action tiles, or acquire/train new students.

In each round, each person will play 3 tiles from their hand and after 4 rounds the game ends. The player with the most points wins.

老師敬服が２人専用のゲームとして生まれ変わりました。
プレイヤーは道場の老師となり、道場の繫栄のために奮闘します。
アクションタイルを使用し、素材を集め、弟子や奥義を獲得していき、勝利点が最も高いプレイヤーが勝利します。
老師敬服でもあった「敬服」を行うことで相手プレイヤーの行うアクションと同じアクションができます。「敬服」されたプレイヤーは代わりに勝利点を少し受け取ることになります。
２人用になったことで、「敬服」以外にも相手プレイヤーの手番中にアクション出来るようになったり、弟子に「正派」と「邪教」という要素が追加されています。
