"Avoid the sweet potato."
"But who am I?"

Once upon a time, young buds began to grow in peaceful potato fields and new sweet potato brothers began to grow.
a happy sweet potato that thinks of itself as a potato and a potato that doesn't like it live together.
One day, A hungry raccoon came down into the field.
Oh, my God!
The raccoon is digging up the soil to find sweet potatoes and hurting the potatoes!
If things go on like this, the potato field will be ruined.
If you feed the raccoon with sweet potatoes and fill it up, the raccoon will return.
Let's all restore peace in the potato field.

from now on every player is a potato. 
You can only say 'PATATA'.

each player lifts their a secret card, placing it on their forehead so that all of the other players can see it, but the player cannot see his own.
Put the card down to the back in front of each player.

When it's your turn, you can choose a card.
- if it's a event card, open it and play the action.
- if it's a secret card, placing it on your forehead. and You can exchange an your secret card.

The round ends when 3 raccoon cards come out.
At the end of the round, they reveal their secret and receive points.

The game ends with the second round.
The player with the most points wins.

Girls, boys, tomorrow is our big day.  The day of the Dead!  Aren't you glad?  What happen to you?  Oh yes, our friends the monsters will not come… They go out tonight to look for candy and tomorrow they will be tired.  We throw some epic parties and they'll never know!  Unless…
You must take advantage of the rolls of the dice, yours and your rivals, to get the treats that each monster likes the most, move from neighborhood to neighborhood and invite them to your party.

Be careful because not all monsters want to be close to what others... Although that's not a problem for a professional party skeleton like you, right?

Also, you can always use the help of the pumpkin to change your luck.

Bring the thunder: Pretend play as you race to the finish line in Brain Storm by getting your team to think lightning fast and guess the answers on the cards based on your quick descriptions.

How to play: Land on a category, then one teammate has to describe words in that category while the rest of the team guesses. Repeat this using that same category for as many cards as you can until time's is up. Move forward based on how many cards are guessed correctly. First team to the finish line wins.

Categories: Each answer falls into one of six simple categories: movies and tv science and nature (like the weather), actions (like winning), objects (like this board game), and places and landmarks, plus random topics for the Lightning Spaces.

Fun for friends and family: Brain Storm is a family friendly trivia game meant for 4 or more players ages 12 and up. Each game takes about 30 minutes.

What's in the box: Brain Storm includes a gameboard, 5 cloud pieces, a sand timer, the rules sheet, and 500 cards, each having answers to all 6 categories on them. With 3,000 possible answers, you can play Brainstorm as many times as you want for endless fun!

—description from the publisher

The rebellious French forces controlling England are demanding additional funds to support their war effort. As supporters of King John, Wimentun village, having already paid taxes to the English crown, are resisting paying for a second time.  As villages scatter, rushing to squirrel away their last pennies, mail-clad soldiers have already arrived to collect.

Death & Taxes is the first supplement designed for The Barons' War skirmish game. Play your way through the linked scenario-driven campaign, choosing either the forces of the Sheriff of Nottinghamshire or local outlaws sworn to protect the villages.

Further rules are included for developing and running exciting stand-alone seasonal campaigns where individual Commanders strive to be the most chivalrous while battling it out over a campaign map to conquer territory.

This digital supplement contains 112 pages (plus original cover art by Peter Dennis) which include the Death & Taxes linked campaign, the Outlaw retinue list and rules for running a map-based campaign.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dungeon Degenerates: Tribulations at the Tunnel of Terror
description:
Tribulations at the Tunnel of Terror is a dungeon crawl adventure module for Dungeon Degenerates by Erick Bouchard &  illustrated by Sean Äaberg. Tribulations at the Tunnel of Terror is a weird wandering in the hideous hole in the Highlands of the Würstreich with the dreadfully distorted Nackt Drek, the Eyeball Kid & his watchful friend as Lord Scrott plots...  This adventure uses the Eyeball Kid card which is included with Die Würst #4. But the stats are included in the book.

Tribulations at the Tunnel of Terror comes with the exclusive Drek Stack card for use with Dungeon Degenerates & a full-color map.

—description from the publisher
Thought provoking tales and unforgettable collectibles that explore mind control from revenants to espionage.

Mentor is a logic machine that has a mathematical strategy to match every move the player makes in any games that are played. The player can win games by looking ahead and carefully calculating his moves. However, if he makes a mistake, Mentor will win. In some games Mentor makes one or more intentional mistakes so that the player will have a better chance to win. If you are unable to win a game against Mentor, he will teach you step by step the correct strategy.

Game cards are placed on the playing board and the Pawn is inserted in the start position. Take turns with Mentor to move one, two, or three spaces. You win if you move the Pawn to finish before Mentor does.

Kitten Dinner is a family game for 2-5 players, ages 8 and up. Can you feed the hungry kittens their dinner to win the game?

IT'S DINNER TIME!
Hungry kittens wander the streets. Can you lure them back to your warm home with the promise of good meal? If you feed enough kitties their dinner, you win!

COLLECT YOUR CARDS!
Roll the dice and decide which direction to move the Kitten mover around the circle of Kittens. Collect cards equal to the number you've rolled and add them to your hand. Don't roll all blanks or a kitty will get away!

FEED THE KITTIES!
Lure the Kitties back to your home by matching their symbols. Spell D-I-N-N-E-R using your food cards to feed the Kittens and earn their point value.

HOW TO WIN: The player who feeds kitties and earns the predetermined points (9-21 depending on player count) wins the game.

—description from the designer

Find the hidden treasure deep in the cave.
Roll the dice to make your way, and fight other competitors for treasure.
Even if you think you'll be in first place, you may lose all your treasures in a sudden attack.
A racing game that can be played by 2 to 6 players.

—description from the publisher

Blabbi is a game of made-up words.

Overview:

Blabbi is a game of made-up words! Rack up points by coming up with imaginary words using topic cards, then let your buddies decide if your definitions are gold or goof!

––––

How to Play:

Blabbi is a party crosswords game where every word is entirely made-up!

On your turn, use any number of your 8 letters and 3 topic cards to invent a fake word and pitch a definition on the spot.
But get ready to explain yourself because other players can ask questions about your word until they've voted: Yes (they believe it) or No (they don't buy it).

If the majority votes Yes, you keep the topic card and earn points.

At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins!

• Easy to learn to start playing in 2 minutes or less!
• Great news for bad spellers: Blabbi is a twist on word games that leans more on humor and creativity!
• Designed for people who hate rules, so play how you want and in any language you want!
• Never gets old. Blabbi is all about language creativity, so you’ll never play the same way twice!
• Big on content? Our box includes a built-in phone stand to record your favorite definitions.

Game Details:
Players: 3+
Age: 12+
Set Up Time: 2 mins
Game Length: 30-60 mins
Type: Word Game / Improv Game / Party Game
Difficulty Level: Easy
Ideal for: Creatives, storytellers, actors, and everyone in between.

What’s in the Box:

100 Squishy Nonslip/Waterproof Letter Tiles
80 Oddly Specific Topic Cards
8 Voting Cards
1 Built-in Phone Stand

Dimensions:
9.9" x 7" x 2.4"

—description from the designer

The Best of Town Cryer collects some of the best articles from White Dwarf about Mordheim: City of the Damned allowing the game to be expanded in multiple ways, including new warbands, random events and rules for multiplayer skirmishes.

The Pandemic Expansion brings the exciting pandemic mode to your table and is - of course compatible with all other modes in HE.R.O!
There's 4 new diagnoses and a total of 10 new patients. You'll also find pandemic dice and pandemic markers, which are needed for the Pandemic Mode.

Any new patient shows up? Roll the dice! if it's a green side, everything continues as usual. But if you roll a red side, the patient is infected! At the end of the round, every infected patient that didn't get treated will spread the infection to adjacent patients! So you can plan ahead and isolate them accordingly.

With that not being challenging enough, if you treat a patient, the treatment station (or ICU, or OR,....) will stay dirty! Any new patient brought in there will automatically be infected.

If you have no more "clean" areas, you automatically lose.

—description from the publisher

Probability Magic Kingdom Chess is a 2P abstract game where players take turns moving pieces on a board. Rather than simply capturing a piece that would be landed upon, the pieces show a pie chart of the probabilities of certain results that will happen when each player rolls a standard D6 to determine the combat results.  Each piece has a side at full health and can be flipped over to an injured side.

The first player to kill an opponent's king or move their king into the opponent's king's space is the winner.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Broken Realms – Mortevell's Helcourt
description:
Amongst the deluded cannibals of the Ymetrican Flesh-eaters, there are none more devoted to Nagash than Gorstane Mortevell. With screeching, howling invective, the ghoulish emperor drives his court into a frenzy of religious devotion. To their victims, it is nothing more than a rabid display of madness. 
 Unleash a nightmarish host of ghouls on an (un)holy crusade, united under the banner of an ignoble anti-hero. Combine the models in the set with the rules found in Broken Realms: Teclis, and you’ll be able to field the Mortevell's Helcourt warscroll battalion, transforming them into Gorstane Mortevell and his fanatically loyal followers. This box also saves you money compared to getting the models separately!
 This set contains 3 Crypt Horrors, 20 Crypt Ghouls, and 1 Abhorrant Archregent. The Crypt Horrors can alternatively be built as Crypt Flayers or Vargheists. The kit includes a variety of variety of options and accessories.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Skaven – Plague Monks
description:
Driven into battle by their frenzied faith, the Plague Monks of Clan Pestilens are a repulsive tidal wave of filth that smashes into their enemies with the power of a flood. Wildly slashing, hacking and stabbing with their dirt-encrusted blades, they spread disease and foetidness wherever they fight, infecting with every blow and gnawing at foes with their rotting - yet still formidable - fangs.

This multi-part plastic kit gives you everything needed to build a chittering horde of twenty Plague Monks, with the option of arming them with a pair of Foetid Blades or a Foetid Blade and Woe-stave. One model can be built as a Bringer-of-the-Word - he clutches either a Plague Scroll or Book of Woe. A model can be assembled as an Icon Bearer with either a Contagion Banner or Icon of Pestilence, and a model may be assembled as a Plague Harbinger, carrying either a Doom Gong or a dreaded Bale-chime.

Soularium is a roll-and-write game for any number of players (1 - ∞). Your goal is to build Heaven & Hell from scratch, respectively endowing them with various resources such as: Angels and Demons; Virtues and Sins; Pure Souls and Dark Souls. And, somewhere in between, there is the Purgatory. A key element of the game is trying to avoid Celestial Imbalance by not overpowering one of the two Eternal Places.

Gatefall is a strategic miniatures game of tactics, deck building, and resource management.

In the game, a rift has opened between worlds, causing a war between very different kinds of characters. The base game mode is designed for two players to fight head-to-head. Arena mode lets 2-8 players fight one another in a battle royale setting. In a two player game, players choose which team to control, the fantasy team consisting of Brog, Gildri, and Randar, or the Wasteland team consisting of Exile, Firebug, Penny, and Lost Boy.

When a character is killed, the player who killed that character earns the number of victory points printed on the character’s upgrade shop card, and the player whose character was killed draws a “zero” card from the “zeroes” pile for every victory point listed on the killed character’s upgrade shop card and adds it to their discard pile. The killed character respawns in their base immediately, but that character cannot move or attack on their next turn, and effectively loses a turn while respawning.

The first player to reach ten victory points wins the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Slaves to Darkness – Corvus Cabal
description:
Agile and avaricious, the murder-cultists of the Corvus Cabal descend on their prey from above. Always hunting for worthy trinkets to offer the Great Gatherer, chaotic patron of cutthroats and thieves, the Cabal strike without warning or mercy.

Do your enemies dare cower in cover, rather than bringing themselves to your Slaves to Darkness army for a jolly good slaughtering? Then you need the Corvus Cabal! These shadowy reavers are terrifyingly mobile, making them perfect for last-minute objective grabs or hunting distant foes, capable of bringing them down with a mix of ranged and melee attacks. Alternatively, you can use this set to bolster your roster for Warcry if you already own your Fighter Cards and Abilities Card for the Corvus Cabal.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Slaves to Darkness – The Unmade
description:
The island of Tzlid, drifting ever closer to the Shyish Nadir, is home to the cannibal tribes of the Unmade. The self-mutilation performed by these pain worshippers is horrifying to behold, and their leaders are visions from the darkest of nightmares.

The Unmade don't just look scary – they're a cracking choice for the battlefields of Warhammer Age of Sigmar, too! These self-mutilating maniacs won’t just carve your enemies up in close combat, but can rob them of their ability to run away, too. This lets you lock down key foes while the rest of your Slaves to Darkness run roughshod on your foe’s squishiest units! They'll also dish out some nasty penalties to Bravery for anyone nearby. Alternatively, you can use this set to bolster your roster for Warcry if you already own your Fighter Cards and Abilities Card for the Unmade.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): Khan Queso Promo Card
description:
A new mercenary card.

Special ability: Plagoue and Spread
Khan Queso comes bearing the Plague ability, spreading danger across the battlefield as he advances. It is possible to even chain Plague across multiple units with clever positioning, causing all sorts of mayhem around the battlefield!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): Blacksaber Promo Card
description:
A new mercenary card.

Special ability: Treacherous
Blacksaber comes into play for a very affordable cost for the stats she has. However, money (or magic?) does the talking here, and control of her can switch at a moment’s notice if your opponent is willing to pay to recruit Blacksaber over to their side! Who will win over Blacksaber in the end? Are you willing to take the risk?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: G.I. JOE Deck-Building Game: Coldsnap Bonus Box #3
description: A promo pack for G.I. Joe Deck-Building Game containing a new player leader (Beach Head), and a buildable punchboard towables yard!

boardgame: What's Next? A Life-Size Drinking Game
description:
Step away from the typical beer pong table and check out What's Next? A Life-Size Drinking Game. This is the ultimate game to get your party started!

This adult party game will get you moving, laughing, and toasting to the good times.

It's the only party game where YOU are the game piece! Drink, laugh, and challenge your friends with this brand-new party game. It's easy to learn — even if you've had a few drinks.

What will you do to win... AND how tipsy will you be when you do? Buy it now and get ready to have a blast!

What's Next? Social Skills Add-on is the first expansion set to What’s Next? A Life-Size Game — designed for special needs folks.

It was specifically developed to...


     Provide all-inclusive options for special-needs families.
     Ensure that every player feels completely comfortable with all actions of the game.
     Enhance player's social skills, while engaging in physical activity.


Here’s how it works…

Lay out all tiles from What’s Next? A Life-Size Game.

Switch the original tile with the Social Skills tile that has the same number. Then just play as usual.

—description from the publisher

North Africa, 1942. The burning desert sands witnessed the fiercest tank battles of World War II. Imagine yourself commanding a squad of tanks and tank destroyers. Do you think you are more adept at tactics and strategy than your opponents? On the battlefield you will be able to prove it, because the action already begins with Tobruk.

90's Argentinian wargame.

Merit is a fast-playing, no luck required, skirmish style game in a hex based arena!

Take turns maneuvering your heroes on the map, battle to control powerful Realm Stones to unlock devastating new abilities, and unleash brutal attacks on your opponents’ heroes!

To win, either defeat all of your opponents’ heroes, or control each Realm Stone on the map.

—description from the publisher

In Mandala Mind: The Card Game, you play as the frame of mind of a person who is meditating on an image of a mandala. This person is distracted at the beginning of their meditation, and your goal is to help them focus. Once focus has been achieved, you will have succeeded in bringing them into the joy of the present moment!

A game is played over a series of rounds, where you will move a pawn around a 3 x 3 grid of mandala cards removing distraction using frame of mind actions and mandala abilities. Before each game, you will choose a scenario to play, which provides a unique puzzle to solve. Each scenario has two phases that have different goals for you to complete. You win the game by achieving optimal focus at scenario-specific mandala cards in the warm-up (first) phase and by removing all distraction from every mandala in the meditation (second) phase.

-description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Take part in the frenzy of trading in crypto, dealing with the 'pumps' and the 'dumps' in the most volatile way. There has never been a more wacky, back and forth trading situation than with crypto, especially now.

Hodl It is played in a group of 3-8 players, where there are up to 8 different coin cards in play for everyone to collect within a round. These coin cards come in sets, and in order to win the game, a player must endeavour to collect all of one set. They can however, also utilise wildcards mixed into the deck which can have a positive, or a negative effect.

As the round progresses, players are trading 1, 2, 3 or more cards at a time. These trades are likely made up of coin cards but can include wildcards that the player doesn't want in their hand by the end. In turn, they are aiming to collect the cards they need from other players from that trade, so effectively it is fastest person first providing they aren't blindly competing for the same coin card. The round ends as soon as one player has a full winning set in hand and claims 'HOLD IT'!

The round can be played in as little as 5 minutes, depending on how volatile you make your trades, providing they follow some simple rules such as all the cards being traded, are of the same suit. As the rounds can be so short, this is a game of score and how well you can build that score up over several rounds, with each potential win in a round being of a varied score in itself.

The game is solely played with cards within one card deck. There are no other components.

—description from the designer

Stash720 is a quick-playing, fast-investing, stock market card game, played in a way similar to the classic board game, Monopoly. To win, be the first player to collect 3 complete Stock Card sets in different colours. This card game is a great way to help learn basic economics, risk and reward, while dealing with stock trading in as little as 15 minutes. It comes with 110 cards, including Stock Cards, Stock Growth Cards, Wild Stock Cards and Power Cards. Power Cards let players do things such as charge Stock Growth and force favourable investments. Wild Stock Cards help players build Stock sets. Players pay their debts with Bitcoin Cards, or their stock cards if they are out of Bitcoin.

A hilarious Dutch party game based on statements that have actually appeared on the internet. From bizarre conspiracy theories to silly sales ads. From right-wing 'wappies' statements to left-wing bicycle boomer quotes. Everything passes by.

The goal of the game is similar to the goal of life: raking in as many likes as possible. You do this by making status updates. Is your status the most hilarious one on the table or is it so tasteless that your fellow players like it? Then you will receive likes.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Lost Ones: A Dark Path Micro Expansion
description:
Assume the role of a character who has been kidnappedby Fae and taken to the Otherworld.
You must explore this magical realm of dreams, evade recapture and discover a way home.
Lost ones is a board gamethat can be played solo or cooperatively with up to four players.

Adds: 1x Storybook, 9x Map Cards, 1x Boon card, 2x Foe cards, 1 Plot token

In the game Halloween, players search a haunted castle for candies and try to avoid being cursed.

Player collect cards from the 12+1 rooms of the castle to get the best candy combos for victory points.

Granja Colorida is a board game designed for the little ones to have a nice time with the family. The object of this game is to mark all the colored squares on the individual board with the corresponding marker tiles, matching the cards that are drawn.

An economical deck-builder for managing an amusement park, designed for 1-4 players, with a game time from 45 to 60 minutes. By running our own amusement park, we will be able to purchase attractions that generate happiness, which in turn will attract more visitors to our kingdom of fun. And these are an irreplaceable source of income. We will be able to spend the earned money, for example, on the purchase of decorations for our park, which will give us victory points. But not to be so rosy, people visiting the funfair like to litter and be fussy; attractions break down all the time and the employees are demanding payment! In addition, we must deliberately manage the park space, which is not unlimited. The goal of the game is to collect as many park recognition points as possible

Wild Island - My Father's Dragon is a family board game based on the film My Father's Dragon. Each player represents a team of boy Elmer and dragon Boris. The island on which they find themselves is sinking, shaking, and changing shape all the time. But there is still time to save the island by smartly moving around the island visiting different animals and finding the right energy source. The first one to do so is the winner of the game.

The island consists of 16 tiles, randomly placed in a 4x4 grid. Each tile has nine spaces with trees and paths. Players move around the island by rolling a special die trying to reach their objectives. From the second round, the island starts to shake intensely and the tiles are shifted and moved by the players. There are multiple icons that can help players reach their objectives faster: Elmer and dragon Boris can go into the caves and come out in any other cave on the island, fly icons allow dragon Boris to fly to any other adjacent tile, and courage icons represent their bravery when trying to save the island. With them, they get courage tiles to be even more flexible when moving around and shaking the island. Some of them have to be used immediately and some can be saved in Elmer's backpack and used in one of the later turns.

The objective tiles each player has, are in the shape of jigsaw puzzle pieces. When the player visits their objective, they are turned around and when all four objectives are reached the image of a saved island is assembled as a small jigsaw puzzle.

-description from publisher

At the height of the Minoan civilization, it is said that there was a game that the nobles used to play, in which they tried to dominate a board by placing borders and pawns, using strategy as well as cunning. The game you are about to play is an approach to this ancient game, one of the first board games ever made!

Overview: 
Διαίρεσις (Division) is a tactical game for two players. During the game, players place borders on the game board, dividing it into areas in which they try to gain the majority by placing pawns.
At the end of the game, each player counts the number of squares in the areas on which they have the majority, and the player with the most squares is the winner.
In case of a tie the game ends in a draw.

Contents:
1 Game Board
18 borders (6 small, 6 medium, 6 large) in two colors
12 pawns (8 small and 4 large) in two colors

boardgame: Cthulhu: Death May Die – Season 4 Expansion
description:
The Season 4 expansion brings a lot of content to expand your experience with Death May Die. From a whole roster of unique Investigators, to 6 brand new Episodes, introducing all manner elements to the game, utilizing a new set of game tiles, and pitting a host of terrifying new monsters in all shapes and sizes against the players!

—description from the publisher

There's work to be done in the fairy-tale kingdom, from transporting golden eggs to unblocking elven sewers.

Can you assemble the best team of wizards for each job?

You play as magical agents who are recruiting wizards as they walk into the local fairytale gathering-house looking for work. Chris invented and drew twenty-one original wizards - from Hercule the Impressive, master of mechanical magic to Small Norman, who is fairly good at not causing trouble. There's also a host of clients offering all sorts of gigs - your wizards might end up doing maze-building work for a Minotaur, putting in a shift at the magical bakery, or joining a dance troupe.

In each round, players compete to recruit wizards for the four different roles that the client needs, possibly bringing a familiar along to help out. Only the best team gets the job. So will you trust Sporegard Drizzle to put on a firework display for the king, or should you really wait to hire someone less damp?

—description from the publisher

This expansion adds a small board where the neutral grey leaders are placed. The cost of buying a gray leader now changes during the rounds. They get cheaper every round until one is bought. In that case, the price rises for one or two of the remaining.

Piter, Genius Advisor

description: The Curator Pack contains all the goodies and additional content unlocked during the original Museum Kickstarter campaign, including 4 mini-expansions, player figurines, Solo Mode, new Favour, Patron and Headline cards, and more.

It’s picture day, but…
The ducks need help. Their hair’s a mess.
Others look nice. Their hair looks great.
Grab the mousse. Help the ducks.

Game-play involves several different sets of rules:

     Normal rules (similar to the "Duck Duck Goose" children's game) - Players throw down a card at a time until mousse appears. The first player to slap the mousse gets it and mousses one of their ducks. The first player to mousse all their ducks wins.
     In Style rules (a sets game) - Players use the non-duck animal cards to search for sets of 3. One animal card is chosen as wild. First player to get 3 sets of 3 wins.
     Blowout rules - Players divvy up all cards evenly, then play as fast as possible to get 5 ducks and 5 moose. A less-intense version involves stealing extra ducks or mousse left in the center discard pile, and the craziest version involves stealing directly from others as they uncover cards from their respective decks.


—description from the publisher

Kickstarter campaign begins July 11, 2023!

boardgame: Where's Wally Now?: The card game
description:
Using the cards you can assemble "A crazy party in Paris" (XIXth century) or an "the last days of the Aztecs", with 16 cards each in a 4x4 matrix.

The first player who finds Wally gets 10 points. But there are other 10 questions (1 point each) that you have to answer finding the information in the cards

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Free Folk Attachments 1

There are an unknown number of tribes that live north of the Wall. These tough and hardy people will often form up and head south, looking for more hospitable land to call their own. While mostly a motley crew, there are still tribal chiefs and other veterans that have honed their skills against the terrors in the far north. boardgame: Змеи и лестницы: Бродилка (Snakes and Ladders: Adventure)
description:
In the far, far jungle... a very unusual and extremely rare crunchfruit grows. The animals decide to hold a race: the winner will get the mysterious fruit. They collect various equipment in the jungle, scattered by unfortunate seekers, and gather at the start. During this dynamic and fun race, the participants will have to create the board themselves! Players roll the dice, move their characters around the field, mark successes on the tablets and interfere with rivals in every possible way. Make your way with ladders, trap opponents with vines, scare away snakes to avoid rolling down them, arm yourself with scissors to deal with vines. Let the race begin!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Волк идёт! ("The Wolf is Coming!") is a family board game in which all players will need to team up and throw a great birthday party for their friend Wolf. The recipe for such a party is known to everyone - cakes, candles, balls, gifts... But the Wolf is already going home, and the surprise is not ready! Squirrel, Badger, Fawn and Magpie need to hurry up, because if the Wolf comes to an empty house, he will be upset that his friends have forgotten about him. So they run through the forest, looking for goods and gifts - the main thing is to get ahead of the birthday boy and be the first to be at his house with everything you need.

The players take turns. On their turn, the player performs several actions in sequence:

     Moves the hero's standee.
     Performs an action with a forest token.
     Moved the Wolf standee one step closer to the house (this action is mandatory).


All the animals move on different numbers of steps and can carry different numbers of objects in their paws. They can also encounter various obstacles in the forest. In order to complete them, they need to perform various tasks.

The game will end when the Wolf reaches the house. If all is ready for the party, the team wins. If the party does not happen, the wolf will be disappointed. Unfortunately, in that case, the team loses.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Mit der Zeitmaschine in die Urwelt
description:
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach space 189 (genesis of earth) with his time machine wins the game.

The Game was published as a companion to the magazine "akut" (issue #1 from 25.01.1971).

A Lovecraftian Card Game by Hugo Bustilho from QG BUSTILHO where players summon monsters and investigators to have the greatest cult in Elder Rites.

Object
Build your Cult and perform Summon Rituals! To win you must have more Invocation Points at the end of the game. The first player to reach 21 Invocation triggers the end of the game.

Gameplay
The game is played in turns. The first player begins his turn, with one move passing to the next player on the left. 
In one turn, follow this order (some card effects you previously played may modify these steps).
1. Buy two cards from the principal line.
2. Make a Invocation or Play a card into your Cult or Pass.
3. Stay with 7 cards in your hand.

Um Jogo de Cartas Lovecraftiano de Hugo Bustilho do QG BUSTILHO onde jogadores incocam monstros e investigadores para ter o maior Culto em Ritos Ancestrais.

Objetivo
Construa seu Culto e realize Rituais de Invocação! Para vencer você deve ter mais Pontos de Invocação no final do jogo. O primeiro jogador a chegar a 21 de Invocação dispara o fim do jogo.

Gameplay
O jogo é jogado em turnos. O primeiro jogador começa seu turno, com uma jogada passando para o próximo jogador à esquerda. 
Em um turno, siga esta ordem (alguns efeitos de cartas que você  jogou anteriormente podem modificar essas etapas).

1. Compre duas cartas da linha principal de compras.
2. Faça uma Invocação ou jogue uma carta da sua mão no seu Culto ou Passe a vez.
3. Fique com no máximo 7 cartas na sua mão.

10 (5 in English, 5 in Spanish) new Cards Postal service decree.
These cards mix with those present in the basic games and bring new objectives.

Was available in pack E of the Dice Tower 2022 Kickstarter campaign.

boardgame: Wargaming in History: The American Civil War
description:
The purpose of this 'Wargaming in History' series is to offer, in detail, hints, tips, and advice on specific periods and wars that are wargaming favourites. All the necessary information, - campaigns, battles, weapons, tactics, etc - is included and the books are illustrated with tactical diagrams, maps, contemporary illustrations and photographs.

Brother against brother, family against family, in a long, drawn out, numbing, never-ending surge of battlefield drama - this was the American Civil War. At Gettysburg, "the enemy came on yelling and running with the fixed bayonet charge which few troops can withstand; but the patriots did not waver ... it was a melée, a carnival of death."

Now many carnivals of death spring to life in one of wargaming's greatest actions. Here is everything the warmer could possibly want: meticulous descriptions of Confederate and Union uniforms, artillery, cavalry units, rifles, and colorful battle flags, plus authentic terrain maps, combat photographs, rundowns on the strategies and tactics of such crucial battles as Vicksburg, Antietam, and Bull Run - even evaluations of army commanders for charisma, reliability, and aggression (Grant scored 22 out of a possible 30, Lee 28).

In a Union cavalry raid, see how campaigns are successful if objectives and aims are clearly defined and how the battles are won or lost by master manoueuvres or crucial failures.

The history, panoply, and roaring destruction of the Civil War, tabletop-style, are here. Charge!

—description from the publisher

description:
Reclaim is a flip-and-write game where players will be drawing polyominos on their board and drawing triangles within certain terrains of already placed shapes to pay the cost of the new shapes.

Once a shape has been entirely filled with such triangles, the triangles within that polyomino are shaded in.

After 10 rounds, an initial scoring happens, where players earn points based on the number of castles connected by paths of hollow triangles.  After a few more rounds, where the costs of placing a piece have doubled, players again score for connecting castles with hollow triangles but also the largest rectangle of shaded triangles (and castles). The player with the most points wins.

Create an mathematically inspired modular artwork by buying pieces of art to match the work you already created. To buy the pieces you have to bluff or buy wisely to get the most for your money. Watch out that you do not buy a counterfeit piece that doesn't fit in your artwork.

During the game, players construct a painting out of tiles, that are arranged in a 3x3 grid. Each turn, several of these tiles are revealed, and players bid on them using money cards. When a player wins a tile, they have to add it to their existing tiles in such a way that its edges matches the adjacent edges. If this cannot be done, the tile is considered counterfeit, and is not added to the painting, but kept aside.

Players now get new money cards depending on the value of the tile they gained during the bid, with tiles that are worth more points giving less money.

The game continues until someone completes their 3x3 painting. Then, players add together the point value of their tiles and subtract the point value of their counterfeit tiles. If only one player completed their painting, they get a bonus of two points. The player with the most points wins the game.

The march of technology across BattleTech’s eras is relentless—but some BattleMech designs never die. Each installment of this PDF-only series not only includes a brand new BattleMech or OmniMech, but also details Classic ’Mech designs from both the Inner Sphere and the Clans, now fully rebuilt with Dark Age technology (3085 and beyond).

Volume 26 Includes Rules and Record Sheets for:

• Condor Heavy Hover Tank (Ultra/Fusion/Laser/LAC/C)
• Maxim Heavy Hover Transport (Escort)
• Flea FLE-21
• Hermes II HER-7A
• Starslayer STY-4C
• Merlin MLN-1D/1E/1P/C
• Quickdraw QKD-9G
• Caesar CES-5R
• Naga II Prime/A/B/C/H/W

In this game 2-4 players team up with unicorn Milly Muffin to find the three butterflies hidden in magical trees and bring them back to the meadow.

Players take turns rolling a special die and according to the result move either the unicorn or the butterfly-hunting raccoon.

Whenever the unicorn lands on a space accompanied by a tree the turn player shakes that tree, then all players guess whether there's a butterfly or a dung beetle hidden inside.

Muffins found along the way can be used to distract the raccoon or take Milly to a tree of the player's choice.

The players win if they find all the butterflies and the unicorn reaches the meadow before the raccoon does.

boardgame: The Outsiders Expansion (fan expansion for Root)
description:
THE OUTSIDERS EXPANSION 
”Step outside the norm…”

See an unseen side of Root with the Outsiders Expansion, a collection of free fanmade content adding six new factions, a vagabond variant, a double-sided map board including varying modes of play and even an all-new deck of cards to the world of Root. In fact, there’s never been so many outrageous and totally unorthodox ways to play this game in one box before!

  — 6 Factions of Varying Complexity!  —
BONE PATROL!  A brash bunch of bullies with only one thing on the brain. Bones! Bones! Bones! Bones! Patrol the board, dig up those bones and get ‘em back to your stash, pronto! Good dog! (Moderate)
WINGED MENACE:  Agents of chaos which infest the board, swarm into clearings with strong numbers and make an absolute mess of the place (yuck) reveling in the screams of a terrorized Woodland. (Moderate)
ORDER of the FOREST:  An ancient sect of altruists, this faction of all factions is only looking to help. Can they help if witnessing their faith brings others to their cause, adding them into their own ranks? (High)
CROAKERS COVEN : Beware the abominous amphibians and their dark, board-warping magic. First, they turn your warriors to stone. Then they collect power from those statues for more insidious spells! (High)
THE TWELVE COLONIES : A two-party system of Libertarian reptiles, these snakes and turtles work together towards instituting a new form of goverment for every creature across all twelve clearings. (Very High)
OLD MAN TINKER: This bearded old beaver, a complete nutjob, bustles about the forest with his powerful trio of robots like a total mad man. Madder yet, he claims to have come from the future! (Very High)

 — A Vagabond Variant!  —
THE GROUCH! A sour and ill-tempered crow who’d just as soon sit at home in his trash can with his precious collection of fish heads as he would tell you to take a hike. Yikes! What a grouch!

 — A NeW Double-Sided Map!  —
DEEP WOODS MAP: Experience the harsh beginnings of your favourite Woodland by taking a trip into the distant past. This frontier themed map features a Settler mechanic that will force you into new, unexplored strategies. It also serves as a modular map for more everyday play!
WASTELANDS MAP: Experience the war-torn future of the Woodland, ravaged by generations of moving and battling and moving and battling. Every warrior and pawn on this board will be thirsty for action and, if you play it right, you’ll have just the Water you need to make it happen! Actions tied to Water Rations intensifies the clash on this barren wasteland of tomorrow.

 — A NeW Shared Deck of Cards!  —
UPSTARTS & RENEGADES DECK : 54 Cards with 22 New Craftable Improvements and 3 Unique Components for use with certain cards!

 (description from the gamebox)

boardgame: Dust 1947: Commissar Poon Hay-Wing Chinese Pla
description:
The World Revolution can only be successfully achieved through political purity and unfaltering courage! Political commissars of the Sino-Soviet Union make sure that front-line troops and those that command them follow every order to the letter. Every thought must be monitored before and during a battle, and there’s no-one better at this than Commissar Poon Hay-Wing of the People’s Liberation Army. He has spent so much time fighting against the invaders of his country that he can inspire greatness in all around him like nobody else. Commissar Poon doesn’t adapt to the area he’s fighting in, the area must adapt to him! He keeps his Commissar leather coat wherever he’s assigned to.

This miniature comes with its own Dust 1947 Unit Card.

Quipu is a party game where players write words on cards, hoping to have them spread-out on axis' created by another player.

The game involves using a push-pin to stick each answer onto the game board, and a quipu-inspired string for measuring the area surrounded by the players' answers.

The player with the most points after a number of rounds wins.

Collect ingredients from the board game until you have gathered all items indicated on your dessert card. But watch out! Mr. Gourmand will try to eat your ingredients and your opponents might rummage through your stash of food!

Goal of the game : Complete two dessert recipes. 
 To complete a recipe, you must collect the three basic ingredients indicated on your card.

How to play? Draw a card and move your pawn on the board the number of spaces indicated on the card to collect the ingredient associated with the square where you end your move. You can also choose to move your pawn and take a slimy fly waffles.

If you draw a Mr. Gourmand card, all players (including you) must feed Mr. Gourmand by putting one of their own ingredient on his stomach.

The third time Mr. Gourmand is drawn, he has eaten too
much and his belly explodes! The player who drew
Mr. Gourmand chooses an ingredient from
Mr. Gourmand’s belly.

If you have a slimy fly waffles, you can steal an opponent’s
ingredient. You can also use them to feed Mr. Gourmand to get a protection card (that will protect you from next Mr. Gourmand visit).

This board game asks children to think about what they need and how to get it. With the different elements of surprise, the game makes them laugh too! M. Gourmand is quick to install and play. And it's fun to play for all ages!

As a bonus, the 15 recipes of the game are available on the Internet to make them in real life. Ideal for prolonging family fun and feasting!

—description from the designer

Komorebi Parade is played over 2 phases, each consisting of a variable number of rounds, depending upon the player count. On a turn, a player drafts a tile from a public selection and places it on their player board.  The player then can move some tiles on their board depending upon the tile's movement rules.

At the end of the first phase, each player scores for the three public goals which were chosen during setup.  At the end of the second phase, the players again score for the three public goals, but also their private goal.

The player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Never Mind The Billhooks: Wargaming Late Medieval Small Battles and Big Skirmishes – Deluxe
description:
Billhooks is a fast-action game of late medieval European warfare, set at the small battle/big skirmish level – think Nibley Green rather than Towton.

You will need around 100 figures a side, lots of D6, and a 6 x 4 foot table – everything else you need to play the game is included in this book. A typical game can be played through in around 90 minutes (or rather less if the Dice Goddess wills it!).

Billhooks Deluxe gives you a ‘Core’ Wars of the Roses ruleset and then expands that to cover seven new Theatres and Conflicts from across Western European Christendom c.1350-1525. You will find troop stats and special rules for Irish Gallowglass, Hussite War Wagons, and Landsknecht Pikemen along with all the other troop types to be found on battlefields of the period, from Hundred Years’ War France to Renaissance Italy.

The game uses a card-driven turn sequence and simple but exciting combat mechanisms (with loads of dice being rolled!) to produce battles that are full of period flavour but always unpredictable.

The following are the Theatres and Conflicts in this book:


     Albion – The Wars of the Roses
     Gallia – The Hundred Years’ War
     Bohemia – The Hussite Wars
     Helvetia – The Swiss-Burgundian War
     Italia – The Italian Wars
     Northumbria – The Anglo-Scottish Border Reivers
     Lusitania – Late Medieval Portugal
     Hibernia – Warfare in Ireland


Finally, there is a Modelling Masterclass chapter with a comprehensive guide to painting, modelling, and kitbashing 28mm plastic figures.

The book is an A4, 180-page, full-colour, softback (perfect bound) publication.

—description from the publisher's website

description:
Zum is a trick-taking card game for 2-4 players, but up to 6 can play with one deck of cards. “The best of all for skill and fun”.

The game consists of Forty-eight playing cards, numbered 1 to 48, and 12 score cards. Eleven of the score cards bear a picture of the “Zum” Girl with the words “Score” or “Lose”. The twelfth score card is called the “Chance-It” card.

The object of the game is to capture the “Score” cards while forcing your opponents to take the “Lose” cards.

In Zum each player is dealt 12 playing cards. The 12 score cards are shuffled into a separate face up pile with only the top card visible. The winner of each trick (highest card) takes the top card from the score card pile. Scoring cards taken by players are kept face down until all of the playing cards have been played at which point each player counts their score.

Ignacy Lukasiewicz has just invented a way to convert oil into kerosene. The black gold fever begins in Galicia. With little capital, one begins to invest in oil parcels, build oil wells, merge into companies and try to be more successful than the competition.

The way to success is to invest in technology, because with inventions you can get ahead of your opponents. The one of you who has the most parcels, cash and patents for inventions at the end of the game will win. Are you ready to follow in the footsteps of Ignacy Lukasiewicz?

Łukasiewicz: Nafta i Gaz is a game for 2 to 5 players aged 12 or higher, which enables us to recreate the early days of the oil extraction in Galicia. Players incarnate entrepreneurs, who will compete for land, contracts and inventions. The most resourceful will emerge victorious.

The addictive bubble popping classic meets fast fun electronic gaming. 3 modes of gameplay, Including a clever multiplayer mode and a solo player blitz mode. Pop all the lit bubbles as soon as they appear, and then press the big button on the back to reset the bubbles before time runs out. Keep popping the bubbles fast to level up.

Troia! Una Guerra di Eroi is a Strategic  level board-wargame for two players or sides, which simulates the Trojan War.
 The two players respectively control the Achaeans with their allies and the Trojans with their allies. The game reproduces the last period of the war, with approximately monthly turns. The game map includes the entire area where the war was fought, namely the western coasts of Asia Minor and Thrace. The two armies were composed of a multitude of contiguous allies, coming from different regions of Greece and Asia Minor, represented in the game by combat units and Heroes. The Heroes have the names of the Homeric tradition, but can be thought of as chiefs of armed contingents of the Bronze Age. Players must ponder the use of their forces and the stratagems at their disposal to win the last battle: the conquest of the fortress of Troy.

Free for Para Bellum subscribers

artists: Franz Josef Karl Edler von Matsch

description:
This family-friendly game is fast-paced, simple to play yet strategic, and so much freaking fun!

House Pets vs Aliens is a play on the classic card game "War".

Each player commands their own army of House Pets or Aliens (or Plants or Robots with the expansion packs), battling for control of Earth.  Each army has 50 cards of varying numbers from 1 to 10.  Additionally there is a deck of Battle cards that further influence the game by increasing or decreasing values, preventing other players from playing cards, stealing played cards, etc.  A round consists of each player flipping a card from their deck.  Players can then play battle cards or draw additional cards from their deck with the goal of being closest to 10 without going over.  In the event of a tie where multiple players have the same number close to 10, or 10, then each tied player draws a single card from their deck and the highest card wins.  If the newly drawn card results in another tie, keep drawing cards until their is no tie and then whomever had the highest last drawn card wins.  Winners of a hand take all the cards played by all players and put them in a second stack by their deck.

Play continues as the above until one player deck of 50 has been depleted.  Then players count up the number of cards collected.  Highest amount wins.

RIZE is a fun and challenging memory game for 1-4 players in which players try to remember their cards and race to get rid of them first.

To play, quickly memoRIZE your cards, turn them face-down, and play one that matches the color, number, or shape on the face-up card in the center. The first person to play all their cards wins. But be careful, because you’ll be penalized if you choose the wrong one. And as you draw more cards, it gets even harder to remember what’s in front of you.

Throughout the game, use special cards to gain advantages or thwart your opponents as you work to be the first player with no cards left.

-description from the publisher

Ariel, Moana, Belle and Tiana are excited to join the other princess for afternoon tea at the castle, but the other princess have got lost in the forest! Help find her so they can be reunited again and enjoy a magical afternoon together.

How to play the game; roll the dice and race around the enchanted landscape. Search under the decorative sculpts and then race to the finish space when you've found them and win their cards.

—description from the publisher

Vines is a suite of more than 20 original games and variants with a unique 5-suited deck. This suite of games has been more than five years in the making, and we are thrilled to finally bring you the finished deck with custom artwork!

The Vines deck has 55 cards in five suits, with stunning artwork by Cheyenne Wright, and the usual meticulous attention to detail and unnecessary backstory that you have come to expect from Crab Fragment Labs.  Games played with the Vines deck include Coralon, Thief, Ducks and Geese, Bishop, Baronet, Chevalier, Up and Down, and Farmer's Market.

The Postas deck is a forerunner of the same deck, used for a different family of games: Prima Carta, Holdout, and Lobo. The decks are sort of interchangeable, being 55-card five-suited decks, but each is better matched to its own set of games.

Players are about to start a trail running race, taking place in a mountain valley.

First player to reach the finish line wins.

In the valley, there are different terrains : forest, grass, rocks and snow. Throughout the race, players will have to choose their own path according to their own abilities on each terrain.

They must manage their effort : tiredness will slow them down.

Players can improve their abilities, restore their stamina and slow down their opponents through playing cards, picked at aid stations.

There are 3 game modes : 

     competitive (2-4 players)
     2vs2
     solo

Help Raven and her friends prepare for the next seasonal festival!

You must help your character friend gather and organize their books, candles, crystals and ingredients so that they will be ready for the magical night. Can you collect all your ingredients before the others? All it takes is a bit of magic!

—description from the publisher

Adds 18 cards to the original Wiccats card game including:
Characters (Lily, Trix, Laluna)
Items (9 additional components)
Spells (Curse, Steal)
Oops! (Lost, Soaked, Dropped!)
Aura Cards (Mystery)

Aura cards add a new way to play! Your aura is strong, and it will protect you. Put an aura card into play to protect against a Spell or 'OOPS!' card.

Adds 18 cards to the original Wiccats card game including:
Characters (Jinx, Rosey, Naga)
Items (9 additional components)
Spells (Beautiful Festival, Recharge)
Aura Cards (Courage, Peace, Love, Vision)

Aura cards add a new way to play! Your aura is strong, and it will protect you. Put an aura card into play to protect against a Spell or 'OOPS!' card.

I’m not a human is a fast-paced game where the AI-generated artwork meets the human ingenuity. One of you takes on the role of a human pretending to be a robot in the world of AI supremacy. Relying on other players’ associations they try to find the image password for the secret AI site… And even if the human is at a loss they still can outsmart the robots and win!
Before the game players lay down pictures in a 3X3 grid and give each card a number plate from 1 to 9. Then they choose one random bag with cards and deal them to everyone according to the rules.
One player may become human and all other robots will know what picture is the correct one.
One by one robot players name their associations to show other players that they are robot and to confuse the human. Human tries to pass of as a robot.
When all players named their associations human may try to guess the correct picture. If he is not doing that all players explain their associations and vote on who's the human.

boardgame: Krimi total Fall 18: Der Fall einer Lady
description:
Bad news for the family of Lady Amelia Bryson: Although she spent her in time in the mountains of Switzerland, her medical condition got worse dramatically. So her doctor determines to summon the family for her last days.
The family and their companions meet at a secluded house in a swiss mountain village after an long and onerous way. A severe thunderstorm hit the town. What a bad omen: Lady Amelia just dies before dawn.
Now all the attendees sit together to resolve the circumstances of her death.

Every player gets new information about the case in each round. An open discussion follows after reading them in your personal booklet. Some hidden information in envelopes may also help to find the murderer.

In 1812: The Retreat the players are captains of a company leading their soldiers out of Russia during Napoleon's catastrophic campaign of 1812. The players must survive the retreat by making it safely to Vilnius or the Polish border. A soldier that loses all his strength dies on the side of the road or freezes at the bivouac and is disqualified. They can also be captured by the Cossacks. At the end of the game, each soldier is worth a number of victory points. The player with the most points wins the game...but remember: there WILL be casualties!
1812 is a resource management/survival game very high in theme. It is fresh, daring and cutthroat...

The game is played in 3 phases consisting of a number of rounds: From Moscow to Smolensk, from Smolensk to the Berezina and from the Berezina to Vilnius. At the end of each phase, benefits lie in wait for the soldiers in the vanguard arriving at the location first. At the end of each round, a main event card will be drawn which will have consequences for all the soldiers on the board: this can be cold, snow, an attack of the cossacks or maybe a village in the distance that can be explored...

All players have a number of soldiers under their control: an officer, a musician, light infantry, heavy cavalry... Each of them have their own advantages. The loss of any of your soldiers during the game will always have consequences for your company for the remainder of the retreat.             


—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Tales of the Unsolved: The Blackthorn Manor
description:
The Blackthorn Manor is an Adventure Puzzle Book by Tales of the Unsolved. Solve a puzzle in the book, the answer of which is called a "Key". Those Keys act as clues and will be needed moving forward to solve later puzzles. Work your way through the book, solve the puzzles, and unfold the mystery that surrounds this seemingly abandoned house.

The Blackthorn Manor is a 154 page physical book with 63 puzzles to solve. Will you dare to enter and manage to solve them all?

Each puzzle is unique, and will test your ability to think outside the box. Numbers, ciphers, pattern recognition and manipulation of the book - fold, search, tear, draw and more, can all prove critical to your progress.

For some puzzles, you will be requested to visit a URL where the puzzle leaves the book and comes to life in a 3d environment thematic to the room in which you are in the story. Not only will you need to think outside the box to solve the puzzle, you will need to explore the rooms you are in to find the clues to do so.

A travelling and quiz game with roundabout 6000 questions from Upper Swabia.

You roll a die and travel to six target cities in Upper Swabia answering questions on each stop and then return to your starting city. For each correctly answered question you receive points. 
The one with the most points wins.

—user summary

A pack of 12 cards containing 5 Bureaucrat cards, 1 reference card, and 6 base cards.

The Bureaucrat replaces the Duke, and has the Cooperation ability of the Farmer in G54. Cooperation lets you take 3 coins from the Treasury, but you must give one of those coins to another player.

This promo was available as part of the KickStarter edition of Coup: Rebellion G54.
Spielbox magazine issue 2/2013 published only the oracle card.

boardgame: Кто украл репку? (Who Stole the Turnip?)
description:
You play detective chickens who are looking for turnip thief. All seven suspects are depicted on the game board this is: grandfather, grandmother, granddaughter, dog, cat, mouse and the turnip itself. Next to each suspect lies a round token, on the underside of which it is indicated, who is this character: innocent, kidnapper or accomplice.
Chickens walk around the game board, collecting cards of the investigation: clues, signs and witnesses. If you got a card, you can go to the suspect, present it, tell why it points to this character, and look at his token in secret. When you find the kidnapper, you will have to come to him and bring charges: a motive card and three matching consequence cards. Tell why the attacker stole the turnip, how do you managed to figure him out and whether he had an accomplice. If you do everything right you will win.

26-Across is a multiplayer crossword game using a board that is either:

     a single magnetic board and 26 magnetic A-Z alphabet tiles with point values
     a dry erase form and marker.


Solo or multiplayer modes.

It is played solo using either a web app or preprinted challenges.

SOLO GOAL: Beat the predetermined score of each challenge.

MULTIPLAYER GOAL: Win three rounds.

CONCEPT: Using just the 26 A-Z alphabetic letters, form as many crossed words as possible and score the result.

SETUP:

Each player grabs the necessary items as indicated above.

MULTIPLAYER GAME PLAY:

At the start of each round, the next player calls out a 4- or 5-letter word as the first "seed" word.
The word can only comprise unique letters (as duplicates are not possible), for example GAME but not FUNNY.
All players place the letters of the seed word anywhere on their form, left to right or top to bottom.
Players have three minutes to play the round.

Players form crosswords using just the remaining letters.
Players can rearrange letters as needed to maximize their score.
Players are all playing simultaneously in secret.

At the end of the time period, players score their words and show their boards for all players to validate.
Orphaned letters and words must be removed.
Only interlinked crosswords are scored.

The player with the highest score wins the round and gains one point. If tied, no one wins the round.

Players continue to play additional rounds until someone has won three rounds.

SOLO GAME PLAY:
Review a challenge and place the indicated letters on the board in their designated spots. Place as many of the remaining tiles to form crosswords. Score the connected words. If the score meets or exceeds the target score, you win the challenge.

Mist is a trick-taking game for exactly 4 players using a standard deck of 52 cards, but for which you can see the suits on the rear of the cards.

Each player's hand begins face down in front of them with a chip on it.  During the game, players can remove chips from their cards, 2 at a time, to look at their values; however, they must remember not to forget the value, as they cannot look at it again.  The chips which are removed in this way will be used as a player's bid.

Chips remaining on cards which are played to tricks form a pool which is given to the player who wins a trick.  Chips acquired in this way are used to convert a player's unplayed card to a trump card.

The player with the most points after 4 rounds wins.

In "The Tournament" of Dungeons & Procrastination, you play as an enchanter participating in a summoning tournament. The arena's stands are packed with spectators!

Estimate the power of the opponent's summons in a tense face-off and cast a support summons if necessary, or would you rather bluff your way through a stunt? 
That's when the dice are rolled, these 6-sided artifacts will grant your team its full power! 
Which of the enchanters will win this tournament? 
During each round, you must discard your opponent's cards and place them in your "Champions" pile with the highest score.

A round consists of 5 phases:

     The Duel
     Summoning Support 
     Artifact Throwing (Dice) 
     Combat Resolution 
     End of Round 


During this rounds each player will secretly choose a card from their hand as fighter and may add a support. Each card have a value and a special effect that can lead to victory!

Then each player will roll a 6-sided die and add the value of the die to their card score.

If a player has played a Support card during the round they will roll an additional die and add the value of that die to their score.

The player with the highest value sends the opponent's card(s) to their "Limbo" pile, knocked out, face down. They places their winning cards in their "Champions" pile, face up.

Then each player draw a card of their deck and adds it to their hand.

The game ends when a player has no more cards to play at the beginning of a round.

The player with the most cards in their Champions pile wins the game!

-description from designer
A hundred new cards for Kampf gegen das Spießertum around topics like conspiracy theories, COVID and esoteric

boardgame: Kampf gegen das Spiessertum: Tabubruch Erweiterung
description: A hundred new cards for Kampf gegen das Spiessertum with very dark humor, compared to the base game.

Capital Campaign is a heavy worker placement/tableau-building game featuring variable abilities, unique worker roles, a rapidly-escalating scale of play, and many paths to victory.

The signature mechanism that drives the game is gathering and planning out your resource management within a limited number of turns before they are gone and you start the next year fresh.

Capital Campaign allows players to immerse themselves in a deep, rich world of philanthropy and doing the most good to come out on top.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Chivalry is Dead: Race for the Crown
description:
Chivalry is Dead: Race for the Crown is a Fantasy Chariot Racing Game where the goal is to claim the crown!

Select from one of four unique teams, customize your chariots, and strive to be the first to enter the winners' circle by playing a 1 or 2 lap race. Whether you employ cunning tactics, set clever traps, or launch direct assaults on your adversaries, ensure your triumph in this exhilarating contest where every move counts. Chariots will be destroyed, and debris will be left on the field.

Each player builds a chariot team of 2-3 chariots using one of four unique teams: Humans, Goblins, Dwarves or Kobolds. Each Team has their own stats and abilities. Add an additional 3 teams with the expansion. Customize the track using Terrain Markers around the map. Build your chariot faster, tougher or more defensive. Race, attack other chariots, throw traps or simply outmaneuver your opponents to win the race.

—description from the publisher

This is a quirky card game, where you get a maximum of 20 questions to guess what or who your opponent is.

They could be an animal, vegetable, mineral or something completely random like a stapler or maybe a person like Harry Potter. It all depends on what they choose to be from the card they've picked.

It sounds easy doesn't it, but don't be fooled, as the person can only answer your questions with a Yes or No. Choose your questions wisely as twenty questions will soon fly by or you might be lucky and guess the answer in less!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Manipulate and cooperate your way to dominance of the world’s first bourgeoning democracy in Ancient Athens.

The players aim to score the most points by successfully fulfilling the requirements of their secret Agendas and by building Public Works.

These secret Agendas dictate which Policies the player will want to pass or dismiss.

In order to pass or dismiss a Policy, players vote by spending Influence, which they draw in their turn, and Drachma, which can be used as bribes when voting on Policies or to fund Projects.

Once 7 Policies have been passed the game ends. The themes of these 7 Passed Policies dictate whether a player has been successful or unsuccessful in fulfilling their Agendas. Players then score Victory Points for any Agendas they have successfully fulfilled. Players lose points for any Agendas they have not fulfilled.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Detecteam Kids: Max the Cat аnd Friends
description:
Max the Cat has the best friends in the world! Together they have so much fun, and every day is filled with the most amazing adventures! What will they come up with today? Will they set up an obstacle race, prepare a birthday surprise, or cook up some yummy treats? However, there are so many things that could go wrong... But that’s what friends are for!

Max the Cat and Friends is a co-operative adventure for young detectives. Your goal is to piece the full picture, just like a jigsaw puzzle, and re-create the events. Find out what actually happened in this stand-alone game from the Detecteam Kids range!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ballads & Tales: Journey of the Brave
description:
Ballads & Tales is an atmospheric dungeon-crawling board game for 1-5 players. Up to 4 players can enjoy the game in solo/coop mode. Creatures are divided into three groups, each with their own behavioral patterns, so they can act cleverly and threateningly. Adventure cards offer thrilling discoveries and surprises, with no game master needed.

An additional player can take control of the evil creatures in game master mode to try and defeat the heroes!

Dangerous times have fallen over the Empire of Bromyr. The noticeboards are full of urgent quests: Defeat hordes of creatures, find magical artifacts, face challenging bosses, break mysterious time loops, or remain undiscovered in exciting stealth missions. Help villagers in their time of need and survive the deadly Corruption in infected areas. The base game contains 15 individual adventures and a wide selection of creatures.

The extensive travel mode is part of the Core Box. In this mode, Heroes travel all over the Duchy of Wennheim, across the included map in the style of a sandbox world. Their goal is to enter 6 antique dungeons and to activate the artifacts hidden within. 12 additional adventures are randomly generated. The included Travel cards influence the heroes’ journey with many random events, such as these: A bounty has been posted on the heads of dangerous boss monsters. A quarantine prevents you from accessing a specific village. Homunculi occupy all bridges and keep you from traveling onwards.

At the same time, heroes will need to keep an eye on the weight of their luggage and eat regularly, so they don’t start their next adventure in a weakened state. To increase their maximum load, heroes can buy a donkey.

The artwork of Ballads & Tales is a special highlight. Traditional artist Valbona Atanaskovska (Bona), who earned a degree from the Art Academy of Skopje (North Macedonia) in 1997, has painted all characters in oil on canvas. In addition, she has produced oil paintings of the different cover designs, as well as all objects on the cards. She has also created all drawings for event and spell cards, so the characters are all in the same style.

This game is the card game with the Japanese slot.
The Japanese slot "Pachisuro" is totally different with the normal slot.
The players need some knowledge before playing the slot. For example, there are any staging for the Jackpot.
And players need to stop the reel by three buttons, and must need to aim the same icons.
Is it "777"? No, you need to know the special icons, the players read the books for it.

Of course, it's not all. You need more. And their knowledge is for each slot.

The players try to score more.
The cards are drawn the reel with both side. And players place three cards like a slot.
And check each line.

If there is "777", then the game changes to the Bonus time.
The player can use the card with the back side. The back side is drawn more 7.
However the Bonus time is limited.
When you don't get the bonus, it changes to the normal mode.

This game is the solo game, and try to score more.
But the players play it by comparing with others.

Mini Expansion for Yin Yang. Consists of four tiles that players can collect by visiting the Emperor in the Capital, and which gives you certain privileges.

This was part of the Chinese crowdfounding version, but has also been given out at Essen and at other conventions.

boardgame: トリテ富豪 (Trick-Taking x Career Poker)
description:
トリテ富豪 is a game that combines "Trick Taking" and "Millionaire".

The game uses a deck of cards with ranks and suits, but each card also shows an animal which acts as a second suit.  During the trick-taking phase, players who play off-suit to a trick will take their cards back and they are placed into that player's hand for the next phase; the winner takes the remaining cards into their hand for the next phase. Some cards have special effects and the winner of each trick obtains a yellow cube.

If a player collected no cards for their millionaire hand, the game ends prematurely.  Otherwise, the players take their new hands and play a climbing game. The following possibilities are intricate, due to the game's card distribution and two-suit nature.

The game has a number of unique elements.  For instance:


    The start player of each trick (for both halves) can choose the direction of play for each trick.
    In the millionaire phase, players can only play a single card until "energy bundles" are created.  These go into a central pool and can be spent by any lead player to trigger a round of playing pairs, triple, etc.

Five hordes of wild boars are fighting to conquer "La Granda" (the province of Cuneo)

Blagheur, Viton, Bojanen, Patachin & Baròt: which of them will occupy all eight zones and give preminence to its recipe for "bagna caoda"?

Game elements include cards, dice, secret objectives.

Having to spend most of their time in the sewers, the lives of Tokyo's disreputable Poo Divers consistently suck. The lowest caste of all underdogs sits in the Shinjuku honky-tonk as they do every night after work, digesting their lost time. Delirious thoughts waft through cesspools, spilling into an endless nirvana of drunken stupor and hubris. The only fixed point of their blank stares is the smudged cup with the stomach ulcering mixed drinks of Don’t Ask and Definitely Don’t Ask. A hot, stuffy, yet wild vibe drools through the humid air. It drools saliva that the divers inhale and excrete again as armpit sweat.

The divers brag about their precious finds from Tokyo's sewers. In drunkenness, yellow slime hardens into gold ingots and rat poison mutates into cocaine. Or was there really a giant diamond that a courier frantically shat out shortly before the raid? What is the truth about the silver coins that Akito once claimed to have found? And did he really exist, that mummified politician Tanaka slurs about every night?

Two to four players get up close and personal with the secret society of Poo Divers. Take part in their stuffy discoveries and, like them, rejoice in every bit of dirt. In this world, all finds have equal value. But take the advice of a wise Poo Diver and keep your glued eyes open, lest some greedy asshole snatch the loot from under your nose.

Dragon Ride is a party game.
Each player in turn spin the card which must rotate on itself at least once.
If the meeple is in one of the designated areas after the ride, then you are safe and you can continue playing to the next round, otherwise you are eliminated.
If none remain, there are no human winners (the dragon wins).

—description from designer

BOOMA is a roll & write solitaire game where you will have to manage the workforce to catch the giant snake BOOMA that is ravaging the colony on planet Gliese 3323 b.
You can build electrified walls or generators, create bait or just bomb BOOMA and its clones.

Game Round
Rounds are played in three phases:
- Commander turn
- BOOMA turn
- Advancement on the tracks

During commander turn, perform these steps in order:
1. Roll commander dice
2. Assign values (drawing one of the available symbol on an area, writing the number showing the dice inside the drawing)

During BOOMA turn, perform these steps in order:
1. Roll BOOMA bonus dice and trigger the corresponding effect
2. Move BOOMA and its clone (usually to the adjacent 
orthogonally area with the highest value)
3. Clone growth: If the strength of the clone is less than that of BOOMA, then add 1 to the strength of the clone

Note that BOOMA and its clone are represented by two dice showing the value of the current strength.

You win in case BOOMA and its clone no longer have the 
possibility to move.
You lose as soon as the second city area is devastated.

-description from designer

Your bed is floating in an ocean! Your dog turned into a zombie! How will you survive? To play, the judge for the round draw a scenario card and reads the ridiculous scenario out loud. The other players race to grab object chips! Will that toilet bowl help you? What about the pogo stick? Once you have your chips, you’ll have to stretch your imagination to create your own survival plan. Practice improvisation and make your plan clever, creative or funny – whatever it takes to convince the judge to award you survival rations. How will you defeat a giant llama with hot sauce? Or will you survive lunch-lady ninjas with a bucket of worms? This wild and weird think-on-your-feet game will have your kids in fits of giggles! And whoever has the most survival rations at the end of the game wins!


     Create your best survival plan with only your brain power and whatever 3 wacky objects you manage to grab!
     Kids practice creative thinking, problem-solving and improvisation
     Fun party game for kids and adults
     For 4 or more players
     Includes 55 double-sided object chips, 80 survival scenario cards, 75 scoring rations, 30-second timer and instructions


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Sláine: Kiss My Axe! Starter Set
description:
Sláine: Kiss My Axe! is a skirmish tabletop miniatures battle game for two or more players set in the fantasy world created by the legendary comic creators Pat Mills and Angela Kincaid. Axes and swords clash whilst Earth and Sour magic lash out across the Land of the Young, as the Earth Tribes of the goddess Danu battle the decaying Drune Lords and the relentless Fomorian Sea Demons.

In Sláine, players take control of a small band of Earth Tribe, Drune, or Fomorian warriors as they fight for glory and survival in the mist-shrouded lands of Tir Nan Og and beyond. This starter set contains scenery, tokens, cards and all the rules you need to play, including scenarios, advanced rules and profile lists to create your warbands such as the heroes of the Earth Tribes and Drune Lords included in this set and much more...

—description from the publisher
