description:
Your rideshare is parked somewhere nearby, but the app is malfunctioning! Text messages are going crazy, and the driver can respond only with emojis. You're left asking yourself one simple question: Where's my ride?

In Where's My Ride?, a co-operative game of deduction and miscommunication for 2-7 players, you and your friends are divided into a party of riders and a single driver who are trying to meet up for a rideshare. There's a big problem, though! The app is bugging out big time; it's not sharing rider or driver locations, and the messaging features have gone haywire. Riders will use a deck of quirky, autofilled text messages to ask the driver where they are parked. Unfortunately, the driver can react to the messages only with emojis! Will you find your driver before the app cancels the ride and gives everyone a dreaded 1-out-of-5 star rating!?!

Guess the code and get rich! The Monopoly Secret Vault board game for kids 8+ features Mr. Monopoly's vault filled with valuable items that give players more wealth. Begin by filling the vault with riches. Each time a player unlocks it and gets what's inside, it gets refilled with more valuables. Which means there are always plenty of riches to unlock! To open the vault, players guess the code, which is a random number from 1 to 6. Be sure to pay attention to numbers already guessed! In this exciting family board game for 2-6 players, each time the vault is opened, the code randomly resets. Along with Monopoly Money, there are hotels that players can only find in the vault, plus keys to locked properties. The player with the most money at the end of the game wins!

MONOPOLY GAME WITH VAULT: In the Monopoly Secret Vault board game, Mr. Monopoly gives players chances to take riches from his vault -- if they can guess the code to unlock it

CRACK THE CODE AND GET RICH: Players buy properties and collect rent, but in this edition players try to guess the codes to open the vault as many times as possible

FIND MONEY, HOTELS, AND KEYS TO LOCKED PROPERTIES: Inside the vault, find money, hotels, and cardboard keys that allow players to unlock properties

THEMED MONOPOLY TOKENS: Features tokens including Scottie, Top Hat, T-Rex, Hazel. Racecar, and Rubber Ducky -- all designed to look like they're ready to break into the vault

ENTERTAINING FAMILY GAME: The Monopoly Secret Vault board game for 2-6 players is fun for adults, teens, and kids ages 8 and up. It’s a great choice for family game nights, parties, and get-togethers

—description from the publisher

What stories we believe were fairy tales might indeed be real after all…  We received word of a change to history right in the heart of Germany’s Black Forest and it appears to be something right out of the pages of a storybook.  You always had a Grimm Wish… to have your own happily ever after… but it appears that comes at a cost.  The woods are filled with wonders.  A talking frog begs for a kiss and a tower with hair dangling below waits for you, but there also is danger.  The big, bad wolf stalks you from the shadows, while clever witches tempt you with sweets.  If you are to survive these woods, you might need to brush up on your fairy tales and avoid straying too far from the path.  After all, this Grimm Wish might not be everything you thought it would be…

About Escape the Crate escape room boxes:
Each box has over a dozen unique puzzles which all fit the theme. Every item in the box has a purpose and will help you escape in time. Clue are hidden everywhere and in this game you need to think inside the box. Each box is a complete story.

These missions can be challenging and it is very possible you won't escape before the time limit. You can always reset the game using our instructions or you can continue playing past the time limit to see the mission to completion.
Every challenge you will face will have a set of hints, which you can choose to reveal when your team needs them. If you are completely stumped, even after the hints, the solution is also provided.

Note: You will need a fully charged computer or mobile device to play this escape room.

The time has come to sail!  In this game, we have 2 separate missions in one box.  In the first, you can join a Viking crew as they set sail into the fog on a journey to the mythical lands to the west.  The seas are rough and storms threaten to send you to Valhalla, but if you manage to find your way, a land of riches awaits.  Be careful, though, as some of the crew are planning a mutiny and you might just be caught in the crosshairs.  The second game, is a bit darker than the first.  A rival chieftain has his eyes set on your village and he has sent warriors to pillage your home.  It will be up to you to defend your village at all costs.  Set traps for the invaders, hide your treasures, and prepare to face them head-on.  The choice is yours.  Both missions are available at the start.  Will you bring glory to your people or will you find yourself lost to history?

boardgame: Close Action 3rd Edition: Scenario Starter Pack
description: This module provides 7 ready-to-use Scenarios for Close Action [CA] 3rd Edition.  These scenarios are quicker to set up and play than those in other CA scenario books. The scenarios have been carefully selected from among the hundreds of actual battles from 1739 to 1815 to provide a set of interesting small actions; the first six can be played by no more than 4 players, while the 7th requires a minimum of 5. This selection was done deliberately to serve the very large percentage of wargamers who live in communities with only small gaming groups (perhaps as few as a half-dozen players or so). It has the added benefit of also serving new players and those who simply prefer smaller battles. Other key factors include: (a) an emphasis on balanced battles where victory is usually determined simply by victory points, rather than by special victory rules; (b) a selection of different types of battles, from frigate-vs-frigate actions to Ship-of-the-Line [SOL] squadron actions to combinations of both types; and (c) all ship logs for all seven scenarios have been completely pre-filled to allow you to play them all immediately, without having to fill them out yourself.

description:
Get ready for an unforgettable adventure throughout Chile! Discover and collect postcards of its culture, flora & fauna, cities and history. Manage your ‘chauchas’ and build your itinerary strategically, to achieve the most spectacular postcard collection of Chile’s immense natural and cultural wealth. Compete with your family and friends to be rewarded with the highest amount of ‘Copihues’ at the end of your journey. Embark in this adventure through unimaginable landscapes, delight yourself with Chile’s delicious typical meals, and tour the most majestic parks and pristine reserves. Sign-up for various tours, and expand your collection to become the most intrepid Backpacker!

—description from the publisher (translated)

¡Prepárate para la aventura en un inolvidable viaje a lo largo de Chile! conoce y colecciona su cultura, flora y fauna, ciudades e historia. Embárcate en esta travesía por paisajes fuera de serie, deléitate con ricos platos típicos y recorre los mas majestuosos parques y prístinas reservas. Apúntate en diversos tours y expande tu colección para competir por ser el mas intrépido Mochilero.

—description from the publisher

The other agents who have been assigned this mission have all vanished.  It has become a bit of a curse and now it is your turn to answer the call.  We have all heard of vampires, but very few of us actually believe in them.  Bram Stoker did and he set his famous novel in the very area you are set to explore... for good reason.  Your mission is quite simple.  You need to enter this rotting castle and search for evidence of the missing agents.  Recover and rescue any survivors, but be careful.  The communications we have received from inside tell of creatures with fangs and who only rise at night.  If that is the case, you might need to brush up on your vampire hunting skills before entering.  This game is divided into 2 chapters.  The first is a spooky, family friendly adventure through the crypts and halls of the castle.  The second is a more PG-13 escape from the creatures you have disturbed from their slumber.

Three precious jewels have been stolen from history by our enemy, Mr. X, and have been locked up safely in the Royal Museum.  These need to be returned to their correct time periods, or else a time paradox might occur.  We need you to do the impossible… break into one of the most well-guarded museums on the planet.  We have a team assembled to help, but it will ultimately be up to you to find your way into the grounds, pass the security measures, and nab those jewels.  Along the way, you might come across dinosaur skeletons, Egyptian relics, and priceless works of art…all of which hold clues to help you in your heist.  Be careful, though.  Just getting to the jewels is not enough.  Once you have them in hand, you will need to find a way out of the museum, as alarms inevitably wail.  Can you pull off the heist of the century?

Hungry daring chameleons battle each other to see who is the best hunter. They select five hunting grounds in the tropical forest where they will try to catch as many insects as possible. Each of the five hunting grounds makes them change colour because of the tropical fauna that grows there. At the end of the day, the chameleon who has caught and eaten the most insects wins the battle. But watch out! Flying eagles in the forest are a threat to the hunting chameleons, unless they can hide underneath a tropical leaf. Transform into a colour changing chameleon yourself in this amusing dice game and become the best hunter of the forest!

The Flashback cruise was intended to be a way to revisit the '90s in fun ways that kids from back in the day would describe as "all that and a bag of chips." Some of the most popular child celebrities from the '90s, all managed by Jake Murray, were scheduled to be aboard for a week of sunny nostalgia, discussions about which flip phone was better, and whether or not you prepped for Y2K. When Jake Murray is found dead aboard the ship, all indications point to one of his celebrity clients as the killer. Unfortunately, you are one of those celebrities, and are implicated in the crime by the fact that something belonging to you was used as the murder weapon. Because jurisdiction out on the open water is tricky, you have a few hours before the Ladybird is due back at port to find out who the real killer is and expose them for the criminal they are. Can you Flashback to an era of slap bracelets, Furbies, and floppy disks and find out which of your '90s celebrity friends is really a cold-blooded murderer?

As with all Deadbolt Mystery Society games, you step into the role of the detective. Examining the evidence provided, visiting different locations and using the password protected QR mechanic to uncover the truth.

Players win once they catch the killer, find what's missing or whatever else is needed to solve the case at hand.

Members of an archeological team set out to the jungles of Mexico in search of an ancient Mayan artifact known only in legends. For the members of the group who have dreamt of this sort of thing their entire lives, it feels like the dig of a lifetime. Or, at least it does, until members of the party begin disappearing during the night. The jungle has held secrets for thousands of years. Is the key to the vanishings just one more mystery that the jungle is hiding? Are the disappearances the work of man or could some of the darker Mayan myths actually be true?

Difficulty Rating – 6/10

Players win once they catch the killer, find what's missing or whatever else is needed to solve the case at hand.

An old friend, Simon Newall, owner of Retro Corner Arcade, reaches out to you to help solve a mystery that his father left behind, hidden in secret levels of certain arcade machines. You agree without hesitation, eager to reconnect with one of your childhood buddies. However, your hopes of a reunion are dashed when you arrive at the arcade and learn from his wife that Simon was killed during a burglary gone wrong. As one last favor for an old friend, you decide to do as Simon asked and figure out what secret was worth hiding in such a strange way. Are you ready to play? If so, Insert Coin!

Difficulty Rating – 6/10

The Scourge is an underground organization of serial killers that has secretly plagued Valley Falls from the shadows. Although there have been mentions of them from time to time, they have been dormant for the past couple of years. That all changes when an officer with the Valley Falls Police Department comes across a jar containing 3 human hearts. Minutes after the officer radios in the grotesque findings, a courier delivers a package containing a disturbing VHS tape. Your friend, Phil Herbert with VFPD, gives you a call to immediately bring you in on the case because you were so helpful during Duel, the previous Scourge case. Phil mentions Anubis, The Scourge, and some type of initiation, and your mind immediately goes back to the serial killer competition that gripped the city a few years earlier. Will this be something similar? You pray that isn't the case as it will undoubtedly mean bloodshed on a wide scale. With a deep breath, you grab your things and head to the police station to get the details. What is The Scourge up to this time? Find out in this newest Scourge mystery…Initiation!

S.T.A.L.K. is the premiere group of ghost hunters in Valley Falls.  You will assume the role of Kristina Noble, a local paranormal investigator who comes from a long line of psychics and clairvoyants.  You are being considered for a spot in the group because of your passion for the supernatural and your investigative skills.  While on a weekend camping trip in Easthaven Forest, you and the members of S.T.A.L.K. stumble across an unmarked grave deep in the woods.  Because opportunities like this don't happen every day, the group decides to try and use paranormal investigation techniques to determine the identity of the body and learn what happened.  Investigate The Unmarked Grave and follow wherever the trail leads.  But, be warned!  Easthaven Forest is known for its multitude of secrets.  Who knows what dark truths you will discover once you drag those into the light?

Vacations are rare for the Will Street Detective Agency staff, but only a fool would pass up the opportunity to spend a week in Cape Anna, a sleepy, seaside town on the northeast coast.  The trip is a fringe benefit of a speaking engagement you had earlier in the day at a symposium on modern day crime-solving techniques.  Now that you've given your presentation, you are free for the rest of the week to do nothing but sample all the fresh seafood, bask in the warm sunshine, and revel in the complete lack of responsibility. The activity at the top of your “to-do” list is to stop by the town’s Founder’s Festival.  You've read a lot of interesting stories about the festival and the town itself and think it would be a shame to miss this opportunity.  You reconsider your position on the matter when you arrive at the festival and see an all too familiar site at the end of a row of tents - police tape. The stark yellow tape seems out of place in such an idyllic locale.  As you move closer, you see two men wearing Murphree County Coroner shirts carrying a black body bag.  The sheriff’s deputies who are attempting to investigate the scene seem to be in over their heads. The head detective on the case, Ryan Clark, recognizes you from the symposium and informs you that they've never seen a case quite like this before.  Given your expertise, Clark asks if you’d like to help them on this investigation.  He promises all the lobster rolls and blueberry pie you can eat!  How can you say no to that?  Pull back the curtain and examine the dark heart that beats beneath this sleepy, seaside town.  Can you solve the Murder at Cape Anna?

As with all Deadbolt Mystery Society games, you step into the role of the detective. Examining the evidence provided, visiting different locations and using the password protected QR mechanic to uncover the truth.

Players win once they catch the killer, find what's missing or whatever else is needed to solve the case at hand.

The silence of a quiet night at Valley Falls Dispatch is shattered when a frantic call comes in. The man on the line says he is being chased and rambles about something that happened in the past with him and a group of friends. Then, the call is abruptly disconnected. When the police pinpoint the location of the call and arrive on the scene, they discover the caller's dead body. Written in blood on the wall behind the body is a message that reads, "Follow the Leader 2017."  Something terrible happened 5 years ago, and someone is out for revenge. Old scores will be settled. Old secrets will be uncovered. Will you Follow the Leader and live to tell about it?

As with all Deadbolt Mystery Society games, you step into the role of the detective. Examining the evidence provided, visiting different locations and using the password protected QR mechanic to uncover the truth.

Players win once they catch the killer, find what's missing or whatever else is needed to solve the case at hand.

Bradley Raymond is one of the world's most successful horror writers. So, it only makes sense that he lives in Valley Falls where frightening things seem to lurk around every corner. Raymond is known for writing about things that go bump in the night and about monsters that wear human faces. Yet, when people begin to be murdered in ways that mimic the plots of some of his most famous novels, it becomes clear that someone is doing their best to frame him. Things take a turn for the worst when it is discovered that the most recent victim can be linked to one of his unpublished manuscripts. Is the killer a member of Bradley Raymond's inner circle? Did his novels inspire a murderer to commit these atrocities? Or is there more to Bradley Raymond than meets the eye? Do the Monster Mash and find out!

As with all Deadbolt Mystery Society games, you step into the role of the detective. Examining the evidence provided, visiting different locations and using the password protected QR mechanic to uncover the truth.

Players win once they catch the killer, find what's missing or whatever else is needed to solve the case at hand.

This item is a kickstarter exclusive item that adds new game play. This includes:
- An obelisk miniature (for use with Deluxe core game edition) and
- Corresponding monument tile (for use with standard core game edition)

This monument costs 5 Coins. must be populated by 4 meeples in order to score 4, 8, or 12 victory points in the game, depending on each symbol under the Obelisk at the end of the game.

boardgame: Hunt a Killer: Blair Witch – Season 2
description:
In Hunt A Killer: Blair Witch, you play a 6-episode story and solve the mystery of Burkittsville. Completing each episode solves one piece of the puzzle, and builds your case for the larger mystery.

Blair Witch Season 2 Box Set is a self-directed storyline perfect for experienced detectives looking to test their skills against the supernatural forces that lurk in the woods. This full season will test hardened and new detectives, alike. After the events of Blair Witch Season 1, a team of podcasters arrives in Burkittsville, hoping to find information on the Blair Witch. But when the team vanishes on a seemingly routine trip into the woods, you must help Ed Barrett solve the mystery of what happened to them.

Use your detective skills to decode ciphers, solve cryptic puzzles, and uncover the history of Burkittsville.

You do not have to play the first season to enjoy Blair Witch Season 2.

Difficulty: 4/5 - Medium

In Hunt A Killer: Royal Street, you play a 6-episode story and solve the mystery of the murder of Celestine Broussard. Completing each episode solves one piece of the puzzle, and builds your case for the larger mystery.

Royal Street Box Set is a self-directed storyline perfect for experienced detectives looking to test their skills. When New Orleans artist Celestine Broussard is murdered during Mardi Gras, it’s up to you to uncover the secrets behind this party to end all parties and unmask a killer. Help Celestine’s best friend Sylvia uncover the truth about Celestine and the people in her life — no matter how shocking it might be.

Investigate Celestine’s murder in Hunt A Killer: Royal Street. When you take this case, your first task will be to locate the key to Celestine’s art studio somewhere in New Orleans.

Use your detective skills to decode ciphers, solve cryptic puzzles, and uncover the truth.

For each episode:
Check the Objective Card in each box as your goal may change each episode.

Set aside time to dig into the box and read all the materials.

Examine the evidence to uncover critical clues, corroborate alibis, create timelines, and identify each suspect’s means, motive, and opportunity.

Difficulty: 3/5 - Medium

boardgame: Hunt A Killer: Off the Record
description:
In Hunt A Killer: Off the Record, you play a 6-episode story and solve the mystery of the murder of Ashley Mendoza. Completing each episode solves one piece of the puzzle, and builds your case for the larger mystery.

Off the Record Box Set is a self-directed storyline perfect for experienced detectives looking to test their skills. After reporting on her town’s mayoral election, journalist Ashley Mendoza is found dead. Help her friend Frannie investigate Ashley’s death and expose her murderer.

Use your detective skills to decode ciphers, solve cryptic puzzles, and uncover the truth.

For each episode:
Check the Objective Card in each box as your goal may change each episode.

Set aside time to dig into the box and read all the materials.

Examine the evidence to uncover critical clues, corroborate alibis, create timelines, and identify each suspect’s means, motive, and opportunity.

Difficulty: 4/5 - Medium

In Hunt A Killer: Vindication, you play a 6-episode story and solve the mystery of the murder of a husband and son. Completing each episode solves one piece of the puzzle, and builds your case for the larger mystery.

Vindication Box Set is a self-directed storyline perfect for experienced detectives looking to test their skills.

Use your detective skills to decode ciphers, solve cryptic puzzles, and uncover the truth.

boardgame: ひぐらしのなく頃に－我－ (Higurashi When They Cry: Ware)
description:
ひぐらしのなく頃に－我－ ("Higurashi When They Cry: Ware") is a hidden role game where everyone starts in the same situation and it's possible to play successfully without lying. At the beginning of the game everyone has one of each of seven role cards; over five in-game days, players will give each other votes and discard roles until only one is left, which determines who wins.

Each turn ("day"), a player may choose to either vote or destroy a vote. When you vote you give a vote token to any other player and discard your role cards for the day face down. When you destroy a vote, you discard one vote from any player, including yourself, and your discards for the day are face up. At the end of each of the first three days, all discards are shuffled and revealed in total, so you can follow approximately what cards are still in play.

Roles have a variety of victory conditions.

This game is different from 斯くして我は独裁者に成れり (Kakushite ware ha dokusaisha ni nareri, "And Thus I Became a Dictator...") as it includes a set of different cards, winning conditions and theme.

Note: The game can be played with up to 12 players, but one box only supports up to 6. For more than 6 players you will need to buy a second set and combine them.

—user summary

boardgame: Star Wars: The Mandalorian – La busqueda
description: Grogu has been kidnapped and hidden in a secret location within the town of Nevarro. Players will use Mando, Moff Gideon, Ahsoka, Bo Katan, IG-11, or a Dark Trooper to find Grogu. Throughout the board they will find objects that will help them fulfill their mission. However, during the search, players must fight each other, so strategy will be essential to find Grogu while fighting to survive. The player who manages to take Grogu with him and flee from the town of Nevarro will be the winner.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The aim of Beat That! is to collect as many points as possible by betting on your ability to successfully complete a series of ridiculous challenges using an assortment of seemingly random objects. All players attempt the exact same challenges, so get ready for the ultimate battle of abilities.

Add a fresh twist to your game of Beat That! with these hilarious new challenges using everyday objects found in your home.

CONTAINS: 80 Challenge Cards, 2 Rule Cards.

Hangulnal ("Hangeul Day") is a game where you look at picture cards and make words associated with them. 'Hangeul' means Korean characters.
If you create associated words, you can get additional points and tiles.
Look at picture cards and combine consonant and vowel tiles to create associated words to get a lot of points.

Set Up

     Place the board in the center of the table.
     Each person selects the color of the meeples and get the same number of meeples as the number of participants. Then place one at each load start point on the board.
     Make a deck of picture cards as many as the number of people participating in the game, and place them next to each load with the back side facing up so that the front side is not visible. (4 decks for 4 people, 3 decks for 3 people, 2 decks for 2 people)
     The tiles are divided into consonant and vowel pockets, 


mixed well, and then each gets 10 consonant tiles and 8 vowel tiles.

Playing the Game

     Players all shout “one, two, three,” and flip the top card from the card deck in front of you so that the front of the card is visible.
     All players select one of the open picture cards to create a word associated with that card. The word must be at least 


2 characters long. (The words on the picture card or the English pronunciation of those words cannot be created.)

     You can create words until the last person to create the words comes up, and the person who created associated words should shout “Hangeul Day!”.
     From the first player who shouted 'Hangeul Day', each player one by one reveals the associated words that they created, and moves the meeples one block from the load with corresponding picture card. (If other player think the associated words don't fit at all, they can nullify with a majority vote.)
     After moving meeples, take the same number of tiles from the pocket as the number of icons displayed in the stopped 


column and finish your turn. Taking additional tiles makes the game easier because there are more possible characters to create as the game progresses.

         You can replace the 3 tiles you have until the end of the game (consonants, vowels, or consonants+vowels) with 1 tile of your choice.
    
     When first person's turn ends, the second person to shout “Hangeul Day” takes the turn, and the next person to shout take the next turn.
     If you created the another word on the same load as the previous player in your turn, move it to the nearest blank space from that location. (Multiple meeples cannot be in the same column.) 


If you select load and create words that are same as the previous player, only the person who originally created the word will be recognized and you will not be able to move your meeples.

End of the Game
The game ends when three meeples arrive at the final destination point of 10 points, and the person with the most points win by adding up the points written in the space where each meeple is placed.


         Contact Publisher
    


MAGICBEANGAMES(Republic of Korea)
e-mail : magicbean@magicbeangame.com

—description from the publisher

Each player is a cat person looking to adopt stray cats until their house is full. Each of the cats comes with its own special ability, and sometimes they just go out for a walk without you knowing it.

This revised version, Box of Cats 2022, offers a modified gameplay with additional cats and new abilities for greater fun!

				
				
					How to PlayThe Active player takes their turn with 3 phases.
1. Cat Phase, 2. Owner Phase and 3. Refill Phase

1. Cat Phase: At the beginning of your turn, use the ability of each cat you have played into your House: from left to right.

2. Owner Phase: Spend exactly 2 Action Points on the following actions. (You can choose the same action twice.)
- Cat in the House (1 Action Point): Choose 1 cat card from your hand and put it into play in front of you - from left to right. This area is your House.
- Stray Cats from the Box (1 Action Point): Choose 1 Stray Cat's Box (from the 3 available) and take all cards in that box into your hand.

You can play any number of Action Cards during your turn. This counts as a free action.
Only Nope can be used in another player's turn.

3. Refill Phase: Fill each Stray Cat's Box with cards from the draw pile until each box has exactly 2 face-up cards.  The turn passes to the player on the left.


        note**
    


- You can play any number of Action Cards during your turn. This counts as a free action.
Only Nope can be used in another player's turn.
- When Cats Walk is drawn, reveal it to every player and immediately use its ability. 
“Each player chooses one cat in their House (not in their hand) and gives it to the player on their left - putting it into their House.”

				
				
					Game End & ScoringThere are 2 ways to end the game.
1. When a player has five cats in their House, the player immediately wins the game.
2. When the 4th Cats Walk is revealed, the game ends without each player giving a cat to the player on their left. Total the cat scores and the player with the highest scores wins.
In case of a tie, the player with more cats in their House wins. If there is still a tie, the players share the victory.

-description from designer

I Dare You is a soccer game for 2 players aged 12 and up.
It is a simulation of a football match between two teams. Of course, the team that scores the most goals wins. Winning the game depends on the use of various moves and tactical moves. There is also an element of randomness in the game, such as throwing dice, cards drawn and then resolved.
Players take on the role of coaches of teams of 15 players (11 quarterback players and 4 reserve football players). Each trainer will be represented by a unique trainer card, which, thanks to his skills, will influence the course of the game.
The main inspiration behind the creation of this game is that there are very few board soccer simulations available. Thanks to this game, every young person who loves football can sit at the table, feel like a team coach and lead them to victories. Become a coach, learn the secrets of football and have fun fully making decisions affecting the course of the game.

—description from the designer

Aim to be an accomplished developer who can contribute the most to the new city's development plans!

In this may-follow game, you are aiming to have the most pieces on top of the buildings in each of the four areas or the piece on the top of the tallest building. The people who play the highest and lowest ranked card in the suit which is most present in the trick will earn the right to place their pieces onto the board.

The game plays over three rounds. In the second round, the players will have one unplayed card, and they will earn points if they have the majority of pieces on top of buildings in that area.  In the third round, the players will score similarly, but the card must be revealed at the start of the hand.

The player with the most points wins.
publishers: iop games (イオピーゲームズ)

description:
Shortcut Longcut is a card-based party game for 3-10 players, perfect for college, friends and family gatherings. You can even combine 2 packs to play with up to 20 players.

Players are traveling to a cafe by collecting Travel Cards in every round, and taking Shortcuts and Longcuts to control their speed.  The aim is to reach the cafe WITH someone (yes, anyone), rather than reaching first alone. However, players' cards are hidden, so everyone uses vague Status Updates to communicate, leading to confusion and laughter.

Over a set of 5 quick games, the winner is the player who reaches the cafe WITH friends the most number of times. So... Make friends, betray them, even bluff a little... Do whatever it takes to win!

Fun surprise: The comic-style artwork on the cards depicts funny situations on India's roads...like a cow sitting in the middle of the road, or people dancing on the road as part of a marriage procession (baraat). Enjoy!

-description from designer

Collect Magic Gems and learn Magic Spells. Prove that you are the True Wizard! Use your Spells smartly against your opponents and destroy evil items to win the most points!

How to play: In every turn, collect a Magic Gem or spend Magic Gems to buy a Spell Card. Then use Spell Cards to either destroy Evil items for victory points, or cast the unique power of your Spell to weaken your opponents. Decide when to use each Spell or combos of Spells for maximum impact!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Seep is a classic Indian card game played between 2 or 4 players using a standard 52-card pack without jokers. Seep is more over popular in Indian, Pakistan and few other Asian countries.

The aim of the Seep game is to capture cards worth points from a layout on the table (also known as the floor). The game ends when one team has accumulated a lead of at least 100 points over the other team (this is called a baazi). Players can decide in advance how many games (baazis) they want to play.

At the end of the seep round the scoring value of the captured cards is counted: (1) All cards of the Spade suit have point values corresponding to their capture value (from the king, worth 13, down to the ace, worth 1), (2) The aces of the other three suits are also worth 1 point each, and (3) The ten of diamonds is worth 6 points.

Players can also score for a seep, which occurs when a player captures all the cards from the layout, leaving the table empty. Normally a seep is worth 50 points, but a seep made on the very first play is worth only 25 points, and a seep made on the last play is worth no points at all.

On Planet Kepler, a new world is being created and life is beginning to emerge. Start the fight for survival and see which will be the dominant species in this arena style game.
Take on the role of a cell and evolve to adapt to your environment. It is a fight of cunning, luck and constant change and the first step to dominate the planet!

Choose what food your species will eat, between plants and other cells. Of course, you can evolve and adapt to start eating something different if the environment has changed and food is scarce. Carefully choose the evolutions that best suit your strategy, between attack, defense, movement or special. Trigger one-time use events to give you that little edge that makes the difference between life and death for your kind.

The first player who manages to get 20 evolution points will be the winner and the first species to go to earth to dominate this new planet.

Kepler the Creation is the first in a series of 4 games, all different, that will follow the story of your species on their way to conquer the galaxy:


     Your species as a cell
     Your species as an animal
     Your species as a civilization
     Your species as space explorers


—description from the designer

En el planeta Kepler, un nuevo mundo se está creando y la vida está comenzando a emerger. Pequeñas formas de vida surgen en el océano primordial y están listas para crecer y evolucionar. ¡Empieza la lucha por la supervivencia y ver cuál será la especie dominante en este juego estilo arena.

Toma el rol de una célula y evoluciona para adaptarte a tu entorno. Es una lucha de astucia, suerte y cambio constante y el primer paso para dominar el planeta!

Elige qué alimento comerá tu especie, entre plantas y otras células. Por supuesto, puedes evolucionar y adaptarse para empezar a comer algo diferente si el entorno ha cambiado y la comida escasea. Elige cuidadosamente las evoluciones que se adaptan mejor a tu estrategia, entre ataque, defensa, movimiento o especiales. Desencadena eventos de un solo uso para darte esa pequeña ventaja que hace la diferencia entre la vida y la muerte de tu especie.

El primer jugador que logre obtener 20 puntos de evolución será el ganador y la primera especie en salir a tierra para dominar este nuevo planeta.

Kepler, la creación es el primero en una serie de 4 juegos, todos diferentes, que seguirán la historia de tu especie en su camino a conquistar la galaxia:


     Tu especie como célula
     Tu especie como animal 
     Tu especie como civilización
     Tu especie como exploradores espaciales 


Cada juego se integra con el siguiente o puedes jugarlos

—description from the designer (Spanish)

In the not-so-distant future, every day jobs have been replaced by specialized robots. YOU are one of the premiere manufacturers of these robots. Create intricate circuits to produce the resources necessary for your success. But hurry, the Mainframe at the center of it all is unstable and bound to crash at any moment! Be the player with the most points after the mainframe crashes to win!

Each round you’ll be drafting circuit tiles to increase your resource production, or sabotage other players. Roll dice based on your circuit to earn resources, and spend those resources to buy and build robots. You’ll also get a special helper bot each turn with unique abilities that help you get ahead.

When the mainframe crashes the game is over, you’ll earn points based on how many robots you have in each faction. The more robots you have, the more points you’ll earn. Tally up your points; the player with the most points wins!

—description from publisher

The game starts with every player being dealt ten letter cards. In their turn, each player attempts to make a word worth at least seven points. If the word is worth 12 points or more, the player will receive an action card for their next turn. If their word is seven letters long or more, they will receive a combo card to keep in addition to the ten cards in their hand.

From then on, each player can either make a word of their own or choose to steal another player's word by adding onto it. If they can't play, they can choose to use their turn to replace as many letters as they desire. The catch is that their discarded letters will go into the center of the table face up for anyone to use at any time! If your word hasn't been stolen by your next turn, then you add the points to your score and set the letters aside to be shuffled back into the deck.

If you have an action card you'd like to play, you can do so at the beginning of your turn, to either help yourself or stab your friends in the back! There are four levels of action cards depending on how interesting of a game you want to play, ranging from cards like "Skip 1 Turn" on Level One to "Divide Your Score By 2 If You Don't Play A 14 Point Word On Your Next Turn" on Level Four.

Combo cards feature some of the most common letter combinations in the English language, and are worth a significant amount of points if you manage to use them in a word!

-from the Kickstarter page

Can you help Hilde to hang up the laundry? Not really a problem, but beware: It's pretty windy outside today and that makes the game an entertaining challenge for children! Throw the Weather Die: If the sun is shining you're lucky and you can hang up a piece of laundry, but if there's a storm you have to press the button and the rotary clothesline will start spinning. Hilde can just hold on tight, but if the clothes fall off the line, you have to start hanging them up again! Whoever hangs up all the clothes of their color first wins.

Golden Journal No. 48: Black Helicopters is built around the 28 helicopter pieces that were used in the old Secret Weapons book (part of a larger sheet including the heavy tanks in Golden Journal No. 37: Heavy Metal).

You’ll need Parachutes Over Crete (for the German mountain troops), River Battleships and Fire in the Steppe (for the maps and most of the other pieces) to play all of the scenarios.

-description from publisher's website
family: Series: Avalanche Press Gold Club Golden Journal Supplements

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Baratheon Heroes 3
description:
House Baratheon’s claim to the Iron Throne is made all the stronger by King Stannis Baratheon being the only king to face the growing threat beyond the Wall. In the field, King Stannis is a juggernaut, rallying his forces to stand against ever-growing odds. Of his loyal retainers Justin Massey, Dale Seaworth, and his squire, Bryen Farring, stand amongst his most skilled commanders. Off the field but of even equal value is his wise counselor Maester Cressen, and even the court fool, Patchface, proves his value to his king with his bizarre but insightful mutterings.


        Details:**
    
     The Baratheon Heroes 3 Box gives House Baratheon, a new range of characters to add to their battles, especially for Stannis supporters.
     Contains popular and well-known characters like Stannis Baratheon and his loyal supporters Maester Cressen, Dale Seaworth and the like.
     The miniatures are highly detailed and come pre-assembled so they can be played with and painted straight out of the box.



        Contains:**
    


6 Miniatures
5 Attachments
2 NCUs
12 Tactics Cards
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Baratheon Heroes 4
description:
Whether commanding units of their own or standing united around King Renly Baratheon in support of his claim, the heroes of the Rainbow Guard comprise some of the most skilled warriors to take the field in the War of the Five Kings. They count among their number Ser Loras Tyrell, Ser Robar Royce, Lord Bryce Caron, Ser Emmon Cuy, Ser Guyard Morrigen, Brienne of Tarth, and Ser Parmen Crane. Each a capable commander in their own right, they are at their most formidable
standing together with King Renly himself on the field, not just for their expertise, but as a symbol for all King Renly’s troops to rally behind.


        Details:**
    
     The Baratheon Heroes 4 box presents a brand new unit of elite knights to fight fpr the Iron throne in the name of Renly Baratheon.
     Contains the 7 rainbow guard and their Lord Commander, Loras Tyrell as well as the 'king' himself, Renly Baratheon.
     The miniatures are highly detailed and come pre-assembled so they can be played with and painted straight out of the box.



        Contains:**
    


12 Miniatures
1 Infantry movement tray
8 Attachment Cards
6 Tactics Cards
1 Special Rules Card
1 Unit Card

Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Casterly Rock Honor Guards
description:
Equipped with the finest plate armor, the Casterly Rock Honor Guard’s high defensive capabilities allow them to hold the line while exploiting opponents’ vulnerabilities and instilling them with panic. When facing the Honor Guard, opponents with Condition tokens should think twice, lest they wish to suffer the full might of the Honor Guard’s ‘Lannister Oppression’ ability! This unit includes a Sentinel Enforcer attachment. When attached to the unit, it provides players the Order Ability, ‘Taunt’ to coerce opponents into attacking the Honor Guard.

Approximate Height
Bannerman: 59mm
Sentinel Enforcer: 60mm
Honor Guard A: 49mm
Honor Guard B: 40mm
Honor Guard C: 41mm

description:
Its wiener takes all in Dash-Hound Derby! Fetch cards and match 3 or more costumes or color ribbons. Score points to out race the other players and move around the board. Unleash special action cards and be the furthest across the finish line to win!

Draft cards from one of three piles, and do your best to manage your hand to earn big points. Every point you score gets your dog further along the finish line, so be on the lookout for the perfect card to earn additional point bonuses. Play special action cards to give yourself a leg up on the competition.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Walpurgisnacht: la danza de las brujas
description:
Every March 31, on the eve of the feast of Santa Walpurga, Mephistopheles organizes a great coven and the witches come out to celebrate. Wisps are lit, leading or losing villagers on Mount Brocken. The villagers must protect themselves, but who is a witch and who is a villager?

Walpurgisnacht: The Dance of the Witches is an occult role-playing game, in which players will belong to one side: witches or villagers. In each turn they will try to reveal information to know which players are from their own side, and in this way they will manage to obtain the most points possible for their side.

In each turn each player will have to carry out three phases:


     The path of the will-o'-the-wisps.
     The delivery of the rites.
     The dance of the witches.


The game will end immediately once the Walpurga card has been revealed. The side with the most points will be the winner.

—description from the publisher

Cada 31 de marzo, en la víspera de la fiesta de Santa Walpurga, Mefistófeles organiza un gran aquelarre y las brujas salen a celebrar. Los fuegos fatuos se encienden, guiando o perdiendo aldeanos en el monte Brocken. Los aldeanos deben protegerse, pero ¿quién es bruja y quién es aldeana?

Walpurgisnacht: la danza de las brujas es un juego de roles ocultos, en los que los jugadores pertenecerán a un bando: brujas o aldeanas. En cada turno intentarán revelar información para saber qué jugadores son de su propio bando, y así lograr obtener la mayor cantidad de puntos posibles para su bando.

En cada turno cada jugador tendrá que realizar tres fases:


     El camino de los fuegos fatuos.
     La entrega de los ritos.
     La danza de las brujas.


El juego terminará inmediatamente una vez la carta de Walpurga se haya revelado. El bando que tenga más puntos, será el ganador.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

boardgame: Path of Light and Shadow: Solstice – Promo Pack
description:
16 promo cards created as stretch goals during the Path of Light and Shadow: Solstice kickstarter campaign. This includes:

- 10 New player cards (Dreadbanner, Fearbender, Palestar, Shatteredmask, Heretic, Informant, Nomad, Proxy, Spirit, and Warlord)
- 2 Title cards (The Arcane and The Mad)
- 4 Relic cards (Forbidden Gate, Sojourner's Blessing, Eye of the Prophetess, and Gift of the Ancients)

Hot-Pota-toh! is a press your luck game where the deck has an exploding potato that players must avoid drawing, while trying to get the cards that have "Phew" points. The deck will be rotated among the players and each player will draw a card or play a card until someone draws the BOOM Card and explodes, losing the points accumulated, then a new round begins. The best part of the game are the power cards which cointain other press your luck and hot potato mechanics!

In this second edition you will see an improvement in the illustrations, and new cards with new powers, which allow you to play with 2 players.

Hot-Pota-toh! es un juego de presionar la suerte, en cuyo mazo hay una patata explosiva que los jugadores deben evitar sacar, al mismo tiempo que tratan de conseguir cartas que den puntos "Phew". El mazo se rota entre los jugadores y cada jugador puede, o sacar una carta del mazo, o jugar una carta de su mano (no las dos cosas) hasta que alguien saque la papa caliente y explote, perdiendo todos los puntos que había acumulado en la ronda. Luego comienza una nueva ronda. Lo mejor de juegos con los poderes de las cartas, que contienen más mecánicas de presión de suerte.

En esta segunda edición verás una mejora en las ilustraciones, y nuevas cartas con nuevos poderes, que te permiten jugar desde 2 jugadores.

Players control city-states in Ancient Greece, and vie for control of other cities in the Greek mainland.

Players acquire power through the form of four influence types: Military, Diplomatic, Cultural, and Economic.  Each type has unique ways of counteracting the others, and players must be adaptable to succeed.  Multiple winners are possible, so forging alliances and well-timed betrayals can help players end the game among the victors.

The main goal of the game is to gain the most Glory, and the game ends when the pool of Glory Tokens is depleted.  Players may erect monuments, which provide alternate paths to victory once the game has ended.

GAMEPLAY OVERVIEW

Players start by selecting a city state, each which has two advantages and a disadvantage, and two focus influence types; there's also a jack-of-all-trades city which doesn't have a focus, but its advantages let it switch among the influence types more easily.  Each city is not a specialization, but more of a slight nudge off center.  If a city needs to switch tactics based on the situation, they are each able to gain cards and influence of other types to meet the challenge.  Each player city contains three districts, which can be developed using building and tradesmen cards; other cities only have 1 district each.

Influence is gained in mainly two ways.  Players may select what influence to gain each turn (with extra cards if for each focus type chosen), but this happens at the end of each turn, so players must plan ahead for what they will do.  The second way is at the start of each turn, but is more randomized.  The buildings and tradesmen used to develop a district each have a production number, and gain an influence if that number is rolled.  Players may also trade influence and cards on their turn; trading has the extra benefit of making a one-round truce between the trading players, which gives some measure of safety and ensures the traded items won't be immediately used against them.

For those influence-gaining rolls, the current player rolls two 8-sided dice:  one applies to all players, and the other only to the rolling player.  If a rolled number matches a production number the district the card is on gains an influence; however, there is a limit to the influence each district can store, so the same number getting rolled repeatedly doesn't grant extra influence.  Also, the game setup makes it so at the start of the game each player has access to cards with the same numbers on them, so players gain influence at the same rate; later cards are needed to diversify their numbers. The starting cards have a 1, 2, or 5 on them (the 8 is only for rare cards, so will mostly be unavailable for players), so 50% of the values are already matching at the beginning.  Furthermore, the values 1-4 are only on buildings, and 5-7 are only on tradesmen; since tradesmen must be added onto buildings, the higher numbers are less accessible.

Each district can perform one action every turn, so the more cities you control the more you can do.  Since most actions cost influence, it's often best to not use all your actions to save your influence for later.  Actions allow players to use abilities, which mainly come from two sources: a set of basic abilities available at all times, and event cards a player draws to their hand.  The event cards correspond to the four influence types, and usually require influence of that type to use.

Victory points, called "Glory" in the game, are gained by placing your pawns (called "Officials") on the board, as well as by winning special events called Contention Rolls.  Contention rolls represent things like battles and votes, and utilize a push-your-luck mechanic to determine the outcome.

Each influence type has a contention roll, and are the main way officials are added (there is a basic ability to add an official to a neutral tile; the contention rolls are used mainly against other players).  First, the player who initiated the roll allocates all their resources, plays abilities, gets allies, and rolls; then once their value is determined, the targeted player does the same.  This allows for a player to defend with few or no resources if the odds are too stacked against them; they can always use the influence they saved for their own contention roll later).  The player who initiated the roll has the added benefit of rerolling their die before the targeted player allocates any resources; however, doing so costs some of the influence allocated to the contention roll, so their total potential value will weaken with each reroll.

Turns start with drawing an Adversity Card, which is the "bad stuff happens" card.  Based on the officials and buildings a player controls, it's more or less likely that one of their cities will revolt.  A city under revolt more or less returns it to a neutral state, and makes it more vulnerable to contention rolls.  There is also the Olympics card, which makes a universal truce for a round, which is great if you need a breather in the game to consolidate your gains, but can be disruptive if you've been preparing a big move which now has to wait another round.  The setup also makes sure the Olympics is on the first round, so it has somewhat of a built-in tutorial to allow players to get the gist of a turn before targeting one another.

The four influence types counteract one another, but not in a rock-paper-scissors way: instead they have unique ways of interacting with the board and other players.  Diplomatic influence deals mostly with how cities are connected to one another, and a disconnected city cannot use the influence or actions available to it; this influence can strike anywhere and limit the options available to other players.  Economic influence doesn't gain more influence, but does allow it to be moved to where it's most effective, making it more efficient; furthermore they allow players to take the influence of others as well as being a requirement for each building card.  Cultural influence is more defensive in nature, and helps defend against both contention rolls and adversity cards; on the flip side, players can use it against opponents to get more revolt tokens, and is the only type which can modify rolls due to adversity cards, acting as an alternative to contention rolls.  Finally, military influence is able to remove effects from all influence types from a city, but its gains are the most vulnerable from all influence types as well, making it have a more easy-come-easy-go effect.

Players will need to select which influence types to focus on as the game goes on, and might need to change based on the available resources and actions of other players.  Trying to focus on all four types will generally just mean you're weak in all four, whereas focusing exclusively on one leaves you very vulnerable to the other three types.  Most of the time focusing on two and dabbling in a third is the way to go; stockpiling the influence type not being used can help defending against contention rolls of that type.  Since up to half of the players can win a game, cooperating with other players and trading for the influence you need is the best way to make sure you're prepared for what comes up next.

The instruction manual is set up in a learn-as-you-go format, so you perform steps as you read them rather than having to read the whole thing before starting the game.  Relevant rules are referenced as you go through a section, so you know what to read when.  A quick-reference sheet is on the back of the manual, the setups for the various maps are in the first opened page, and the rules for how Contention Rolls affect the game board are in a distinctly styled page where the staples are, so the frequently referenced rules have quick access.  There is also an appendix which gives an example of each game component, some historical context, as well as suggestions on how the game components interact with one another.

-description from designer

Whether you’re newly in love or already married: turn your everyday life into an experience and have a fabulous time full of fun and closeness. Because this game is all about you!

Flitterkiste is a legacy game for two people who love each other. It offers a completely new gaming experience – because the challenges are played for and with each other in everyday life. As a gift, it suits any occasion and can be combined with other presents.

How to play and win:
On your turn each of you draws a secret card with a task from the shared deck. You can solve it within a few days. After this period, you draw the next card. Completed tasks reward you with hearts – the currency of the game. You collect them and can use them to unlock added content and gifts. It is your choice. There are ten locked chapters within the chronicle. When you open and complete the last chapter both of you win the game.
In between you will receive shared cards. They are not secret, and you can solve the challenges together at any time. They will help you to change the rules of the game and give you bonus hearts.

The perpetual calendar always gives you an overview of what is next in the game. He functions as a game board, because this is where you can see your progress and record your goals.

During play you will write, paste over or use up cards and other components. This is how the Flitterkiste develops further and adapts to the interests of the couple.

Components:
133 Unique cards
76 Pages game book
1 Perpetual calendar
31 Sticker
2 Game markers
& lots of love
publishers: Raphael Meyersieck und Jan Schmitz GbR

boardgame: キングダム盤上大戦 (Kingdom Banjo Taisen)
description:
Two players boardgame based on Japanese famous comic and animation series "Kingdom". Banjo Taisen means epic battle on the board.

The basic setting of the game is abstract, and does not represent specific battles in the story, but the general and tactics cards give it a "Kingdom" feel.
Both players have the same number of Troops, Generals and Tactics cards. Depending on the stage of the rules, the size of the battlefield board and the number of pieces can be changed.

In each turn, player can move a single piece or a stack of pieces, play a tactical card, draw a new tactical card or place reserve piece in own camp zone.

A battle occurs when the pieces enter a square with an opponent's pieces, and each player uses their own strength to remove the opponent's pieces. The strength is based on the number of pieces, and is determined by the effects of facing, general cards and tactical cards.

The player who first eliminates the opponent's commander-in-chief or occupies the opponent's headquarters wins.

description:
Your goal is to be the most prestigious tea shop in the neighborhood.

With the halloween expansion pack you get a new cat friend called Nikki, she has special abilities in combination with a new pumpkin card.
A tabletop skirmish warband game, 18+ mature content, a player versus player game, average game duration 2 hours, an out of the box game.

boardgame: Storyteller Cards: Fantasy – Storyteller Manual: Fantasy edition
description:
Games, activities, and other creative ways to use Storyteller Cards: Fantasy and Storyteller Cards.

The Manual includes rules for different ways to play. From strategic coloring to push-your-luck storytelling, there are game variations for everyone. Many variants are story based gaining points for each card played, best poker hands and matching card elements, but others include strategic coloring (where players must complete illustration quadrants to earn points), and endless possibilities (where the players create and change the rules in a race to 30 points).

The Manual for the Regular deck of Storyteller Cards can be used with Storyteller Cards: Fantasy, and vice-versa.

Games, activities, and other creative ways to use Storyteller Cards and Storyteller Cards: Fantasy.

The Storyteller Manual includes rules for 16 different ways to play. From strategic coloring to push-your-luck storytelling, there are game variations for everyone. Many variants are story based gaining points for each card played, best poker hands and matching card elements, but others include strategic coloring (where players must complete illustration quadrants to earn points), and endless possibilities (where the players create and change the rules in a race to 30 points).

boardgame: Storyteller Cards: Fantasy – Swords & Storytellers Bonus Card Set
description:
11 cards that can be used with the Swords & Storytellers game variant or with the Storyteller Cards in general.

Swords and Storytellers, included in the Storyteller Manual: Fantasy edition, is a fantasy adventure role playing game for 2 to 5 players! One player is designated the Dungeon Master. He controls the entire world; the innkeepers, monsters, weather, dank dungeons, fair maidens, dragons, piles of acidic ooze and sentient man-eating treasure chests. The other players control their own characters that they dream up in their imagination. The Dungeon Master guides the players through an adventure he creates trying to challenge and entertain the players. The players try to navigate the world created by the Dungeon Master. Most of all, everyone tries to tell a great story!

boardgame: Men of War: Rhodesian Bush War – Forward Together
description:
Men of War (MoW) Rhodesian Bush War-Forward Together- Solitaire Tactical Book Wargame is designed to re-create modern low-intensity conflicts at the tactical level. MoW is an infantry-centric old-school hex and counter wargame system that mimics the nuances of modern small unit leadership, fire, and maneuver, primarily team and squad-based formations.
In this first Module; “Rhodesian Bush War-Forward Together” the player controls Rhodesian security forces attempting to eradicate an ever-threatening insurgency backed by communist superpowers. The scale of the game is at the tactical level with each hex grid representing 25 meters, turns representing a few minutes, and units representing teams of two to four-man “sticks”. A dynamic enemy artificial intelligence bot keeps players guessing enemy disposition on every turn.

Features
Solitaire Small Unit Tactical Gameplay.
Unique Hexagonal Geomorphic Hexagonal maps depicting African Terrain linked for larger scenarios.
Ten Historically Inspired Mission.
Support Weapons including Light machine guns, grenades, and claymores.
Easy to Learn Six-Page Rulebook.
Deep tactical gameplay.
A Dynamic A.I. Bot/Enemy System keeps you guessing every turn.
Portable; all you need is this book a D10 and D6 dice (or smartphone dice app) and a marker.

—description from the designer

description:
Magna Itinera is a small expansion that includes 3 modules:
- Legendary Animals (15 cards) - Give you access to their unique powers.
- Events (45 cards) - Add changing conditions to the game that might help or hinder your strategy.
- Additional Experts (10 cards) - Grant unique bonuses.

Deluxe Hero Pack #1 is a set of Hero and Minion cards in alternative color palettes so you can pit them against themselves using two sets of hero cards.

Contains one alternate card for each of the following Heroes/Minions:

     Akhena
     Mutiny
     RIG
     Whispyr
     Aura
     Forge

How about some more heroes? With more heroes you can get a lot of variety out of your games. You can also play them in solo (either as or against them) and even play 3-4P team games.

Includes:

     Korvu Hero set and AI sheet (for solo play).
     Pavl Hero set and AI sheet (for solo play).
     Hype Hero set and AI sheet (for solo play).



What is the mark of a true champion? Legendary heroes clash across the Neververse to find out.

Battlecrest is a card-based tactical skirmish game for 2 players (1-4 with expansions). With perfect information, players must truly outwit their opponent to win.

Characters are composed of 6-card sets featuring a hero card, a health tracker, and action cards. Occasionally a minion or item will replace an action card.

The map is built of 6 cards as well, with the cards randomly placed in a set pattern.

Players take turns maneuvering their characters around the dynamic, and often dangerous map while unleashing powerful attacks. As action cards are used they open up new possibilities and empower the character’s battlecrests. Build up to decisive strikes or refocus to get powers back. Every character has a unique set of actions and preferred tactics, so no two battles play the same.

—description from the publisher

Nassor is a family game for 3 to 4 players for more than 10 years in which each player will have to manage a team of 2 archaeologists (2 meeples of the same color) whose objective is to travel through the desert starting from the initial town of Dairú until find the golden city of Nassor. The archaeologists will move through the desert according to what we roll on the archaeologists die (8-sided die).

The desert is represented in 6 board modules that have an A and a B side, so the faces could be interchanged so that each game is different. The path to Nassor is made up of 96 squares, but if we use the shortcuts available on the map well, the journey will be shorter.

In addition to all the difficulties that the desert has prepared for us on our way, you will also have to be careful with the other archaeologists, since they will be able to capture you and send you to the previous town where you have been  as well as Nassor's guardians, who  will be lurking throughout the game for their own route hidden in the dense jungle, and if we coincide with them in adjacent squares, they will capture the archaeologists sending them to the dungeon.

In order to survive and advance in the desert, the players will seek to fall with their archaeologists in the long-awaited power boxes, which will grant us one of the forty existing powers in the deck, all of them different, to use for our benefit or to harm the others. other players.  To end the game, you must reach the final square with your two archaeologists, at which point you officially declare yourself the discoverer of the golden city of Nassor and the winner of the game.

-description from publisher
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Story
The dumplings made by the master are excellent. You are in charge of skewering as an apprentice! Make beautiful skewered dumplings and earn praise from your master!

Game Outline
(1) Preparation
Players place the first two dumpling cards (hereafter referred to as “cards”) in their work area.

(2) Round
Players gain one card per round and place it in their work area.
After 6 rounds, 8 cards are finally placed on the work area so that the number of dumplings is 4 x 5 (regardless of the length or width).

(3) Skewering
Players skewer 3 or 4 connected dumplings in their work area to make dumplings.

(4) Score calculation
The scores of the skewered dumplings are added up for each player. The player with the highest score is the winner of the game.
publishers: natriumlamp games (ナトリウムランプゲームス)

description: Players play matching cards from their hands to upgrade their spaceships or to gain bonuses on future dice rolls, then roll dice against terrain cards to gather crystals. Once the last crystal is removed from a terrain card, players battle to steal crystals from their opponent and to keep the terrain card. When a player gains their second terrain card the game ends. Points are scored for terrain cards and crystals collected.

One thousand unique mutations to bring a heavy dose of random weirdness to your games of Frostgrave! From extra limbs, animal heads, and ink spray to insect vomiting, powerful static discharge, and bodies of stone, each mutation changes the figure in some mechanical way. Whether you’ve been cursed by a witch, dropped in a toxic pool, bombarded with gamma radiation, or just drunk the wrong potion, you’ve now got the perfect table to roll on to see what happens!

The d20 was not enough. Now is the time of the d1,000!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cthulhu: Death May Die – Ithaqua the Wind-Walker
description:
Ithaqua the Wind-Walker is an expansion for Cthulhu: Death May Die. This box brings a brand-new Elder One for you to use in combination with any Episode (from any Season!) creating a whole new gaming experience as you try to vanquish the perils of the Wind-Walker and his Shantak followers!

The Ithaqua the Wind-Walker box comes with:


     1 Ithaqua figure
     2 Shantak figures
     8 Mythos cards
     5 Elder One cards
     Tokens


Ithaqua, the son of Hastur and Lord of the Winds is a powerful Great Old One in his own right; a horrifying giant that stalks the frozen wastes and slays whoever he comes across in pursuit of his goal to free the other Great Old Ones. His is the power to gather and control blizzards and violent snowstorms, and it is no different in Death May Die, for whenever Ithaqua is the chosen Elder One, a blizzard token is placed on the board, forcing any investigator who enters it to fully max out stress and be unable to heal it. Worst yet, every time Ithaqua advances, the blizzard moves toward the investigators, summoning a Shantak from amidst the storm. From there on, things only get rougher for the investigators. As Ithaqua edges near our realm, the blizzard grows more intense, moving more frequently around the board, causing more stress, damage, and preventing movement. Of course, as the Lord of the Winds, all of his cultists are completely immune to the freezing weather, and roam free to attack the investigators. Ithaqua brings with him his devastating weather effects to the board, offering a whole-new experience in fighting the Old Ones.

Shantaks are enormous avian monsters with horse-like heads that attend the Old Ones. These creatures are incredibly tough, and their resilience makes it hard for investigators to eliminate them quickly. With their long affiliation with Ithaqua, Shantaks have learned ways to utilize the Great One's violent blizzards to enter our realm, and worse yet, as long as they're near a blizzard, they are at their more frightening form, forcing investigators to take stress before they attack or are attacked. Thus, Shantaks are constantly ticking down the investigators' stress, hampering their ability to manipulate dice rolls. The faster you get rid of these monsters, the better - which would be a lot easier if not for their aforementioned resilience...

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Cthulhu: Death May Die – Fear of the Unknown: Animal Allies
description:
Animal Allies is a Kickstarter Exclusive expansion for Cthulhu: Death May Die. This expansion brings a set of new Relic cards to the game, except, this time, they are animal allies that will join investigators on the board, following them around!

The Animal Allies box comes with:

     10 Animal Ally figures
     5 Relic cards
     Rules leaflet


Animal Allies are a special type of Relic that can be added to the pool of available Unknown Relics. Just like other Relics, each Animal Ally comes in 2 different forms: an initial form and an upgraded form. Unlike other Relics, however, each animal ally is also represented by 2 figures that move around the board (1 for the starting side and 1 for the upgraded side) and interact with enemies.

During setup, the starting ally figures are placed on the starting space along with their investigators. From then on, allies move on the board, either following investigators or on their own, attacking enemies, luring monsters away from their tutors, taking wounds instead of investigators, allowing them to reroll, and protecting them against enemies.

Allies do not count as figures for game purposes (for example, effects like Psychotic Outbreaks that state “Move to the nearest space with at least 1 figure”. Allies are ignored for this effect), instead, they count as tokens and have their own set of rules for moving. Whenever an investigator leaves a space where there’s an ally, loyal as they are, that ally may follow them (even if the investigator is not the controller of the Relic). In addition, investigators in control of an ally may move their pet companions up to 3 spaces as a free action. This is particularly important, as the placement of each ally is crucial to achieve maximum benefit out of their effects.

The connection between animals and their tutors is undeniable, and so, just like with the other Relics, as the investigator’s insanity rises, their ally’s behavior reacts accordingly. Upon reaching the 3rd insanity milestone, the card is flipped, reflecting their tutor’s insanity and a new, upgraded, behavior from their allies. Their figures on the board are also replaced with their upgraded counterpart to represent their state. Conversely, if the investigator is killed, their ally is removed from the board, as they run away in despair.

—description from the publisher
