boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Ironjawz – Gore Gruntas
description:
Enormous, foul-tempered porcine beasts of a strength and fury that even orruks respect, gruntas trample down all but the biggest foe, devouring the remains and noisily smashing apart everything in their path – much to the vivid delight of the Ironjawz who bounce gleefully upon their somewhat interestingly-fragranced backs. Gruntas will cheerfully and greedily eat anything, including iron (the result of devouring people wearing it, more often than not.) This undigested metal is harvested by orruks for use as weapons and armour, and called pig-iron. A well-timed charge of Gore-gruntas can easily smash apart an enemy army in a riot of stomping hooves, piercing tusks and unpleasant smells.

This multi-part plastic kit contains all the parts necessary to assemble three orruk Gore-gruntas, savage orruks riding massive, armoured, betusked beasts. The orruks can be armed with pig-iron choppas, or jagged gore-hackas, with an optional Gore-grunta boss featuring his own head, weapon and grunta options. 100 components in total, with three Citadel 90x52mm Oval bases.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Ironjawz – Weirdknob Shaman
description:
Wildly unpredictable, savage, untamed and violent, Waaagh! magic cannot be controlled by anything with even a shred of sanity. Luckily, the Ironjawz don’t have many shreds of sanity amongst them, and the least-shredded of all are the Weirdnob Shamans. Seemingly never truly in control of their own bodies, they twist and twitch and gibber as Waaagh! energy courses unstoppably through them, staring at sights only they see and talking to nothing. The larger the gathering of Ironjawz around one of these strange orruks, the bigger the fireworks display when it all kicks off – maybe a blast of Waaagh! energy will hammer into the enemy like a particularly smelly gust, or maybe a massive green foot the size of a mountain will kick the entire enemy army in the face. Nobody’s exactly sure, and that’s half the fun!

This multi-part plastic kit contains the components necessary to assemble one Weirdnob Shaman, the bizarre and unpredictable wizard of the Ironjawz. Posed in a twitching, staggering stance as the power of the Waaagh! flows through him, he’s covered in detailed shamanic fetishes and is armed with a smouldering Waaagh! staff. 13 components, supplied with a Citadel 40mm Round base.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Ironjawz – Megaboss
description:
The biggest – and therefore the best – of the Ironjawz, the orruk Megabosses lead the Ironjaw warclans with a ramshackle unity, smashing and stomping and breaking and battering everything in reach. The more fights an orruk wins, the bigger they get; Megabosses are understandably massive, and more than happy to use their immense, muscular bulk to kick in the heads of anything and everything in their path. Covered in scraps of iron taken from bested foes, festooned with trophies of war and armed with some utterly brutal weaponry, orruk Megabosses are absolutely terrifying to even behold, let alone actually take on in battle.

This multi-part plastic kit contains the parts necessary to assemble one orruk Megaboss, covered in nailed-on iron armour, armed with a boss choppa and rip-tooth fist. Fourteen components are included, along with a Citadel 60mm Round base.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Ironjawz – Brutes
description:
Lumbering, heavy-set creatures with furrowed brows and heavy armour, orruk Brutes are unconcerned with the complex things in life. Their minds are filled with one thing only - violence. Bigger and harder than most warriors of the mortal realms, Brutes fill out the warclans in a seemingly endless tide of green skin, angry shouting and tiny-minded aggression. After all, as they say – if smashing it ain’t working, get more boys to smash it with! Brutes seek out the largest enemies – or trees, or walls – that they can find, and set about them with determination, often being joined by dozens more, all clamouring to bring down whatever enemy (or object) has the temerity to be bigger than them.

This multi-part plastic kit contains the components you’ll need in order to assemble five orruk Brutes! Festooned with trophies of war, featuring massive metal or bone jaws, they can be armed with two choppas, or a double-handed jagged gore-hacka. One in five can be armed with a massive gore-choppa, and one model can be assembled as a Brute Boss, armed with either a boss choppa or a boss klaw and brute smasha. 102 Components in total, with 5 Citadel 40mm Round bases.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Ironjawz – Megaboss on Maw-krusha
description:
Orruk Megabosses are pretty killy – it’s how they become as huge as they do, all that stompin’ causes them to grow and grow and grow until the power of the Waaagh! flows through them. But for some Megabosses, this isn’t enough! These particularly angry orruks seek out and subdue enormous Maw-krushas (often by yelling really, really loudly at them) – ill-tempered and powerful creatures who enjoy smashing stuff up almost as much as the orruks themselves. This uneasy pairing never really gets along especially well, and the Megaboss needs to constantly remind his Maw-krusha who’s in charge with the liberal application of an iron boot to the back of the head. Despite this, almost nothing on the battlefield can withstand their noisy rampage, and the immense number of kills they rack up inspires nearby orruks to fight ever harder.

This multi-part plastic kit contains all the components necessary to assemble one Megaboss on Maw-krusha. Standing atop his fierce steed, the Megaboss is armed with either a boss gore-hacka and scrap-tooth or a choppa and rip-tooth fist – both options are suitably killsome. The Maw-krusha features an alternative mask and bladed tail. This kit contains 63 components, includes a Citadel 160mm Round base, and can be optionally assembled as Gordrakk, the Fist of Gork.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Start Collecting – Ironjawz
description:
Huge, hulking brutes, much taller and broader than a man, the Ironjawz rumble across the mortal realms in search of a good scrap. To them, nothing is so pleasing as the sound of battle – blades on metal, screams of the dying, the clamour and rattle of absolute violence is music to their ears. The most fightsome of all orruks, the Ironjawz are rightly feared all across the realms as their numbers swell and their Waaagh! grows ever larger.

This is a great-value box set that gives you an immediate collection of fantastic Ironjawz miniatures, which you can assemble and use right away in games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar! You’ll receive an Orruk Warchanter, three Orruk Gore-gruntas, ten Orruk ‘Ardboyz and an exclusive Ironjawz Warscroll Battalion rules sheet, allowing you to collect, assemble and play with your new miniatures right away!

The Road Traffic, Upgraded Service Stations, and Wine Import Mini Expansions are included in the deluxe edition of Autobahn, or available to purchase separately to add to the base game.

Road Traffic adds 12 Traffic meeples that block roads into growing cities. Upgrade the roads to clear the traffic and earn promotions.

Upgraded Service Stations provide more opportunities to use Service Station Actions, racing for additional bonuses on a shared board by building upgrades to your Service Station Network.

Wine Imports reverses the direction of transport. Ship wine from neighbouring countries into Berlin to earn promotions.

-description from publisher

The Great Horse Knowledge game Equi Quiz is a board game for everyone who loves horses.
The more you know about these noble animals, the faster you can build your dream stable.
The board game is for all horse lovers from 12 years old. You play the game with 2 to 4 people.
It is also possible to play with several people, but then teams must be made.
The questions are divided into 6 different categories:
Behaviour, Anatomy, Sports & Recreation, Nutrition & Health, Grooming & Equipment, and Breeds & History.
There are 60 different questions per category – so 360 in total.

Answer a question each turn. You can earn victory points by answering the question correctly. With these profit points you buy dream stable parts. There are 7 different dream stable parts that must be collected by each player. The first player to collect all 7 different dream stable parts wins the game.

Along the way you will face windfalls and obstacles, just like in real life.
You will come across “The masked horse” and “The prince on the horse”. If you end up on “The Horse Stable” then parts can be exchanged. But you know the well-known proverb: “Exchanging leads to crying”. Who knows, you might even laugh! Be surprised.

It doesn't matter if you are a professional or a hobby rider, this horse knowledge board game is challenging for all horse lovers of all levels.
The playing time is between 30 and 60 minutes.
Equi Quiz is a true must-have for anyone who loves horses!

-translated from the publisher site'

The Catan - Malta Scenario was created for the 13th Catan World Championship held in Maleta, Malta November 2022.

Catan and Cities & Knights: Replace the desert hex with the Malta hex. Build board starting with the Malta hex on the edge of the board. Seafarers: Replace a random water tile near the center of the board.

Then place the Malta "10/11" number token on Malta with "11" side up.

You may not place a starting settlement or road within the Malta hex during set-up, but you may build on the hex's intersections and/or outer paths.

The Malta Hex: The hex has 6 routes (dotted paths) to enter the island of Malta. 
Malta Routes: Building a malta route requires 1 lumber + 1 wool.

This is compatible with Catan, Catan - Seafarers, Catan - Cities & Knights, and their 5-6 Player Extensions.

boardgame: 63: The Great Game of Welsh Politics
description:
63 is a two or three-player map-based strategy game themed around the competing ideologies in Welsh politics after 1963.  Players represent the Welsh national movement, the left or socialist movement, or British unionism.  The Liberal Party is represented as a 'non-human player' who any player can choose to play as, instead of as themselves, during their turn.

The aim of the game is to place all pieces held in a player's hand onto the board, with the pieces representing bases on key locations in Wales.

Play is turn-based, with fixed and visible movement abilities and options (which include base-building, grid movement and rail movement), and no chance mechanisms at all. There is no player elimination.  As well as outright victory, two types of draws can end the game - one with all the human players losing.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Rogue Hero Pack
description:
Cursed with an uncontrollable ability to absorb the powers and memories of anyone she touches, Anna Marie ran away from home as a teenager. Professor X offered her a place at his school where she could learn to harness her power for good. Now, she fights for justice as a member of the X-Men, Rogue!

This southern belle blasts her way into battle with this expansion pack, which introduces Rogue as a new playable hero along with her sixteen signature cards. With a pre-built Protection deck ready to play from the get-go, you’ll be able to stop the villain’s attacks in their tracks from the moment you open the box. With her abilities changing depending on who she touches, Rogue is a versatile hero who can face any threat.

This hero pack also includes a bonus modular encounter set featuring the cybernetically-enhanced Reavers!

For players eager to challenge Magneto and his Brotherhood of Mutants, they can get started with the Rogue Hero Pack! This expansion comes with a 40-card pre-built deck, giving players the chance to start playing right out of the box.

-description from publisher's website

description: Flip a card and then race your opponents to match the pattern using the correct size and color frames. Arrange face up or, when a black line appears on the card, be quick to build vertically. Arrange your frames to match the pattern and then be first to slap the card to score it. Score 10 cards first and win!

This fun family board game will teach the entire family entrepreneurial skills! Children will comprehend how companies operate. They will understand how companies make money and why some companies are more, and others less successful.

Firma Kids is the ideal tool for quality and pleasant socializing. While your child runs his or her first imaginary company, you will proudly watch him or her acquire knowledge that he or she will possess for the rest of his or her life. Firma Kids is a great choice which will give your child abundance of knowledge that he or she can use throughout his or her life.
HITSTER Demo Deck comes with some sample cards from the original game, Hitster.

More socks are added during each round of the game - first they are placed face up, then turned face down in the washing machine. After the machine has spun 3 times, players will need to pick out the right socks to complete the triplets.

The 2022 DiceFest Goody Box is filled with Promos and the DiceFest exclusive game miniature, the Veteran Bandido, made specifically for DiceFest! Except for the keychain, all the contents are for Shadows of Brimstone.

This game fosters the comprehension of the history, architecture and daily life of the monks of the Abbey of Mont-Saint-Michel as the players exchange explicative and illustrated cards (photos, drawings). With the help of these cards, the players can recompose the ground plan of the buildings of the Abbey, level by level, from the crypts below to the Archangel above.

—description from the back of the box

Two players (or four players as teams) compete to build their own tri-level plan of Mont St Michel. Cards are drawn to get a map card matched with two corresponding game cards. When this happens, one map card can be laid down. 12 map cards need to be played to finish the plan with a last card, "The Archangel St. Michael", played for the player (or 2-player team) to win the game.

Power Poker Baseball is a socially interactive poker experience. It combines uniquely, a 5-card poker Texas Holdem format, with the dynamic scoring and play action of baseball. It is packed with fun game banter and competitive play. The game is for 2-8 players. Ages 13 – adult. The more players, the better the fun.

This game began many years ago as a wood product and has morphed into a powerhouse of fun poker play. This will be the most fun poker game you will ever play. Players will be standing on the last cards flipped in the 9th inning. The supportive website makes the game easy to learn and offers free replacement downloadable scoresheets and a little of the game's early manufacturing history.

This game’s rule action is so true to baseball, in that it starts out easy, with a lot of table laughter. As the innings go buy, it grows into a naturally competitive and strategic challenge. All players will be standing in the ninth inning, on every card flipped. There are bunts to first, stealing a base, picking off another runner, stealing home and yes, you can be thrown out at the plate. Dealing is crucial in this game, where the dealer scores on the flop. You have Pinch Hitters, Attempts to Rally and Rally Play.

Players can play for the table and give runs to try to create a tied position, keeping all players, still in the game, going into extra innings.

You can play conservative and Fold to Score often, or you can play risky and “bet to win” and maybe get into a big run scoring rally. Watch out though, other players may try to stop you, by calling in one of their Relief Pitchers.
This will be one of the most fun poker games, you will ever play.

Start your own tournament play with family and friends.

—description from the publisher

Each player chooses a paragon and takes their deck of 7 unique cards. Every turn, players choose a card from their hand and play them simultaneously. Both cards are revealed and then characters either take damage, have their card canceled and sent back to their hand, or are defeated. Play the right card at the right time and have a strong read of your opponent's intentions to win!

This box features 8 paragons from the Acquisitions Incorporated universe and two dials to track health and energy.

-description from the publisher

Each player chooses a paragon and takes their deck of 7 unique cards. Every turn, players choose a card from their hand and play them simultaneously. boardgame: Epic Seven Arise: The Boardgame – For Hope
description:
This expansion grows as the campaign progresses, it includes 3 new chapter boxes with new artifacts and encounter tokens to further increase replay value, it also brings the epic finale of Episode 1 and the side story "Two Keys to Freedom".

In the final chapter of Episode 1, players battle the final boss Archdemon Mercedes and her powerful minions.

The side story is mainly about the two new heroes, Sez and Bellona, whose memories players collect and decide which hero we will face in the final part of the game. With the expansions, new mechanics will bring even more excitement to our core box and to this expansion set.

Object of game: Play beast and phenomena cards in turn to win gems.    Win gems by playing high-ranking beasts that cannot be defeated by another player or by playing the same value beast to trigger a war. Be the first player to earn enough gems to win the game.

Cards: There are 72 unique beasts with 6 unique beasts of the same value with varying single or double elements and 48 phenomena cards that are either +1 or -1 modifiers for two, three or four elements.

Gameplay: The first beast card played can be any value and is called the current beast. Play moves clockwise to the next player attempting to beat the current beast card.  The current player must play only one beast card that is of equal or higher value than the current beast.  Phenomena can only be played on your turn and can act on any individual beast; you can play as many phenomena as you wish. There is only one active beast at a time, once a beast is beaten it can be discarded with all the phenomena attached to that beast.  The round will continue until all players cannot beat the current beast.  Ties will result in a war occurs.
End of Game - If at any time during the game the draw pile has been exhausted just re-shuffle the discard pile.  Game ends once one player reaches 10 or greater gems for 2 players.
3 players – 9 gems
4 players – 8 gems
5 players – 7 gems
6 players – 6 gems

—description from the designer

boardgame: A game about setting scenarios that people would do for different lengths of time.
description:
Wacky scenarios and empathy. Mad-lib-style cards with which you may combine a purpose, task, companion, and up to 2 modifiers.

Eg. "For all the money in the world pretend to be best friends with a chihuahua who is covered with bees, whilst your parents stare at you."

How long would you do that for? How long would everyone else do that for?

 Take turns to set a scenario, using 3-5 cards. Other players vote how long they would be willing to do that scenario for. 
  The scenario-setter gets pts for each unique vote. Try to confuse everyone!
 Other players get pts for each identical vote. Try to vote like everyone else!

After everyone has had a turn, whoever has the most points wins!

-description from designer

boardgame: Mystery Party in the Box: Sueño de una Noche junto al Río
description:
This original game from Japanese lands swells the catalogs of the murder mystery genre, increasingly widespread in the international recreational arena.

What is a murder mystery?
In this deductive genre, each player plays the role of a character with the dual purpose of clarifying a mystery and simultaneously achieving their own goals. The investigation, the negotiations with the rest of the cast and the exchange of information are fundamental pieces to solve the crime, but we must not forget that the murderer is one of you. You cannot believe everything they tell you, but neither can you win without trusting someone ... Enjoy this unique experience that, although used only once, will leave you wanting more.

England, late 16th century. A man is found dead in a town in the English countryside, apparently after his executioner plunged his head into the river that runs next to the inn where he was staying. The guests, the white witch so beloved in the municipality and the employees of the establishment themselves look at each other suspiciously trying to identify the culprit.

Il circo dei contrari has 50 tiles of varying difficulty, to be shuffled, placed face down and matched in pairs according to the opposing quality of the situation that characterises the character illustrated on the tile (tiger, lion, trapeze artist, clown, giraffe, elephant, mouse). If in doubt about the pairing, bring the sides of the tiles together at the point where the white symbol is located. If the symbol is completed by matching the two tiles, the pair is correct and the player continues the game. Whoever wins the most pairs wins.
The pairs are divided into two levels of increasing difficulty; you start with a limited number of pairs to learn the game mechanism, then increase the number of tiles and 'turn on' the challenge.
The game proposal, organised by quantity and quality of images, allows the stimulus to be customised according to the players' skills.

—description from the publisher (translated)

DIFFERO is a game for two players. It is played with a hexagonal board and 13 black and white pieces each.

In this game, you win by calculating the difference in strength between your side and the enemy side, and by supporting your own side or blocking the opponent’s side to reach the goal.

While steadily advancing your own piece, you often find yourself unable to stop the opponent’s pieces that is closing in. This is a fun abstract game with simple rules and thrills.

Components

     13 white and 13 black pieces each
     Hexagonal board with 5 on a side


End condition

     You win when one of your piece reach the goal.
     You lose if you cannot move any of your pieces in your turn.


Preparation

     Place pieces in predetermined positions.
     Choose your own color. White is the first turn.


Play procedure
On your turn, you moves only one of your pieces according to the following rules

     The piece moves on a line. The piece may jump over any number of pieces at once, either your own and your opponent’s.
     The number of steps to move the piece is always (number of your pieces) − (number of your opponent’s pieces) on the line to be moved. If this value is less than or equal to 0, the piece cannot move on the line.
     You cannot move the piece off the board or into a place already occupied by another piece. Also, you cannot move the piece into the opponent’s goal.


—description from the designer

A Labyrinth type game with a wooden tilt board where users must twist 2 knobs to move their marble(s) to the center of the maze while avoiding cut out holes. Each hole on the board has a printed point value, going up 5 points a hole until the center is reached.  The further players go in the maze, the higher their score.  Highest score or fastest run wins.

Published mid 20th century in a plain brown box (likely WWII era). Bug-out is US military slang.

Printing on board is Don't Bug-Out
Title on rules is Bug-Out

—user summary

Kockázat (Battle of Dice Armies) is a quick, fun 2 player asymmetrical fantasy skirmish game. Players select a tribe ( Humans, Elfs, Orks, Gnomes, Dwarves, Undead, Fire and Ice ) and assemble an army using different dice ( d4,d6,d8,d10 and d12 ). Dices will be the units that the player activates to move on the square grid board or attack the opponent's units. You start the game with all your dice set to it's highest value ( e.g a d6 starts on 6 ).
During a standard game you have an army of 100 points - each die in your army uses points equal to its highest value - so 5 pieces of d10 will use 50 points.Each tribe consists of 5 cards, one for each type of dice - these cards define what actions you can take.

On a player's turn he/she activates one group of dice ( by tapping the related card ), and performs 2 actions with each dice: Move, Attack, Rest and Special Action paying the cost of activation by reducing the dice's value. Whenever a dice's value reaches zero it is removed from the board. You can not activate a group again until you have activated all of your groups.
Some of the abilities will place status effects on a unit ( Bless, Curse, Burning, Shield, etc. ) - these are marked by putting colored discs under the dice.

You win the game by eliminating all of your opponents dice.

description:
New Ingredient cards for The Family Dinner.

The Kids Table expansion adds new menu items and short, childish prompts to bring out your inner child.

New Ingredient cards for The Family Dinner.

New Ingredient cards for The Family Dinner.

Nimmerlichtern is a cooperative board game for up to 4 players, throwing roleplay, area-control and fortress building mechanics into the mix. Each player takes over one kid out of a ragtag group of survivors and enters the world of a stodgy 90s suburb - where kids' dreams quite literally become reality. The goal is to fight back the monsters borne from the children's own subconsciousness, before the horde of evil dreams swallows the town completely.

To make matters worse, each kid is haunted by their very own primal fear, drafted randomly at the games' beginning out of a pool of six: The fears of loneliness, illness, pain, darkness, deceipt and decay. Starting as mere forebodings, these Grand Nightmares grow stronger and gain abilities at the end of each round, the pace heavily depends on the decisions of the players. At some point the primal fears manifest themselves and enter the game board to lead their minions against the last remaining refuge: the childrens' headquarters in the attic of an empty house. A game is lost once a Grand Nightmare and its pack reaches the headquarters and the subsequent battle is lost by the players.

To avoid that outcome, the players must use their rounds – each representing a day, from dawn to dusk - to break the curse cast upon their formerly cozy town. Gather resources, build weapons, craft fortifications and barricades and banish the monsters back to the realm of nightmares where they belong.

But foremost, they've got to learn to harness the force of dreams to their advantage: At the beginning of each round –in the morning – every player draws a card from their unique deck of dream cards, containing good dreams, bad dreams, dreams that affect the game board itself and also continuous dreams that can be collected in order to unleash unparalleled powers. Dream diaries, night time seances, REM manipulators and other bizarre objects can be used to get the decks under control – to a point, because, if Nimmerlichtern teaches you one thing, it's that the force of dreams is something to be reckoned with.

So be careful what you wish for: Your dreams just might come true.

—description from the publisher

description:
Somewhere in a 3rd world country, you are a laborer who tries to survive daily without enough state welfare benefits. When a pandemic occurs, you have to decide to risk your life by working outside or staying safe at home without cash to spend.

In 3rd world labour, there are construction sites that players have to build them up. When the 4th building was completed, the richest player will be a winner. Yes, in the 3rd world country the richest man always wins.

Each round, players have to decide to take action between "Go to work outside" or "Stay safe at home".

"Go to work outside" 

     Pick a construction site to work
     Place the corresponding color of blocks on the construction site.
     If the block is placed on a star symbol, take a bonus event card.
     Take income equal to the number of blocks placed and spend the expense.
     If there are other players on the same site, the player has to take a sickness card. When there are more than 2 sickness cards, reveal the rightmost card and take effect.


"Stay safe at home"

     Roll the dice and spend the expense.
     Discard all the sickness cards.
     Spend the money to buy the new skill card.


The player can take the free action to buy the lotto, pay their debt, and take more loans anytime in their turn.

For each building that was completed.

     The player who finished that building gets 3 coins.
     all players have to pay the interest according to their debt card.
     The lotto prize was announced.


And the winner is the richest player when the 4th building was completed!

Why ?

Because the richest man can survive by buying the better service that their government can't provide to citizens by themself.

—description from publisher

World of Tanks Miniatures Game is an easy to learn, quick play game, where you take command of a unit of tanks, seeking to destroy your opponent and secure victory! All the models in World of Tanks come ASSEMBLED, PAINTED, and BATTLE READY!

The Soviet SU-85 is a highly mobile Tank Destroyer, best used manoeuvering around the back of the battlefield looking to snipe enemies. Without a turret and having low Initiative, protecting your flanks or adding the V-2-34 engine is a good investment.

kCaloria gives you the chance to build an expansive and intricate food chain that must overcome obstacles at every turn! Sow the seeds to a thriving ecosystem with precious water tiles, grow verdant gardens with every fungi/plant tile, and fill this space with the perfect balance of herbivores, omnivores, carnivores, and humans too!

The objective of kCaloria is to play greater and more complex tiles in order to increase your final score. You will alternate placing tiles with your opponents and expand your respective food chains to dominate the table!

Jump Drive: Terminal Velocity is an expansion to Jump Drive. It adds new game cards, optional start worlds and goals, material for a fifth player, and a series of five challenges for solo play.

boardgame: そういうお前はどうなんだ？(Surprise Ending)
description:
Surprise Ending simulates a murder investigation. After a party in a mansion, one character turns up dead. Everyone at the table is a suspect, and the incompetent police does not care whether they catch the murderer, as long as they can arrest someone.

The game consists entirely of cards. Each player gets a character card and a character trait card. One of the unused characters is flipped open - that's the murder victim. Some details about the victim and the way they were murdered are also randomly assigned. Each player has four face-down "exposed" cards in front of them. On a turn, flip one card in front of another player open. This reveals some information about that player which is somehow suspicious. The entire group now has 30 seconds to discuss why this player is clearly the murderer. Then the accused player has 30 seconds to defend themselves. Afterwards, they flip another player's card up, etc. Once everyone was exposed once, more information about the victim is revealed, and another round starts (with 3 players, a third round as well). 
Afterwards, everyone points their finger at the most suspicious player at the table. The player(s) with the most fingers pointing at them will be arrested, draw an ending card and read out the ending of the story. 
Players who pointed at an arrested player and were not arrested themselves, win the game.

HEADS. Those of us who have one know how hard they work. They sniff, chew, listen, salivate, look around, kiss, spit, sing karaoke, get slapped, and so much more. I love elbows as much as anyone, but on a good day, my favorite elbow barely does half of these things.

When a head makes up its mind to do something, it needs to pick the right cranial nerve and send a signal through a hole in the skull called a foramen. (The plural of foramen is “foramina” for some reason.)

In Foramina, you and up to three friends each build a tableau of cranial nerves. By rolling dice you activate the cranial nerves with the same number as your roll, hopefully earning axons. You can then spend axons to gain more cranial nerves and eventually purchasing foramina. The first player to three foramina wins the game.

EHCG is mainly deck construction, dice rolling and role playing strategy game set in world of ice hockey.

Your task as a manager of ice hockey club is to pick your coach and lineup of 12 players, assign the role for each player, choose the tactics, and lead your team to the victory.

Each player chooses unique situational cards, which can be used throughout the game. During the match, you have to guide your players in different situations from the faceoff to shot. Significant part of the game are 1v1 battles of players, where your preparation and skill can truly shine. If you are successful, you will gain many bonuses and you can affect the strategy of both teams. Additionally, as the manager, you have many options in every situation to change the course of the match and turn the result in your favor.

EHCG is game for 2 players, who can battle over and over again in a single match. However, it contains setup of 20 teams, therefore it offers option to integrate up to 20 people, who can compete in different modes such as tournament mode or season mode.

In the entertaining memo game "Lenny's Limo", the children drive through the city in their limousines and collect new passengers of a special kind such as Yetis, paddlers or a beach family. But watch out: only matching couples are allowed on board! All children start with a front and a rear part of their limousine. Whoever can place the most passenger tiles in between during the course of the game wins.

As in many memo games, two face down tiles are always revealed per turn. But "Lenny's Limo" is not about finding the exact counterparts, but about finding matching pairs that result in a common, funny motif. This can be the two yetis, the paddlers or the beach family.
The players take turns and answer questions in order to be the first with a full set of colour cards (5 colour cards).

The Subantarctic Islands: bleak, inhospitable rocks in the stormy Southern Ocean. You are on the shipwrecked crew which finds itself castaway, bedraggled, and needing a strong leader.

On your turn, you'll choose between adding crew of varying value to one of two challenges, or else attempting to perform a challenge with the crew already attached. When you add a crew member to a challenge, you may play them face-up (to achieve the challenge effect), or face-down (keeping their value a secret). If all the crew members on a challenge meet the required challenge value, you succeed in the challenge and collect its points. If not, your opponent gets the respect points instead. First player to 6 points wins!

Beep Bapp Boom is a last man standing gameplay. Game start with default of 30 seconds timer. This timer is adjustable depends on the player.

During player's turn, this timer will be reduced by 1 as default. Player can use any attack or defensive card to manipulate the timer. When the timer become 0 at the player's turn, the player will be eliminated if there is no defense or counter cards.

So player need to think smart to avoid the timer reached 0 at their turn.

-description from publisher

Each player is dealt a stockpile of 15 cards. The winner will be whoever manages to empty their stockpile first. Cards are played onto four shared building piles in numerical sequence from 1 to 12. On each turn, a player draws until they have five cards in their hand, and then plays cards from: their hand, a top card of their discard piles, or their stockpile, or they can use a coin in place of a card, or they can use a coin to take a card from their opponents stock pile or discard pile. Some cards require using coins to pay a toll. At the end of their turn, a player must discard onto one of their four personal discard piles. Strategy involves the organizing of cards into the discard piles and using your coins wisely, care in not setting up the next players for good plays, knowing when to play from which option, and especially the timing of playing a valuable "Skip-Bo" wildcard. Players still try and get rid of all the cards from their Stock Pile, but the coins can be used to help fill in a sequence of cards or even to buy a card from another player's Discard Pile or Stock Pile!

DUEL BOY is a trading card game with unique seven-card packs. The game was first distributed at the 2022 Fall Tokyo Game Market. The packs include seven cards for playing the game, one divider card, and a sticker the owner can wear to help find opponents to play the game.

The game was designed to be played while standing (for instance, while in line to buy a game) and does not require a table. After a match, the players swap two cards, with each player leaving with five cards they entered the game with and two of their opponent's cards.

After an initial shuffle of cards, a player has one chance to reshuffle, but otherwise must play their hand as it has been drawn. A game is made up of several rounds, and the first player to win three hands or cause their opponent to not be able to play a card on their first turn of a hand wins the game.

Players can play cards only from the rightmost end of their hand and must pay the cost of cards with the cards from the left end of their hand. Once per hand, a player can put the cards from their discard pile on the bottom of the deck — in the exact order they were discarded.

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach space 50 wins the game.

The game was published about 1930 as a giveaway for various department stores.

—user summary

WordUp! Is a quick-playing word guessing game built for 2 or more players. The game plays well individually or in teams. You’ll flip over a card for a category (for this example, Animal) and then roll the die for the number of letters (let’s say 6). You have 60 seconds to give a 6 Letter Animal. Your score is based on the letters used in your answer.  If you were to write down “Walrus”, you would receive 22 points, because...

W = 10 Points

A = 1 Point

L = 2 Points

R = 2 Points

U = 5 Points

S = 2 Points.

But there's a catch! If two people write down the same word, they both receive 0 points for that round.

—description from the designer

This is a wargame rule system to recreate simple, fast playing World War Two era submarine actions with miniatures on the tabletop. The rules are for solo or co-op play where the players command the U-boats and the game handles the convoy of allied merchants ships.

Players will roll command dice against their submarine's crew skill in order to take actions.  Failed actions will allow the convoy to move for each fail.  The U-boats will be in a race to sink or damage as many ships as possible before the convoy makes it safely off the board.  It takes skill and luck to maneuver and line up your shots.

The game can also add hazards to the U-boats in the form of escorts and aircraft that will attempt to sink them by bombing, ramming or depth charges if they are detected during play.

Victory is determined by the number of ships sunk, if any, before the convoy exits the board.  Submarine players loose if they do poorly or are sunk during the contact.

description:
“Once more unto the breach, dear friends, once more?”—Cry God for King Harry, England and St. George” speech from Shakespeare’s Henry V, Act III, 1598.

The siege of Harfleur marked the beginning of King Henry V of England's invasion of France. The bitter and protracted siege nearly ended King Hal's ambitions, and the French garrison's prolonged resistance allowed the French King to mobilize a huge army. The two forces would meet at Agincourt in a decisive battle that shaped the future destinies of both countries.

Players use a standard deck of playing cards and six sided die to conduct the siege. Actions include bombardments, sallies and counter-mining by the French garrison, mining and assault actions for the British. Both sides must make the most of their activations to bring about an end to the siege, while also not running up a butcher's bill in the process. The game is very interactive, true to the history, plays in an hour of less and is easily learned and taught.

boardgame: Villa's Vengeance: The Raid on Columbus, New Mexico, March 9, 1916
description:
The Mexican Revolution that began in 1910 with the overthrow of dictator Porfirio Diaz degenerated into civil war following the assassination of President Francisco Madero. By end of 1915 the forces of President Carranza were arrayed against a variety of leaders supporting either themselves or General Victoriano Huerta. Pancho Villa, commander of the Mexican Army del Norte, at first supported Carranza, but then went rogue after the US Government recognized Carranza’s government. Historians still debate Villa’s reasons, but in early March 1916 he decided to enter the US and raid the town of Columbus, New Mexico. Although the town was where the 13th Regiment US Cavalry was garrisoned, he knew most of the troopers were scattered along the border. Believing the town was only weakly guarded, his force of just under 500 men were to steal arms, provisions and horses while also wreaking as much havoc as possible. Such a raid he believed could galvanize the US government into a retaliatory strike that would drag Carranza’s government into a war with the US it could not win, or a humiliating capitulation that would outrage the Mexican people. Either way, Villa reasoned, it would mean the downfall of Carranza.

Villa’s Vengeance is an introductory level wargame simulation portraying the March 9, 1916 raid on Columbus, New Mexico. Players are cast in the roles of Villa’s raiding force and the defending US regular and militia forces.

—description from the publisher

description:
The GOTHIES Deckbuilding Game is set in the mythical Underworld of Xibalba where players enter the underworld as Dreamers, or Streya, where they can play head-to-head with the goal of waking the other player up in order to win the game.

The game can also be played in a solitaire or cooperative mode where the players try and defend the well of Ceiba, or the gateway from the underworld to the real world, from attack by the monsters sent by the God of the Well. The God, who is no longer satisfied with the underworld, wants to send his monsters to the real world to expand his power.

The Players begin the game with 45 Timestones (life total) 10 starter cards, 6 of which grant seed power (purchase new cards) and 4 have song power (attack). As players buy GOTHIE (creature) cards and Land (shield) cards from the Mercado (tableau) they add them to their decks, where they can achieve synergy bonuses based on the heart rune of the cards played. The game plays similarly to other head-to-head deckbuilders in this respect, but can be converted to play cooperatively using the same deckbuilding mechanics.

In the coop mode, this plays very similar to a tower defense game like Castle Panic, but the threats all come from a single direction, and the monsters can attack the well and "possess" the GOTHIES that the players have in their played card zone. This ups the difficulty because the players' former GOTHIE cards now fight against them until they are restored.

The game is for 2 players, and can be expanded to 4 with two sets. The game comes with 162 cards and counters to track timestones.

-description from publisher

boardgame: マストフォロー(大嘘) (Must Follow (Big Lie))
description:
Must Follow (Big Lie) is a trick-taking adjacent game which uses cards that have words on them, rather than the expected suit and rank cards.

The lead person plays a card from their hand, and the other players "must follow", but rather than following suit, they will follow through word association.

The lead person then declares who has done the worst job of following, and that player is eliminated from contention of winning the trick. Next, the lead person will declare who has done the best job of following, and that player is also eliminated from contention of winning the trick!  The remaining player wins the trick.

Players are, of course, encouraged to plead their case.

description:
Each round in Demigods, each player trains a hero by taking a demigod card and artifact card at random from the center of the table and pairing them together. The demigod cards feature a strength value and possibly symbols, while the artifact cards (possibly) modify the value of the paired demigod card in some way, with the maximum value for any combination being 30.

A starting pot of 1 Aischune (meaning "dishonor") is created, then in turn players take one of three actions:


     Adding more Aischune to the pot up to a maximum of 10.
     Knocking on the table to stay in without raising.
     Discarding their cards to fold the hand, with the first folder gaining 3 Aischune and each other folder gaining 2 Aischune.


Once the maximum pot has been reached or all players have passed, players reveal their totals, with the weakest player "winning" the pot. In ties, the pot is split between those with the lowest score.

Continue playing rounds until someone has collected at least 30 Aischune at which time the game ends and whoever has the fewest Aischune wins. In the event of a tie, keep playing rounds until one player has the lowest score.

The Full Ahead! miniatures wargame rules were devised to enable wargamers to refight the seemingly forgotten naval battles in the last years of the nineteenth and the early years of the twentieth century (1894-1905). During this period naval technology advanced at a rapid pace but tactically everything was still uncertain, heavy guns, light quick firers, the ram and torpedo all having their proponents, with nobody having the concrete evidence of a major action to confirm or refute their theories.

It is hoped that these rules have captured the spirit of the period they purport to represent without too much of its complexity. Indeed these rules were written with full scale fleet actions in mind with each player assigned the role of a commander in charge of anything from a squadron to an entire fleet. The rules are therefore designed to enable players to concentrate on tactics rather than book keeping and complicated time consuming gun v armour charts etc.

These rules were written with particular reference to the naval actions of the following wars:

Brazilian Civil War 1893-1894
Sino-Japanese War 1894-1895
Spanish-American War 1898
Russo-Japanese War 1904-1905

—description from the rulebook

Privateer is the first title in the Articles of War series. It is a fast-paced, hex-based naval engagement where 2 to 6 players use a combination of sailing, cannon-fire, and the occasional boarding action to sink or capture the ships belonging to their opponents. For each enemy ship dispatched, a gold doubloon is awarded to the attacker with ultimate victory going to whichever player has the most gold at the end of the game.

The central mechanic of AoW: Privateer is a special dice economy, where the active player rolls a combination of sail and cannonball dice based on the number of ships they have active and their strategic preferences. After rolling, they spend these dice one-by-one in order to maneuver their ships into position and target their cannons to eliminate enemies. Islands and reefs, placed by the players during setup, provide a limitless variety of map arrangements.

description:
There is a tribe of people that can communicate with animals (Wanyama). You, the player, have the ability to borrow the animals’ powers. There is a law in this tribe, that the leader’s ability to communicate with the animals is extraordinary.

In this game, you will compete with other players to become the leader of the tribe. The object of the game is to reach the goal with the help of the Wanyama.

This game is played by gaining points as you draw cards and play them against your opponents, you can play this game in a short time with a steady rhythm.

—description from the designer

Oh dear, a thick rain cloud is gathering over the farm, but the animals are still in the pasture! Together, the children try to get the animals into the barn by rolling the dice before the rain cloud is full of drops. Depending on the game variant, the die shows either the shape or the color of the animal that may be placed in the barn or a drop of water (blue colour) that moves into the cloud.
All players win or lose together against the rain cloud and thus experience an intensive "we-feeling". In addition to the differentiation of shapes and colors, the game also promotes concentration, initial understanding of rules, fine motor skills and language development.

boardgame: Mutant Year Zero: Zone Wars – Robots & Psionics
description:
In the Robots & Psionics expansion set for Zone Wars two new factions are introduced: the robotic Mechatron Hive and the Nova Cult psionics. With these you can now play three- or four-player games, turning the fun and mayhem up to a new level!

The Robots & Psionics expansion also includes new terrain pieces, additional Zone and artifact cards, and a further ten beautifully sundropped miniatures, ready to be used right away.


     Mechatron Hive: A collective of robots having recently awoken to become self-aware, seeking their place in the post-apocalyptic world.
     Nova Cult: A secretive cult of human mutants with powerful psionic mutations and unknown ambitions.

It’s the 1960s. Each Player is at the helm of a zoomy Advertising Agency on New York’s Madison Avenue. They compete to acquire the Advertising Accounts of major brands spending millions of dollars each year on advertising. There are 32 Accounts with annual Ad Budgets ranging from $1 million to $10 million. 
 Your Personnel are the key to success in the ad game. To aquire accounts you must employ both ‘Creatives’ and Account handlers (business types known as ‘Suits’). This will require strategic risk-taking in the deployment of the Agency’s two resources: Personnel and Working Capital.

Players may choose any of four different Strategies for playing the game: Ethical, Co-Operative, Payola and Peacenik. Each strategy involves both an upside and downside and may be abandoned or switched during the game.
 The winner of the game is the first Player to acquire Accounts whose Advertising Budgets total $50 million or more.  Or you can impose a time limit, the winner being the one commanding the most Advertising Budgets at the end of play. The maximum possible target is $80 million, which could take a long time.

A spinner decides if the active player is asked a question or if he has to choose 1 of 4 characteristics. If the characteristics are positive, the chosen one should apply best to him. If they are negative, he chooses the one whom he disagrees the most with. 
Before the active player reveals his decision, all other players guess secretly the answer of the active player and may bid in addition with chips. Then the answers are revealed. The guessing players receive chips, if they guessed correctly. The active player receive as many chips as the most successful guesser. If nobody guessed correctly, he may lose chips. The player with the most chips wins.

—user summary

In the year 1996, the tectonic plates shifted and destroyed an underwater city. With the city abandoned, two nations fight over the remaining resources. Each player controls a submarine. Players must build their decks by combining sea life and scrap in order to destroy their opponent.

Tectonic Shift is a multi-deck-builder where players build 3 decks at a time. Cards provide different functions and uses depending on the deck they are added to with the goal to deplete your opponent’s health down to 0 to win.

—description from the designer

Oda Nobunaga once said that he would hunt his enemies down "on every hill and valley." That term, in Japanese, is "yama yama tani tani".

In Yama Yama Tani Tani 2 or 4 players choose from among 6 different Chinese warlords of the Three Kingdoms era or from among 6 different Japanese daimyo of the Warring States period to battle each other in a no-luck, no-dice faceoff for supremacy in their respective countries.

Each leader comes with his 9 most trusted generals and must be divided into two armies of five units each. Players select their first 5-man army for the first battle, and assign each general a troop type based on that general's allowances and the strategic advantages that player wants to pursue. Then each player assigns those units to the first battlefield.

Generals move about the battlefield, attacking enemy generals, but with a twist. The skills that each general has allow him to do more than just make basic attacks. They allow him to both manipulate the enemy units and his own to his advantage, but, more importantly, the correct use of a skill can trigger another friendly general to use one of his skills, adding more damage to the attack. That general's use of a skill could trigger another, an so on. The real strategy is trying to make the most of your generals' skills and build wildly powerful combos of attacks on the battlefield to destroy your opponent's army.

After each battle, the winning player gains Mandate according to the number of his generals still alive. After 6 battles, if one player has more Mandate, that player is the winner. If players are tied, there will be a 7th and final battle, using all 10 of those players' generals, to determine the victor.

-description from designer

Two to four players play cross-shaped pieces to form pairs, triples, or four-in-a row of identical symbols. The more combinations formed, the more the player scores.

Each player has 12 cross pieces in their color. Players shuffle their tiles and form a face-up stack in front of them. In turn, each player takes the top piece from the stack and plays it to the board, trying to match the symbols already in play. The tiles must be nestled together, not end to end.

Play continues until all crosses are played. The winner is the player with the most points at the end.

This is a symbol-matching game similar to others, but different in that it uses cross-shaped pieces instead of dominoes, triangles, or hexagons.

Rise of the Gummies is a hybrid collectible card game that brings your play to life! Cards are played by showing them to an app on your device, which keeps track of the rules and game state so that you don't have to.

Rise of the Gummies takes about five minutes to learn, and can be played in fifteen minutes by one or two players aged 8+. The goal of the game is to build the tallest stack of Gummies. To build a stack, players simultaneously reveal cards from their hand to summon Gummies onto their side. When a card is played, the app recognizes it, adds the Gummy to the game, and executes any of its special abilities.

At the end of each round, the player with the taller stack gains a point. The first player to earn five points wins the game! But watch out - whoever loses the round earns a Salt, which some Gummies can spend to perform powerful abilities.

Build a deck that combines the strengths of four unique Gummy planets to create the tallest stack and be crowned the Champion of the Gummiverse!

—description from the publisher

UNknown SUBject is a fast-paced social deduction game based on the hit TV show Criminal Minds! Play as familiar FBI Behavioral Analysis Unit Agents to identify the killer in your midst, or work against the goals of the BAU to get away with your crimes and escape! Investigate, use the profile, and accuse your fellow players to come out on top!


    FAST & EASY—The average game takes about 20 minutes and once you get started it's easy to play over and over again!
    FAN-FAVORITE—UNknown SUBject is full of nods to Criminal Minds, from the characters to the profile you build, with simple enough gameplay for fans to play alongside those unfamiliar with the show!
    NEW GAME EVERY TIME—With different characters and different roles for each game you play, no two playthroughs are exactly alike!


SOCIAL DEDUCTION—Use your knowledge of your fellow players to try and uncover the Unsub or convince the group of your innocence!

    BE THE CHARACTERS—The game includes J.J., Garcia, Morgan, Reid, and Rossi alongside the Eyewitness and Unsubs! 


—description from the publisher

Allow Me to Mansplain is a conversational party game that places an emphasis on the words that men say before they mansplain.  With light elements of improv and roleplaying, players must mansplain topics to their teammates in three categories: define, explain, and compare. The team with the most correctly guessed mansplained topics wins.

—description from the publisher

A dying world. A closing storm. One chance to endure.

Three Souls: Rivals is a competitive card-battle game where timing and positioning decide everything. In a world torn apart by a relentless storm, the last surviving tribes send their chosen champions into the wasteland.

Each player controls a Soulboard with three Rows, tracking its own Life and Resistance. Players draw from a shared deck of Attack, Support, and Artifact Cards to strike opponents, react to threats, and turn the flow of battle. As Rows are broken and players are eliminated, every decision becomes more critical.

When only two champions remain, the game shifts into a Duel with a unique turn structure.

Three Souls: Rivals — Easy to learn. Deeply tactical. Unforgiving.

Only one champion will stand!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Cthulhu: Death May Die – Unknowable Box
description:
Unknowable Box contains all Kickstarter exclusive stretch goals for Cthulhu: Death May Die - Fear of the Unknown unlocked during the campaign.


     28 Investigators miniatures and boards
     7 Monsters with Unknown Monster card
     NUG & YEB elder ones box

     Lost Episode 4 Box: Hypnos
     Lost Episode 5 Box: Dreams in the Witch House
     Lost Episode 6 Box: The Colour Out of Space
     Lost Episode 7 Box: The Horror in the Museum

Campsite has players competing for the best camping spot. Play cards on top of each other to link elements like forests, mountains, and camping trails together to score the most points.

A clever card-based "tile" laying game that focus on maximizing your limited space!

Luck and strategy combine in the great outdoors (no actual camping required). Includes 72 cards 42 tokens, scorepad, and pencil.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cthulhu: Death May Die – Bonus Relic Cards
description:
Two Kickstarter Exclusive Relic cards included with the Season 4 Expansion when acquired through the Cthulhu: Death May Die – Fear of the Unknown Kickstarter campaign:

- Skull Bracelet
- Ivory Figure

boardgame: Ashes Reborn: Red Rains – The Corpse of Viros
description:
Ashes Reborn: Red Rains offers players both new and old an exciting new format in which to wield their favorite Ashes cards! The Red Rains have returned and with them the Chimera — towering monstrosities that threaten to wipe humanity from the face of the planet! Only the Phoenixborn, those superhuman wizard-warriors of Argaia, stand between humanity and extinction.

The Red Rains turns Ashes Reborn into a solitaire or cooperative experience, with players banding together to fend off and slay one of the fiendish Chimera. Choose your Phoenixborn and build your deck, then face this foe alone or with a friend you can trust. Wield cards already in your collection, or add in the new spells and allies included here! Everything old is new again, including fresh takes on classic Phoenixborn!

Prepare for an all-new Ashes experience, and prepare to see this beloved game in a brand new light!

-description from publisher

family: Game: Ashes – Rise of the Phoenixborn

description:
Remnant is a build your character card game where you build your character by buying and selecting cards of your choice to prepare your character for the final epic battle.

2-6 players

On your turn, you roll the dice. Moving your character holder that many spaces in the direction of your choosing. Resolve what you land on to collect cards or coins. Once completed you have the opportunity to buy cards for your character. Once you have 3 features or 3 characteristic cards you can no longer buy those until you have 3 of the opposite.

Choose the cards you want and even replace cards if you get the same types. Once you become a full character you can even buy weapons and armor to deck out your fantastic creation, giving you attack and defense benefits as well as special interactions with different cards on other players.

Find your opponent to attack and roll a dice to multiply your attack or defense.

Final circle occurs when all players become a "full" character. This forces battle after battle in a brutal all out war.
Last one standing wins!

A talking electronic plush sloth acts as the timer, judge and prop in this two team charades type game.

The sloth will randomly assign one of the three super slo-mo clue categories: Sloth Charades, SL-ACTING or sloth-ercise. The active team then has 45 seconds to guess the correct clue. The player assigned to be the round actor, though, cannot move too quickly or else the sloth will get upset and buzz. Three buzzes and your turn is over.

The non-active team can steal the clue whenever it is not guessed by the active team. Guessing a clue correctly results in one point. Alternating play continues until one team has accumulated 5 points and therefore wins the game.

Fila consists of a 21-card deck, with each card having a point value. To start, reveal a number of cards face up based on the player count.

On a turn, draw a card from the deck and without looking at it show this card to 2-3 other players, then place it face down in front of you. After all players have three cards in front of them, write down your predictions for which player has the most points or whether there's a tie for high score, then predict how many points each player has. Players win or lose points based on the accuracy of their predictions, and whoever has the highest score wins.

9Peak is an abstract game about collecting hats. Players are dealt a hand of numbered cards, and gradually play cards from their hand in order to predict the highest possible scoring sets. The cards on the board increase the “value range” of a particular number, allowing the player to aim for a higher hand.

The game ends when all the cards in the hand have been played out, and the player with the highest total of cards wins the game!

華武拳! (Kabuken!) is a battling game with the theme of a tournament of martial artists from many different countries, fighting to decide the true success of the legendary martial art "Kabuken!". The goal of the game is to be the player with the most HP at the end of the game, after player(s) are KO'd or the draw pile has been depleted a number of times.

In this game you can either replenish your hand or play 1 of 6 types of melds to attack the opponent to your left. This in turn can be raw damage taken, defended (reduced damage), or counterattacked by the defending player. Counterattacking involves playing 1-3 specific cards which cause the next clockwise player to take damage, this in turn can be counterattacked, and so on until the player that does not counterattack takes damage (and replenishes their hand).

The advanced rules of the game include personal Character Cards that have different special abilities and Special Technique Cards that have either 1-time or passive effects.

While normally this game is only playable by 2-4 players, there are 5-7 player variant rules included which retain many of the same rules, with the addition of a first KO incentive.

The game is about exploring uncharted waters and finding and bringing back valuable creatures and artifacts.

In the game, players move freely through three different types of ocean areas with different risk/reward, acquiring tiles as they go. They can also procure equipment for their exploration vessels at junk stores on the map. Some equipment can be used to steal items from other ships, so players need to consider the ship's current position as they take action.

After an item tile is acquired, it must be brought back to the designated square before it is counted as a victory point. Each item tile has a symbol on it, and matching the same symbol will give you a bonus to your victory points.

The player with the most victory points after 12 rounds wins.

-description from designer

The Advanced Tobruk System returns to Finland with six new scenarios and four new maps to further explore the remarkable defense of Suomi from the overwhelming might of Soviet invaders.

The scenarios include:
- Louhenjoki Loss – Louhenjoki, Finland, December 5th 1939

- Vuoksi Hold-Outs – Vuoksi River Sector, Karelian Isthmus, Finland, March 1st 1940

- Wooden Redoubts – Along Lake Suvanto and the Taipale River, east-of Rauti, Finland, December 30th 1939

- Between a Jarvi and a Hard Place – Between Lake Kuolema and Lake Muolaa, Finland, February 8th 1940

- Following Orders – Tolvajarvi, Finland, December 12th 1939

- Icy Frog-March – Near Suomussalmi, Finland, December 30th 1939

This package includes one sheet of vehicle and heavy weapons (88 counters), one sheet of personnel and support weapons (155 counters), two sheets of AFV cards, four 18' x 12" cardstock maps and the usual Battlefield Walkaround with module-specific rules.

All the combat counters required for these scenarios are included in this folio package, but players will need the ATS rulebook, play aids and standard system markers to play, plus one or more 10-sided dice.

description:
Princess of Seasons is a may-follow trick taking game similar to Napoleon but with a few wrinkles thrown in. The theme is that there is a Kingdom of Seasons that controls the seasons, but mischievous fairies threaten to disrupt them. One player (and an helper) will play as the Kingdom of Seasons and the rest as the mischievous fairies.

You go around bidding to decide the Princess of Seasons player. You bid the number of Picture Cards you will take (Maximum of 18) and name the Trump Season, this effects the hierarchy of rank. If Spring (3, 4, and 5) are chosen, the ranking of number cards is 5>4>3>2>1>12>...>8>7>6. Once the princess has been decided, they name a specific card and the player that possesses that card joins the Princess team secretly (it is possible to name a card you have or in the kitty so as to be alone).

Then a may-follow trick taking hand occurs. If the princess team meets or exceeds their bet of acquired cards, they win and points are tallied. There are 4 fairy cards that beat all number cards,1 princess card that forces fairy cards to be played (and wins a hand), and a mischievous fairy card that removes a card from play.

Micro Mars: 2-player PvP Edition is a fun and fast Roll&Write/Print&Play game of open space adventure where the players make decisions about how to capture more territory on the map.

To start a game, players roll 2 dice in turn. In the following, you can find the elements for the rolled values 
of the dice in the route field. Since you have two elements, you can craft a figure.

The goal of the game is to fill the space of the map with your figures. To get more points, you need to fill in more spaces in the points field. Your points per round are all the cells that you filled in the points field minus the unfilled cells on the map. A player who collects more points wins the game.

—description from the publisher

In pre-war Poznań, a German spy network operates, transmitting secret information to the Reich. In order to detect the spy, Polish cryptologists and agents of the 'two' try to decipher the intercepted information and determine the meeting place of the agents.
The game takes place on the map of interwar Poznań, in which the training center for Polish codebreakers was located. By visiting special locations, players collect more tokens, attributes and hints helpful in deciphering the message.
The objective of the German part of the game is to make a meeting between an agent and a spy to exchange information and then leave the city without being caught by any Polish agent.
The most important element of the game is breaking an encrypted message prepared on the basis of one of the simplest ciphers - the Caesar cipher. However, without knowing the keyword, it can be quite a challenge.

—user summary
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
You work in a science lab and secretly build new monsters. These monsters come from peas and are called Creepeas. Create as many complete monsters as possible. But watch out! There can also be mismonsters, which will spoil the fun if you don’t complete them. Combine the funniest limbs and create your own crazy monsters! Can you build more complete monsters than your opponents? Whoever earns the most points wins this monstrously fun game!

Creepeas is a card game for 2 to 5 players, and takes about 20 minutes to complete. Combine cards together to earn points and win the game by having the most points of all players.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): Shadow Elves Faction Deck
description: Striking from the darkness comes the Shadow Elves; an elite band of assassins led by the summoner Selundar. There is no sacrifice too great for Selundar; delve deep into forbidden knowledge that can corrupt your mind, or tether your very soul into your most powerful warriors. Strike from the shadows or with the speed of light to defeat any summoner who stands in your way!

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): Wayfarers Faction Deck
description: Embark on an adventure across Itharia with the Wayfarers! This eclectic band of travelers have an endless appetite for adventure, and will follow their leader Celeste to the ends of the earth if it means gaining another tale to tell. Slip past your adversaries, confound them with performing arts, and make your way to new horizons! So pack your bags and join Celeste for a journey like no other!

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Trederran Banshee Shock Troopers Enemy Pack
description:
Armed with devastating Shockwave guns, the Banshee Shock Troopers of Trederra are close range heavy assault troops, beaming out rays of sonic death to blow apart fortifications and scatter defenders. They are deployed as a vanguard to a larger force, clearing barricades and toppling buildings to clear a path for troop formations and the steel beasts of the tank corps. Formed into small teams of specialists, they often accompany other Trederran formations on the battlefield.

This Enemy Pack contains everything you need to engage the heavy Trederran Banshee Shock Troopers in battle.  Included are 3 Banshee Shock Trooper models, the large Enemy Record Sheet for them, the Beam Template and counters, and all of the Threat Cards and Faction Cards needed to add these deadly Enemies to your games of Shadows of Brimstone.

Models are Thermal Plastic Miniatures manufactured in the USA and include 3 unique sculpts for Trederran Banshee Shock Troopers, and 3 x 30mm slotted Bases.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Long Arm of the Law Game Supplement
description:
This 15 card supplement adds a grouping of new cards, including the new Warrant Cards for use with keyword Law Heroes, the new 'On the Run' Enemy Trait, new Gear and Mine Encounters, and new rules for making any mission 'A Matter of Justice' mission, as long as a keyword Law Hero is in the Hero Party.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Holiday 2022 Promo Cards
description:
Black Friday and Holiday 2022 exclusive cards for Shadows of Brimstone, received from orders of $100 or more on their website during this sale. Includes:

Trederran War Skull - Trederran Artifact - (Once per adventure, draw a growing dread card and add it to the stack to gain +2 combat or +2 shots for an attack).

Rotting Within - Darkness - (Every hero must immediately make a spirit 5+ test, rolling one fewer die than normal for each corruption they currently have (minimum 1 die). If failed, roll once on the mutation chart).

Also made available during the GenCon 2023 sale.

Also made available during the Labor Day 2025 sale.

This a card flipping fast pace card game.

Aim of the Game is to pick up and align in a row, numbers 1-8 followed by the winner card. You can play the game two ways, easiest is collecting 1-8 of any color you find first, or to make it harder, you must find match all your number cards with the color of the number 1 card you picked up first. You can also remove numbers to make the game shorter in playing time.

How to play the game  
1)Place the Token cards to the sides so it is easily reached by all players. This is where used special cards will be placed.

2)Each player takes one My Turn card to keep until they wish to use it at any point in the game. The rest are put to one side.

3)Decide if you are collecting different colors or the same colors of each number.

4)Place all the cards face down on the table, mix them up, and spread them evenly.

5)Anyone goes first by rolling the dice and starts the game. Pass the dice around to the left until someone rolls the BBD a number 6.

6) The player who gets a 6 then starts to pick up cards to try and find a number 1 card, once found the No.2 then the number 2 card, and so on. You must pick up cards in number order. (so if you don’t have a 1, you cannot pick up a 2).

7) You can only pick up one card at a time and you must place it back on the table face down if not needed or not next in the sequence required.

8) While one player is searching for number cards, the other players take it in turn to roll the dice to try and get a 6.

9) When another player rolls the BBD a 6, it becomes their turn to search for number cards. The previous player searching for cards has to stop searching and join the other players in rolling the dice.

10) Game continues until one person has aligned numbers 1-8 plus the winner card

Special cards 
1) My turn - Use this when you want to jump in the game
2) Steal - Take a card from any other player
3) Bye Bye Dice - If dice is on the table, pick it up and throw it anywhere you want
4) Any Number - This can be used as any number
5) Stop searching - If you pick this up you must stop searching for cards for five seconds
6) 5 Seconds - All rollers of the dice must stop for five seconds

-description from designer

boardgame: Dungeon Drop: Tavern Tales – Talk of the Tavern and Inside out Dungeon Scenario
description:
This double-sided card offers two new ways to play/ scenarios for Dungeon Drop: Tavern Tales
- Talk of the Tavern
- Inside out Dungeon

It was given out at Gen Con 2022 from Phase Shift Games

"Postcard Stalingrad" is a relatively fast and simple but historical 9-turn wargame about the 23Aug42-19Nov42 Battle for Stalingrad city.
12 starting units - mostly divisions - for Germans and Russians, each, with some Russian reinforcements and replacements (if they make it across the Volga) and (more) time pressure of Turn 7 withdrawal of 1 motorized and 1 panzer division to cover campaign's flanks which were becoming "worrisome." }:-)
German airpower and 1 later turn of Pioneers/Engineers bonus.  Pressed attack.
Russian east bank artillery and "hugging" tactic.  Chuikov.  Snipers Zaitsev vs. Koenig.  
Russian "No land East of the Volga" No Retreat! orders.

And anyone is free to print this or my other 5 (so far) postcard wargames off in postcard size/format only (4x6/A6 or 5x8/A5)... as long as my copyright statement and the Dedication are retained ... to mail/give to family and friends.

(Of course, for your own personal copy you will want to photocopy-enlarge the components.)

—description from the designer

description:
Let's PLAAY Tennis is a “pro tennis toolbox” that includes several components, allowing you to dial in to the level of detail you desire. If quick play is what you’re looking for, the Tournament Mode module will let you simulate the early and middle rounds of an entire tournament in about 20 minutes, reducing the field to a handful of finalists. You can then use the “For The Set” and “No Rally” modules to resolve the remaining tournament matches in 10 to 20 minutes more.

On the other hand, if you’re looking for detail, the “point-by-point” module will let you watch the action unfold as if you were courtside, with serves, returns, difficult shots, winners, momentum and even unusual and rare results.

The “For the Set” module is the main tool in the box. It serves as a bridge between the two extremes of speed and detail. It will instantly resolve the preliminary action of a set and take you to the potential set-deciding game--one player with the opportunity to win the sixth and deciding game of the set. From there, you can get to your final result with extended drama, point-by-point, or instantly via the “No Rally” results.

"Battle of Poltava" is a low to moderate complexity two player tactical wargame covering the climatic battle of the Great Northern War in 1709. The game takes about 90 minutes to play. 
One player commands the Swedish forces and the other player commands the Russian forces.
