description:
Formula1cm is a (Print and Play) paper&pencil racing game where you challenge one or more opponents in a one-lap Grand Prix.
Each race is a 2-player race, but you can also play solo, against the clock, or against more opponents in a World Cup championship. 
During your turn, you must pick a movement value, choose the best path, and mark (with a little dot) the new position of your car.
You must guess your movement value (in centimeters) "by eye," and if your opponent is unsure, he can check your move with the ruler; if you make a mistake, you lose the engine, tyres, and you get penalties.

—description from the designer

This compilation combines three previously published Unlock! scenarios and presents them for the first time in the Nordic languages.

The scenarios are:

    The Noside Show released in Timeless Adventures.
    The Dragon's Seven Tests released in Epic Adventures.
    The Tonipal's Treasure released in Mystery Adventures.

Two competing secret societies: On the one hand, the mystical and traditional Thorn Society, whose followers come from the most influential families. With their strict structures and their eloquent appearance, they have been operating in secret for many decades.
On the other hand, the new "machine clan" with a soft spot for steam-powered devices. Emerging from the underground, their behavior often seems clumsy, as they like to rely on their muscular strength when exchanging arguments.

But which one is most likely to give you power and money? If you play cold-blooded enough and change sides at the right moment, you will triumph in the end.

Gameplay:
It is played clockwise. On your turn, you must perform one of the following two actions:
ACT or PAUSE

ACT
Play 1 card from your hand face up in front of you and immediately resolve the card effect.

PAUSE
Return all of your played cards back to your hand.

Then it is the next person's turn.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Egri Csillagok is a cooperative board game for 4-6 people, which evokes the spirit of the heroes of Eger and Géza Gárdonyi. The players play the roles of the defenders of the castle, i.e. the protagonists of the famous novel, such as Dobó, Mekcsey, Bornemissza, and their goal is to keep the Turkish attackers outside the walls. To do this, they not only have to repel the continuous Turkish attacks against the walls, but they also have to neutralize the minework of the traitor(s) inside. In each round, four events occur at the four wall sections of the castle, for the solution of which cards must be submitted secretly, and the success of the defense depends on their results. If you succeed in handing in the cards corresponding to the prescribed task together, the defenders will receive a reward, otherwise the Turkish armies will approach the inner castle, the will to fight will deteriorate, or the players will suffer dangerous injuries.

description: Újrajátszott Trianon ("Trianon Replayed") is a fully cooperative game in which players are members of an imaginary government from a hundred years ago - it can also be played alone - and must simultaneously face all the challenges of the post-Great War era: enemy troops are constantly attacking the country, the amount of coal and grain limited and constantly decreasing as we lose more and more regions, and events follow each other according to the real historical situation."

DESCEND INTO A WORLD FORSAKEN TO THE DARKNESS

In Torch & Shield, Dwarven steel clashes during fierce confrontations between rival clans in the ruined halls of the fallen Dwarf empire. Within the sealed vaults lies treasure unimaginable, and each clan believes it is theirs to take. Oaths have been sworn, and feuds will be born in blood that will last a century. But the Dwarves have more to worry about than each other. Ancient enemies have awoken in the lower levels and foul beasts still hunt in the dark halls eager to feast on Dwarven flesh.

In the Torch & Shield miniatures game, you will assemble and control a hardy delving crew from one of the twelve great Dwarf clans and head into your ancestral underworld to claim the gold within.  You must choose your equipment well and carefully weigh your tactics if you are to defeat your rival kinsmen and get to the riches before they do. Most of all you must keep your torches burning, for when all the lights go out the monsters will come from the deep.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Gorilla Warfare: A social deduction battle royale
description:
Gorilla Warfare is a social deduction battle royale. Each turn represents a day in the jungle, leaving you as the player to decide how to use your limited energy for the day.

On your turn you can: MOVE in the jungle, SEARCH for useful items, ATTACK other gorillas, GIVE your energy to other gorillas or stay still, and do nothing..

Your energy gets depleted by the end of the turn, so think carefully about how you want to use your energy! Gorillas eliminated from the game form the Spirit Council, who unleash their otherworldly powers upon the players each turn - so be careful! Your actions in the jungle might come back to haunt you - literally!

Don't be afraid to bluff, form alliances, or make threats - right words at the right time might win you the game. Just make sure you're not the one getting played...

—description from the designer

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – White Sunlion Armor
description:
The People of the Sun - White Sunlion Armor Erza is packaged in a box that includes:

1 x Hard plastic White Sunlion Armor Erza miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and insert
1 x Large matte art print
1 x Character art card
1 x Terrain tile and card, 1 x strange resource, 2 x pattern cards, 2 x pattern gear cards, 1 x hybrid armor set card.

Erza of the Sun wears modified armor adapted to the scorching conditions created by living under the sleeping sun. This White Sunlion hybrid armor is added to People of the Sun campaigns. It grants the wearer more movement, less armor, and the ability to ignore the extreme heat that prevents survivors from wearing heavy furs.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Screaming Sun Armor
description:
Screaming Sun Armor - Lucy Promo was included with 1 x gear and 1 x hybrid armor set bonus card, while the hard plastic version was included with 2 x gears, 2 x Pattern cards, 1 x Vermin resource and 1 x hybrid armor set bonus card (not part of the Kingdom Death: Monster Base Game).

Gear card sneak peek: Screaming Oni Mask (armor, bone, mask) - Scold. Non-deaf knocked down survivors suffer brain damage and stand. Limit, once per round.

The Screaming Sun Armor - Lucy is packaged in a hand-stamped kraft box and limited to 500. It includes:

1 x Photoresin Screaming Sun Armor - Lucy
1 x Hard Plastic Kingdom Death 30mm round insert and base
2 x Beta Gameplay cards: 1 x gear, 1 x hybrid armor set bonus card
First run collector's edition: Signed and numbered authenticity note
Large Matte Art Print
Character Art Card

The hard plastic version of Screaming Sun Armor - Lucy is packaged in a graphic box. It includes:
1 x Hard Plastic People of the Sun - Screaming Sun Armor
1 x Hard Plastic Kingdom Death 30mm round insert and base
2 x Pattern cards
2 x Pattern gear cards
1 x Hybrid Armor Bonus card
1 x Vermin resource
Large Matte Art Print
Small character art card

Lucy wears the Screaming Oni hybrid armor that transforms the reckless abandon of the screaming armor into a precise tool for maiming monsters.

Bear Jelly Poker is a set collector with the goal of making the strongest 6-card Poker Hands from exchanging and acquiring cards from a tableau.

This is a TGM 2022 Fall Entry and so only contains 32 cards, 1-9 in 4 colors (4-6 have reduced copies and include bicolored cards). Starting with a 3-5 card hand, exchange any cards with a row/diagonal/column in the tableau. Then acquire a row/diagonal/column in the tableau to get to an 8 card hand (rules are slightly different for a 4-player game). Players are rewarded based on how many melds are weaker than theirs. After a certain number of rounds, the winner is the player with the most points.

boardgame: Float: From the Deep – God From the Deep  Booster Pack
description:
A God From the Deep is a booster pack for Float: From the Deep.

The first booster pack for FLOAT comes with a bigger than life enemy, an experienced leader, eldritch relics, equipment, downgrades, and more.

boardgame: Float: From the Deep – The Bowels of Davy Jones' Locker Promo Card
description:
"The Bowels of Davy Jones' Locker" is a KS Exclusive 'From the Deep' promo card.

Unbeknown to the survivors of the crash, they had arrived in water riddled with death. Below them lay centuries of bodies lost to Davy Jones' Locker. Now these bodies are but servants, commanded by the sea to bring it more souls. When the Bowels of Davy Jones' Locker opens, hell will follow.

The card reads:

"Swallowed by the sea many years ago. Reborn in the raging bowels below. They haunt the waves as their legion grows."

The card stats are:

8 Hit Points required to defeat creature

-1 Castaway and -1 Equipment if player is defeated

This promo was free for Day One backers and included with pledges $55 or higher. It was then available later for $3.

boardgame: G.I. JOE Mission Critical: Vehicle Pack #1
description:
Eight new Vehicle cards bring fore firepower in the fight against Cobra.

Includes: HAL, PAC/RAT, Toss n'Cross, Skystriker XP-14F, Killer Whale, APC, AWE Striker, and Slugger.

boardgame: G.I. JOE Mission Critical: Retro Snake Eyes
description:
A blast from the past!

Recruit this classic character to join in the battle!

Retro Snake Eyes has a different, unique ability compared to the Snake Eyes included in the base game.

Text says: Legendary Commando
Once per battle, when any hero rolls 2 or more O results during an attack, you may deal 3 damage to an enemy card of your choice,

Requires G.I. Joe Mission Critical to play.

Card game with fast food restaurant theme in which 2-5 players compete who will be first to fullfill customers orders.

There are 6 types of food/drink items (hamburger, french fries, pizza, ice-cream, lemonade, coffee) and orders with 1, 2 or 3 coins representing tip money.

Each player gets 6 random cards with food or drink and they also draw 1 card each with order. When everyone is ready, they put their order cards on table at once. Players take turns in throwing 2 dice with food/drink icons to complement cards on hand to be able to fullfill orders on table. Order can be fullfilled with maximum of 2 cards and at least 1 die.

Everyone can fullfill any order on a table. The harder it is to fullfill an order the better tip money it rewards.

If players can fullfill order, he needs to ring a bell and if order is fullfilled, he throws away food cards and take order card instead as reward. Then players draw cards to fill 6 cards in hand from food/drink set.

Game last untill there is not enough food/drink cards for everyone to have 6 cards in hand or if there are no orders cards left.

Player with most tip money on his order cards wins.

This expansion contains all of the stretch goal content unlocked during the initial funding campaign.

Introducing two new card types: Installations and Modifications.Installations bring a new dimension to the battlefield giving commanders the option to expand the infrastructure of their faction's hive. Modifications give commanders options to enhance their units. Creating specialized forces on the battlefield tailored to their strategy.

The Federalist Papers Game is a short, simple, solitaire, card-driven game about the ratification of the U.S. Constitution between 1787 and 1790.  The player’s goal is to get as many of the 13 states as possible to ratify the Constitution.  The game takes around 15-20 minutes to play and can be used as a teaching exercise in a classroom.

The game includes a deck of 29 cards, 13 of which are convention cards--one for each of the 13 states.  The other cards are Federalist Papers cards representing a selection of the 85 historical Federalist Papers political essays. When a convention card is drawn, the player holds that state's ratification convention.  Starting with a base number representing the state's starting level of support for the Constitution, the player rolls two dice to add to the base number.  Twelve or higher and the state ratifies; less than 12 and the state rejects it.

The player may choose to add Federalist Papers cards to the convention to increase the odds, but may also spend them for die rolls to advance along two tracks to gain bonuses in future conventions.  The player mat is used to keep track of which states ratify as well as progress along the tracks.

Most of the Federalist Papers cards appear early in the game when the easy states hold conventions.  The player must conserve them for use late in the game when the difficult states hold their conventions.

To win, the player must get at least 9 states to ratify--the minimum number required for the Constitution to go into effect.  But each state has a number of victory points, and the more states that ratify, the higher the score.

—description from the designer

Player spins the wheel which determines the category from list of 10 categories. After this a card is flipped to determine the starting letter and players have 10 seconds to write a word on their whiteboard. If the word that they have written fits the category, starts with the given letter and is not on any other players' answer then they score a point. First to 10 points wins the game.

Dungeon Bowl is the game of subterranean sporting mayhem, played between two Blood Bowl coaches. In this expansion set, the College of Life's Emerald Crusaders face off against the College of Death's Black Widows, as two teams explore a sprawling dungeon with a customisable layout – including the lair of a deadly werewolf. Will you be able to score a crucial touchdown, or will you be scuppered by the traps and opponents you encounter along the way?

—description from the publisher

In ダイダラ (Daidara), players cooperate with each other to overthrow the giant creature called Daidara. The game is set in a distant land in the far future. It’s an untamed land filled with the swirling energies of “earth veins.” Daidara has pushed the once-prosperous Cobbit tribe to the verge of extinction. The Cobbit race has no future unless they can defeat Daidara.

The players take on the roles of leaders of the Cobbit tribe. The mighty Daidara is difficult to defeat without the right preparation. Players develop the play area, creating tall trees, mountains, and towers. As they build the map, random footprints appear, triggering details about how the Daidara will move and its special abilities. When the fourth footprint is drawn, the Daidara appears and Cobbits can be killed permanently and removed from the game. Players must use proper preparation, available items, and teamwork to defeat Daidara before they're all destroyed.

The game can be played solo or with multiple people controlling the 4 Cobbits. Daidara moves on its own with random movements determined by which tiles are drawn in a particular game. For replaybility, five different Daidaras are available, each with unique special attacks.

Ask questions of which the answer is YES or NO to "rule and divide". The question must be built with two words out of four on your card. Everyone shows with his/her thumb up or down his/her opinion. You will get point according the minority votes.

—user summary

Chain is a set collector combined with shedding/climbing (and a bit of trick-taking thrown in). A 2-phase game with the tagline of "Build & Release" as you build the best hand of cards to score, then release them as fast as possible. All 54 cards have unique and mesmerizing hand-drawn art to stare at as you play through this tactical game.

In the first half, the Build Phase, you collect cards from a tableau to crate a 12-card hand, scoring for the number of duplicate numbers and runs you have.

The second half, the Release Phase, has you shedding your hand by playing sets of runs and same numbers. It adopts a trick-taking structure where after all players have played a card or passed, the winner takes all the cards as points. When a player sheds out, all remaining players remove a random card from their hand as negative points. The game continues until only 1 player remains, then scores are tallied and the winner declared.

Telepathy TongTong is a warm empathy board game that allows families, friends, and lovers to learn what they think through sound and form a consensus. Can we hear the same sound and think the same thing? If you hear the same sound and select the same token, you succeed, and if you select at least one other token, you fail. Send telepathy to the person next to you now. Telepathy, TongTong!

Skin Scuba refers to scuba diving. It is a simple set collection game where you can become a diver and meet various animal friends in the cool sea. Players become divers, move through oxygen, dive, and meet fish. Fish have a special method of calculating scores by color. Whenever you collect fish by type, you can meet special animal friends. There are fish that have a big advantage, and there are fish that have a small advantage, but can keep the other person in check. But it's not easy. You have to avoid scary sharks, too! Who will be the best diver?

Romantic Crocodile is a simple push your luck + influence-based party game that becomes an alligator escaping from the zoo and goes on a trip. Here is an alligator with romance. The crocodile dreams of leaving the narrow, dark zoo, making love around the world, and heading for its distant hometown of Amazon. But it's not easy. The zoo is trying to catch the alligator again. The longer the adventure, the deeper the risk. Can the alligator's romance come true? Make the Alligator's Romance Come True with Your Hands!

The Midnight Edition of the Original Bookgame Bismarck Solitaire adds additional game plays of each mission, plus a new scenario and a new map with three missions for the Battle of Denmark Strait.

Command the pride of the German Navy in Bismarck Solitaire, an Original Bookgame by Worthington Publishing. Lead the German ships Bismarck, Prinz Eugen, and U-boat support as they take on the determined Royal Navy with one goal, Sink the Bismarck!

You have 11 missions of varying objectives and difficultly, plus a training cruise. Each mission gives you 4-6 tries to best the Royal Navy. Some missions require you to find convoys, others expect you to sink a few British warships, while some just hope your ships can survive the sortie.

The only thing you must provide is 2 dice, a pen, and a sense of adventure.

Midnight Edition Details:

Bismarck Solitaire Midnight Edition is a Black and White version of the Original Bookgame Bismarck Solitaire with additional game plays of each mission, plus a new scenario with three missions for the Battle of Denmark Strait.

The few differences between the original color version and this black and white version are:

     The Colored zones have changed to numbered and shaded zones.
     Contrast throughout the bookgame is optimized for black-and-white reproduction.
     The original eight mission bookgame had three gameplays in the color version, but now has four gameplays each.
     58 pages in the original color version vs. 106 pages in the Midnight Edtion


We added a new game set: The Battle of Denmark Strait.

What's Unique to Denmark Strait

     New cruisers were added, HMS Norfolk and HMS Suffolk.
     New Map, focusing on the Denmark Strait between Iceland and Greenland. This is the critical pathway for German warships to get out to the open sea and attack the shipping lanes supporting Great Britain.
     No convoys in this battle.
     Allows for the focus of the sea battles to be fierce fighting between the capital ships of the navies.
     Sixteen game plays spread across three missions that vary in difficulty.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
(Nie)Powinieneś 2, which means "You Should(n’t) 2" in English, is a card game for awful adults (18+) who like to laugh. It comes with two decks of cards. It is similar to Cards Against Humanity or and can be explained in two minutes. All players (from 3 to 15 people) draw 10 cards from orange deck at the start and then first main player draws one card from black deck.

Main player read black cards with some funny phrase and other participants have to choose on of their orange cards with other funny phrases that in their opinion are a best match for the phrase in the drawn black card. Cards are given to the main player in secret, whom then reads them aloud and chooses the best one and owner of this card scores the point.

After that all players that used orange cards draws new ones (so they have 10 again) and the next player become the main player and the cycle continues. After one of the decks become empty players stop the game, they count their score (1 black cards = 1 point) and the winner is a player with the higher score.

All cards have phrases printed in english and polish language both.

Decks from (Nie)Powinieneś 2 can be merged with ones from (Nie)Powinieneś.

Bearly Surviving is a turn-based strategy board game, where players are given workers that double as their health. The more workers players have, the more resources they get each turn, and the faster they can build a colossal army to destroy their opponents.

Through action cards, Bearly Surviving offers unique battle mechanics that are easy to learn but hard to master. Players need to efficiently use their resources and cleverly utilise their units to plan and react to their opponent’s move to destroy their enemies.

During your turn, you collect resources depending on how you distributed your workers in your previous turn. So thinking ahead is very important in this game. These resources help you build up a strong army that you can use to attack other players with the help of the action cards.

An attack on another player not only damages their army but also their workers. Once a player has no more workers, they are eliminated from the game. The game ends when a player either collects all ROAR cards or eliminates all other players.

Each player has to get 4 meeples from start to the finish at the face of Pumuckl. The players take turns to roll two dice and move. To enter the second to fourth meeple into the game, one die has to show a "6". The track consists of three rings which are connected by colored spaces, which can only be used by the meeples of the same color and only in direction to the face of Pumuckl. If a player rolls doubles, the turntable is rotated by 180 degrees. Now the meeples move away from the finish when they have to use the colored spaces! When the first meeple has entered the finish ring or the middle ring, Pumuckl (large black meeple) enters the game. If a player rolls "1" on one of the dice, he may place Pumuckl on any space in the middle or finish ring to hinder his opponents. There he stays till he is moved by the next player who rolls "1". Pumuckl can't be passed by any meeple.

The first player to get his 4 meeple on his finish space wins the game.

Like any self-respecting extension, Thematic Adventures allows a broad renewal of the basic game: more than 40 additional bookmarks (including new explorations very requested by the players) to be added directly to those of the basic box.
More content for even more varied adventures!

But Thematic Adventures is much more than a simple extension! It also offers 4 new ways to play, each in a different universe:


     Horror: Escape a ferocious and horrifying beast chasing you in the book!
     Polar: Find and arrest Von X by conducting a police investigation.
     Fantasy: Explore a fantastic world and prove that you are "The Lus"
     Science fiction: Travel from book to book like so many worlds that you will explore through space-time portals!


—description from the publisher

You play as baby monkeys going back home. Alas, they must cross a clearing. Their mother told them to be careful of predators by always hiding when moving on open grounds. To do so, monkeys hide under coconut halves. However, they can't see through the coconuts! So the monkeys keep running until they hit an obstacle. Help the little monkeys reach home!

The game can either be two-player or four-player (two teams). Either way, any player can move any coconut, or reveal any coconut but a monkey will not move unless hidden under one. The game quickly turns into a memory and abstract puzzle where you block your opponent's moves.

boardgame: Maui: Catch the Wave Mini Expansion
description:
With this mini extension, when you choose your towel, you will now have to take into account the pattern represented on your surf tile to score more points and obtain bonuses while advancing on the surf track.

Catch the Wave introduces a new mechanic to the game that you'll find so easy to introduce that you'll want to use it every game.

-description from publisher's website

The aim of Poldark The Board Game is to travel around Cornwell, gaining as many coins and crown notes as possible along the way, before arriving in Truro before the time is up.

The length of the game is agreed to before the game starts. 35 to 45 minutes is suggested by the manufacturer.

In a 2 or 3 player game, each player will play 2 characters as a team, in a 4 (or 6) player game each player will play one character as part of a team, and in a 5 player game, 4 players will play one character each as part of a team and the fifth player will play 2 characters as the third team.

Gift Words is a friendly but challenging word rummy game. When one player adds a letter to another player's word, they both score.
There is also an advanced version where players get replacement letters when they expand words or make long words for themselves.
The basic and advanced games are for 3-6 players. There are additional rules for solitaire, and for a 2-player cooperative game.

—description from the designer

1859. This is the story of the other — the nephew, the other French emperor, the other Napoleon for the other Italian campaign. Even if the names and places are similar, the events are quite different.
This campaign of 1859 is not the campaign of 1796 or even 1800. The revolutionary tide has passed, and the military movement no longer has the same epic sense. Nevertheless, a king has a responsibility to respond to the call of his people to rid them of the Austrian yoke and lead them to independence and unity. This king found the man who could support him against this formidable adversary; a man with an illustrious name, at the head of a powerful nation with a legendary army.
Everything plays out in Po Valley, from Turin to Verona by way of Milan, with no less than an imperial invasion to push back. They will have to cross waterways like so many hedges standing between them and freedom. They must be victorious in the bloody combats of Magenta and Solferino … Unless the Empire counter-attacks to save the Kingdom of Lombardy-Venetia.

1866 is the story of a young nation searching for its identity and seeking to take back the last part of its territory from despised Austria. However, this nation has not yet come to terms with its recent unification and will struggle against a tough adversary. The austrian army will achieve it’s greatest exploit of 1866, nevertheless so contrary to victory by force of arms.

VaeVictis invites you to experience these two campaigns with the  Gaiter Button system,  inspired by the Marshals. Incorporating feedback from the previous volume on 1870, the system offers improved fluidity while still perfectly simulating the European operation art of the middle of the 19th century.

Game components:

Two 59 x 41 cm maps
216 die-cut counters and markers
One booklet of rules
Four scenarios and one campaign
Two player aid cards

—description from the publisher

This game is part of the family games Bouton de Guêtre ("Gaiter Button") which includes also La guerre de 1870 (https://boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/311190/la-guerre-de-1870-la-chute-de-napoleon-iii-juillet) and Sedan 1870 (https://boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/306065/sedan-1870))
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Bouton de Guêtre - Gaiter Button (Vae Victis)

description:
Team up with your favourite pet to create the best winning sets in different point categories. From initial starting hands, players lay three card sets in front of random placed score point cards and then replenish. Deception and bluff occurs as to what sets are placed where. One card in each set is always face down.

But beware, you may be swapped a high point losing hand, or is it a winning hand you swap straight back without realising? Other dilemmas include: Do you play a weak set now to build a better one later? Which initial score points to play sets to?

Which opposite swap to make? Identifying key score point cards. How many sideway swaps do you make that would incur penalties? Whether to make a losing set to use in an opposite swap.

After the pets have completed their hands, the fur revealing showdown decides who will be Top Cat or Top Dog. Which clever pet would you be? A light fun fast paced strategy card game for two players. 8+. 5-8 minutes per round. Up to best of 5.

—description from the designer

Scoppia la coppia ("Clash of Couples") is already the must-have couples game to test your knowledge of your partner with questions about everyday life, personality traits and your intimacy…

Each turn, vote for the situation that suits you best and find out how well your partner knows you… or not! So, who will sleep on the sofa tonight?
Rules:
"Just the two of us" Version (2 Players):
ASK Draw a question card
VOTE Vote face down for the answer that suits you best
GUESS The other player in turn votes face down for the answer that best suits their partner
REVEAL Both flip their answer card over to reveal their answer
SCORE If the second player has the same vote as their partner, then the former scores 1 point
REPEAT Roles are inverted and a new round starts!
WIN When you want to stop the game, count your points to find out who the winner is.

"Clash of Couples" Version (2 or more couples mode) : same as the 2 player version, but this time couples are going to play against each other!

Immerse yourself in the world of the deaf. Forget all sound sensations for a while, guess and express familiar words only by sign language or lip reading.

Cards about sign shows a guide picture of what the sign looks like and a brief description of the basic movement of the hands.

On the Cards about lip-reading are written words from everyday life, knowledge and place names.

Players play in teams. Each team has one colored pieces which puts on the start field on game board. The first playing team moves their piece to the first field with the sign-in picture. After that, one player from the team draws one card of their choice (heavier or lighter) from the respective deck of cards.
The player performs a word (sign only with his hands). If the rest of his team guesses the word within 30 seconds, the team's figure is moved by as many fields as it was on the card (lighter 2 fields, heavier 3 fields). If the team does not guess the word or expression, it is moved forward by 1 field.

After that, the other team plays and so on. During the game the pieces passes a fields with signing or lip reading.

The team that reaches the finish line first wins.

The game is not "just about winning" but about familiarizing the general public with sign language.

This game contents 1 game board, 240 cards (60 about lighter variant of sign, 60 about more complex of sign) and 60 about lip-reading), 6 pieces and 1 hourglass.

Welcome to the kingdom of bees! You take over the leadership of a bee colony and try to use your workers wisely so that the colony has enough food. In addition, the bee colony should also grow and pollinate as many flowers as possible and store their nectar and pollen in the honeycombs. Without the pollination work of bees, there would hardly be any berries, fruits, such numerous vegetables and the variety of flowers. But there are also dangers lurking from voracious animals, so that individual bees must also be used as guardians. For defense against mites and bacteria, resin can be collected from the forest and used to line the honeycombs. Now a race against time begins because a bee year passes quickly and only those who are diligent and have a large bee colony with enough stored honey survive the upcoming winter!

—description from the designer

Walk on the whimsical Grand Staircase, take part in various classes and race through the corridors to the Great Hall! BE CAREFUL, magical challenges await you along the way... Collect THREE Subject Tokens first to enter the Great Hall. You can choose any player, you can be Harry, Ron, Hermione, Draco or Luna.

A licensed tie-in with the Elf on the Shelf franchise, Twisted Twistmas is a game based on getting rid of all your cards, with an underlying theme of connecting a collection of strings of Christmas lights. That is combined with a couple attack cards to make another player skip their next turn or draw a card.

Snowpeople is the first print-at-home game published by Twin City Games. You are digging through a field of snow as you uncover and collect objects to decorate your snowman. Throughout the game, you use dice to choose paths to dig through the snow to uncover objects, as well as collect snow for the different body parts of your snowfolk's body. Whoever has excavated the largest and diverse set of objects to decorate their snowperson by the end of the game will be declared Best in Snow!

1. Every round one player rolls two dice, and each player records these results on the game sheet. All players use these results to dig around objects on their own player sheet.

2. Players use one result to choose a polyomino to draw within the grid. Players use the second result to choose a dig type for the polyomino and mark spaces in the grid.

3. After a player completely surrounds an object they count the number of squares that object occupies and marks that number in the matching row of that objects type. Players are encouraged to collect sets of objects using each type.

4. That player then marks an X in each row that matches the dig types used to surround the object scored. If there was only one dig type used to surround the shape, then a player marks three X's in that row.

5. Throughout the game players will earn Snowflakes that can be used for bonuses, and Stars that will earn them additional points at the end of the game.

6. At the end of the game players count the number of points earned from their objects collected, stars earned, as well as any unused bonus snowflakes.

-description from designer

Even More Adventures is an expansion for Crazy Adventures. It adds more 'Ta-Da!', 'Good/bad', and 'Joe's store' cards as well as two new characters. But most importatntly, it presents unique abilities for each character! For example, if Ernest the Gecko enters the game, other players have to be more careful. Ernest may steal their cards!

This expansion works better with the Crazy Adventures in Dice Lands, but can still be used with Crazy Adventures: On The Way to the Desire Throne for extra cards for each deck.

— description from the publisher

The Backwards Game is a party game played in teams in which all of the questions and challenges are incredibly easy...if you do them forwards. However, in this game, EVERYTHING happens in reverse.

At the start of a game, each team decides on a secret five letter word, e.g., B-R-E-A-D, which they write backwards on their "backwards board" and cover up with five mystery cards. Each team has thirty seconds to whizz through as many backwards clues as possible across five types of clue card. After thirty seconds, the team scores how many correct answers they got by ticking them off inside the circles on the backwards board. When a team ticks off three of the circles, they can try to guess a letter on the rival team's backwards board. If the letter they guess is in the other team's word, that team must reveal it by removing the mystery card.

The first team to correctly guess the rival's team secret word and say it forwards wins. However, if they make an incorrect guess, the opposing team can remove any mystery card from their opponent's backwards board...

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Uncanny X-Men Affiliation Pack
description: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Uncanny X-Men Affiliation Pack contains highly detailed miniatures of the X-Men Storm, Cyclops, Beast, and Wolverine in one convenient package. These miniatures are joined by the stat cards for these characters updated with the most recent graphic design. Finally, this pack also contains all the Team Tactic cards originally found in CP40 - Wolverine & Sabretooth and CP41 - Cyclops & Storm along with the Advanced R&D Team Tactic card found in CP43 - Mystique & Beast, ensuring players have everything they need to defend mutantkind from any threat

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Brotherhood of Mutants Affiliation Pack
description: Bringing together highly detailed miniatures of Magneto, Mystique, Sabretooth, and Toad in one package, Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Brotherhood of Mutants Affiliation Pack gives players everything they need to begin playing with the Brotherhood of Mutants Affiliation. Along with these miniatures, players will also find the stat cards for these characters featuring new graphic design. Rounding out the pack are the Asteroid M, X-Ceptional Healing, Weapon X Program, Magnetic Refraction, Magnetic Crush, Heave Ho, The Books of Truth, and Deception Team Tactic Cards, ensuring players have everything they need to cut down any who oppose their Brotherhood.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – The Blob & Pyro
description:
Marvel: Crisis Protocol – The Blob & Pyro adds two new mutants to this game system.

Fred Dukes, known to many as the "Blob", has the superhuman ability to become virtually immovable at a whim while in touch with the earth. Joining him is St. John Allerdyce, more commonly known as Pyro. Allerdyce can shape fire, increasing or diminishing its heat, intensity, and size, making him a menacing addition to any team. Together, these characters supplement the Brotherhood of Mutants Affiliation with their mutant abilities and three Team Tactic cards that give them even more ways to work together with their teammates.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Crashed X-Wing Battlefield Expansion
description:
Players can add even more thematic flair and play out a unique scenario with Star Wars: Legion – Crashed X-Wing Battlefield Expansion.

Featuring four miniatures depicting the scattered rubble from a crashed X-wing starfighter, this pack more deeply immerses players in the ground battles of a galaxy far, far away with thematic terrain. In addition to these miniatures, this pack also includes a Rebel pilot miniature that becomes the focal point of a daring rescue mission scenario where players battle to salvage the wreckage and rescue the pilot or capture them.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Boba Fett (Daimyo) Operative Expansion
description: The infamous Boba Fett returns to Star Wars: Legion in the Boba Fett (Daimyo) Operative Expansion! Featuring a new version of this iconic character based on his appearance in The Mandalorian and The Book of Boba Fett, this expansion gives players powerful new Operative that can fight alongside their mercenary and Rebel forces. The beautifully sculpted Boba Fett miniature found in this expansion menaces his foes with his gaderffii stick and can be assembled with or without his Mandalorian helmet. Additionally, players will find three command cards that make Boba Fett even more versatile in combat. Finally, an upgrade card allows players to invite Boba Fett with his Flame Projector for even more firepower.

Renaissance Historical battles is the fourth book in a series that allows the wargamer to re-fight actual historical battles with his own preferred set of wargame rules. I have based this book on WRG De Bellis Renationis rules plus I have included a section allowing the army lists to be read using other similar sets of rules.t

My book gives enough information for you to re-enact the battle described in a concise ad compact way covering why it took place, the forces engaged, given in elements and actual numbers of men; a deployment guide and illustration of the battles initial positions; the victory conditions and the historical outcome.

-from the introduction

24 scenarios are included.

Renaissance Battles Book II is the fifth book in a series of books that allows the wargamer to re-fight actual historical battles with his own preferred set of wargame rules. I have based this book on WRG De Bellis Renationis rules, and I have included explanations for troop types for other rule sets, including a standard key for troop types.

My book gives enough information for you to re-enact the battle described in a concise ad compact way, covering why it took place, the forces engaged, given in elements and actual numbers of men, it gives a deployment guide and illustration of the battle's initial positions, the victory conditions and historical outcome.

-from the introduction

22 scenarios are included.
