boardgame: Tekhenu: Obelisk of the Sun – Warmth of a Cat mini-expansion
description:
This mini-expansion contains 8 identical cat meeples, each player gets 2 at the start of the game.

During the Bastet action, instead of gaining a scribe, players can place a cat on top of a house placed by an opponent - on Osiris, Hathor, or if you're playing with the expansion, Seth.

The space is resolved as if a house was just built. The owner of the house can choose between gaining 2 happiness or a random Blessing.

Cats don't count as houses during scoring, each house can only have one cat on it, and you cannot place a cat on your own house. Botankhamun never places a cat, if you place a cat Botankhamun always chooses 2 happiness, and when playing with the mini-expansion Botankhamun starts with 4VP extra.

The promo pack was available during Turczi Junior Promo Pack gamefound campaign.
family: Game: Tekhenu – Obelisk of the Sun

boardgame: The Normans: A Revenge Module for European Warfare 1000 AD to 1170 AD
description:
Descended from the Viking raiders of Scandanavia, the Normans cut a swath of terror through the very heart of Europe. They rose to dominate western Europe, conquering England, Ireland, and large parts of France and Italy. Here are the great battles of their era, complete with scenarios, maps of battlefields, and army lists.

—description from the back of the book

The book includes 12 scenarios, from Clontarf 1014 to Dublin 1171.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Together the players must remember the path through each dungeon, avoid traps and wandering monsters, solve puzzles and find loot to help them gain the strength that will make it possible to beat the evil critters guarding the dungeon's lower levels.

To progress in the game the players must reveal Land Cards and find matching pairs, which may then be removed. Each removed pair of cards will activate good or bad events that the players must overcome. They will find treasure, monsters, traps, challenges, hallways and rooms to rest in. And finally, the Boss Monster, one of Forhgot the Wizard King’s generals. To manage and beat the problems along the way the players must remember and turn personal Memory Tiles, and match them correctly with those of the problems at hand. 
To win, the team must survive the events and encounters along the way, and in the final battle match the symbols on their Memory Tiles to all the Boss Monster's tiles before the players run out of energy.

—description from the designer

A multi-expansion for Lords of Vegas, featuring all new boards and components representing the other gambling meccas of the United States. Contains gameplay for playing in Atlantic City, Reno, and 1881 Tombstone. Each game is for 2–6 players, except Lords of Reno is 2 or 3. Requires the Lords of Vegas base set (any edition) to play.

Components include:

     4 Boards (Reno, Tombstone, Atlantic City, and New Orleans)
     1 deck of 55 cards
     60 Tombstone lot markers
     1 Reno arch tile
     5 New Orleans riverboat tiles
     5 wooden Ferris wheels
     6 wooden cowboys
     5 resident tokens
     1 Rulebook

During the 1800s, powerful steamboats made their way up and down the waters of beautiful Keuka Lake in the Finger Lakes region of New York. They carried people and goods from one lakeside port to another. Two big companies owned the boats. They competed with each other to provide the fastest service and do the most business. The competition wasn't always friendly: sometimes lawsuits and collisions were involved. But the steamboats themselves were glorious machines and helped lakeside communities flourish.

Steamboat Races is a simple two-player postcard game for all ages. Each player takes on the role of a steamboat captain (either blue or red). Your goal is to be the first steamboat to circumnavigate Keuka Lake. All you need is a regular deck of cards to play.

—description from the designer

A cooperative game with the goal of saving humanity from self-destruction.

The game’s objective is to reduce the global greenhouse gasses of the 9 regions by 2/3 before turn 36.

Each player takes the role of a knowledgeable leader by using special cards from the 6 influences in the game: Political, Industrial, Military, Education, Media and NGO. Each player also chooses to work with an influential employer, giving them access to different skills and powers.

Each turn, the active player draws and adds influence cards to their hand, before revealing an event for the turn. After the event, the player can perform up to 2 actions to finish their turn.

The events represent: a number of major tipping points for the planet; a drop in oil production; or human consequences, which can be changed by a players' action using their influence cards.

The characters can move to different regions and change the electricity production, or to implement some Permanent Solutions with long term effects.

Different calamities come from the events, like riots becoming conflicts and then into war, or shortages of food becoming increasingly scarce, before a famine occurs, and lastly epizootics become epidemics and then pandemics. All of these calamities create refugees in search of a better life.

Each region has a specific "level of life" from 1 to 4, mirrored by the equivalent consumption of oil and electricity.

The players run versus the GHG, the global shortage of oil and demography.

The global greenhouse gasses of the 9 regions starts at 100, and must be reduced to 33 before turn 36.

Bataille pour le Climat is a full cooperative game, where each new run plays drastically different from the last.

Some actions are determined by rolling a D8, which can be modified with the use of certain cards.

The game is based on the courses of Jean-Marc Jancovici at the Ecole des Mines de Paris, and the solutions proposed for decarbonization are those of the French think tank, The Shift Project. All the equivalent statistics and data found in the game have been checked by the volunteers of the Shift Project, "les shifteurs", to be globally accurate with the real world.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Welcome to the Kingdom of Mushrooms! You will begin the game with a set of mushrooms tokens and cards. You choose which cards to place in ecosystems to grow and eventually forage into your shroom basket. Strategize to gain more mushrooms, water droplets from the waterfall, and more. Strive to become a “Master Mycologist” and learn more about the fungi kingdom with true-to-life images and extraordinary facts. Mushroom facts and features are scientifically accurate. Players compete for the basket with the highest points, earning “mycochievements” (™ pending) along the way! You and your friends will become immersed in unique gameplay that blends science and fun in this remarkable world of fungi.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Go Bum: the cheeky version of Go Fish
description:
Do you have a Golden Bum? How about a Wiggle Bum?

Go Bum is the cheeky version of Go Fish where you match (family friendly) bums to win!

There are optional rules that make this new version of a classic game more interactive and fun. For example, some matches give you extra points or turns, while some matches have funny actions (e.g. talking like a pirate).

Go Bum was made by Levi (7) and Evan (mid 30s-ish) as a creative activity during covid lockdown home schooling. It was a great project to do together and soon had family and friends asking for a copy.

There are even more laughs with Go Bum Animals Edition! You'll soon be laughing asking things like "Do you have a Bum of Destruction?" and "Do you have a Bouncy Bum?"!

With over 37k copies sold Go Bum has become a much loved family game.

—description from the designer

In Wacky Cousin Wendell, 1-2 players will assume the role of cousins who are pitted against each other in another one of Wacky Cousin Wendell’s games. During the game, players will have a hand of five cards each round where they'll simultaneously place and/or tuck one each turn to their tableau to form polyomino patterns for various scoring opportunities.

After a card has been played, players then simultaneously point their finger at a score condition they'd like to claim with a polyomino pattern that matches one they've created in their tableau. The claimed score condition is placed in the players tableau on top of the symbols that created the pattern. If players point to the same score condition, the player nearest to the card will choose who receives it while the other player will draw two cards and choose one to claim.

After all five cards have been placed, players set aside all claimed score conditions and reset the game. After three rounds, tally up the scores and whoever has the most points wins!

Wacky Cousin Wendell is the third game in the Risible Relatives Series. Each game in the series is a standalone 54-card game for 1-2 players that can also be combined with each game in the series for a different experience.

—description from the publisher

Can you name...  The Seven Dwarfs? Two countries beginning with Z? Santa Claus's eight reindeer? The Famous Five? The four Hogwart's Houses? Six actors who've played Dr. Who?
How many would you know?!

In this general knowledge quiz game, the more examples you can name, the more points you’ll score!

Effetto Terra is a game aiming at increasing our carbon footprint awareness.

Competitive version

Each player receives 5 CO2 cards and places them face-down with the CO2 side value covered. In the centre of the table a random face-up card is placed with its CO2 value in view.
The goal of the game is to succeed in playing all the cards in your hand before anyone else.
On your turn, choose a card in your hand and place it in the centre of the table in line with those already present so that all the cards issuing less CO2 are to its left and all those which issue more to its right.
After you decide where to place your card, turn it down immediately. If you have placed it correctly, the card remains in play and you haven’t to draw nothing, while if it is wrong it’s discarded and you have to draw a new card from the pack.

Cooperative version

The team that faces the first mission, as shown in card n.1, draws 4 CO2 cards without disclosing the related values.
The goal of the team is to place cards in order from the lowest to the highest CO2 issued value. For each level, the team will have to locate the number of indicated cards from the related mission card in the centre of the table, always face-down – the goal is to lay out the cards in rising numerical order, from the left to the right.
The team can talk to take decisions on the layout of the cards.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Wander: The Cult of Barnacle Bay – Dragon Bow Promo Card
description: The Dragon Bow is a level 1 Treasure Card. It is able to be used as a Ranged or Melee weapon. You must declare before rolling if you are doing a Ranged or Melee attack. With 5 damage Melee it is one of the hardest hitting level 1 Treasure Cards!

boardgame: Wander: The Cult of Barnacle Bay – Miniature Monthly Shield Promo Card
description: The Miniature Monthly Shield is a level 1 Treasure Item. It grants the hero +1 Defense, and +1 Knowledge. Bonuses to Knowledge are quite rare and is a huge benefit to exploring darkness and putting out fires.

Open your box, examine the contents, and begin solving the puzzles behind this Halloween mystery!

Some components include QR codes that will need to be scanned with a phone or tablet. This will lead you to more clues or evidence about each case via the internet. You might also be asked to visit websites, send e-mails, etc as part of your investigation.

Summary: Hyde Manor is one of those places in Valley Falls that is notorious for its reputation as a haunted house. The house is abandoned, in a serious state of disrepair, and should have been condemned long ago. But, tonight, on an evening when the air is crisp and filled with the acrid smell of burning leaves, there is a light on in one of the downstairs windows. Tonight...on Halloween night!

As president of the Neighborhood Watch, do you investigate and try to figure out what is going on at Hyde Manor? What will you find at the bottom of this Halloween mystery?

This is a special seasonal One-Time Box for Halloween and is not part of the subscription service.
Can you find the twin first? Each player has exactly one twin animal in their home that matches one of the animals on the card in the middle of the table. Race to find Twins as quickly as you can.

boardgame: Fire in the Library: Forge Gloves Promo Card
description: A promo for the game. It lets the user gain two additional safe spaces to be used at any time on their turn.

boardgame: Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game – Midnight Sons
description: This new set expansion allows players to recruit the Midnight Sons to battle against unholy hordes of the damned. The set adds 5 Heroes, 2 Masterminds, 2 Villain groups and 4 Schemes. The Heroes in this expansion include Morbius, Blade, the Werewolf by Night and more, and all cards included in this set feature original art based on the Marvel Comics. The game box comes with 100 playable cards and a rulesheet.

SPEED AND MIMING GAME

Watch your teammate as they mime, and work out which character they’re pretending to be.

The fastest player to recognize the mime of his teammate wins the round. The first team to win 5 rounds wins the game.

Quick thinking and great concentration are key
if you want to win. Fun, giggles and games guaranteed!

-description from publisher

Today's special is a cake. Everyone has their own favorite cake. But if you're bring the same cake as someone else, the atmosphere will be terrible! But if you bring a cake that's not too trendy or too expensive, the atmosphere will be terrible! What's the best way to do that?

Each round players secretly and simultaneously select a cake to bring to the party. After the cakes are revealed players score points. Trendy cakes are most valuable but points are only earned if no one else has brought the same cake as you have.

TEAM CONNECT GAME
A mentalist is in town to perform their new show for you.
Bring all your friends!

What colour is the grape? Blue, grey, red, green...
There are 6 possible colours, but in this team game, you need to connect to your partner’s mind and both imagine the same thing!

If two players from the same team choose the same colour, they win the card. The first team to have 10 cards wins the game.

The stronger you’re in sync, the sweeter the victory!

+++  Being able to explain your choices and understand the ones your partner makes will be crucial for building a better connection!

-description from publisher

In The Shrimp Curse, players are competing in a trick-taking game, aiming to match low ranked cards and high ranked cards. The higher ranked cards show one or two fried shrimp icons, and the lower ranked cards show empty plates.

When a player wins a trick, any cards showing shrimp stay face up in front of themselves.  If they later win plate cards, these can be matched and moved to a score pile.  However, if a player acquires a plate before a shrimp, the plate is removed from the game.

Players who still have shrimp in front of them at the end of a hand cannot score positive points.

The person with the most points at the end of the game wins.

There is a greater power in this realm than monarchs or magic: dragons. Born of ancient arcane elements, their shadow overhead is enough to make mortals cower in terror. Let the conflict unfold over the eras and see which dragon reigns supreme.

In Ascent of Dragons, players get to be epic dragons battling over who will dominate the realm. Each player uses a dragon with unique cards and upgrades to ravage the lands, cast spells, and vie for supremacy in battle. Combining hand-management, worker-placement, and area-control mechanisms, players have a huge wealth of options for how they want to approach their strategy. Some may build intricate combos of spells while others dominate control of battlefields. Between loyal troops, monstrous allies, and the dragons themselves, the game looks as epic on the table as it feels to play.

After four eras of collecting gems to unlock upgrades and compete, the dragon who has amassed the greatest hoard of treasure is declared the victor and ruler of the realm.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Night Parade of a Hundred Yokai: Monkey King Expansion
description:
New yokai clan for "Night Parade of a Hundred Yokai" which uses unique versatility to create havoc in the islands. 
(Source: Kickstarter page)

From the publisher website:

This Zombie World Bonus Pack of Kickstarter exclusive cards includes:

2 Enclaves (2 cards each)
4 Advantages
5 Population cards
6 Past cards
6 Present cards
6 Trauma cards

WELCOME TO THE CARAVAN

Seems crazy that I live in a bus now. But that's how it's gotta be if we're going to reach Refuge before we all die.

WELCOME TO THE AMUSEMENT PARK

FunWorld's weird, but not too weird—and now we're settling in. Even unloaded a bunch of us off the buses and into game stands. Not exactly what we'd had in mind, but it's beginning to feel like home.

Not available in retail stores.
Requires core game to play

-description from publisher's website

A simple roll and move game by the greek firm EPA.
Move plastic ship pawns by dice, from TROIA to ITHAKA.
Nice colourful board and a box with gor the time outständing graphics.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Spellbook is a fast-paced puzzle-based dueling game that puts players in the role of a powerful wizard in the pursuit of potent elemental magic. They will balance researching spells, binding spellbooks, and battling their opponent all within the course of the duel.

Players draft spell pages that function dually as elemental resources and as combat spells to battle their opponent. Unlike many deck-builders, the cards you draft are never discarded or shuffled away, giving players full agency on how to use and reuse the pages they draft.

Each of the five elements focus on different mechanics and flavors that can be combined in a litany of ways, with a large variety of viable strategies.

Turns are short, with most spells taking multiple turns to cast, putting an emphasis on timing, tempo, and interactive counter-play as you try to destroy the opposing wizard.

—description from the designer

Scarabeo for two players is a quick and addictive edition of Scarabeo, the word game most loved by Italians. This edition is a head to head race to the last letter to score 150 points and win. The classic Scrabble game in a compact format that can be played wherever you want. The great novelty of this version are the letters of the international alphabet (J, K, W, X, and Y) which enrich the challenge with the possibility of creating meaningful words of English origin.

In Scotland Yard, one of the players takes on the role of Mr. X. Their job is to move from point to point around the map of London taking taxis, buses or subways. The detectives – that is, the remaining players acting in concert – move around similarly in an effort to move into the same space as Mr. X. But while the criminal's mode of transportation is nearly always known, their exact location is known only intermittently throughout the game.

Scotland Yard: 40 Jahre Jubiläumsedition features a cult retro design and includes an adventure book in which the reader decides how the story continues. Can the young detectives catch Mister X?

Trapped in darkness, the explorers in Escape the Temple hear strange noises and are constantly being pursued by a wild monkey! Secret passages are hidden under the temple slabs, but also traps from which it is necessary to free oneself. Can they find the keys and escape from the adventure together?

In Privacy Duo, you want to correctly guess how others will answer risqué questions.

On a turn, the active player reveals two question cards: an orange card with answers labeled A, B and C, and a blue card with answers labeled 1, 2, and 3. Questions are along the lines of these two: "What do you like the most in bed?" "What would you do for €500?" The active player determines how they would answer these questions, e.g., A3, then lays face down the answer card containing this letter-number combination. Each other player lays down a card matching what they think the active player will say, after which all the cards are revealed, and whoever guessed correctly scores a point. The first player to score 5 points wins.

The twist in Privacy Duo is that each player has only three answer cards: one showing A1/B2/C3, a second showing A2/B3/C1, and a third showing A3/B1/C2. Thus, you can answer the questions without revealing your exact answers, and others can correctly guess what you'd do without actually knowing what that is!

Rip Thunderstone has raided the podcast again... and he's pretending to be Andy!
Help Chris find the right co-host with this fun 3 player game. The quality of the podcast depends on you (Rip can be a very disruptive influence).

Shuffle the 3 cards and deal one to each player.
Whoever receives the "Chris" card declares themselves by stating, "I am the True Mister Six." Their objective is to successfully find which of the remaining two players has the "Andy" card.
The players who receive the "Andy" and "Rip Thunderstone" cards keep it to themselves. Both of them have the objective of convincing the "Chris" player that they are "Andy" without revealing their card.
If "Chris" identifies the correct "Andy" those two players win. If "Rip Thunderstone" is mis-identified as "Andy" then only the"Rip Thunderstone" player wins.
Will your powers of persuasion win the day and produce a quality podcast, or will the result be an episode filled with bluster and worthless noise?

Gather your best friends
     Read the song
     Guess the song!


A game for anyone who loves songs! All together or in teams, try to guess the songs that were translated by people who think they are bilingual.
Choose the opponent who will answer the 5 questions related to a theme and use your divinatory powers to predict the number of correct answers. Your predictions are correct, score extra points!

With Australian Cash Stream Players attempt to build a viable, 'friendly' financial empire within the constraints of a constantly changing inflation mechanism whilst resisting player takeover bids and personal challenges.

Unlike other board games, Cash Stream allows for all players to 'be in the game' at all times, even when they are without assets or cash.

Intriguing challenge cards allow players to constantly challenge each other player, at any time, thereby Cash Stream is not just about cash management and acquisition.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The In-famous Stalemate of Gutterman's Gulch
description:
The In-famous Stalemate of Gutterman's Gulch is a roll & write game where you assume the role of weasels, trying to trench their way to the edge of the chicken coops at Farmer Brown's. All the while, avoiding egg shot and egg bombs being hurled at you by the enemy.

Each turn you will roll dice to determine what type of event you will encounter and the location of the event. You will perform the event and do four actions. If you successfully maneuver three of your six weasels to each of the three gathering spots, you will be having dinner tonight!

—description from the designer

Cookie Run: High Five is a card game using the exchange mechanic. Players organize a team of five Cookies to fight in the Arena.

Players start with five facedown cards in their play areas. They either draw a card from the deck or draft the top card from the discard pile. And may exchange it with one of their cards in their play areas. The new card is placed faceup.

Players constantly do this and use the abilities on the cards, if they have any, until the deck runs out or a player reveals all of their cards.

The numbers on the cards are the scores. If a player has cards that share the same background(suit), only the card with the highest value counts.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Hunter Herder: A Two Player Print and Play Game
description:
Hunter Herder is a two player game, with a play time of about 20 minutes. Each player will either take on the role of the Herder or the Hunter.

The Herder: In the conifer forests of the northern wastes, you are the Reindeer Herder. But you have heard that an enemy King, who is invading your land, has sent in his Hunter to round up eight reindeer to pull his mighty sleigh. You have no intention of letting this King steal away your herd and so are tasked with using your guile and knowledge to hide them from the enemy King’s Hunter.

The Hunter: The King has called you forth and told you to find eight reindeer to pull his mighty sleigh. The only place to capture these reindeer is the northern wastes in enemy territory and so you have no choice but to head into these harsh lands. One of your minions has told you a Herder protects the reindeer so you must use your hunting prowess to do your King’s bidding.

Aim

Hunter Herder is a two player game. One plays the Hunter and the other the Herder. The Hunter must capture eight reindeer. Using tranquiliser darts, they are downed and carted out, never to return. But the Herder is determined to stop this from happening by obstructing and tricking the Hunter who has only a short amount of time to roundup the required reindeer.

Setup

You will need to print and mount the cards, tiles and counters on card. These are then used during play. You will also need a six sided dice.

—description from the designer

The Fairies are enchanting ordinary items with special powers. You may attach the powerful gear to your mystical beings in order to aid you in your quest. But, Watch Out! Trolls are trying to steal the magical gear and keep it for themselves. Keep far away from the trolls in your search for the Magical Unicorns.

The Magical Unicorn Quest: Mystical Gear is an expansion to Magical Unicorn Quest. The base game is required in order to play.

Components:
15 Mystical Being Cards
30 Mystical Gear Cards
6 player reference Cards
1 Rulebook

-description from publisher's website

Dragons and banshees are terrorizing the Magical Forest. Loot the dragon's lair, craft power potions, and cast mystical magic.

The base game is required in order to play.

Components:
1 Box
1 Rulebook
10 Being Cards
18 Magic cards
18 Potion Cards

-description from publisher's website

OBSERVATION GAME
A group of monsters has just arrived. Please help them
get into their dormitories by matching them up 5 by 5
based on their distinctive marking. Enjoy your stay!

Each player in turn will try to find the common characteristic of the 5 visible monsters. The yellow arm? 4 pink legs? a scar? The first player to find the common element wins the round. The first player to win 3 rounds wins the game.

-description from publisher

MEMORY GAME
The veggie patch is an absolute mess!
There are fruit and vegetables all over the garden!

Veggy Memo is a memory game for young learners. The tiles are placed face down in a square on the table. The matching cards are placed in several piles, face up, to one side. The players have to match up the pairs of tiles and cards, just like a game of pairs.

To do so, The players take turns to tun over one of the tiles. If the tile matches one of the visible cards, they win the card! The player then turns the tile back over, face down, and takes the card as a point won. A new card is thus revealed on top of the pile, giving the players an opportunity to match any of the face-up cards, that's too bad - the player turns the tile back over, face down, and it's time for the next player to have a go.

+++  Increasing difficulty levels.

Start by finding 4 tiles, then 9 and, finally, 36.

-description from publisher

MEMORY AND STRATEGY GAME
The garden is in full bloom, and the insects are racing to play amongst the petals. Who will be the first to land on a set of flowers – the butterflies or the firebugs?

A clever cross between noughts and crosses and draughts. In this insect battle, you’ll need to put your strategist hat on to win the most points over the 3 rounds.

There are 3 ways to win a round: Line up 4 tokens from top to bottom or 5 tokens lengthways, or place all 4 insects on 4 identical flowers. Who will take the win?!

—description from publisher

Inception is a PnP PvP game (CC BY 4.0).

It is a rule-light, easy-to-carry puzzle that can be played within different timeframes, according to the different scenarios (setups).

It's also a game designed to be easily expanded to new scenarios using the existing assets.

The goal of the game is to build a path (one tile at a time) that takes you from a starting position to a finishing point (a treasure chest) before your opponent can do the same. Your opponent may make things harder for you during the course, though.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tiny Turbo Cars: Time Machine Expansion
description:
Break the boundaries of time with this unique racecar expansion… introducing The Time Machine!

Text says:  At the end of your turn, you may execute again 1 command from the 3rd row of your controller.

boardgame: Tiny Turbo Cars: Speed Up Expansion
description:
Tiny Turbo Cars is a frantic race game for 2 to 4 players where you program the movements of your tiny toy car in advance, with a sliding puzzle acting as your remote controller.

Expand your Tiny Turbo Cars experience with the Speed Up Expansion! Play up to 6 players, face new obstacles, and use Power cards to upgrade your racecar mid-race!

Play up to 6 Players
Add 2 more controllers to your base game: more players, more mayhem… more fun!

Power Up
Cross the Check-Point to grab Power cards and upgrade your racecar with news abilities!

Face the fury of the Elements
Includes 4 double-sided elemental track boards with new unique obstacles: dodge fire, slide on ice, and try to program your commands while in the mist!

Power to the Pup
A brand new racecar joins the game: Battle Pup, a real master of the Power cards, is ready to enter the fray!

-description from publisher's website

COOPERATIVE MEMORY GAME
The 4 Tours company offers animal safaris... with a twist. Whether you’re looking for a lion, a crocodile or a monkey, first you need to put them back together!

The youngest player starts, followed by the other players, taking turns in a clockwise direction.
The player must now try to find the 2 tiles that complete this animal.
He picks 2 of the small tiles in the centre of the table and turns them face up.

     If the 2 tiles match the animal, the player positions them on either side of the central tile.


Well done – the animal is now complete!

     If only 1 tile matches the animal, the player should position that tile next to the central tile and turn the other tile back over, face down, on the table.
     If neither of the tiles matches the animal, the player should turn them both back over, face down, on the table.


The game comes to an end when all of the animals have been completed. Well done – the game is won!

+++  Several difficulty levels available:

Beginner with fewer animals and Expert with a shorter play time.

—description from the publisher

OBSERVATION AND SPEED GAME
A game of hide and seek is starting on the playground… So, where are they all hiding?!
In this discarding game, you need to be the fastest to get rid of all your cards by picking out the character in your hand that matches the dice.
The first player to discard all his cards wins the game.

—description from the publisher

The famous “Close the box” strategy and calculation game reimagined for younger children, from 4 years old. In each round, as many flaps as possible must be lowered before being blocked.

A game is played in 5 rounds. in each round, each player has 1 turn; When it is their turn to play, each player rolls the dice until they are unable to close any more flaps.
After 5 rounds, each player adds up the points they have scored in each round; The winner is the player with the lowest total number of points.

—description from the publisher

AUDITORY MEMORY AND SPEED GAME
The weather is unpredictable, and Théo is wondering which outfit to pick...
Listening, memorisation and transcription are the skills that children will need to find the right outfit and get Théo dressed as quickly as possible!

The players take it in turns to be the "Leader", who describes how the tiger is dressed, and the "Player", who dresses their tiger.
The leader pick a card and describes the tiger's outfit while the other player closes their eyes.
The game ends when there are no cards left in the pile: the player with the most cards wins.

—description from the publisher

DEXTERITY, OBSERVATION AND SPEED GAME
A race is raging between Earth and Mars. Who will get to the Red Planet fastest? To get there, you’ll need to be an ace rocket pilot!
A finger game requiring dexterity and observation to complete the various challenges set by the cards.
The further you get in the game, the harder the challenges become.

Destination Mars is a game of dexterity, observation and speed. The race to Mars unfolds in 5 stages: you’ll have to pass by Saturn, cross an asteroid field, make a stop on Neptune and then on the space station to finally arrive at your destination. For each leg of the trip, the organisers outline your mission,
during which you must use certain colours on your pilot pad. And to up the ante, they’ll add more shape constraints as you get closer to the goal. For each section, the first pilot to correctly use their pad according to the mission and the imposed constraints
will be allowed to move forward! The first to reach Mars wins the game.

The first to get to Mars wins.

+++  Develops motor skills and shape/colour recognition.

—description from the publisher

OBSERVATION, SPATIAL AWARENESS AND SPEED GAME

The astronauts are lost at the edge of the galaxy: let’s help them pick out a path through all the stars!
An observation and spatial awareness game, where each player is given a board and cards that they need to place based on the colours shown on the 3 dice. The fastest player wins the round.

+++  Multiple difficulty levels offer something for everyone and the chance to progress!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Architects of the West Kingdom Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards inspired by Architects of the West Kingdom!

To play this realm, assign numbers to either the Cathedral or to the Field, and gain rewards along the way. Alternatively, you can make smart trades to exchange resources for stars. Will you be the architect to reach 6 stars?

-description from publisher

SPEED AND DEXTERITY GAME
At the carnival, the parade is marching by, but everything is higgledy-piggledy... It all needs to be put back in order at once, which is a real challenge when you can't see!

Caranaval Magnet is a game of dexterity, memory and speed. A start card shows players how to place their counters on their board, which they then put inside their bag. They turn a challenge card over, and the first player to rearrange their counters in the order shown on it wins the card; No peeking!
The first player to win 5 challenge cards wins the game

—description from the publisher

In odds & even, a trick-taking game, the cards have different suit backs depending upon if the number on the front is odd or even.  In each hand, rather than a suit being trump, either odd or even numbers are trump.

Each player is dealt a hand which is half odd cards and half even cards. The players look at the half which is not trump and makes a bid on how many tricks they will win this hand.

Then, each player picks up their trump cards and drafts them, choosing 1 and passing the others, until there are no cards to pass.

Players complete a number of rounds equal to the number of players and earn points for hitting their bid and other ways.  The player with the most points wins.

Discover the Access+ version of the popular Timeline memory and thinking game, which has been adapted for players living with cognitive disorders.

The cards are bigger for easier handling, the events were carefully selected to make them accessible to as many people as possible and a pack of Personal Experiences cards has been created specifically for the Access+ version so that players can look back on events in their own life.

This version has the same instructions as the original game: Players create a timeline by placing the cards they draw in the correct order of the events depicted on them. The adaptions make it possible for everyone to play together and have a great time.

Timeline Access+ stimulates memory, thinking, attention and planning, among other functions.

—description from the publisher

Discover the Access+ version of the popular Dobble speed and observation game, which has been adapted for players living with cognitive disorders.

The cards and symbols are bigger for easier handling and recognition, and progressive levels of difficulty are proposed in the rules to make the game accessible to as many people as possible.

Dobble Access+ remains true to the original version of the game: Players must find the symbol in common between their card and the drawn card. The adaptions make it possible for everyone to play together and have a great time.

Dobble Access+ stimulates attention, observation, speed, memory and motor skills, among other functions.

—description from the publisher

Discover the Access+ version of the popular Cortex Challenge game, which has been adapted for players living with cognitive disorders.

The cards are bigger for easier handling, the challenges were carefully selected to make them accessible to as many people as possible, an answer booklet was added to make caregivers’ work easier, and two levels of difficulty are now proposed in the rules.

The instructions are the same as for the original game: Win the challenges to win the game. The adaptions make it possible for everyone to play together and have a great time.

Cortex Access+ stimulates attention, memory, thinking and calculation skills, among other functions.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Ark Nova Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards inspired by Ark Nova!

To play this realm, activate columns and gain rewards based on the number of outlined circles, and outline a new circle every turn. Will you be the owner of a 6-star zoo?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Micro Dojo Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards inspired by Micro Dojo!

To play this realm, be clever in choosing which dice you'll mark with benefits, and which you'll mark without benefits. Try to complete the grid and gain stars along the way. Are you a 6-star dojo master?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Wrench's Great Big Helper Promo Card
description:
SlugFest Games promo prize card for donation to Extra Life 2022!

Text on the card:
Wrench's Great Big Helper
Prize

When you win this Prize, add it to the Prize Bin, then discard your hand and draw that many cards from your Character Deck.

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Thirsty Thirsty Dragons! Promo Card
description:
SlugFest Games promo card for their 2022-2023 convention season. Later included in The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Melvyn vs. Marah (Kickstarter edition).

Text on the card:

Thirsty Thirsty Dragons!
Drink Event
Each player reveals cards from their Character Deck until they reveal a Sometimes Card, then draws their revealed cards. The player(s) who drew the most cards in this way each reveal and drink a Drink from the Drink Deck. If a player reveals a Drink Event before revealing a Drink, they discard it and reveal again.

boardgame: Five Nights at Freddy's: Scare-in-the-Box Game
description:
Five Nights at Freddy's Scare-in-the-Box Game mixes roulette with a Jack-in-the-box.

To play, pass the box around from player to player. On a turn, choose to either turn the crank and tempt fate, or play a pass token to skip your turn and play it safe. Should you fall into Freddy's clutches, you have a chance to use scare tokens to try to steal the win from any remaining players. Compete to be the last person to survive.
family: Video Game Theme: Five Nights at Freddy's

description:
In Lords of Vegas you start out with empty lots, build small casinos, and expand them as your bankroll grows. Your rivals can build next door, and they just might take you over with a clever paint job or a lucky roll of the dice. Buy, sell, trade, and gamble your way to the top as you build your empire along the storied Strip. Channel your inner casino mogul and build your piece of Paradise!

The Lords of Vegas base set is a new printing of the classic board game. This new edition features redesigned money, cards, tray, and more. All the rules and components are functionally the same as the original edition. The new Lords of Vegas includes the 5 & 6-player expansion Up!, which lets players build their casinos in three dimensions, adding new stories as their towers compete for control of the sky.

Here are some other improvements in the new edition.

-Contains 72 dice!
-Risers and the raise rule are included, at any player count.
-Streamlined property cards work seamlessly with Vegas, as well as the expansion boards for Americana.
-More reference cards with turn orders, replacing the less useful House and Player cards.
-Turn order has been streamlined based on years of player feedback.

Lay hidden traps to sabotage opponents in Scrabble Trap Tiles.

This word-tile game gets an explosive twist on regular Scrabble thanks to the addition of trigger and trap tiles that set off special abilities like stealing points or removing tiles, giving clever players an advantage. Build words to score points, but beware the premium squares! A trap may have been set there, turning the game on its head.

A brand new Gembearer (starting deck) for Gemwielders Deckbuilding Game. It comes with two handy cards to track life and power.

Tome is a Gembearer for those who like to construct a sizable deck, packed with powerful abilities. Not only does it offer direct access to the gem card deck, it enables you to shuffle your discard pile at will, and thereby use your shiniest powers as often as possible.

Contrabando is a strategy game for two players.
The player who moves the smugglers wants to get their pawns across the border (line in red and white).
The player who moves the tax guard and the dogs, pretends to arrest as many smugglers as possible.

The fiscal guard is positioned in the center of the third row (row with the red and white line), with his four dogs at his side, two on each side. The nine smugglers are placed in the 1st row on the other side of the game board.

The smugglers move, square by square, forwards and sideways. Walking backwards is not allowed.
The dogs move diagonally, as many squares as they want, forwards and backwards.
The tax guard moves square by square in all directions.

Dogs and smugglers cannot knock opposing pawns off the board.
Dogs can only impede the smugglers' advance (by positioning themselves in front of them).
The tax guard arrests the smugglers when the first occupies a square occupied by the second. The fiscal guard stays in that square and the smuggler is removed and placed in one of the nine dark gray squares (at the bottom of the board), thus being out of the game.

The game ends when there are no smugglers in the green area of the board.
The smugglers who crossed the border are counted. Then the board is turned over and the player who played with the smugglers now plays with the tax guard and the four dogs.
After the second game, the smugglers who crossed the border are counted again.
The player who manages to get the most smugglers across the border after two games is the winner.

Cid Campeador: Warlord is a strategic-level wargame set in the tumultuous Moorish Spain of the late 11th century. Players step into the shoes of iconic figures from the era, vying for supremacy on the Iberian Peninsula. The game spans the dramatic 15 years from 1085 to 1099, a period marked by El Cid's exile, the expansion of Christian kingdoms, and the growing influence of the Almoravids.

Cards grant players initiative, operation points, and the ability to trigger game-changing events. Treasure, acquired through conquest and plunder, provides additional benefits. Player interaction is intense, with strategic alliances, betrayals, and direct confrontations shaping the outcome. Fate cards add an element of chance and unpredictability.

While designed for four players, Cid Campeador: Warlord can be enjoyed with fewer. The AI takes control of absent factions, ensuring a challenging experience for solo or two-player games.

Victory is determined by accumulating the most victory points, earned through territorial conquest, strategic objectives, and controlling key locations.

-description from designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A game about exploring and settling. Starting with a main island, the players start expanding over the whole board. Depending on which fraction is chosen, the city grows in different ways.

All islands offer different opportunities in form of fertilities. Growing the city offers more taxes which can provide more ships and armies to protect both, land and sea. This is useful because all resources and goods need to be transferred to the main island.

The bigger the city, the higher the demands of the inhabitants. Keeping the inhabitants happy, will unlock better event cards, which will be picked on every turn. If the happiness falls, the events will turn in a bad way. Trade between players and a non player trader supports all and gives even more chances to keep everyone happy.

Who ever builds all required houses and provide all requirements for 3 turns, wins.

—description from the designer

The march of technology across BattleTech’s eras is relentless—but some BattleMech designs never die. Each installment of this PDF-only series not only includes a brand new BattleMech or OmniMech, but also details Classic ’Mech designs from both the Inner Sphere and the Clans, now fully rebuilt with Dark Age technology (3085 and beyond).

Volume 27 Includes Rules and Record Sheets for:

• Kamisori Light Tank (Prime/A/B/C/R)
• Galleon Light Tank (GAL-106/106M/200R/201/300/C)
• Buzzard Hover Tank
• Falcon Hover Tank
• Hawk Hover Tank (LTV-4)
• Axel Heavy Tank (IIC/IIC XL)
• Patton Tank (Taurian/Ultra/XL)
• Phantom (T/F/G/I/J/K/L/R)
• Nightyski NGS-7S
• Penetrator PTR-8D
• Gunslinger GUN-3ERD

boardgame: Tuttu Juttu tuntemisleikki lisäkysymyssarja 300 uutta kysymystä
description:
This is the 300-question version in a rectangular box - the same as was used for concurrent Alias expansions.

There is also a 400-question edition in a square box that was similar, but somewhat smaller than the original game box; both were published in 1992.

Both versions simply have additional question decks inside the box, for the four categories of the game; no new rules or mechanics were introduced.

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Double Down Promo Card
description:
Winter Season Promo 2022 of FryxGames, which you can buy on its own, or get for free if you order something at their webstore during Winter 2022.

Double Down is a Prelude-card without tags.

It copies your other prelude's direct effect.

Promo card X40.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Tiny Turbo Cars: Space Team Expansion
description:
Defy the laws of gravity, venture into the cyberspace, and meet alien creatures from the cosmos with the Space Team Expansion!

3 additional sci-fi themed race cars (Blargop, Space Rover, and Robotron) are accompanied by 2 Junction tiles, introducing more hi-tech hazards to your races.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Tiny Turbo Cars: Adventure Crew Expansion
description:
Perform reckless acrobatic moves, dart around like a ninja, and bomb your way to victory with the Adventure Crew Expansion!

3 additional adventure themed race cars (Sprintarr, Jungle Buggy, and Shino Bike) are accompanied by 2 Junction tiles, introducing more traps and obstacles to your races.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Tiny Turbo Cars: Fantasy Squad Expansion
description:
Hunt monsters, cast magic spells, and join a horde of crazy goblins with the Fantasy Squad Expansion!

3 additional fantasy themed race cars (Bomblin, Dungeon Fighter, and Flying Wish) are accompanied by 2 Junction tiles, introducing more hurdles and perils to your races.

-description from publisher's website

ROLL-AND-MOVE AND COLLECTING GAME

Prepare to board! Which pirate will collect the most loot or find Edward the Terrible’s treasure hidden on Crocodile Island?
You’ll need a real buccaneer’s luck and courage to win! Luck, strategy and courage are the theme of this pirate game, where only making the right decisions will win the race to the treasure. Simple rules for games full of surprises.

“Pirate Island” is a roll-and-move and collecting game. As you move across the board there are tokens to collect, including gold coins, precious jewels, a ship, and a compass or map to help you reach Crocodile Island. Once you get there, you’ll need to hunt for a key and your treasure chest. Watch out for the other pirates though – they might try to pillage your plunder!

The game is over when a player has collected:
• their treasure chest in the correct colour + a key
or
• 7 gold coins
or
• 5 precious jewels

That player is declared the winner, and top pirate.

-description from publisher

STRATEGY GAME
Hidden in the trees, troops of monkeys are fighting over coconuts. Which troop will be the best organized and collect the most coconuts – all while driving out their opponents before the tiger arrives?!

A strategy game where players will have to move with precaution and anticipation in order to avoid being attacked by the other players and to get the maximum of coconuts. When there are no more monkeys or the 3 tigers are out, each player counts his points: 1 point per coconut and 5 points per monkey captured!

-description from publisher

It’s the evening of the major spring festival. All the Tinylys are invited and are getting ready for this year’s masked ball. To be ready, they need to get their companion, a mask, a cake, an invitation and a flower.

“Tinyly party” is a roll-and-move and object collecting game. Players take on the role of a Tinyly, who must collect 5 tokens along the way to get ready for the ball. To collect their tokens, each of them will first have to win stars: the “money” in the world of the Tinylys.

End of the game:
To get to Léopold, a player must have, on their card, all 5 tokens and at least 3 stars.
The game ends once a player has collected their 5 tokens and joined Léopold, who gives the winner a beautiful daisy.

-description from publisher

The children help the little witch to cook a porridge. Only certain ingredients (objects) that fit into a given category are allowed in. Does the ball belong with the toys? Do the shoes match the clothes? Is the tractor a vehicle?

This game stimulates language development and builds vocabulary.

For older children there is a variant:
Each child receives a screen, a category tile and 4 objects. One by one, each player chooses another player and asks them for a specific item that fits their category.
The first to own the 4 items that belong to their category wins.

Mein erstes Sprech-Hexchen is a variant for very young children of Die freche Sprech-Hexe.

boardgame: Skulls of Sedlec: Dig & Write Game
description:
This expansion to Skulls of Sedlec adds a postcard expansion with a dig & write variant game.

Adding in the expansion is easy, just shuffle in the expansion cards to the base game and play.

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Heritage: Reset Pack
description:
This pack includes everything you need to begin a new chronicle using your copy of Vampire: The Masquerade – Heritage. Just replace the characters modified during the course of play, reset the chronicle deck in order, and steel yourself for another 700 years of human and vampiric history.

Includes all 80 character cards and stickers to begin a new campaign
Contents: 80 Double-sided mortals / 106 ability stickers
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Word Capture Tiki-Taka needs speed and wit. This game for 3-6 player, and takes 10 minutes to play.

In Word Capture Tiki-Taka players has to call an associated word following the calling condition. But be aware of calling with another player at the same time unless you might get a penalty point.

To set up, shuffle 60 subject cards and make a face-down deck on the middle of the table and turn the rest back to the box.

When the game begins, one of the players turns the card over and puts it on the table so every player can see the Symbol on the deck and the subject on the front of the card.

All players must check the subject of the symbol matching the dummy on the card just released and shout out the words related to the subject to the letter condition.

If you succeed in saying a word alone, you get a point, and if you shout word at the same time as someone else, you get a minus point.

When the pile of cards in the middle of the table runs out, the person with the most points wins.

IKI: Akebono introduces a new location to discover in Edo: the bridge of Nihonbashi, which expands the main street and offers players new opportunities. Players can stop at the bridge to meet famous personalities from the Edo era (like Hokusai Katsushika, Sanyo Rai or Tokunai Mogami) and to trade with the ships (built by the players) in the river.

The Akebono expansion also adds new character cards, building cards, and new pipe, tobacco, and fish tokens for more strategic depth and decision making.

Villages: Crystal Quest is the 7th expansion for the Villages card game–and it’s the biggest one yet, with a monster-infested dungeon to explore and alternate rules that bring new life to all your existing cards.

In addition to waging war on other players, you can send your entire village to explore the dungeon and fight monsters as a group. Unit abilities interact with each other in new ways and each battle becomes a puzzle as you decide which monster each character should fight in order to defeat them all.

For each monster destroyed, you’ll draw treasure cards, which give you extra gold at the end of the game. But what you’re really searching for are the Crystals–six powerful gems, one in each color. The round will only end after a certain number of these have been discovered, allowing you to spend a little longer developing strategies and building your civilization before moving on to the next one.

Villages: Crystal Quest is an expansion that requires the Villages base game.

Contains 69 cards and a rule booklet.

-description from publisher's website

STACKS is a set collection game built around collecting sets of red, blue, and green cards. The cards may be in a central array on the table, in front of a player, or in a player's hand.

On their turn a player will either play 1 card from their hand or the top card of the deck to the central array.  Alternatively, they can play a set of cards (one of each type) to the central array.  Depending upon the adjacencies of colors and certain card effects, the card which was played may have its effect triggered or the player may be forced to acquire cards.

The game will end when the deck runs out of cards.  Then, each player scores points for cards left in their hand and cards in front of them.  Any sets of cards (one of each type) in front of them can be combined into a single card. The player with the fewest points wins.

Dark City is a game for two to four players in which each player takes the role of an investigator trying to solve his case before other investigators. Through his contacts, he must manage to obtain the right suspect to be the one with the most progress in his case.

The game is fast and dynamic and uses card control and player choices to advance your case. The object of the game is at the last turn to compare the symbol on the card remaining in the player's hand with the symbols on the player's case card. The player whose symbol has the most points is considered the winner. Another way to win is to be the only player left during the round, some cards allow this.

The game has mechanics similar to other pocket games like Love Letter and Lost Legacy. Players accustomed to this type of game will feel familiar with the rules. Not so experienced players will be able to quickly learn the rules of the game.

Peleia is a variant of Stratego, with typical characters from southern Brazil.

It also introduces a few new elements, such as cards and dice, and it's played with an 8x10 board - altogether turning the matches more dynamic than Stratego.

Most pieces move according to an asymmetric dice - which contain three sides 01, two sides 02, and one side 03.

Each player receives 03 cards, with which they can change the regular mechanics of the game.

With the card "Não tá morto quem peleia", you decide on a fight/encounter by rolling a D6 rather than by the characters/pieces' rankings.

With the card "Às brinca", you can cancel an attack that, otherwise, you'd lose.

With the card "Traíra", you can reveal one of your opponent's pieces.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Karate Kid: All Valley Tournament Unofficial Game
description:
Ladies and Gentlemen, welcome once again to the 1984 All Valley Karate tournament! We go live to the mat now, where fighters from Miyagi-Do face off against fighters from Cobra Kai.

The Karate Kid: All Valley Tournament Unofficial Game is a 2-8 player card game where teams of players represent the very best of two dojos featured in the Karate Kid franchise, Miyagi-Do, and Cobra Kai.

Only the strongest will survive with a mix of skill, timing, and luck to determine who is the champion!

Note: This is an unofficial game, and all copyrighted materials are the property of the original holders including artwork, IP material or other design elements.

—description from the designer

Instant Saga is a drafting game where players select a team of Vikings to then attempt to land at four locations.

The Viking cards show certain colors, which indicate which islands they can land on. Viking cards which do not help capture a land card are sent to Valhalla, and may score points in a secondary manner.

If a player does not contribute at least 1 card to a landing attempt, they are eliminated from the game.

The player with the most points wins.
artists: Dougen Rock Boy (ドウゲンロックボーイ)
Yamauchi Rock Boy (ヤマウチロックボーイ)
publishers: ハレルヤロックボーイ (Hallelujah Rockboy)

boardgame: Roll and Rails: Japan Map Pack
description:
Japan map pack expansion for Roll and Rails game. This expansion includes 3 maps:
Kyushu - for solo game
Hokkaido - for duel game
Honshu - for multiplayer game

boardgame: Roll and Rails: Great Britain Map Pack
description:
Great Britain map pack expansion for Roll and Rails game. This expansion includes 3 new maps:
Northern Ireland for solo play
Wales for duel play
England for multiplayer game

boardgame: Roll and Rails: USA Map Pack 1
description:
USA map pack 1 expansion for Roll and Rails game. It includes 3 new maps:
New England for solo play
Mid Atlantic for duel play
Midwest for multiplayer game

boardgame: Roll and Rails: USA Map Pack 2 Expansion
description:
USA map pack 2 expansion for Roll and Rails game. It includes 3 new maps:
California for solo play
Texas for duel play
South for multiplayer game

boardgame: Roll and Rails: Railway Moguls variant
description: Railway Moguls variant game for Roll and Rails. Manage the companies, expand the railway connections and multiply the company resources in this roll and write game. This variant includes special maps designed for Railway moguls variant of the game.

Circle of Power is a free mecha-themed print & play game that you can play using ANY deck of cards. Yes, ANY! Print out a Fighter sheet for both players, grab some dice and cards, and you’re good to go!

Each Fighter has various Parts that they can activate. Attach cards to Parts to Charge them up. Once they're ready to go, Discharge them to unleash their effects, allowing you to do things like attack your opponent or disrupt their card economy. Discharging also generates Gauge, which you can later spend to activate more powerful effects. Reduce your opponent's life to 0 to win the game!

KALDOM is a skirmish game opposing 2 players on various places. The main advantage of KALDOM is to offer a game system of extreme fluidity and accessibility, to share the pleasure of miniature games with family and friends, and to make discover this pleasure to people who are not familiar with this hobby. The simplicity of the game, thanks to a unique and ingenious system, allows to keep the tactical interest of this type of game. You can play with the various tribes designed specifically for the game, or create your own team with an intuitive character generator. The various powers of the magic stones will make no two games the same and the tactical interest will be constantly renewed.

—description from the designer

KALDOM est un jeu d’escarmouche opposant 2 joueurs sur divers théâtres d’opérations. L’atout majeur de KALDOM est d’offrir un système de jeu d’une extrême fluidité et accessibilité, pour partager le plaisir des jeux de figurines en famille, entre amis, et faire découvrir ce plaisir à des personnes étrangères à ce « hobby ». La simplicité du jeu, grâce à un système unique et ingénieux, permet de garder l’intérêt tactique de ce type de jeu. Vous pouvez jouer avec les diverses peuplades conçues spécifiquement pour le jeu, où créer de toutes pièces votre équipe avec un générateur intuitif de personnages très complet. Les pouvoirs variés des pierres magiques feront qu’aucune partie ne se ressemblera et l’intérêt sera sans cesse renouvelé.

Flieg, Dino is a cooperative game about the world of feelings. Together with the flying dinosaur Tika, kids learn to explore and identify different emotions and learn how to deal with them.
‘Scene cards’ picture various scenarios showcasing different emotions (joy, sorrow, anger, fear, pride and shame). By rolling dice, players are trying to match the shown emotions to acquire feathers. The objective of this game is to gather enough of these feathers to enable Tika to finally fly and escape a rumbling volcano.
Furthermore, some of the ‘Scene cards’ also address the topic of addiction, and emotions which may come up for kids confronted with those topics.
This game was developed in cooperation with NACOA, and its usage is intended for Kindergartens. Kindergarten teachers can utilize Flieg, Dino to introduce children to the topic of addiction in a safe, pedagogical and easy to grasp way. Simultaneously kids are encouraged to talk about their feelings in a playful environment.

Torfitz is a management game about structural change developed for students of ninth grade or higher, university students, and apprentices.

Players take on different roles (such as citizen, mayor, entrepreneur, etc.) and are confronted with different tasks. Together, from their character’s perspective, they have to communicate and develop ideas for projects. Afterwards, they have to present their ideas to a committee, which chooses the best ideas on the basis of different criteria. During that selection, a shift of perspective happens, because players have to slip in different roles as part of the committee.
The exchange of arguments in constructive discussions and finding compromises creates a competency development in democratic, social and environmentally sustainable reasoning.

Torfitz can be played with 6 to 30 players. There are 3 different variations of the game with varying complexity and game durations of 45 min, 60 min and 90 min. Game and rules are set to allow a smooth start into the game requiring no previous knowledge and minimal effort.

description:
Deluxe Guadalcanal: The Battle for Henderson Field is a grand tactical game on the efforts of the U.S. Marine Corps and U.S. Army to capture and hold the island of Guadalcanal during the beginning of the island-hopping campaign to push back the Japanese empire. 
Errors in strategy and logistics at large made this effort extremely difficult for the Marines, as they held on to the island in the face of Japanese onslaught with dwindling supplies. 
Players experience huge swings of fortune from one side to the other and back again as the battle for the island unfolds. Critical to the efforts of both sides is the capture and holding of Henderson Field, an air strip whose control dictates air superiority and the ability to land supplies and much needed reinforcements.

The game includes a huge 17” by 55” map, provided in easily printed letter- and tabloid-sized sheets (8.5x11” and 11x17” respectively), that covers the northern extents of the island. Combat units are mostly battalions, with a smattering of regiments and companies, representing infantry, armor, and artillery. There are also specialized units such as engineers and LVTs that aid in the maintenance or destruction of Henderson Field as well as movement across the difficult jungle terrain.

The basis for Deluxe Guadalcanal is the original Guadalcanal game published by The Avalon Hill Game Company in 1966. The project began as an effort to create an updated version of the original game’s map, providing some tweaks here an there for accuracy or playability. Once completed, it was not that much of an effort to also create a new, colorful set of counters (including new informational markers) that simplifies play. It was then a short, additional step to rewrite the rules in a manner that incorporates some of the “advanced” rules, some new rules and mechanics, and known errata to create a new, cohesive rules set of under twelve pages.

Now, YOU can refight the battle for Guadalcanal!

—description from the publisher

Game Changer is a collection of 15 types of board games designed as an introduction box to modern board games.

Descriptions of the games:

The Action Game:
Summary: Are your reactions fast enough to be the first to snap on cards? Players take turns placing cards on the table as they race to ‘snap’ on all the correct cards. Similar to the classic game of snap. The game is played in rounds where players compete to be the first to fulfill a customer’s order (3 resource items).

Objective: Be the first player to snap on all the items that each customer wants in order to collect the active customer card. The first player to collect 3 customer cards wins the game!


The Abstract Game:
Summary: This game is like chess for the modern era. Two players sit on either side of the board and move Player Tokens across. Unlike chess, the board’s tiles dictate how the Player Tokens can be moved, not the Player Tokens.

Objective: Win by being the first player to move all 3 of your Player Tokens to the opposite side of the board.

The Bidding Game:
Summary: Players are at an auction each with the same amount of money. Can you outsmart your opponents by spending the least to gain the most? The game consists of 10 auctions where players bid on resources, aiming to have the most points at the end of the game.

Objective: Win by having the most points at the end of the game (after 10 auctions).

The Bluffing Game:
Summary: Can you lie without getting caught? Can you tell when others are lying? Each player starts with 5 coins and one animal card, each with its own special ability to earn or remove coins. Players can choose to lie or tell the truth about which animal card they have in order to earn or remove coins from other players.

Objective: Win by being the last player remaining with coins. This is a game of elimination.

The Co-op Game:
Summary: Players must work as a team to find and arrest the 2 Diamond thieves while protecting the innocent bystanders. To do this, each player must choose a card from their hand and perform the actions stated on the card. Winning will take real teamwork.

Objective: When both Diamond thieves are arrested everyone wins, but when 2 innocent bystanders are arrested everyone loses!

The Dice Game:
Summary: Seven animals race around a track while all players watch and bet, aiming to earn the most money by the end of the game. There are multiple ways of earning money including betting on the winners/losers or buying land (like Monopoly) that earns money when animals land on them.

Objective: The player who has the most money when the first animal reaches the finish line wins the game.

The Hidden Identity Game:
Summary: Can you figure out who among you are lying? Players are assigned a secret identity, which will either be on the innocent team or the guilty team. Players close their eyes while listening to an audio recording from Game Changer’s website, which instructs certain animals to perform specific actions while everyone else has their eyes closed. In the second phase of the game, all players open their eyes and discuss what happened in order to determine who the guilty team is, while the guilty team lie to try to not get caught!
​
Objective: After 5 minutes of discussion, all players vote on who they think is the guilty team. If the majority vote is correct, the innocent team wins. If the majority vote is incorrect, the guilty team wins.

The Market Game:
Summary: Players visit a bustling market to buy and sell valuable resources in order to earn the most amount of money. Players must decide whether to claim, swap, trade, or sell resources to customers in order to profit the most.

Objective: The first player to reach $40 worth of coins wins the game!

The Memory Game:
Summary: This game combines memory and strategy. Resource Cards are placed face down on the table with players able to peek at any 2 cards to start their turn. The goal is to collect sets of matching Resource Cards by remembering their location on the table. Each player has their own memory bank where they can strategically store up to 2 sets of cards that will help them complete sets and stop other players from collecting them.

Objective: The game ends when there are no more Resource Cards left in the middle of the table. The player with the most cards collected wins.

The Numbers Game:
Summary: Are your reactions fast enough to be the first to snap on cards? Players take turns placing cards on the table as they race to ‘snap’ on all the correct cards. Similar to the classic game of snap. The game is played in rounds where players compete to be the first to fulfill a customer’s order (3 resource items).

Objective: Be the first player to snap on all the items that each customer wants in order to collect the active customer card. The first player to collect 3 customer cards wins the game!

The Party Game:
Summary: Are your reactions fast enough to be the first to snap on cards? Players take turns placing cards on the table as they race to ‘snap’ on all the correct cards. Similar to the classic game of snap. The game is played in rounds where players compete to be the first to fulfill a customer’s order (3 resource items).
​
Objective: Be the first player to snap on all the items that each customer wants in order to collect the active customer card. The first player to collect 3 customer cards wins the game!

The Realtime Game:
Summary: This game involves a lot of yelling! Players simultaneously yell out the actions written on their cards to try to find another player yelling out the same action. Once a player has found a match, the matching players both perform that action e.g. ‘Team Spirit’, ‘Bro Bump’ or ‘Pinky Swear’. Both players then discard the Action Cards. The goal is to be the first player who finds matches for all their cards, therefore having zero Action Cards remaining in their hand.

Objective: The first player to discard all of the Action Cards in their hand wins.

The Set Collecting Game:
Summary: Players attempt to create the strongest sets to win, like in Poker. However, unlike Poker, there are 7 Resource Cards face up on the table which players compete for. Each resource card states a rule that explains how that card can be won. The player who satisfies the rule at the end of the game wins that resource card, and the player with the most resource card points wins the game.

Objective: The player with the highest total value of resource cards wins the game.

The Storytelling Game:
Summary: Players tell a funny group story using cards from their hand and trying to be the first player to use up all their cards. To use up their cards faster, players may interrupt other players’ stories using special cards. Each player also has their own pre-written custom ending which they need to use.

Objective: The first player to use up all their cards and end the story with their ending wins the game.

The Tile Game:
Summary: Place tiles on the table next to each other to create a continuous road. Be the first player to collect 3 Treasure Chests. Along the way, you’ll need to slow down your opponent(s) by re-routing and blocking their path(s) to ensure you are the first to collect the Treasure Chests.

Objective: The first player to collect 3 Treasure Chests wins the game.

Small Store Survival is about an expensive shopping street where high rents make it hard for new businesses to survive. Most stores will not make it past their start up period and quickly get replaced by others. Only a few shops manage to claim a permanent spot in this shopping street. In Small Store Survival players are entrepreneurs who are up to the challenge. These cunning businesspeople will discover what it takes to let businesses thrive at this challenging location and successfully open store after store. Will you be the most successful one of them, who shapes the future of this prestigious shopping street?

Small Store Survival combines hand management and action selection in a new and challenging way: Each player starts the game with the same deck of ten cards. These ten cards represent the ten different stores you will try to open during the game.

Players start the game with five cards in their hand and five in their draw piles. Each round players choose a card from their hand and place it on top of a card on the table that was played in the previous round (one store replaces another store). When you replace a store, you are allowed to take the action of the replaced store. The game itself also has a store in play each round. This means that each round one store won’t get replaced. This is the store that manages to stay in the shopping street permanently and scores points at the end of the game. The cards that are played during this round are the cards that can be chosen in the next one. In order to let your store, claim a permanent spot, you will need to anticipate which action will not be picked in the next round.

Common actions like simply drawing new cards or reshuffling your draw pile are no free actions in this game. You can only perform them by replacing the stores that grant these actions. This makes hand management very tight. Store survival is all about selecting actions that allow you to effectively manage your hand. But even more about misleading your opponents by picking different actions then they expect and trying to read which actions other players will want or need to take. This makes Small Store Survival a highly interactive game which forces you to consider multiple aspects when selecting an action.

—description from the designer

5D20 Football is a game built for the solo gamer. Everything runs off the roll of 5 d20 dice. The game is fairly simple yet has depth in how plays turnout. The tabletop version uses charts and dice.

To begin with the dice are rolled to get the play type, depth, and the who which is all based off a teams' offensive chart. The plays take into account the down, distance, and the ball location on the field. Later in the game, time left and the score can affect the play calls as well. This roll also on occasion can trigger a fumbled snap or a pre-snap penalty.

Once the play is known we progress to the play itself which require rolling 5d20 dice. To start, the red die gets you the defender for the play (this player comes off the defensive teams chart), and who the player is also depends on the game situation (a passing situation is more likely to get a player from the secondary while a run play is more likely to have a lineman).

For a PASS: The white die gives you how the defender affects the play. They can get pressure on a QB, get a chance to break up a pass, allow an open receiver, which will result in the receiver making a catch, a drop, or yards after a catch. The blue die is checked against the QB or the receiver, depending on what the defensive player allowed. The black die gets your yardage and the yellow die is for a possible penalty.

For a RUN: The white die gives you how the defender affects the play. They can get penetration and hit a runner in the backfield, hit the runner at the line, or allow a hole for the runner to run through. The blue die gets how the runner reacts to what the defense gave them by spinning away from a loss, to breaking a run through a hole. The black die gets you the yardage gained and the yellow die is used for a possible penalty.

Special team plays are as simple as rolling the 5d20 and using them to get distance, the returner and yardage, along with chances for penalties and fumbles.

Players are rated so that their impact in the game reflect their real life styles. So while the game may seem simple there is a ton of interaction allowing everything that happens in a football game to happen on the tabletop with 5D20 Football.

The campaign expansion to Siege of Valeria introduces rules for a multi-game series that will test your skill at defeating the troops and siege engines arrayed against you. A powerful Boss and Elite Troops will join each battle under an enemy Commander while penalties and bonuses follow you from one game to the next. A Duke is there to assist you, but it is your own skills that will lead the campaign to glory. . .or to failure.

 • Bosses - One Boss card is added to the Siege Engines deck and attacks you twice each round when it comes out. You need to defeat it twice before you remove it from the battle.

 • Elite Troops - Four Elite Troop cards are added to the Troop deck and each one requires two specific die values to defeat.

 • Commanders & Dukes - A Valeria Duke and a Monster-kin Commander are drawn at the start to give each side special benefits.

 • Lose the Battle, Win the War - A campaign is three games, each worth more VP than the one before. You might lose one siege and still win the war. . .if you're skillful.
