description:
YOU START WITH THE ANSWERS
All the answers you need in Colourbrain are in your hand. And they’re all colours!

300 COLOURFUL QUESTIONS
What colour is the lid on full-fat milk… what colour is a sharon fruit… which colour cards would you put down?

ALL NEW QUESTIONS
This mini version of the game contains 120 brand new questions. Play it on its own or combine it with the full-sized version

The game lasts for 5 or 6 rounds (years). Each round consists of 4 phases:

     Planning Phase — Players simultaneously plan their actions behind their player screens.
     Turn Order Phase — Players change the turn order and receive the track bonus.
     Action Phase — In turn order, players perform the planned actions.
     Structure Activation Phase — Players may use the effects of their Structures.


Actions are performed on 4 game boards, each representing 4 ways to interact with its cells:
Pollution board — Reduce pollution and repopulate clean cells.
Ruins board — Clear the area, gather valuable modules, and plant the groves.
Mutant board — Study mutants and extract genes to recreate animals.
Creation board — Spend resources to recreate animals and repair structures.

Your goal is to earn the most Victory Points by doing the following:
Complete Goals
Study Mutants
Reduce Pollution and gather valuable resources
Gather Compatible Modules
Recreate Animals
Build Eco-Friendly Structures
Plant Groves

boardgame: Typical Spanish: Un Juego Muy Nuestro
description:
Typical Spanish is a pop culture familiar party game. Create 2 groups and win by imitating, drawing, mimicking or answer correctly trivia questions about Spanish culture. Let's start the party and have some fun with friends ¡Olé!

Typical spanish es el primer juego de mesa familiar tipo party con pruebas sobre la cultura española más gamberra, ¡olé!
Imitar, dibujar, hacer mímica o adivinar solo serán algunas de las cosas que tendréis que hacer para ganar el juego de mesa más Typical Spanish
¡Qué comience el sarao! Tira un dado para saber de qué categoría tendréis que jugar la tarjeta durante esta ronda. El primer equipo que tenga una tarjeta de cada categoría ganará Typical Spanish, ¿quieres saber cuáles son?
Cómo está la people: si imitases a personajes muy nuestros como un bailaor flamenco, ¿crees que tu equipo lo adivinaría?
Hazte un Picasso: conviértete en un gran artista y pinta cosas como Naranjito para que los demás lo adivinen
Aquí hay tomate: hay frases míticas como ¡Hola, corazones!, ¿sabes quién las dijo por primera vez?
Spanish Trivial: preguntas sobre las cosas más curiosas de nuestra cultura y tres posibles respuestas. Sólamente una es la correcta
Blanco y en botella: decir expresiones españolas es muy sencillo, pero hacerlo moviendo los labios y gesticulando sin hablar para que el equipo las adivine es todo un reto
Pregúntale a Reverte: ¿podréis adivinar si las palabras propuestas están o no aceptadas por la RAE?
Ocupa poco espacio y sus cartas laminadas de calidad hacen que lo podáis jugar en casa, en la piscina, de viaje o en cualquier otro sitio

Sense of Time is a real-time strategy game for 2 to 4 players, where hourglass-powered robots battle for control of energy across a contested field.

All players act simultaneously—no turns, no waiting. Each player controls up to five robots (represented by actual hourglasses) that can be moved only when their sand runs out. Timing becomes everything.

Use your robots to claim batteries from the central board and drag them back to your zone to score energy—or snatch them from your rivals. When robots collide, dice determine the winner of the clash.

The game is played over four 10-minute phases, each allowing different numbers of robots in play. At the end of the final phase, the player with the most energy wins.

description:
Revolution: State of War is a 2 player asymmetric military strategy on the Armed struggle for American Independence.  The primary objective is to establish and maintain the support of the population for your cause, but to do so often requires one to have control or possession of the territory where people live, and for that one must take command of your nation's forces, maneuver and engage in battles to secure the colonies.

Both sides have very unique and distinct advantages.  The British with their professional force and naval prowess can dominate coastal positions, but may have some difficulty when getting too far away from their coastal bases.  They know the longer the war drags on the more chances Americans will have to secure French support along with building up their forces to a higher standard.  As such, the British will want to hit the ground running and maintain the pressure, seeking out engagement after engagement to decimate the rebel forces so when they are better trained it will be immaterial at that point, or a day late and a dollar short.

The American's on the other hand need time to secure an alliance with the French and convert this rag-tag band of revolutionaries into a professional army to really flip the script but one doesn't always get the army they want, when they need it and have to fight with the army they have.  As such, to buy time and save face with the population the American forces will need to make the British advance as costly as possible by occupying advantageous terrain, leveraging the support of the population with Militia forces, and knowing when to simply run away.

The game incorporates cards but is not a card driven game, the cards provide enhancements and bonus abilities as well as enable deception actions that can really through a wrench in the works for your opponent.

When forces contest territory on the Game Board, the game zooms in to a unique battle sequence, which is a mini-game itself; units deploy into lanes of combat and roll dice to score hits and kills with bonuses and enhancements for aspects like rout, exposed flank (enfilade), and Dragoon Charge.

Can you lead your nations forces to victory?

—description from the designer

description:
The challenge of the game is how to combine the basic colours red, blue and yellow to create new colours. The winner is the first player to produce his favorite colours.

Things get tricky when players compete for the basic colours and colour plans are intercepted. Experienced players develop the talent to detect their opponents colour plans and include them in their own colour strategy.

The gameplay is as follows. Every player gets a few hidden goal cards with colors. Than the active player draws one colour disc from the bag. Using his colour discs he can move the current marker in the direction of the chosen discs on the rondel. Once the player reaches his secret goal he can reveal one of his cards.

St. Paul didn't spread the gospel alone. In fact, the book of Acts refers to numerous individuals who played key roles in his ministry. Each person had a different critical part to play, including those who delivered St.Paul's letters to the newly established churches. This was a dangerous and arduous task. Messengers braved harsh wilderness, barren deserts — even the deadly Roman Legion! — to help St. Paul get his Epistles to Christian brothers and sisters in need of guidance and encouragement.

In Journeys of Saint Paul, you will be one of several messengers dispatched to deliver one of St. Paul's momentous letters. Your mission: Be the first to cross the city gate and deliver St. Paul's letter to its secret destination. But beware! The Romans are intent on seeking and destroying all Christians, so the chase may be on! This adventure requires cleverness, courage, and tenacity — with billions of souls at stake.

—description from the publisher

Achô! is a 9-in-1 card game that offers multiple ways to play and grows with your child. In this little box, you will find fun and the development of specific skills for various age groups.

Each game mode is designed for a specific age, offering just the right amount of fun and challenge!

Some games included:

Achô!
Flip cards and show them to your little ones. They must get the name of the figure right, which can have additional details such as color and size.

WHERE DID IT GO?
Show some cards and then turn them face down. Your little ones should try to remember where the requested card ended up.

We are at the dawn of a new era. Architects and environmentalists have come together to design the Arcology: a self-sufficient high-tech building to house large populations in a sustainable environment. Huge construction sites are underway to shape the home of tomorrow. You have been chosen to build these Arcologies and welcome citizens to their new home.

Type of game:
Arcology is both a competitive and cooperative card management game.

Description:

     Administer 3 boards at once: your Arcology board and 2 shared boards.
     Upgrade your board to house citizens, collect victory points and generate resources for your other boards.
     Cooperate with neighbouring players to collect victory points together.


Goal:
The player with the most victory points on these 3 boards wins the game.

Conclusion:
A highly interactive game, where each player has the pleasure to contribute to the progress of the game. Despite all this complicity, only one of them will be chosen to guide humanity towards this ecological transition.

—description from the publisher

Build a kingdom, trade, and face the Vikings in this captivating hybrid strategy game with the setting in the 11th century Ireland!

Irish clans and Vikings are competing for power and the island is split up between provincial kings, all of them trying to take the lead by exploring the land and be the first to take control of scarce resources. But no clan is safe from the Vikings who battles and plunder.

As a provincial king you lead your own Irish clan, with the ambition of winning the game. You always have to deal with the circumstances unique for each game play and there are several paths to victory: by kingdom building, conquest or economy.

The game flow follows a seasonal calendar, and through the year you explore new territories, gather resources and construct buildings and ships. You may also move your clan units and start battles.

During the fair military units may be recruited and you may trade resources in a challenging and unique dynamic trade system. The numerous development cards give you a possibility to gain a variety of new skills and enhance your military capabilities during the game.

Although the game contains an intriguing battle system, battle between players is downplayed. Focus for the battles is instead the exciting and automated Viking system, which includes both sea raids and ravaging Viking warbands to deal with.

The game is designed for high replayability, where each game session provides a new challenge and experience. 8 different kings can be combined with 4 different clans. The development of the economy, the activities of the Vikings, and alternative development cards are also factors that make each game session unique.

So, will you succeed with your chosen path, take control as the High King and proclaim yourself ‘Emperor of the Gaels’?

-description from designer

Exclusive Mastery cards: These 24 Mastery cards allow you to gain Knowledge points during your opponents’ turns when they produce a certain amount!

The Bunuments: the Bunuments are back by (very) popular demand! These rabbit-themed Monuments replace the vertical elements of the standees for The Glorious Library, The Mausoleum, The Great Pyramid and The Colossus.

Capitals: This mini expansion allows you to turn your Cities into Capital Cities to increase your power and Influence!

Traits : This mini expansion allows you to bestow specific traits on your Civilization, giving asymmetrical starting conditions and additional Objectives to complete.

The Domination Token: This module allows you to gain additional points at the end of the game, by betting on how many Objectives you’ll be able to fulfil!

The Stranger Domination card: This mysterious character gives a helping hand to any player who is not able to claim a Domination card at the end of an Age.

"Tank Commander: The Ardennes" is a Tactical-level, narrative-style Solitaire-only "print and play" board-wargame using a tables-based game engine where the player commands an American Army Sherman M4A3 tank fighting enemy German forces in the Belgian Ardennes during late 1944 to early 1945. As the commander of a Sherman M4A3 tank, your ultimate goal is to survive the 'Battle of the Bulge' until late January 1945, while at the same time killing as many German enemy units as possible.

With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with detailed damage modeling, numerous crewmen skills, 12 types of enemy German Army units, battle weather effects, terrain types, random events, and a multitude of table modifiers, Tank Commander: The Ardennes offers memorable experiences and high replay value. Are you up to the challenge of surviving the Battle of the Bulge?

The player will move his tank around on the combat mat, fighting enemy units.

Game components include a crewmen placement mat, combat mat, tables book, crewmen status sheet, campaign log sheet, and counter sheet representing individual tanks, enemy units, etc.

As the pilot of a RAF Vickers Wellington bomber, your ultimate goal is to survive a 30-mission tour of duty. boardgame: Twin Stars: Adventure Series III – Scenario 14: Save the Spacewhales
description:
Two cosmic adventurers are in a tricky scenario and must work together. By rolling and manipulating dice for special abilities and combos, you can be the one that guides them to victory.

Save the Spacewhales is a scenario in the Twin Stars solo system by Jason Tagmire and Mike Mullins. The game consists of just 3 cards and players will provide their own dice and coins/tokens. Each scenario comes with 2 all-new characters and can be interchanged with others for a very different gaming experience.


This scenario was released as part of the Numbsters Kickstarter.

The moon is the next frontier of human civilization and mega-corporations rush to build colonies on the harsh lunar landscape. Joint ventures must be formed to complete the behemoth cities of the future, but the corporations, desperate to maximize profits and secure their legacy, never stop fighting for majority control.

In Lunar Chess, you and an opponent play as two such corporations. On your turn, you will add a card to the shared colony, enlarging groups of various development types: housing, crystal farms, and solar arrays. Every card you lay also activates the movement of your Speculator who is dispatched to make investments on your behalf. At the end of the game, you will identify the largest group of each development type. The player who made the majority of investments in these groups is the winner.

Lunar Chess borrows elements from Western chess and blends them with modern mechanisms like area control and tile-laying to create a unique gaming experience that feels part abstract and part strategy game. Lunar Chess is both tactical and strategic and is full of surprises.

-description from designer

x-Chess is a series of Chess variants, all of them played on a 5x6 board, each introducing one or more fairy pieces to a different setup.

x-Chess: The First Mutant (CC BY 4.0) is the first of the x-Chess variants, in which only one piece is a mutation: the Archbishop.

It's a game designed to be quick and dynamic.

Each player has 05 Pawns, 02 Rooks, 02 Archbishops, and 01 King. There are no Queens, Bishops, or Knights.

White pieces: Pawns fill the row from A2 to E2; Rooks are placed on A1 and E1; Archbishops, B1 and D1; King goes on C1. 
Black pieces: mirror the white pieces.

Rules

     Archbishops move like a bishop or a knight. Although they cannot jump over other pieces when moving as a bishop, they may do so when moving as a knight. They can checkmate all by themselves. 
     Castling is not allowed.
     Pawns cannot perform two-step moves as in regular Chess. Therefore, there is no en passant.
     Pawns can be promoted only to higher pieces already captured by your opponent. If there are no higher pieces available to proceed with the promotion, pawns cannot move to the last row of the board.
     The first player to checkmate their opponent wins. 


—description from the designer

3 CLUES Body, Bandage, White… MUMMY!! Itchy, Dog, Scratchy... FLEA!! Blow, Raise, Kite… WIND!!

Different "keys" appear on the cards, for example: "Sphinx". You have to give 3 clues, 3 words to your partner to discover the key... For example: "Egypt, huge" and your partner says: "Pyramid"; you say: "feline" and he yells: "Sphinx!", very good!

In this game the players will impersonate tangueros who will perform a Milonga (4 tandas of Tango, 1 of vals, 1 of milonga). At each turn they have to perform the mirada choosing their partner among the people sitting next to the dance floor and contend for him with their opponents; the loser will have to dance with a beginner who, instead of giving bonuses as the partner would do, will cause malus during the dance. Once on the dance floor, the players took turns playing their "figure" cards which they must replicate by moving on the dance floor according to the rules of the tango, i.e. without overtaking the opposing dancers, without colliding and so on; each correctly completed figure will award victory points. All this will be repeated for each tanda and at the end of the evening, the player who has collected the most gems will be the best tanguero.

In questo gioco i giocatori impersoneranno dei tangueri che si esibiranno in una Milonga (4 tande di Tango, 1 di vals, 1 di milonga). Ad ogni turno dovranno eseguire la mirada, ossia scegliere il proprio partner tra le persone sedute accanto alla pista di ballo e contenderselo con gli avversari; il perdente dovrà ballare con un principiante che anziché dare bonus come farebbe il partner, causerà dei malus durante il ballo. Una volta scesi in pista, a turno i giocatori dovranno giocare le proprie carte "figura" che dovranno replicare muovendosi sulla pista da ballo secondo le regole del tango, ossia senza superare i ballerini avversari, senza scontrarsi e via dicendo; ogni figura correttamente completata, regalerà punti vittoria. Tutto ciò si replicherà per ogni tanda ed alla fine della serata, il giocatore che avrà collezionato più gemme sarà il tanguero migliore.

boardgame: Pirate Blast!: Battle for Monkey Island
description:
Ready to play like a pirate? In this exciting fast paced card game, you are the pirate. Throw cannonballs at your friends ships. There are monkeys, parrots, coconuts and more to help you become the ruler of the seas. Pirate Blast is fun for all ages. See if you AARRGH the craftiest pirate and claim Monkey Island.

Using the cards you draw, sink your friends ships while trying to keep your ship afloat. Have the least damaged ship when everyone runs out of cards to win! Game contains enough cards for four ships. Each player gets 19 supply cards that includes monkeys, dolphins, cannon balls, bananas, coconuts, rum bottles and an octopus.

Did you enjoy the sweet feeling of playing Munchkin Babies? Have you been experiencing signs of baby fever? You’re in luck: Munchkin Babies 2 – Stork Naked will give you that newborn feeling again, without any labor on your part! Add this 56-card expansion to your Munchkin Babies game – or any fantasy Munchkin game.
It's all Developmentally Appropriate, and after all that fighting, the babies will surely Sleep Through the Night.

This is an abstract strategy game for two players. One player controls the Kindom of Hungary, the other controls the Ottoman Empire.
Each player has armies with 1, 2 or 3 movement points, and a General. These game pieces can move between cities connected by land and sea routes.
The player who can first capture the enemy Leader and move one of his pieces to the opponents Capital is the winner of the game.

On the other side of the board, you can play a Rome - Carthage war with the same rules on a different map.

Word Wipeout is the fast-paced word game that's sure to elevate your next family game night!  Race to collect the most letters by spelling a word related to the category.  The rounds get progressively more challenging as fewer letters remain.  Pick from 200 beginner or advanced categories in a race to get the word out!

boardgame: Dice Kingdoms of Valeria: Winter Expansion
description:
After a summer spent fortifying your duchy and subduing the monster-kin, winter's first snowfall puts you right back at square-one, but this time the monster-kin are better organized and harder to find. This winter-themed expansion for Dice Kingdoms of Valeria contains snowy castle and forest player pads (50 sheets each) featuring a new map with unfamiliar roads to your favorite domains and some new monster-slaying mechanics.

-description from Kickstarter campaign

Otete Sama is a horror dexterity game about a school girl who was found with no hands, hanging from a double-bay window, which had a bloody hand print on it.  Newspapers at the time attributed her death to a tragic accident caused by Western architecture.

The game comes with a set of cards and 3 red strings. One of the cards shows Otete's bloody hand print on the reverse side.

The cards are placed face down on the table, and surrounded by a red string.  One player takes on the role of Otete and the other are investigators from the Occult Research Society. The investigators close their eyes and hold a second red string with their pinky fingers.

The investigators each turn over cards; if they reveal the hand card, the Otete player grabs their wrist and that investigator can no longer reveal cards. However, those players can pull on the red string as a way to communicate to the remaining investigators.

Once the remaining investigators think they know where the hand is, they can announce it. The investigators carefully set down the red string and each remaining player has once chance to reveal the card.

Expansion #1
Series C

A werewolf and vampire descend on Fogmoor Manor, in search of a powerful artifact. But a secretive force lurks in the shadows,  pulling the strings and getting the Shivers up to their necks in trouble and chills!

This expansion adds The Creepy Crypt Pop-Up Room, 4 new episode guides, 8 new story cards, and 12 new NPC standees.
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

boardgame: The Shivers: It's All Fun and Games Until...
description:
Expansion #2
Series D

Requires base game and Expansion 1.

A few ghostly former residents of Fogmoor Manor pull the Shivers in for some light-hearted games, but the fun quickly turns diabolical as the family becomes trapped at the mercy of a devious child!

This expansion adds The Naughty Nursery Pop-Up Room, 4 new episode guides, 10 new story cards, and 14 new NPC standees.
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
This festive collection of brainteasers and challenges will entertain the whole family! Great for groups and easy to play. Just spin the dial to choose a category and draw a card.

—description from the publisher

A drawing game about compromise. The players work at a design firm and are torn between their bosses demands and their clients.  For instance the boss wants them to draw an elephant, but the client wants them to draw a giraffe.

In the game, a theme card is revealed, and the players each try to draw something that will be a compromise between the two themes.  The lead payer for the round will create a line of the drawings - from most fitting with the boss's theme to most fitting with the client's theme. The player whose drawing is in the middle position earns 1 point.

After a number of rounds, the person with the most points wins.

In "Floristry", the player becomes a flower shop trainee and creates colorful bouquets in response to customer requests.
In order to do so, it is necessary to procure flowers as materials while responding to changes in distribution and movements of other players.

Even if you start out with a small bouquet, with experience you will be able to create a wide variety of bouquets.

Players roll dice using dice pips to acquire flowers of different colors. Then players may use their flowers to gain bouquet contracts for points and other abilities. The game ends when one player has 8 contracts and the most points win.

Slipstream is a two-player abstract racing game.

Both players run four near-future machines, accumulate points obtained by being the first to pass some of the seven checkpoints and the first-come-first-served goal, and hope that their team will get more than half of all points.

The only random element is creating the race course. Players alternate moving their machines. The course has oval and circle spots. Machines must stop on oval spots but may continue through circles. Players should take advantage of slip streaming behind vehicles in the lead as it allows passing through oval spots.
